<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-integration-multi-tenant
3	description: Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation, with their own access + refresh tokens. Use whenever building a multi-tenant SaaS on Xero — every customer authorizes their own Xero and the app acts as that customer. Covers the full OAuth2 + PKCE lifecycle with the official `xero-node` SDK, per-user encrypted token storage, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits). Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see "Do you actually need this?" at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach.
4	---
5	
6	# Multi-tenant Xero (each user connects their own Xero)
7	
8	This skill is for a **multi-tenant SaaS**: every customer links **their own** Xero
9	organisation, and the app reads/writes Xero **as that customer**, with **their**
10	tokens. That means owning the entire OAuth2 lifecycle yourself.
11	
12	## Do you actually need this?
13	
14	Multi-tenant is real work — a per-user token vault, refresh rotation, tenant
15	selection. Don't take it on unless the app genuinely needs it. If the app talks to
16	a **single** Xero that the *builder* owns — an internal tool, a dashboard over your
17	own books, a single-merchant storefront, a prototype — **stop and use the
18	single-tenant approach instead**:
19	
20	- **Custom Connection** (recommended for single-org, server-to-server): a
21	  machine-to-machine OAuth2 client-credentials app tied to **one** organisation. No
22	  user-facing consent redirect, no refresh-token rotation to manage — you exchange
23	  client id/secret for a short-lived access token and call the API. Set this up in
24	  the Xero developer portal as a *Custom Connection* app.
25	- **A single stored refresh token**: register a normal Web App, run the consent
26	  flow **once** yourself, and persist the one resulting token set. Same `xero-node`
27	  SDK as below, but one token row instead of a vault.
28	
29	Use this multi-tenant skill **only** when each end user must connect their *own*
30	Xero. The rest of this document assumes that.
31	
32	> **Don't confuse Xero multi-org with multi-tenant.** `GET /connections` returns
33	> several *organisations* under ONE login — that is still one principal. Multi-tenant
34	> means many *separate* Xero logins, one per customer.
35	
36	## Decision check before building
37	
38	Build the full flow below only if ALL of these are true:
39	
40	- Each customer connects their **own** Xero org (not yours).
41	- The app acts under **each customer's** Xero identity, not a shared one.
42	- You can securely store and rotate per-user refresh tokens.
43	
44	If any is false, the single-tenant approach above is enough.
45	
46	## Use the official SDK: `xero-node`
47	
48	Multi-tenant means you own app registration, the per-user consent redirect, the
49	callback, token storage keyed by *your* user, refresh, and sending the right tenant
50	on every call. The official SDK handles PKCE, the code exchange, refresh, and date
51	parsing for you, so use it:
52	
53	```bash
54	# Install in whichever package owns your server code. Match the repo's package
55	# manager — check for the lockfile first:
56	#   pnpm-lock.yaml  -> pnpm add xero-node
57	#   package-lock.json -> npm install xero-node
58	#   yarn.lock       -> yarn add xero-node
59	#   bun.lockb       -> bun add xero-node
60	npm install xero-node
61	```
62	
63	Keep **all** Xero access in one provider module so paths, tenant handling, refresh,
64	and the gotchas below live in one place. Don't scatter token logic across the codebase.
65	
66	## 1. Register your own Xero app
67	
68	In the Xero developer portal (developer.xero.com) create a **Web App** OAuth2 app:
69	
70	- Grab the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
71	- Register your redirect URI **exactly** — scheme, host, port, and path must match
72	  byte-for-byte what your code sends, or the callback fails. Register both prod and
73	  local dev (Xero allows multiple redirect URIs):
74	  - prod: `https://your-app.example.com/oauth/xero/callback`
75	  - local: `http://localhost:3000/oauth/xero/callback`  *(adjust the port to your dev server)*
76	
77	  Xero permits `http://localhost` for development; every other redirect must be
78	  `https`. **Tell the user the exact redirect URIs to register for their prod and
79	  dev domains** rather than guessing.
80	- Decide your scopes (see the **Scopes** section). You MUST include
81	  **`offline_access`** or Xero returns no refresh token.
82	
83	Store **only your app's** Client ID/Secret as environment variables via your secret
84	manager — a local `.env` that is git-ignored for development, and your platform's
85	secret store in production. Also add a 32-byte **`TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (base64) for
86	encrypting stored tokens. **Never commit secrets, and never put per-user tokens in
87	env** — those go in the database, encrypted.
88	
89	## Scopes
90	
91	Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run the OAuth consent
92	flow to add more later. You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user
93	must re-consent. To get a refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
94	
95	### User / OpenID scopes (identity)
96	
97	| Scope | Description |
98	| --- | --- |
99	| `offline_access` | required for a refresh token (offline connection) |
100	| `openid` | use the user's identity |
101	| `profile` | first name, last name, full name, Xero user id |
102	| `email` | email address |
103	
104	### Accounting API — prefer granular scopes
105	
106	Xero is replacing **broad** scopes with **granular** ones. Since **March 2026** all
107	new and existing Web/PKCE apps are assigned granular scopes; custom connections
108	since **29 April 2026**. Broad scopes keep working for apps that already used them
109	only until **September 2027**. **Always prefer the granular scopes** — use
110	`accounting.invoices`, NOT `accounting.transactions`.
111	
112	Deprecated broad scope → the granular scopes that replace it:
113	
114	| Deprecated (broad) | New granular scopes |
115	| --- | --- |
116	| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` |
117	| `accounting.transactions.read` | `accounting.invoices.read`, `accounting.payments.read`, `accounting.banktransactions.read`, `accounting.manualjournals.read` |
118	| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read`, `accounting.reports.balancesheet.read`, `accounting.reports.banksummary.read`, `accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read`, `accounting.reports.executivesummary.read`, `accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`, `accounting.reports.trialbalance.read`, `accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |
119	
120	What each granular scope covers (read variants are the same but GET-only):
121	
122	| Scope | Grants |
123	| --- | --- |
124	| `accounting.invoices` | Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items |
125	| `accounting.payments` | Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments |
126	| `accounting.banktransactions` | BankTransactions, BankTransfers |
127	| `accounting.manualjournals` | ManualJournals |
128	| `accounting.reports.*.read` | the matching report only (balancesheet, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports = GSTReport/BASReport, etc.) |
129	
130	### Accounting scopes that were never broad (use as-is)
131	
132	| Scope | Grants |
133	| --- | --- |
134	| `accounting.settings` / `accounting.settings.read` | Accounts, BrandingThemes, Currencies, Items, InvoiceReminders, Organisation, TaxRates, TrackingCategories, Users |
135	| `accounting.contacts` / `accounting.contacts.read` | Contacts, ContactGroups |
136	| `accounting.attachments` / `accounting.attachments.read` | attachments across most resources |
137	| `accounting.journals.read` | Journals (general ledger) |
138	| `accounting.budgets.read` | Budgets |
139	
140	A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants:
141	
142	```
143	openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices
144	```
145	
146	Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change):
147	https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
148	
149	## 2. Per-user token store (database, encrypted)
150	
151	One row per (user, Xero tenant). `xero-node` hands you a full `tokenSet` — store it
152	encrypted as JSON, plus the selected `tenantId`. The example below uses Drizzle +
153	Postgres; adapt the column types to whatever ORM/DB the project already uses, but
154	keep the shape (your user id, the tenant id, the encrypted token set, the expiry).
155	
156	```ts
157	// schema/xeroConnections.ts
158	import { pgTable, serial, text, timestamp, uniqueIndex } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
159	
160	export const xeroConnections = pgTable(
161	  "xero_connections",
162	  {
163	    id: serial("id").primaryKey(),
164	    userId: text("user_id").notNull(),               // YOUR app's user
165	    tenantId: text("tenant_id").notNull(),           // Xero org id
166	    tenantName: text("tenant_name"),
167	    tokenSetEnc: text("token_set_enc").notNull(),     // ENCRYPTED JSON of the xero-node tokenSet
168	    expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull(),
169	    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
170	    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
171	  },
172	  (t) => ({ uq: uniqueIndex("xero_user_tenant").on(t.userId, t.tenantId) }),
173	);
174	```
175	
176	AES-256-GCM helpers (key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`):
177	
178	```ts
179	import { createCipheriv, createDecipheriv, randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
180	const KEY = Buffer.from(process.env.TOKEN_ENC_KEY!, "base64"); // 32 bytes
181	
182	export const encrypt = (plain: string) => {
183	  const iv = randomBytes(12);
184	  const c = createCipheriv("aes-256-gcm", KEY, iv);
185	  const enc = Buffer.concat([c.update(plain, "utf8"), c.final()]);
186	  return [iv, c.getAuthTag(), enc].map((b) => b.toString("base64")).join(".");
187	};
188	export const decrypt = (blob: string) => {
189	  const [iv, tag, enc] = blob.split(".").map((s) => Buffer.from(s, "base64"));
190	  const d = createDecipheriv("aes-256-gcm", KEY, iv);
191	  d.setAuthTag(tag);
192	  return Buffer.concat([d.update(enc), d.final()]).toString("utf8");
193	};
194	```
195	
196	## 3. The `xero-node` client (one per request — never cache)
197	
198	```ts
199	// lib/xeroClient.ts
200	import { XeroClient } from "xero-node";
201	
202	export const makeXeroClient = (state?: string) =>
203	  new XeroClient({
204	    clientId: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID!,
205	    clientSecret: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_SECRET!,
206	    redirectUris: [process.env.XERO_REDIRECT_URI!],
207	    scopes: (process.env.XERO_SCOPES ??
208	      "openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices").split(" "),
209	    state, // CSRF + carries which of YOUR users started the flow
210	  });
211	```
212	
213	Make a fresh client per request and `setTokenSet(...)` from the DB. Do not keep a
214	long-lived client — tokens expire and clients are per-user.
215	
216	## 4. Start consent (per user, with state)
217	
218	`state` is your CSRF defense and your link back to the initiating user. Persist it
219	server-side (short-lived row, or a signed cookie bound to the session) — never trust
220	it blind on return. `xero-node` does PKCE internally.
221	
222	```ts
223	// GET /oauth/xero/start
224	const state = randomBytes(16).toString("base64url");
225	await saveFlowState({ state, userId: req.user.id, expiresAt: Date.now() + 10 * 60_000 });
226	const xero = makeXeroClient(state);
227	const consentUrl = await xero.buildConsentUrl();
228	res.redirect(consentUrl);
229	```
230	
231	### Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)
232	
233	- **Redirect URI must match exactly.** The most common local failure is a port or
234	  trailing-slash mismatch between `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and what's registered in the
235	  portal. If consent returns `unauthorized_client` or an invalid-redirect error,
236	  this is almost always why. Run the dev server on the same port you registered.
237	- **`http://localhost` only.** Xero accepts `http` solely for `localhost`; any other
238	  host (including `127.0.0.1` in some setups, or a LAN IP) must be `https`. Use a
239	  real `localhost` URL in dev.
240	- **Don't embed the consent page in an iframe.** Xero's login page sets
241	  `frame-ancestors` and refuses to be framed — a same-frame redirect from inside an
242	  embedded preview/iframe shows a **blank white page**. If your front end runs in an
243	  iframe context, open consent as a top-level navigation (a new tab), and open the
244	  tab **synchronously inside the click handler** (before the `await` that fetches the
245	  URL) so the browser doesn't block it as a popup:
246	
247	  ```ts
248	  const inIframe = window.self !== window.top;
249	  const popup = inIframe ? window.open("about:blank", "_blank") : null;
250	  const { url } = await fetchConsentUrl();   // your GET /oauth/xero/start
251	  if (popup) popup.location.href = url;      // iframe: new tab
252	  else window.location.href = url;           // normal: same tab
253	  ```
254	
255	  A normally-served app isn't iframed, so a plain same-tab redirect is fine — handle
256	  both if you're unsure of the embedding context.
257	
258	## 5. Callback — exchange, pick tenant(s), store
259	
260	```ts
261	// GET /oauth/xero/callback?code=...&state=...
262	const flow = await loadFlowState(String(req.query.state)); // must exist, match session, unexpired
263	if (!flow) return res.status(400).send("Invalid state");
264	
265	// MUST recreate the client with the SAME state you used to build the consent URL.
266	// `apiCallback` runs openid-client's state check: because the consent URL carries a
267	// `state` param, the callback client must also know it, or you get the cryptic
268	// `TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` and every connect fails.
269	const xero = makeXeroClient(flow.state);
270	const tokenSet = await xero.apiCallback(req.url);   // exchanges code (+ PKCE) for tokens
271	await xero.updateTenants(false);                    // populates xero.tenants
272	
273	// A single consent can authorize MULTIPLE Xero orgs that all SHARE ONE token set.
274	// Either store all, or let the user choose which org this app should act on. Do NOT
275	// assume tenants[0]. Encrypt the token set ONCE and write the identical ciphertext to
276	// every row — this lets refresh (below) find and update all sibling rows at once.
277	const tokenSetEnc = encrypt(JSON.stringify(tokenSet));
278	for (const t of xero.tenants) {
279	  await upsertXeroConnection({
280	    userId: flow.userId,
281	    tenantId: t.tenantId,
282	    tenantName: t.tenantName,
283	    tokenSetEnc,
284	    expiresAt: new Date(tokenSet.expires_at! * 1000),
285	  });
286	}
287	await deleteFlowState(flow.state);
288	```
289	
290	## 6. Use it per request (refresh + persist rotation)
291	
292	```ts
293	async function getXeroFor(userId: string, tenantId: string) {
294	  const conn = await loadXeroConnection(userId, tenantId);
295	  if (!conn) throw new Error("User has not connected this Xero org");
296	
297	  const xero = makeXeroClient();
298	  await xero.setTokenSet(JSON.parse(decrypt(conn.tokenSetEnc)));
299	
300	  if (xero.readTokenSet().expired()) {
301	    const refreshed = await xero.refreshToken(); // throws if refresh token is dead
302	    // CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: when one consent authorized several orgs, they all
303	    // share ONE token set. Refreshing rotates the refresh token and INVALIDATES the
304	    // old one, so updating only THIS row leaves every sibling org holding a dead token
305	    // (they'll wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on their next call). Update ALL rows
306	    // that shared the previous token set — e.g. match on the old ciphertext:
307	    await updateXeroTokensForSharedSet(conn.userId, conn.tokenSetEnc, {
308	      tokenSetEnc: encrypt(JSON.stringify(refreshed)), // refresh token ROTATES — store it
309	      expiresAt: new Date(refreshed.expires_at! * 1000),
310	    });
311	  }
312	  return { xero, tenantId };
313	}
314	
315	// Example write (note the explicit tenantId, summarizeErrors, unitdp, idempotency):
316	const { xero } = await getXeroFor(userId, tenantId);
317	await xero.accountingApi.createInvoices(
318	  tenantId,
319	  { invoices: [{ /* type, contact, lineItems, ... */ }] },
320	  /* summarizeErrors */ false,
321	  /* unitdp */ 4,
322	  { idempotencyKey: order.idempotencyKey ?? crypto.randomUUID() },
323	);
324	```
325	
326	## Date + money + tax normalisation
327	
328	- **Dates.** The `xero-node` SDK parses Xero's Microsoft-JSON date form
329	  (`/Date(1518685950940+0000)/`) into JS `Date` for you. If you ever drop to raw REST
330	  (e.g. a paged report endpoint), `new Date()` **cannot** parse that string — run it
331	  through a normaliser and emit ISO 8601:
332	
333	  ```ts
334	  export function normalizeXeroDate(v: unknown): Date | null {
335	    if (v == null) return null;
336	    if (v instanceof Date) return isNaN(v.getTime()) ? null : v;
337	    if (typeof v === "string") {
338	      const m = v.match(/\/Date\((-?\d+)([+-]\d{4})?\)\//);
339	      const d = m ? new Date(Number(m[1])) : new Date(v);
340	      return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? null : d;
341	    }
342	    return null;
343	  }
344	  ```
345	
346	  When **sending** dates, use `YYYY-MM-DD`.
347	
348	- **Money.** Pass numbers, not pre-formatted currency strings. Control rounding with
349	  the `unitdp` (unit decimal places, up to 4) argument on create calls. If you store
350	  money in Postgres `numeric` (returned as strings by Drizzle), convert back to
351	  numbers before any response validation/parse. Amounts like `AmountDue` come back as
352	  numbers.
353	
354	- **Tax is the org's, not yours.** Each connected organisation applies **its own** tax
355	  config to the invoice, so `AmountDue` / `Total` may exceed the sum of your line
356	  amounts — e.g. a 15% GST org turns a 2495 line into 2869.25. The `UnitAmount` you
357	  posted is still correct; do **not** "fix" this discrepancy. Set `lineAmountTypes`
358	  (`Exclusive`, `Inclusive`, `NoTax`) deliberately if you need control. **In
359	  multi-tenant this varies per customer** — never hard-code a tax assumption; whatever
360	  each org returns is correct for that org.
361	
362	## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)
363	
364	Writes can be duplicated by retries and double-clicks. Defend at two layers.
365	
366	**Layer 1 — app level (cheapest):** don't call Xero twice for the same logical
367	action. Upsert contacts by email (see gotchas) and dedup the order itself on a stable
368	identifier (client-supplied token, or a DB unique constraint) so a double-submitted
369	form never reaches Xero twice.
370	
371	**Layer 2 — Xero idempotency (the safety net):** pass an idempotency key on the
372	mutating call (`xero-node`: the `{ idempotencyKey }` options argument; raw REST: the
373	`Idempotency-Key` header). Xero caches the first response and returns it for any
374	repeat with the same key, so a network-timeout retry can't create a second invoice.
375	
376	Rules that bite (from Xero's idempotency guide):
377	
378	- **Only POST/PUT/PATCH** honour the key; it is ignored on GET.
379	- **Max 128 characters.** A single `crypto.randomUUID()` (36 chars) is plenty unique —
380	  use that as the default. Xero's "concatenate four UUIDs" advice is for extreme
381	  volume; if you do that, strip hyphens (4 × 32 = 128) to stay in limit.
382	- **Keys expire after ~6 minutes.** They protect transient retries, not long-term
383	  dedup. Long-term dedup is Layer 1's job.
384	- **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A
385	  request differs if the URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key alongside the
386	  record and reuse the *identical* request when retrying.
387	- **Errors are cached too.** If the keyed request errored internally, retrying with the
388	  *same* key returns the *same cached error* even after the underlying problem is
389	  fixed. To recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if
390	  not, create again with a **new** key.
391	
392	When a user *re-submits* a previously **failed** order later, mint a **new** key (the
393	old one has expired, and if it hasn't, a cached error would block you) — but first GET
394	to confirm no invoice was actually created, so you don't duplicate one that silently
395	succeeded.
396	
397	## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)
398	
399	- **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and you get an access token
400	  that dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.
401	- **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on
402	  every refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time or the next
403	  refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.
404	- **Always pass `tenantId` explicitly** to every `accountingApi.*` call. There is no
405	  implicit "current org." With multiple connected orgs, the wrong tenantId silently
406	  writes to the wrong company's books.
407	- **`updateTenants()` is required** after callback (and worth re-running periodically)
408	  — the connected-org list can change as users add/remove orgs.
409	- **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new
410	  contact name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or
411	  `ContactID`) via `getContacts` and reuse the id; otherwise you litter the org with
412	  duplicates.
413	- **Invoice status flow:** `DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`. Only `AUTHORISED`
414	  invoices are real receivables and expose an online payment URL (`getOnlineInvoice`).
415	  Use `ACCREC` (sales) vs `ACCPAY` (bills) correctly.
416	- **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide
417	  per-minute cap and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header
418	  and back off — multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Spread/queue
419	  calls per tenant.
420	- **`summarizeErrors`:** pass `false` on batch create calls to get per-item validation
421	  errors back instead of one opaque failure.
422	
423	## Security checklist (do not skip)
424	
425	- **Encrypt** the stored token set at rest; the key (`TOKEN_ENC_KEY`) lives in your
426	  secret manager / env and is never committed.
427	- **Verify `state`** on every callback and bind it to the session (CSRF).
428	- PKCE is handled by `xero-node` — don't disable it.
429	- **Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage.
430	- On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — do not
431	  silently retry forever.
432	- Keep all Xero access in **one module** so paths, tenant handling, and gotchas live
433	  in one place.
434	- Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`; never commit credentials.
435	
436	## Don't want to hand-roll it?
437	
438	Managed token-vault platforms (**Nango**, **Composio**, etc.) implement per-user OAuth
439	+ refresh + encryption for Xero and many other providers. For a multi-provider SaaS
440	this is often the pragmatic choice — you still call the Xero API the same way, but the
441	vault and rotation are managed for you.
442	
443	## Verify
444	
445	1. Two app users each connect a **different** Xero org; confirm two distinct token rows
446	   and that each user's writes land in their own org (check `tenantId`).
447	2. Force a near-expiry token and confirm transparent refresh **and** that the rotated
448	   refresh token is persisted.
449	3. Revoke the app in one user's Xero and confirm your app surfaces a "reconnect" state
450	   instead of looping on failed refreshes.
451	4. Post the same invoice twice with the same idempotency key and confirm only one
452	   invoice is created.
453
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/lib/oauth.ts`

```text
1	import {createServer, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse} from 'node:http'
2	import {randomBytes, createHash} from 'node:crypto'
3	import {select} from '@inquirer/prompts'
4	
5	const XERO_AUTH_BASE = 'https://login.xero.com/identity'
6	const XERO_TOKEN_BASE = 'https://identity.xero.com'
7	const REDIRECT_URI = 'http://localhost:8742/callback'
8	const CALLBACK_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000
9	
10	const REQUIRED_OAUTH_SCOPES = ['openid', 'profile', 'email', 'offline_access']
11	
12	const SCOPES = [
13	  ...REQUIRED_OAUTH_SCOPES,
14	  // Contacts & settings
15	  'accounting.contacts',
16	  'accounting.settings',
17	  // Granular transaction scopes
18	  'accounting.invoices',
19	  'accounting.payments',
20	  'accounting.banktransactions',
21	  'accounting.manualjournals',
22	  // Granular report scopes
23	  'accounting.reports.aged.read',
24	  'accounting.reports.balancesheet.read',
25	  'accounting.reports.profitandloss.read',
26	  'accounting.reports.trialbalance.read',
27	  // Other
28	  'accounting.budgets.read',
29	  'accounting.attachments',
30	].join(' ')
31	
32	interface TokenSet {
33	  access_token: string
34	  refresh_token?: string
35	  expires_in?: number
36	  expires_at?: number
37	  id_token?: string
38	  token_type?: string
39	  scope?: string
40	}
41	
42	interface XeroTenant {
43	  id: string
44	  authEventId: string
45	  tenantId: string
46	  tenantType: string
47	  tenantName: string
48	}
49	
50	function generateCodeVerifier(): string {
51	  return randomBytes(32).toString('base64url')
52	}
53	
54	function generateCodeChallenge(verifier: string): string {
55	  return createHash('sha256').update(verifier).digest('base64url')
56	}
57	
58	function resolveScopes(scopes?: string): string {
59	  if (!scopes) return SCOPES
60	  const requested = scopes.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean)
61	  return [...new Set([...REQUIRED_OAUTH_SCOPES, ...requested])].join(' ')
62	}
63	
64	function buildAuthUrl(clientId: string, codeChallenge: string, state: string, scopes?: string): string {
65	  const params = new URLSearchParams({
66	    response_type: 'code',
67	    client_id: clientId,
68	    redirect_uri: REDIRECT_URI,
69	    scope: resolveScopes(scopes),
70	    code_challenge: codeChallenge,
71	    code_challenge_method: 'S256',
72	    state,
73	  })
74	  return `${XERO_AUTH_BASE}/connect/authorize?${params.toString()}`
75	}
76	
77	function waitForCallback(expectedState: string): Promise<string> {
78	  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
79	    const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
80	      server.close()
81	      reject(new Error('OAuth callback timed out after 2 minutes. Please try again.'))
82	    }, CALLBACK_TIMEOUT_MS)
83	
84	    const server = createServer((req: IncomingMessage, res: ServerResponse) => {
85	      const url = new URL(req.url ?? '/', `http://localhost:8742`)
86	      if (url.pathname !== '/callback') {
87	        res.writeHead(404)
88	        res.end('Not found')
89	        return
90	      }
91	
92	      const code = url.searchParams.get('code')
93	      const state = url.searchParams.get('state')
94	      const error = url.searchParams.get('error')
95	
96	      if (error) {
97	        const desc = url.searchParams.get('error_description') ?? error
98	        res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/html'})
99	        res.end(`<html><body><h1>Authentication Failed</h1><p>${desc}</p><p>You can close this window.</p></body></html>`)
100	        clearTimeout(timeout)
101	        server.close()
102	        reject(new Error(`OAuth error: ${desc}`))
103	        return
104	      }
105	
106	      if (!code || state !== expectedState) {
107	        res.writeHead(400, {'Content-Type': 'text/html'})
108	        res.end('<html><body><h1>Invalid Request</h1><p>Missing code or state mismatch.</p></body></html>')
109	        return
110	      }
111	
112	      res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/html'})
113	      res.end('<html><body><h1>Success!</h1><p>You are now logged in. You can close this window.</p></body></html>')
114	      clearTimeout(timeout)
115	      server.close()
116	      resolve(code)
117	    })
118	
119	    server.listen(8742, '127.0.0.1', () => {
120	      // Server ready
121	    })
122	
123	    server.on('error', (err) => {
124	      clearTimeout(timeout)
125	      if ((err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
126	        reject(new Error('Port 8742 is already in use. Close any other login attempts and try again.'))
127	      } else {
128	        reject(err)
129	      }
130	    })
131	  })
132	}
133	
134	async function exchangeCodeForTokens(clientId: string, code: string, codeVerifier: string): Promise<TokenSet> {
135	  const body = new URLSearchParams({
136	    grant_type: 'authorization_code',
137	    client_id: clientId,
138	    code,
139	    redirect_uri: REDIRECT_URI,
140	    code_verifier: codeVerifier,
141	  })
142	
143	  const response = await fetch(`${XERO_TOKEN_BASE}/connect/token`, {
144	    method: 'POST',
145	    headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},
146	    body: body.toString(),
147	  })
148	
149	  if (!response.ok) {
150	    const text = await response.text()
151	    throw new Error(`Token exchange failed (${response.status}): ${text}`)
152	  }
153	
154	  return response.json() as Promise<TokenSet>
155	}
156	
157	async function fetchTenants(accessToken: string): Promise<XeroTenant[]> {
158	  const response = await fetch('https://api.xero.com/connections', {
159	    headers: {Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`},
160	  })
161	
162	  if (!response.ok) {
163	    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch Xero tenants (${response.status})`)
164	  }
165	
166	  return response.json() as Promise<XeroTenant[]>
167	}
168	
169	export async function performLogin(
170	  clientId: string,
171	  scopes?: string,
172	): Promise<{tokenSet: TokenSet; tenantId: string; tenantName: string}> {
173	  const {default: open} = await import('open')
174	
175	  const codeVerifier = generateCodeVerifier()
176	  const codeChallenge = generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier)
177	  const state = randomBytes(16).toString('hex')
178	
179	  const authUrl = buildAuthUrl(clientId, codeChallenge, state, scopes)
180	
181	  // Start the callback server before opening the browser
182	  const codePromise = waitForCallback(state)
183	  await open(authUrl)
184	
185	  const code = await codePromise
186	  const tokenSet = await exchangeCodeForTokens(clientId, code, codeVerifier)
187	
188	  // Resolve tenant
189	  const tenants = await fetchTenants(tokenSet.access_token)
190	
191	  if (tenants.length === 0) {
192	    throw new Error('No Xero organisations found. Please connect at least one organisation to your app.')
193	  }
194	
195	  let tenant: XeroTenant
196	  if (tenants.length === 1) {
197	    tenant = tenants[0]
198	  } else {
199	    const selected = await select({
200	      message: 'Select a Xero organisation:',
201	      choices: tenants.map((t) => ({name: t.tenantName, value: t.tenantId})),
202	    })
203	    tenant = tenants.find((t) => t.tenantId === selected)!
204	  }
205	
206	  return {tokenSet, tenantId: tenant.tenantId, tenantName: tenant.tenantName}
207	}
208	
209	export async function refreshAccessToken(
210	  clientId: string,
211	  refreshToken: string,
212	): Promise<TokenSet> {
213	  const body = new URLSearchParams({
214	    grant_type: 'refresh_token',
215	    client_id: clientId,
216	    refresh_token: refreshToken,
217	  })
218	
219	  const response = await fetch(`${XERO_TOKEN_BASE}/connect/token`, {
220	    method: 'POST',
221	    headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},
222	    body: body.toString(),
223	  })
224	
225	  if (!response.ok) {
226	    const text = await response.text()
227	    throw new Error(`Token refresh failed (${response.status}): ${text}`)
228	  }
229	
230	  return response.json() as Promise<TokenSet>
231	}
232
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/lib/auth.ts`

```text
1	import {existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync} from 'node:fs'
2	import {join} from 'node:path'
3	import {encrypt, decrypt, getOrCreateKey, CONFIG_DIR, EncryptionKeyError} from './crypto.js'
4	
5	export interface TokenEntry {
6	  accessToken: string
7	  refreshToken: string
8	  expiresAt: number // Unix timestamp in ms
9	  tenantId: string
10	  tenantName?: string
11	}
12	
13	interface EncryptedTokenEntry {
14	  accessToken: string // encrypted
15	  refreshToken: string // encrypted
16	  expiresAt: number
17	  tenantId: string
18	  tenantName?: string
19	}
20	
21	interface TokenCache {
22	  [profileName: string]: EncryptedTokenEntry
23	}
24	
25	const TOKEN_PATH = join(CONFIG_DIR, 'tokens.json')
26	const TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000 // Refresh 60s before expiry
27	
28	function ensureConfigDir(): void {
29	  if (!existsSync(CONFIG_DIR)) {
30	    mkdirSync(CONFIG_DIR, {recursive: true})
31	  }
32	}
33	
34	function readTokenCache(): TokenCache {
35	  ensureConfigDir()
36	  if (!existsSync(TOKEN_PATH)) {
37	    return {}
38	  }
39	  try {
40	    return JSON.parse(readFileSync(TOKEN_PATH, 'utf-8')) as TokenCache
41	  } catch {
42	    return {}
43	  }
44	}
45	
46	function writeTokenCache(cache: TokenCache): void {
47	  ensureConfigDir()
48	  writeFileSync(TOKEN_PATH, JSON.stringify(cache, null, 2), {mode: 0o600})
49	}
50	
51	export async function getCachedTokenSet(profileName: string): Promise<TokenEntry | null> {
52	  const cache = readTokenCache()
53	  const entry = cache[profileName]
54	  if (!entry) return null
55	
56	  try {
57	    const key = await getOrCreateKey()
58	    return {
59	      accessToken: decrypt(entry.accessToken, key),
60	      refreshToken: decrypt(entry.refreshToken, key),
61	      expiresAt: entry.expiresAt,
62	      tenantId: entry.tenantId,
63	      tenantName: entry.tenantName,
64	    }
65	  } catch (error) {
66	    if (error instanceof EncryptionKeyError) throw error
67	    throw new EncryptionKeyError(
68	      'Could not decrypt cached tokens. Run "xero login" to re-authenticate.',
69	    )
70	  }
71	}
72	
73	export function isTokenExpired(entry: TokenEntry): boolean {
74	  return Date.now() >= entry.expiresAt - TOKEN_BUFFER_MS
75	}
76	
77	export async function cacheTokenSet(
78	  profileName: string,
79	  tokenSet: {access_token?: string; refresh_token?: string; expires_in?: number; expires_at?: number},
80	  tenantId: string,
81	  tenantName?: string,
82	): Promise<void> {
83	  const accessToken = tokenSet.access_token
84	  const refreshToken = tokenSet.refresh_token
85	  if (!accessToken || !refreshToken) return
86	
87	  let expiresAt: number
88	  if (tokenSet.expires_at) {
89	    // expires_at is in seconds since epoch
90	    expiresAt = tokenSet.expires_at * 1000
91	  } else if (tokenSet.expires_in) {
92	    expiresAt = Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000
93	  } else {
94	    // Default 30 min
95	    expiresAt = Date.now() + 1800 * 1000
96	  }
97	
98	  const key = await getOrCreateKey()
99	  const cache = readTokenCache()
100	  cache[profileName] = {
101	    accessToken: encrypt(accessToken, key),
102	    refreshToken: encrypt(refreshToken, key),
103	    expiresAt,
104	    tenantId,
105	    tenantName,
106	  }
107	  writeTokenCache(cache)
108	}
109	
110	export function clearCachedToken(profileName: string): void {
111	  const cache = readTokenCache()
112	  delete cache[profileName]
113	  writeTokenCache(cache)
114	}
115
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/python/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-integration-multi-tenant-python
3	description: Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration in PYTHON where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation, with their own access + refresh tokens. Use whenever building a multi-tenant Python SaaS on Xero (Flask, FastAPI, Django, etc.) — every customer authorizes their own Xero and the app acts as that customer. Covers the OAuth2 + PKCE consent leg with an OAuth client (Authlib), the official `xero-python` SDK for API calls and refresh, per-user encrypted token storage, the global token getter/saver trap, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits). Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code in Python. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see "Do you actually need this?" at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach. For a JavaScript/TypeScript app, use the `xero-integration-multi-tenant` (xero-node) skill instead.
4	---
5	
6	# Multi-tenant Xero in Python (each user connects their own Xero)
7	
8	This skill is for a **multi-tenant SaaS written in Python**: every customer links
9	**their own** Xero organisation, and the app reads/writes Xero **as that customer**,
10	with **their** tokens. That means owning the entire OAuth2 lifecycle yourself.
11	
12	The official `xero-python` SDK is **not** a full OAuth client. It manages the *token*
13	(storage hook, refresh, API calls) but does **not** perform the consent redirect or
14	the code exchange — you bring your own OAuth client (Authlib below) for that leg, then
15	hand the resulting token to `xero-python`. Internalise that split before you start.
16	
17	## Do you actually need this?
18	
19	Multi-tenant is real work — a per-user token vault, refresh rotation, request-scoped
20	tenant context. Don't take it on unless the app needs it. If the app talks to a
21	**single** Xero that the *builder* owns — an internal tool, a dashboard over your own
22	books, a single-merchant storefront, a prototype — **stop and use the single-tenant
23	approach instead**:
24	
25	- **Custom Connection** (recommended for single-org, server-to-server): a
26	  machine-to-machine client-credentials app tied to **one** organisation, no consent
27	  redirect. `xero-python` exchanges your client id/secret directly:
28	  ```python
29	  api_client = ApiClient(Configuration(oauth2_token=OAuth2Token(
30	      client_id=CLIENT_ID, client_secret=CLIENT_SECRET)))
31	  api_client.get_client_credentials_token()   # no user-facing redirect
32	  AccountingApi(api_client).get_organisations("")  # tenant id often "" for custom connections
33	  ```
34	- **A single stored refresh token**: register a normal Web App, run the consent flow
35	  **once** yourself, persist the one resulting token dict, and refresh it on a schedule.
36	
37	Use this multi-tenant skill **only** when each end user must connect their *own* Xero.
38	
39	> **Don't confuse Xero multi-org with multi-tenant.** `GET /connections` returns several
40	> *organisations* under ONE login — still one principal. Multi-tenant means many
41	> *separate* Xero logins, one per customer.
42	
43	## Decision check before building
44	
45	Build the full flow below only if ALL are true:
46	
47	- Each customer connects their **own** Xero org (not yours).
48	- The app acts under **each customer's** Xero identity, not a shared one.
49	- You can securely store and rotate per-user refresh tokens.
50	
51	If any is false, the single-tenant approach above is enough.
52	
53	## Libraries
54	
55	```bash
56	# Match the project's package manager — check for the lockfile first:
57	#   uv.lock          -> uv add xero-python authlib cryptography sqlalchemy
58	#   poetry.lock      -> poetry add xero-python authlib cryptography sqlalchemy
59	#   Pipfile.lock     -> pipenv install xero-python authlib cryptography sqlalchemy
60	#   requirements.txt -> pip install xero-python authlib cryptography sqlalchemy
61	pip install xero-python authlib cryptography sqlalchemy
62	```
63	
64	- **`xero-python`** — official SDK: API calls, token refresh, tenant discovery.
65	- **`authlib`** — the OAuth client for the consent + code-exchange leg, with clean PKCE
66	  support. (The official Xero samples use `flask-oauthlib`, which is **deprecated** —
67	  prefer Authlib.) Works framework-agnostically with Flask, FastAPI, or Django.
68	- **`cryptography`** — AES-GCM encryption for tokens at rest.
69	- **`sqlalchemy`** — example token store; swap for the project's existing ORM.
70	
71	Keep **all** Xero access in one module so paths, tenant handling, refresh, the
72	getter/saver wiring, and the gotchas below live in one place.
73	
74	## 1. Register your own Xero app
75	
76	In the Xero developer portal (developer.xero.com) create a **Web App** OAuth2 app:
77	
78	- Grab the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
79	- Register your redirect URI **exactly** — scheme, host, port, and path must match
80	  byte-for-byte what your code sends. Register both prod and local dev:
81	  - prod: `https://your-app.example.com/oauth/xero/callback`
82	  - local: `http://localhost:8000/oauth/xero/callback`  *(match your dev server port)*
83	
84	  Xero permits `http://localhost` for development; every other redirect must be `https`.
85	  **Tell the user the exact redirect URIs to register** rather than guessing.
86	- Decide your scopes (see **Scopes**). You MUST include **`offline_access`** or Xero
87	  returns no refresh token.
88	
89	Store **only your app's** Client ID/Secret as environment variables via your secret
90	manager — a git-ignored `.env` locally, your platform's secret store in prod. Also add a
91	32-byte **`TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (base64) for encrypting stored tokens. **Never commit
92	secrets, and never put per-user tokens in env** — those go in the database, encrypted.
93	
94	## Scopes
95	
96	Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run consent to add more.
97	You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user must re-consent. To get a
98	refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
99	
100	### User / OpenID scopes (identity)
101	
102	| Scope | Description |
103	| --- | --- |
104	| `offline_access` | required for a refresh token (offline connection) |
105	| `openid` | use the user's identity |
106	| `profile` | first name, last name, full name, Xero user id |
107	| `email` | email address |
108	
109	### Accounting API — prefer granular scopes
110	
111	Xero is replacing **broad** scopes with **granular** ones. Since **March 2026** all new
112	and existing Web/PKCE apps are assigned granular scopes; custom connections since
113	**29 April 2026**. Broad scopes keep working for apps that already used them only until
114	**September 2027**. **Always prefer the granular scopes** — use `accounting.invoices`,
115	NOT `accounting.transactions`.
116	
117	Deprecated broad scope → the granular scopes that replace it:
118	
119	| Deprecated (broad) | New granular scopes |
120	| --- | --- |
121	| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` |
122	| `accounting.transactions.read` | `accounting.invoices.read`, `accounting.payments.read`, `accounting.banktransactions.read`, `accounting.manualjournals.read` |
123	| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read`, `accounting.reports.balancesheet.read`, `accounting.reports.banksummary.read`, `accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read`, `accounting.reports.executivesummary.read`, `accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`, `accounting.reports.trialbalance.read`, `accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |
124	
125	What each granular scope covers (read variants are the same but GET-only):
126	
127	| Scope | Grants |
128	| --- | --- |
129	| `accounting.invoices` | Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items |
130	| `accounting.payments` | Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments |
131	| `accounting.banktransactions` | BankTransactions, BankTransfers |
132	| `accounting.manualjournals` | ManualJournals |
133	| `accounting.reports.*.read` | the matching report only (balancesheet, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports = GSTReport/BASReport, etc.) |
134	
135	### Accounting scopes that were never broad (use as-is)
136	
137	| Scope | Grants |
138	| --- | --- |
139	| `accounting.settings` / `accounting.settings.read` | Accounts, BrandingThemes, Currencies, Items, InvoiceReminders, Organisation, TaxRates, TrackingCategories, Users |
140	| `accounting.contacts` / `accounting.contacts.read` | Contacts, ContactGroups |
141	| `accounting.attachments` / `accounting.attachments.read` | attachments across most resources |
142	| `accounting.journals.read` | Journals (general ledger) |
143	| `accounting.budgets.read` | Budgets |
144	
145	A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants:
146	
147	```
148	openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices
149	```
150	
151	Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change):
152	https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
153	
154	## 2. Per-user token store (database, encrypted)
155	
156	One row per (user, Xero tenant). The token is an oauthlib-style dict
157	(`access_token`, `refresh_token`, `expires_at`, `token_type`, `scope`, `id_token`) —
158	store it encrypted as JSON, plus the selected `tenant_id`. Example uses SQLAlchemy;
159	adapt column types to the project's ORM but keep the shape.
160	
161	```python
162	# models.py
163	from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, DateTime, UniqueConstraint, func
164	from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base
165	
166	Base = declarative_base()
167	
168	class XeroConnection(Base):
169	    __tablename__ = "xero_connections"
170	    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
171	    user_id = Column(String, nullable=False)        # YOUR app's user
172	    tenant_id = Column(String, nullable=False)       # Xero org id
173	    tenant_name = Column(String)
174	    token_set_enc = Column(String, nullable=False)   # ENCRYPTED JSON of the token dict
175	    expires_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
176	    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
177	    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())
178	    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint("user_id", "tenant_id", name="xero_user_tenant"),)
179	```
180	
181	AES-256-GCM helpers (key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`):
182	
183	```python
184	# crypto.py
185	import os, base64
186	from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
187	
188	KEY = base64.b64decode(os.environ["TOKEN_ENC_KEY"])  # 32 bytes
189	
190	def encrypt(plain: str) -> str:
191	    nonce = os.urandom(12)
192	    ct = AESGCM(KEY).encrypt(nonce, plain.encode(), None)
193	    return base64.b64encode(nonce + ct).decode()
194	
195	def decrypt(blob: str) -> str:
196	    raw = base64.b64decode(blob)
197	    return AESGCM(KEY).decrypt(raw[:12], raw[12:], None).decode()
198	```
199	
200	## 3. The biggest difference from the JS SDK: token getter/saver are GLOBAL
201	
202	`xero-python` does not take a token per call. Instead you register two callbacks on the
203	`ApiClient` — a **getter** it calls to read the current token before each request, and a
204	**saver** it calls after a refresh. The official sample wires these to the Flask
205	`session`, i.e. **one user**. In multi-tenant that is a trap: a global session means
206	every request shares one token. **Wire the getter/saver to request-scoped context** that
207	resolves *which* connection the current request is acting as. Use a `ContextVar` so it is
208	correct under threads and async.
209	
210	```python
211	# xero.py
212	import json, contextvars
213	from dataclasses import dataclass
214	from xero_python.api_client import ApiClient
215	from xero_python.api_client.configuration import Configuration
216	from xero_python.api_client.oauth2 import OAuth2Token
217	from .crypto import encrypt, decrypt
218	
219	@dataclass
220	class ConnCtx:
221	    user_id: str
222	    tenant_id: str
223	    token: dict          # current oauthlib token dict
224	    token_set_enc: str   # ciphertext of the token set as loaded (for sibling refresh)
225	
226	current_conn: contextvars.ContextVar[ConnCtx | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
227	    "current_conn", default=None)
228	
229	api_client = ApiClient(
230	    Configuration(oauth2_token=OAuth2Token(
231	        client_id=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_ID"],
232	        client_secret=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"],
233	    )),
234	    pool_threads=1,
235	)
236	
237	@api_client.oauth2_token_getter
238	def _get_token():
239	    ctx = current_conn.get()
240	    if ctx is None:
241	        raise RuntimeError("No Xero connection context set for this request")
242	    return ctx.token
243	
244	@api_client.oauth2_token_saver
245	def _save_token(token: dict):
246	    ctx = current_conn.get()
247	    # CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: one consent can authorize several orgs that SHARE one
248	    # token set. A refresh rotates (and invalidates) the old refresh token, so update
249	    # EVERY row that shared the previous ciphertext — not just this tenant's row — or the
250	    # siblings will hold a dead token and wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on next call.
251	    update_tokens_for_shared_set(ctx.user_id, ctx.token_set_enc, encrypt(json.dumps(token)),
252	                                 expires_at=token["expires_at"])
253	    ctx.token = token
254	```
255	
256	## 4. Start consent (per user, with state AND code_verifier)
257	
258	Authlib runs the OAuth leg. PKCE means there is a **`code_verifier`** the callback needs;
259	because the callback is a *separate* HTTP request, persist both `state` (CSRF + link to
260	the initiating user) **and** `code_verifier` server-side, keyed to the user. The SDK will
261	not remember either for you.
262	
263	```python
264	from authlib.integrations.requests_client import OAuth2Session
265	
266	AUTH_URL = "https://login.xero.com/identity/connect/authorize"
267	TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token"
268	SCOPES = os.environ.get("XERO_SCOPES",
269	    "openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices")
270	
271	def build_consent_url(user_id: str) -> str:
272	    oauth = OAuth2Session(
273	        client_id=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_ID"],
274	        client_secret=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"],
275	        scope=SCOPES,
276	        redirect_uri=os.environ["XERO_REDIRECT_URI"],
277	        code_challenge_method="S256",          # PKCE
278	    )
279	    url, state = oauth.create_authorization_url(AUTH_URL)
280	    save_flow_state(user_id=user_id, state=state,
281	                    code_verifier=oauth.code_verifier,   # MUST persist for the callback
282	                    expires_at=now() + timedelta(minutes=10))
283	    return url
284	```
285	
286	### Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)
287	
288	- **Redirect URI must match exactly.** A port or trailing-slash mismatch between
289	  `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and the portal registration is the most common local failure
290	  (`unauthorized_client` / invalid redirect). Run the dev server on the registered port.
291	- **`http://localhost` only.** Xero accepts `http` solely for `localhost`.
292	- **`oauthlib` refuses plain http.** For local `http://localhost` testing set
293	  `os.environ["OAUTHLIB_INSECURE_TRANSPORT"] = "1"` **in dev only** — never in prod.
294	- **Don't embed the consent page in an iframe.** Xero's login sets `frame-ancestors` and
295	  refuses framing — a same-frame redirect from an embedded preview shows a blank page.
296	  Open consent as a top-level navigation / new tab.
297	
298	## 5. Callback — exchange, discover tenants, store
299	
300	```python
301	from xero_python.identity import IdentityApi
302	
303	def handle_callback(request_url: str, state: str):
304	    flow = load_flow_state(state)                       # must exist, match session, unexpired
305	    if not flow:
306	        raise ValueError("Invalid state")
307	
308	    oauth = OAuth2Session(
309	        client_id=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_ID"],
310	        client_secret=os.environ["XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"],
311	        redirect_uri=os.environ["XERO_REDIRECT_URI"],
312	        code_verifier=flow.code_verifier,               # PKCE: same verifier as consent
313	    )
314	    token = oauth.fetch_token(TOKEN_URL, authorization_response=request_url)  # dict
315	
316	    # Hand the token to the SDK via a one-shot context so IdentityApi can use it.
317	    ctx = ConnCtx(user_id=flow.user_id, tenant_id="", token=dict(token),
318	                  token_set_enc=encrypt(json.dumps(dict(token))))
319	    current_conn.set(ctx)
320	
321	    # A single consent can authorize MULTIPLE orgs that all SHARE ONE token set.
322	    # Don't assume connections[0]: store all, or let the user pick. Encrypt ONCE and write
323	    # the identical ciphertext to every row so refresh can find and update siblings.
324	    connections = IdentityApi(api_client).get_connections()
325	    token_set_enc = encrypt(json.dumps(dict(token)))
326	    for c in connections:                               # c.tenant_id, c.tenant_name, c.tenant_type
327	        if c.tenant_type != "ORGANISATION":
328	            continue
329	        upsert_connection(user_id=flow.user_id, tenant_id=c.tenant_id,
330	                          tenant_name=c.tenant_name, token_set_enc=token_set_enc,
331	                          expires_at=token["expires_at"])
332	    delete_flow_state(state)
333	```
334	
335	## 6. Use it per request (set context, refresh, persist rotation)
336	
337	```python
338	import time, json
339	from contextlib import contextmanager
340	from xero_python.accounting import AccountingApi, Invoice, Invoices, Contact
341	
342	@contextmanager
343	def xero_for(user_id: str, tenant_id: str):
344	    conn = load_connection(user_id, tenant_id)
345	    if not conn:
346	        raise LookupError("User has not connected this Xero org")
347	    token = json.loads(decrypt(conn.token_set_enc))
348	    ctx = ConnCtx(user_id, tenant_id, token, conn.token_set_enc)
349	    reset = current_conn.set(ctx)
350	    try:
351	        # Proactively refresh if within ~2 min of expiry. refresh_oauth2_token() calls the
352	        # saver, which rotates ALL sibling rows (see section 3).
353	        if token.get("expires_at", 0) - time.time() < 120:
354	            api_client.set_oauth2_token(token)
355	            api_client.refresh_oauth2_token()           # raises if the refresh token is dead
356	        yield api_client, tenant_id
357	    finally:
358	        current_conn.reset(reset)
359	
360	# Example write (explicit tenant_id, summarize_errors, unitdp, idempotency_key):
361	with xero_for(user_id, tenant_id) as (client, tid):
362	    accounting = AccountingApi(client)
363	    invoices = Invoices(invoices=[Invoice(...)])   # type, contact, line_items, ...
364	    accounting.create_invoices(
365	        tid, invoices,
366	        summarize_errors=False,                     # get per-item validation errors
367	        unitdp=4,
368	        idempotency_key=order.idempotency_key or str(uuid.uuid4()),
369	    )
370	```
371	
372	On a failed refresh (dead/expired refresh token), **mark the connection broken and prompt
373	re-connect** — do not loop on failed refreshes.
374	
375	## Date + money + tax normalisation
376	
377	- **Dates.** `xero-python` parses Xero's Microsoft-JSON date form
378	  (`/Date(1518685950940+0000)/`) into `datetime` for you, and accepts `datetime`/`date`
379	  on the way in. If you ever drop to raw REST (e.g. a paged report endpoint),
380	  `datetime.fromisoformat` **cannot** parse the MS-JSON string — normalise it:
381	
382	  ```python
383	  import re
384	  from datetime import datetime, timezone
385	
386	  def normalize_xero_date(v):
387	      if v is None or isinstance(v, datetime):
388	          return v
389	      if isinstance(v, str):
390	          m = re.search(r"/Date\((-?\d+)([+-]\d{4})?\)/", v)
391	          if m:
392	              return datetime.fromtimestamp(int(m.group(1)) / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
393	          try:
394	              return datetime.fromisoformat(v.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
395	          except ValueError:
396	              return None
397	      return None
398	  ```
399	
400	- **Money.** The SDK uses `float` for amounts. If you need exact arithmetic, convert to
401	  `Decimal` deliberately (`Decimal(str(amount))`, never `Decimal(float)`). Control rounding
402	  on writes with `unitdp` (unit decimal places, up to 4). Amounts like `amount_due` come
403	  back as numbers.
404	
405	- **Tax is the org's, not yours.** Each connected organisation applies **its own** tax
406	  config, so `amount_due` / `total` may exceed the sum of your line amounts — e.g. a 15%
407	  GST org turns a 2495 line into 2869.25. The `unit_amount` you posted is still correct;
408	  do **not** "fix" the discrepancy. Set `line_amount_types` (`Exclusive`, `Inclusive`,
409	  `NoTax`) deliberately if you need control. **In multi-tenant this varies per customer** —
410	  never hard-code a tax assumption; whatever each org returns is correct for that org.
411	
412	## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)
413	
414	Defend at two layers.
415	
416	**Layer 1 — app level (cheapest):** don't call Xero twice for the same logical action.
417	Upsert contacts by email (see gotchas) and dedup the order on a stable identifier (a DB
418	unique constraint) so a double-submitted form never reaches Xero twice.
419	
420	**Layer 2 — Xero idempotency (the safety net):** pass `idempotency_key=...` on the
421	mutating call. The accounting create methods accept it as a keyword argument; the order is
422	`create_invoices(tenant_id, invoices, summarize_errors, unitdp, idempotency_key)` and
423	similar for other resources. Xero caches the first response and returns it for any repeat
424	with the same key, so a network-timeout retry can't create a second invoice.
425	
426	Rules that bite (from Xero's idempotency guide):
427	
428	- **Only POST/PUT/PATCH** honour the key; ignored on GET.
429	- **Max 128 characters.** A single `uuid.uuid4()` (36 chars) is plenty unique — use that as
430	  the default.
431	- **Keys expire after ~6 minutes.** They protect transient retries, not long-term dedup.
432	  Long-term dedup is Layer 1's job.
433	- **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A request
434	  differs if URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key with the record and reuse the
435	  *identical* request when retrying.
436	- **Errors are cached too.** A keyed request that errored internally returns the *same
437	  cached error* on retry with the same key, even after the underlying problem is fixed. To
438	  recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if not, create again
439	  with a **new** key.
440	
441	When a user *re-submits* a previously **failed** order later, mint a **new** key — but
442	first GET to confirm no invoice was actually created, so you don't duplicate a silent
443	success.
444	
445	## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)
446	
447	- **Getter/saver are global — keep context per request.** Restated because it is the #1
448	  Python multi-tenant bug: never resolve "the current token" from a global/module/session
449	  variable. Always go through the `ContextVar` set at the start of the request.
450	- **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and the access token dies in
451	  ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.
452	- **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on every
453	  refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time (the saver does this) or the
454	  next refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.
455	- **Always pass `tenant_id` explicitly** to every `AccountingApi` call. There is no implicit
456	  "current org"; the wrong id silently writes to the wrong company's books.
457	- **Re-fetch connections periodically.** `IdentityApi.get_connections()` can change as users
458	  add/remove orgs; re-run it, not just at first callback.
459	- **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new contact
460	  name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or `contact_id`)
461	  via `get_contacts` and reuse the id.
462	- **Invoice status flow:** `DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`. Only `AUTHORISED` invoices
463	  are real receivables and expose an online payment URL (`get_online_invoice`). Use `ACCREC`
464	  (sales) vs `ACCPAY` (bills) correctly.
465	- **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide per-minute cap
466	  and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header and back off —
467	  multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Queue/space calls per tenant.
468	- **`summarize_errors=False`** on batch creates to get per-item validation errors instead of
469	  one opaque failure.
470	
471	## Security checklist (do not skip)
472	
473	- **Encrypt** the stored token set at rest; the key (`TOKEN_ENC_KEY`) lives in your secret
474	  manager / env and is never committed.
475	- **Verify `state`** on every callback and bind it to the session (CSRF); persist and check
476	  the PKCE `code_verifier` too.
477	- Keep PKCE on (`code_challenge_method="S256"`) — don't disable it.
478	- **Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage.
479	- Set `OAUTHLIB_INSECURE_TRANSPORT` **only** in local dev, never in production.
480	- On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — don't retry
481	  forever.
482	- Keep all Xero access in **one module**; resolve tenant context per request.
483	- Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`.
484	
485	## Don't want to hand-roll it?
486	
487	Managed token-vault platforms (**Nango**, **Composio**, etc.) implement per-user OAuth +
488	refresh + encryption for Xero and many providers. For a multi-provider SaaS this is often
489	the pragmatic choice — you still call the Xero API the same way, but the vault and rotation
490	are managed for you.
491	
492	## Verify
493	
494	1. Two app users each connect a **different** Xero org; confirm two distinct token rows and
495	   that each user's writes land in their own org (check `tenant_id`), with no context bleed
496	   between concurrent requests.
497	2. Force a near-expiry token and confirm transparent refresh **and** that the rotated
498	   refresh token is persisted to all sibling rows.
499	3. Revoke the app in one user's Xero and confirm your app surfaces a "reconnect" state
500	   instead of looping on failed refreshes.
501	4. Post the same invoice twice with the same `idempotency_key` and confirm only one invoice
502	   is created.
503
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/lib/xero-client.ts`

```text
1	import {XeroClient} from 'xero-node'
2	import {getCachedTokenSet, cacheTokenSet, clearCachedToken, isTokenExpired} from './auth.js'
3	import {EncryptionKeyError} from './crypto.js'
4	import {refreshAccessToken} from './oauth.js'
5	import {getClientHeaders} from './get-client-headers.js'
6	
7	export {clearCachedToken}
8	
9	export async function createXeroClient(
10	  profileName: string,
11	  clientId: string,
12	): Promise<{xero: XeroClient; tenantId: string}> {
13	  const cached = await getCachedTokenSet(profileName)
14	  if (!cached) {
15	    throw new Error(`Not logged in. Run "xero login" to authenticate.`)
16	  }
17	
18	  let accessToken = cached.accessToken
19	  const tenantId = cached.tenantId
20	
21	  // If token is expired, try to refresh
22	  if (isTokenExpired(cached)) {
23	    try {
24	      const newTokenSet = await refreshAccessToken(clientId, cached.refreshToken)
25	      await cacheTokenSet(profileName, newTokenSet, cached.tenantId, cached.tenantName)
26	      accessToken = newTokenSet.access_token
27	    } catch {
28	      clearCachedToken(profileName)
29	      throw new Error(`Session expired. Run "xero login" to re-authenticate.`)
30	    }
31	  }
32	
33	  const xero = new XeroClient({clientId, clientSecret: ''})
34	  xero.setTokenSet({access_token: accessToken})
35	
36	  const {headers} = getClientHeaders()
37	  ;(xero.accountingApi as any).defaultHeaders = {
38	    ...(xero.accountingApi as any).defaultHeaders,
39	    ...headers,
40	  }
41	
42	  return {xero, tenantId}
43	}
44	
45	export async function withRetry<T>(
46	  profileName: string,
47	  clientId: string,
48	  operation: (xero: XeroClient, tenantId: string) => Promise<T>,
49	  maxRetries = 2,
50	): Promise<T> {
51	  let lastError: Error | undefined
52	
53	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
54	    try {
55	      const {xero, tenantId} = await createXeroClient(profileName, clientId)
56	      return await operation(xero, tenantId)
57	    } catch (error) {
58	      if (error instanceof EncryptionKeyError) throw error
59	      lastError = error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error))
60	
61	      const {statusCode, parsed} = parseXeroError(lastError)
62	
63	      // Handle 401: try refreshing token
64	      if (statusCode === 401 && attempt < maxRetries) {
65	        const cached = await getCachedTokenSet(profileName)
66	        if (cached?.refreshToken) {
67	          try {
68	            const newTokenSet = await refreshAccessToken(clientId, cached.refreshToken)
69	            await cacheTokenSet(profileName, newTokenSet, cached.tenantId, cached.tenantName)
70	            continue
71	          } catch {
72	            clearCachedToken(profileName)
73	            throw new Error(`Session expired. Run "xero login" to re-authenticate.`)
74	          }
75	        }
76	        clearCachedToken(profileName)
77	        continue
78	      }
79	
80	      // Handle rate limit
81	      if (statusCode === 429) {
82	        const retryAfter = extractRetryAfter(parsed, lastError.message)
83	        throw new Error(`Rate limited. Retry after ${retryAfter} seconds.`)
84	      }
85	
86	      // Handle 404
87	      if (statusCode === 404) {
88	        throw new Error('Resource not found.')
89	      }
90	
91	      throw sanitizeApiError(lastError, {statusCode, parsed})
92	    }
93	  }
94	
95	  throw lastError ? sanitizeApiError(lastError) : new Error('Operation failed after retries')
96	}
97	
98	interface ParsedXeroError {
99	  statusCode?: number
100	  parsed?: Record<string, unknown>
101	}
102	
103	export function parseXeroError(error: Error): ParsedXeroError {
104	  const message = error.message
105	  if (typeof message !== 'string') return {}
106	  const trimmed = message.trim()
107	  if (!trimmed.startsWith('{')) return {}
108	
109	  let parsed: unknown
110	  try {
111	    parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed)
112	  } catch {
113	    return {}
114	  }
115	  if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object') return {}
116	
117	  const obj = parsed as Record<string, unknown>
118	  const response = obj.response as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
119	  const rawStatus = response?.statusCode ?? response?.status
120	  const statusCode = typeof rawStatus === 'number' ? rawStatus : undefined
121	  return {statusCode, parsed: obj}
122	}
123	
124	export function sanitizeApiError(error: Error, hint?: ParsedXeroError): Error {
125	  const {statusCode, parsed} = hint ?? parseXeroError(error)
126	
127	  // Non-Xero error (network, our own thrown messages, etc.) — pass through.
128	  if (!parsed) return new Error(error.message)
129	
130	  try {
131	    const response = parsed.response as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
132	    const body = (parsed.body ?? response?.body) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
133	    const statusSuffix = statusCode ? ` (${statusCode})` : ''
134	
135	    if (body) {
136	      const elements = body.Elements as Array<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined
137	      if (elements?.length) {
138	        const messages = elements
139	          .flatMap(el => (el.ValidationErrors as Array<{Message?: string}> | undefined) ?? [])
140	          .map(ve => ve.Message)
141	          .filter((m): m is string => Boolean(m))
142	        if (messages.length) {
143	          return new Error(`Xero API error${statusSuffix}: ${messages.join('; ')}`)
144	        }
145	      }
146	
147	      const topMessage = body.Message ?? body.message
148	      if (typeof topMessage === 'string' && topMessage) {
149	        return new Error(`Xero API error${statusSuffix}: ${topMessage}`)
150	      }
151	    }
152	
153	    return new Error(`Xero API error${statusSuffix}`)
154	  } catch {
155	    return new Error(statusCode ? `Xero API error (${statusCode})` : 'Xero API error')
156	  }
157	}
158	
159	function extractRetryAfter(parsed: Record<string, unknown> | undefined, fallbackMessage: string): number {
160	  const headers = (parsed?.response as Record<string, unknown> | undefined)?.headers as
161	    | Record<string, unknown>
162	    | undefined
163	  const headerValue = headers?.['retry-after']
164	  if (typeof headerValue === 'string' || typeof headerValue === 'number') {
165	    const n = Number(headerValue)
166	    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return n
167	  }
168	  const match = /retry-after[":\s]+(\d+)/i.exec(fallbackMessage)
169	  return match ? Number(match[1]) : 60
170	}
171
```
</details></tool-use>
