```md
# Xero MCP Server — Fact Sheet

Source: `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server` (XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server, `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server` v0.0.16 — package.json:2-3). All paths below relative to that repo root.

## 1. Registered tools (51 total)

Registration flow: `src/index.ts:10-11` (`ToolFactory(server)` + `StdioServerTransport`); `src/tools/tool-factory.ts:11-27` registers Delete, Get, Create, List, Update tool arrays via `server.tool(name, description, schema, handler)`. Every tool is built with `CreateXeroTool(name, ...)` (`src/helpers/create-xero-tool.ts:5-17`). Names below are the exact registered strings; citation = where the name string appears; handler = the `src/handlers/*.handler.ts` file the tool delegates to.

### Accounting — create (10)
| Tool | Purpose | Name at | Handler |
|---|---|---|---|
| `create-contact` | Create a contact | src/tools/create/create-contact.tool.ts:8 | create-xero-contact.handler.ts |
| `create-invoice` | Create ACCREC/ACCPAY invoice (DRAFT) | src/tools/create/create-invoice.tool.ts:28 | create-xero-invoice.handler.ts |
| `create-payment` | Record payment against an invoice | src/tools/create/create-payment.tool.ts:7 | create-xero-payment.handler.ts |
| `create-credit-note` | Create a credit note | src/tools/create/create-credit-note.tool.ts:15 | create-xero-credit-note.handler.ts |
| `create-bank-transaction` | Create a bank transaction | src/tools/create/create-bank-transaction.tool.ts:15 | create-xero-bank-transaction.handler.ts |
| `create-manual-journal` | Create a manual journal | src/tools/create/create-manual-journal.tool.ts:9 | create-xero-manual-journal.handler.ts |
| `create-item` | Create an inventory/service item | src/tools/create/create-item.tool.ts:18 | create-xero-item.handler.ts |
| `create-quote` | Create a quote | src/tools/create/create-quote.tool.ts:15 | create-xero-quote.handler.ts |
| `create-tracking-category` | Create a tracking category | src/tools/create/create-tracking-category.tool.ts:6 | create-xero-tracking-category.handler.ts |
| `create-tracking-options` | Add options to a tracking category | src/tools/create/create-tracking-options.tool.ts:7 | create-xero-tracking-option.handler.ts |

### Accounting — list/read (18)
| Tool | Purpose | Name at | Handler |
|---|---|---|---|

| `list-payroll-employees` | Payroll employees | src/tools/list/list-payroll-employees.tool.ts:6 | list-xero-payroll-employees.handler.ts |
| `list-payroll-employee-leave` | Employee leave records | src/tools/list/list-payroll-employee-leave.tool.ts:7 | list-xero-payroll-employee-leave.handler.ts |
| `list-payroll-employee-leave-balances` | Employee leave balances | src/tools/list/list-payroll-employee-leave-balances.tool.ts:7 | list-xero-payroll-employee-leave-balances.handler.ts |
| `list-payroll-employee-leave-types` | Employee leave types | src/tools/list/list-payroll-employee-leave-types.tool.ts:7 | list-xero-payroll-employee-leave-types.handler.ts |
| `list-payroll-leave-periods` | Leave periods | src/tools/list/list-payroll-leave-periods.tool.ts:7 | list-xero-payroll-leave-periods.handler.ts |
| `list-payroll-leave-types` | Org leave types | src/tools/list/list-payroll-leave-types.tool.ts:6 | list-xero-payroll-leave-types.handler.ts |
| `list-timesheets` | Payroll timesheets | src/tools/list/list-payroll-timesheets.tool.ts:9 | list-xero-timesheets.handler.ts |
| `get-timesheet` | One timesheet by ID | src/tools/get/get-payroll-timesheet.tool.ts:9 | get-xero-payroll-timesheet.handler.ts |
| `create-timesheet` | Create timesheet | src/tools/create/create-payroll-timesheet.tool.ts:10 | create-xero-payroll-timesheet.handler.ts |
| `delete-timesheet` | Delete timesheet | src/tools/delete/delete-payroll-timesheet.tool.ts:9 | delete-xero-payroll-timesheet.handler.ts |
| `approve-timesheet` | Approve timesheet | src/tools/update/approve-payroll-timesheet.tool.ts:9 | approve-xero-payroll-timesheet.handler.ts |
| `revert-timesheet` | Revert timesheet to draft | src/tools/update/revert-payroll-timesheet.tool.ts:9 | revert-xero-payroll-timesheet.handler.ts |
| `add-timesheet-line` | Add line to timesheet | src/tools/update/update-payroll-timesheet-add-line.tool.ts:12 | update-xero-payroll-timesheet-add-line.handler.ts |
| `update-timesheet-line` | Update timesheet line | src/tools/update/update-payroll-timesheet-update-line.tool.ts:12 | update-xero-payroll-timesheet-update-line.handler.ts |

## 2. Confirmed ABSENT

Verified by `grep -ri "purchase|attachment|history" src/`:
- **No purchase order tool exists.** No tool name contains "purchase". The only "purchase" hits are item `purchaseDetails`/`purchaseDescription` fields (src/tools/create/create-item.tool.ts, src/tools/update/update-item.tool.ts, src/tools/list/list-items.tool.ts) and the word "purchase invoices" in the create-invoice description (src/tools/create/create-invoice.tool.ts). No `getPurchaseOrders`/`createPurchaseOrder` SDK call anywhere.
- **No attachment tool exists.** The only "attachment" hits are read-only `hasAttachments` display flags (src/tools/list/list-manual-journals.tool.ts, list-bank-transactions.tool.ts, list-contacts.tool.ts). No upload/download/list-attachments capability.
- **No history/notes tool exists.** The only "history" hit is the word "leave history" in a description string (src/tools/list/list-payroll-employee-leave.tool.ts:7). No `getInvoiceHistory`/`createHistoryRecord` calls.
- Also absent: no get-invoice-by-id tool, no delete/void tools for invoices, no email-invoice tool, no batch payments.

## 3. Running against a Custom Connection (client_credentials)

- Env vars read (src/clients/xero-client.ts:14-16): `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN`. Optional `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated, xero-client.ts:129-131). Loaded via `dotenv.config()` (xero-client.ts:12). Boot assert at xero-client.ts:19-21: throws unless `bearer_token || (client_id && client_secret)`.
- Mode decision (xero-client.ts:223-231): `bearer_token ? BearerTokenXeroClient : CustomConnectionsXeroClient` — i.e. `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` takes precedence; otherwise client_credentials with hardcoded `grant_type = "client_credentials"` (xero-client.ts:17).
- Command: `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` with env vars (README.md:71-76), or local build `node dist/index.js` (README.md:226-230). Transport is stdio only (src/index.ts:14-15).
- Tenant id: NOT an env var. Client-credentials mode fetches the token from `https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` then calls `https://api.xero.com/connections` and takes `connections[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:178-190). Bearer mode calls `updateTenants()` and takes `tenants[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:35-41, 219). Multi-org: always the first tenant, no selection mechanism.
- Default scopes if `XERO_SCOPES` unset: tries legacy V1 scope set first (`accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.reports.read payroll.*`, xero-client.ts:81-89), and on `invalid_scope` (HTTP 400) falls back to granular V2 set (`accounting.invoices accounting.payments ...`, xero-client.ts:92-106, retry logic 137-155).

## 4. `create-payment`

- Schema (src/tools/create/create-payment.tool.ts:18-34): required `invoiceId: string`, `accountId: string`, `amount: number` (`.positive()`); optional `date: string` (YYYY-MM-DD), `reference: string`.
- Handler (src/handlers/create-xero-payment.handler.ts:15-45): builds `Payment { invoice: {invoiceID}, account: {accountID}, amount, date: date || today, reference }` and calls `xeroClient.accountingApi.createPayment(tenantId, payment, undefined /* idempotencyKey */, getClientHeaders())` (line 36-41). **idempotencyKey is passed as `undefined`** — no idempotency.
- Constraints: only in the description text (tool.ts:8-13): invoice must be AUTHORISED and not fully paid, amount must not exceed amount due — Xero enforces these, not this code. Success text is mislabeled "Invoice created successfully" (tool.ts, response block).

## 5. `update-invoice` — cannot change status

- Schema (src/tools/update/update-invoice.tool.ts:36-50): required `invoiceId`; optional `lineItems[]` (description, quantity, unitAmount, accountCode, taxType, itemCode?, tracking?), `reference`, `dueDate`, `date`, `contactId`. **There is no `status` parameter.**
- Handler hard-blocks non-draft invoices (src/handlers/update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84):
  \```ts
  // Only allow updates to DRAFT invoices
  if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) {
    return { result: null, isError: true,
      error: `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ${invoiceStatus}`, };
  }
  \```
  The `Invoice` object it sends (handler.ts:39-45) contains only lineItems/reference/dueDate/date/contact — no status field. **Conclusion: the MCP server cannot move an invoice to AUTHORISED (or any status).** Approving a draft requires the raw Accounting API (POST Invoices with `"Status": "AUTHORISED"`).
- Warning in description: all line items must be re-supplied; omitted line items are removed (tool.ts:31-32).

## 6. `list-invoices`

- Schema (src/tools/list/list-invoices.tool.ts:16-22): `page: number` (required), `contactIds: string[]` optional, `invoiceNumbers: string[]` optional (providing invoiceNumbers also returns line items, tool.ts:59, 96-98).
- Handler SDK call (src/handlers/list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:15-31): `getInvoices(tenantId, ...)` with `where: undefined`, `order: "UpdatedDateUTC DESC"`, `invoiceNumbers`, `contactIDs`, **`statuses: undefined`** (line 22 — no status filter exposed), `page`, `includeArchived: false`, `summaryOnly: false`, **`pageSize: 10` hardcoded** (line 28), `searchTerm: undefined`. No date, no status, no where-clause filtering available through the tool.

## 7. `list-aged-payables-by-contact`

- Schema (src/tools/list/list-aged-payables-by-contact.tool.ts:10-18): required `contactId: string`; optional `reportDate` (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to end of current month), `invoicesFromDate`, `invoicesToDate`.
- Handler (src/handlers/list-aged-payables-by-contact.handler.ts:15-22): `accountingApi.getReportAgedPayablesByContact(tenantId, contactId, reportDate, invoicesFromDate, invoicesToDate, getClientHeaders())`. Returns report rows as raw JSON text.

## 8. Rate limiting: NONE

- `grep -rni "429|retry|rate" src/` — no retry, no backoff, no throttling, no rate-limit-remaining header reads anywhere in src/.
- The only 429 handling is a cosmetic error message: src/helpers/format-error.ts:35-36 maps HTTP 429 to `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."`. Callers must implement their own backoff/batching.

## 9. SDK and token lifecycle

- SDK: `xero-node` ^13.3.0 plus `axios` for the token/connections calls, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.23.4, `zod` 3.25 (package.json:27-31). `XeroClient` from xero-node is subclassed as `MCPXeroClient` (xero-client.ts:23).
- Token is NOT cached: every handler starts with `await xeroClient.authenticate()` (e.g. create-xero-payment.handler.ts:23, list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:13). In client-credentials mode `authenticate()` (xero-client.ts:195-203) requests a brand-new token from `identity.xero.com/connect/token` plus a `GET /connections` on every single tool call — 2 extra HTTP round-trips per call, no expiry check, no reuse. Bearer mode just re-sets the static token and re-fetches tenants (xero-client.ts:214-220).
- All SDK calls send header `user-agent: xero-mcp-server-<version>` via `getClientHeaders()` (src/helpers/get-client-headers.ts:3-9).
- Writes are non-idempotent: `idempotencyKey` is explicitly `undefined` in createPayment (create-xero-payment.handler.ts:39) and updateInvoice (update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:54).

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Strip HTML tags to plain text for all three doc pages

```bash
cd /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad && python3 - <<'EOF'
import html.parser, re

class T(html.parser.HTMLParser):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.parts=[]; self.skip=0
    def handle_starttag(self,tag,attrs):
        if tag in ('script','style'): self.skip+=1
        if tag in ('p','div','li','h1','h2','h3','h4','tr','br','pre','td','th'): self.parts.append('\n')
    def handle_endtag(self,tag):
        if tag in ('script','style'): self.skip=max(0,self.skip-1)
    def handle_data(self,d):
        if not self.skip: self.parts.append(d)

for f in ['custom-connections','limits','requests-responses']:
    p=T(); p.feed(open(f+'.html',encoding='utf-8').read())
    txt=''.join(p.parts)
    txt=re.sub(r'\n\s*\n+','\n',txt)
    open(f+'.txt','w',encoding='utf-8').write(txt)
    print(f, len(txt))
EOF
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Xero prompt-library skill-writing style: fact sheet

Sources (all in `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/`):
- `lovable/xero-integration-single-account/SKILL.md` (707 lines)
- `lovable/xero-integration-multi-account/SKILL.md` (868 lines)
- `javascript/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant`, 452 lines)
- `python/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`, 502 lines)
- `replit/xero-integration-single-account-skill` (plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter, 293 lines)
- `README.md` (repo index, no skill discipline content)

---

## 1. Document structure conventions

### Frontmatter
Exactly two fields, always: `name` and `description`. No version, no tools, no metadata.

- `name` is the kebab-case skill id (`xero-integration-single-account`, `xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`).
- `description` is a single long paragraph that does four jobs, in roughly this order:
  1. **What it builds**, with the discriminating word in CAPS: "Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation".
  2. **When to use it**: "Use whenever building a multi-tenant SaaS on Xero".
  3. **What it covers**, as a comma list of the hard parts: "Covers the full OAuth2 + PKCE lifecycle with the official `xero-node` SDK, per-user encrypted token storage, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits)."
  4. **A read-first command and a routing rule to the sibling skill**: "Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see 'Do you actually need this?' at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach."

  The Replit variant adds an anti-pattern warning right in the description: "Read this before writing any Xero code; do NOT rely on generic xero-node knowledge."

### Section order (consistent across all four full skills)
1. `# H1` restating the variant in plain words: "Multi-tenant Xero (each user connects their own Xero)".
2. **Variant check / decision gate first.** A blockquote or `## Do you actually need this?` section forcing the reader to confirm they picked the right skill, with an explicit pointer to the sibling: "Stop and use **`xero-integration-single-account`** instead." Each pair of skills cross-references the other by exact name. The deciding question is stated as one sentence: "The deciding question is *how many Xero logins exist*".
3. **Runtime context** ("Where this code runs in Lovable") — the platform facts that shape all code below, stated as numbered "runtime facts".
4. `## Decision check before building` — a 3-item ALL-must-be-true checklist.
5. `## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)` — see §2 below.
6. Numbered build sections in lifecycle order: `## 1. Register your own Xero app` → `## Scopes` → `## 2. Per-user token store` → `## 3.` (client/redirect resolver) → `## 4.` (start consent) → `## 5.` (callback) → `## 6.` (use per request / status UX).
7. Cross-cutting sections: `## Date + money + tax normalisation`, `## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)`, `## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)`, platform-gotcha subsection, `## Security checklist (do not skip)`, `## Don't want to hand-roll it?` (managed alternatives: Nango, Composio).
8. `## Verify` — always last. A numbered end-to-end acceptance list (they even use step `0.` and `0.5.` to force pre-click checks before step 1). Every item is observable behavior, e.g. "Post the same invoice twice with the same idempotency key and confirm only one invoice is created."

### Description/heading phrasing
- Headings carry the verdict, not just the topic: "The redirect-URI resolver — read carefully", "Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)", "Security checklist (do not skip)", "CRITICAL: path mapping", "Failure handling (the rule that bites)".
- Section intros state *why the section exists* in one or two sentences before any code.
- Every code block opens with a file-path comment (`// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts`) so the reader knows where it lives.
- Sync-maintenance notes between sibling docs: "(This table is identical to the single-account skill — keep the two in sync if Xero's assignments change.)"
- External source-of-truth links with a staleness warning: "Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes"

---

## 2. Pre-flight check discipline (verbatim quotes)

The Lovable skills open the build with a dedicated section. Header, verbatim:

> ## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
>
> Three checks. Each one prevents a class of bug that produces a misleading
> upstream error from Xero. Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a
> human.

**Check 1 — assert every secret non-empty, with the causal chain stated** (verbatim):

> ### 1. Assert every secret is set and non-empty
>
> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Fail fast
> with a clear message inside `xeroStart` **before** building the URL:

\```ts
function requireEnv(name: string) {
  const v = process.env[name];
  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
    throw new Error(`${name} is empty — set it in Project Secrets and retry.`);
  }
  return v;
}
\```

> Call `requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID")`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_SCOPES`, and
> `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` at the top of every handler that uses them.

**Check 2 — log the constructed request once, never the secret** (verbatim):

> You want a server log you can read when something is wrong, without ever
> printing the secret value itself:

\```ts
console.log("[xero] authorize", {
  client_id_len: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID?.length,
  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
  scope: process.env.XERO_SCOPES,
});
\```

> If the next failure is `unauthorized_client` or `invalid redirect_uri`, this
> line tells you *exactly* which value Xero rejected.

Note the trick: log `client_id_len` (the length), never the value.

**Check 3 — smoke-test before any human clicks** (verbatim):

> ### 3. Smoke-test `xeroStart` BEFORE clicking Connect
>
> Call the server function and inspect the returned URL: `client_id` must be a
> non-empty hex string, and `redirect_uri` must **exactly** match one of the URIs
> registered in your Xero app (including scheme, host, and path). If either is
> wrong, fix it here. Do not proceed to the popup — Xero's errors past this point
> are far less useful than the URL you can read right now.

**Check 4 — validate key material shape, with the full failure story** (verbatim):

> AES-256-GCM requires a **32-byte** key. A 32-character random string is NOT 32
> bytes once base64-decoded (it's ~24), and `createCipheriv` throws
> `Invalid key length` from deep inside `encrypt()` — which surfaces as an
> opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the
> single-use `code`. The user sees "something went wrong" with no way to retry
> without re-consenting, and no breadcrumb in the logs points at the key.

Pattern to copy: each pre-flight check names (a) the invariant, (b) the misleading downstream symptom if skipped, (c) the exact code to assert it, (d) the moment to run it ("before you wire the Connect button to a human"). Boot-time assertion + named error, exactly the CLAUDE.md rule "Secrets in `.env` only, asserted non-empty at boot with named errors."

They also make the *failure path itself* testable — Verify step 0.5, verbatim:

> 0.5. **Force an encryption failure once.** Temporarily set `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` to
>    a 16-char string and click Connect. Confirm `/dashboard` shows
>    `?error=callback&detail=…` (with a readable message) rather than a blank
>    500 — this proves both the callback try/catch (§4) and the dashboard
>    banner (§6) actually wire failures back to the user. Restore the real
>    key afterwards.

---

## 3. Error → real-cause mappings (verbatim — the gold)

These docs mostly encode error→cause as prose sentences and blockquote "traps", not literal tables. Every mapping found, copied verbatim with source:

**`unauthorized_client` ← empty/missing client_id** (lovable single + multi, Pre-flight §1):

> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause.

**`invalid redirect_uri` / `unauthorized_client` ← three redirect-origin traps** (lovable, §3 header block, verbatim):

> ### ⚠️ Three traps, all of which produce `invalid redirect_uri` or `unauthorized_client`
>
> **Trap A — `getRequest()` / `request.url` on the server returns
> `http://localhost:8080`.** Under the Worker runtime the inbound request you
> see inside a server function is on the internal dev host, not the public URL.
> Never derive `redirect_uri` from it.
>
> **Trap B — In the Lovable editor, `window.location.origin` is
> `https://<project-id>.lovableproject.com`** (the iframe host), which is NOT
> a registered URI. Sending it raw produces `invalid redirect_uri` every time.
>
> **Trap C — A single `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret can't cover both preview and
> published.** If you set it to the published host the preview breaks (and
> vice-versa). Use the resolver below instead.

**`unauthorized_client` / invalid-redirect ← port or trailing-slash mismatch** (javascript, Local-dev gotchas):

> **Redirect URI must match exactly.** The most common local failure is a port or
> trailing-slash mismatch between `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and what's registered in the
> portal. If consent returns `unauthorized_client` or an invalid-redirect error,
> this is almost always why. Run the dev server on the same port you registered.

**403 ← missing write scope** (lovable multi, Scopes; wrong-secret-shaped failures are scope failures here):

> Shipping a read-only token and then calling `POST /Invoices` fails
> at runtime with a 403 / insufficient-scope error — and because scopes **cannot be
> widened on an existing token**, the only fix is to send every connected customer
> back through the whole consent flow. So decide up front: read, write, or both.

and the compressed single-account form:

> Mismatched scope → 403 at runtime and the only fix is
> to send the user through consent again.

and the silent-default variant (lovable multi):

> **Make the `XERO_SCOPES` fallback in your shared Xero config match your intent** — an
> all-`.read` default will silently hand every customer a read-only token if the
> secret is missing, and your first write will 403.

**Opaque 500 on callback ← wrong-length `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (lovable, Pre-flight §4): see §2 above ("`createCipheriv` throws `Invalid key length` … surfaces as an opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the single-use `code`").

**`TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` ← client recreated without state** (javascript, §5 callback, verbatim code comment):

> // MUST recreate the client with the SAME state you used to build the consent URL.
> // `apiCallback` runs openid-client's state check: because the consent URL carries a
> // `state` param, the callback client must also know it, or you get the cryptic
> // `TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` and every connect fails.

**401 `No connection found for replid ...` ← Repl not bound to the connector** (replit, setup checklist):

> **Until the Repl is bound,
> every live call fails with `401` and a body like
> `No connection found for replid ... with connector: xero`.** If you see that
> 401, the code is probably fine — the binding is missing.

**404 ← friendly alias paths** (replit, CRITICAL: path mapping):

> The connector blueprint advertises friendly aliases like `/accounting/contacts` and
> `/accounting/invoices` — **these are wrong and return 404.** Use Xero's real
> REST paths with PascalCase resource names.

> Verified empirically: `/connections` → 200, `/accounting/organisations` → 404,
> `/api.xro/2.0/Organisations` → 200.

**Blank white page ← Xero consent framed in an iframe** (lovable + javascript):

> Xero's login refuses to be framed (`frame-ancestors`), so a same-frame
> redirect from the Lovable preview iframe shows a **blank white page** that
> looks like the button is broken.

**Access token dies in ~30 min, no recovery ← `offline_access` omitted** (all four):

> **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and you get an access token
> that dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.

**400 ← same idempotency key, different request** (all four, Idempotency "Rules that bite"):

> **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A
> request differs if the URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key alongside the
> record and reuse the *identical* request when retrying.

**Cached error ← retrying an errored keyed request** (all four):

> **Errors are cached too.** If the keyed request errored internally, retrying with the
> *same* key returns the *same cached error* even after the underlying problem is
> fixed. To recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if
> not, create again with a **new** key.

**429 ← rate limits, with the recovery** (all four):

> **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide
> per-minute cap and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header
> and back off — multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Spread/queue
> calls per tenant.

**Silent wrong-books write ← missing/wrong tenant header** (all four):

> **Always send `Xero-Tenant-Id`** on every API call. There is no implicit "current
> org." With multiple connected orgs, the wrong id silently writes to the wrong
> company's books.

**Next refresh fails ← rotated refresh token not persisted** (all four):

> **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on
> every refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time or the next
> refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.

**Sibling orgs flip to needs_reconnect ← only one row updated after rotation** (javascript §6, code comment, verbatim):

> // CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: when one consent authorized several orgs, they all
> // share ONE token set. Refreshing rotates the refresh token and INVALIDATES the
> // old one, so updating only THIS row leaves every sibling org holding a dead token
> // (they'll wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on their next call). Update ALL rows
> // that shared the previous token set — e.g. match on the old ciphertext:

**Duplicate contacts ← posting invoices with a contact name** (all four):

> **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new
> contact name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or
> `ContactID`) via `getContacts` and reuse the id; otherwise you litter the org with
> duplicates.

The one true error-shaped *table* in the corpus is the Replit **path mapping table** (Purpose | Path) plus its "verified empirically" line — the pattern being: verified request → expected status, wrong path → 404.

---

## 4. Scope minimalism rules

Canonical statement, repeated near-verbatim in all four skills:

> Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run the OAuth consent
> flow to add more later. You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user
> must re-consent. To get a refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.

Supporting rules:

- "**Always prefer the granular scopes** — use `accounting.invoices`, NOT `accounting.transactions`." (Broad scopes deprecated: granular assigned to all Web/PKCE apps since March 2026, custom connections since 29 April 2026, broad dead September 2027.)
- "**Read vs write — choose deliberately; you can't change it later without re-consent.** `.read` scopes are GET-only."
- Lovable adds an agent-behavior rule: "Do not ask the user what scopes to use - set the appropriate scopes for the integration to function."
- Replit: "**Scopes** — see the dedicated section below. Request the minimum set for what you're doing".

Exact scope strings quoted in the docs:

- Read-only dashboard (lovable single):
  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`
- Write / invoicing app ("A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants", all four):
  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`
- Typical invoice flow (replit):
  `offline_access openid email profile accounting.settings.read accounting.invoices accounting.contacts`

Scope reference tables they maintain (structure worth copying): (1) identity scopes `offline_access` / `openid` / `profile` / `email`; (2) "Deprecated (broad) → New granular scopes" migration table (`accounting.transactions` → `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals`; same for `.read`; `accounting.reports.read` → eight `accounting.reports.*.read` scopes); (3) "Scope | Grants" table mapping each granular scope to the resources it unlocks (`accounting.invoices` → Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items; `accounting.payments` → Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments); (4) "never broad, use as-is" table (`accounting.settings`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.journals.read`, `accounting.budgets.read`). Always footed with the developer.xero.com scopes URL and "re-check periodically — dates and assignments change".

---

## 5. Token handling rules

- **Lifetimes, stated with numbers every time:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days, **rotates on every refresh**, dies if unused for 60 days. Expiry stored as `expires_at` computed from `expires_in` seconds: `new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()` (JS) / `token["expires_at"]` epoch-seconds dict key (Python).
- **Refresh early, not at expiry:** JS refreshes when `expires_at <= now + 60_000` (60-second buffer); Python "Proactively refresh if within ~2 min of expiry" (`expires_at - time.time() < 120`).
- **Persist rotation immediately or break:** "tokenSet = await res.json();   // refresh_token ROTATES — must persist". And update **all sibling rows** sharing the old ciphertext (multi-org consents share one token set) — match siblings on the OLD ciphertext.
- **No long-lived clients / no caching:** "Make a fresh client per request and `setTokenSet(...)` from the DB. Do not keep a long-lived client — tokens expire and clients are per-user." Replit: "Never cache this — create a fresh instance per request (tokens rotate)."
- **Refresh failure is a state, not a retry loop:** "On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — do not silently retry forever." Status enum in the schema: `'active' | 'needs_reconnect'`. Surface it in UI with a reconnect CTA; a 60-day-idle token dies silently, so add a scheduled keep-alive refresh, not just reactive detection.
- **Storage:** tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM, key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` in the secret store, exactly 32 bytes base64); rotating token lives in the **database (a mutable store), never in a secret**; "never put per-user tokens in env".
- **Never-log / never-expose:** log `client_id_len`, not the value ("without ever printing the secret value itself"); "**Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage"; "Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`; never commit credentials"; status endpoints return "only non-sensitive metadata (never the token)".
- **`state` discipline:** random, persisted server-side with a 10-minute expiry, verified on callback, deleted after use — "single-use, replay-proof".

---

## 6. Tone and format

- **Imperative second person, no hedging.** "Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a human." "Stop and use X instead." "If you reach for `xero-node` or start writing token-refresh code, stop — you are on the wrong path."
- **Warnings are structural, not decorative.** The single warning device is `⚠️` in a heading (`## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST`, `> ### ⚠️ Three traps...`, `> ### ⚠️ Open consent in a NEW TAB, opened SYNCHRONOUSLY in the click handler`) plus a blockquote body. Bold and CAPS carry emphasis inline: MUST, NOT, NEVER, ONE, THEIR OWN, FIRST, "byte-for-byte", "byte-match".
- **Every rule ships its consequence.** Not "include offline_access" but "Omit it and the access token dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent." Failure narratives are causal chains told to the end (wrong key → throw deep inside encrypt → opaque 500 → single-use code consumed → user must re-consent → no breadcrumb in logs).
- **Named recurring rubrics:** "Rules that bite", "(these bite in production)", "Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)", "Gotcha that fools people", "(do not skip)", "(skipping it costs hours)", "CRITICAL multi-tenant trap".
- **Tables vs prose:** tables only for enumerable reference data (scopes, scope grants, deprecation mapping, REST paths). Everything judgment-shaped (gotchas, security, idempotency rules) is bold-lead-in bullets: `**Rule.** Consequence and recovery.` Checklists (Decision check, Security checklist, Verify) are lists, with Verify numbered.
- **Code is placed, minimal, and commented with intent** — comments mark traps ("// sync!", "// refresh_token ROTATES — must persist", "// REQUIRED — wrong id writes to wrong books"), not narration. File-path comment on line 1 of each block.
- **Distrust of upstream errors is a theme:** fail fast locally because "Xero's errors past this point are far less useful than the URL you can read right now."
- **Empirical claims are labeled:** "Verified empirically", "**The paths below are verified — trust them, do not re-probe.**", and for unknowns: "probe it **read-only first** rather than guessing".
- Punctuation habits: heavy em-dash usage, `→` arrows for flows and mappings (`DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`; "Same key + different request → `400`"), backticked literals for every identifier, header, error string, and scope.

---

## 7. Platform-specific guidance — do NOT copy into a NestJS project

Everything below is Lovable/Supabase/Replit plumbing, not Xero discipline. In NestJS: config assertion at boot (ConfigModule + named errors), one Xero module (`api/src/modules/xero/`), Postgres via your ORM, guards instead of RLS.

**Lovable / TanStack Start / Cloudflare Workers:**
- `createServerFn` server functions; server routes under `src/routes/api/public/*` to "bypass Lovable's published-site auth gate". NestJS controllers are just public or guarded routes.
- "Do not use Supabase Edge Functions"; the legacy-port note (Deno Edge Functions, `Deno.env.get`, redeploy after secret change, `verify_jwt = false`, Web Crypto two-part `iv.ciphertext` blob) — all Deno/Supabase-specific.
- "Read `process.env.*` **inside** `.handler()`, not at module scope" (Worker per-request env injection). NestJS reads env once at boot — the opposite discipline.
- The whole `resolveRedirectUri` host-mapping resolver, `PROJECT_ID`, `id-preview--*.lovable.app` / `project--*.lovable.app` hosts, `*.lovableproject.com` iframe host, `getRequest()` returning `http://localhost:8080`. A NestJS app has one configured redirect URI per environment.
- Iframe/popup dance (`window.open("about:blank", "_blank")` synchronously before `await`) — Lovable editor-preview-specific; keep only the generic fact that Xero consent can't be framed.
- "Never import `@/integrations/supabase/client.server` at module scope of a `*.functions.ts` or route file" — client-bundle stripping concern, no NestJS equivalent.
- Lovable Cloud secrets / `generate_secret` with `length: 44` — keep the underlying rule (base64 of 32 random bytes), drop the tool.

**Supabase data layer:**
- `supabaseAdmin`, `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, "Service-role key never goes to the browser".
- RLS incantations: `grant all ... to service_role`, `enable row level security`, "INTENTIONALLY no anon/authenticated policies", `auth.users(id)` FKs, `requireSupabaseAuth` middleware, `has_role(userId, 'admin')`.
- Supabase client returning Postgres `numeric` as strings.
- The unauthenticated-prototype "escape hatch" (warning banner, accepted-risks list) — Lovable demo-flow specific.

**Replit connector:**
- `@replit/connectors-sdk` proxy pattern, `connectors.proxy("xero", path, ...)`, Repl binding (`proposeIntegration`/`addIntegration`), the `No connection found for replid` 401, redirect URI `https://replit.com/connectors/oauth/callback`, `pnpm --filter @workspace/<server>` install rule, Integrations → Manage reset.

**Portable despite appearing in platform sections** (keep these): exact-match redirect URIs registered per environment; state persisted server-side because HTTP steps are separate invocations; callback errors funneled to a user-visible `?error&detail` redirect (never a bare 500, because the OAuth `code` is single-use); `Xero-Tenant-Id` on every call; idempotency keys on writes; `summarizeErrors=false`; `unitdp=4`; the `/Date(ms+offset)/` normaliser; encrypt-at-rest with validated 32-byte key; `needs_reconnect` status + keep-alive refresh; all Xero access isolated in one module.

python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('$SP/att-pd.json'))
def walk(o, path=''):
    if isinstance(o, dict):
        for k,v in o.items(): walk(v, path+'/'+k)
    elif isinstance(o, list):
        for i,v in enumerate(o): walk(v, path+f'[{i}]')
    else:
        s=str(o)
        print(path, '::', s[:120])
walk(d)
" | head -60

- Read all 5 target docs plus replit single-account skill (it is a plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter).
- Structure: frontmatter is name+description only; description = what/when/covers/read-first + routing to sibling skill; fixed section order ending in a numbered Verify list (with steps 0 and 0.5).
- Pre-flight discipline captured verbatim: requireEnv non-empty assertion, client_id_len logging (never the secret), smoke-test authorize URL before clicking, 32-byte TOKEN_ENC_KEY validation.
- Error->cause gold copied verbatim: unauthorized_client <- client_id=undefined; invalid redirect_uri <- 3 origin traps + port/trailing-slash; 403 <- read-only scope (unfixable without re-consent); 500-on-callback <- bad enc key after single-use code burned; 401 "No connection found for replid"; 404 <- alias paths; 400 <- reused idempotency key with different body; cached errors; 429/Retry-After.
- Scope minimalism: additive/minimum/re-consent rule, granular-over-broad migration tables, 3 exact scope strings.
- Tokens: access ~30 min, refresh ~60 days rotating, 60s/120s early-refresh buffers, expires_in*1000 persistence, sibling-row rotation, needs_reconnect state, never-log/never-expose rules.
- Section 7 isolates Lovable/Supabase/Replit-only guidance (createServerFn, Edge Functions/Deno, RLS/service_role, redirect resolver, connector proxy) that must not enter NestJS, plus a keep-list of portable rules.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Fact sheet: xero-agent-toolkit + xero-command-line (auth & API-call patterns)

Source repos (local checkouts under `.tmp/xero-refs/`):
- `xero-agent-toolkit/` — examples repo. **Contains zero auth code and zero tool definitions of its own.** Every example spawns `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` via `npx` and hands it `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars. The actual client_credentials flow lives in the MCP server (`xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts`, also checked out here), cited below as the delegated path.
- `xero-command-line/` — oclif CLI. Uses **PKCE authorization-code OAuth (browser login), NOT client_credentials**. No client secret anywhere.

---

## 1. client_credentials flow specifics

### xero-agent-toolkit itself: none
No match for `client_credentials`, `identity.xero.com`, or `connect/token` anywhere in the repo. The README mandates a **Custom Connection** app type:

> "Make sure you are create a [Custom Connection](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections) type of app - Web app and PKCE apps will not work with these examples" — `xero-agent-toolkit/README.md:67`

Credentials are passed as env vars to the spawned MCP server, e.g. `xero-agent-toolkit/python/langchain/langchain.py:40-48`:

\```python
client = MultiServerMCPClient({
    ...
        "args": ["-y", "@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest"],
        ...
            "XERO_CLIENT_ID": os.getenv("XERO_CLIENT_ID"),
            "XERO_CLIENT_SECRET": os.getenv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"),
\```

Same pattern in `python/google-adk/google-adk.py:30-37`, `python/openai/openai_agents.py:369-376`, `typescript/openai-agents/index.ts:383-391`, `typescript/langchain/index.ts:39-47`, `python/google-adk/cloud-run-invoicing-agent/xero_invoicing_agent/agent.py:9-21`.

### Delegated implementation (xero-mcp-server, what the toolkit actually runs)
`xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:159-174` (`requestToken`):

- **Token endpoint:** `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`
- **Auth:** HTTP **Basic** header, `base64(clientId:clientSecret)` — NOT body params
- **Body:** form-encoded string `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<urlencoded scopes>`
- **Headers:** `Authorization: Basic ...`, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `Accept: application/json`

\```ts
const credentials = Buffer.from(`${this.clientId}:${this.clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
const response = await axios.post(
  "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token",
  `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
  { headers: {
      Authorization: `Basic ${credentials}`,
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
      Accept: "application/json" } },
);
\```

**Scopes ARE passed explicitly** for custom connections (contrary to some docs suggesting they can be omitted). Selection logic at `xero-client.ts:127-157`:
1. If `XERO_SCOPES` env var set → use it verbatim.
2. Else try legacy V1 scope set (`accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.reports.read payroll.settings payroll.employees payroll.timesheets`, lines 82-90).
3. On HTTP 400 with `error === "invalid_scope"` → retry with granular V2 set (`accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals accounting.reports.aged.read accounting.reports.balancesheet.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read accounting.reports.trialbalance.read accounting.contacts accounting.settings payroll.*`, lines 93-107).

Alternative path: if `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` is set, a `BearerTokenXeroClient` skips token issuance entirely (`xero-client.ts:16, 206-231`).

### xero-command-line: PKCE, not client_credentials
`xero-command-line/src/lib/oauth.ts:5-7`:

\```ts
const XERO_AUTH_BASE = 'https://login.xero.com/identity'
const XERO_TOKEN_BASE = 'https://identity.xero.com'
const REDIRECT_URI = 'http://localhost:8742/callback'
\```

- Authorize URL: `https://login.xero.com/identity/connect/authorize` with `code_challenge_method: 'S256'` (`oauth.ts:64-75`).
- Token exchange `oauth.ts:134-155`: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`, form-encoded body `grant_type=authorization_code&client_id=...&code=...&redirect_uri=...&code_verifier=...`. **No Basic auth, no client secret — client_id goes in the body** (public PKCE client).
- Scopes: default list at `oauth.ts:12-30` (granular: `accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals accounting.reports.*.read accounting.budgets.read accounting.attachments`); `openid profile email offline_access` always prepended (`oauth.ts:10, 58-62`); overridable via `xero login --scope` or `XERO_SCOPES` env (`src/commands/login.ts:25-29`).

---

## 2. Tenant id handling

### Custom connections (MCP server path used by agent-toolkit)
The tenant id is **fetched from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` immediately after every token grant, and the FIRST connection is used** — it is not omitted or left empty. `xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:176-190`:

\```ts
// Get the tenant ID from the connections endpoint
const token = response.data.access_token;
const connectionsResponse = await axios.get(
  "https://api.xero.com/connections",
  { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" } },
);
if (connectionsResponse.data && connectionsResponse.data.length > 0) {
  this.tenantId = connectionsResponse.data[0].tenantId;
}
\```

Every tool handler then passes it as the first SDK arg (which xero-node sends as the `xero-tenant-id` header), e.g. `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:18-22`:

\```ts
await xeroClient.authenticate();
... xeroClient.tenantId,
\```

One empty-string fallback exists: `getOrganisations(this.tenantId || "")` at `xero-client.ts:47-49` — i.e. an empty `xero-tenant-id` is tolerated for the org lookup on custom connections, but everywhere else the resolved tenantId is sent.

### xero-command-line
Tenant resolved once at login from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` with `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` (`src/lib/oauth.ts:157-167`); if multiple orgs, user picks one interactively (`oauth.ts:195-204`). The chosen `tenantId` is persisted per profile in the token cache (`src/lib/auth.ts:5-11, 100-106`) and passed as the first argument to every `accountingApi` call, e.g. `src/commands/invoices/list.ts:48-50`:

\```ts
const result = await this.xeroCall(flags, async (xero, tenantId) => {
  const response = await xero.accountingApi.getInvoices(tenantId, ...)
\```

---

## 3. Token caching / expiry / refresh-on-401

### xero-agent-toolkit / MCP server: NO caching
`authenticate()` is called by **every handler before every API call** and unconditionally requests a fresh client_credentials token (no expiry check, no cache) — `xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:195-203`:

\```ts
public async authenticate() {
  const tokenResponse = await this.getClientCredentialsToken();
  this.setTokenSet({
    access_token: tokenResponse.access_token,
    expires_in: tokenResponse.expires_in,
    token_type: tokenResponse.token_type,
  });
}
\```

That is one extra token round-trip (plus one `/connections` call) per tool invocation. Do better in our `api/src/modules/xero/`: cache until `expires_in` with a safety buffer.

### xero-command-line: encrypted cache + 60s pre-expiry refresh + refresh-on-401
- Cache: AES-256-GCM-encrypted tokens in `~/.config/xero-command-line/tokens.json` (`src/lib/auth.ts:25`, `src/lib/crypto.ts:11,121`), key in OS keychain / scrypt passphrase / file.
- Expiry buffer — `src/lib/auth.ts:26, 73-75`:

\```ts
const TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000 // Refresh 60s before expiry
export function isTokenExpired(entry: TokenEntry): boolean {
  return Date.now() >= entry.expiresAt - TOKEN_BUFFER_MS
}
\```

- `expiresAt` derived from `expires_at` (seconds) or `expires_in`, defaulting to 30 min (`auth.ts:87-96`).
- Proactive refresh before building the client (`src/lib/xero-client.ts:22-31`), via `grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=...&refresh_token=...` — again no Basic auth (`src/lib/oauth.ts:209-231`).
- **Refresh-on-401 retry loop** — `src/lib/xero-client.ts:45-78` (`withRetry`, `maxRetries = 2`):

\```ts
// Handle 401: try refreshing token
if (statusCode === 401 && attempt < maxRetries) {
  const cached = await getCachedTokenSet(profileName)
  if (cached?.refreshToken) {
    try {
      const newTokenSet = await refreshAccessToken(clientId, cached.refreshToken)
      await cacheTokenSet(profileName, newTokenSet, cached.tenantId, cached.tenantName)
      continue
    } catch {
      clearCachedToken(profileName)
      throw new Error(`Session expired. Run "xero login" to re-authenticate.`)
    }
  }
  clearCachedToken(profileName)
  continue
}
\```

---

## 4. Tool/command inventory

### xero-agent-toolkit
Defines **no tools itself** — it exposes whatever `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server` publishes. The TS examples attach the server unfiltered ("basic Xero agent with all tools", `typescript/openai-agents/index.ts:35-48`); the Google ADK examples filter to subsets via `tool_filter` (`python/google-adk/google-adk.py:40-55`: `list-contacts, create-contact, list-invoices, create-invoice, get-timesheet, list-accounts, list-items, list-tax-rates, list-tracking-categories, update-contact, update-invoice, create-payment, list-payments, list-organisation-details`; `cloud-run-invoicing-agent/xero_invoicing_agent/agent.py:22-31`: 8-tool invoicing subset).

Full underlying surface (from `xero-mcp-server/src/tools/{create,delete,get,list,update}/`), 50 tools:
- create: `create-bank-transaction, create-contact, create-credit-note, create-invoice, create-item, create-manual-journal, create-payment, create-payroll-timesheet, create-quote, create-tracking-category, create-tracking-options`
- list: `list-accounts, list-aged-payables-by-contact, list-aged-receivables-by-contact, list-bank-transactions, list-contact-groups, list-contacts, list-credit-notes, list-invoices, list-items, list-manual-journals, list-organisation-details, list-payments, list-payroll-employee-leave, list-payroll-employee-leave-balances, list-payroll-employee-leave-types, list-payroll-employees, list-payroll-leave-periods, list-payroll-leave-types, list-payroll-timesheets, list-profit-and-loss, list-quotes, list-report-balance-sheet, list-tax-rates, list-tracking-categories, list-trial-balance`
- update: `approve-payroll-timesheet, revert-payroll-timesheet, update-bank-transaction, update-contact, update-credit-note, update-invoice, update-item, update-manual-journal, update-payroll-timesheet-add-line, update-payroll-timesheet-update-line, update-quote, update-tracking-category, update-tracking-options`
- get: `get-payroll-timesheet`; delete: `delete-payroll-timesheet`

**Answers:** purchase order tools: **NO**. Attachment tools: **NO**. History tools: **NO**. Payment tools: **YES** (`create-payment`, `list-payments` — no update/delete payment). The toolkit does NOT cover more than the MCP server; it is strictly a consumer of it. POs/attachments/history must go through our raw Accounting API module as planned.

### xero-command-line (from `src/commands/`)
`login`, `logout`; `profile add|list|remove|set-default`; `org details`; `contacts list|create|update`; `contact-groups list`; `accounts list|update`; `invoices list|create|update`; `quotes list|create|update`; `credit-notes list|create|update`; `manual-journals list|create|update`; `bank-transactions list|create|update`; `payments list|create`; `items list|create|update`; `currencies list`; `tax-rates list`; `tracking categories list|create|update`; `tracking options create|update`; `reports trial-balance|profit-and-loss|balance-sheet|aged-receivables|aged-payables`.

No purchase orders, no attachments, no history endpoints here either (though the default login scope list includes `accounting.attachments`, `oauth.ts:29`).

---

## 5. Rate limits / retry / Retry-After

- **xero-agent-toolkit:** nothing concrete — README marketing lines only (`README.md:98` "Robust error management and retry logic"; `typescript/langchain/README.md:162` mentions "Xero API rate limits" in a troubleshooting list). No code.
- **xero-command-line:** `withRetry` in `src/lib/xero-client.ts:81-84` does NOT auto-retry 429s — it surfaces the wait time:

\```ts
if (statusCode === 429) {
  const retryAfter = extractRetryAfter(parsed, lastError.message)
  throw new Error(`Rate limited. Retry after ${retryAfter} seconds.`)
}
\```

  `extractRetryAfter` (`xero-client.ts:159-170`) reads the `retry-after` response header from the parsed SDK error, falls back to regex `/retry-after[":\s]+(\d+)/i` over the error message, defaults to **60 seconds**.
- **xero-mcp-server** (delegated): no retry either; maps 429 to a friendly string for the LLM — `src/helpers/format-error.ts:35-36`: `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."`

---

## 6. SDK vs raw HTTP

| Repo | API calls | Token/identity calls |
|---|---|---|
| xero-agent-toolkit | none directly (MCP server does everything); frameworks: `langchain-mcp-adapters`, `@langchain/mcp-adapters`, `google-adk MCPToolset`, `@openai/agents MCPServerStdio` | none |
| xero-mcp-server (delegated) | `xero-node` SDK (`accountingApi.*`, class extends `XeroClient`, `src/clients/xero-client.ts:1-8,23`) | raw **axios** for `connect/token` and `/connections` (`xero-client.ts:164, 178`) |
| xero-command-line | `xero-node` **^13** SDK (`package.json`; client built with `new XeroClient({clientId, clientSecret: ''})` + `setTokenSet({access_token})`, `src/lib/xero-client.ts:33-34`) | raw **fetch** for authorize/token/refresh/connections (`src/lib/oauth.ts:143, 158, 219`) |

Extra pattern worth copying: the CLI injects a `user-agent: xero-command-line-<version>` default header into the SDK (`src/lib/get-client-headers.ts:3-9`, applied at `src/lib/xero-client.ts:36-40`).

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 11:37:40Z)**_

WROTE /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md

- xero-agent-toolkit has ZERO auth code and ZERO own tools; it just spawns @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server with XERO_CLIENT_ID/SECRET env vars.
- Real client_credentials flow (in the delegated MCP server): POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token, HTTP Basic base64(id:secret), form body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...`; scopes ARE sent (XERO_SCOPES env, else legacy V1 set, fallback granular V2 on invalid_scope).
- Custom-connection tenant id: fetched from GET api.xero.com/connections after every grant, first tenant used, passed as xero-tenant-id via xero-node; one `tenantId || ""` empty fallback for getOrganisations.
- MCP server does NO token caching — fresh token + /connections call per tool invocation. Our api/ module should cache with expiry buffer instead.
- Toolkit/MCP surface (50 tools): NO purchase orders, NO attachments, NO history; payments = create-payment + list-payments only. POs/attachments need raw Accounting API as planned.
- xero-command-line uses PKCE browser OAuth (no secret, client_id in body), AES-256-GCM token cache, 60s pre-expiry refresh, refresh-on-401 retry loop (maxRetries=2).
- 429 handling (CLI only): no auto-retry; reads retry-after header, regex fallback, default 60s. MCP server just returns a friendly 429 string.
- Both use xero-node SDK for API calls; raw axios/fetch only for identity endpoints. CLI also injects a versioned user-agent header — worth copying.

      uid=1_251 heading "Purchasing Custom Connection subscriptions" level="2"
      uid=1_252 link "anchor for purchasing custom connection subscriptions" url="https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections/#purchasing-custom-connection-subscriptions"
      uid=1_253 StaticText "Before a Xero customer can authorise and connect Custom Connection apps to their organisation, the organisation needs to have purchased a subscription with a sufficient quantity of Custom Connections. There is more Custom Connection information for Xero customers on "
      uid=1_254 link "Xero Central" url="https://central.xero.com/s/article/Create-custom-integrations-and-apps-with-Xero"
        uid=1_255 StaticText "Xero Central"
      uid=1_256 StaticText "."
      uid=1_257 StaticText "Xero customers can now have multiple Custom Connection apps connected to their organisation. The organisation needs a subscription with enough Custom Connections for all of their apps. If a Xero customer has already purchased a subscription but their organisation is unavailable to connect to your app ensure they have purchased sufficient Custom Connections."
      uid=1_258 StaticText "A Custom Connection subscription is purchased from "
      uid=1_259 link "the Custom Connections page" url="https://connect.xero.com/custom"
        uid=1_260 StaticText "the Custom Connections page"
      uid=1_261 StaticText " or from within the Xero organisation on the "
      uid=1_262 link "connected apps" url="https://apps.xero.com/connected"
        uid=1_263 StaticText "connected apps"
      uid=1_264 StaticText " page where the subscription can be managed. Any user with the Standard, Advisor, or Administrator user role with the Connected Apps permission can buy or manage a Custom Connection. Practice administrators and staff can only purchase a Custom Connection for their own practice's Xero organisation. We recommend clients purchase their organisation's Custom Connection themselves."
      uid=1_265 image "A screenshot of the page used to purchase your Custom Connection" description="Purchase Custom Connection page" url="https://developer.xero.com/static/images/documentation/purchase-custom.png"

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 11:37:48Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Extract markdown doc content from each page-data JSON to files

```bash
SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
python3 << 'EOF'
import json
SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
for name, f in [("attachments","att-pd.json"),("history","hist-pd.json"),("reports","rep-pd.json"),("contacts","con-pd.json")]:
    d = json.load(open(f"{SP}/{f}"))
    pc = d['result']['pageContext']
    out = []
    def dump(o, depth=0):
        if isinstance(o, dict):
            for k,v in o.items():
                if isinstance(v, str):
                    out.append(f"### FIELD {k}\n{v}\n")
                else:
                    dump(v, depth+1)
        elif isinstance(o, list):
            for v in o: dump(v, depth+1)
    ep = pc.get('endpoints')
    dump(ep if ep else pc)
    open(f"{SP}/{name}.md","w").write("\n".join(out))
    print(name, sum(len(x) for x in out))
EOF
```

```text
attachments 31343
history 29385
reports 84832
contacts 64732
```
</details></tool-use>

1725

1727	Attachments
1728	
1729	### FIELD slug
1730	attachments
1731	
1732	### FIELD title
1733	Attachments
1734	
1735	### FIELD sidebar_label
1736	Attachments
1737	
1738	### FIELD route
1739	/accounting-api/attachments
1740	
1741	### FIELD value
1742	Overview
1743	
1744	### FIELD value

1746	
1747	### FIELD value
1748	GET Attachment Content
1749	
1750	### FIELD value

1754	Include with Online Invoice
1755

1758	
1759	### FIELD fileAbsolutePath
1760	/devx/packages/site/.cache/gatsby-source-git/XeroDocs/api-references/Accounting-API/attachments.md
1761	
1762	### FIELD rawMarkdownBody
1763	
1764	## Overview
1765	
1766	|                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
1767	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1768	| URL               | https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments                                                                                                                                                |
1769	| Methods Supported | [GET](/documentation/api/accounting/attachments#get-attachments), [PUT](/documentation/api/accounting/attachments#put-attachments), [POST](/documentation/api/accounting/attachments#post-attachments)        |
1770	| Description       | Allows you to upload an attachment against an existing document Allows you to retrieve a list of attachments against a single existing document Allows you to download the content of a specific attachments. |
1771	
1772	## GET Attachments
1773	
1774	The GET method supports retrieving a list of attachments or receiving the content of a single attachments.
1775	
1776	To retrieve a list of attachments that have been uploaded against a document, you will need to construct a url that contains the Guid of the parent object e.g. Invoice. This url is in the format:
1777	
1778	```
1779	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/
1780	```
1781	
1782	e.g.
1783	
1784	```
1785	GET /api.xro/2.0/Receipts/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/
1786	Authorization: Bearer...
1787	Accept: text/xml
1788	
1789	```
1790	
1791	The response message will contain an xml or json summary of the attachments against the specified document:
1792	
1793	```
1794	{
1795	  "Attachments": [
1796	    {
1797	      "AttachmentID": "e59a2c7f-1306-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9",
1798	      "FileName": "Image00394.png",
1799	      "Url": "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Receipts/220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734/Attachments/Image00394.png",
1800	      "MimeType": "image/png",
1801	      "ContentLength": "10294"
1802	    }
1803	  ]
1804	}
1805	
1806	```
1807	
1808	#### GET Attachment Content
1809	
1810	To retrieve the content of an attachment, the URL element shown in the response of a 'GET Attachments' will retrieve the file.
1811	
1812	The url will typically be in the following format:
1813	
1814	```
1815	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
1816	```
1817	
1818	e.g.
1819	
1820	```
1821	 GET /api.xro/2.0/Receipts/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/Image00394.png
1822	Authorization: Bearer...
1823	```
1824	
1825	The response message will contain the raw file content that was originally uploaded. The response won't contain any xml or json encoded information:
1826	
1827	```
1828	HTTP/1.1 200 OK
1829	Content-Type: image/png
1830	Content-Disposition: attachment; Image00394.png
1831	
1832	{RAW-IMAGE-CONTENT}
1833	```
1834	
1835	## POST Attachments
1836	
1837	Attachments can be uploaded to Xero using the PUT or POST method. To upload an attachment, a PUT or POST http request is made to a specific url created for the each attachment. The body of the http request contains the raw attachment content, not xml or json data that is normally used to upload data to Xero. Attachments can be uploaded to the following:
1838	
1839	- Invoices
1840	- Receipts
1841	- Credit Notes
1842	- Repeating Invoices
1843	- Bank Transactions
1844	- Bank Transfers
1845	- Contacts
1846	- Accounts
1847	- Manual Journals
1848	- Purchase Orders
1849	- Quotes
1850	
1851	When uploading an attachment to Xero, the url that you're posting to will typically be in the following format:
1852	
1853	```
1854	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
1855	```
1856	
1857	|            |                                                                                        |
1858	| ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1859	| {Endpoint} | The name of the parent endpoint (e.g. Receipts, Invoices)                              |
1860	| {Guid}     | The guid of the document that that attachment belongs to (e.g. ReceiptID or InvoiceID) |
1861	| {Filename} | The filename of the attachment that you are uploading. Filenames including any of the characters `<`, `>`, `:`, `"`, `/`, `\`, `\|`, `?`, `*`, `\0`, `+` will be rejected as a `Bad Request`.  |
1862	
1863	**Note:** 10 attachments can be uploaded per document (each up to 10mb in size). You can replace any attachment already uploaded by specifying the URL of the existing attachment e.g. https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
1864	
1865	e.g.
1866	
1867	```
1868	POST /api.xro/2.0/Receipts/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/Image00394.png
1869	Authorization: Bearer...
1870	Content Type: image/png
1871	Content-Length: 10495
1872	Accept: text/xml
1873	
1874	{RAW-IMAGE-CONTENT}
1875	```
1876	
1877	The response message will contain a summary of the attachment that has been accepted into the API:
1878	
1879	```
1880	{
1881	  "Attachments": [
1882	    {
1883	      "AttachmentID": "e59a2c7f-1306-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9",
1884	      "FileName": "Image00394.png",
1885	      "Url": "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Receipts/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/Image00394.png",
1886	      "MimeType": "image/png",
1887	      "ContentLength": "10294"
1888	    }
1889	  ]
1890	}
1891	
1892	```
1893	
1894	#### Include with Online Invoice
1895	
1896	You can set an attachment to be included with the invoice when viewed online (through Xero). This functionality is available for accounts receivable invoices and accounts receivable credit notes. To enable an attachment to be viewed with the online invoice add the parameter IncludeOnline=true to the end of your request.
1897	
1898	e.g.
1899	
1900	```
1901	POST /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/Image00394.png?IncludeOnline=true
1902	Authorization: Bearer...
1903	Content Type: image/png
1904	Content-Length: 10495
1905	Accept: text/xml
1906	
1907	{RAW-IMAGE-CONTENT}
1908	```
1909	
1910	If you have set IncludeOnline to true, the element will be returned in the response message:
1911	
1912	```
1913	{
1914	  "Attachments": [
1915	    {
1916	      "AttachmentID": "e59a2c7f-1306-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9",
1917	      "FileName": "Image00394.png",
1918	      "Url": "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Receipts/e59a2c7f-4078-a0f3-73537afcbba9/Attachments/Image00394.png",
1919	      "MimeType": "image/png",
1920	      "ContentLength": "10294",
1921	      "IncludeOnline" : true
1922	    }
1923	  ]
1924	}
1925	
1926	```
1927	
1928	## PUT Attachments
1929	
1930	The PUT method is identical to the POST method. If an attachment already exists on the specified document, then the attachment being uploaded will overwrite it.
1931	
1932	**Note:** When the file name includes special characters, the characters should not be encoded unless they're brackets. Brackets must be encoded in order for the call to go through, and all other characters must be unencoded.
1933	
1934	
1935	### FIELD apiTitle
1936	Accounting API
1937
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Extract rawMarkdownBody from history, reports, contacts page-data

```bash
SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
python3 << 'EOF'
import json
SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
for name, f in [("history","hist-pd.json"),("reports","rep-pd.json"),("contacts","con-pd.json")]:
    d = json.load(open(f"{SP}/{f}"))
    bodies = []
    def walk(o):
        if isinstance(o, dict):
            for k,v in o.items():
                if k == 'rawMarkdownBody' and isinstance(v, str): bodies.append(v)
                else: walk(v)
        elif isinstance(o, list):
            for v in o: walk(v)
    walk(d)
    open(f"{SP}/{name}-body.md","w").write("\n\n=====\n\n".join(bodies))
    print(name, len(bodies), sum(len(b) for b in bodies))
EOF
```

```text
history 1 4534
reports 1 58889
contacts 1 39395
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/history-body.md`

```text
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	|                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
5	| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
6	| URL               | https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
7	| Methods Supported | [GET](/documentation/api/accounting/historyandnotes#get-history), [PUT](/documentation/api/accounting/historyandnotes#put-history), [POST](/documentation/api/accounting/historyandnotes#post-history)                                                                                   |
8	| Description       | Allows you to retrieve the history of changes to a single existing document <br />Allows you add notes against a single existing document ([See supported document types](/documentation/api/accounting/historyandnotes#supported-document-types-for-adding-notes-and-retrieving-history)) |
9	
10	## GET History
11	
12	Response elements returned for GET History:
13	
14	|         |                                                                                                                                               |
15	| ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
16	| Changes | The type of change recorded against the document. See [here for a full list of possible changes](https://help.xero.com/Payments_HistoryNotes) |
17	| DateUTC | UTC date that the history record was created                                                                                                  |
18	| User    | The user responsible for the change ("System Generated" when the change happens via API)                                                      |
19	| Details | Description of the change event or transaction                                                                                                |
20	
21	Example response retrieving History
22	
23	```
24	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history
25	```
26	
27	```
28	{
29	  "HistoryRecords": [
30	     {
31	      "Changes": "Approved",
32	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
33	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
34	      "User": "System Generated",
35	      "Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"
36	    },
37	    {
38	      "Changes": "Edited",
39	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
40	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
41	      "User": "Mac Haag",
42	      "Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."
43	    }
44	    ...
45	  ]
46	}
47	
48	```
49	
50	## PUT History
51	
52	Use this method to add notes to a document. Notes are the only type of Change that can be manually created. All other types of Change are created automatically when certain actions occur (e.g. an invoice is paid).
53	
54	The note will be displayed in history & notes showing the date of creation and "System Generated" as the user that created it.
55	
56	The following elements are used in a PUT request:
57	
58	|         |                                                                                |
59	| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
60	| Details | The note to be recorded against a single document. Max Length 2500 characters. |
61	
62	Example request to add notes
63	
64	```
65	PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history
66	```
67	
68	```
69	{
70	  "HistoryRecords": [
71	    {
72	      "Details": "Previous payment failed"
73	    },
74	    {
75	      "Details": "Defer the payment"
76	    }
77	    ...
78	  ]
79	}
80	
81	```
82	
83	## POST History
84	
85	In this case a POST is exactly the same as a PUT. It's not possible to update history and notes.
86	
87	## Supported document types for adding notes and retrieving History
88	
89	- BankTransactions
90	- BatchPayments
91	- BankTransfers (this can be accessed via the BankTransactions endpoint)
92	- Contacts
93	- Creditnotes
94	- Invoices
95	- Items
96	- ManualJournals
97	- Overpayments
98	- Payments
99	- Prepayments
100	- Purchase Orders
101	- Repeating Invoices
102	- Quotes
103
```
</details></tool-use>
