```json
"OAuth 2.0 API limits\nUncertified app limits\n\nNew apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections. Moving up to Core will give you up to 50 connections. To be listed on the App Store, you will need to be in the Plus tier or above. We recommend applying to be listed on the Xero App Store once you’ve onboarded at least 10 beta customers. If you would like to discuss how the limit and certification applies to your use case please contact us.\n\nAdditionally, each organisation or practice is limited to connecting a maximum of two uncertified apps. There is no limit on connecting certified apps.\n\nAPI rate limits\n\nThere are limits to the number of API calls that your application can make against a particular tenant (organisation, account or practice):\n\nConcurrent Limit: 5 calls in progress at one time\nMinute Limit: 60 calls per minute\nDaily Limit: 1,000 calls per day for starter, 5,000 calls per day for higher tiers\n\nThere is also a limit to the number of API calls your app can make per minute across all tenants.\n\nApp Minute Limit: 10,000 calls per minute\n\nEach API response you receive will include the X-DayLimit-Remaining, X-MinLimit-Remaining and X-AppMinLimit-Remaining headers telling you the number of remaining against each limit.\n\nExceeding a rate limit\n\nExceeding a rate limit will result in an HTTP 429 (too many requests) response. It will include an X-Rate-Limit-Problem header telling you which limit you have reached.\n\nIf you have exceed the minute or daily limit you will also receive a Retry-After http header that tells you how many seconds to wait before making another request. Requests are counted against a fixed window which will reset at different times for each tenant. It is important to use the Retry-After header to know when you can start making calls again.\n\nRate limit FAQ\nWhat if I need more than 1,000 calls a day?\n\nApps in the starter tier have access to 1000 calls a day. If you need more than 1000 calls a day, you will need to move to the Core tier or above.\n\nWhat if I need more than 5,000 calls a day?\n\nIf you are in Core or above and believe you would exceed the Xero API rate limits, you most likely can in fact work within the limits by analysing the structure of how you intend to use the Xero API:\n\nYou can do more than one thing in a single request: For example, you can create more than one Invoice in a single PUT or POST Invoices API call. While there is no upper limit in the number of nodes that can be sent at one time, a ceiling of about 50 nodes per request is practical – this will ensure a request does not exceed the maximum size of 3.5MB.\n\nWhat is the best way to handle reaching a limit?\n\nWhen you reach a rate limit the Retry-After header will tell you how long to wait before making another call. You should pause requests to that tenant until that time.\n\nWhat if I need to retrieve large amounts of data from Xero?\n\nOne function which can cause an application to exceed the usage limits is extracting data from Xero eg:\n\nRetrieving all invoices does not return line item details for individual invoices. You can use pagination to retrieve line item details for 100 invoices at a time. Endpoints that currently support pagination include invoices, credit notes, contacts, bank transactions and manual journals.\nWhen working with other endpoints, a request for each individual object may be required to get full details.\nAn organisation can have many thousand Journals, which can be retrieved only in batches of 100. Keep in mind the Journals endpoint is a premium feature for apps in the Advanced tier and above.\n\nIn these situations, it may take some time to extract the required data – it is recommended that an application is structured to schedule or queue this function so there is no user expectation of an immediate response.\n\nDoes my application only have 5,000 requests for all my users?\n\nNo, the limits are per tenant. For example if two separate Xero organisations are connected to your application, each connection would have 5000 API calls available in a given 24 hour period for apps in the Core tier and above and 1000 API calls available for apps in the Starter tier.\n\nRequest size Limit\n\nThe maximum request size limit for all APIs is 10MB. However, to ensure you receive a timely response from the API (large inserts can take quite a while), we recommend you look to batch elements in bundles of up to 50.\n\nSystem limits\n\nXero is suitable for different types of business, including those with high transaction volumes.\n\nInvoicing\n\nXero is designed for volumes of up to 5,000 Sales invoices (Accounts Receivables) and 5,000 Purchases bills (Accounts Payables) per month, dependent also on the frequency of invoicing during the month, variability of amounts and the frequency of sales tax reporting requirements.\n\nBank transactions – spend and receive money\n\nXero is designed for volumes of up to 5,000 bank transactions per month, also dependent on the frequency of transactions during the month and variability of transaction values.\n\nInventory items\n\nXero recommends a maximum number of 4,000 tracked inventory items per organisation. Performance issues may occur when the total number of tracked items in an organisation exceeds this limit. We recommend you only create or update 100 items per API request.\n\nContacts\n\nContact lists of greater than 10,000 could cause performance issues.\n\nFixed assets\n\nXero is designed to support up to 500 fixed assets. Having more assets than this can cause problems trying to work with the assets and with running depreciation. Xero will work with higher levels than this but the performance of some features and reports may become degraded.\n\nXero pricing plan limits\nStarter plan\n\nXero organisations using the “Early” pricing plan can enter up to 20 approved Accounts Receivable invoices and 5 approved Accounts Payable invoices per month. The invoice date (not the creation date) is used to determine which month an invoice was entered.\n\nIf you exceed this invoice limit when using the Xero API you will receive an HTTP 400 response code with the following error message:\n\n<validationerror>\n  <message>You have reached the limit of invoices you can approve.</message>\n</validationerror>\ncopy code\nPartner edition plans\n\nXero partner edition plans such as cashbook and ledger organisations, can be connected to the Xero API.\n\nAuthorisation of connections to cashbook or ledger organisations must be done by a member of the practice staff – managed client or cashbook client roles cannot authorize an API connection. Note that as these plans do not include invoicing functionality, any invoices created via the API could not be edited or modified, so this function is recommended to be avoided.\n\nOn this page\nUncertified app limits\nAPI rate limits\nRate limit FAQ\nRequest size Limit\nSystem limits\nXero pricing plan limits\nAI toolkit\nMCP Server\nAgents Toolkit\nPrompt Library\nDocumentation\nOAuth 2\nAPI reference\nWebhooks\nCommunity\nGitHub\nProduct Feedback\nConnect to the Community\nWeekly Office Hours\nDev Day Discord Server\nFind a developer\nNews\nDevBlog\nAPI status\nChangelog\nSupport\nContact us\nFAQ\n© 2026 Xero Limited. All rights reserved. \"Xero\", \"Beautiful business\" and \"Your business supercharged\" are trademarks of Xero Limited.\nAbout Xero\nDeveloper Platform Terms and Conditions\nThe Xero Developer Platform Commercial Terms\nXero ecosystem principles\nDev Council Terms and Conditions"
```
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/reports-body.md`

```text
1	
2	[Try in API Explorer](https://api-explorer.xero.com/accounting/reports)
3	
4	## Overview
5	
6	Xero's most commonly viewed reports (listed below) can be retrieved using the Xero API. These reports typically contain a summary of data that may be useful for your own application. e.g. it may be more easier and more efficient to use a Report endpoint to fetch data rather than retrieve individual invoices and total these yourself.
7	
8	### Endpoints
9	
10	Each report has its own endpoint e.g. `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/[Report Name]` and can be returned in either XML (default) or JSON format.
11	
12	### Permissions
13	
14	An application that has been authorised by a Standard user without the "reports" role will not be able to access the Reports, Journals, and ManualJournals endpoints (A HTTP 403 error will be returned in this case).
15	
16	### Parameters
17	
18	Each report has different optional parameters. The report parameters should be added as a separate query strings. For example:  
19	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/AgedReceivablesByContact?fromDate=2010-01-01&toDate=2011-01-01
20	
21	### Layout
22	
23	The layout of each report is a collection of rows and cells. Rows can be of various types (e.g. header, section, row and summary row elements). Cells can contain values and attributes with nested values.
24	
25	Try the [API Explorer](https://api-explorer.xero.com/) to get familiar with the response format for each report or see the [Trial Balance](/documentation/#TrialBalance) example below.
26	
27	More details about each reporting endpoint are listed below. To learn more about the composition of specific reports refer to the [Report Centre](https://go.xero.com/Reports/Default.aspx).
28	
29	|                                                                                                  |
30	| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
31	| [1099 Report](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#1099-report) (US organisations only)         |
32	| [Aged Payables By Contact](/documentation/api/accounting/reports##aged-payables-by-contact)      |
33	| [Aged Receivables By Contact](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#aged-receivables-by-contact) |
34	| [Balance Sheet](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#balance-sheet)                             |
35	| [Bank Summary](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#bank-summary)                               |
36	| [BAS Report](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#bas-report) (Australia organisations only)    |
37	| [Budget Summary](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#budget-summary)                           |
38	| [Executive Summary](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#executive-summary)                     |
39	| [GST Report](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#gst-report) (New Zealand organisations only)  |
40	| [Profit And Loss](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#profit-and-loss)                         |
41	| [Trial Balance](/documentation/api/accounting/reports#trial-balance)                             |
42	
43	## 1099 Report
44	
45	|                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
46	| ----------------- |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
47	| URL               | https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/TenNinetyNine                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
48	| Methods Supported | GET                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
49	| Description       | Returns the 1099 report, for all years from 2012, as configured by the rules in the Xero app.<br/>The 1099 report includes both the 1099-NEC and 1099-MISC forms for all reports in years 2020 onward. For reports 2019 and prior, only the 1099-MISC form applies.<br/>This report is available to US organizations only. To access this report, the user must have the advisor user role in the organization.|
50	
51	### Optional parameters for GET 1099
52	
53	|            |                              |
54	| ---------- |------------------------------|
55	| reportYear | Year of the report e.g. 2025 |
56	
57	List of supported Federal Tax Classification values:
58	* SOLE_PROPRIETOR
59	* PARTNERSHIP
60	* TRUST_OR_ESTATE
61	* NONPROFIT
62	* C_CORP
63	* S_CORP
64	* OTHER
65	
66	Example response for GET TenNinetyNine (2025 and after)
67	
68	```
69	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/TenNinetyNine?reportYear=2025
70	```
71	
72	```json
73	{
74	  "Id": "8b474ddb-9ef4-457c-8640-1c0e3670ea0e",
75	  "Status": "OK",
76	  "ProviderName": "Hornblower Enterprises",

78	  "Reports": [

80	      "ReportName": "1099-NEC report",

```text
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"Skip to main content\nGetting Started\n2026 App Awards\nDocumentation\nTools\nSupport\nSign up\nLog in\nAPIs\nAccounting API\nOverview\nAccounts\nAttachments\nBank Statements\nBank Transactions\nBank Transfers\nBatch Payments\nBranding Themes\nBudgets\nContact Groups\nContacts\nCredit Notes\nCurrencies\nExpense Claims (Deprecated)\nHTTP Requests and Responses\nHistory and Notes\nInvoice Reminders\nInvoices\nItems\nJournals\nLinked Transactions\nManual Journals\nOrganisation\nOverpayments\nPayment Services\nPayments\nPrepayments\nPurchase Orders\nQuotes\nReceipts (Deprecated)\nRelease Notes\nRepeating Invoices\nReports\nResponse Codes\nTax Rates\nTracking Categories\nTypes\nUsers\nAssets API\nBank Feeds API\nFiles API\nFinance API\nPayroll AU API\nPayroll NZ API\nPayroll UK API\nPractice Manager 3.0\nPractice Manager 3.1\nProjects API\nXero App Store API\neInvoicing API\nHTTP Requests and Responses\nHTTP GET\nRetrieving individual resources\n\nTo retrieve a specific resource you can append its identifier to the end of the URL. The example below shows retrieving a specific contact using the HTTP GET method.\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts/fe61ead1-8afc-4f0b-beda-066620227aad\ncopy code\nSuccessful responses return with a HTTP 200 status code\nBy default all successful responses on the accounting API are returned as XML. Responses on the other APIs will vary.\nIndividual Invoices, Individual Quotes and Credit Notes can also be returned in PDF format by setting the “Accept” value in the http header to “application/pdf”.\nSome documents may have attachments. You can retrieve these via the API too. Learn More\nJSON responses and date formats\n\nJSON formatted responses are also supported by setting the “Accept” value in the http header to “application/json” when making a request.\n\nAt Xero we use .NET, and used the Microsoft .NET JSON date format available at the time of original development. We know it's ugly but not something we can fix without a breaking change or a new version of the API. We're really sorry about this. To help you along, below we explain how to handle this date format. An example date/time in JSON is returned like this:\n\n\"DateTimeUTC\": \"\\/Date(1439434356790)\\/\" or \"PeriodLockDate\": \"\\/Date(1419937200000+0000)\\/\"\n\nIn both cases, the date/time value is a unix timestamp value, but in miliseconds rather than seconds (so divide by 1,000 for seconds). For some elements we also include a date string value on JSON responses to help with reading date values. e.g. DateString, DueDateString.\n\nAll resources\n\nWith some endpoints you may need to retrieve a full list of resources e.g. TaxRates or BrandingThemes. You can do this by simply using a HTTP GET with the resource name.\n\nGET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/TaxRates\ncopy code\n\nWe recommend all calls to any endpoints that have large result sets use the if-modified-since parameter.\n\nRetrieving modified resources\n\nThe easiest way to retrieve resources that have been created or modified since a previous request is to specify a UTC timestamp filter using the If-Modified-Since http parameter. Only items created or updated since the specified timestamp will be returned (accurate to the second).\n\nPlease note: Not all changes will trigger a change of the UpdatedDateUTC field. These include changes to partially paid transactions which don't generate a journal such as DueDate or SentToContact, and Contact fields pulled from other sources such as Balances, IsSupplier, and isCustomer. As a result, transactions with these changes may not be returned with an If-Modified-Since query.\n\nRetrieving paged resources\n\nTo ensure efficiency when retrieving multiple records, paging is recommended.\n\nPaged results are available on the Invoices, Contacts, CreditNotes, BankTransactions, ManualJournals, Payments, PurchaseOrders, Prepayments and Overpayments endpoints. For endpoints with summarised unpaginated behaviour e.g. Contacts details on an invoice, paged results will have extra detail (e.g. line items) and may mean you don’t have to retrieve individual resources to get the information you need.\n\nTo utilise paging you must append a page query parameter to the URL e.g. ?page=1.\n\nIn addition to paging, you can set the page size to determine the number of items returned per page. The default page size is 100, with a maximum of 1000 and a minimum of 1. If users set a value outside the supported range, we automatically adjust it to the nearest supported page size. For example, ?page=1&pageSize=5000 will return 1000 items. If users don't specify a value, the default is applied. For example, ?page=1 would return 100 items.\n\nThe example below shows the response users will receive if they use the query https://.../BankTransactions?page=1&pageSize=10. A Pagination object is returned as part of the HTTP response, containing the paging metadata.\n\n{\n  \"Id\": \"...\",\n  \"Status\": \"OK\",\n  \"DateTimeUTC\": \"...\",\n  \"pagination\": {\n    \"page\": 1,\n    \"pageSize\": 10,\n    \"pageCount\": 1,\n    \"itemCount\": 1\n  },\n  \"BankTransactions\": [\n    {\n      \"BankTransactionID\": \"...\",\n      \"Type\": \"SPEND\",\n      \"Reference\": \"BT-1\",\n      \"IsReconciled\": false,\n      \"HasAttachments\": false,\n      ...\n    }\n  ]\n}\n\ncopy code\nSample HTTP response in XML\n\nPreviously, to fetch all pages, it was recommended to continue to fetch the next page until no more items are returned. e.g. If there are 100 records in the response you will need to check if there is any more items by fetching the next page e.g. ?page=2 and continuing this process until no more items are returned. This is superseded by the pagination object.\n\nRetrieving a filtered set of resources\n\nThe number of parameters is only limited by the maximum url length. To ensure you receive a timely response from the API, we recommend you look to retrieve in a batch of 40.\n\nOur busiest endpoints ( Invoices and Contacts ) have explicit query parameters for commonly used filters. These parameters allow you to pass a comma-separated list of values in a single request. For faster response times we recommend using these where available.\n\nExample 1 : Retrieve Invoices with specific InvoiceID(s)\n\nGET https://.../Invoices?IDs=220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734,88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0\ncopy code\n\nExample 2 : Retrieve all Invoices for a specific set of Contacts\n\nGET https://.../Invoices?ContactIDs=3138017f-8ddc-420e-a159-e7e1cf9e643d,4b2df4a1-7aa5-4ce3-9e9c-3c55794c5283\ncopy code\n\nExample 3 : Retrieve all unpaid Invoices against a particular Contact\n\nGET https://.../Invoices?ContactIDs=3138017f-8ddc-420e-a159-e7e1cf9e643d&Statuses=AUTHORISED\ncopy code\n\nExample 4 : Retrieve all DELETED or VOIDED Invoices\n\nGET https://.../Invoices?Statuses=DELETED,VOIDED\ncopy code\nRetrieving a filtered set of resources using the “where” parameter\n\nThe where parameter allows you to filter on endpoints and elements that don't have explicit parameters.\n\nit can reference most elements on the resource.\nit must be encoded using percent encoding before it is appended to the URL.\n\nPlease note: even though the where filter supports complex queries we recommend you keep them as simple as possible. Long, complex where queries can cause time outs. To ensure your calls run efficiently against larger organisations it's a good idea to restrict your queries to simple == operations. For more information on optimised queries, please refer to tips to efficient data retrieval.\n\nExample 1 : Retrieve all Bank Accounts using the Accounts endpoint\n\nType==\"BANK\"\ncopy code\n\nThis would translate to the following URL once percent encoded.\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts?where=Type%3D%3D%22BANK%22\ncopy code\n\nExample 2: Retrieve all invoices with AmountDue amount greater than a specific amount, and DueDate after a date using the Invoices endpoint (use and or && to connect conditions)\n\nAmountDue > 5000 and DueDate > DateTime(2015, 01, 01)\ncopy code\n\nExample 3: Retrieve invoices with an invoice date between a date range\n\nDate >= DateTime(2015, 01, 01) && Date < DateTime(2015, 12, 31)\ncopy code\nRetrieving a smaller lightweight response using the “SummaryOnly” parameter\n\nUse summaryOnly=true in GET Contacts and Invoices endpoint to retrieve a smaller version of the response object. This returns only lightweight fields, excluding computation-heavy fields from the response, making the API calls quick and efficient.\n\nPlease note: This query parameter works with other filters, but not when filtering on the fields that are exluded in the response.\nTo get more details around what fields are excluded, please refer to the Contacts and Invoices documentation.\n\nExample 1 : Retrieve smaller lightweight response for Get Invoices\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?summaryOnly=True\ncopy code\n\nExample 2 : Retrieve smaller lightweight response for Get Contacts\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/contacts?summaryOnly=True\ncopy code\nOrdering of results\n\nA list of items can be returned in a specific order. To specify the ordering, append an order query string to the endpoint URL.\nExample : To order contacts by email address the following url could be used\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts?order=EmailAddress\ncopy code\n\nYou can get results in descending order by using DESC parameter.\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts?order=EmailAddress%20DESC\ncopy code\n\nSecondary ordering, using the ID field, is now applied by default across a range of endpoints – Contacts, Payments, Batch Payments, Credit Notes, Invoices and Bank Transactions. This will ensure consistency in ordering across pages. The default order is UpdatedDateUTC ASC, [ID] ASC.\n\nHTTP POST and PUT\nCreating resources\n\nThe HTTP PUT and POST methods are used for sending information to the API.\n\nA PUT method will create new data in Xero, whereas a POST will either create new data or update existing data in Xero.\nXML or JSON formatted requests are accepted.\nSupported Content-Types include: – application/x-www-form-urlencoded\napplication/xml\napplication/json\nThe Encoding type should be set as “UTF-8”\n\nYou should check the response from each API call and not assume that it will be completed successfully. This includes\n\nchecking that you get a HTTP 200 response and a status of OK. Learn more\nchecking that you have received an identifier (e.g. InvoiceID) for the new object created.\nCreating many resources\n\nIt is possible to submit more than one invoice, credit note, contact, item or other entities of the same type in a single API call. If you plan to submit more than one entity per API call, we recommend that you append the summarizeErrors=false querystring to your API call. This ensures that every entity is returned back to you, each having their own status attribute.\n\nPOST /api.xro/2.0/Invoices?summarizeErrors=false\ncopy code\n\nNote: The summarizeErrors option is only available for the Accounting API. The summarizeErrors option will not affect AU Payroll API calls.\n\nThe following xml is a sample response of a bulk upload to the Invoices endpoint.\n\nA status attribute is included for each Invoice can be OK, WARNING or ERROR.\nIf you have a validation error in any of your objects you will receive a HTTP 200 status code (rather than HTTP 400) if you are using the summarizeErrors parameter\n{\n  \"Invoices\": [{\n    \"InvoiceID\": \"5ceffde5-6786-4987-b03a-bf88d262c286\",\n    ...\n    \"StatusAttributeString\": \"OK\"\n  },{\n    \"InvoiceID\": \"5ceffde5-6786-4987-b03a-bf88d262c286\",\n    ...\n    \"StatusAttributeString\": \"OK\"\n  },{\n    \"InvoiceID\": \"5ceffde5-6786-4987-b03a-bf88d262c286\",\n    ...\n    \"StatusAttributeString\": \"WARNING\",\n    \"Warnings\": [{\n      \"Message\": \"Only AUTHORISED invoices may have SentToContact updated.\"\n      }]\n  },{\n    \"InvoiceID\": \"5ceffde5-6786-4987-b03a-bf88d262c286\",\n    ...\n    \"StatusAttributeString\": \"ERROR\",\n    \"ValidationErrors\": [{\n      \"Description\": \"Invoice not of valid type for creation\"\n      }]\n  }]\n}\n\n\ncopy code\nA WARNING status indicated that the entity was successfully processed, but there are additional warnings added to the response.\nAn ERROR status indicates that the entity could not be saved to Xero due to a validation error.\nUpdating resources\n\nWhen using POST to update an invoice or contact, you can specify the id of the object being updated in the url: e.g\n\nhttps://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/INV-000394\ncopy code\nDebugging requests and responses\n\nIf you receive a HTTP 400 response this is due to a validation error. The response will include an \"APIException\" element that contains a useful summary of the reason for the validation error. Learn more\n\nHandling validation warnings\n\nValidation warnings indicate that a request was processed successfully, but something about the request may not be correct. We strongly recommend that your integration:\n\nLog all validation warnings returned in API responses. This ensures you have visibility into potential issues.\nSurface warnings to users where appropriate, so they can review and correct data if needed.\nInvestigate the root cause of the warning. Check the warning message for details on what may be wrong.\nDo not suppress or ignore warnings. Even though the request succeeded, the underlying data may be inaccurate and could affect the organisation's financial reporting.\n\nExamples of validation warnings include:\n\nCurrencyRate validation warnings — returned when a potentially incorrect exchange rate is detected on a multicurrency request.\nOn this page\nHTTP GET\nHTTP POST and PUT\nAI toolkit\nMCP Server\nAgents Toolkit\nPrompt Library\nDocumentation\nOAuth 2\nAPI reference\nWebhooks\nCommunity\nGitHub\nProduct Feedback\nConnect to the Community\nWeekly Office Hours\nDev Day Discord Server\nFind a developer\nNews\nDevBlog\nAPI status\nChangelog\nSupport\nContact us\nFAQ\n© 2026 Xero Limited. All rights reserved. \"Xero\", \"Beautiful business\" and \"Your business supercharged\" are trademarks of Xero Limited.\nAbout Xero\nDeveloper Platform Terms and Conditions\nThe Xero Developer Platform Commercial Terms\nXero ecosystem principles\nDev Council Terms and Conditions"
```

546	## Aged Payables By Contact
547	
548	|                   |                                                                     |
549	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
550	| URL               | https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/AgedPayablesByContact      |
551	| Methods Supported | GET                                                                 |
552	| Description       | Returns aged payables up to the end of the current month by default |
553	
554	### Required parameters for GET AgedPayablesByContact
555	
556	|           |                                                      |
557	| --------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
558	| contactID | Contact ID e.g. 5040915e-8ce7-4177-8d08-fde416232f18 |
559	
560	### Optional parameters for GET AgedPayablesByContact
561	
562	|          |                                                                                      |
563	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
564	| date     | Shows payments up to this date e.g. 2014-04-30. Defaults to end of the current month |
565	| fromDate | Show all payable invoices from this date for contact                                 |
566	| toDate   | Show all payable invoices to this date for the contact                               |
567	
568	Example response for GET AgedPayablesByContact
569	
570	```
571	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/AgedPayablesByContact?ContactID=5040915e-8ce7-4177-8d08-fde416232f18
572	```

```bash
SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
grep -n "searchTerm\|IsSupplier\|IsCustomer\|ContactStatus\|page=\|pageSize\|Unique\|unique\|max length\|Max Length\|max. length\|EmailAddress\|optimised\|Optimised\|where=" "$SP/contacts-body.md" | head -60
```

```text
14:The business rules around contacts in Xero may be changing in the future and 'Contact Name' may no longer be a unique field.
16:_We recommend all developers use ContactID to uniquely reference contacts in Xero and do not rely on ContactName as a way to reference contact data uniquely in Xero._
24:| ContactID                 | Xero identifier (unique within organisations)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
27:| ContactStatus             | Current status of a contact – see contact status [types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#contacts)                                                                                                                                                                                          |
31:| EmailAddress              | Email address of contact person                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
41:| IsSupplier                | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact that has any AP invoices entered against them                                                                                                                                                                                               |
42:| IsCustomer                | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact has any AR invoices entered against them                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
76:| EmailAddress    | Email address of person                                                             |
84:| Modified After    | The ModifiedAfter filter is actually an HTTP header: ' **If-Modified-Since**'. A UTC timestamp (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss) . Only contacts created or modified since this timestamp will be returned e.g. 2009-11-12T00:00:00 **Note:** changes to the Balances, IsCustomer or IsSupplier values will not trigger a contact to be returned with the Modified-After filter |
85:| IDs               | Filter by a comma-separated list of ContactIDs. Allows you to retrieve a specific set of contacts in a single call. See [details.](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#optimised-use-of-the-where-filter)                                                                                                                                                        |
86:| Where             | Filter using the _where_ parameter. We recommend you limit filtering to the [optimised elements](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#optimised-use-of-the-where-filter) only.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
88:| page              | 100 contacts will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1   
89:| pageSize              | Used with the page parameter. Sets the # of contacts to be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used with the page parameter e.g. page=1&pageSize=250.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
92:| searchTerm        | Search parameter that performs a case-insensitive text search across the fields: Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress.                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
99:- Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k contacts will be denied with a 400 response code
109:### Optimised use of the where filter
111:The most common filters have been optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes. We recommend you restrict your filtering to the following optimised parameters. These parameters are case- and accent-insensitive and should not be appended with `ToLower()` or `ToUpper()`.
117:| Name          | equals   | where=Name="ABC limited"               |
118:| EmailAddress  | equals   | where=EmailAddress="email@example.com" |
119:| AccountNumber | equals   | where=AccountNumber="ABC-100"          |
121:### Optimised filtering on a list of values
129:### Optimised ordering:
131:The following parameters are optimised for ordering:
139:### Avoid unoptimised filtering:
140:For large organisations, unoptimised queries can result in request rejections and hitting system thresholds. The following examples of unoptimised queries are quite common, but there are more efficient alternatives available.
144:Unoptimised queries:
147:Contacts?where=Name.Contains("Peter")
148:Contacts?where=Name.StartsWith("P")
149:Contacts?where=Name.EndsWith("r")
152:Optimised query:
162:Unoptimised query:
164:EmailAddress!=null&&EmailAddress.StartsWith("boom")
167:Optimised query:
173:For both examples, using an exact match on the `Name` or `EmailAddress` field with the `=` operator will have the best performance.
183:- IsCustomer
184:- IsSupplier
203:      "ContactStatus": "ACTIVE",
208:      "EmailAddress": "a.dutchess@abclimited.com",
239:      "IsSupplier": false,
240:      "IsCustomer": true,
258:      "ContactStatus": "ACTIVE",
263:      "EmailAddress": "a.dutchess@abclimited.com",
294:      "IsSupplier": false,
295:      "IsCustomer": true,
340:| Name  | Full name of contact/organisation (max length = 255). Does not support: angle brackets, white space at the start or end, or repeating spaces.|
347:| ContactNumber               | This can be updated via the API only i.e. This field is read only on the Xero contact screen, used to identify contacts in external systems (max length = 50). If the Contact Number is used, this is displayed as Contact Code in the Contacts UI in Xero.                                    |
348:| AccountNumber               | A user defined account number. This can be updated via the API and [the Xero UI](https://help.xero.com/ContactsAccountNumber) (max length = 50)                                                                                                                                                |
349:| ContactStatus               | Current status of a contact – see contact status [types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#ContactStatuses)                                                                                                                                                                                  |
350:| FirstName                   | First name of contact person (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
351:| LastName                    | Last name of contact person (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
353:| EmailAddress                | Email address of contact person (umlauts not supported) (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
357:| TaxNumber                   | Tax number of contact – this is also known as the ABN (Australia), GST Number (New Zealand), VAT Number (UK) or Tax ID Number (US and global) in the Xero UI depending on which regionalized version of Xero you are using (max length = 50)                                                   |
362:| IsSupplier                  | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact that has any AP invoices entered against them. Cannot be set via PUT or POST – it is automatically set when an accounts payable invoice is generated against this contact.                                                                 |
363:| IsCustomer                  | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact has any AR invoices entered against them. Cannot be set via PUT or POST – it is automatically set when an accounts receivable invoice is generated against this contact.                                                                   |
407:      "EmailAddress": "ben.bowden@24locks.com",
412:          "EmailAddress": "john.smith@24locks.com",
431:  "EmailAddress": "john.smith@gmail.com",
454:  "ContactStatus": "ARCHIVED"

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/contacts-body.md`

```text
1	
2	[Try in API Explorer](https://api-explorer.xero.com/accounting/contacts)
3	
4	## Overview
5	
6	| Property          | Description                                                                                                                                                                          |
7	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
8	| URL               | https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts                                                                                                                                            |
9	| Methods Supported | [POST](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#post-contacts), [PUT](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#put-contacts), [GET](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#get-contacts) |
10	| Description       | Allows you to retrieve, add and update contacts in a Xero organisation <br />Allows you to attach files to a contact <br />Allows you to retrieve history <br />Allows you to add notes    |
11	
12	### Important Update
13	
14	The business rules around contacts in Xero may be changing in the future and 'Contact Name' may no longer be a unique field.
15	
16	_We recommend all developers use ContactID to uniquely reference contacts in Xero and do not rely on ContactName as a way to reference contact data uniquely in Xero._
17	
18	## GET Contacts
19	
20	The following elements are returned in the Contacts response
21	
22	| field                     | description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
23	| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
24	| ContactID                 | Xero identifier (unique within organisations)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
25	| ContactNumber             | This field is read only in the Xero UI, used to identify contacts in external systems. It is displayed as Contact Code in the Contacts UI in Xero.                                                                                                                                              |
26	| AccountNumber             | A user defined account number. This can be updated via the API and [the Xero UI](https://help.xero.com/ContactsAccountNumber)                                                                                                                                                                   |
27	| ContactStatus             | Current status of a contact – see contact status [types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#contacts)                                                                                                                                                                                          |
28	| Name                      | Full name of contact/organisation                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
29	| FirstName                 | First name of contact person                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
30	| LastName                  | Last name of contact person                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
31	| EmailAddress              | Email address of contact person                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
32	| SkypeUserName             | \*\*This field is no longer supported\*\*.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
33	| BankAccountDetails        | Bank account number of contact. You must have the BankAccountAdmin permission enabled to add or edit bank account details – see [user roles and permissions](https://central.xero.com/s/article/User-roles-and-permissions-in-Xero-Business-edition-GL#UserrolesandpermissionsinXeroexplained)  |
34	| CompanyNumber             | Company registration number. Max 50 char.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
35	| TaxNumber                 | Tax number of contact – this is also known as the ABN (Australia), GST Number (New Zealand), VAT Number (UK) or Tax ID Number (US and global) in the Xero UI depending on which regionalized version of Xero you are using                                                                      |
36	| TaxNumberType             | Identifier of the regional type of tax number, such as US, UK, or other regional tax identifiers                                                                                                                                                                                                |
37	| AccountsReceivableTaxType | Default tax type used for contact on AR invoices                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
38	| AccountsPayableTaxType    | Default tax type used for contact on AP invoices                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
39	| Addresses                 | Store certain address types for a contact – see [address types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#addressess)                                                                                                                                                                                 |
40	| Phones                    | Store certain phone types for a contact – see [phone types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#phones)                                                                                                                                                                                         |
41	| IsSupplier                | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact that has any AP invoices entered against them                                                                                                                                                                                               |
42	| IsCustomer                | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact has any AR invoices entered against them                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
43	| DefaultCurrency           | Default currency for raising invoices against contact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
44	| UpdatedDateUTC            | UTC timestamp of last update to contact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
45	
46	The following are only retrieved on GET requests for a single contact or when pagination is used
47	
48	| Field                          | Description                                                                                                                       |
49	| ------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
50	| ContactPersons                 | See contact persons. A contact can have a maximum of 5 ContactPersons                                                             |
51	| XeroNetworkKey                 | Store XeroNetworkKey for contacts.                                                                                                |
52	| MergedToContactID              | ID for the destination of a merged contact                                                                                        |
53	| SalesDefaultAccountCode        | The default sales [account code](/documentation/api/accounting/accounts) for contacts                                             |
54	| PurchasesDefaultAccountCode    | The default purchases [account code](/documentation/api/accounting/accounts) for contacts                                         |
55	| SalesTrackingCategories        | The default sales [tracking categories](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/trackingcategories/) for contacts |
56	| PurchasesTrackingCategories    | The default purchases [tracking categories](/documentation/api/accounting/trackingcategories/) for contacts                       |
57	| SalesDefaultLineAmountType     | The default sales line amount types for contact. Possible values INCLUSIVE, EXCLUSIVE or NONE                                     |
58	| PurchasesDefaultLineAmountType | The default purchases line amount types for contact. Possible values INCLUSIVE, EXCLUSIVE or NONE                                 |
59	| TrackingCategoryName           | The name of the Tracking Category assigned to the contact under SalesTrackingCategories and PurchasesTrackingCategories           |
60	| TrackingOptionName             | The name of the Tracking Option assigned to the contact under SalesTrackingCategories and PurchasesTrackingCategories             |
61	| PaymentTerms                   | The default payment terms for the contact – see [Payment Terms](/documentation/api/accounting/types#payments)                     |
62	| ContactGroups                  | Displays which contact groups a contact is included in                                                                            |
63	| Website                        | Website address for contact                                                                                                       |
64	| BrandingTheme                  | Default branding theme for contact – see [Branding Themes](/documentation/api/accounting/brandingthemes)                          |
65	| BatchPayments                  | batch payment details for contact                                                                                                 |
66	| Discount                       | The default discount rate for the contact                                                                                         |
67	| Balances                       | The raw AccountsReceivable(sales invoices) and AccountsPayable(bills) outstanding and overdue amounts, converted to base currency |
68	| HasAttachments                 | A boolean to indicate if a contact has an attachment                                                                              |
69	
70	Elements for ContactPerson
71	
72	| Field           | Description                                                                         |
73	| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
74	| FirstName       | First name of person                                                                |
75	| LastName        | Last name of person                                                                 |
76	| EmailAddress    | Email address of person                                                             |
77	| IncludeInEmails | boolean to indicate whether contact should be included on emails with invoices etc. |
78	
79	### Optional parameters for GET Contacts
80	
81	| Field             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
82	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
83	| **Record filter** | You can specify an individual record by appending the value to the endpoint, i.e. `GET https://.../Contacts/{identifier}`<br/><hr/>**ContactID** – The Xero identifier for a contact e.g. 297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9<br/><hr/>**ContactNumber** – A custom identifier specified from another system e.g. a CRM system has a contact number of CUST100     |
84	| Modified After    | The ModifiedAfter filter is actually an HTTP header: ' **If-Modified-Since**'. A UTC timestamp (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss) . Only contacts created or modified since this timestamp will be returned e.g. 2009-11-12T00:00:00 **Note:** changes to the Balances, IsCustomer or IsSupplier values will not trigger a contact to be returned with the Modified-After filter |
85	| IDs               | Filter by a comma-separated list of ContactIDs. Allows you to retrieve a specific set of contacts in a single call. See [details.](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#optimised-use-of-the-where-filter)                                                                                                                                                        |
86	| Where             | Filter using the _where_ parameter. We recommend you limit filtering to the [optimised elements](/documentation/api/accounting/contacts#optimised-use-of-the-where-filter) only.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
87	| order             | Order by any element returned ( _see [Order By](/documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses/#retrieving-a-smaller-lightweight-response-using-the-summaryonly-parameter)_ )                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
88	| page              | 100 contacts will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1   
89	| pageSize              | Used with the page parameter. Sets the # of contacts to be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used with the page parameter e.g. page=1&pageSize=250.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
90	| includeArchived   | e.g. includeArchived=true – Contacts with a status of ARCHIVED will be included in the response                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
91	| summaryOnly       | When set to true, this returns lightweight fields, excluding computation-heavy fields from the response, making the API calls quick and efficient. More details [here.](#retrieving-a-smaller-lightweight-response-using-the-summaryonly-parameter)                                                                                                                 |
92	| searchTerm        | Search parameter that performs a case-insensitive text search across the fields: Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress.                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
93	
94	### High volume threshold limit
95	
96	In order to make our platform more stable, we've added a high volume threshold limit for the GET Contacts Endpoint.
97	
98	- Requests that have more than 100k contacts being returned in the response will be denied
99	- Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k contacts will be denied with a 400 response code
100	
101	Please continue reading to find out how you can use paging and optimise your filtering to ensure your requests are always successful. Be sure to check out the [Efficient Data Retrieval](/documentation/api/efficient-data-retrieval) page for tips on query optimisation.
102	
103	### Paging contacts (recommended)
104	
105	By using paging all the elements in each individual contact are returned in the response which avoids the need to retrieve each individual contact to get all the details (GET Contacts without paging only returns a subset of elements)
106	
107	More information about [retrieving paged resources](/documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses#retrieving-paged-resources).
108	
109	### Optimised use of the where filter
110	
111	The most common filters have been optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes. We recommend you restrict your filtering to the following optimised parameters. These parameters are case- and accent-insensitive and should not be appended with `ToLower()` or `ToUpper()`.
112	
113	_When using individually or combined with the AND operator:_
114	
115	| Field         | Operator | Query                                  |
116	| ------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------- |
117	| Name          | equals   | where=Name="ABC limited"               |
118	| EmailAddress  | equals   | where=EmailAddress="email@example.com" |
119	| AccountNumber | equals   | where=AccountNumber="ABC-100"          |
120	
121	### Optimised filtering on a list of values
122	
123	The IDs query parameter allows you to filter based on a list of ContactIDs:
124	
125	| field | query parameter                                                                |
126	| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
127	| IDs   | ?IDs=220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734,88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0 |
128	
129	### Optimised ordering:
130	
131	The following parameters are optimised for ordering:
132	
133	- ContactID
134	- UpdatedDateUTC
135	- Name
136	
137	The default order is _UpdatedDateUTC ASC, ContactID ASC_. Secondary ordering is applied by default using the ContactID. This ensures consistency across pages.
138	
139	### Avoid unoptimised filtering:
140	For large organisations, unoptimised queries can result in request rejections and hitting system thresholds. The following examples of unoptimised queries are quite common, but there are more efficient alternatives available.
141	
142	**Example 1:** Retrieve all contacts with specific text in the contact name
143	
144	Unoptimised queries:
145	
146	```
147	Contacts?where=Name.Contains("Peter")
148	Contacts?where=Name.StartsWith("P")
149	Contacts?where=Name.EndsWith("r")
150	```
151	
152	Optimised query:
153	
154	```
155	Contacts?SearchTerm=peter
156	Contacts?SearchTerm=p
157	Contacts?SearchTerm=r
158	```
159	
160	**Example 2:** Retrieve all contacts whose email address starts with specific text
161	
162	Unoptimised query:
163	```
164	EmailAddress!=null&&EmailAddress.StartsWith("boom")
165	```
166	
167	Optimised query:
168	
169	```
170	Contacts?SearchTerm=boom
171	```
172	
173	For both examples, using an exact match on the `Name` or `EmailAddress` field with the `=` operator will have the best performance.
174	
175	### Retrieving a smaller lightweight response using the 'summaryOnly' parameter
176	
177	Use _summaryOnly=true_ in GET Contacts endpoint to retrieve a smaller version of the response object. This returns only lightweight fields, excluding computation-heavy fields from the response, making the API calls quick and efficient. The following fields will be **excluded** from the response:
178	
179	- Addresses
180	- Balances
181	- ContactGroups
182	- ContactPersons
183	- IsCustomer
184	- IsSupplier
185	- PurchasesDefaultAccountCode
186	- PurchasesTrackingCategories
187	- SalesDefaultAccountCode
188	- SalesTrackingCategories
189	
190	```
191	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/contacts?summaryOnly=True
192	```
193	
194	The _summaryOnly_ parameter works with other filters, but not when filtering on the excluded fields. And when this parameter is used, pagination is enforced by default.
195	
196	Example response for GET Contacts
197	
198	```
199	{
200	  "Contacts": [
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/contacts-body.md`

```text
330	## POST Contacts
331	
332	Use this method to create or update one or more contact records
333	
334	When you are updating a contact you don’t need to specify every element. If you exclude an element then the existing value will be preserved.
335	
336	The following is required to create a contact
337	
338	| Field | Description                                          |
339	| ----- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
340	| Name  | Full name of contact/organisation (max length = 255). Does not support: angle brackets, white space at the start or end, or repeating spaces.|
341	
342	The following are optional when creating/updating contacts
343	
344	| Field                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
345	| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
346	| ContactID                   | Xero identifier                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
347	| ContactNumber               | This can be updated via the API only i.e. This field is read only on the Xero contact screen, used to identify contacts in external systems (max length = 50). If the Contact Number is used, this is displayed as Contact Code in the Contacts UI in Xero.                                    |
348	| AccountNumber               | A user defined account number. This can be updated via the API and [the Xero UI](https://help.xero.com/ContactsAccountNumber) (max length = 50)                                                                                                                                                |
349	| ContactStatus               | Current status of a contact – see contact status [types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#ContactStatuses)                                                                                                                                                                                  |
350	| FirstName                   | First name of contact person (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
351	| LastName                    | Last name of contact person (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
352	| CompanyNumber               | Company registration number. Max 50 char.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
353	| EmailAddress                | Email address of contact person (umlauts not supported) (max length = 255)                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
354	| SkypeUserName               | \*\*This field is no longer supported\*\*.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
355	| ContactPersons              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
356	| BankAccountDetails          | Bank account number of contact. You must have the BankAccountAdmin permission enabled to add or edit bank account details – see [user roles and permissions](https://central.xero.com/s/article/User-roles-and-permissions-in-Xero-Business-edition-GL#UserrolesandpermissionsinXeroexplained) |
357	| TaxNumber                   | Tax number of contact – this is also known as the ABN (Australia), GST Number (New Zealand), VAT Number (UK) or Tax ID Number (US and global) in the Xero UI depending on which regionalized version of Xero you are using (max length = 50)                                                   |
358	| AccountsReceivableTaxType   | Default tax type used for contact on AR invoices                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
359	| AccountsPayableTaxType      | Default tax type used for contact on AP invoices                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
360	| Addresses                   | Store certain address types for a contact – see [address types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#Addresses)                                                                                                                                                                                 |
361	| Phones                      | Store certain phone types for a contact – see [phone types](/documentation/api/accounting/types#phones)                                                                                                                                                                                        |
362	| IsSupplier                  | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact that has any AP invoices entered against them. Cannot be set via PUT or POST – it is automatically set when an accounts payable invoice is generated against this contact.                                                                 |
363	| IsCustomer                  | true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact has any AR invoices entered against them. Cannot be set via PUT or POST – it is automatically set when an accounts receivable invoice is generated against this contact.                                                                   |
364	| DefaultCurrency             | Default currency for raising invoices against contact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
365	| XeroNetworkKey              | Store XeroNetworkKey for contacts.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
366	| SalesDefaultAccountCode     | The default sales [account code](/documentation/api/accounting/accounts) for contacts                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
367	| PurchasesDefaultAccountCode | The default purchases [account code](/documentation/api/accounting/accounts) for contacts                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
368	| SalesTrackingCategories     | The default sales [tracking categories](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/trackingcategories/) for contacts                                                                                                                                                              |
369	| PurchasesTrackingCategories | The default purchases [tracking categories](/documentation/api/accounting/trackingcategories/) for contacts                                                                                                                                                                                    |
370	| TrackingCategoryName        | The name of the Tracking Category assigned to the contact under SalesTrackingCategories and PurchasesTrackingCategories                                                                                                                                                                        |
371	| TrackingOptionName          | The name of the Tracking Option assigned to the contact under SalesTrackingCategories and PurchasesTrackingCategories                                                                                                                                                                          |
372	| PaymentTerms                | The default payment terms for the contact – see [Payment Terms](/documentation/api/accounting/types#PaymentTerms)                                                                                                                                                                              |
373	
374	Example of minimum elements required to add a new contact
375	
376	```
377	{
378	  "Name": "ABC Limited"
379	}
380	
381	```
382	
383	Example of minimum elements required to add many contacts
384	
385	```
386	{
387	  "Contacts": [
388	    {
389	      "Name": "ABC Limited"
390	    },{
391	      "Name": "DEF Limited"
392	    }
393	  ]
394	}
395	
396	```
397	
398	Example of creating a contact record with additional contact people
399	
400	```
401	{
402	  "Contacts": [
403	    {
404	      "Name": "24 locks",
405	      "FirstName": "Ben",
406	      "LastName": "Bowden",
407	      "EmailAddress": "ben.bowden@24locks.com",
408	      "ContactPersons": [
409	        {
410	          "FirstName": "John",
411	          "LastName": "Smith",
412	          "EmailAddress": "john.smith@24locks.com",
413	          "IncludeInEmails": "true"
414	        }
415	      ]
416	    }
417	  ]
418	}
419	
420	```
421	
422	Example of updating a contact
423	
424	```
425	{
426	  "ContactID": "eaa28f49-6028-4b6e-bb12-d8f6278073fc",
427	  "ContactNumber": "ID001",
428	  "Name": "ABC Limited",
429	  "FirstName": "John",
430	  "LastName": "Smith",
431	  "EmailAddress": "john.smith@gmail.com",
432	  "Addresses": [
433	    {
434	      "AddressType": "POBOX",
435	      "AddressLine1": "P O Box 123",
436	      "City": "Wellington",
437	      "PostalCode": "6011"
438	    }
439	  ],
440	  "BankAccountDetails": "01-0123-0123456-00",
441	  "TaxNumber": "12-345-678",
442	  "AccountsReceivableTaxType": "OUTPUT",
443	  "AccountsPayableTaxType": "INPUT",
444	  "DefaultCurrency": "NZD"
445	}
446	
447	```
448	
449	Example of archiving a contact
450	
451	```
452	{
453	  "ContactID": "9b9ba7f9-7eab-465d-8d10-d74d4f8a9ab7",
454	  "ContactStatus": "ARCHIVED"
455	}
456	
457	```
458	
459	### Uploading an Attachment
460	
461	You can upload up to 10 attachments (each up to 25mb in size) per contact, once the contact has been created in Xero. To do this you'll need to know the ID of the contact which you'll use to construct the URL when POST/PUTing a byte stream containing the attachment file. e.g. https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts/ _f0ec0d8c-6fce-4330-bb3b-8306278c6fd8_/Attachments/ _image.png_. See the [Attachments](/documentation/api/accounting/attachments) page for more details.
462	
463	Example of uploading an attachment to a contact
464	
465	```
466	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts/f0ec0d8c-4330-bb3b-83062c6fd8/Attachments/Image002932.png
467	```
468	
469	```
```
</details></tool-use>

SP="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad"
curl -sL --max-time 60 -A "Mozilla/5.0" "https://developer.xero.com/__gatsby/page-data/documentation/api/accounting/types/page-data.json" -o "$SP/types-pd.json" -w "types %{http_code} %{size_download}\n"
python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('$SP/types-pd.json'))
bodies=[]
def walk(o):
    if isinstance(o, dict):
        for k,v in o.items():
            if k=='rawMarkdownBody': bodies.append(v)
            else: walk(v)
    elif isinstance(o, list):
        for v in o: walk(v)
walk(d)
body='\n'.join(bodies)
i=body.find('Contact Status')
print(body[i-200:i+700] if i>=0 else 'not found; searching ACTIVE')
"
```
