```md
# Xero API Fact Sheet: Attachments, History & Notes, Reports, Contacts

Verified against official Xero developer docs on 2026-07-04. Pages are client-rendered; facts extracted from the raw markdown embedded in each page's Gatsby page-data JSON (same content the site renders). Every fact is tagged with its source URL. Anything not stated in the docs is marked UNVERIFIED.

Sources:
- ATT = https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/attachments
- HIST = https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/historyandnotes
- REP = https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/reports
- CON = https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/contacts
- TYPES = https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/types

---

## 1. Attachments

### Upload mechanics [ATT]
- Base URL: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments` with methods GET, PUT, POST.
- Upload with **POST or PUT** to `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}`.
  - `{Endpoint}` = parent endpoint name (e.g. Receipts, Invoices). `{Guid}` = the parent document's ID (e.g. ReceiptID or InvoiceID). `{Filename}` = the attachment filename.
  - Example: `POST /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}/Attachments/Image00394.png`.
- Body is the **raw attachment content**, "not xml or json data that is normally used to upload data to Xero". [ATT]
- Headers in the doc's example upload: `Authorization: Bearer...`, `Content Type: image/png` (i.e. Content-Type set to the file's MIME type), `Content-Length: 10495`, `Accept: text/xml`. [ATT]
- **PUT vs POST**: "The PUT method is identical to the POST method. If an attachment already exists on the specified document, then the attachment being uploaded will overwrite it." Also under POST: "You can replace any attachment already uploaded by specifying the URL of the existing attachment." [ATT]

### Size and count limits
- [ATT] verbatim: "**10 attachments can be uploaded per document (each up to 10mb in size)**."
- CONFLICT: [CON] verbatim (Uploading an Attachment section): "You can upload up to 10 attachments (**each up to 25mb in size**) per contact, once the contact has been created in Xero."
- Confirmed: 10 attachments per document. Per-file size cap: the Attachments page says 10MB, the Contacts page says 25MB — the two official pages disagree, so the exact enforced cap is UNVERIFIED (assume 10MB from the canonical Attachments page for safety; do not rely on anything above 10MB without testing).

### Filename rules [ATT]
- "Filenames including any of the characters `<`, `>`, `:`, `"`, `/`, `\`, `|`, `?`, `*`, `\0`, `+` will be rejected as a `Bad Request`."
- URL-encoding: "When the file name includes special characters, the characters should not be encoded unless they're brackets. Brackets must be encoded in order for the call to go through, and all other characters must be unencoded."

### IncludeOnline query param [ATT]
- Append `?IncludeOnline=true` to the upload (POST/PUT) request URL, e.g. `POST /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}/Attachments/Image00394.png?IncludeOnline=true`.
- Effect: "You can set an attachment to be included with the invoice when viewed online (through Xero)."
- Constraint: "This functionality is available for **accounts receivable invoices and accounts receivable credit notes**." (Not AP bills, not other entity types.)
- When set, the response Attachment object includes `"IncludeOnline": true`.
- No file-type (e.g. PDF-only) or size constraint specific to IncludeOnline is stated on this page — any further constraint is UNVERIFIED.

### Listing and fetching content [ATT]
- List: `GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/` returns an `Attachments` array (xml default or json per Accept header), each item: `AttachmentID`, `FileName`, `Url`, `MimeType`, `ContentLength`.
- Content: `GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}` (the `Url` element from the list response) returns the raw file bytes — "The response won't contain any xml or json encoded information". Response carries `Content-Type` of the file and a `Content-Disposition: attachment; ...` header.

### Entity types supporting attachments [ATT]
Verbatim list: Invoices, Receipts, Credit Notes, Repeating Invoices, Bank Transactions, Bank Transfers, Contacts, Accounts, Manual Journals, Purchase Orders, Quotes.

---

## 2. History and Notes

### Endpoint shape [HIST]
- URL: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history` — methods GET, PUT, POST.

### Supported document types (for adding notes AND retrieving history) [HIST]
Verbatim list: BankTransactions, BatchPayments, BankTransfers ("this can be accessed via the BankTransactions endpoint"), Contacts, Creditnotes, Invoices, Items, ManualJournals, Overpayments, Payments, Prepayments, Purchase Orders, Repeating Invoices, Quotes.

### GET history [HIST]
- `GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history`
- Response: `HistoryRecords` array; each record has:
  - `Changes` — type of change recorded against the document (e.g. "Approved", "Edited")
  - `DateUTC` (and `DateUTCString` in the example) — UTC date the history record was created
  - `User` — user responsible ("System Generated" when the change happens via API)
  - `Details` — description of the change event or transaction

### PUT history (add a note) [HIST]
- `PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history`
- Body wraps notes in a `HistoryRecords` array; only element is `Details`:
\```
{ "HistoryRecords": [ { "Details": "Previous payment failed" }, { "Details": "Defer the payment" } ] }
\```
- `Details`: "The note to be recorded against a single document. **Max Length 2500 characters.**"
- Multiple notes can be added in one call (array).
- "The note will be displayed in history & notes showing the **date of creation** and '**System Generated**' as the user that created it."

### What you CANNOT do [HIST]
- POST is not an update: "In this case a POST is exactly the same as a PUT. **It's not possible to update history and notes.**"
- "Notes are the only type of Change that can be manually created. All other types of Change are created automatically when certain actions occur (e.g. an invoice is paid)." — you cannot fabricate other Change types via the API.
- No date/timestamp field exists on the PUT payload and the note always shows the date of creation — i.e. **notes cannot be backdated** (direct consequence of the documented payload/behaviour; the literal word "backdate" does not appear — that phrasing is inference).
- Deleting notes: not mentioned anywhere on the page; no DELETE method is listed (only GET/PUT/POST) — deletion is unsupported per the documented method list, but an explicit "cannot delete" statement is UNVERIFIED.

---

## 3. Reports — Aged Payables By Contact

### Endpoint [REP]
- URL: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/AgedPayablesByContact` — exact report name in URL is `AgedPayablesByContact`. Method: GET only.
- Description verbatim: "Returns aged payables up to the end of the current month by default."
- General: every report endpoint is `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/[Report Name]`, returned as XML (default) or JSON. [REP]
- Permissions: "An application that has been authorised by a Standard user without the 'reports' role will not be able to access the Reports, Journals, and ManualJournals endpoints (A HTTP 403 error will be returned in this case)." [REP]

### Required parameters [REP]
| param | doc description |
|---|---|
| `contactID` | "Contact ID e.g. 5040915e-8ce7-4177-8d08-fde416232f18" — **REQUIRED** |

Note: the parameter table lists it as `contactID`; the doc's own example URL uses `?ContactID=...` — both casings appear in official docs, so casing appears case-insensitive but is UNVERIFIED; safest to match the example: `?ContactID={guid}`.

### Optional parameters [REP]
| param | doc description |
|---|---|
| `date` | "Shows payments up to this date e.g. 2014-04-30. Defaults to end of the current month" |
| `fromDate` | "Show all payable invoices from this date for contact" |
| `toDate` | "Show all payable invoices to this date for the contact" |

### Response structure (trimmed to what a table renderer needs) [REP]
\```
{
  "Reports": [
    {
      "ReportID": "AgedPayablesByContact",
      "ReportName": "Aged Payables By Contact",
      "ReportType": "AgedPayablesByContact",
      "ReportTitles": ["Invoices", "Xero", "To 28 February 2018", "Showing payments to 28 February 2018"],
      "ReportDate": "23 February 2018",
      "UpdatedDateUTC": "/Date(1519357171249)/",
      "Rows": [
        { "RowType": "Header",
          "Cells": [{"Value":"Date"},{"Value":"Reference"},{"Value":"Due Date"},{"Value":""},{"Value":"Total"},{"Value":"Paid"},{"Value":"Credited"},{"Value":"Due"}] },
        { "RowType": "Section",
          "Rows": [
            { "RowType": "Row",
              "Cells": [ { "Value": "2018-01-15T00:00:00",
                           "Attributes": [ { "Value": "935fc854-...", "Id": "invoiceID" } ] }, ... ] },
            { "RowType": "SummaryRow",
              "Cells": [ {"Value":"Total"},{"Value":""},{"Value":""},{"Value":""},{"Value":"107.80"},{"Value":"107.80"},{"Value":"0.00"},{"Value":"0.00"} ] }
          ] }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
\```
- RowType values seen: `Header` (column labels), `Section` (contains nested `Rows`), `Row` (data; each Cell = `{Value, Attributes}` where Attributes carries `{Id: "invoiceID", Value: <invoice guid>}` for drill-down), `SummaryRow` (totals).
- Header columns for this report: Date, Reference, Due Date, (blank), Total, Paid, Credited, Due.
- General layout note verbatim: "The layout of each report is a collection of rows and cells. Rows can be of various types (e.g. header, section, row and summary row elements). Cells can contain values and attributes with nested values." [REP]

---

## 4. Contacts (supplier matching)

### Key fields [CON]
- `ContactID` — "Xero identifier (unique within organisations)".
- `Name` — "Full name of contact/organisation". Required to create a contact; "(max length = 255). Does not support: angle brackets, white space at the start or end, or repeating spaces."
- `EmailAddress` — "Email address of contact person (umlauts not supported) (max length = 255)".
- `ContactStatus` — status codes [TYPES]: `ACTIVE` ("active and can be used in transactions"), `ARCHIVED` ("can no longer be used in transactions"), `GDPRREQUEST` ("subject of a GDPR erasure request and can no longer be used in transactions").
- `IsSupplier` — "true or false – Boolean that describes if a contact that has any AP invoices entered against them. **Cannot be set via PUT or POST** – it is automatically set when an accounts payable invoice is generated against this contact." (`IsCustomer` is the AR analogue.)
- `HasAttachments` — "A boolean to indicate if a contact has an attachment" (only returned on single-contact GET or when pagination is used).

### Name uniqueness [CON]
- Verbatim "Important Update": "The business rules around contacts in Xero may be changing in the future and 'Contact Name' **may no longer be a unique field**. We recommend all developers use ContactID to uniquely reference contacts in Xero and do not rely on ContactName as a way to reference contact data uniquely in Xero."
- I.e. Name is currently effectively unique per org but Xero explicitly warns not to rely on it; key on ContactID.

### where-filter examples (optimised) [CON]
- "The most common filters have been optimised... These parameters are **case- and accent-insensitive** and should not be appended with `ToLower()` or `ToUpper()`." Optimised = `equals` only:
  - `where=Name="ABC limited"`
  - `where=EmailAddress="email@example.com"`
  - `where=AccountNumber="ABC-100"`
- Avoid unoptimised filters like `Name.Contains("Peter")`, `Name.StartsWith("P")`, `Name.EndsWith("r")`, `EmailAddress!=null&&EmailAddress.StartsWith("boom")` — use `searchTerm` or exact `=` match instead. "Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k contacts will be denied with a 400 response code."

### searchTerm param [CON]
- Verbatim: "Search parameter that performs a **case-insensitive text search** across the fields: **Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress**."
- Doc examples: `Contacts?SearchTerm=peter`, `Contacts?SearchTerm=boom`.

### Pagination [CON]
- `page` — "100 contacts will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1".
- `pageSize` — "Used with the page parameter. Sets the # of contacts to be returned per call... e.g. page=1&pageSize=250."
- Paging recommended: with paging, all elements of each contact are returned; "GET Contacts without paging only returns a subset of elements".
- Optimised ordering fields: ContactID, UpdatedDateUTC, Name. "The default order is UpdatedDateUTC ASC, ContactID ASC."
- Other useful params: `IDs` (comma-separated ContactIDs, e.g. `?IDs=guid1,guid2`), `includeArchived=true`, `summaryOnly=true` (lightweight; **excludes IsCustomer and IsSupplier** among others, and "when this parameter is used, pagination is enforced by default" — do NOT use summaryOnly when supplier matching needs IsSupplier), Modified-After via `If-Modified-Since` header (note: "changes to the Balances, IsCustomer or IsSupplier values will not trigger a contact to be returned with the Modified-After filter").
- High volume threshold: "Requests that have more than 100k contacts being returned in the response will be denied."

```

WROTE undefined/docs-evidence.md (/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-evidence.md)
- WebFetch timed out 2x on all URLs; extracted official markdown from Gatsby page-data JSON of each docs page instead (verbatim source content).
- Attachments: POST/PUT raw bytes to /api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}, Content-Type = file MIME; 10 attachments/document; size cap CONFLICTS in official docs (Attachments page: 10MB, Contacts page: 25MB) — flagged, assume 10MB.
- Filename chars rejected as Bad Request: < > : " / \ | ? * \0 +; encode brackets only.
- IncludeOnline=true on upload; AR invoices and AR credit notes only.
- Supported entities: Invoices, Receipts, CreditNotes, RepeatingInvoices, BankTransactions, BankTransfers, Contacts, Accounts, ManualJournals, PurchaseOrders, Quotes.
- History: GET/PUT/POST {Endpoint}/{Guid}/history; PUT body {"HistoryRecords":[{"Details":...}]}, Details max 2500 chars; cannot update notes, always "System Generated" + creation date (no backdating); 14 supported doc types listed.
- Reports: GET /Reports/AgedPayablesByContact, contactID REQUIRED; optional date/fromDate/toDate; Rows RowType Header/Section/Row/SummaryRow, cells carry invoiceID Attributes.
- Contacts: Name max 255 (uniqueness being deprecated — use ContactID), optimised where= equals on Name/EmailAddress/AccountNumber, searchTerm searches 6 fields, page/pageSize (default 100), IsSupplier read-only auto-set by AP invoices, summaryOnly EXCLUDES IsSupplier.
- ContactStatus values ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/GDPRREQUEST confirmed from official Types page.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Convert dumped JSON page text to plain text files

```bash
ls -la raw-*.json && for f in raw-*.json; do echo "== $f: $(wc -c < $f) bytes"; done && python3 -c "
import json
for n in ['invoices','purchaseorders','payments','types']:
    d=json.load(open(f'raw-{n}.json'))
    s=d if isinstance(d,str) else str(d)
    open(f'raw-{n}.txt','w').write(s)
    print(n, len(s), 'chars')"
```

Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"Client Credentials\n\nXero’s API offers a client credentials grant-type for apps to manage their own app-data via non-tenanted endpoints. With this grant-type apps are acting on behalf of themselves and therefore don’t require user consent.\n\nNote: Accessing a Xero user’s organization / tenant data is done via tenanted endpoints and cannot be accessed directly using the client credentials grant type. If you’re just getting started, the OAuth2.0 page can help you access tenanted endpoints.\n\nWeb apps that use the code flow grant-type for tenanted endpoints can simultaneously make use of the client credentials grant-type for non-tenanted endpoints. A separate access token must be requested for accessing the non-tenanted endpoints. Both code flow and client credential tokens can both be obtained using the same client id and client secret. PKCE type apps don't have a client secret and cannot use this client credentials grant-type to access non-tenanted endpoints.\n\nThere is also a Post Collection for Client Credentials which makes it easy to try it out.\n\nCustom Connection apps use a modified version of client credentials which is not described on this page. They can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints. See Custom Connections for more details.\n\n1. Retrieve your client id and client secret\n\nThe client id will be available on the Configuration tab of the App details page, where you can generate the client secret.\n\nThe client secret is private and should not be shared.\n\n2. Requesting a non-tenanted access token\n\nYou can now exchange the client id and client secret for a non-tenanted access token. To do this you will need to make a POST request to our token endpoint:\n\nhttps://identity.xero.com/connect/token\ncopy code\n\nThe request will require an authorization header containing your app’s client_id and client_secret\n\nAuthorization: \"Basic \" + base64encode(client_id + \":\" + client_secret)\n\nThe request body will need to contain the grant type (client_credentials) and a space-separated list of required scopes. The list of scopes can be found on the Scopes page. Note: this differs from the Code and PKCE grant types where scopes are requested as parameters in the URL.\n\ngrant_type=client_credentials\nscope={scopes}\n\ncopy code\n3. Receive your tokens\n\nThe token endpoint will verify all the parameters in the request and, if everything checks out, it will generate your non-tenanted access token and return it in the response.\n\nThe response will contain the following parameters:\n\naccess_token: The token used to call the API.\nexpires_in: The amount of seconds until the non-tenanted access token expires.\ntoken_type: Bearer\n4. Call the API\n\nMake calls against the Xero non-tenanted APIs with your client credentials token by simply adding the following headers to your request:\n\nAuthorization: \"Bearer \" + access_token\nGET ../api.xro/2.0/Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\nAuthorization: \"Bearer \" + access_token\nAccept: application/json\n\n\ncopy code\nOn this page\n1. Retrieve your client id and client secret\nAI toolkit\nMCP Server\nAgents Toolkit\nPrompt Library\nDocumentation\nOAuth 2\nAPI reference\nWebhooks\nCommunity\nGitHub\nProduct Feedback\nConnect to the Community\nWeekly Office Hours\nDev Day Discord Server\nFind a developer\nNews\nDevBlog\nAPI status\nChangelog\nSupport\nContact us\nFAQ\n© 2026 Xero Limited. All rights reserved. \"Xero\", \"Beautiful business\" and \"Your business supercharged\" are trademarks of Xero Limited.\nAbout Xero\nDeveloper Platform Terms and Conditions\nThe Xero Developer Platform Commercial Terms\nXero ecosystem principles\nDev Council Terms and Conditions"
```

1	FALLBACK:Client Credentials
2	
3	Xero’s API offers a client credentials grant-type for apps to manage their own app-data via non-tenanted endpoints. With this grant-type apps are acting on behalf of themselves and therefore don’t require user consent.
4	
5	Note: Accessing a Xero user’s organization / tenant data is done via tenanted endpoints and cannot be accessed directly using the client credentials grant type. If you’re just getting started, the OAuth2.0 page can help you access tenanted endpoints.
6	
7	Web apps that use the code flow grant-type for tenanted endpoints can simultaneously make use of the client credentials grant-type for non-tenanted endpoints. A separate access token must be requested for accessing the non-tenanted endpoints. Both code flow and client credential tokens can both be obtained using the same client id and client secret. PKCE type apps don't have a client secret and cannot use this client credentials grant-type to access non-tenanted endpoints.
8	
9	There is also a Post Collection for Client Credentials which makes it easy to try it out.
10	
11	Custom Connection apps use a modified version of client credentials which is not described on this page. They can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints. See Custom Connections for more details.
12	
13	1. Retrieve your client id and client secret
14	
15	The client id will be available on the Configuration tab of the App details page, where you can generate the client secret.
16	
17	The client secret is private and should not be shared.
18	
19	2. Requesting a non-tenanted access token
20	
21	You can now exchange the client id and client secret for a non-tenanted access token. To do this you will need to make a POST request to our token endpoint:
22	
23	https://identity.xero.com/connect/token
24	copy code
25	
26	The request will require an authorization header containing your app’s client_id and client_secret
27	
28	Authorization: "Basic " + base64encode(client_id + ":" + client_secret)
29	
30	The request body will need to contain the grant type (client_credentials) and a space-separated list of required scopes. The list of scopes can be found on the Scopes page. Note: this differs from the Code and PKCE grant types where scopes are requested as parameters in the URL.
31	
32	grant_type=client_credentials
33	scope={scopes}
34	
35	copy code
36	3. Receive your tokens
37	
38	The token endpoint will verify all the parameters in the request and, if everything checks out, it will generate your non-tenanted access token and return it in the response.
39	
40	The response will contain the following parameters:
41	
42	access_token: The token used to call the API.
43	expires_in: The amount of seconds until the non-tenanted access token expires.
44	token_type: Bearer
45	4. Call the API
46	
47	Make calls against the Xero non-tenanted APIs with your client credentials token by simply adding the following headers to your request:
48	
49	Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token
50	GET ../api.xro/2.0/Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}
51	Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token
52	Accept: application/json
53	
54	
55	copy code
56	On this page
57	1. Retrieve your client id and client secret
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Verify payments text extraction and sanity check other pages

```bash
python3 -c "
import json
s=json.load(open('raw-payments3.json'))
print(len(s),'chars; fallback:', s.startswith('FALLBACK'))
open('raw-payments.txt','w').write(s)
# also sanity check others
for n in ['invoices','purchaseorders','types']:
    t=open(f'raw-{n}.txt').read()
    print(n, len(t), 'ACCPAY' in t or 'BILLED' in t or 'NoTax' in t)"
```

```text
...
FALLBACK:Xero Tenants

Xero is a multi-tenanted platform with different types of tenants. This means that through our API, you can access multiple Xero services.

Core Xero APIs (e.g. Accounting, Payroll, Files) operate within the context of an organisation tenant but other tenant types exist: a Practice Manager account or a XeroHQ practice.

A user may have access to multiple tenants and will choose which ones to connect to your app. The type of tenant they can select will be determined by the scopes your app requests when requesting authorisation.

You can only request scopes for a single tenant type at a time. For example, you cannot request an accounting scope alongside a practice manager scope. This will produce an error for the user. If you require scopes for multiple tenant types you must send the user through a separate authentication flow for each tenant type.

Some of our API's (Eg. Xero App Store) are non-tenanted as they are app specific. You must use the Client Credentials grant type to access them.

Connections and limits

Connections represent the link between an app and a tenant that was authorised by a user. They are created after the authorisation flow where the user consents to an app accessing their tenant’s data. This means that a connection is created for each unique user-tenant combination connected to your app.

Multiple users can connect your app to the same tenant, but only one of those connections will count towards your connection limit. For example, if you have 3 users connected to one organisation, and 1 user connected to another organisation, then only 2 of those 4 connections count towards the limit.

New apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections. Moving up to Core will give you up to 50 connections. To be listed on the App Store, you will need to be in the Plus tier or above. We recommend applying to be listed on the Xero App Store once you’ve onboarded at least 10 some beta customers. If you would like to discuss how the limit and certification applies to your use case please contact us.

Connections are only created when accessing tenant scoped data and won’t be created for accessing user scoped data (e.g. profile, email) or for accessing non-tenanted APIs. Connections will continue to exist and stay active indefinitely even if no API calls are being made or if the access token expires.

Connections are however deleted if:

The user disconnects your app from the Connected Apps page.
The user is removed or loses permissions in the tenant.
The connection is manually deleted via the connections endpoint.
The token is manually revoked via the revocation endpoint.
The tenant is deleted.
You delete your app in the Developer Centre.
Check the tenants you're authorised to access

Find out which tenants the user has consented to by calling the connections endpoint with the access token provided during the authorisation flow.

GET https://api.xero.com/connections
copy code

This endpoint requires an Authorization header,

Header Name	Content
```

This means that a connection is created for each unique user-tenant combination connected to your app.\n\nMultiple users can connect your app to the same tenant, but only one of those connections will count towards your connection limit. For example, if you have 3 users connected to one organisation, and 1 user connected to another organisation, then only 2 of those 4 connections count towards the limit.\n\nNew apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections. We recommend applying to be listed on the Xero App Store once you’ve onboarded at least 10 some beta customers. If you would like to discuss how the limit and certification applies to your use case please contact us.\n\nConnections are only created when accessing tenant scoped data and won’t be created for accessing user scoped data (e.g. profile, email) or for accessing non-tenanted APIs. Connections will continue to exist and stay active indefinitely even if no API calls are being made or if the access token expires.\n\nConnections are however deleted if:\n\nThe user disconnects your app from the Connected Apps page.\nThe user is removed or loses permissions in the tenant.\nThe connection is manually deleted via the connections endpoint.\nThe token is manually revoked via the revocation endpoint.\nThe tenant is deleted.\nYou delete your app in the Developer Centre.\nCheck the tenants you're authorised to access\n\nFind out which tenants the user has consented to by calling the connections endpoint with the access token provided during the authorisation flow.\n\nGET https://api.xero.com/connections\ncopy code\n\nThis endpoint requires an Authorization header,\n\nHeader Name\tContent\nAuthorization\t\"Bearer \" + access_token\n\nThis also accepts one optional query parameter,\n\nQuery Param\tDescription\nauthEventId\t(optional) Filters by the authentication_event_id from your access token. Excluding this will return all available tenants.\n\nThis is a good first call to make after completing the authorisation process to confirm you are able to connect to the Xero API on behalf of the consenting user.\n\nIf the user has authorised your app previously, they may have existing tenant connections. All of the user's connected tenants can be accessed with the most recent access token.\n\nIf you want to retrieve the connected tenants that were recently authorised in your most recent auth auth flow, you can filter by the authEventId query parameter (e.g. ?authEventId=d0ddcf81-f942-4f4d-b3c7-f98045204db4). You do this using the authentication_event_id found on the decoded access token.\n\nEach connection will have a created date and an updated date. If they differ, that means the user has reconnected this tenant to your app (having previously connected and disconnected it).\n\nThe response is a JSON array with an object for each connected tenant.\n\nObject Key\tDescription\nid\tThe ID for this connection.\nauthEventId\tThe authentication event ID that connected this tenant.\ntenantId\tThe ID for the tenant that is connected.\ntenantType\tThe type of tenant, can be one of: ORGANISATION for a Xero organisation; PRACTICEMANAGER for a Practice Manager account; PRACTICE for a Xer"}

```text
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"Idempotent requests\nIntroduction\n\nDuplicate requests can happen unintentionally when persisting changes via an API and they lead to unintended consequences such as duplicate records, unintended side effects, and inconsistent data. They occur when a request is made to change a resource, but a transient network issue occurs and a response isn't received, leading to the original request being retried and therefore processed again. Responses may not be received for a number of reasons including network issues, send failures or timeouts but in each case retrying the call risks making a duplicate request.\n\nBy marking a request as idempotent, you are making a contract with the API that if the same request is made again, the request will not be re-processed and instead the original cached response will be returned. This allows you to safely retry requests without the risk of duplicate processing.\n\nHow it Works\n\nTo guarantee idempotency, you need to include the ‘Idempotency-Key’ header on your requests. Xero will then cache the response to these requests and if subsequent requests are made with the same idempotency key, they won't be processed and instead the cached response will be returned.\n\nIdempotency-Key: KEY_VALUE\ncopy code\n\nXero will only process idempotency for requests that mutate data, I.e. POST, PUT and PATCH. The idempotency key header will be ignored for all other HTTP methods as they are idempotent by nature and thus will be processed as usual.\n\nConcurrent requests, within the concurrency limit will be processed for idempotency. Once the first call is processed, the cached response will be returned for all remaining requests.\n\nTo ensure the system exhibits expected behaviour, repeating the same key with a different request will result in an error. Idempotency keys are intended to help resolve transient issues only and so keys are stored for 6 minutes from the time of the first call, after which they expire. Repeating the same key after expiry won't produce this error and will instead be processed as a new key, this should be avoided.\n\nExample Use Case\n\nBy specifying 'Idempotency-Key' as a header and changing its value each time, the following request can be made multiple times in quick succession while creating only one account without duplicates.\n\nPUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts\ncopy code\n\nHeaders:\n\nAuthorization: \"Bearer \" + access_token\nAccept: application/json\nXero-tenant-id: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx\nIdempotency-Key: KEY_VALUE\n\ncopy code\n\nBody:\n\n{\n  \"Code\": \"201\",\n  \"Name\": \"Sales - clearance lines\",\n  \"Type\": \"SALES\"\n}\n\ncopy code\nGetting Started\n\nSelect a key generation method: Xero recommends generating four UUIDs and concatenating them. Most languages have readily available libraries or functions for generating UUIDs. Idempotency keys can also be derived from other object IDs or UUIDs associated with requests so long as they are unique.\n\nAdd the idempotency key header: When constructing new POST, PUT or PATCH requests generate and include a key as an Idempotency-Key header. The header is not case-sensitive.\n\nUpdate retry logic: Add or update retry logic where necessary to carry out retries with the same idempotency key. Transient network issues are often indicated by timeouts or errors from lower level frameworks or libraries. 4XX and 5XX status codes generally indicate an issue that needs to be resolved before retrying with a new idempotency key, but any retry can be attempted with the same idempotency key where you'd like to remove the risk of duplicate requests. The response codes for our Accounting API can be found on the Response Codes page.\n\nBest Practices\nOnly include an idempotency key when making a POST, PUT or PATCH request.\nMake use of standard libraries and built-in functions for key generation and retrying.\nConcatenate four GUIDs for the idempotency key to avoid collisions across a high volume of requests.\nStore keys appropriately to be able to identify and retry requests up until the key expires.\nHandle key expiry to ensure your calls are always idempotent; never repeat an idempotency key.\nApply necessary retry backoff strategies to reduce the load on your app.\nError Handling\n\nWhen reusing an idempotency key with a different request, Xero will return a 400 response as the idempotency key conflicts with the request. The response body will include the message:\n\nIdempotency Key: KEY_VALUE is used with a different request.\ncopy code\n\nIf your idempotency key is over 128 characters, Xero will return a 400 response.\n\nIdempotency Key: KEY_VALUE with length KEY_LENGTH is too long.\ncopy code\n\nOnly failed requests due to transient network errors can be re-run to receive a different outcome. If an idempotent request errors out internally, the error will be cached and returned when the request is re-run even if the internal error is resolved. This ensures that no part of a request is run twice, even if the request did not finish processing. If you receive an error multiple times you should instead make a GET request to inspect the resource and verify whether it has already been created. If not, you can use a new idempotency key to safely create the resource.\n\nLimitations\nRate limiting\n\nIdempotency is checked after rate limits have been applied and thus duplicate requests will count towards rate limits and reduce the limit remaining headers.\n\nKey Re-Use\n\nWhen defining a new idempotent request a unique key must be used. Key re-use is procesed per app. If a key has been re-used by the same app on a different request a 400 server error will be returned. A different request is considered where any part of the endpoint URL, request body or HTTP method has changed.\n\nKey Validation\n\nWe don't apply any validations to the supplied idempotency keys and any string will be accepted. It is the responsibility of the caller to ensure that idempotency keys are unique.\n\nFailure Mode\n\nWe've got a great track record for uptime for our API, but should we experience any "
```
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Save and validate iframe-extracted payments text

```bash
python3 -c "
import json
d=json.loads(json.load(open('raw-payments5.json')))
print('host:', d['hostUrl']); print('iframe:', d['iframeUrl']); print('len:', d['len'])
open('raw-payments.txt','w').write(d['text'])
t=d['text']
import re
for kw in ['AUTHORISED','bank account','Amount','CurrencyRate','DELETED','Code']:
    print(kw, t.count(kw))"
```

```text
host: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/idempotent-requests/idempotency/
iframe: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/payments
len: 17640
AUTHORISED 8
bank account 1
Amount 26
CurrencyRate 10
DELETED 1
Code 15
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/raw-invoices.txt`

```text
1	Invoices
2	
3	Try in API Explorer
4	
5	Overview
6	Property	Description
7	URL	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices
8	Methods Supported	GET, PUT, POST
9	Description	Retrieve sales invoices or purchase bills
10	Create sales invoices or purchase bills
11	Update draft or submitted sales invoices or purchase bills (paid sales invoices and paid purchase bills can also be updated, with restrictions)
12	Delete draft sales invoices or purchase bills
13	Void approved sales invoices or purchase bills
14	Retrieve the online invoice URL for sales invoices
15	Attach files to sales invoices or purchase bills
16	Email sales invoices
17	Retrieve history for invoices and purchase bills
18	Add notes to invoices and purchase bills
19	GET Invoices
20	
21	Use this method to retrieve one or many invoices.
22	
23	By default responses are formatted as XML. You can also retrieve responses in JSON format.
24	When you retrieve multiple invoices, only a summary of the contact is returned and no line details are returned – this is to keep the response more compact.
25	The line item details will be returned when you retrieve an individual invoice, either by specifying Invoice ID, Invoice Number, querying by Statuses or by using the optional paging parameter (below).
26	When you retrieve invoices by querying by Statuses, pagination is enforced by default.
27	Individual invoices (e.g. Invoices/97c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9) can also be returned as PDF's see our HTTP GET documentation
28	
29	Amounts are in the invoice's currency.
30	
31	The following elements are returned in the Invoices response.
32	
33	Field	Description
34	Type	See Invoice Types
35	Contact	See Contacts
36	Date	Date invoice was issued – YYYY-MM-DD
37	DueDate	Date invoice is due – YYYY-MM-DD
38	Status	See Invoice Status Codes
39	LineAmountTypes	See Line Amount Types
40	LineItems	See LineItems. The LineItems collection can contain any number of individual LineItem sub-elements.
41	SubTotal	Total of invoice excluding taxes
42	TotalTax	Total tax on invoice
43	Total	Total of Invoice tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)
44	TotalDiscount	Total of discounts applied on the invoice line items
45	UpdatedDateUTC	Last modified date UTC format
46	CurrencyCode	The currency that invoice has been raised in (see Currencies)
47	CurrencyRate	For base currency invoices, has a value of 1. For non-base-currency invoices, see CurrencyRate meaning.
48	InvoiceID	Xero generated identifier for invoice (unique within organisations)
49	InvoiceNumber	ACCREC – Unique alpha numeric code identifying invoice (printable ASCII characters only)
50	ACCPAY – non-unique alpha numeric code identifying invoice (printable ASCII characters only). This value will also display as Reference in the UI.
51	Reference	Additional reference number
52	BrandingThemeID	See BrandingThemes
53	Url	URL link to a source document – shown as "Go to [appName]" in the Xero app
54	SentToContact	Boolean to indicate whether the invoice in the Xero app displays as "sent"
55	ExpectedPaymentDate	Shown on sales invoices (Accounts Receivable) when this has been set
56	PlannedPaymentDate	Shown on bills (Accounts Payable) when this has been set
57	HasAttachments	boolean to indicate if an invoice has an attachment
58	RepeatingInvoiceID	Xero generated identifier for repeating invoice template (unique within organisations). Present only if the invoice is created as part of a Repeating Invoice.
59	Payments	See Payments
60	CreditNotes	Details of credit notes that have been applied to an invoice
61	Prepayments	See Prepayments
62	Overpayments	See Overpayments
63	AmountDue	Amount remaining to be paid on invoice
64	AmountPaid	Sum of payments received for invoice
65	CISDeduction	CISDeduction withheld by the contractor to be paid to HMRC on behalf of subcontractor (Available for organisations under UK Construction Industry Scheme)
66	FullyPaidOnDate	The date the invoice was fully paid. Only returned on fully paid invoices
67	AmountCredited	Sum of all credit notes, over-payments and pre-payments applied to invoice
68	SalesTaxCalculationTypeCode	See Tax calculation type. Only applicable for organisations in the US - Invoices with an auto sales tax calculation type are read only.
69	InvoiceAddresses	Only applicable for organisations in the US - Invoices with an auto sales tax calculation type are read only.
70	
71	Elements for LineItems
72	
73	Field	Descripton
74	Description	The description of the line item
75	Quantity	LineItem Quantity
76	UnitAmount	Lineitem unit amount. By default, unit amount will be rounded to two decimal places. You can opt in to use four decimal places by adding the querystring parameter unitdp=4 to your query. See the Rounding in Xero guide for more information.
77	ItemCode	See Items
78	AccountCode	See Accounts
79	AccountId	See Accounts
80	Item	Details of the item. See Items
81	LineItemID	The Xero generated identifier for a LineItem
82	TaxType	Used as an override if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode is not correct – see TaxTypes.
83	TaxAmount	The tax amount is auto calculated as a percentage of the line amount based on the tax rate
84	LineAmount	The line amount reflects the discounted price if a DiscountRate has been used i.e LineAmount = Quantity * Unit Amount * ((100 – DiscountRate)/100)
85	DiscountRate	Percentage discount being applied to a line item. Only supported on ACCREC invoices and quotes. ACCPAY invoices and credit notes in Xero do not support discounts
86	DiscountAmount	Discount amount being applied to a line item. Only supported on ACCREC invoices and quotes. ACCPAY invoices and credit notes in Xero do not support discounts
87	Tracking	Section for optional Tracking Category – see TrackingCategory. Any LineItem can have a maximum of 2 TrackingCategory elements.
88	SalesTaxCodeID	Only applicable for organisations in the US - Invoices with an auto sales tax calculation type are read only.
89	Taxability	Only applicable for organisations in the US - Invoices with an auto sales tax calculation type are read only.
90	TaxBreakdown	Only applicable for organisations in the US - Invoices with an auto sales tax calculation type are read only.
91	
92	Elements for InvoiceAddresses
93	
94	Field	Description
95	InvoiceAddressType	Indicates whether the address is defined as origin (FROM) or destination (TO)
96	AddressLine1	First line of a physical address
97	AddressLine2	Second line of a physical address
98	AddressLine3	Third line of a physical address
99	AddressLine4	Fourth line of a physical address
100	City	City of a physical address
101	Region	Region or state of a physical address
102	PostalCode	Postal code of a physical address
103	Country	Country of a physical address
104	
105	Elements for Item
106	
107	Field	Description
108	ItemID	Xero generated identifier for an item
109	Name	User defined item code
110	Code	The name of the item
111	
112	Elements for TrackingCategory
113	
114	Field	Description
115	Name	Name of the tracking category
116	TrackingCategoryID	Xero assigned unique ID for the category
117	Option	Name of the option (required)
118	
119	Elements for TaxBreakdown
120	
121	Field	Description
122	TaxComponentId	The unique ID number of the component
123	Type	The type of the jurisdiction
124	Name	The name of the jurisdiction
125	TaxPercentage	The percentage of the tax
126	TaxAmount	The amount of the tax
127	TaxableAmount	The amount that is taxable
128	NonTaxableAmount	The amount that is not taxable
129	ExemptAmount	The amount that is exempt
130	StateAssignedNo	The state assigned number of the jurisdiction
131	JurisdictionRegion	Name identifying the region within the country
132

267	      "SubTotal": "28.50",
268	      "TotalTax": "3.56",
269	      "Total": "32.06",
270	      "UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1518685950940+0000)\/",
271	      "CurrencyCode": "NZD",
272	      "InvoiceID": "03778e72-d541-404a-ab9b-2757aeda76a3",
273	      "InvoiceNumber": "OIT00542",
274	      "AmountDue": "32.06",
275	      "AmountPaid": "0.00",
276	      "AmountCredited": "0.00"
277	    }
278	  ]
279	}
280	
281	
282	copy code
283	Optional parameters for GET Invoices
284	Field	Description
285	Record filter	You can specify an individual record by appending the value to the endpoint, i.e. GET https://.../Invoices/{identifier}
286	InvoiceID - The Xero identifier for an Invoice
287	e.g. 297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9
288	InvoiceNumber - The InvoiceNumber
289	e.g. INV-01514
290	Modified After	The ModifiedAfter filter is actually an HTTP header: ' If-Modified-Since'.
291	A UTC timestamp (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss) . Only invoices created or modified since this timestamp will be returned e.g. 2009-11-12T00:00:00
292	IDs, InvoiceNumbers, ContactIDs, Statuses	Filter by a comma-separated list of InvoicesIDs, InvoiceNumbers, ContactIDs or Statuses. See details.
293	Where	Filter using the where parameter. We recommend you limit filtering to the optimised elements only.
294	createdByMyApp	When set to true you'll only retrieve Invoices created by your app
295	order	Order by any element returned ( see Order By )
296	page	100 invoices will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1
297	pageSize	Used with the page parameter. Sets the # of invoices to be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used with the page parameter e.g. page=1&pageSize=250.
298	summaryOnly	When set to true, this returns lightweight fields, excluding computation-heavy fields from the response, making the API calls quick and efficient.
299	SearchTerm	Search parameter that performs a case-insensitive text search across the fields: InvoiceNumber, Reference.
300	Example: GET https://.../Invoices?SearchTerm="REF12"
301	High volume threshold limit
302	
303	In order to make our platform more stable, we've added a high volume threshold limit for the GET Invoices Endpoint.
304	
305	Requests that have more than 100k invoices being returned in the response will be denied and receive a 400 response code
306	Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k invoices will be denied with a 400 response code
307	
308	Please continue reading to find out how you can use paging and optimise your filtering to ensure your requests are always successful. Be sure to check out the Efficient Data Retrieval page for tips on query optimisation.
309	
310	Paging invoices (recommended)
311	
312	By using paging all the line item details for each invoice are returned which may avoid the need to retrieve each individual invoice.
313	
314	More information about retrieving paged resources.
315	
316	Optimised use of the where filter
317	
318	The most common filters have been optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes. We recommend you restrict your filtering to the following optimised parameters.
319	
320	Range Operators in Where clauses
321	
322	Indicated fields also support the range operators: greater than, greater than or equals, less than, less than or equals (>, >=, <, <=).
323	
324	Range operators can be combined with the AND operator to query a date or numerical range. eg where=Date>=DateTime(2020, 01, 01) AND Date<DateTime(2020, 02, 01), where=AmountDue>=5000 AND AmountDue<6000
325	
326	When using individually or combined with the AND operator:
327	
328	Field	Operator	Query
329	Status	equals	where=Status="AUTHORISED"
330	Contact.ContactID	equals	where=Contact.ContactID=guid("96988e67-ecf9-466d-bfbf-0afa1725a649")
331	Contact.Name	equals	where=Contact.Name="ABC limited"
332	Contact.ContactNumber	equals	where=Contact.ContactNumber="ID001"
333	Reference	equals	where=Reference="REF12"
334	InvoiceNumber	equals	where=InvoiceNumber="INV-001"
335	InvoiceId	equals	where=InvoiceId=guid("220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734")
336	Date	equals, range	where=Date=DateTime(2020, 01, 01)
337	where=Date>DateTime(2020, 01, 01)
338	Type	equals	where=Type="ACCREC"
339	AmountDue	equals, range	where=AmountDue=1000
340	where=AmountDue<=5
341	AmountPaid	equals	where=AmountPaid=1000
342	DueDate	equals, range	where=DueDate=DateTime(2023,11,25)
343	where=Date<DateTime(2024, 01, 01)
344	
345	Example: Retrieve all ACCPAY Invoices (bills) with an AUTHORISED status
346	
347	?where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"
348	copy code
349	
350	This would translate to the following URL once encoded.
351	
352	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?where=Type%3d%3d%22ACCPAY%22+AND+Status%3d%3d%22AUTHORISED%22
353	copy code
354	
355	When using with the OR operator:
356	
357	Field	Operator	Query
358	InvoiceId	equals	where=InvoiceId=guid("220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734") or InvoiceId=guid("88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0")
359	
360	Although where=InvoiceId=guid("220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734") or InvoiceId=guid("88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0") and ?IDs=220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734,88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0 are functionally equivalent, using ?IDs= can reduce the size of the request. Additionally, the optimisation of the or operator is restricted to InvoiceId field only. Using or with other fields is not optimised and may lead to exceeding the threshold limit.
361	
362	For optimal performance when querying multiple values for a field, please refer to the next section.
363	
364	Optimised filtering on a list of values
365	
366	The following query parameters allow you to filter on a comma separated list of values:
367	
368	Field	Query Parameter
369	Statuses	?Statuses=AUTHORISED,DRAFT
370	IDs	?IDs=220ddca8-3144-4085-9a88-2d72c5133734,88192a99-cbc5-4a66-bf1a-2f9fea2d36d0
371	InvoiceNumbers	?InvoiceNumbers=INV-001,INV-002,INV-003
372	ContactIDs	?ContactIDs=3138017f-8ddc-420e-a159-e7e1cf9e643d,4b2df4a1-7aa5-4ce3-9e9c-3c55794c5283
373	
374	Example: Retrieve all AUTHORISED and PAID Invoices belonging to two specific contacts
375	
376	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?Statuses=AUTHORISED,PAID&ContactIDs=3138017f-8ddc-420e-a159-e7e1cf9e643d,4b2df4a1-7aa5-4ce3-9e9c-3c55794c5283
377	copy code
378	Optimised ordering:
379	
380	The following parameters are optimised for ordering:
381	
382	InvoiceId
383	UpdatedDateUTC
384	Date
385	
386	The default order is UpdatedDateUTC ASC, InvoiceId ASC. Secondary ordering is applied by default using the InvoiceId. This ensures consistency across pages.
387	
388	Retrieving a smaller lightweight response using the summaryOnly parameter
389	
390	Use summaryOnly=true in GET Invoices endpoint to retrieve a smaller version of the response object. This returns only lightweight fields, excluding computation-heavy fields from the response, making the API calls quick and efficient. The following fields will be excluded from the response:
391	
392	Payments
393	HasAttachments
394	LineItems
395	CISDeduction
396	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?summaryOnly=True
397	copy code
398	
399	The summaryOnly parameter works with other filters, but not when filtering on the excluded fields. And when this parameter is used, pagination is enforced by default.
400	
401	Examples response when retrieving a collection of invoices using paging
402	
403	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1
404	
405	
406	copy code
407	{
408	  "Invoices": [
409	      {
410	        "Contact": {
411	          "ContactID": "9b9ba9e5-e907-4b4e-8210-54d82b0aa479",
412	          "Name": "PowerDirect"
413	        },

499	Retrieving the online invoice url
500	
501	To integrate your application with Xero's online invoicing, you can retrieve the online invoice url for sales (ACCREC) invoices. Note: you cannot retrieve an online invoice url for DRAFT invoices.
502	
503	Example for retrieving an online invoice url
504	
505	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/9b9ba9e5-e907-4b4e-8210-54d82b0aa479/OnlineInvoice
506	
507	
508	copy code

510	  "OnlineInvoices": [

512	      "OnlineInvoiceUrl": "https://in.xero.com/iztKMjyAEJT7MVnmruxgCdIJUDStfRgmtdQSIW13"
513	    }
514	  ]
515	}
516

"HTTP Response Codes & Errors\nCodes summary\n\nA summary of HTTP Response Codes returned by the Xero API is shown below.\n\nHTTP Code\tSummary\tDescription\n200\tOK\tSuccessful API call.\n400\tBad Request\tA validation exception has occurred.\n401\tUnauthorized\tInvalid authorization credentials.\n403\tNot Permitted\tUser doesn't have permission to access the resource.\n404\tNot Found\tThe resource you have specified cannot be found\n412\tPrecondition Failed\tOne or more conditions given in the request header fields were invalid. This code will also be returned if you're using TLS1.0\n429\tRate Limit Exceeded\tThe API rate limit for your organisation/application pairing has been exceeded. Learn more\n500\tInternal Error\tAn unhandled error with the Xero API. Contact the Xero API team if problems persist\n501\tNot Implemented\tThe method you have called has not been implemented (e.g. POST Organisation\n503\tNot Available\tAPI is currently unavailable – typically due to a scheduled outage – try again soon.\n503\tOrganisation offline\tThe organisation temporarily cannot be connected to.\nCommon response codes\nHTTP 200 OK\nThe Xero API will return with a HTTP 200 for successful requests\nIf you are utlilising the summarizeErrors=false querystring parameter you’ll always receive a HTTP 200 response even though some of the elements may have failed. Learn more\nHTTP 400 Bad Request\n\nHTTP 400 responses include an “ApiException\" element in the response that contains a useful summary of the reason for the error.\n\n{\n  \"ErrorNumber\": 10,\n  \"Type\": \"ValidationException\",\n  \"Message\": \"A validation exception occurred\",\n  \"Elements\": [{\n    \"ValidationErrors\": [{\n      \"Message\": \"Email address must be valid\"\n    }]\n  }]\n}\n\n\ncopy code\nHTTP 401 Unauthorized\n\nA customer may disconnect your application from within Xero at anytime so at the very least you should implement functionality to handle a 401 error and allow a customer to easily reauthorize your application.\n\nLearn more about limits\n\nHTTP 503 Not Available\n\nFrom time to time we might require a short outage to carry out maintenance or upgrades. In most cases the Xero API will respond with a HTTP 503 when it is not available, and provide one of the following response bodies:\n\nThe Xero API is currently offline for maintenance\ncopy code\nThe Xero API is temporarily unavailable\ncopy code\nHTTP 503 Organisation Offline\n\nA specific organisation may not be available though the Xero APIs themselves are online. In this instance, the below response will be returned. Typically this situation may occur for several minutes. A retry interval of 5mins or so is recommended.\n\nThe Organisation is offline\ncopy code\nOn this page\nCodes summary\nCommon response codes\nAI toolkit\nMCP Server\nAgents Toolkit\nPrompt Library\nDocumentation\nOAuth 2\nAPI reference\nWebhooks\nCommunity\nGitHub\nProduct Feedback\nConnect to the Community\nWeekly Office Hours\nDev Day Discord Server\nFind a developer\nNews\nDevBlog\nAPI status\nChangelog\nSupport\nContact us\nFAQ\n© 2026 Xero Limited. All rights reserved. \"Xero\", \"Beautiful business\" and \"Your business supercharged\" are trademarks of Xero Limited.\nAbout Xero\nDeveloper Platform Terms and Conditions\nThe Xero Developer Platform Commercial Terms\nXero ecosystem principles\nDev Council Terms and Conditions"

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/raw-invoices.txt`

```text
519	POST Invoices
520	
521	Use this method to create or update an invoice.
522	
523	Amounts are in the invoice's currency.
524	
525	Note: Be sure to check Organisation Actions to verify you can create invoices for the user.
526	
527	The following are required to create a draft invoice
528	
529	Field	Description
530	Type	See Invoice Types
531	Contact	See Elements for Contact. Only ContactID should be provided when creating or updating an invoice.
532	LineItems	See LineItems. The LineItems collection can contain any number of individual LineItem sub-elements. At least one is required to create a complete Invoice.
533	
534	The following are optional when creating or updating invoices
535	
536	Field	Description
537	Date	Date invoice was issued – YYYY-MM-DD. If the Date element is not specified it will default to the current date based on the timezone setting of the organisation
538	DueDate	Date invoice is due – YYYY-MM-DD
539	LineAmountTypes	Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element. See Line Amount Types
540	InvoiceNumber	ACCREC – Unique alpha numeric code identifying invoice ( when missing will auto-generate from your Organisation Invoice Settings) (max length = 255)
541	ACCPAY – non-unique alpha numeric code identifying invoice. This value will also display as Reference in the UI. (max length = 255)
542	Reference	Additional reference number (max length = 255)
543	BrandingThemeID	See BrandingThemes
544	Url	URL link to a source document – shown as "Go to [appName]" in the Xero app
545	CurrencyCode	The currency that invoice has been raised in (see Currencies). Defaults to the organisation's base currency.
546	CurrencyRate	For how and when to set, see our multicurrency guide. A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect.
547	Status	See Invoice Status Codes
548	SentToContact	Boolean to set whether the invoice in the Xero app should be marked as "sent". This can be set only on invoices that have been approved
549	ExpectedPaymentDate	Shown on sales invoices (Accounts Receivable) when this has been set
550	PlannedPaymentDate	Shown on bills (Accounts Payable) when this has been set
551	
552	Elements for LineItems
553	
554	Field	Description
555	Description	Description needs to be at least 1 char long. A line item with just a description (i.e no unit amount or quantity) can be created by specifying just a Description element that contains at least 1 character (max length = 4000)
556	Quantity	LineItem Quantity (max length = 13)
557	UnitAmount	Lineitem unit amount. By default, unit amount will be rounded to two decimal places. You can opt in to use four decimal places by adding the querystring parameter unitdp=4 to your query. See the Rounding in Xero guide for more information.
558	ItemCode	See Items
559	AccountCode	See Accounts
560	LineItemID	The Xero generated identifier for a LineItem. It is recommended that you include LineItemIDs on update requests. If LineItemIDs are not included with line items in an update request then the line items are deleted and recreated.
561	TaxType	Used as an override if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode is not correct – see TaxTypes.
562	TaxAmount	The tax amount is auto calculated as a percentage of the line amount (see below) based on the tax rate. This value can be overriden if the calculated TaxAmount is not correct.
563	LineAmount	The line amount reflects the discounted price if a DiscountRate has been used i.e LineAmount = Quantity * Unit Amount * ((100 – DiscountRate)/100) (can't exceed 9,999,999,999.99 )
564	DiscountRate or DiscountAmount	Percentage discount or discount amount being applied to a line item. Only supported on ACCREC invoices and quotes. ACCPAY invoices and credit notes in Xero do not support discounts
565	Tracking	Section for optional Tracking Category – see TrackingCategory. Any LineItem can have a maximum of 2 TrackingCategory elements.
566	
567	Elements for Contact
568	
569	Field	Description
570	ContactID	Xero identifier (unique within organisations)
571	
572	Elements for TrackingCategory
573	
574	Field	Description
575	Name	Name of the tracking category (required)
576	Option	Name of the option (required)
577	Creating, updating and deleting line items when updating invoices
578	
579	In an update (POST) request:
580	
581	Providing an existing LineItem with its LineItemID will update that line item.
582	Providing a LineItem with no LineItemID will create a new line item.
583	Not providing an existing LineItem with it's LineItemID will result in that line item being deleted.
584	Addresses on Invoices
585	
586	By default, the address applied to an invoice will use the postal (POBOX) address of the Contact. However, This can be changed in Xero by applying a custom invoice template. It is not possible to manually override the address on a per-invoice basis.
587	
588	Invoice Status Codes
589	
590	When creating new invoices or modifying existing invoices you can optionally specify a "Status" element. New invoices can have either one of the following three status codes :
591	
592	Field	Description
593	Status	Description
594	DRAFT	- The default status if this element is not provided with your API call
595	- Can contain incomplete line items (e.g. missing account codes)
596	- No journals are created so the details will not be used in any reports
597	SUBMITTED	- Useful if there is an approval process required
598	- No journals are created so the details will not be used in any reports
599	AUTHORISED	- The "approved" state of an invoice ready for sending to a customer
600	- Journals are created in Xero so all details will be used in reports
601	- You can now apply payments to the invoice
602	- Once an invoice is fully paid invoice the status will change to PAID
603	Invoice status changes
604	
605	The following state changes are valid when updating invoices.
606	
607	Existing status	New status
608	DRAFT	DRAFT
609	DRAFT	SUBMITTED
610	DRAFT	AUTHORISED
611	DRAFT	DELETED
612	SUBMITTED	SUBMITTED
613	SUBMITTED	AUTHORISED
614	SUBMITTED	DRAFT
615	SUBMITTED	DELETED
616	AUTHORISED	AUTHORISED
617	AUTHORISED	VOIDED
618	Updating Invoices
619	
620	You can only update an invoice depending on the type of invoice, and its payment status.
621	
622		ACCREC	ACCPAY
623	Unpaid	Yes	Yes
624	Paid (in part, or in full)	Yes – with exceptions below	Yes – with exceptions below
625	In a locked period	No	No
626	
627	If a sales invoice (ACCREC) is paid in part or in full, you can only update the following fields:
628	
629	Reference
630	DueDate
631	InvoiceNumber
632	BrandingThemeID
633	Contact, unless the payment is made with a Credit Note
634	Url
635	LineItems
636	Description
637	AccountCode, except for Construction Industry Scheme (CIS) account codes
638	Tracking
639	
640	If a purchase bill (ACCPAY) is paid in part or in full, you can only update the following fields:
641	
642	Reference
643	DueDate
644	InvoiceNumber
645	BrandingThemeID
646	Contact, unless the payment is made with a Credit Note or line items contain a CisLabourExpense account
647	Url
648	LineItems
649	Description
650	AccountCode, except for Construction Industry Scheme (CIS) account codes
651	Tracking
652	PlannedPaymentDate
653	Creating a PAID Invoice
654	
655	Creating a PAID invoice is a two step process:
656	
657	First an AUTHORISED invoice needs to be made using the Invoices endpoint.
658	Second a payment for the outstanding amount needs to be applied using the payments endpoint.
659	
660	Once the invoice has been fully paid the system will move it to the PAID status.
661	
662	Example of a draft sales ( ACCREC) invoice with enough detail to be approved once it’s been created.
663	
664	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices
665	
666	
667	copy code
668	{
669	  "Type": "ACCREC",
670	  "Contact": {
671	    "ContactID": "eaa28f49-6028-4b6e-bb12-d8f6278073fc"
672	  },
673	  "Date": "\/Date(1518685950940+0000)\/",
674	  "DateString": "2009-05-27T00:00:00",
675	  "DueDate": "\/Date(1518685950940+0000)\/",
676	  "DueDateString": "2009-06-06T00:00:00",
677	  "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
678	  "LineItems": [
679	    {
680	      "Description": "Consulting services as agreed (20% off standard rate)",
681	      "Quantity": "10",
682	      "UnitAmount": "100.00",
683	      "AccountCode": "200",
684	      "DiscountRate": "20"
685	    }
686	  ]
687	}
688	
689	
690	copy code
691	
692	Example of minimum elements required to add an approved sales (ACCREC) invoice to ABC Limited
693	
694	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices
695	copy code
696	{
697	  "Type": "ACCREC",
698	  "Contact": {
699	    "ContactID": "eaa28f49-6028-4b6e-bb12-d8f6278073fc"
700	  },
701	  "DueDate": "\/Date(1518685950940+0000)\/",
702	  "LineItems": [
703	    {
704	      "Description": "Services as agreed",
705	      "Quantity": "4",
706	      "UnitAmount": "100.00",
707	      "AccountCode": "200"
708	    }
709	  ],
710	  "Status": "AUTHORISED"
711	}
712	
713	
714	copy code
715

863	Deleting and Voiding Invoices
864	
865	You can delete a DRAFT or SUBMITTED invoice by updating the Status to DELETED.
866	
867	If an invoice has been AUTHORISED it cannot be deleted but you can set it's status to VOIDED
868	
869	If you send invoices from your app you can mark them as sent in Xero by setting SentToContact as true
870	
871	Example of deleting a draft invoice
872	
873	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/INV-239
874	copy code
875	{
876	    "InvoiceNumber": "INV-239",
877	    "Status": "DELETED"
878	}
879	
880	
881	copy code
882	
883	Example of voiding an approved invoice that has no payments applied to it
884	
885	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/INV-123
886	copy code
887	{
888	    "InvoiceNumber": "INV-123",
889	    "Status": "VOIDED"
890	}
891	
892	
893	copy code
894	
895	Example of updating an invoice to mark it as sent
896	
897	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/8694c9c5-7097-4449-a708-b8c1982921a4
898	copy code
899	{
900	    "InvoiceID": "8694c9c5-7097-4449-a708-b8c1982921a4",
901	    "SentToContact": "true"
902	}
903	
904	
905	copy code
906	SummarizeErrors
907	
908	If you are entering many invoices in a single API call then we recommend you utilise our response format that shows validation errors for each invoice. Each Invoice will be returned with a status element that contains the value OK or ERROR. If an invoice has a error then one or more validation errors will be returned.
909	
910	Example of the altered response format using the SummarizeErrors=false parameter
911	
912	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?SummarizeErrors=false
913	
914	
915	copy code
916	{
917	  "Invoices": [
918	    ...
919	    {
920	      "ValidationErrors": [
921	        {
922	          "Message": "...."
923	        }
924	      ]
925	    }
926	    ...
927	  ]
928	}
929	
930	
931	copy code
932	Uploading an Attachment
933	
934	You can upload up to 10 attachments (each up to 25mb in size) per invoice, once the invoice has been created in Xero. To do this you'll need to know the ID of the invoice which you'll use to construct the URL when POST/PUTing a byte stream containing the attachment file. e.g. https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/ f0ec0d8c-6fce-4330-bb3b-8306278c6fd8/Attachments/ image.png. See the Attachments page for more details.
935	
936	Example of uploading an attachment
937	
938	POST /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/f0ec0d8c-4330-bb3b-83062c6fd8/Attachments/Image002932.png
939	copy code
940	    Headers:
941	    Authorization: Bearer...
942	    Content Type: image/png
943	    Content-Length: 10293
944	    Body:
945	    {RAW-IMAGE-CONTENT}
946	
947	
948	copy code
949	Emailing an invoice
950	
951	You can use the API to trigger the email of a sales invoice out of Xero. The invoice must be of Type ACCREC and a valid Status for sending (SUBMITTED,AUTHORISED or PAID).
952	
953	The email will be sent to the primary email address of the contact on the invoice and any additional contact persons that have IncludeInEmails flag set to true. The sender will be the user who authorised the app connection.
954	
955	The subject and body of the email will generated from the organisation’s default template. You can read more about email templates here.
956	
957	There is a daily limit per Xero organisation for sending invoices. This includes emails sent via the app and the API:
958	
959	1,000 per day for paying organisations
960	20 per day for trial organisations
961	0 per day for demo organisations
962	
963	We recommend that you monitor the number of emails sent per organisation to avoid hitting these limits. Xero monitors the emails it sends to detect potential spam and protect our email reputation. It's important to us that our customers’ email gets through. If your application receives a 400 rate limit error prior to exceeding the email limits above this is a result of the organisation being restricted due to suspicious activity. We suggest you either try again later or direct the customer to make contact with Xero Support.
964	
965	Please note: The response code returned for a successful request is a 204 (no content). Bad requests, including rate limit hits, will be returned as a 400 (e.g. if the invoice is an invalid status); this includes trying to send invoice emails from organisations who are not allowed to send emails, such as those on Xero’s Partner Edition Plans.
966	
967	An example request to trigger an email
968	
969	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/aa682059-c8ec-44b9-bc7f-344c94e1ffae/Email
970	copy code
971	Request Body: <Empty>
972	copy code
973	PUT Invoices
974	
975	The PUT method is similar to the POST Invoices method, however you can only create new invoices with this method.
976	
977	Webhooks
978	
979	You can create a subscription to get invoice events. Create and update events are available (incuding when invoices are archived). See the Webhooks page for more details.
980	
981	Retrieving History
982	
983	View a summary of the actions made by all users to the invoice. See the History and Notes page for more details.
984	
985	Example of retrieving a invoice's history
986	
987	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{Guid}/History
988	copy code
989	{
990	  "HistoryRecords": [
991	     {
992	      "Changes": "Updated",
993	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
994	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
995	      "User": "System Generated",
996	      "Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"
997	    },
998	    {
999	      "Changes": "Created",
1000	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
1001	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
1002	      "User": "Mac Haag",
1003	      "Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."
1004	    }
1005	    ...
1006	  ]
1007	}
1008	
1009	
1010	copy code

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/raw-purchaseorders.txt`

```text
1	Purchase Orders
2	
3	Try in API Explorer
4	
5	Overview
6		
7	URL	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders
8	Methods Supported	GET, PUT, POST
9	Description	Allows you to retrieve purchase orders
10	Allows you to add or update purchase orders
11	Allows you to delete purchase orders
12	Allows you to retrieve history
13	Allows you to add notes
14	GET PurchaseOrders
15	
16	Use this method to retrieve one or many purchase orders.
17	
18	By default responses are formatted as XML. You can also retrieve responses in JSON format.
19	When you retrieve multiple purchase orders, paging is enforced by default. 100 purchase orders are returned per page.
20	Individual purchase orders (e.g. PurchaseOrders/97c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9) can also be returned as PDF's see our HTTP GET documentation
21	
22	Amounts are in the purchase order's currency.
23	
24		
25	Contact	See Contacts.
26	Date	Date purchase order was issued – YYYY-MM-DD
27	DeliveryDate	Date the goods are to be delivered – YYYY-MM-DD
28	LineAmountTypes	See Line Amount Types
29	PurchaseOrderNumber	Unique alpha numeric code identifying purchase order
30	Reference	Additional reference number
31	LineItems	The LineItems collection can contain any number of individual LineItem sub-elements.
32	BrandingThemeID	See BrandingThemes
33	CurrencyCode	The currency that purchase order has been raised in (see Currencies)
34	Status	See Purchase Order Status Codes
35	SentToContact	Boolean to set whether the purchase order should be marked as "sent"
36	DeliveryAddress	The address the goods are to be delivered to
37	AttentionTo	The person that the delivery is going to
38	Telephone	The phone number for the person accepting the delivery
39	DeliveryInstructions	A free text field for instructions
40	ExpectedArrivalDate	The date the goods are expected to arrive.
41	PurchaseOrderID	Xero generated identifier for purchase order (unique within organisations)
42	CurrencyRate	For base currency purchase orders, has a value of 1. For non-base-currency purchase orders, see CurrencyRate meaning.
43	SubTotal	Total of purchase order excluding taxes
44	TotalTax	Total tax on purchase order
45	Total	Total of Purchase Order tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)
46	TotalDiscount	Total of discounts applied on the purchase order line items
47	HasAttachments	boolean to indicate if a purchase order has an attachment
48	UpdatedDateUTC	Last modified date UTC format
49	
50	Elements for Line Items
51	
52		
53	Description	The description of the line item. A line item can be created with only a description (i.e no unit amount or quantity)
54	Quantity	LineItem Quantity. If Quantity is specified then a UnitAmount must be specified
55	UnitAmount	Lineitem unit amount. Will be rounded to four decimal places
56	ItemCode	See Items
57	ItemID	See Items
58	AccountCode	See Accounts
59	AccountID	See Accounts
60	TaxType	Used as an override if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode/AccountID is not correct – see TaxTypes.
61	DiscountRate	Percentage discount being applied to a line item
62	Tracking	Tracking Category – see Tracking. Any LineItem can have a maximum of 2 TrackingCategory elements.
63	LineItemID	The Xero generated identifier for a LineItem. If LineItemIDs are not included with line items in an update request then the line items are deleted and recreated.
64	TaxAmount	The tax amount is auto calculated as a percentage of the line amount based on the tax rate.
65	LineAmount	The line amount reflects the discounted price if a DiscountRate has been used . i.e LineAmount = Quantity * Unit Amount * ((100 – DiscountRate)/100)
66	Optional parameters
67		
68	Record filter	You can specify an individual record by appending the value to the endpoint, i.e. GET https://.../PurchaseOrders/{identifier}
69	PurchaseOrderID – The Xero identifier for a PurchaseOrder e.g. 297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9
70	PurchaseOrderNumber – The PurchaseOrderNumber e.g. PO-01514
71	Modified After	The ModifiedAfter filter is actually an HTTP header: ' If-Modified-Since'. A UTC timestamp (yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss) . Only purchase orders created or modified since this timestamp will be returned e.g. 2015-11-12T00:00:00
72	Status	Filter by purchase order status (e.g. GET https://.../PurchaseOrders?status=DRAFT)
73	DateFrom and DateTo	Filter by purchase order date (e.g. GET https://.../PurchaseOrders?DateFrom=2015-12-01&DateTo=2015-12-31)
74	order	Order by any element returned ( see Order By )
75	page	100 purchase orders will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1
76	pageSize	Used with the page parameter. Up to 1000 purchase orders will be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used e.g. page=1&pageSize=250.
77	
78	Example response retrieving PurchaseOrders
79	
80	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders
81	copy code
82	{
83	  "PurchaseOrders": [
84	    {
85	      "PurchaseOrderID": "44d3f8a4-7031-45e8-b252-e92914e43c7e",
