204	
205	copy code
206	High volume threshold limit
207	
208	In order to make our platform more stable, we've added a high volume threshold limit for the GET Purchase Orders Endpoint.
209	
210	Requests that have more than 100k purchase orders being returned in the response will be denied
211	Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k purchase orders will be denied with a 400 response code
212	
213	Please continue reading to find out how you can use paging to ensure your requests are always successful. Be sure to check out the Efficient Data Retrieval page for tips on query optimisation.
214	
215	Paging purchase orders (recommended)
216	
217	More information about retrieving paged resources.
218	
219	POST PurchaseOrders
220	
221	Use this method to create or update purchase orders.
222	
223	Amounts are in the purchase order's currency.
224	
225	The following are required to create a purchase order
226	
227		
228	Contact	The PurchaseOrders endpoint does not create new contacts. You need to provide the ContactID or ContactNumber of an existing contact. For more information on creating contacts see Contacts.
229	LineItems	The LineItems collection can contain any number of individual LineItem sub-elements. At least one LineItem is required to create a complete PurchaseOrder.
230	
231	The following are optional for a PUT / POST request
232	
233		
234	Date	Date purchase order was issued – YYYY-MM-DD. If the Date element is not specified then it will default to the current date based on the timezone setting of the organisation
235	DeliveryDate	Date the goods are to be delivered – YYYY-MM-DD
236	LineAmountTypes	Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element. See Line Amount Types
237	PurchaseOrderNumber	Unique alpha numeric code identifying purchase order ( when missing will auto-generate from your Organisation Invoice Settings)
238	Reference	Additional reference number
239	BrandingThemeID	See BrandingThemes
240	CurrencyCode	The currency that purchase order has been raised in (see Currencies). Defaults to the organisation's base currency.
241	CurrencyRate	For how and when to set, see our multicurrency guide. A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect.
242	Status	See Purchase Order Status Codes
243	SentToContact	Boolean to set whether the purchase order should be marked as "sent". This can be set only on purchase orders that have been approved or billed
244	DeliveryAddress	The address the goods are to be delivered to
245	AttentionTo	The person that the delivery is going to
246	Telephone	The phone number for the person accepting the delivery
247	DeliveryInstructions	A free text field for instructions (500 characters max)
248	ExpectedArrivalDate	The date the goods are expected to arrive.
249	PurchaseOrderID	Xero generated unique identifier for purchase order
250	
251	The following contain the Required Elements for LineItems
252	
253		
254	Description	The description of the line item. A line item can be created with only a description (i.e no unit amount or quantity)
255	Quantity	LineItem Quantity. If Quantity is specified then a UnitAmount must be specified. A line item can be created with only Quantity and UnitAmount (i.e. no description)
256	UnitAmount	Lineitem unit amount. Will be rounded to four decimal places
257	
258	The following are the Optional Elements for LineItems
259	
260		
261	Description	The description of the line item. A line item can be created with only a description (i.e no unit amount or quantity)
262	Quantity	LineItem Quantity. If Quantity is specified then a UnitAmount must be specified
263	UnitAmount	Lineitem unit amount. Will be rounded to four decimal places
264	ItemCode	See Items
265	ItemID	See Items
266	AccountCode	See Accounts
267	AccountID	See Accounts
268	TaxType	Used as an override if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode/AccountID is not correct – see TaxTypes.
269	DiscountRate	Percentage discount being applied to a line item
270	Tracking	Optional Tracking Category – see Tracking. Any LineItem can have a maximum of 2 TrackingCategory elements.
271	Tracking category can be provided via Name or TrackingCategoryID.
272	Tracking option can be provided via Option or TrackingOptionID.
273	LineItemID	The Xero generated identifier for a LineItem. If LineItemIDs are not included with line items in an update request then the line items are deleted and recreated.
274	Creating, updating and deleting line items when updating purchase orders
275	
276	In an update (POST) request:
277	
278	Providing an existing LineItem with its LineItemID will update that line item.
279	Providing a LineItem with no LineItemID will create a new line item.
280	Not providing an existing LineItem with it's LineItemID will result in that line item being deleted.
281	
282	Example request to create a simple draft purchase order
283	
284	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders
285	copy code
286	{
287	  "Contact": { "ContactID": "eaa28f49-6028-4b6e-bb12-d8f6278073fc" },
288	  "Date": "2015-11-30",
289	  "DeliveryDate": "2015-12-20",
290	  "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
291	  "LineItems": [
292	    {
293	      "Description": "Office Chairs",
294	      "Quantity": 5.0000,
295	      "UnitAmount": 120.00
296	    }
297	  ]
298	}
299	
300	
301	copy code
302	
303	Example request to create a purchase order with all elements specified
304	
305	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders
306	copy code
307	{
308	  "PurchaseOrderNumber": "PO-0292",
309	  "Date": "2015-11-12",
310	  "DeliveryDate": "2015-12-12",
311	  "Reference": "REF123",
312	  "Contact": { "ContactID": "d7b78c9c-b34a-4dad-b999-c3c2504c7877" },
313	  "BrandingThemeID": "4c82c365-35cb-467f-bb11-dce1f2f2f67c",
314	  "Status": "AUTHORISED",
315	  "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
316	  "LineItems": [
317	    {
318	      "ItemID": "b952198a-cc14-400f-a328-9f801b00b6f7",
319	      "Description": "Golf balls - white single. Wholesale catalog item #020812-1",
320	      "UnitAmount": 4.2595,
321	      "TaxType": "INPUT2",
322	      "AccountID": "c6fb0038-7941-4297-987a-303d51536e60",
323	      "Tracking": [
324	        {
325	          "Name": "Region",
326	          "Option": "Eastside"
327	        },
328	        {
329	          "TrackingCategoryID": "be4a1c69-28a0-4639-93eb-3c0af2e115fc",
330	          "TrackingOptionID": "9f988c39-facb-4f8b-85db-4b5e29dd4c43"
331	        }
332	      ],
333	      "Quantity": 1.0000,
334	      "DiscountRate": 10.00
335	    }
336	  ],
337	  "CurrencyRate": 0.615310,
338	  "CurrencyCode": "EUR",
339	  "DeliveryAddress": "23 Main Street, Central City, Marineville,1234",
340	  "AttentionTo": "Bob",
341	  "Telephone": "0800 1234 5678",
342	  "DeliveryInstructions": "Don't forget the secret knock",
343	  "ExpectedArrivalDate": "2015-12-12"
344	}
345	
346	
347	copy code
348	
349	Example request to update a draft purchase order to a status of deleted.
350	
351	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders/22b3fab4-ef56-4d70-a110-a7cc3c1a26cd
352	copy code
353	{
354	  "PurchaseOrderNumber": "PO-239",
355	  "Status": "DELETED"
356	}
357	
358	
359	copy code
360	
361	Example request to update a purchase order to mark it as sent
362	
363	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders/8694c9c5-7097-4449-a708-b8c1982921a4
364	copy code
365	{
366	  "PurchaseOrderID": "8694c9c5-7097-4449-a708-b8c1982921a4",
367	  "SentToContact": true
368	}
369	
370	
371	copy code
372	PUT PurchaseOrders
373	
374	The PUT method is similar to the POST PurchaseOrders method, however you can only create new purchase orders with this method.
375	
376	Summarize Errors
377	
378	If you are entering many purchase orders in a single API call then we recommend you utilise the summarizeErrors parameter which provides a response format that shows validation errors for each purchase order. Note that each Purchase Order is returned with a status element which will either contain the value OK or ERROR. If a purchase order has an error then one or more validation errors will be returned.
379	
380	Example of the altered response format using the SummarizeErrors=false parameter
381	
382	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders?SummarizeErrors=false
383	copy code
384	{
385	  "PurchaseOrders": [
386	    {
387	      ...
388	      "StatusAttributeString": "OK"
389	    },{
390	      ...
391	      "StatusAttributeString": "ERROR",
392	      "ValidationErrors": [
393	        { "Message": "Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR" },
394	        { "Message": "A Contact must be specified for this type of transaction" }
395	      ]
396	    },{
397	      ...
398	      "StatusAttributeString": "OK"
399	    }
400	  ]
401	}
402	
403	
404	copy code
405	Retrieving History
406	
407	View a summary of the actions made by all users to the purchase order. See the History and Notes page for more details.
408	
409	Example of retrieving a purchase order's history
410	
411	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders/{Guid}/History
412	copy code
413	{
414	  "HistoryRecords": [
415	     {
416	      "Changes": "Updated",
417	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
418	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
419	      "User": "System Generated",
420	      "Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"
421	    },
422	    {
423	      "Changes": "Created",
424	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
425	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
426	      "User": "Mac Haag",
427	      "Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."
428	    }
429	    ...
430	  ]
431	}
432	
433	
434	copy code
435	Add Notes to a Purchase Order
436	
437	Add a note which will appear in the history against a purchase order. See the History and Notes page for more details.
438	
439	Example of creating a note against a purchase order
440	
441	PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders/{Guid}/History
442	copy code
443	{
444	  "HistoryRecords": [
445	    {
446	      "Details": "Note added by your favourite app!"
447	    }
448	    ...
449	  ]
450	}
451	
452	
453	copy code
454	On this page
455	Overview
456	GET PurchaseOrders
457	POST PurchaseOrders
458	PUT PurchaseOrders
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/raw-payments.txt`

```text
1	Payments
2	
3	Try in API Explorer
4	
5	Overview
6	Property	Description
7	URL	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments
8	Methods Supported	GET, PUT, POST
9	Description	Retrieve either one or many payments for invoices and credit notes
10	Apply payments to approved AR and AP invoices
11	Allow you to refund AR and AP credit notes
12	Delete (reverse) a payment
13	Allows you to refund spend and receive prepayments and overpayments
14	Allows you to retrieve history
15	Allows you to add notes
16	
17	To pay multiple AR or AP invoices in a single transaction use the BatchPayments endpoint.
18	
19	Multicurrency payments
20	
21	A payment is multicurrency if the payment account, or the invoice paid against, are in a currency other than the base currency of the organisation. To understand how to correctly create and interpret multicurrency payments, refer to our multicurrency guidance.
22	
23	GET Payments
24	
25	Use this method to retrieve either one or many payments for invoices, credit notes, overpayments or prepayments
26	
27	Field	Description
28	Date	Date the payment is being made (YYYY-MM-DD) e.g. 2009-09-06
29	CurrencyRate	Exchange rate when payment is made or received. For non-multicurrency payments, has a value of 1. For multicurrency payments, see CurrencyRate meaning.
30	Amount	The amount of the payment in the currency of the Invoice the payment was made against. Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice e.g. 200.00
31	BankAmount	The amount of the payment in the currency of the Account.
32	Reference	An optional description for the payment e.g. Direct Debit
33	IsReconciled	An optional parameter for the payment. Conversion related apps can utilise the IsReconciled flag in scenarios when a matching bank statement line is not available.
34	Status	The status of the payment.
35	PaymentType	See Payment Types.
36	UpdatedDateUTC	UTC timestamp of last update to the payment.
37	BatchPaymentID	Present if the payment was created as part of a batch.
38	BatchPayment	Details of the Batch the payment was part of. See Batch Payments for more details.
39	Account	The Account the payment was made from.
40	Invoice	The Invoice the payment was made against.
41	Optional parameters for GET Payments
42	Field	Description
43	Record filter	You can specify an individual record by appending the PaymentID to the endpoint, i.e.
44	GET https://.../Payments/297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9
45	Modified After	The ModifiedAfter filter is actually an HTTP header: ‘If-Modified-Since‘. note payments created or modified since this timestamp will be returned e.g. 2009-11-12T00:00:00
46	Where	Filter by any element ( see Filters ). Only certain elements are optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes.
47	Order	Order by any element returned ( see Order By ). Only certain elements are optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes.
48	page	100 contacts will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1
49	pageSize	Used with the page parameter. Up to 1000 contacts will be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used e.g. page=1&pageSize=250.
50	High volume threshold limit
51	
52	In order to make our platform more stable, we've added a high volume threshold limit for the GET Payments Endpoint.
53	
54	Requests that have more than 100k payments being returned in the response will be denied and receive a 400 response code
55	Requests using unoptimised fields for filtering or ordering that result in more than 100k payments will be denied with a 400 response code
56	
57	Please continue reading to find out how you can use paging and optimise your filtering to ensure your requests are always successful. Be sure to check out the Efficient Data Retrieval page for tips on query optimisation.
58	
59	Paging payments (recommended)
60	
61	More information about retrieving paged resources.
62	
63	Optimised filtering using the where parameter
64	
65	The most common filters have been optimised to ensure performance across organisations of all sizes. We recommend you restrict your filtering to the following optimised parameters.
66	
67	Range Operators in Where clauses
68	
69	Indicated fields also support the range operators: greater than, greater than or equals, less than, less than or equals (>, >=, <, <=).
70	
71	Range operators can be combined with the AND operator to query a date range. eg where=Date>=DateTime(2020, 01, 01) AND Date<DateTime(2020, 02, 01)
72	
73	When using individually or combined with the AND operator:
74	
75	Field	Operator	Query
76	PaymentType	equals	where=PaymentType="ACCRECPAYMENT"
77	Status	equals	where=Status="AUTHORISED"
78	Date	equals, range	where=Date=DateTime(2020, 01, 01)
79	where=Date>DateTime(2020, 01, 01)
80	Invoice.InvoiceId	equals	where=Invoice.InvoiceID=guid("96988e67-ecf9-466d-bfbf-0afa1725a649")
81	Reference	equals	where=Reference="INV-0001"
82	
83	When using with the OR operator:
84	
85	Field	Operator	Query
86	PaymentId	equals	where=PaymentID=guid("0a0ef7ee-7b91-46fa-8136-c4cc6287273a") OR PaymentID=guid("603b8347-d833-4e65-abf9-1f465652cb42")
87	Invoice.InvoiceId	equals	where=Invoice.InvoiceId=guid("0b0ef7ee-7b91-46fa-8136-c4cc6287273a") OR Invoice.InvoiceId=guid("693b8347-d833-4e65-abf9-1f465652cb42")
88	
89	Example: Retrieve all ACCRECPAYMENT payments with an AUTHORISED status
90	
91	?where=PaymentType=="ACCRECPAYMENT" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"
92	copy code
93	
94	This would translate to the following URL once encoded.
95	
96	https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments?where=PaymentType%3D%3D%22ACCRECPAYMENT%22+AND+Status%3D%3D%22AUTHORISED%22%0D%0A
97	copy code
98	Optimised ordering:
99	
100	The following parameters are optimised for ordering:
101	
102	UpdatedDateUTC
103	Date
104	PaymentId
105	
106	The default order is UpdatedDateUTC ASC, PaymentId ASC. Secondary ordering is applied by default using the PaymentId. This ensures consistency across pages.
107	
108	The example below is fetching a payment on an AR invoice in the base currency of the organisation
109	
110	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments/b26fd49a-cbae-470a-a8f8-bcbc119e0379
111	copy code
112	{
113	  "Payments": [
114	    {
115	      "PaymentID": "b26fd49a-cbae-470a-a8f8-bcbc119e0379",
116	      "BatchPaymentID": "b54aa50c-794c-461b-89d1-846e1b84d9c0",
117	      "BatchPayment": {
118	        "Account": {
119	          "AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c"
120	        },
121	        "BatchPaymentID": "b54aa50c-794c-461b-89d1-846e1b84d9c0",
122	        "Date": "\/Date(1455667200000+0000)\/",

128	        "DateString": "1970-01-01T00:00:00"
129	      },
130	      "Date": "\/Date(1455667200000+0000)\/",
131	      "BankAmount": 500.00,
132	      "Amount": 500.00,
133	      "Reference": "INV-0001",
134	      "CurrencyRate": 1.000000,
135	      "PaymentType": "ACCRECPAYMENT",
136	      "Status": "AUTHORISED",
137	      "UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1289572582537+0000)\/",
138	      "HasAccount": true,
139	      "IsReconciled": true,
140	      "Account": {

142	        "Code": "090",

144	        "CurrencyCode": "NZD"
145	      },
146	      "Invoice": {
147	        "Type": "ACCREC",

170	        "DueDateString": "2025-02-27T00:00:00",
171	        "DueDate": "\/Date(1740614400000+0000)\/",
172	        "BrandingThemeID": "e275691c-523d-409c-a10d-ccb6663142fc",
173	        "Status": "AUTHORISED",
174	        "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",

185

238	
239	copy code
240	
241	Due to JSON serialization behavior, a null object may be represented as an empty array. As a result, an empty array does not necessarily indicate that the attribute has no value.
242	
243	PUT Payments
244	
245	Use this method to apply payments to approved AR and AP invoices, refund AR or AP credit notes, refund spend or receive overpayments, or refund spend or receive prepayments.
246	
247	Provide only one identifier object (Invoice, CreditNote, Prepayment, or Overpayment). Each object contains nested attributes (as described in the table below) that can be used to identify the document you are applying the payment to. It is essential that the nested attributes correspond to the chosen identifier object. Refer to the example below for correct usage.
248	
249	Field	Description	
250	InvoiceID or CreditNoteID or PrepaymentID or OverpaymentID	ID of the invoice, credit note, prepayment or overpayment you are applying payment to e.g. 297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9	
251	
252	Account
253	
254		Field	Description
255	either	AccountID	ID of account you are using to make the payment e.g. 294b1dc5-cc47-2afc-7ec8-64990b8761b8. This account needs to be either an account of type BANK or have enable payments to this accounts switched on (see GET Accounts) . See the edit account screen of your Chart of Accounts in Xero if you wish to enable payments for an account other than a bank account
256	or	Code	Code of account you are using to make the payment e.g. 001 ( note: not all accounts have a code value)
257	Field		Description
258	Date		Date the payment is being made (YYYY-MM-DD) e.g. 2009-09-06
259	Amount		The amount of the payment in the currency of the Invoice the payment was made against. Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice e.g. 200.00
260	BankAmount		The amount being paid in the currency of the account. When this is set, Xero will calculate the appropriate CurrencyRate. If you know this amount, specifying it is preferred to setting CurrencyRate. A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the calculated rate appears incorrect. BankAmount is currently only supported for payments of AR and AP invoices.
261	CurrencyRate		Exchange rate when payment is received. For how and when to set, see our multicurrency guide. A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect.
262	Reference		An optional description for the payment e.g. Direct Debit
263	IsReconciled		A boolean indicating whether the payment has been reconciled.
264	Status		The status of the payment.
265	Example – single payment
266	
267	Below is an example of applying a $32.06 payment to invoice OIT00545 from Account Code 001 on 8 Sept 2009.
268	
269	{
270	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
271	  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
272	  "Date": "2009-09-08",
273	  "Amount": 32.06
274	}
275	
276	
277	copy code
278	Example – multiple payments
279	
280	Below is an example of applying multiple payments to various Invoices (identified by InvoiceID) from various Accounts (identified by AccountID) across various dates.
281	
282	{
283	  "Payments": [
284	    {
285	      "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
286	      "Account": { "AccountID": "297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9" },
287	      "Date": "2009-07-13",
288	      "Amount": 3375.00
289	    },
290	    {
291	      "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "0a1d0d71-b001-4c71-a260-31e77c9d4a92" },
292	      "Account": { "AccountID": "a65b0dac-b444-4b41-959b-c1580cd6268f" },
293	      "Date": "2009-09-01",
294	      "Amount": 393.75
295	    },
296	    {
297	      "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "93c9be81-1df4-4338-b5dc-e67a89cd2d7c" },
298	      "Account": { "AccountID": "a65b0dac-b444-4b41-959b-c1580cd6268f" },
299	      "Date": "2009-07-21",
300	      "Amount": 398
301	    }
302	  ]
303	}
304	
305	
306	copy code
307	Example – payments or refunds for different type
308	
309	The following example demonstrates how to apply a payment to different types of accounting documents. Ensure that the correct object type is used for the corresponding document type (e.g., use CreditNoteID when applying a payment to a Credit Note).
310	
311	{
312	  "Payments": [
313	    {
314	      "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
315	      "Account": { "AccountID": "297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9" },
316	      "Date": "2009-07-13",
317	      "Amount": 3375.00
318	    },
319	    {
320	      "CreditNote": { "CreditNoteID": "8648fa8c-3d0c-4ea3-9f6b-3a3ec05d0e26" },
321	      "Account": { "Code": "090" },
322	      "Date": "2013-09-04",
323	      "Amount": 50.00,
324	      "Reference": "Full refund as we couldn't replace item"
325	    },
326	    {
327	      "Prepayment": { "PrepaymentID": "262c3049-cbf2-4b4b-9fca-60d55b076e35" },
328	      "Account": { "Code": "090" },
329	      "Date": "2015-03-25",
330	      "Amount": 100.00,
331	      "Reference": "Full refund as the customer cancelled their subscription"
332	    },
333	    {
334	      "Overpayment": { "OverpaymentID": "1ced4be7-ea6d-4f46-8279-4203e461de80" },
335	      "Account": { "Code": "090" },
336	      "Date": "2015-04-01",
337	      "Amount": 200.00,
338	      "Reference": "Refunded overpayment made by mistake"
339	    }
340	  ]
341	}
342	
343	copy code
344	Example – Creating a reconciled payment
345	
346	Below is an example for applying an automatically reconciled payment to an invoice (useful for conversion purposes)
347	
348	{
349	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceNumber": "OIT00619" },
350	  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
351	  "Date": "2009-09-08",
352	  "Amount": 20.00,
353	  "IsReconciled": true
354	}
355	
356	
357	copy code
358	Example – Creating a multicurrency payment
359	
360	Below is an example for multicurrency AR/AP payment using the preferred BankAmount field
361	
362	{
363	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceNumber": "OIT00619" }, // AR - AUD
364	  "Account": { "Code": "001" }, // NZD
365	  "Date": "2009-09-08",
366	  "Amount": 20.00,
367	  "BankAmount": 25.00
368	}
369	
370	
371	copy code
372	SummarizeErrors
373	
374	If you are entering many payments in a single API call then we recommend you utilise our response format that shows validation errors for each invoice. Each Invoice will be returned with a status element that contains the value OK or ERROR. If an invoice has a error then one or more validation errors will be returned.
375	
376	Example of the altered response format using the SummarizeErrors=false parameter
377	
378	POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments?SummarizeErrors=false
379	
380	
381	copy code
382	{
383	  "Payments": [
384	    ...
385	    {
386	      "ValidationErrors": [
387	        {
388	          "Message": "...."
389	        }
390	      ]
391	    }
392	    ...
393	  ]
394	}
395	
396	
397	copy code
398	POST Payments
399	
400	Use this method to delete (reverse) payments to invoices, credit notes, prepayments & overpayments. Note that payments created via batch payments and receipts are not supported. Payments cannot be modified, only created and deleted.
401	
402	Required parameters for POST Payments
403	Field	Description
404	PaymentID	The Xero identifier for an Payment e.g. 297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9
405	Example – delete a payment
406	
407	Below is an example of deleting a payment. In order to delete a payment, you must POST to the resource ID for the payment. e.g POST /Payments/b05466c8-dc54-4ff8-8f17-9d7008a2e44b
408	
409	{
410	  "Status": "DELETED"
411	}
412	
413	
414	copy code
415	Retrieving History
416	
417	View a summary of the actions made by all users to the payment. See the History and Notes page for more details.
418	
419	Example of retrieving a payment's history
420	
421	GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments/{Guid}/History
422	copy code
423	{
424	  "HistoryRecords": [
425	     {
426	      "Changes": "Updated",
427	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
428	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
429	      "User": "System Generated",
430	      "Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"
431	    },
432	    {
433	      "Changes": "Created",
434	      "DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
435	      "DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
436	      "User": "Mac Haag",
437	      "Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."
438	    }
439	    ...
440	  ]
441	}
442	
443	
444	copy code
445	Add Notes to a Payment
446	
447	Add a note which will appear in the history against a payment. See the History and Notes page for more details.
448	
449	Example of creating a note against a payment
450	
451	PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments/{Guid}/History
452	copy code
453	{
454	  "HistoryRecords": [
455	    {
456	      "Details": "Note added by your favourite app!"
457	    }
458	    ...
459	  ]
460	}
461	
462	
463	copy code
464	On this page
465	Overview
466	GET Payments
467	PUT Payments
468	POST Payments
```
</details></tool-use>

441	Tax Rates
442	Tax Status Codes
443		
444	ACTIVE	The tax rate is active and can be used in transactions
445	DELETED	The tax rate is deleted and cannot be restored or used on transactions
446	ARCHIVED	The tax rate has been used on a transaction (e.g. an invoice) but has since been deleted. ARCHIVED tax rates cannot be restored or used on transactions.
447	Tax Types
448	
449	New tax rates can be setup for a Xero organisation. All new tax rates added have a TaxType of the format TAX001, TAX002 etc.

451	Below are a list of Tax Types included by default in each version of Xero. Tax Types that are system defined cannot be updated via the API.

454

# Xero Auth + API Conventions — Verified Fact Sheet

Verified 2026-07-04 against the live official docs at developer.xero.com. Pages are client-side rendered (plain HTTP fetch returns an empty shell), so each page was rendered in Chrome and the visible text extracted. Every fact below is tagged with the source URL. Facts that could not be confirmed on the fetched pages are marked UNVERIFIED.

---

## 1. Custom Connections

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections/

- **Token endpoint**: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`
- **Auth on the token request**: `Authorization: "Basic " + base64encode(client_id + ":" + client_secret)` with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`
- **grant_type**: `client_credentials` — "The request body will need to contain the grant type (client_credentials) and scope"
- **Scopes — configured at BOTH ends**:
  - At app creation: "Next you'll need to select the API scopes your integration will need and who will authorise the connection." (step 2)
  - In the token request body: `scope=A space separated list of the scopes required`
  - The Client Credentials guide adds: "Note: this differs from the Code and PKCE grant types where scopes are requested as parameters in the URL." (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/client-credentials/)
  - Scope changes coming: "From 29 April 2026, we're changing how scopes work for custom connections... All custom connections will have access to granular scopes... New custom connections will no longer have access to the `accounting.journals.read` scope... If you have an existing custom connection, you can continue using broad scopes until September 2027."
- **Token response fields**: `access_token`, `expires_in` ("The amount of seconds until the access token expires"), `token_type: Bearer`, `scope`. The access token is a JWT.
- **Token lifetime**: The custom-connections page itself gives no number (its example JWT has `nbf: 1589363023`, `exp: 1589364823` = 1800 s). The explicit statement is on the Token types page: "They are only valid for up to 30 minutes" — https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/token-types/
- **Bound to ONE organisation** (exact quote): "Custom Connections are a premium integration option that utilise the client credentials grant type to access data from a single Xero organisation. If your app needs to connect to multiple Xero organisations then you should use the code flow or PKCE flow instead."
- **Region restriction**: "Custom Connections are only available for Xero organisations in Australia, New Zealand, the UK, and the US and require an additional monthly subscription." Free connection to the Xero Demo Company for development; Custom Connection apps do not count toward the two-uncertified-app limit.
- **xero-tenant-id header**: UNVERIFIED as an explicit statement — the current custom-connections page never mentions the header. What the page does show (step 7, "Call the Xero API"): "Make calls against the Xero APIs by simply adding the following headers to your request:" followed by an example containing ONLY two headers:
  \```
  GET ../api.xro/2.0/Invoices
  Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token
  Accept: application/json
  \```
  i.e. no `xero-tenant-id` header in the documented call. Contrast with standard (code-flow) tokens, where the Token types page requires it: "Make calls against the Xero APIs by simply adding the following headers to your request: `Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token`; `xero-tenant-id: The tenantId context for the request.`" with example `xero-tenant-id: 45e4708e-d862-4111-ab3a-dd8cd03913e1` (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/token-types/). So the header is omitted from the custom-connection call example, but the sentence "the xero-tenant-id header is not required" does not appear on the current page.
- Related distinction (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/client-credentials/): "Custom Connection apps use a modified version of client credentials which is not described on this page. They can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints." Plain client-credentials tokens are the opposite: non-tenanted endpoints only.

---

## 2. Rate limits

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/

- **Per tenant** ("organisation, account or practice"):
  - Concurrent Limit: **5 calls in progress at one time**
  - Minute Limit: **60 calls per minute**
  - Daily Limit: **1,000 calls per day for starter, 5,000 calls per day for higher tiers**
- **App-wide**: "There is also a limit to the number of API calls your app can make per minute across all tenants. App Minute Limit: **10,000 calls per minute**"
- **Quota headers** (exact quote): "Each API response you receive will include the `X-DayLimit-Remaining`, `X-MinLimit-Remaining` and `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining` headers telling you the number of remaining against each limit."
- **429 behaviour** (exact quote): "Exceeding a rate limit will result in an HTTP 429 (too many requests) response. It will include an `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` header telling you which limit you have reached."
- **Retry-After** (exact quote): "If you have exceed the minute or daily limit you will also receive a `Retry-After` http header that tells you how many seconds to wait before making another request. Requests are counted against a fixed window which will reset at different times for each tenant. It is important to use the Retry-After header to know when you can start making calls again."
- **Limits are per tenant, not shared**: "the limits are per tenant. For example if two separate Xero organisations are connected to your application, each connection would have 5000 API calls available in a given 24 hour period for apps in the Core tier and above and 1000 API calls available for apps in the Starter tier."
- **Request size limit**: "The maximum request size limit for all APIs is 10MB." Recommended batching: "bundles of up to 50" elements. (Elsewhere on the page: "a ceiling of about 50 nodes per request is practical – this will ensure a request does not exceed the maximum size of 3.5MB.")
- **Uncertified app limits**: "each organisation or practice is limited to connecting a maximum of two uncertified apps." New apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections; Core gives up to 50 connections.

---

## 3. Accounting API request conventions

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses

- **Base URL / format**: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Resource}`. "By default all successful responses on the accounting API are returned as XML." JSON via `Accept: application/json`. Individual Invoices, Quotes and Credit Notes can be returned as PDF via `Accept: application/pdf`.
- **JSON date format**: Microsoft .NET style, e.g. `"DateTimeUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790)\/"` — "a unix timestamp value, but in miliseconds rather than seconds".

### Pagination
- Endpoints (exact quote): "Paged results are available on the **Invoices, Contacts, CreditNotes, BankTransactions, ManualJournals, Payments, PurchaseOrders, Prepayments and Overpayments** endpoints."
- "To utilise paging you must append a page query parameter to the URL e.g. `?page=1`."
- Page size (exact quote): "The default page size is **100**, with a maximum of **1000** and a minimum of **1**. If users set a value outside the supported range, we automatically adjust it to the nearest supported page size. For example, `?page=1&pageSize=5000` will return 1000 items."
- Responses include a pagination object: `"pagination": { "page": 1, "pageSize": 10, "pageCount": 1, "itemCount": 1 }` (supersedes the old "fetch next page until empty" approach).
- The Limits page adds: endpoints supporting pagination let you "retrieve line item details for 100 invoices at a time"; Journals are "retrieved only in batches of 100" (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/).

### If-Modified-Since
- Exact quote: "specify a UTC timestamp filter using the If-Modified-Since http parameter. Only items created or updated since the specified timestamp will be returned (accurate to the second)."
- "We recommend all calls to any endpoints that have large result sets use the if-modified-since parameter."
- Caveat (exact quote): "Not all changes will trigger a change of the UpdatedDateUTC field. These include changes to partially paid transactions which don't generate a journal such as DueDate or SentToContact, and Contact fields pulled from other sources such as Balances, IsSupplier, and isCustomer. As a result, transactions with these changes may not be returned with an If-Modified-Since query."

### "where" filter
- "The where parameter allows you to filter on endpoints and elements that don't have explicit parameters... it must be encoded using percent encoding before it is appended to the URL."
- Exact examples from the docs:
  1. Strings use double quotes: `Type=="BANK"` → percent encoded: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts?where=Type%3D%3D%22BANK%22`
  2. Numbers and dates, `and`/`&&` to connect conditions: `AmountDue > 5000 and DueDate > DateTime(2015, 01, 01)`
  3. Date range: `Date >= DateTime(2015, 01, 01) && Date < DateTime(2015, 12, 31)`
- Performance warning: "Long, complex where queries can cause time outs... restrict your queries to simple == operations."
- Invoices and Contacts also have explicit comma-separated filter params, e.g. `?IDs=...,...`, `?ContactIDs=...&Statuses=AUTHORISED`, `?Statuses=DELETED,VOIDED`.
- `summaryOnly=true` on GET Contacts and Invoices returns a lightweight response.

### order
- "append an order query string to the endpoint URL", e.g. `?order=EmailAddress`; descending: `?order=EmailAddress%20DESC`.
- "Secondary ordering, using the ID field, is now applied by default across a range of endpoints – Contacts, Payments, Batch Payments, Credit Notes, Invoices and Bank Transactions... The default order is `UpdatedDateUTC ASC, [ID] ASC`."

### summarizeErrors + validation error envelope
- "If you plan to submit more than one entity per API call, we recommend that you append the `summarizeErrors=false` querystring... This ensures that every entity is returned back to you, each having their own status attribute." Status per entity: OK, WARNING or ERROR.
- "If you have a validation error in any of your objects you will receive a HTTP 200 status code (rather than HTTP 400) if you are using the summarizeErrors parameter."
- "The summarizeErrors option is only available for the Accounting API."
- Bulk response example (quoted from the page, abridged):
  \```json
  {
    "Invoices": [
      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "OK" },
      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "WARNING",
        "Warnings": [{ "Message": "Only AUTHORISED invoices may have SentToContact updated." }] },
      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "ERROR",
        "ValidationErrors": [{ "Description": "Invoice not of valid type for creation" }] }
    ]
  }
  \```
- HTTP 400 envelope (Elements[].ValidationErrors) — exact example from the Response Codes page (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/responsecodes): "HTTP 400 responses include an 'ApiException' element in the response that contains a useful summary of the reason for the error."
  \```json
  {
    "ErrorNumber": 10,
    "Type": "ValidationException",
    "Message": "A validation exception occurred",
    "Elements": [{
      "ValidationErrors": [{
        "Message": "Email address must be valid"
      }]
    }]
  }
  \```
- PUT vs POST: "A PUT method will create new data in Xero, whereas a POST will either create new data or update existing data in Xero."

---

## 4. Idempotency

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/idempotent-requests/idempotency/
(Not mentioned on the three primary pages; this is the official guide, reached via the docs nav "Idempotent Requests".)

- **Idempotency-Key header IS supported** (exact quote): "To guarantee idempotency, you need to include the 'Idempotency-Key' header on your requests. Xero will then cache the response to these requests and if subsequent requests are made with the same idempotency key, they won't be processed and instead the cached response will be returned."
- Mutating methods only: "Xero will only process idempotency for requests that mutate data, I.e. POST, PUT and PATCH. The idempotency key header will be ignored for all other HTTP methods."
- Key lifetime: "keys are stored for **6 minutes** from the time of the first call, after which they expire."
- Key reuse with a different request → HTTP 400: `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE is used with a different request.` (Scope is per app; a "different request" = any change to endpoint URL, request body, or HTTP method.)
- Max key length **128 characters** (over → 400: `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE with length KEY_LENGTH is too long.`). Header name is not case-sensitive. No server-side validation of key format — uniqueness is the caller's responsibility. Xero recommends concatenating four UUIDs.
- Errors are cached too: "If an idempotent request errors out internally, the error will be cached and returned when the request is re-run even if the internal error is resolved."
- Interaction with rate limits: "Idempotency is checked after rate limits have been applied and thus duplicate requests will count towards rate limits and reduce the limit remaining headers."
- The guide's example request includes headers `Authorization`, `Accept`, `Xero-tenant-id`, `Idempotency-Key` on `PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts`.

---

## Fetch notes
- All three requested primary URLs returned HTTP 200 (no 404s) but required JS rendering; WebFetch timed out repeatedly, so pages were rendered in Chrome and text extracted from the live DOM.
- Supplementary official pages used (reached via the docs nav): Client Credentials, Token types, Tenants, Idempotent requests, Response Codes — all under developer.xero.com.
- Single UNVERIFIED item: an explicit docs sentence stating the `xero-tenant-id` header is/isn't required for Custom Connections (the current page only implies "not required" by omitting it from the canonical call example).

```

WROTE undefined/docs-auth.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md)

- Custom Connections: POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token, grant_type=client_credentials, scopes set at app creation AND passed space-separated in token body; token = JWT, "valid for up to 30 minutes" (token-types page); bound to "a single Xero organisation" (quoted).
- xero-tenant-id: current custom-connections page never states it explicitly; call example omits the header (only Authorization + Accept) — explicit sentence marked UNVERIFIED; standard-token requirement quoted from token-types page.
- Rate limits: 5 concurrent / 60 per min / 1,000-5,000 per day per tenant; 10,000 per min app-wide; headers X-DayLimit-Remaining, X-MinLimit-Remaining, X-AppMinLimit-Remaining; 429 + X-Rate-Limit-Problem + Retry-After (seconds, fixed window) all quoted.
- Conventions: page/pageSize pagination (default 100, max 1000, 9 endpoints listed), If-Modified-Since semantics + UpdatedDateUTC caveat, 3 exact where examples incl. Type=="BANK" and DateTime(2015, 01, 01), order + DESC, summarizeErrors=false, Elements[].ValidationErrors 400 envelope quoted from Response Codes page.
- Idempotency: Idempotency-Key header supported on POST/PUT/PATCH, 6-min key cache, 128-char max, per-app scope, errors cached, counts against rate limits (official Idempotent Requests guide).
- Method note: all pages are JS-rendered; WebFetch/curl got empty shells, so content was extracted from Chrome-rendered DOM of official developer.xero.com pages. No 404s.

```md
# Xero Accounting API — Verified Fact Sheet (Invoices / Purchase Orders / Payments / Types)

Verified 2026-07-04 against the OFFICIAL Xero developer docs, rendered in Chrome directly from:
- INV: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/invoices
- PO:  https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/purchaseorders
- PAY: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/payments
- TYP: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/types

Every fact is tagged with its source page. Anything not confirmed on these four pages is marked UNVERIFIED.

---

## 1. Invoices (focus: Type=ACCPAY bills)

Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [INV]

### Essential fields [INV]

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Type | `ACCPAY` (bill) or `ACCREC` (sales invoice). Required on create. [INV][TYP] |
| Contact | Required on create. "Only ContactID should be provided when creating or updating an invoice." [INV] |
| Date | Date issued, YYYY-MM-DD. Optional; defaults to current date in org timezone. [INV] |
| DueDate | Date due, YYYY-MM-DD. Optional. [INV] |
| LineItems | Required: "At least one is required to create a complete Invoice." [INV] |
| LineItems[].Description | Min 1 char, max 4000. A line item can be created with only a Description. [INV] |
| LineItems[].Quantity | Max length 13. [INV] |
| LineItems[].UnitAmount | Rounded to 2dp by default; opt in to 4dp with querystring `unitdp=4`. [INV] |
| LineItems[].AccountCode | See Accounts. [INV] |
| LineItems[].TaxType | Override "if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode is not correct". [INV] |
| LineItems[].LineAmount | `LineAmount = Quantity * UnitAmount * ((100 – DiscountRate)/100)`; can't exceed 9,999,999,999.99. Note: DiscountRate/DiscountAmount are ACCREC-only — "ACCPAY invoices and credit notes in Xero do not support discounts". [INV] |
| InvoiceNumber | ACCREC: unique alphanumeric. **ACCPAY: NON-unique alphanumeric code; "This value will also display as Reference in the UI."** Max length 255. Auto-generated for ACCREC if missing. [INV] |
| Reference | "Additional reference number" (max length 255). [INV] |
| Status | See status codes below. Optional on create; defaults to DRAFT. [INV] |
| LineAmountTypes | "Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element." [INV] |
| SubTotal / TotalTax / Total | Total = "Total of Invoice tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)". Read/computed. [INV] |
| CurrencyCode | "Defaults to the organisation's base currency." [INV] |
| CurrencyRate | 1 for base-currency invoices; validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect. [INV] |
| AmountDue / AmountPaid / AmountCredited / FullyPaidOnDate | Returned computed payment-state fields. [INV] |

### Statuses and LEGAL transitions [INV]

New invoices may be created with one of three statuses: DRAFT (default; may contain incomplete line items; no journals), SUBMITTED (approval workflows; no journals), AUTHORISED ("approved" state; journals created; "You can now apply payments to the invoice"). [INV]

Documented valid state changes when updating ("Invoice status changes" table, verbatim) [INV]:

| Existing status | New status |
|---|---|
| DRAFT | DRAFT, SUBMITTED, AUTHORISED, DELETED |
| SUBMITTED | SUBMITTED, AUTHORISED, DRAFT, DELETED |
| AUTHORISED | AUTHORISED, VOIDED |

- Irreversible: once AUTHORISED, an invoice can never return to DRAFT/SUBMITTED and "cannot be deleted but you can set it's status to VOIDED". [INV]
- Delete: "You can delete a DRAFT or SUBMITTED invoice by updating the Status to DELETED." [INV]
- PAID is set by the system: "Once an invoice is fully paid the status will change to PAID." You cannot create PAID directly — create AUTHORISED then apply a payment ("Creating a PAID Invoice" is a two-step process). [INV]
- No transitions are documented out of PAID, VOIDED, or DELETED (the table lists none) — treat as terminal. [INV]
- Voiding example shown "for an approved invoice that has no payments applied to it". [INV]
- Locked: invoices "In a locked period" cannot be updated (ACCREC or ACCPAY). [INV]
- Partially/fully PAID ACCPAY bills can only have these fields updated: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (unless payment was via Credit Note or lines contain a CisLabourExpense account), Url, LineItems Description, AccountCode (except CIS account codes), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate. [INV]

### Create / update semantics [INV]

- POST Invoices: "Use this method to create or update an invoice."
- PUT Invoices: "similar to the POST Invoices method, however you can only create new invoices with this method."
- Envelope: a single invoice object, or an `{"Invoices": [ ... ]}` array for multiple (both shown in official examples).
- Batch validation: `?SummarizeErrors=false` returns per-invoice `ValidationErrors` with status OK/ERROR per element.
- Updates target `/Invoices/{InvoiceID}` or `/Invoices/{InvoiceNumber}` (official examples POST to both).
- Line items on update (POST): providing LineItem WITH its LineItemID updates it; WITHOUT LineItemID creates a new one; **omitting an existing LineItemID DELETES that line item** ("If LineItemIDs are not included with line items in an update request then the line items are deleted and recreated"). [INV]

### Filtering (Type=="ACCPAY", Status) [INV]

- Official example: `?where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"` (encoded: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?where=Type%3d%3d%22ACCPAY%22+AND+Status%3d%3d%22AUTHORISED%22`).
- Optimised where fields (equals): Status, Contact.ContactID, Contact.Name, Contact.ContactNumber, Reference, InvoiceNumber, InvoiceId, Type, AmountPaid; (equals+range >, >=, <, <=): Date, DueDate, AmountDue.
- Optimised comma-separated list params: `?Statuses=AUTHORISED,DRAFT`, `?IDs=...`, `?InvoiceNumbers=...`, `?ContactIDs=...`.
- `SearchTerm` does case-insensitive text search across InvoiceNumber and Reference.
- High volume threshold: requests returning >100k invoices, or unoptimised filter/order resulting in >100k, are denied with 400.
- `summaryOnly=true` excludes Payments, HasAttachments, LineItems, CISDeduction; pagination enforced when used.

### Pagination [INV]

- `page`: "100 invoices will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1".
- `pageSize`: "Sets the # of invoices to be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used with the page parameter e.g. page=1&pageSize=250."
- Max pageSize for Invoices is NOT stated on the invoices page — UNVERIFIED (the Purchase Orders and Payments pages both state "Up to 1000" for their endpoints).
- Paging returns full line item details for each invoice; without paging, multi-invoice GETs return contact summary and NO line details. Querying by Statuses enforces pagination by default. [INV]
- Default order: "UpdatedDateUTC ASC, InvoiceId ASC". Optimised order fields: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date. [INV]

---

## 2. LineAmountTypes — exact values [TYP]

Verbatim from the Types page ("LineAmount Types"):

| Value | Meaning (verbatim) |
|---|---|
| `Exclusive` | "Line items are exclusive of tax" |
| `Inclusive` | "Line items are inclusive tax" |
| `NoTax` | "Line have no tax" |

Default when omitted on create: Exclusive ("Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element"). [INV][PO]

---

## 3. Purchase Orders

Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [PO]

### Trimmed field shape [PO]

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Contact | Required. "The PurchaseOrders endpoint does not create new contacts. You need to provide the ContactID or ContactNumber of an existing contact." |
| LineItems | Required. "At least one LineItem is required to create a complete PurchaseOrder." Line fields: Description (a line can be Description-only), Quantity ("If Quantity is specified then a UnitAmount must be specified"), UnitAmount (rounded to FOUR decimal places), ItemCode/ItemID, AccountCode/AccountID, TaxType, DiscountRate, Tracking (max 2), LineItemID (same delete-if-omitted update rule as invoices), TaxAmount, LineAmount. |
| Date | Optional, YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to current date in org timezone. |
| DeliveryDate | Optional. "Date the goods are to be delivered – YYYY-MM-DD". |
| PurchaseOrderNumber | "Unique alpha numeric code identifying purchase order (when missing will auto-generate from your Organisation Invoice Settings)". |
| Reference | "Additional reference number". |
| Status | See Purchase Order Status Codes. Optional on create (examples create DRAFT by default or pass "AUTHORISED" explicitly). |
| LineAmountTypes | Exclusive by default if not specified. |
| CurrencyCode / CurrencyRate | Defaults to org base currency; CurrencyRate 1 for base-currency POs. |
| SubTotal / TotalTax / Total | Total = "Total of Purchase Order tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)". |
| DeliveryAddress / AttentionTo / Telephone / DeliveryInstructions (500 max) / ExpectedArrivalDate | Optional delivery block. |
| PurchaseOrderID | Xero-generated GUID, unique within org. |

### Status enum [TYP][PO]

Purchase Order Statuses (Types page lists exactly, with no per-status descriptions): `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED`.

- How to update status: POST to `/PurchaseOrders/{PurchaseOrderID}` (or with PurchaseOrderNumber in the body) with the new `Status`. Official example updates a draft PO to `"Status": "DELETED"`. [PO]
- Marking as billed: BILLED appears as a valid status value ([TYP]) and in GET response examples ([PO]), so POST with `"Status": "BILLED"` is the implied mechanism, but neither page shows an explicit mark-as-billed example or a PO status transition table — transition legality between PO statuses is UNVERIFIED on these pages.
- SentToContact "can be set only on purchase orders that have been approved or billed". [PO]

### Create/update endpoints and envelope [PO]

- POST PurchaseOrders: "Use this method to create or update purchase orders."
- PUT PurchaseOrders: "you can only create new purchase orders with this method."
- Envelope: single object or `{"PurchaseOrders": [ ... ]}` (array form shown in SummarizeErrors example); `?SummarizeErrors=false` gives per-PO `StatusAttributeString` OK/ERROR + `ValidationErrors`.
- Line item update rules identical to invoices (LineItemID present=update, absent=delete+recreate). [PO]

### Filtering and pagination [PO]

- Query params: `Status` (e.g. `?status=DRAFT`), `DateFrom`/`DateTo` (e.g. `?DateFrom=2015-12-01&DateTo=2015-12-31`), `order`, `page`, `pageSize`; ModifiedAfter via `If-Modified-Since` header. A generic `where` parameter is NOT listed among the PO optional parameters (unlike Invoices) — where-clause support on POs: UNVERIFIED.
- Pagination: "When you retrieve multiple purchase orders, paging is enforced by default. 100 purchase orders are returned per page." `page` alone = 100 per call; `pageSize`: "Up to 1000 purchase orders will be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used e.g. page=1&pageSize=250."
- High volume threshold: >100k POs in response denied; unoptimised filter/order resulting in >100k denied with 400.

---

## 4. Payments (paying an ACCPAY bill)

Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [PAY]

### Exact fields to pay an ACCPAY invoice — PUT Payments [PAY]

Minimum (official single-payment example):

\```json
{
  "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
  "Date": "2009-09-08",
  "Amount": 32.06
}
\```

- Identifier: provide exactly ONE identifier object (Invoice, CreditNote, Prepayment, or Overpayment) — for bills, `Invoice` with `InvoiceID` (an example also identifies by `InvoiceNumber`). [PAY]
- Account: either `AccountID` or `Code` ("note: not all accounts have a code value"). [PAY]
- Date: "Date the payment is being made (YYYY-MM-DD)". [PAY]
- Amount: "The amount of the payment in the currency of the Invoice... Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice". [PAY]
- Optional: Reference, IsReconciled (boolean; useful for conversion scenarios), BankAmount, CurrencyRate, Status. [PAY]
- Envelope: single object, or `{"Payments": [ ... ]}` for multiple. `?SummarizeErrors=false` returns per-payment ValidationErrors. [PAY]
- Batch alternative: "To pay multiple AR or AP invoices in a single transaction use the BatchPayments endpoint." [PAY]

### Account prerequisites [PAY]

Verbatim: the account "needs to be either an account of type BANK or have enable payments to this accounts switched on (see GET Accounts). See the edit account screen of your Chart of Accounts in Xero if you wish to enable payments for an account other than a bank account." (The flag name `EnablePaymentsToAccount` lives on the Accounts endpoint — the exact property name is not printed on the Payments page: field name UNVERIFIED here, requirement itself CONFIRMED.)

### Partial payments [PAY][INV]

- Amount may be any value "less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing" — partial payments are allowed. [PAY]
- Invoice status stays AUTHORISED while partially paid: AUTHORISED = "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" [TYP]; "Once an invoice is fully paid the system will move it to the PAID status." [INV]

### Required invoice status [PAY][INV]

- PUT Payments is for "approved AR and AP invoices" ("Apply payments to approved AR and AP invoices"). [PAY]
- Invoices page: only at AUTHORISED — "You can now apply payments to the invoice." DRAFT/SUBMITTED create no journals and cannot take payments. [INV]

### Documented rejection/validation causes

- Amount greater than outstanding amount owing (violates the documented Amount constraint). [PAY]
- Account not of type BANK and not payments-enabled (violates documented account prerequisite). [PAY]
- "A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect" (also for calculated rate from BankAmount). [PAY]
- Per-item `ValidationErrors` surfaced via SummarizeErrors=false; examples elsewhere show messages like "Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR" and "A Contact must be specified for this type of transaction" (from the PO page's example). [PAY][PO]
- An explicit list of payment rejection error messages is NOT published on the Payments page — UNVERIFIED beyond the above.

### Delete / reverse a payment [PAY]

- "Payments cannot be modified, only created and deleted."
- POST Payments = "delete (reverse) payments". POST to the resource ID: `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` with body `{ "Status": "DELETED" }`.
- Exclusion: "payments created via batch payments and receipts are not supported" for this delete method.
- Payment Status Codes: `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`. [TYP]

### Multicurrency / CurrencyRate [PAY]

- "A payment is multicurrency if the payment account, or the invoice paid against, are in a currency other than the base currency of the organisation."
- `Amount` is in the INVOICE currency; `BankAmount` is "the amount being paid in the currency of the account. When this is set, Xero will calculate the appropriate CurrencyRate. If you know this amount, specifying it is preferred to setting CurrencyRate. BankAmount is currently only supported for payments of AR and AP invoices."
- `CurrencyRate`: "Exchange rate when payment is received... For non-multicurrency payments, has a value of 1."

---

## 5. From the Types page [TYP]

### Invoice Types
| `ACCPAY` | "A bill – commonly known as an Accounts Payable or supplier invoice" |
| `ACCREC` | "A sales invoice – commonly known as an Accounts Receivable or customer invoice" |

### Invoice Status Codes (verbatim)
| DRAFT | "A Draft Invoice (default)" |
| SUBMITTED | "An Awaiting Approval Invoice" |
| DELETED | "A Deleted Invoice" |
| AUTHORISED | "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" |
| PAID | "An Invoice that is completely Paid" |
| VOIDED | "A Voided Invoice" |

### Payment Types (verbatim)
| ACCRECPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Payment |
| ACCPAYPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Payment |
| ARCREDITPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Credit Payment (Refund) |
| APCREDITPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Credit Payment (Refund) |
| AROVERPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Overpayment Payment (Refund) |
| ARPREPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Prepayment Payment (Refund) |
| APPREPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Prepayment Payment (Refund) |
| APOVERPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Overpayment Payment (Refund) |

### Payment Status Codes
`AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`.

### Purchase Order Statuses
`DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED` (no descriptions or transition rules given on the Types page).

### Currency handling on Types page
The Types page contains NO section on currency codes or CurrencyRate. All CurrencyRate/multicurrency facts above come from the Payments/Invoices/PO pages ([PAY][INV][PO]). Anything about supported currency code lists: UNVERIFIED on these four pages.

---

## Cross-cutting notes for the match/pay engine

- Idempotency check-by-reference: for ACCPAY, `InvoiceNumber` is NON-unique [INV], so existence checks must combine fields (e.g. Contact + InvoiceNumber + Total) or store InvoiceID after create.
- Correct bill-payment flow per docs: create bill (POST, Type=ACCPAY, ContactID, LineItems) -> transition DRAFT/SUBMITTED -> AUTHORISED (POST status update) -> PUT Payments (Invoice.InvoiceID, Account.Code|AccountID, Date, Amount) -> Xero flips to PAID when fully paid. Reverse a payment via POST /Payments/{PaymentID} {"Status":"DELETED"}; void an unpaid approved bill via Status=VOIDED. [INV][PAY]

PROJECT STACK AND HARD RULES (non-negotiable):
- Backend is NestJS + TypeScript (strict). HTTP approach for ALL snippets: plain fetch (native fetch, Node 18+). Do NOT use xero-node SDK in snippets. Be consistent.
- No top-level await in snippets (they must compile under module=commonjs): wrap in "async function main()" + "main().catch(...)" or export async functions.
- Env vars: XERO_CLIENT_ID, XERO_CLIENT_SECRET (no tenant id needed for custom connections — but state WHY in the skill).
- Secrets live in .env at repo root, never committed, never logged. Snippets assert env non-empty at start with named errors (e.g. "MissingEnv: XERO_CLIENT_ID is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client").
- Skills contain FACTS + RUNNABLE SNIPPETS only. Zero business logic, zero match/approval decisions — those live in api/src/modules/. If you catch yourself writing a decision, delete it.
- Runnable multi-line snippets that matter go in real files: <skill-dir>/scripts/<name>.ts, referenced from SKILL.md. Short illustrative fragments can stay inline in SKILL.md fenced blocks.
- Every .ts file must compile standalone under: strict=true, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, esModuleInterop, types=["node"]. Imports only from node builtins (fs, path, process) — no npm deps.

SKILL FORMAT (match Xero's own prompt-library discipline — read undefined/style.md FIRST):
- Directory: one skill dir with SKILL.md at root. Frontmatter: exactly "name:" and "description:". The description MUST state WHEN to trigger ("Use when ...").
- Section order: pre-flight checks FIRST (env/scopes/org assertions before any code), then the factual body, then snippets, then a "## Traps" section near the end, then an error->real-cause table.
- Terse and factual. Tables over prose for enumerable facts. No filler, no marketing, no "simply".
- Every fact you cannot verify from the fact sheets in undefined/ or from the cloned repos in undefined/ gets the literal marker: UNVERIFIED — check before relying.

When to use the Xero MCP server vs the raw Accounting API in THIS project. Reproduce this decision table from architecture.md verbatim as the anchor:
| Call | Via | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |
Then: how to run the MCP server locally against our custom-connection env vars (exact command + env, from the mcp.md fact sheet), and the tool-name -> handler mapping for ONLY the ~10 tools this project actually uses (exact registered tool names, their key params, handler file paths). State explicitly and loudly: NO purchase order tools, NO attachment tools, NO history tools exist in the MCP server — those route to the xero-accounting-api skill. Note any param limitations of the MCP tools we use (e.g. what list-invoices can/cannot filter) so nobody discovers them mid-build.
Traps: e.g. MCP update-invoice capabilities vs status flips (what the handler actually supports — quote from mcp.md), rate limits shared with raw API calls (same 60/min tenant budget).

SKILL: xero-demo-seed (dir: undefined/xero-demo-seed/)
Seeding the demo Xero org so the data tells a story. This skill is the SPEC the seed/ scripts will implement — facts and shapes only, the actual seeding code lives in seed/ later.
Cover: (1) the cast: ~15 realistic UK trade suppliers (name them: timber merchants, plumbers' merchants, electrical wholesalers, plant hire, skip hire, builders' merchants — plausible names like "Thameside Timber Ltd", with plausible amounts £80-£8,000 and UK VAT 20% via LineAmountTypes Exclusive); (2) the story: most bills fully matchable, exactly 2 deliberate gaps — one bill with NO goods receipt (the demo star: Thameside Timber, £4,200), one bill with an amount mismatch vs its PO (outside the ±2%/£5 tolerance); (3) matching POs raised in Xero via raw API (AUTHORISED status so they read as real orders) with PO numbers the bills reference; (4) the Google Sheet receipt log schema — columns exactly: date, po_ref, supplier, items, qty, signed_by, photo_link — with 3 example rows that join to the seeded POs; (5) idempotency: seed safe to re-run — check-by-reference before every create (InvoiceNumber for bills, PurchaseOrderNumber for POs, Contact Name for contacts), never duplicate; (6) ordering: contacts -> POs -> bills -> sheet rows, and why (referential joins).
Include the exact where-filters used for each check-by-reference lookup. Include a table of the full 15-supplier cast with PO number, bill number, amount, and match-state so the seed script is a transcription job.
Traps: re-running with changed amounts creates near-duplicates (check by reference not by amount); Xero auto-uppercases some references UNVERIFIED — check before relying (verify or drop); deleting seeded data (bills can only be voided/deleted depending on status).
