```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
//
// Gate G0 — proves the Xero Custom Connection works end to end, before any
// feature code is written. Run from repo root:
//
//   npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
//
// What it does:
//   1. Parses <repo-root>/.env itself (no dotenv dep); vars already in
//      process.env win over the file.
//   2. Asserts XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named errors —
//      an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`).
//   3. Fetches a client_credentials token from identity.xero.com.
//      On 400 `invalid_scope` (granular-only app) it retries once with the
//      granular equivalents of the project's broad scopes.
//   4. GETs the connected organisation (name via GET /connections — our
//      scopes deliberately exclude accounting.settings, so GET /Organisation
//      would 403) and 1 invoice.
//   5. Prints org name, invoice count, and the rate-limit headers.
//
// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Compiles standalone under
// strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].

import * as fs from "node:fs";
import * as path from "node:path";

const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
const INVOICES_URL = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1&pageSize=1";

// The exact scopes this project needs — nothing more. See SKILL.md.
const PROJECT_SCOPES =
  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";

// Granular equivalents (post-29-April-2026 custom connections may reject the
// broad scopes with 400 invalid_scope). Mapping per Xero's scope migration:
// accounting.transactions -> invoices+payments+banktransactions+manualjournals,
// accounting.reports.read -> the eight accounting.reports.*.read scopes.
const GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES = [
  "accounting.invoices",
  "accounting.payments",
  "accounting.banktransactions",
  "accounting.manualjournals",
  "accounting.contacts",
  "accounting.attachments",
  "accounting.reports.aged.read",
  "accounting.reports.balancesheet.read",
  "accounting.reports.banksummary.read",
  "accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read",
  "accounting.reports.executivesummary.read",
  "accounting.reports.profitandloss.read",
  "accounting.reports.trialbalance.read",
  "accounting.reports.taxreports.read",
].join(" ");

class MissingEnvError extends Error {
  constructor(varName: string) {
    super(
      `${varName} is empty — set it in .env at repo root. ` +
        `An empty client id surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client, which points away from the real cause.`,
    );
    this.name = "MissingEnv";
  }
}

class XeroTokenError extends Error {
  constructor(
    public readonly status: number,
    public readonly body: string,
  ) {
    super(`token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
  }
}

class XeroApiError extends Error {
  constructor(
    public readonly status: number,
    url: string,
    body: string,
  ) {
    super(`GET ${url} failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
    this.name = "XeroApiError";
  }
}

// --- 1. Tiny .env parser (existing process.env wins; missing file tolerated) ---

function loadDotEnv(envPath: string): void {
  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
    console.warn(`[verify-auth] no .env at ${envPath} — relying on process.env only`);
    return;
  }
  for (const rawLine of fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const line = rawLine.trim().replace(/^export\s+/, "");
    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
    if (eq <= 0) continue;
    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
    if (
      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"') && value.length >= 2) ||
      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'") && value.length >= 2)
    ) {
      value = value.slice(1, -1);
    }
    const existing = process.env[key];
    if (existing === undefined || existing === "") {
      process.env[key] = value;
    }
  }
}

// --- 2. Named env assertions (fail fast, never log the value itself) ---

function requireEnv(name: string): string {
  const v = process.env[name];
  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
    throw new MissingEnvError(name);
  }
  return v.trim();
}

// --- 3. client_credentials token (Basic auth + form-encoded body, never JSON) ---

interface TokenResult {
  accessToken: string;
  expiresIn: number;
  scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" | "granular fallback";
}

async function requestToken(
  clientId: string,
  clientSecret: string,
  scope: string,
): Promise<{ accessToken: string; expiresIn: number }> {
  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
      Accept: "application/json",
    },
    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
  });
  const text = await res.text();
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
  }
  const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
  if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
  }
  return {
    accessToken: parsed.access_token,
    expiresIn: typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0,
  };
}

async function getTokenWithFallback(clientId: string, clientSecret: string): Promise<TokenResult> {
  const scopeOverride = process.env["XERO_SCOPES"];
  const scopes =
    scopeOverride !== undefined && scopeOverride.trim() !== "" ? scopeOverride.trim() : PROJECT_SCOPES;
  try {
    const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
    return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" };
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof XeroTokenError && err.status === 400 && err.body.includes("invalid_scope")) {
      console.warn(
        "[verify-auth] broad scopes rejected (invalid_scope) — this app only has granular scopes. " +
          "Retrying with granular equivalents. Set XERO_SCOPES in .env to the granular set to silence this.",
      );
      const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES);
      return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "granular fallback" };
    }
    throw err;
  }
}

// --- 4. Organisation + one invoice ---

interface XeroConnection {
  tenantId?: string;
  tenantName?: string;
}

async function getJson(url: string, accessToken: string): Promise<{ body: unknown; res: Response }> {
  // Per the custom-connections docs, API calls need exactly these two headers.
  // No xero-tenant-id: a custom connection is bound to a single organisation.
  const res = await fetch(url, {
    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, Accept: "application/json" },
  });
  const text = await res.text();
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new XeroApiError(res.status, url, text);
  }
  return { body: JSON.parse(text) as unknown, res };
}

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  loadDotEnv(path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"));

  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
  // Log lengths only — never the values.
  console.log(
    `[verify-auth] env ok (client_id_len: ${clientId.length}, client_secret_len: ${clientSecret.length})`,
  );

  const token = await getTokenWithFallback(clientId, clientSecret);
  console.log(
    `[verify-auth] token ok — scope set: ${token.scopeSetUsed}, expires_in: ${token.expiresIn}s`,
  );

  const { body: connectionsBody } = await getJson(CONNECTIONS_URL, token.accessToken);
  const connections = Array.isArray(connectionsBody) ? (connectionsBody as XeroConnection[]) : [];
  if (connections.length === 0) {
    throw new XeroApiError(
      200,
      CONNECTIONS_URL,
      "token issued but no organisation is connected. Has a Xero org admin authorised this " +
        "Custom Connection app in the developer portal? Tokens are useless until that is done.",
    );
  }
  const first = connections[0];
  const orgName = first?.tenantName ?? first?.tenantId ?? "(unnamed connection)";
  console.log(`[verify-auth] organisation: ${orgName}`);

  const { body: invoicesBody, res: invoicesRes } = await getJson(INVOICES_URL, token.accessToken);
  const inv = invoicesBody as { Invoices?: unknown[]; pagination?: { itemCount?: unknown } };
  const returned = Array.isArray(inv.Invoices) ? inv.Invoices.length : 0;
  const itemCount = typeof inv.pagination?.itemCount === "number" ? inv.pagination.itemCount : null;
  console.log(
    `[verify-auth] invoices returned: ${returned}` + (itemCount !== null ? ` (org total: ${itemCount})` : ""),
  );

  console.log(
    "[verify-auth] rate limits remaining — " +
      `minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-MinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
      `day: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-DayLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
      `app-minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-AppMinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}`,
  );
  console.log("[verify-auth] G0 PASS");
}

main().catch((err: unknown) => {
  if (err instanceof Error) {
    console.error(`[verify-auth] FAIL — ${err.name}: ${err.message}`);
  } else {
    console.error("[verify-auth] FAIL —", err);
  }
  console.error("[verify-auth] see the error table in .claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md");
  process.exit(1);
});

```

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-payments/scripts/create-payment.ts
//
// Create a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY invoice via the raw Accounting API.
// Custom connection (client_credentials): the token is bound to exactly ONE Xero
// organisation, so no xero-tenant-id header and no XERO_TENANT_ID env var.
//
// Usage:
//   ts-node create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE]
//   or via env: INVOICE_ID, ACCOUNT_CODE, AMOUNT, PAYMENT_DATE, PAYMENT_REFERENCE
//
// Env (export from .env at repo root before running; never commit, never log):
//   XERO_CLIENT_ID, XERO_CLIENT_SECRET   required
//   XERO_SCOPES                          optional, default "accounting.invoices accounting.payments"
//
// Compiles standalone: strict, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, types=["node"].
// Node 18+ (native fetch). No npm deps.

const XERO_TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
const XERO_API = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";

function requireEnv(name: string): string {
  const v = process.env[name];
  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
    throw new Error(
      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. Set it in .env and retry.`,
    );
  }
  return v;
}

function arg(pos: number, envName: string): string | undefined {
  return process.argv[2 + pos] ?? process.env[envName];
}

interface ValidationError {
  Message?: string;
}
interface ApiException {
  ErrorNumber?: number;
  Type?: string;
  Message?: string;
  Elements?: Array<{ ValidationErrors?: ValidationError[] }>;
}
interface XeroPayment {
  PaymentID?: string;
  Status?: string;
  Amount?: number;
  Date?: string;
  Reference?: string;
}
interface XeroInvoice {
  InvoiceID?: string;
  Type?: string;
  Status?: string;
  AmountDue?: number;
  AmountPaid?: number;
  CurrencyCode?: string;
  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
}
interface InvoiceEnvelope {
  Invoices?: XeroInvoice[];
}
interface PaymentEnvelope {
  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
}

async function getToken(clientId: string, clientSecret: string, scopes: string): Promise<string> {
  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
  const res = await fetch(XERO_TOKEN_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
    },
    // Custom connections take scopes in the BODY (unlike code/PKCE flows, where
    // scopes ride in the authorize URL).
    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope: scopes }).toString(),
  });
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(`TokenError: ${res.status} from identity.xero.com — ${await res.text()}`);
  }
  const json = (await res.json()) as { access_token?: string };
  if (!json.access_token) {
    throw new Error("TokenError: token response contained no access_token");
  }
  return json.access_token;
}

async function getInvoice(token: string, invoiceId: string): Promise<XeroInvoice> {
  // No xero-tenant-id header: a custom connection token is scoped to a single org.
  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" },
  });
  if (res.status === 404) {
    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: no invoice with InvoiceID ${invoiceId}`);
  }
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(`InvoiceFetchError: ${res.status} — ${await res.text()}`);
  }
  const json = (await res.json()) as InvoiceEnvelope;
  const inv = json.Invoices?.[0];
  if (!inv) {
    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: empty Invoices envelope for ${invoiceId}`);
  }
  return inv;
}

function formatRejection(status: number, bodyText: string): string {
  try {
    const api = JSON.parse(bodyText) as ApiException;
    const msgs = (api.Elements ?? [])
      .flatMap((e) => e.ValidationErrors ?? [])
      .map((v) => v.Message)
      .filter((m): m is string => typeof m === "string");
    if (msgs.length > 0) {
      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} ${api.Type ?? ""} — ${msgs.join(" | ")}`;
    }
    if (api.Message) {
      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${api.Message}`;
    }
  } catch {
    // not JSON — fall through to raw body
  }
  return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${bodyText}`;
}

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
  const scopes = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || "accounting.invoices accounting.payments";

  const invoiceId = arg(0, "INVOICE_ID");
  const accountCode = arg(1, "ACCOUNT_CODE");
  const amountRaw = arg(2, "AMOUNT");
  const date = arg(3, "PAYMENT_DATE") ?? new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD
  const reference = arg(4, "PAYMENT_REFERENCE");

  if (!invoiceId || !accountCode || !amountRaw) {
    throw new Error(
      "Usage: create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE] (or set INVOICE_ID/ACCOUNT_CODE/AMOUNT env vars)",
    );
  }
  const amount = Number(amountRaw);
  if (!Number.isFinite(amount) || amount <= 0) {
    throw new Error(`BadAmount: "${amountRaw}" is not a positive number`);
  }

  // Never log the secret — length only.
  console.log("[xero] token request", { client_id_len: clientId.length, scopes });
  const token = await getToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);

  // ---- Pre-flight FACT-CHECK (not business logic) --------------------------
  // These asserts restate Xero's documented constraints so a bad write fails
  // here with a named local error instead of an opaque ValidationException.
  // The decision to pay was made upstream in api/src/modules/ — nothing below
  // decides anything.
  const inv = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);

  if (inv.Status !== "AUTHORISED") {
    throw new Error(
      `InvoiceNotAuthorised: invoice ${invoiceId} has status ${inv.Status ?? "unknown"} — ` +
        `Xero applies payments to AUTHORISED invoices only (DRAFT/SUBMITTED create no journals). ` +
        `Approve it first: POST /Invoices/${invoiceId} {"Status":"AUTHORISED"}.`,
    );
  }
  if (typeof inv.AmountDue !== "number" || inv.AmountDue < amount) {
    throw new Error(
      `AmountExceedsAmountDue: AmountDue is ${inv.AmountDue ?? "unknown"}, requested ${amount} — ` +
        `Amount must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice.`,
    );
  }

  // Idempotency check-by-reference: this project requires every Xero write to
  // check for an existing record before creating. Best-effort here via the
  // invoice's Payments array; the durable record is PaymentID persisted in
  // Postgres by the caller. Pass a unique REFERENCE to make reruns no-ops.
  if (reference) {
    const existing = (inv.Payments ?? []).find(
      (p) => p.Reference === reference && p.Status !== "DELETED",
    );
    if (existing) {
      console.log(
        `[xero] payment already exists — idempotent skip. PaymentID=${existing.PaymentID ?? "?"} Reference=${reference}`,
      );
      console.log(`[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${inv.Status} AmountDue=${inv.AmountDue}`);
      return;
    }
  }

  // ---- The write. PUT /Payments creates; POST /Payments/{id} deletes. ------
  const payment = {
    Invoice: { InvoiceID: invoiceId },
    Account: { Code: accountCode },
    Date: date,
    Amount: amount,
    ...(reference ? { Reference: reference } : {}),
  };
  // Deterministic Idempotency-Key: identical retries within Xero's 6-minute
  // cache window return the cached response instead of double-paying. This is
  // a retry guard only — the durable guarantee is the check above + Postgres.
  const idempotencyKey = `pay:${invoiceId}:${accountCode}:${amount}:${date}:${reference ?? ""}`.slice(0, 128);

  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Payments`, {
    method: "PUT",
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
      Accept: "application/json",
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "Idempotency-Key": idempotencyKey,
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(payment),
  });
  const bodyText = await res.text();
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(formatRejection(res.status, bodyText));
  }

  const created = (JSON.parse(bodyText) as PaymentEnvelope).Payments?.[0];
  if (!created?.PaymentID) {
    throw new Error(`PaymentParseError: 2xx response but no PaymentID — body: ${bodyText}`);
  }
  console.log(
    `[xero] created PaymentID=${created.PaymentID} Status=${created.Status ?? "?"} Amount=${created.Amount ?? amount}`,
  );

  // Re-read the invoice: AmountDue drops by Amount; Status flips to PAID only
  // when fully paid (system-set), stays AUTHORISED while partially paid.
  const after = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);
  console.log(
    `[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${after.Status ?? "?"} AmountDue=${after.AmountDue ?? "?"} AmountPaid=${after.AmountPaid ?? "?"}`,
  );
}

main().catch((err: unknown) => {
  console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
  process.exitCode = 1;
});

export {};

```

Terse and factual, no filler. If you cannot verify a fact from the cloned repos or official docs, mark it `UNVERIFIED — check before relying`.

27	### `xero-auth`

Include a `verify-auth.ts` snippet that lists 1 invoice and prints the org name — this is Gate G0.

31	The endpoints this project touches, with request/response shapes trimmed to fields we use:

33	- **Purchase Orders: NOT in the MCP server — raw API only.** Full CRUD shapes, status flow, marking billed.

```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/token-cache.ts
//
// In-memory client_credentials token cache for a Xero Custom Connection.
// Copy into api/src/modules/xero/ and register as a NestJS provider via
// useFactory (see SKILL.md) — this file itself has zero framework imports so
// it compiles standalone under strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
//
// Why this exists: Xero's own MCP server requests a brand-new token (plus a
// GET /connections) on EVERY call — two wasted round-trips per call, all
// counted against the 60/min tenant rate limit. Do the opposite:
//   - cache the token in memory until expiry minus a 60s buffer
//     (pattern: xero-command-line TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000),
//   - single-flight concurrent token requests,
//   - on 401 invalidate + fetch a fresh token + retry exactly once
//     (pattern: xero-command-line withRetry).
// client_credentials issues NO refresh_token (response fields: access_token,
// expires_in, token_type, scope) — "refresh" simply means asking again.

const XERO_TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";

/** Refresh this long before expiry, so in-flight calls never race the deadline. */
const EXPIRY_BUFFER_MS = 60_000;

export class MissingCredentialError extends Error {
  constructor(name: string) {
    super(
      `${name} is empty — an empty client id surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client, ` +
        `which points away from the real cause. Set it in .env and fail at boot, not mid-request.`,
    );
    this.name = "MissingCredential";
  }
}

export class XeroTokenError extends Error {
  constructor(
    public readonly status: number,
    public readonly body: string,
  ) {
    super(`Xero token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
  }
}

export interface XeroTokenCacheConfig {
  clientId: string;
  clientSecret: string;
  /** Space-separated scope list, sent in the token request body. */
  scope: string;
}

export class XeroTokenCache {
  private accessToken: string | null = null;
  private expiresAtMs = 0;
  private inflight: Promise<string> | null = null;

  constructor(private readonly config: XeroTokenCacheConfig) {
    if (config.clientId.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
    if (config.clientSecret.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
    if (config.scope.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("scope");
  }

  /** Returns a valid access token, reusing the cached one when fresh. */
  async getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
    if (this.accessToken !== null && Date.now() < this.expiresAtMs - EXPIRY_BUFFER_MS) {
      return this.accessToken;
    }
    // Single-flight: concurrent callers share one token request instead of
    // each burning a round-trip against the rate limit.
    if (this.inflight === null) {
      this.inflight = this.requestToken().finally(() => {
        this.inflight = null;
      });
    }
    return this.inflight;
  }

  /** Drop the cached token (call on a 401 before retrying). */
  invalidate(): void {
    this.accessToken = null;
    this.expiresAtMs = 0;
  }

  private async requestToken(): Promise<string> {
    const basic = Buffer.from(`${this.config.clientId}:${this.config.clientSecret}`).toString(
      "base64",
    );
    const res = await fetch(XERO_TOKEN_URL, {
      method: "POST",
      headers: {
        Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
        // Form-encoded, NEVER JSON — the token endpoint is not a JSON API.
        "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
        Accept: "application/json",
      },
      body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(this.config.scope)}`,
    });
    const text = await res.text();
    if (!res.ok) {
      // Body carries Xero's OAuth error (unauthorized_client, invalid_scope, ...)
      // and never contains the secret, so it is safe to surface.
      throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
    }
    const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
    if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
      throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
    }
    const expiresInSec = typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0;
    this.accessToken = parsed.access_token;
    this.expiresAtMs = Date.now() + expiresInSec * 1000;
    return this.accessToken;
  }
}

export interface XeroFetchInit {
  method?: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
  body?: string;
}

/**
 * fetch against the Xero API with the cached token; on 401, invalidate,
 * fetch a fresh token, and retry exactly once. 403 is NOT retried — that is
 * a missing scope, and the same token can never succeed.
 *
 * No xero-tenant-id header: a Custom Connection is bound to a single
 * organisation and the documented call sends only Authorization + Accept.
 */
export async function xeroFetch(
  cache: XeroTokenCache,
  url: string,
  init?: XeroFetchInit,
): Promise<Response> {
  const doFetch = async (): Promise<Response> => {
    const token = await cache.getAccessToken();
    return fetch(url, {
      method: init?.method ?? "GET",
      headers: {
        Accept: "application/json",
        ...(init?.headers ?? {}),
        Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
      },
      body: init?.body,
    });
  };

  const first = await doFetch();
  if (first.status !== 401) {
    return first;
  }
  cache.invalidate();
  return doFetch();
}

```

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-mcp-usage
3	description: Decide whether a Xero call in THIS project goes through the Xero MCP server (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server) or the raw Accounting API. Use when wiring any Xero read or write, when launching the MCP server against our Custom Connection env (XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET), or when an MCP tool seems to be missing. Covers the exact registered tool names and params for the ~10 tools this project uses, their hard limitations (list-invoices cannot filter by status, update-invoice cannot flip status, no idempotency on writes), and the confirmed MCP gaps — Purchase Orders, Attachments, and History have NO MCP tools and route to the xero-accounting-api skill. Read this BEFORE assuming an MCP tool exists.
4	---
5	
6	# Xero MCP server vs raw Accounting API — routing for this project
7	
8	The MCP server (`@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server`, facts verified against **v0.0.16**) covers invoices, contacts, payments, and reports. It does NOT cover Purchase Orders, Attachments, or History — those go through the raw Accounting API (`.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`). This skill is the routing table plus the exact capabilities and limits of the MCP tools we use. Tool inventory in any version newer than 0.0.16: UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are set and non-empty.** Empty secrets surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. The MCP server boot-asserts this itself (`xero-client.ts:19-21`, throws unless `bearer_token || (client_id && client_secret)`), but assert in our code first — the message is better.
13	2. **There is no tenant-id env var, on purpose.** In client_credentials (Custom Connection) mode the MCP server fetches a token from `identity.xero.com/connect/token`, then calls `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` and silently uses `connections[0].tenantId`. No selection mechanism exists. So the pre-flight invariant is: **exactly ONE org authorised on this Custom Connection**, or writes can land in the wrong books.
14	3. **Set `XERO_SCOPES` explicitly in `.env`** to the scopes assigned to the Custom Connection app (see `.env.example` at repo root). If unset, the MCP server requests its own hardcoded legacy V1 set (including `payroll.*`) and only falls back to a granular V2 set on an `invalid_scope` HTTP 400. Whether a scope request that is only partially assigned to a custom connection fails with `invalid_scope` or returns a narrower token: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Setting `XERO_SCOPES` verbatim to the app's assigned scopes sidesteps the question.
15	4. **Do not set `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN`** unless you mean it — it takes precedence over client id/secret and switches the server to bearer mode.
16	5. **Run the smoke test before wiring anything to the MCP server:**
17	
18	```sh
19	npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
20	```
21	
22	It performs the exact same token grant + `/connections` call the MCP server does on every tool invocation, prints the tenant the server will silently pick, and fails with a named error (`MissingEnv`, `TokenGrantFailed`, `NoConnections`) instead of the misleading upstream one.
23	
24	## The routing table (from `context/architecture.md` — verbatim, the pitch anchor)
25	
26	| Call | Via | Purpose |
27	|---|---|---|
28	| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
29	| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
30	| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
31	| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
32	| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
33	| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
34	| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
35	| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |
36	
37	> ⚠️ **One row of this table is wrong and will burn you: "approve bill (status update) | MCP tool".** Verified against the MCP source: `update-invoice` has **no `status` parameter** and its handler hard-blocks anything that is not a draft — `if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) { return { isError: true, error: "Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ..." } }` (`update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84`). The `Invoice` object it sends contains only lineItems/reference/dueDate/date/contact — no status field. **The MCP server cannot move an invoice to AUTHORISED (or any status).** Build the approve path on the raw Accounting API: `POST /Invoices` with `"Status": "AUTHORISED"` — see `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`. Keep the table verbatim for the pitch; route the code correctly.
38	
39	## Confirmed ABSENT from the MCP server — do not go looking
40	
41	Verified by grep over `xero-mcp-server/src/` (all 51 registered tools inventoried):
42	
43	- **NO Purchase Order tools.** No tool name contains "purchase". The only hits are item `purchaseDetails` fields and the phrase "purchase invoices" in the create-invoice description.
44	- **NO Attachment tools.** Only read-only `hasAttachments` display flags. No upload/download/list.
45	- **NO History/Notes tools.** No `getInvoiceHistory` / `createHistoryRecord` anywhere.
46	- Also absent: get-invoice-by-id, delete/void invoice, email-invoice, batch payments.
47	
48	All of those route to the raw Accounting API → `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`. If an MCP tool for them appears to exist, you are hallucinating it.
49	
50	## Running the MCP server locally (Custom Connection)
51	
52	- **Command:** `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` (or a local build: `node dist/index.js`). Transport is **stdio only** — no port, no HTTP; the MCP client spawns it as a child process.
53	- **Env consumed** (`src/clients/xero-client.ts:14-16`): `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, optional `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` (takes precedence), optional `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated). The server calls `dotenv.config()` at boot, so launching it with cwd = repo root picks up our `.env` directly:
54	
55	```sh
56	# from repo root — .env supplies XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET / XERO_SCOPES
57	npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest
58	```
59	
60	If the spawning MCP client controls cwd, pass the three vars through its `env` block instead — values injected from the environment, never literals in a committed config file.
61	
62	- **Auth per call, not per boot:** every tool handler starts with `authenticate()`, which requests a **brand-new** client_credentials token plus a fresh `GET /connections` on every single tool call — 2 extra HTTP round-trips per call, no caching, no expiry check. Budget for the latency; never loop tool calls.
63	- **Token grant shape** (what the smoke-test script reproduces): `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`, HTTP **Basic** auth `base64(clientId:clientSecret)` — not body params — with form-encoded body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<urlencoded>`.
64	
65	## The 10 tools this project uses
66	
67	Exact registered names, key params, handler files (paths under `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/`).
68	
69	| Tool | Key params | Handler | Project use |
70	|---|---|---|---|
71	| `list-invoices` | `page: number` **required**; `contactIds?: string[]`; `invoiceNumbers?: string[]` (supplying invoiceNumbers also returns line items) | list-xero-invoices.handler.ts | ingest ACCPAY bills already in Xero |
72	| `create-invoice` | `contactId: string`; `lineItems[]` (`description`, `quantity`, `unitAmount`, `accountCode`, `taxType`, `itemCode?`, `tracking?`); `type: "ACCREC" \| "ACCPAY"` — **pass `"ACCPAY"`, default is ACCREC**; `reference?`; `date?` (YYYY-MM-DD) | create-xero-invoice.handler.ts | bills arriving by email; always created **DRAFT** |
73	| `update-invoice` | `invoiceId` required; `lineItems?`, `reference?`, `dueDate?`, `date?`, `contactId?`. **No `status` param.** | update-xero-invoice.handler.ts | fix a DRAFT bill before approval — nothing else |
74	| `create-payment` | `invoiceId`, `accountId`, `amount` (positive) required; `date?` (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults today), `reference?` | create-xero-payment.handler.ts | the money moment |
75	| `list-payments` | `page` (default 1); `invoiceNumber?`, `invoiceId?`, `paymentId?`, `reference?` | list-xero-payments.handler.ts | check-by-reference before create-payment |
76	| `list-aged-payables-by-contact` | `contactId` required; `reportDate?` (defaults end of current month), `invoicesFromDate?`, `invoicesToDate?` (all YYYY-MM-DD) | list-aged-payables-by-contact.handler.ts | dashboard context strip |
77	| `list-contacts` | `page?`; `searchTerm?` (case-insensitive over Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, EmailAddress) | list-xero-contacts.handler.ts | resolve supplier name → `contactId` |
78	| `create-contact` | `name` required; `email?` (must be valid email), `phone?` | create-xero-contact.handler.ts | new supplier arriving by email |
79	| `list-accounts` | none | list-xero-accounts.handler.ts | `accountCode` for invoice lines, `accountId` for payments |
80	| `list-organisation-details` | none | list-xero-organisation-details.handler.ts | org sanity check |
81	
82	## Param limitations — know these before building
83	
84	- **`list-invoices` cannot filter by status, date, or where-clause.** The handler hardcodes `where: undefined`, `statuses: undefined`, `order: "UpdatedDateUTC DESC"`, `includeArchived: false`, `summaryOnly: false`, and **`pageSize: 10`**. You cannot ask MCP for "all AUTHORISED bills" or "bills since Tuesday" — page through and filter in our code, or use the raw API.
85	- **`list-invoices` is also the only "get one invoice" path** (no get-by-id tool): pass `invoiceNumbers: ["INV-123"]` — that variant returns line items. It matches by invoice **number**, not InvoiceID.
86	- **`update-invoice` replaces the whole line set.** All line items must be re-supplied; omitted line items are **removed**. DRAFT invoices only.
87	- **`create-invoice` always creates DRAFT** (handler sets `Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT`) and returns a deep link. Type defaults to ACCREC — always pass `"ACCPAY"` for bills.
88	- **No MCP write is idempotent.** `idempotencyKey` is explicitly passed as `undefined` in createPayment and updateInvoice. Our CLAUDE.md rule (check-by-reference before create) is the only duplicate protection: `list-payments` with `reference`/`invoiceId` before `create-payment`; `list-invoices` with `invoiceNumbers` before `create-invoice`.
89	- **`create-payment` constraints are enforced by Xero, not the tool:** invoice must be AUTHORISED and not fully paid; amount must not exceed amount due. The tool only validates `amount > 0`.
90	- **`list-payments` pages at 10/page** (hardcoded `pageSize: 10` in the handler). `list-contacts` passes `pageSize: undefined` (Xero default; the tool description says 100/page).
91	
92	## Snippet
93	
94	- `scripts/check-mcp-env.ts` — plain-fetch smoke test of the Custom Connection env, reproducing the MCP server's own auth path (token grant with Basic auth + `GET /connections`, first-tenant selection). Run it before wiring the MCP server; run it again whenever auth errors appear mid-build.
95	
96	## Traps
97	
98	- **Status flips are not an MCP capability.** See the warning under the routing table. `update-invoice` has no `status` param and hard-blocks non-DRAFT invoices. DRAFT → AUTHORISED goes through the raw Accounting API, full stop.
99	- **Rate limits are shared.** Every Accounting API call the MCP server makes consumes the SAME per-tenant budget (60 calls/min, 5,000/day) as our raw-fetch calls from `api/src/modules/xero/`. The MCP server has **zero** rate-limit handling — no retry, no backoff, no `Retry-After` read; its only 429 behaviour is a cosmetic error string: `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."` (`format-error.ts:35-36`). Our module's budget accounting must count MCP tool calls too. Whether the `identity.xero.com` token calls and `GET /connections` also count against the Accounting API tenant budget: UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
100	- **Every tool call costs 2 hidden HTTP round-trips** (fresh token + `/connections`) because the server never caches auth. A 5-tool sequence is 15 HTTP calls. Batch reads on our side; never poll via MCP.
101	- **First-tenant roulette.** More than one org authorised on the Custom Connection means the MCP server silently picks `connections[0]` — the wrong-books failure mode. The smoke-test script warns when it sees more than one connection.
102	- **`create-payment` success text lies:** it reports "Invoice created successfully". It created a Payment. Do not string-match success messages; check for the error shape instead.
103	- **`update-invoice` deletes lines you forget.** Re-supply every line item on every update.
104	- **Unset `XERO_SCOPES` triggers scope guessing** (legacy V1 set with `payroll.*` first, granular V2 on `invalid_scope`). Set it explicitly to the app's assigned scopes.
105	- **`@latest` drift.** Everything here is verified against v0.0.16. Pin or re-verify the tool inventory if the version moves.
106	
107	## Error → real cause
108	
109	| Symptom | Real cause |
110	|---|---|
111	| MCP server throws at boot before any tool runs | Neither `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` nor `XERO_CLIENT_ID`+`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` present (boot assert, `xero-client.ts:19-21`) — check `.env` and the spawner's env block |
112	| `unauthorized_client` on token grant | Empty/missing client id or secret (`client_id=undefined` got sent) — run `scripts/check-mcp-env.ts` |
113	| HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` | Requested scope not available to this Custom Connection; happens when `XERO_SCOPES` is unset and the server guesses. Set `XERO_SCOPES` to the app's assigned scopes |
114	| Tool calls fail though the server booted | Token grant works but no org authorised: `GET /connections` returned 0 — the org admin has not authorised the Custom Connection |
115	| `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ...` | `update-invoice` hard-blocks non-DRAFT invoices; approve via raw Accounting API instead |
116	| `Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment.` | 429 — shared 60/min tenant budget exhausted (our raw calls count too); MCP does no backoff, ours must |
117	| Writes appear in the wrong Xero org | Multiple orgs on the connection; server silently used `connections[0]` |
118	| "Invoice created successfully" after a payment | Mislabeled success text in `create-payment` — it is a Payment |
119	| Line items vanished after `update-invoice` | Omitted lines are removed; re-supply the full line set |
120	| Duplicate bill/payment after a retry | No idempotency key on MCP writes — check-by-reference (`list-invoices` / `list-payments`) before every create |
121	| An MCP tool for POs/Attachments/History "should exist" but does not | It does not. Route to `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api` |
122

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
2	//
3	// Smoke-test the Custom Connection env EXACTLY the way the Xero MCP server
4	// (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server v0.0.16) consumes it on every tool call:
5	//   1. POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token  (client_credentials,
6	//      HTTP Basic base64(clientId:clientSecret), form-encoded body)
7	//   2. GET  https://api.xero.com/connections          (server uses connections[0])
8	//
9	// Run from repo root:  npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
10	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Never logs secret values.
11	
12	import * as fs from "node:fs";
13	import * as path from "node:path";
14	// `process` is the Node global (typed via types:["node"]) — importing the
15	// namespace would make `process.exitCode` a read-only binding under strict TS.
16	
17	// Default = the scopes .env.example says the Custom Connection app was created
18	// with. Override with XERO_SCOPES in .env (recommended: always set it, so the
19	// MCP server skips its own scope-guessing fallback).
20	const DEFAULT_SCOPES =
21	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
22	
23	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
24	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
25	
26	interface TokenResponse {
27	  access_token: string;
28	  expires_in: number;
29	  token_type: string;
30	}
31	
32	interface XeroConnection {
33	  tenantId: string;
34	  tenantName?: string;
35	  tenantType?: string;
36	}
37	
38	// Minimal .env loader (KEY=VALUE lines, # comments) — no dotenv dependency.
39	// Only fills vars that are not already set in the process environment.
40	function loadDotEnv(): void {
41	  const candidates = [
42	    path.resolve(process.cwd(), ".env"),
43	    path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"),
44	  ];
45	  const envPath = candidates.find((p) => fs.existsSync(p));
46	  if (!envPath) {
47	    return; // vars may come from the shell; requireEnv() decides below
48	  }
49	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split("\n");
50	  for (const rawLine of lines) {
51	    const line = rawLine.trim();
52	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
53	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
54	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
55	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
56	    const value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
57	    if (process.env[key] === undefined || process.env[key] === "") {
58	      process.env[key] = value;
59	    }
60	  }
61	  console.log(`[check-mcp-env] loaded ${envPath}`);
62	}
63	
64	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
65	  const value = process.env[name];
66	  if (value === undefined || value.trim() === "") {
67	    throw new Error(
68	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. ` +
69	        `Set it in .env at the repo root (see .env.example) and retry.`,
70	    );
71	  }
72	  return value.trim();
73	}
74	
75	async function main(): Promise<void> {
76	  loadDotEnv();
77	
78	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
79	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
80	
81	  const rawScopes = process.env.XERO_SCOPES;
82	  const scope =
83	    rawScopes !== undefined && rawScopes.trim() !== "" ? rawScopes.trim() : DEFAULT_SCOPES;
84	
85	  if (rawScopes === undefined || rawScopes.trim() === "") {
86	    console.warn(
87	      "[check-mcp-env] XERO_SCOPES is not set. This script defaults to the .env.example scope set, " +
88	        "but the MCP server would GUESS (legacy V1 set incl. payroll.*, then granular V2 on invalid_scope). " +
89	        "Set XERO_SCOPES explicitly to the app's assigned scopes.",
90	    );
91	  }
92	
93	  if (process.env.XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN !== undefined && process.env.XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN !== "") {
94	    console.warn(
95	      "[check-mcp-env] XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN is set — the MCP server would use bearer mode and " +
96	        "IGNORE XERO_CLIENT_ID/XERO_CLIENT_SECRET. Unset it unless that is intended.",
97	    );
98	  }
99	
100	  // Log lengths, never values.
101	  console.log("[check-mcp-env] token request", {
102	    client_id_len: clientId.length,
103	    client_secret_len: clientSecret.length,
104	    scope,
105	  });
106	
107	  // 1. client_credentials grant — Basic auth header, form-encoded body.
108	  const credentials = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
109	  const tokenRes = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
110	    method: "POST",
111	    headers: {
112	      Authorization: `Basic ${credentials}`,
113	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
114	      Accept: "application/json",
115	    },
116	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
117	  });
118	
119	  if (!tokenRes.ok) {
120	    const body = await tokenRes.text();
121	    throw new Error(
122	      `TokenGrantFailed: HTTP ${tokenRes.status} from ${TOKEN_URL} — body: ${body}. ` +
123	        `unauthorized_client = wrong/empty client id or secret; ` +
124	        `invalid_scope = a requested scope is not assigned to the Custom Connection app.`,
125	    );
126	  }
127	
128	  const token = (await tokenRes.json()) as TokenResponse;
129	  console.log(
130	    `[check-mcp-env] token grant OK (token_type=${token.token_type}, expires_in=${token.expires_in}s)`,
131	  );
132	
133	  // 2. GET /connections — the MCP server silently uses connections[0].tenantId.
134	  const connRes = await fetch(CONNECTIONS_URL, {
135	    headers: {
136	      Authorization: `Bearer ${token.access_token}`,
137	      Accept: "application/json",
138	    },
139	  });
140	
141	  if (!connRes.ok) {
142	    const body = await connRes.text();
143	    throw new Error(
144	      `ConnectionsFailed: HTTP ${connRes.status} from ${CONNECTIONS_URL} — body: ${body}.`,
145	    );
146	  }
147	
148	  const connections = (await connRes.json()) as XeroConnection[];
149	
150	  if (connections.length === 0) {
151	    throw new Error(
152	      "NoConnections: token grant succeeded but GET /connections returned 0 orgs — " +
153	        "the org admin has not authorised the Custom Connection yet. " +
154	        "The MCP server would boot, then every tool call would fail.",
155	    );
156	  }
157	
158	  const first = connections[0];
159	  console.log(
160	    `[check-mcp-env] ${connections.length} connection(s). ` +
161	      `MCP server will use connections[0]: tenantId=${first.tenantId} (${first.tenantName ?? "unnamed"})`,
162	  );
163	
164	  if (connections.length > 1) {
165	    console.warn(
166	      "[check-mcp-env] WARNING: more than one org is authorised on this Custom Connection. " +
167	        "The MCP server always uses the FIRST with no selection mechanism — wrong-books risk. " +
168	        "Keep exactly one org authorised.",
169	    );
170	  }
171	
172	  console.log("[check-mcp-env] OK — this env is safe to hand to `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest`.");
173	}
174	
175	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
176	  console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
177	  process.exitCode = 1;
178	});
179
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# Xero MCP Server — Fact Sheet
2	
3	Source: `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server` (XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server, `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server` v0.0.16 — package.json:2-3). All paths below relative to that repo root.
4	
5	## 1. Registered tools (51 total)
6	
7	Registration flow: `src/index.ts:10-11` (`ToolFactory(server)` + `StdioServerTransport`); `src/tools/tool-factory.ts:11-27` registers Delete, Get, Create, List, Update tool arrays via `server.tool(name, description, schema, handler)`. Every tool is built with `CreateXeroTool(name, ...)` (`src/helpers/create-xero-tool.ts:5-17`). Names below are the exact registered strings; citation = where the name string appears; handler = the `src/handlers/*.handler.ts` file the tool delegates to.
8

76	## 2. Confirmed ABSENT
77	
78	Verified by `grep -ri "purchase|attachment|history" src/`:
79	- **No purchase order tool exists.** No tool name contains "purchase". The only "purchase" hits are item `purchaseDetails`/`purchaseDescription` fields (src/tools/create/create-item.tool.ts, src/tools/update/update-item.tool.ts, src/tools/list/list-items.tool.ts) and the word "purchase invoices" in the create-invoice description (src/tools/create/create-invoice.tool.ts). No `getPurchaseOrders`/`createPurchaseOrder` SDK call anywhere.
80	- **No attachment tool exists.** The only "attachment" hits are read-only `hasAttachments` display flags (src/tools/list/list-manual-journals.tool.ts, list-bank-transactions.tool.ts, list-contacts.tool.ts). No upload/download/list-attachments capability.
81	- **No history/notes tool exists.** The only "history" hit is the word "leave history" in a description string (src/tools/list/list-payroll-employee-leave.tool.ts:7). No `getInvoiceHistory`/`createHistoryRecord` calls.
82	- Also absent: no get-invoice-by-id tool, no delete/void tools for invoices, no email-invoice tool, no batch payments.
83	
84	## 3. Running against a Custom Connection (client_credentials)
85	
86	- Env vars read (src/clients/xero-client.ts:14-16): `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN`. Optional `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated, xero-client.ts:129-131). Loaded via `dotenv.config()` (xero-client.ts:12). Boot assert at xero-client.ts:19-21: throws unless `bearer_token || (client_id && client_secret)`.
87	- Mode decision (xero-client.ts:223-231): `bearer_token ? BearerTokenXeroClient : CustomConnectionsXeroClient` — i.e. `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` takes precedence; otherwise client_credentials with hardcoded `grant_type = "client_credentials"` (xero-client.ts:17).
88	- Command: `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` with env vars (README.md:71-76), or local build `node dist/index.js` (README.md:226-230). Transport is stdio only (src/index.ts:14-15).
89	- Tenant id: NOT an env var. Client-credentials mode fetches the token from `https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` then calls `https://api.xero.com/connections` and takes `connections[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:178-190). Bearer mode calls `updateTenants()` and takes `tenants[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:35-41, 219). Multi-org: always the first tenant, no selection mechanism.
90	- Default scopes if `XERO_SCOPES` unset: tries legacy V1 scope set first (`accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.reports.read payroll.*`, xero-client.ts:81-89), and on `invalid_scope` (HTTP 400) falls back to granular V2 set (`accounting.invoices accounting.payments ...`, xero-client.ts:92-106, retry logic 137-155).
91	
92	## 4. `create-payment`
93	
94	- Schema (src/tools/create/create-payment.tool.ts:18-34): required `invoiceId: string`, `accountId: string`, `amount: number` (`.positive()`); optional `date: string` (YYYY-MM-DD), `reference: string`.
95	- Handler (src/handlers/create-xero-payment.handler.ts:15-45): builds `Payment { invoice: {invoiceID}, account: {accountID}, amount, date: date || today, reference }` and calls `xeroClient.accountingApi.createPayment(tenantId, payment, undefined /* idempotencyKey */, getClientHeaders())` (line 36-41). **idempotencyKey is passed as `undefined`** — no idempotency.
96	- Constraints: only in the description text (tool.ts:8-13): invoice must be AUTHORISED and not fully paid, amount must not exceed amount due — Xero enforces these, not this code. Success text is mislabeled "Invoice created successfully" (tool.ts, response block).
97	
98	## 5. `update-invoice` — cannot change status
99	
100	- Schema (src/tools/update/update-invoice.tool.ts:36-50): required `invoiceId`; optional `lineItems[]` (description, quantity, unitAmount, accountCode, taxType, itemCode?, tracking?), `reference`, `dueDate`, `date`, `contactId`. **There is no `status` parameter.**
101	- Handler hard-blocks non-draft invoices (src/handlers/update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84):
102	  ```ts
103	  // Only allow updates to DRAFT invoices
104	  if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) {
105	    return { result: null, isError: true,
106	      error: `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ${invoiceStatus}`, };
107	  }
108	  ```
109	  The `Invoice` object it sends (handler.ts:39-45) contains only lineItems/reference/dueDate/date/contact — no status field. **Conclusion: the MCP server cannot move an invoice to AUTHORISED (or any status).** Approving a draft requires the raw Accounting API (POST Invoices with `"Status": "AUTHORISED"`).
110	- Warning in description: all line items must be re-supplied; omitted line items are removed (tool.ts:31-32).
111	
112	## 6. `list-invoices`
113	
114	- Schema (src/tools/list/list-invoices.tool.ts:16-22): `page: number` (required), `contactIds: string[]` optional, `invoiceNumbers: string[]` optional (providing invoiceNumbers also returns line items, tool.ts:59, 96-98).
115	- Handler SDK call (src/handlers/list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:15-31): `getInvoices(tenantId, ...)` with `where: undefined`, `order: "UpdatedDateUTC DESC"`, `invoiceNumbers`, `contactIDs`, **`statuses: undefined`** (line 22 — no status filter exposed), `page`, `includeArchived: false`, `summaryOnly: false`, **`pageSize: 10` hardcoded** (line 28), `searchTerm: undefined`. No date, no status, no where-clause filtering available through the tool.
116	
117	## 7. `list-aged-payables-by-contact`
118	
119	- Schema (src/tools/list/list-aged-payables-by-contact.tool.ts:10-18): required `contactId: string`; optional `reportDate` (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to end of current month), `invoicesFromDate`, `invoicesToDate`.
120	- Handler (src/handlers/list-aged-payables-by-contact.handler.ts:15-22): `accountingApi.getReportAgedPayablesByContact(tenantId, contactId, reportDate, invoicesFromDate, invoicesToDate, getClientHeaders())`. Returns report rows as raw JSON text.
121	
122	## 8. Rate limiting: NONE
123	
124	- `grep -rni "429|retry|rate" src/` — no retry, no backoff, no throttling, no rate-limit-remaining header reads anywhere in src/.
125	- The only 429 handling is a cosmetic error message: src/helpers/format-error.ts:35-36 maps HTTP 429 to `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."`. Callers must implement their own backoff/batching.
126	
127	## 9. SDK and token lifecycle
128	
129	- SDK: `xero-node` ^13.3.0 plus `axios` for the token/connections calls, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.23.4, `zod` 3.25 (package.json:27-31). `XeroClient` from xero-node is subclassed as `MCPXeroClient` (xero-client.ts:23).
130	- Token is NOT cached: every handler starts with `await xeroClient.authenticate()` (e.g. create-xero-payment.handler.ts:23, list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:13). In client-credentials mode `authenticate()` (xero-client.ts:195-203) requests a brand-new token from `identity.xero.com/connect/token` plus a `GET /connections` on every single tool call — 2 extra HTTP round-trips per call, no expiry check, no reuse. Bearer mode just re-sets the static token and re-fetches tenants (xero-client.ts:214-220).
131	- All SDK calls send header `user-agent: xero-mcp-server-<version>` via `getClientHeaders()` (src/helpers/get-client-headers.ts:3-9).
132	- Writes are non-idempotent: `idempotencyKey` is explicitly `undefined` in createPayment (create-xero-payment.handler.ts:39) and updateInvoice (update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:54).
133
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md`

```text
1	# Fact sheet: xero-agent-toolkit + xero-command-line (auth & API-call patterns)
2	
3	Source repos (local checkouts under `.tmp/xero-refs/`):
4	- `xero-agent-toolkit/` — examples repo. **Contains zero auth code and zero tool definitions of its own.** Every example spawns `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` via `npx` and hands it `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars. The actual client_credentials flow lives in the MCP server (`xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts`, also checked out here), cited below as the delegated path.
5	- `xero-command-line/` — oclif CLI. Uses **PKCE authorization-code OAuth (browser login), NOT client_credentials**. No client secret anywhere.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. client_credentials flow specifics
10	
11	### xero-agent-toolkit itself: none
12	No match for `client_credentials`, `identity.xero.com`, or `connect/token` anywhere in the repo. The README mandates a **Custom Connection** app type:
13	
14	> "Make sure you are create a [Custom Connection](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections) type of app - Web app and PKCE apps will not work with these examples" — `xero-agent-toolkit/README.md:67`
15	
16	Credentials are passed as env vars to the spawned MCP server, e.g. `xero-agent-toolkit/python/langchain/langchain.py:40-48`:
17	
18	```python
19	client = MultiServerMCPClient({
20	    ...
21	        "args": ["-y", "@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest"],
22	        ...
23	            "XERO_CLIENT_ID": os.getenv("XERO_CLIENT_ID"),
24	            "XERO_CLIENT_SECRET": os.getenv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"),
25	```
26	
27	Same pattern in `python/google-adk/google-adk.py:30-37`, `python/openai/openai_agents.py:369-376`, `typescript/openai-agents/index.ts:383-391`, `typescript/langchain/index.ts:39-47`, `python/google-adk/cloud-run-invoicing-agent/xero_invoicing_agent/agent.py:9-21`.
28	
29	### Delegated implementation (xero-mcp-server, what the toolkit actually runs)
30	`xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:159-174` (`requestToken`):
31	
32	- **Token endpoint:** `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`
33	- **Auth:** HTTP **Basic** header, `base64(clientId:clientSecret)` — NOT body params
34	- **Body:** form-encoded string `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<urlencoded scopes>`
35	- **Headers:** `Authorization: Basic ...`, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `Accept: application/json`
36	
37	```ts
38	const credentials = Buffer.from(`${this.clientId}:${this.clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
39	const response = await axios.post(
40	  "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token",
41	  `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
42	  { headers: {
43	      Authorization: `Basic ${credentials}`,
44	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
45	      Accept: "application/json" } },
46	);
47	```
48	
49	**Scopes ARE passed explicitly** for custom connections (contrary to some docs suggesting they can be omitted). Selection logic at `xero-client.ts:127-157`:
50	1. If `XERO_SCOPES` env var set → use it verbatim.
51	2. Else try legacy V1 scope set (`accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.reports.read payroll.settings payroll.employees payroll.timesheets`, lines 82-90).
52	3. On HTTP 400 with `error === "invalid_scope"` → retry with granular V2 set (`accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals accounting.reports.aged.read accounting.reports.balancesheet.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read accounting.reports.trialbalance.read accounting.contacts accounting.settings payroll.*`, lines 93-107).
53	
54	Alternative path: if `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` is set, a `BearerTokenXeroClient` skips token issuance entirely (`xero-client.ts:16, 206-231`).
55	
56	### xero-command-line: PKCE, not client_credentials
57	`xero-command-line/src/lib/oauth.ts:5-7`:
58	
59	```ts
60	const XERO_AUTH_BASE = 'https://login.xero.com/identity'
61	const XERO_TOKEN_BASE = 'https://identity.xero.com'
62	const REDIRECT_URI = 'http://localhost:8742/callback'
63	```
64	
65	- Authorize URL: `https://login.xero.com/identity/connect/authorize` with `code_challenge_method: 'S256'` (`oauth.ts:64-75`).
66	- Token exchange `oauth.ts:134-155`: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`, form-encoded body `grant_type=authorization_code&client_id=...&code=...&redirect_uri=...&code_verifier=...`. **No Basic auth, no client secret — client_id goes in the body** (public PKCE client).
67	- Scopes: default list at `oauth.ts:12-30` (granular: `accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals accounting.reports.*.read accounting.budgets.read accounting.attachments`); `openid profile email offline_access` always prepended (`oauth.ts:10, 58-62`); overridable via `xero login --scope` or `XERO_SCOPES` env (`src/commands/login.ts:25-29`).
68	
69	---
70	
71	## 2. Tenant id handling
72	
73	### Custom connections (MCP server path used by agent-toolkit)
74	The tenant id is **fetched from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` immediately after every token grant, and the FIRST connection is used** — it is not omitted or left empty. `xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:176-190`:
75	
76	```ts
77	// Get the tenant ID from the connections endpoint
78	const token = response.data.access_token;
79	const connectionsResponse = await axios.get(
80	  "https://api.xero.com/connections",
81	  { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" } },
82	);
83	if (connectionsResponse.data && connectionsResponse.data.length > 0) {
84	  this.tenantId = connectionsResponse.data[0].tenantId;
85	}
86	```
87	
88	Every tool handler then passes it as the first SDK arg (which xero-node sends as the `xero-tenant-id` header), e.g. `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:18-22`:
89	
90	```ts
91	await xeroClient.authenticate();
92	... xeroClient.tenantId,
93	```
94	
95	One empty-string fallback exists: `getOrganisations(this.tenantId || "")` at `xero-client.ts:47-49` — i.e. an empty `xero-tenant-id` is tolerated for the org lookup on custom connections, but everywhere else the resolved tenantId is sent.
96	
97	### xero-command-line
98	Tenant resolved once at login from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` with `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` (`src/lib/oauth.ts:157-167`); if multiple orgs, user picks one interactively (`oauth.ts:195-204`). The chosen `tenantId` is persisted per profile in the token cache (`src/lib/auth.ts:5-11, 100-106`) and passed as the first argument to every `accountingApi` call, e.g. `src/commands/invoices/list.ts:48-50`:
99	
100	```ts
101	const result = await this.xeroCall(flags, async (xero, tenantId) => {
102	  const response = await xero.accountingApi.getInvoices(tenantId, ...)
103	```
104	
105	---
106	
107	## 3. Token caching / expiry / refresh-on-401
108	
109	### xero-agent-toolkit / MCP server: NO caching
110	`authenticate()` is called by **every handler before every API call** and unconditionally requests a fresh client_credentials token (no expiry check, no cache) — `xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:195-203`:
111	
112	```ts
113	public async authenticate() {
114	  const tokenResponse = await this.getClientCredentialsToken();
115	  this.setTokenSet({
116	    access_token: tokenResponse.access_token,
117	    expires_in: tokenResponse.expires_in,
118	    token_type: tokenResponse.token_type,
119	  });
120	}
121	```
122	
123	That is one extra token round-trip (plus one `/connections` call) per tool invocation. Do better in our `api/src/modules/xero/`: cache until `expires_in` with a safety buffer.
124	
125	### xero-command-line: encrypted cache + 60s pre-expiry refresh + refresh-on-401
126	- Cache: AES-256-GCM-encrypted tokens in `~/.config/xero-command-line/tokens.json` (`src/lib/auth.ts:25`, `src/lib/crypto.ts:11,121`), key in OS keychain / scrypt passphrase / file.
127	- Expiry buffer — `src/lib/auth.ts:26, 73-75`:
128	
129	```ts
130	const TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000 // Refresh 60s before expiry
131	export function isTokenExpired(entry: TokenEntry): boolean {
132	  return Date.now() >= entry.expiresAt - TOKEN_BUFFER_MS
133	}
134	```
135	
136	- `expiresAt` derived from `expires_at` (seconds) or `expires_in`, defaulting to 30 min (`auth.ts:87-96`).
137	- Proactive refresh before building the client (`src/lib/xero-client.ts:22-31`), via `grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=...&refresh_token=...` — again no Basic auth (`src/lib/oauth.ts:209-231`).
138	- **Refresh-on-401 retry loop** — `src/lib/xero-client.ts:45-78` (`withRetry`, `maxRetries = 2`):
139	
140	```ts
141	// Handle 401: try refreshing token
142	if (statusCode === 401 && attempt < maxRetries) {
143	  const cached = await getCachedTokenSet(profileName)
144	  if (cached?.refreshToken) {
145	    try {
146	      const newTokenSet = await refreshAccessToken(clientId, cached.refreshToken)
147	      await cacheTokenSet(profileName, newTokenSet, cached.tenantId, cached.tenantName)
148	      continue
149	    } catch {
150	      clearCachedToken(profileName)
151	      throw new Error(`Session expired. Run "xero login" to re-authenticate.`)
152	    }
153	  }
154	  clearCachedToken(profileName)
155	  continue
156	}
157	```
158	
159	---
160	
161	## 4. Tool/command inventory
162	
163	### xero-agent-toolkit
164	Defines **no tools itself** — it exposes whatever `@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server` publishes. The TS examples attach the server unfiltered ("basic Xero agent with all tools", `typescript/openai-agents/index.ts:35-48`); the Google ADK examples filter to subsets via `tool_filter` (`python/google-adk/google-adk.py:40-55`: `list-contacts, create-contact, list-invoices, create-invoice, get-timesheet, list-accounts, list-items, list-tax-rates, list-tracking-categories, update-contact, update-invoice, create-payment, list-payments, list-organisation-details`; `cloud-run-invoicing-agent/xero_invoicing_agent/agent.py:22-31`: 8-tool invoicing subset).
165	
166	Full underlying surface (from `xero-mcp-server/src/tools/{create,delete,get,list,update}/`), 50 tools:
167	- create: `create-bank-transaction, create-contact, create-credit-note, create-invoice, create-item, create-manual-journal, create-payment, create-payroll-timesheet, create-quote, create-tracking-category, create-tracking-options`
168	- list: `list-accounts, list-aged-payables-by-contact, list-aged-receivables-by-contact, list-bank-transactions, list-contact-groups, list-contacts, list-credit-notes, list-invoices, list-items, list-manual-journals, list-organisation-details, list-payments, list-payroll-employee-leave, list-payroll-employee-leave-balances, list-payroll-employee-leave-types, list-payroll-employees, list-payroll-leave-periods, list-payroll-leave-types, list-payroll-timesheets, list-profit-and-loss, list-quotes, list-report-balance-sheet, list-tax-rates, list-tracking-categories, list-trial-balance`
169	- update: `approve-payroll-timesheet, revert-payroll-timesheet, update-bank-transaction, update-contact, update-credit-note, update-invoice, update-item, update-manual-journal, update-payroll-timesheet-add-line, update-payroll-timesheet-update-line, update-quote, update-tracking-category, update-tracking-options`
170	- get: `get-payroll-timesheet`; delete: `delete-payroll-timesheet`
171	
172	**Answers:** purchase order tools: **NO**. Attachment tools: **NO**. History tools: **NO**. Payment tools: **YES** (`create-payment`, `list-payments` — no update/delete payment). The toolkit does NOT cover more than the MCP server; it is strictly a consumer of it. POs/attachments/history must go through our raw Accounting API module as planned.
173	
174	### xero-command-line (from `src/commands/`)
175	`login`, `logout`; `profile add|list|remove|set-default`; `org details`; `contacts list|create|update`; `contact-groups list`; `accounts list|update`; `invoices list|create|update`; `quotes list|create|update`; `credit-notes list|create|update`; `manual-journals list|create|update`; `bank-transactions list|create|update`; `payments list|create`; `items list|create|update`; `currencies list`; `tax-rates list`; `tracking categories list|create|update`; `tracking options create|update`; `reports trial-balance|profit-and-loss|balance-sheet|aged-receivables|aged-payables`.
176	
177	No purchase orders, no attachments, no history endpoints here either (though the default login scope list includes `accounting.attachments`, `oauth.ts:29`).
178	
179	---
180	
181	## 5. Rate limits / retry / Retry-After
182	
183	- **xero-agent-toolkit:** nothing concrete — README marketing lines only (`README.md:98` "Robust error management and retry logic"; `typescript/langchain/README.md:162` mentions "Xero API rate limits" in a troubleshooting list). No code.
184	- **xero-command-line:** `withRetry` in `src/lib/xero-client.ts:81-84` does NOT auto-retry 429s — it surfaces the wait time:
185	
186	```ts
187	if (statusCode === 429) {
188	  const retryAfter = extractRetryAfter(parsed, lastError.message)
189	  throw new Error(`Rate limited. Retry after ${retryAfter} seconds.`)
190	}
191	```
192	
193	  `extractRetryAfter` (`xero-client.ts:159-170`) reads the `retry-after` response header from the parsed SDK error, falls back to regex `/retry-after[":\s]+(\d+)/i` over the error message, defaults to **60 seconds**.
194	- **xero-mcp-server** (delegated): no retry either; maps 429 to a friendly string for the LLM — `src/helpers/format-error.ts:35-36`: `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."`
195	
196	---
197	
198	## 6. SDK vs raw HTTP
199	
200	| Repo | API calls | Token/identity calls |
201	|---|---|---|
202	| xero-agent-toolkit | none directly (MCP server does everything); frameworks: `langchain-mcp-adapters`, `@langchain/mcp-adapters`, `google-adk MCPToolset`, `@openai/agents MCPServerStdio` | none |
203	| xero-mcp-server (delegated) | `xero-node` SDK (`accountingApi.*`, class extends `XeroClient`, `src/clients/xero-client.ts:1-8,23`) | raw **axios** for `connect/token` and `/connections` (`xero-client.ts:164, 178`) |
204	| xero-command-line | `xero-node` **^13** SDK (`package.json`; client built with `new XeroClient({clientId, clientSecret: ''})` + `setTokenSet({access_token})`, `src/lib/xero-client.ts:33-34`) | raw **fetch** for authorize/token/refresh/connections (`src/lib/oauth.ts:143, 158, 219`) |
205	
206	Extra pattern worth copying: the CLI injects a `user-agent: xero-command-line-<version>` default header into the SDK (`src/lib/get-client-headers.ts:3-9`, applied at `src/lib/xero-client.ts:36-40`).
207
```

```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/xero-fetch.ts
//
// Thin typed HTTP helper for the Xero Accounting API over a Custom Connection
// (client_credentials grant, single organisation). Transport only: env
// assertion, token caching, JSON Accept header, query encoding, 429 retry
// honouring Retry-After, and error-envelope parsing. No business logic —
// match/approval decisions live in api/src/modules/.

export const ACCOUNTING_BASE = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";

// Custom connections set scopes at app creation AND in each token request
// body. Override with XERO_SCOPES (space-separated); default mirrors the
// scopes named in .env.example.
const DEFAULT_SCOPES =
  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";

export function requireEnv(name: string): string {
  const value = process.env[name];
  if (!value || value.trim() === "") {
    throw new Error(
      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — set it in .env at the repo root. ` +
        `An empty client id/secret surfaces from Xero as "unauthorized_client", ` +
        `which points away from the real cause.`,
    );
  }
  return value;
}

interface TokenResponse {
  access_token: string;
  expires_in: number; // seconds; access tokens live at most ~30 minutes
  token_type: string;
  scope?: string;
}

let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAtMs: number } | null = null;

/** Client-credentials token, cached in-process until 60s before expiry. */
export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
  if (cachedToken && cachedToken.expiresAtMs > Date.now() + 60_000) {
    return cachedToken.token;
  }

  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
  const scope = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || DEFAULT_SCOPES;

  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Basic ${Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64")}`,
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
    },
    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope }).toString(),
  });

  if (!res.ok) {
    // Body carries Xero's OAuth error code (e.g. unauthorized_client, invalid_scope).
    // Never log the secret itself.
    const body = await res.text();
    throw new Error(`XeroTokenError: ${res.status} ${body.slice(0, 500)}`);
  }

  const token = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
  cachedToken = {
    token: token.access_token,
    expiresAtMs: Date.now() + token.expires_in * 1000,
  };
  return cachedToken.token;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Error envelope (HTTP 400 ApiException, and per-element validation shapes)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export interface XeroValidationError {
  /** The 400 ApiException envelope uses "Message". */
  Message?: string;
  /** The docs' summarizeErrors=false bulk example uses "Description". */
  Description?: string;
}

export interface XeroErrorElement {
  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

/** HTTP 400 body shape: { ErrorNumber, Type, Message, Elements[].ValidationErrors[] } */
export interface XeroApiException {
  ErrorNumber?: number;
  Type?: string;
  Message?: string;
  Elements?: XeroErrorElement[];
}

export function flattenValidationErrors(envelope: XeroApiException): string[] {
  const out: string[] = [];
  for (const element of envelope.Elements ?? []) {
    for (const err of element.ValidationErrors ?? []) {
      const text = err.Message ?? err.Description;
      if (text) out.push(text);
    }
  }
  return out;
}

export class XeroHttpError extends Error {
  constructor(
    readonly status: number,
    readonly envelope: XeroApiException | null,
    readonly rawBody: string,
  ) {
    const details = envelope ? flattenValidationErrors(envelope) : [];
    super(
      `XeroHttpError ${status}: ${envelope?.Message ?? rawBody.slice(0, 300)}` +
        (details.length > 0 ? ` | ValidationErrors: ${details.join("; ")}` : ""),
    );
    this.name = "XeroHttpError";
  }
}

/**
 * With ?summarizeErrors=false a batch write returns HTTP 200 even when an
 * element failed — each element carries StatusAttributeString OK/WARNING/ERROR.
 */
export interface StatusAttributed {
  StatusAttributeString?: "OK" | "WARNING" | "ERROR";
  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
  Warnings?: XeroValidationError[];
}

export function assertElementOk<T extends StatusAttributed>(element: T, context: string): T {
  if (element.StatusAttributeString === "ERROR") {
    const messages = (element.ValidationErrors ?? [])
      .map((e) => e.Message ?? e.Description ?? "unknown")
      .join("; ");
    throw new Error(`XeroValidationError: ${context}: ${messages}`);
  }
  return element;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Core request
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export interface XeroRequestInit {
  method?: "GET" | "PUT" | "POST";
  /** JSON payload — serialised and sent with Content-Type: application/json. */
  json?: unknown;
  /** Raw bytes (attachment uploads). Requires contentType. */
  body?: Uint8Array;
  /** Content-Type for raw `body` uploads — must be the file's MIME type. */
  contentType?: string;
  /** Query params (where, order, page, pageSize, ...). Values are URL-encoded here. */
  query?: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined>;
  /** Sent as Idempotency-Key. POST/PUT only; Xero caches 6 min; max 128 chars. */
  idempotencyKey?: string;
  /** Sent as If-Modified-Since (UTC, accurate to the second). */
  ifModifiedSince?: Date;
  /** Override Accept. Default application/json (Xero's default is XML). */
  accept?: string;
}

const MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES = 3;

function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

/**
 * Low-level call: auth + headers + query encoding + 429 retry. Throws
 * XeroHttpError on any non-2xx (with the parsed ApiException when present).
 *
 * No xero-tenant-id header: a Custom Connection is bound to exactly ONE
 * organisation, so the tenant is implicit — the documented custom-connection
 * call carries only Authorization + Accept (see SKILL.md).
 */
export async function xeroRequest(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<Response> {
  const token = await getAccessToken();

  const url = new URL(`${ACCOUNTING_BASE}${path}`);
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(init.query ?? {})) {
    if (value !== undefined) url.searchParams.set(key, String(value));
  }

  const headers: Record<string, string> = {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
    Accept: init.accept ?? "application/json",
  };
  if (init.json !== undefined) headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
  if (init.body !== undefined) {
    if (!init.contentType) {
      throw new Error("MissingContentType: raw body uploads must set the file's MIME type");
    }
    headers["Content-Type"] = init.contentType;
  }
  if (init.idempotencyKey !== undefined) {
    if (init.idempotencyKey.length > 128) {
      throw new Error(
        `IdempotencyKeyTooLong: ${init.idempotencyKey.length} chars — Xero rejects keys over 128 with a 400`,
      );
    }
    headers["Idempotency-Key"] = init.idempotencyKey;
  }
  if (init.ifModifiedSince !== undefined) {
    headers["If-Modified-Since"] = init.ifModifiedSince.toUTCString();
  }

  let body: string | Uint8Array | undefined;
  if (init.json !== undefined) body = JSON.stringify(init.json);
  else if (init.body !== undefined) body = init.body;

  for (let attempt = 0; ; attempt++) {
    const res = await fetch(url, { method: init.method ?? "GET", headers, body });

    if (res.status === 429 && attempt < MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES) {
      // Xero sends Retry-After (seconds) on minute/daily limits and
      // X-Rate-Limit-Problem naming the limit hit. Honour Retry-After.
      const retryAfterSec = Number(res.headers.get("retry-after") ?? "0");
      const waitMs = retryAfterSec > 0 ? retryAfterSec * 1000 : 1_000;
      console.warn(
        `[xero] 429 (limit: ${res.headers.get("x-rate-limit-problem") ?? "unknown"}) — retrying in ${waitMs}ms`,
      );
      await sleep(waitMs);
      continue;
    }

    if (!res.ok) {
      const raw = await res.text();
      let envelope: XeroApiException | null = null;
      try {
        envelope = JSON.parse(raw) as XeroApiException;
      } catch {
        envelope = null;
      }
      throw new XeroHttpError(res.status, envelope, raw);
    }

    return res;
  }
}

/** JSON call — the default for everything except raw attachment content. */
export async function xeroFetch<T>(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<T> {
  const res = await xeroRequest(path, init);
  return (await res.json()) as T;
}

```
