<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-payments/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-payments
3	description: Create a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY bill via the raw Xero Accounting API (PUT /Payments, plain fetch, Custom Connection client_credentials — no tenant id). Use when recording a bill payment in Xero, checking why a payment write was rejected, verifying the payment-account prerequisite, or reversing a payment. Covers required fields (Invoice.InvoiceID, Account.Code/AccountID, Date, Amount), the BANK-or-payments-enabled account rule, partial vs full payment effects on invoice Status/AmountDue, idempotency discipline, and the rejection causes that bite. Read this BEFORE writing any payment code; do NOT use the MCP server's create-payment (no idempotency — see xero-mcp-usage skill).
4	---
5	
6	# Paying an ACCPAY bill: `PUT /Payments`
7	
8	Money moves outside Xero (bank rail). Xero is the ledger: a Payment row records that the bill was paid. This skill is facts + runnable snippets only — the decision to pay lives in `api/src/modules/`, never here.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	Each check prevents a misleading upstream error.
13	
14	1. **Assert secrets non-empty.** Empty `XERO_CLIENT_ID` surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), pointing away from the real cause. At boot:
15	
16	```ts
17	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
18	  const v = process.env[name];
19	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
20	    throw new Error(`MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. Set it in .env and retry.`);
21	  }
22	  return v;
23	}
24	```
25	
26	2. **Request BOTH scopes in the token body.** The pre-flight invoice GET needs `accounting.invoices`; the write needs `accounting.payments` (grants Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments). Granular scopes apply to custom connections since 29 April 2026 — use granular, not `accounting.transactions`. Missing write scope → 403 at `PUT /Payments`, and a token's scope cannot be widened — mint a new token.
27	
28	3. **Fact-check the payment account once per org.** The account must be type `BANK` **or** have "enable payments to this account" switched on (edit account screen, Chart of Accounts). Check via `where` filter (GET /Accounts likely needs `accounting.settings.read` — scope-to-Accounts mapping UNVERIFIED — check before relying; if you get 403 here, add that scope):
29	
30	```ts
31	const where = encodeURIComponent(`Code=="${accountCode}"`);
32	const res = await fetch(`https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts?where=${where}`, {
33	  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" },
34	});
35	const { Accounts } = (await res.json()) as { Accounts?: Array<{ Type?: string }> };
36	const acc = Accounts?.[0];
37	if (!acc) throw new Error(`AccountNotFound: no account with Code ${accountCode}`);
38	if (acc.Type !== "BANK") console.warn(`[xero] account is ${acc.Type} — confirm payments are enabled on it`, acc);
39	```
40	
41	4. **Fact-check the invoice: `Status === "AUTHORISED"` and `AmountDue >= amount`.** Built into `scripts/create-payment.ts` below — it GETs the invoice and fails with `InvoiceNotAuthorised` / `AmountExceedsAmountDue` before attempting the write. This restates Xero's documented constraints as named local errors; it is not approval logic.
42	
43	## Auth: why there is no tenant id
44	
45	Custom Connection = `client_credentials` grant, bound to **one** Xero organisation ("access data from a single Xero organisation" — multi-org needs code/PKCE flow instead). One org means nothing to select, so no `XERO_TENANT_ID` env var and no `xero-tenant-id` header: the documented custom-connection API call carries only `Authorization: Bearer` + `Accept`. (The docs omit the header from the canonical example; an explicit "not required" sentence does not appear — UNVERIFIED — check before relying.)
46	
47	Token: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` with `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`, body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...` (scopes in the BODY — differs from code/PKCE where scopes ride in the URL). Access token is a JWT, valid up to 30 minutes. Only `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are needed, from `.env`, never committed, never logged (log `client_id_len`, not the value).
48	
49	## The write
50	
51	Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments` — **PUT creates. POST deletes (reverses). There is no update** ("Payments cannot be modified, only created and deleted").
52	
53	Minimum body (official single-payment example shape):
54	
55	```json
56	{
57	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
58	  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
59	  "Date": "2026-07-04",
60	  "Amount": 32.06
61	}
62	```
63	
64	| Field | Facts |
65	|---|---|
66	| `Invoice` | Exactly ONE identifier object per payment (Invoice, CreditNote, Prepayment, or Overpayment). Identify by `InvoiceID`. `InvoiceNumber` also works but is NON-unique on ACCPAY — always use `InvoiceID`. |
67	| `Account` | `Code` or `AccountID`. "Not all accounts have a code value" — `AccountID` is always safe. Must satisfy the BANK-or-payments-enabled prerequisite. |
68	| `Date` | Date the payment is being made, `YYYY-MM-DD`. |
69	| `Amount` | In the **invoice's** currency. Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing (`AmountDue`). |
70	| Optional | `Reference`, `IsReconciled`, `BankAmount`, `CurrencyRate`, `Status`. |
71	
72	Envelope: single object, or `{"Payments": [...]}` for multiple (add `?summarizeErrors=false` for per-element `ValidationErrors`). Multiple bills in one transaction → `BatchPayments` endpoint instead. Send `Accept: application/json` or you get XML.
73	
74	### Partial vs full payment
75	
76	- Partial payments are allowed (any Amount ≤ AmountDue). Invoice `Status` stays `AUTHORISED` — the enum's own definition: "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid". `AmountDue` drops, `AmountPaid` rises (computed fields).
77	- Full payment: "Once an invoice is fully paid the status will change to PAID." `PAID` is system-set — you cannot write it. Creating a paid bill is always two steps: create/approve to `AUTHORISED`, then `PUT /Payments`.
78	- After partial/full payment, an ACCPAY bill is mostly frozen: only Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (with exceptions), Url, line Description/AccountCode (non-CIS), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate remain updatable.
79	
80	### Created payment
81	
82	Response envelope `{"Payments": [...]}` with `PaymentID`. Payment status codes: `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`. A bill payment shows PaymentType `ACCPAYPAYMENT`. Reverse with `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` body `{"Status": "DELETED"}` (payments created via batch payments and receipts are excluded from this delete method).
83	
84	### Multicurrency
85	
86	A payment is multicurrency if the payment account or the invoice is in a non-base currency. `Amount` is always invoice-currency. If the bank account currency differs, set `BankAmount` (account currency) and let Xero calculate `CurrencyRate` — "If you know this amount, specifying it is preferred to setting CurrencyRate". Non-multicurrency payments have `CurrencyRate` 1; a CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect.
87	
88	## Runnable snippet
89	
90	`scripts/create-payment.ts` — standalone (strict TS, commonjs, Node 18+ fetch, zero deps; compile-verified):
91	
92	```
93	ts-node scripts/create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE]
94	```
95	
96	It: asserts env → gets a client_credentials token → GETs the invoice and fails with named errors unless `AUTHORISED` and `AmountDue >= amount` → skips if a non-deleted payment with the same `Reference` already exists on the invoice (check-by-reference) → PUTs the payment with a deterministic `Idempotency-Key` → prints `PaymentID` → re-GETs the invoice and prints new `Status`/`AmountDue`/`AmountPaid`. 3 API calls + 1 token call — well under the 60/min tenant limit.
97	
98	## Traps
99	
100	- **Paying a non-AUTHORISED invoice.** Payments apply to approved AR/AP invoices only; `DRAFT`/`SUBMITTED` create no journals and cannot take payments. Approve first: `POST /Invoices/{InvoiceID}` with `{"Status": "AUTHORISED"}` (legal from DRAFT and SUBMITTED; the MCP server cannot do this — raw API only). Exact ValidationError text for this rejection is not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
101	- **Wrong account class.** Non-`BANK` account without payments enabled → validation rejection (exact message not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying). Fix in the UI (edit account screen) or via the Accounts API — SDK field `enablePaymentsToAccount` (confirmed in XeroAPI/xero-command-line `accounts update`); raw JSON property name `EnablePaymentsToAccount` UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
102	- **Currency mismatch.** `Amount` is invoice-currency, not account-currency. A currency the org is not subscribed to fails validation — documented example message: `Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR`. Cross-currency: prefer `BankAmount` over hand-set `CurrencyRate`; a suspect rate returns a validation warning.
103	- **Amount exceeding AmountDue.** Documented constraint: "Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice". Exact rejection message not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Always compare against a FRESH `AmountDue` (another partial payment may have landed since your cache).
104	- **Idempotency: check-by-reference BEFORE create.** Project rule: every Xero write checks for an existing record first. Give each payment a unique `Reference`, check the invoice's `Payments` array before writing, persist the returned `PaymentID` in Postgres. The `Idempotency-Key` header is only a retry guard: keys expire after **6 minutes**, max **128 chars**, same key + different request → 400 `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE is used with a different request.`, and **errors are cached** — a rerun with the same key can replay a stale error after the real cause is fixed (GET to check, then retry with a new key).
105	- **PUT/POST are inverted vs habit.** `PUT /Payments` creates; `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` deletes. Nothing updates a payment — to correct one, delete and re-create.
106	- **`PAID` cannot be written and `AUTHORISED` cannot be re-drafted.** PAID is system-set on full payment. An AUTHORISED invoice can only stay AUTHORISED or go VOIDED (and voiding is shown only for invoices with no payments applied) — delete the payment first if you must void.
107	- **Batch responses lie about success.** With `summarizeErrors=false`, validation failures come back **HTTP 200** with per-element `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` — check `ValidationErrors` per element, never the HTTP status alone.
108	
109	## Error → real cause
110	
111	| You see | Real cause |
112	|---|---|
113	| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` empty or wrong — `client_id=undefined` was sent. Assert env first. |
114	| `invalid_scope` (HTTP 400) from the token endpoint | Scope string not granted to the app (e.g. broad scope on a post-Apr-2026 custom connection). Request granular: `accounting.invoices accounting.payments`. |
115	| `403` on the API call | Token missing the needed scope. Scopes cannot be widened on an existing token — mint a new one with the right scope (and match the app config). |
116	| `400` with `"Type": "ValidationException"` (`ErrorNumber` 10) | Field-level rejection — the truth is in `Elements[].ValidationErrors[].Message`, not the top-level message. |
117	| HTTP 200 but element has `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` | You sent `summarizeErrors=false`; per-element `ValidationErrors` carry the failure. |
118	| `400` `Idempotency Key: ... is used with a different request.` | Same key, changed URL/body/method. Persist the key with the record and retry byte-identically, or use a new key. |
119	| Same error repeats after the cause is fixed | Cached idempotent error (keys live 6 min). GET to check whether the payment exists; if not, re-create with a NEW key. |
120	| `429` + `Retry-After` header | Rate limit: 60/min per tenant, 5 concurrent, 1,000–5,000/day. `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names the limit hit; wait `Retry-After` seconds. |
121	| XML instead of JSON | Missing `Accept: application/json` — XML is the Accounting API default. |
122	| `404` on the invoice GET | Wrong `InvoiceID`, or wrong path — the real path is `/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}` (PascalCase resource names; friendly alias paths 404). |
123	
124	<!--
125	Sources (fact-checked 2026-07-04):
126	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md — §4 Payments (min body, one-identifier rule, Account.Code/AccountID, Amount<=outstanding, account BANK/payments-enabled prerequisite, partial->AUTHORISED / full->PAID, PUT create / POST delete, BankAmount/CurrencyRate, "Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR" example, Payment statuses AUTHORISED|DELETED, ACCPAYPAYMENT), §1 Invoices (status transition table, AUTHORISED="apply payments", ACCPAY InvoiceNumber non-unique, post-payment updatable fields, summaryOnly excludes Payments — basis for the invoice Payments array check).
127	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — §1 Custom Connections (token endpoint, Basic auth, scope in body, single-org quote, two-header call example / xero-tenant-id omission marked UNVERIFIED, 30-min token), §2 rate limits + 429/Retry-After/X-Rate-Limit-Problem, §3 request conventions (XML default, where-filter encoding, summarizeErrors semantics, ApiException envelope, PUT-vs-POST), §4 Idempotency-Key (6 min, 128 chars, different-request 400 message, errors cached).
128	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md — skill format, pre-flight discipline, requireEnv/unauthorized_client causal chain, client_id_len logging, scope grants table (accounting.invoices / accounting.payments), granular-scope dates, idempotency "rules that bite".
129	- .tmp/xero-refs/mcp.md — §4 create-payment passes idempotencyKey undefined, §5 update-invoice cannot set status (why approval must be raw API), §3 custom-connection env (XERO_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, no tenant env).
130	- .tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md — AP surface = /Invoices (bills) + /PurchaseOrders + /Payments + /Contacts; Xero is the ledger, money moves on external rails.
131	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/commands/accounts/update.ts:57,73 + src/lib/validators.ts:181 — enablePaymentsToAccount SDK field.
132	- scripts/create-payment.ts compile-verified: tsc 6.0.3, --strict --target ES2022 --module commonjs --esModuleInterop --types node.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-payments/scripts/create-payment.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-payments/scripts/create-payment.ts
2	//
3	// Create a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY invoice via the raw Accounting API.
4	// Custom connection (client_credentials): the token is bound to exactly ONE Xero
5	// organisation, so no xero-tenant-id header and no XERO_TENANT_ID env var.
6	//
7	// Usage:
8	//   ts-node create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE]
9	//   or via env: INVOICE_ID, ACCOUNT_CODE, AMOUNT, PAYMENT_DATE, PAYMENT_REFERENCE
10	//
11	// Env (export from .env at repo root before running; never commit, never log):
12	//   XERO_CLIENT_ID, XERO_CLIENT_SECRET   required
13	//   XERO_SCOPES                          optional, default "accounting.invoices accounting.payments"
14	//
15	// Compiles standalone: strict, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, types=["node"].
16	// Node 18+ (native fetch). No npm deps.
17	
18	const XERO_TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
19	const XERO_API = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
20	
21	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
22	  const v = process.env[name];
23	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
24	    throw new Error(
25	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. Set it in .env and retry.`,
26	    );
27	  }
28	  return v;
29	}
30	
31	function arg(pos: number, envName: string): string | undefined {
32	  return process.argv[2 + pos] ?? process.env[envName];
33	}
34	
35	interface ValidationError {
36	  Message?: string;
37	}
38	interface ApiException {
39	  ErrorNumber?: number;
40	  Type?: string;
41	  Message?: string;
42	  Elements?: Array<{ ValidationErrors?: ValidationError[] }>;
43	}
44	interface XeroPayment {
45	  PaymentID?: string;
46	  Status?: string;
47	  Amount?: number;
48	  Date?: string;
49	  Reference?: string;
50	}
51	interface XeroInvoice {
52	  InvoiceID?: string;
53	  Type?: string;
54	  Status?: string;
55	  AmountDue?: number;
56	  AmountPaid?: number;
57	  CurrencyCode?: string;
58	  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
59	}
60	interface InvoiceEnvelope {
61	  Invoices?: XeroInvoice[];
62	}
63	interface PaymentEnvelope {
64	  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
65	}
66	
67	async function getToken(clientId: string, clientSecret: string, scopes: string): Promise<string> {
68	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
69	  const res = await fetch(XERO_TOKEN_URL, {
70	    method: "POST",
71	    headers: {
72	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
73	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
74	    },
75	    // Custom connections take scopes in the BODY (unlike code/PKCE flows, where
76	    // scopes ride in the authorize URL).
77	    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope: scopes }).toString(),
78	  });
79	  if (!res.ok) {
80	    throw new Error(`TokenError: ${res.status} from identity.xero.com — ${await res.text()}`);
81	  }
82	  const json = (await res.json()) as { access_token?: string };
83	  if (!json.access_token) {
84	    throw new Error("TokenError: token response contained no access_token");
85	  }
86	  return json.access_token;
87	}
88	
89	async function getInvoice(token: string, invoiceId: string): Promise<XeroInvoice> {
90	  // No xero-tenant-id header: a custom connection token is scoped to a single org.
91	  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
92	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" },
93	  });
94	  if (res.status === 404) {
95	    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: no invoice with InvoiceID ${invoiceId}`);
96	  }
97	  if (!res.ok) {
98	    throw new Error(`InvoiceFetchError: ${res.status} — ${await res.text()}`);
99	  }
100	  const json = (await res.json()) as InvoiceEnvelope;
101	  const inv = json.Invoices?.[0];
102	  if (!inv) {
103	    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: empty Invoices envelope for ${invoiceId}`);
104	  }
105	  return inv;
106	}
107	
108	function formatRejection(status: number, bodyText: string): string {
109	  try {
110	    const api = JSON.parse(bodyText) as ApiException;
111	    const msgs = (api.Elements ?? [])
112	      .flatMap((e) => e.ValidationErrors ?? [])
113	      .map((v) => v.Message)
114	      .filter((m): m is string => typeof m === "string");
115	    if (msgs.length > 0) {
116	      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} ${api.Type ?? ""} — ${msgs.join(" | ")}`;
117	    }
118	    if (api.Message) {
119	      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${api.Message}`;
120	    }
121	  } catch {
122	    // not JSON — fall through to raw body
123	  }
124	  return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${bodyText}`;
125	}
126	
127	async function main(): Promise<void> {
128	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
129	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
130	  const scopes = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || "accounting.invoices accounting.payments";
131	
132	  const invoiceId = arg(0, "INVOICE_ID");
133	  const accountCode = arg(1, "ACCOUNT_CODE");
134	  const amountRaw = arg(2, "AMOUNT");
135	  const date = arg(3, "PAYMENT_DATE") ?? new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD
136	  const reference = arg(4, "PAYMENT_REFERENCE");
137	
138	  if (!invoiceId || !accountCode || !amountRaw) {
139	    throw new Error(
140	      "Usage: create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE] (or set INVOICE_ID/ACCOUNT_CODE/AMOUNT env vars)",
141	    );
142	  }
143	  const amount = Number(amountRaw);
144	  if (!Number.isFinite(amount) || amount <= 0) {
145	    throw new Error(`BadAmount: "${amountRaw}" is not a positive number`);
146	  }
147	
148	  // Never log the secret — length only.
149	  console.log("[xero] token request", { client_id_len: clientId.length, scopes });
150	  const token = await getToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
151	
152	  // ---- Pre-flight FACT-CHECK (not business logic) --------------------------
153	  // These asserts restate Xero's documented constraints so a bad write fails
154	  // here with a named local error instead of an opaque ValidationException.
155	  // The decision to pay was made upstream in api/src/modules/ — nothing below
156	  // decides anything.
157	  const inv = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);
158	
159	  if (inv.Status !== "AUTHORISED") {
160	    throw new Error(
161	      `InvoiceNotAuthorised: invoice ${invoiceId} has status ${inv.Status ?? "unknown"} — ` +
162	        `Xero applies payments to AUTHORISED invoices only (DRAFT/SUBMITTED create no journals). ` +
163	        `Approve it first: POST /Invoices/${invoiceId} {"Status":"AUTHORISED"}.`,
164	    );
165	  }
166	  if (typeof inv.AmountDue !== "number" || inv.AmountDue < amount) {
167	    throw new Error(
168	      `AmountExceedsAmountDue: AmountDue is ${inv.AmountDue ?? "unknown"}, requested ${amount} — ` +
169	        `Amount must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice.`,
170	    );
171	  }
172	
173	  // Idempotency check-by-reference: this project requires every Xero write to
174	  // check for an existing record before creating. Best-effort here via the
175	  // invoice's Payments array; the durable record is PaymentID persisted in
176	  // Postgres by the caller. Pass a unique REFERENCE to make reruns no-ops.
177	  if (reference) {
178	    const existing = (inv.Payments ?? []).find(
179	      (p) => p.Reference === reference && p.Status !== "DELETED",
180	    );
181	    if (existing) {
182	      console.log(
183	        `[xero] payment already exists — idempotent skip. PaymentID=${existing.PaymentID ?? "?"} Reference=${reference}`,
184	      );
185	      console.log(`[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${inv.Status} AmountDue=${inv.AmountDue}`);
186	      return;
187	    }
188	  }
189	
190	  // ---- The write. PUT /Payments creates; POST /Payments/{id} deletes. ------
191	  const payment = {
192	    Invoice: { InvoiceID: invoiceId },
193	    Account: { Code: accountCode },
194	    Date: date,
195	    Amount: amount,
196	    ...(reference ? { Reference: reference } : {}),
197	  };
198	  // Deterministic Idempotency-Key: identical retries within Xero's 6-minute
199	  // cache window return the cached response instead of double-paying. This is
200	  // a retry guard only — the durable guarantee is the check above + Postgres.
201	  const idempotencyKey = `pay:${invoiceId}:${accountCode}:${amount}:${date}:${reference ?? ""}`.slice(0, 128);
202	
203	  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Payments`, {
204	    method: "PUT",
205	    headers: {
206	      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
207	      Accept: "application/json",
208	      "Content-Type": "application/json",
209	      "Idempotency-Key": idempotencyKey,
210	    },
211	    body: JSON.stringify(payment),
212	  });
213	  const bodyText = await res.text();
214	  if (!res.ok) {
215	    throw new Error(formatRejection(res.status, bodyText));
216	  }
217	
218	  const created = (JSON.parse(bodyText) as PaymentEnvelope).Payments?.[0];
219	  if (!created?.PaymentID) {
220	    throw new Error(`PaymentParseError: 2xx response but no PaymentID — body: ${bodyText}`);
221	  }
222	  console.log(
223	    `[xero] created PaymentID=${created.PaymentID} Status=${created.Status ?? "?"} Amount=${created.Amount ?? amount}`,
224	  );
225	
226	  // Re-read the invoice: AmountDue drops by Amount; Status flips to PAID only
227	  // when fully paid (system-set), stays AUTHORISED while partially paid.
228	  const after = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);
229	  console.log(
230	    `[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${after.Status ?? "?"} AmountDue=${after.AmountDue ?? "?"} AmountPaid=${after.AmountPaid ?? "?"}`,
231	  );
232	}
233	
234	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
235	  console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
236	  process.exitCode = 1;
237	});
238	
239	export {};
240
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/attachments.ts
//
// Attachments — NOT in the Xero MCP server; raw Accounting API only.
// Upload = raw file bytes PUT/POST to /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
// (NOT JSON). Content-Type must be the file's MIME type. 10 attachments per
// document, 10MB per file (documented cap on the Attachments page).

import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { basename } from "node:path";
import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";

/** Filenames containing any of < > : " / \ | ? * NUL + are rejected as Bad Request. */
const FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS = /[<>:"/\\|?*␀+]/;

const MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;

export type AttachmentParent = "Invoices" | "PurchaseOrders" | "Contacts";

export interface Attachment {
  AttachmentID: string;
  FileName: string;
  Url: string;
  MimeType: string;
  ContentLength: number;
  IncludeOnline?: boolean;
}

export interface AttachmentsResponse {
  Attachments: Attachment[];
}

/**
 * Docs rule: filename characters "should not be encoded unless they're
 * brackets. Brackets must be encoded in order for the call to go through."
 */
function encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName: string): string {
  return fileName
    .replace(/\(/g, "%28")
    .replace(/\)/g, "%29")
    .replace(/\[/g, "%5B")
    .replace(/\]/g, "%5D");
}

export async function uploadAttachment(options: {
  endpoint: AttachmentParent;
  guid: string; // e.g. the InvoiceID of the bill the evidence belongs to
  filePath: string;
  mimeType: string; // e.g. "application/pdf", "image/png"
  fileName?: string; // defaults to basename(filePath)
  /**
   * IncludeOnline=true shows the attachment on the online invoice. Xero
   * supports this for accounts RECEIVABLE invoices and AR credit notes only —
   * it does not apply to ACCPAY bills.
   */
  includeOnline?: boolean;
}): Promise<Attachment> {
  const fileName = options.fileName ?? basename(options.filePath);
  if (FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS.test(fileName)) {
    throw new Error(
      `BadFileName: "${fileName}" contains a character Xero rejects as Bad Request ` +
        String.raw`(< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +)`,
    );
  }

  const bytes = readFileSync(options.filePath);
  if (bytes.byteLength > MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES) {
    throw new Error(
      `AttachmentTooLarge: ${bytes.byteLength} bytes — the Attachments page caps files at 10MB ` +
        `(max 10 attachments per document)`,
    );
  }

  // PUT and POST are identical here; uploading the same filename onto the
  // same document OVERWRITES the existing attachment.
  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(
    `/${options.endpoint}/${options.guid}/Attachments/${encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName)}`,
    {
      method: "PUT",
      body: new Uint8Array(bytes),
      contentType: options.mimeType,
      query: options.includeOnline ? { IncludeOnline: true } : undefined,
    },
  );
  const attachment = res.Attachments[0];
  if (!attachment) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: upload returned no Attachments element");
  return attachment;
}

export async function listAttachments(
  endpoint: AttachmentParent,
  guid: string,
): Promise<Attachment[]> {
  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/Attachments/`);
  return res.Attachments;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Demo entrypoint:
//   npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const [guid, filePath, mimeType] = process.argv.slice(2);
  if (!guid || !filePath || !mimeType) {
    throw new Error("Usage: npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>");
  }
  const uploaded = await uploadAttachment({ endpoint: "Invoices", guid, filePath, mimeType });
  console.log(`Uploaded ${uploaded.FileName} (${uploaded.ContentLength} bytes)`);
  const all = await listAttachments("Invoices", guid);
  console.log(`Document now has ${all.length}/10 attachments`);
}

if (require.main === module) {
  main().catch((err) => {
    console.error(err);
    process.exitCode = 1;
  });
}

```

import { xeroClient } from "../clients/xero-client.js";
import { XeroClientResponse } from "../types/tool-response.js";
import { formatError } from "../helpers/format-error.js";
import { Invoice, LineItemTracking } from "xero-node";
import { getClientHeaders } from "../helpers/get-client-headers.js";

interface InvoiceLineItem {
  description: string;
  quantity: number;
  unitAmount: number;
  accountCode: string;
  taxType: string;
  itemCode?: string;
  tracking?: LineItemTracking[];
}

async function createInvoice(
  contactId: string,
  lineItems: InvoiceLineItem[],
  type: Invoice.TypeEnum,
  reference: string | undefined,
  date: string | undefined,
): Promise<Invoice | undefined> {
  await xeroClient.authenticate();

  const invoice: Invoice = {
    type: type,
    contact: {
      contactID: contactId,
    },
    lineItems: lineItems,
    date: date || new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0], // Use provided date or today's date
    dueDate: new Date(Date.now() + 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
      .toISOString()
      .split("T")[0], // 30 days from now
    ...(type === Invoice.TypeEnum.ACCPAY
      ? { invoiceNumber: reference }
      : { reference: reference }),
    status: Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT,
  };

  const response = await xeroClient.accountingApi.createInvoices(
    xeroClient.tenantId,
    {
      invoices: [invoice],
    }, // invoices
    true, //summarizeErrors
    undefined, //unitdp
    undefined, //idempotencyKey
    getClientHeaders(),
  );
  const createdInvoice = response.body.invoices?.[0];
  return createdInvoice;
}

/**
 * Create a new invoice in Xero
 */
export async function createXeroInvoice(
  contactId: string,

async function updateInvoice(
  invoiceId: string,
  lineItems?: InvoiceLineItem[],
  reference?: string,
  dueDate?: string,
  date?: string,
  contactId?: string,
): Promise<Invoice | undefined> {
  const invoice: Invoice = {
    lineItems: lineItems,
    reference: reference,
    dueDate: dueDate,
    date: date,
    contact: contactId ? { contactID: contactId } : undefined,
  };

  const response = await xeroClient.accountingApi.updateInvoice(
    xeroClient.tenantId,
    invoiceId, // invoiceId
    {
      invoices: [invoice],
    }, // invoices
    undefined, // unitdp
    undefined, // idempotencyKey
    getClientHeaders(), // options
  );

  return response.body.invoices?.[0];
}

/**
 * Update an existing invoice in Xero
 */
export async function updateXeroInvoice(
  invoiceId: string,
  lineItems?: InvoiceLineItem[],
  reference?: string,
  dueDate?: string,
  date?: string,
  contactId?: string,
): Promise<XeroClientResponse<Invoice>> {
  try {
    const existingInvoice = await getInvoice(invoiceId);

    const invoiceStatus = existingInvoice?.status;

    // Only allow updates to DRAFT invoices
    if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) {
      return {
        result: null,
        isError: true,
        error: `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ${invoiceStatus}`,
      };
    }

    const updatedInvoice = await updateInvoice(
      invoiceId,
      lineItems,
      reference,
      dueDate,
import { xeroClient } from "../clients/xero-client.js";
import { XeroClientResponse } from "../types/tool-response.js";
import { formatError } from "../helpers/format-error.js";
import { Payment } from "xero-node";
import { getClientHeaders } from "../helpers/get-client-headers.js";

type PaymentProps = {
  invoiceId: string;
  accountId: string;
  amount: number;
  date?: string;
  reference?: string;
};

async function createPayment({
  invoiceId,
  accountId,
  amount,
  date,
  reference,
}: PaymentProps): Promise<Payment | undefined> {
  await xeroClient.authenticate();

  const payment: Payment = {
    invoice: {
      invoiceID: invoiceId,
    },
    account: {
      accountID: accountId,
    },
    amount: amount,
    date: date || new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0], // Today's date if not specified
    reference: reference,
  };

  const response = await xeroClient.accountingApi.createPayment(
    xeroClient.tenantId,
    payment,
    undefined, // idempotencyKey
    getClientHeaders(), // options
  );

  return response.body.payments?.[0];
}

 * Create a new payment in Xero
 */

  invoiceId,

}

function formatHttpStatus(status: number): string {
  switch (status) {
    case 401:
      return "Authentication failed. Please check your Xero credentials.";
    case 403:
      return "You don't have permission to access this resource in Xero.";
    case 404:
      return "The requested resource was not found in Xero.";
    case 429:
      return "Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment.";
    default:
      return "";
  }
}

 * Never stringify unknown error objects — the xero-node SDK rejects with a
#!/usr/bin/env node

import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { XeroMcpServer } from "./server/xero-mcp-server.js";
import { ToolFactory } from "./tools/tool-factory.js";

const main = async () => {
  // Create an MCP server
  const server = XeroMcpServer.GetServer();

  ToolFactory(server);

  // Start receiving messages on stdin and sending messages on stdout
  const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
  await server.connect(transport);
};

main().catch((error) => {
  console.error("Error:", error);
  process.exit(1);

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md`

```text
1	# Xero Accounting API — Verified Fact Sheet (Invoices / Purchase Orders / Payments / Types)
2	
3	Verified 2026-07-04 against the OFFICIAL Xero developer docs, rendered in Chrome directly from:
4	- INV: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/invoices
5	- PO:  https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/purchaseorders
6	- PAY: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/payments
7	- TYP: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/types
8	
9	Every fact is tagged with its source page. Anything not confirmed on these four pages is marked UNVERIFIED.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. Invoices (focus: Type=ACCPAY bills)
14	
15	Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [INV]
16	
17	### Essential fields [INV]
18	
19	| Field | Notes |
20	|---|---|
21	| Type | `ACCPAY` (bill) or `ACCREC` (sales invoice). Required on create. [INV][TYP] |
22	| Contact | Required on create. "Only ContactID should be provided when creating or updating an invoice." [INV] |
23	| Date | Date issued, YYYY-MM-DD. Optional; defaults to current date in org timezone. [INV] |
24	| DueDate | Date due, YYYY-MM-DD. Optional. [INV] |
25	| LineItems | Required: "At least one is required to create a complete Invoice." [INV] |
26	| LineItems[].Description | Min 1 char, max 4000. A line item can be created with only a Description. [INV] |
27	| LineItems[].Quantity | Max length 13. [INV] |
28	| LineItems[].UnitAmount | Rounded to 2dp by default; opt in to 4dp with querystring `unitdp=4`. [INV] |
29	| LineItems[].AccountCode | See Accounts. [INV] |
30	| LineItems[].TaxType | Override "if the default Tax Code for the selected AccountCode is not correct". [INV] |
31	| LineItems[].LineAmount | `LineAmount = Quantity * UnitAmount * ((100 – DiscountRate)/100)`; can't exceed 9,999,999,999.99. Note: DiscountRate/DiscountAmount are ACCREC-only — "ACCPAY invoices and credit notes in Xero do not support discounts". [INV] |
32	| InvoiceNumber | ACCREC: unique alphanumeric. **ACCPAY: NON-unique alphanumeric code; "This value will also display as Reference in the UI."** Max length 255. Auto-generated for ACCREC if missing. [INV] |
33	| Reference | "Additional reference number" (max length 255). [INV] |
34	| Status | See status codes below. Optional on create; defaults to DRAFT. [INV] |
35	| LineAmountTypes | "Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element." [INV] |
36	| SubTotal / TotalTax / Total | Total = "Total of Invoice tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)". Read/computed. [INV] |
37	| CurrencyCode | "Defaults to the organisation's base currency." [INV] |
38	| CurrencyRate | 1 for base-currency invoices; validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect. [INV] |
39	| AmountDue / AmountPaid / AmountCredited / FullyPaidOnDate | Returned computed payment-state fields. [INV] |
40	
41	### Statuses and LEGAL transitions [INV]
42	
43	New invoices may be created with one of three statuses: DRAFT (default; may contain incomplete line items; no journals), SUBMITTED (approval workflows; no journals), AUTHORISED ("approved" state; journals created; "You can now apply payments to the invoice"). [INV]
44	
45	Documented valid state changes when updating ("Invoice status changes" table, verbatim) [INV]:
46	
47	| Existing status | New status |
48	|---|---|
49	| DRAFT | DRAFT, SUBMITTED, AUTHORISED, DELETED |
50	| SUBMITTED | SUBMITTED, AUTHORISED, DRAFT, DELETED |
51	| AUTHORISED | AUTHORISED, VOIDED |
52	
53	- Irreversible: once AUTHORISED, an invoice can never return to DRAFT/SUBMITTED and "cannot be deleted but you can set it's status to VOIDED". [INV]
54	- Delete: "You can delete a DRAFT or SUBMITTED invoice by updating the Status to DELETED." [INV]
55	- PAID is set by the system: "Once an invoice is fully paid the status will change to PAID." You cannot create PAID directly — create AUTHORISED then apply a payment ("Creating a PAID Invoice" is a two-step process). [INV]
56	- No transitions are documented out of PAID, VOIDED, or DELETED (the table lists none) — treat as terminal. [INV]
57	- Voiding example shown "for an approved invoice that has no payments applied to it". [INV]
58	- Locked: invoices "In a locked period" cannot be updated (ACCREC or ACCPAY). [INV]
59	- Partially/fully PAID ACCPAY bills can only have these fields updated: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (unless payment was via Credit Note or lines contain a CisLabourExpense account), Url, LineItems Description, AccountCode (except CIS account codes), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate. [INV]
60	
61	### Create / update semantics [INV]
62	
63	- POST Invoices: "Use this method to create or update an invoice."
64	- PUT Invoices: "similar to the POST Invoices method, however you can only create new invoices with this method."
65	- Envelope: a single invoice object, or an `{"Invoices": [ ... ]}` array for multiple (both shown in official examples).
66	- Batch validation: `?SummarizeErrors=false` returns per-invoice `ValidationErrors` with status OK/ERROR per element.
67	- Updates target `/Invoices/{InvoiceID}` or `/Invoices/{InvoiceNumber}` (official examples POST to both).
68	- Line items on update (POST): providing LineItem WITH its LineItemID updates it; WITHOUT LineItemID creates a new one; **omitting an existing LineItemID DELETES that line item** ("If LineItemIDs are not included with line items in an update request then the line items are deleted and recreated"). [INV]
69	
70	### Filtering (Type=="ACCPAY", Status) [INV]
71	
72	- Official example: `?where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"` (encoded: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/invoices?where=Type%3d%3d%22ACCPAY%22+AND+Status%3d%3d%22AUTHORISED%22`).
73	- Optimised where fields (equals): Status, Contact.ContactID, Contact.Name, Contact.ContactNumber, Reference, InvoiceNumber, InvoiceId, Type, AmountPaid; (equals+range >, >=, <, <=): Date, DueDate, AmountDue.
74	- Optimised comma-separated list params: `?Statuses=AUTHORISED,DRAFT`, `?IDs=...`, `?InvoiceNumbers=...`, `?ContactIDs=...`.
75	- `SearchTerm` does case-insensitive text search across InvoiceNumber and Reference.
76	- High volume threshold: requests returning >100k invoices, or unoptimised filter/order resulting in >100k, are denied with 400.
77	- `summaryOnly=true` excludes Payments, HasAttachments, LineItems, CISDeduction; pagination enforced when used.
78	
79	### Pagination [INV]
80	
81	- `page`: "100 invoices will be returned per call as the default when the page parameter is used by itself e.g. page=1".
82	- `pageSize`: "Sets the # of invoices to be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used with the page parameter e.g. page=1&pageSize=250."
83	- Max pageSize for Invoices is NOT stated on the invoices page — UNVERIFIED (the Purchase Orders and Payments pages both state "Up to 1000" for their endpoints).
84	- Paging returns full line item details for each invoice; without paging, multi-invoice GETs return contact summary and NO line details. Querying by Statuses enforces pagination by default. [INV]
85	- Default order: "UpdatedDateUTC ASC, InvoiceId ASC". Optimised order fields: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date. [INV]
86	
87	---
88	
89	## 2. LineAmountTypes — exact values [TYP]
90	
91	Verbatim from the Types page ("LineAmount Types"):
92	
93	| Value | Meaning (verbatim) |
94	|---|---|
95	| `Exclusive` | "Line items are exclusive of tax" |
96	| `Inclusive` | "Line items are inclusive tax" |
97	| `NoTax` | "Line have no tax" |
98	
99	Default when omitted on create: Exclusive ("Line amounts are exclusive of tax by default if you don't specify this element"). [INV][PO]
100	
101	---
102	
103	## 3. Purchase Orders
104	
105	Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [PO]
106	
107	### Trimmed field shape [PO]
108	
109	| Field | Notes |
110	|---|---|
111	| Contact | Required. "The PurchaseOrders endpoint does not create new contacts. You need to provide the ContactID or ContactNumber of an existing contact." |
112	| LineItems | Required. "At least one LineItem is required to create a complete PurchaseOrder." Line fields: Description (a line can be Description-only), Quantity ("If Quantity is specified then a UnitAmount must be specified"), UnitAmount (rounded to FOUR decimal places), ItemCode/ItemID, AccountCode/AccountID, TaxType, DiscountRate, Tracking (max 2), LineItemID (same delete-if-omitted update rule as invoices), TaxAmount, LineAmount. |
113	| Date | Optional, YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to current date in org timezone. |
114	| DeliveryDate | Optional. "Date the goods are to be delivered – YYYY-MM-DD". |
115	| PurchaseOrderNumber | "Unique alpha numeric code identifying purchase order (when missing will auto-generate from your Organisation Invoice Settings)". |
116	| Reference | "Additional reference number". |
117	| Status | See Purchase Order Status Codes. Optional on create (examples create DRAFT by default or pass "AUTHORISED" explicitly). |
118	| LineAmountTypes | Exclusive by default if not specified. |
119	| CurrencyCode / CurrencyRate | Defaults to org base currency; CurrencyRate 1 for base-currency POs. |
120	| SubTotal / TotalTax / Total | Total = "Total of Purchase Order tax inclusive (i.e. SubTotal + TotalTax)". |
121	| DeliveryAddress / AttentionTo / Telephone / DeliveryInstructions (500 max) / ExpectedArrivalDate | Optional delivery block. |
122	| PurchaseOrderID | Xero-generated GUID, unique within org. |
123	
124	### Status enum [TYP][PO]
125	
126	Purchase Order Statuses (Types page lists exactly, with no per-status descriptions): `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED`.
127	
128	- How to update status: POST to `/PurchaseOrders/{PurchaseOrderID}` (or with PurchaseOrderNumber in the body) with the new `Status`. Official example updates a draft PO to `"Status": "DELETED"`. [PO]
129	- Marking as billed: BILLED appears as a valid status value ([TYP]) and in GET response examples ([PO]), so POST with `"Status": "BILLED"` is the implied mechanism, but neither page shows an explicit mark-as-billed example or a PO status transition table — transition legality between PO statuses is UNVERIFIED on these pages.
130	- SentToContact "can be set only on purchase orders that have been approved or billed". [PO]
131	
132	### Create/update endpoints and envelope [PO]
133	
134	- POST PurchaseOrders: "Use this method to create or update purchase orders."
135	- PUT PurchaseOrders: "you can only create new purchase orders with this method."
136	- Envelope: single object or `{"PurchaseOrders": [ ... ]}` (array form shown in SummarizeErrors example); `?SummarizeErrors=false` gives per-PO `StatusAttributeString` OK/ERROR + `ValidationErrors`.
137	- Line item update rules identical to invoices (LineItemID present=update, absent=delete+recreate). [PO]
138	
139	### Filtering and pagination [PO]
140	
141	- Query params: `Status` (e.g. `?status=DRAFT`), `DateFrom`/`DateTo` (e.g. `?DateFrom=2015-12-01&DateTo=2015-12-31`), `order`, `page`, `pageSize`; ModifiedAfter via `If-Modified-Since` header. A generic `where` parameter is NOT listed among the PO optional parameters (unlike Invoices) — where-clause support on POs: UNVERIFIED.
142	- Pagination: "When you retrieve multiple purchase orders, paging is enforced by default. 100 purchase orders are returned per page." `page` alone = 100 per call; `pageSize`: "Up to 1000 purchase orders will be returned per call when the pageSize parameter is used e.g. page=1&pageSize=250."
143	- High volume threshold: >100k POs in response denied; unoptimised filter/order resulting in >100k denied with 400.
144	
145	---
146	
147	## 4. Payments (paying an ACCPAY bill)
148	
149	Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments` — methods GET, PUT, POST. [PAY]
150	
151	### Exact fields to pay an ACCPAY invoice — PUT Payments [PAY]
152	
153	Minimum (official single-payment example):
154	
155	```json
156	{
157	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
158	  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
159	  "Date": "2009-09-08",
160	  "Amount": 32.06
161	}
162	```
163	
164	- Identifier: provide exactly ONE identifier object (Invoice, CreditNote, Prepayment, or Overpayment) — for bills, `Invoice` with `InvoiceID` (an example also identifies by `InvoiceNumber`). [PAY]
165	- Account: either `AccountID` or `Code` ("note: not all accounts have a code value"). [PAY]
166	- Date: "Date the payment is being made (YYYY-MM-DD)". [PAY]
167	- Amount: "The amount of the payment in the currency of the Invoice... Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice". [PAY]
168	- Optional: Reference, IsReconciled (boolean; useful for conversion scenarios), BankAmount, CurrencyRate, Status. [PAY]
169	- Envelope: single object, or `{"Payments": [ ... ]}` for multiple. `?SummarizeErrors=false` returns per-payment ValidationErrors. [PAY]
170	- Batch alternative: "To pay multiple AR or AP invoices in a single transaction use the BatchPayments endpoint." [PAY]
171	
172	### Account prerequisites [PAY]
173	
174	Verbatim: the account "needs to be either an account of type BANK or have enable payments to this accounts switched on (see GET Accounts). See the edit account screen of your Chart of Accounts in Xero if you wish to enable payments for an account other than a bank account." (The flag name `EnablePaymentsToAccount` lives on the Accounts endpoint — the exact property name is not printed on the Payments page: field name UNVERIFIED here, requirement itself CONFIRMED.)
175	
176	### Partial payments [PAY][INV]
177	
178	- Amount may be any value "less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing" — partial payments are allowed. [PAY]
179	- Invoice status stays AUTHORISED while partially paid: AUTHORISED = "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" [TYP]; "Once an invoice is fully paid the system will move it to the PAID status." [INV]
180	
181	### Required invoice status [PAY][INV]
182	
183	- PUT Payments is for "approved AR and AP invoices" ("Apply payments to approved AR and AP invoices"). [PAY]
184	- Invoices page: only at AUTHORISED — "You can now apply payments to the invoice." DRAFT/SUBMITTED create no journals and cannot take payments. [INV]
185	
186	### Documented rejection/validation causes
187	
188	- Amount greater than outstanding amount owing (violates the documented Amount constraint). [PAY]
189	- Account not of type BANK and not payments-enabled (violates documented account prerequisite). [PAY]
190	- "A CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect" (also for calculated rate from BankAmount). [PAY]
191	- Per-item `ValidationErrors` surfaced via SummarizeErrors=false; examples elsewhere show messages like "Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR" and "A Contact must be specified for this type of transaction" (from the PO page's example). [PAY][PO]
192	- An explicit list of payment rejection error messages is NOT published on the Payments page — UNVERIFIED beyond the above.
193	
194	### Delete / reverse a payment [PAY]
195	
196	- "Payments cannot be modified, only created and deleted."
197	- POST Payments = "delete (reverse) payments". POST to the resource ID: `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` with body `{ "Status": "DELETED" }`.
198	- Exclusion: "payments created via batch payments and receipts are not supported" for this delete method.
199	- Payment Status Codes: `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`. [TYP]
200	
201	### Multicurrency / CurrencyRate [PAY]
202	
203	- "A payment is multicurrency if the payment account, or the invoice paid against, are in a currency other than the base currency of the organisation."
204	- `Amount` is in the INVOICE currency; `BankAmount` is "the amount being paid in the currency of the account. When this is set, Xero will calculate the appropriate CurrencyRate. If you know this amount, specifying it is preferred to setting CurrencyRate. BankAmount is currently only supported for payments of AR and AP invoices."
205	- `CurrencyRate`: "Exchange rate when payment is received... For non-multicurrency payments, has a value of 1."
206	
207	---
208	
209	## 5. From the Types page [TYP]
210	
211	### Invoice Types
212	| `ACCPAY` | "A bill – commonly known as an Accounts Payable or supplier invoice" |
213	| `ACCREC` | "A sales invoice – commonly known as an Accounts Receivable or customer invoice" |
214	
215	### Invoice Status Codes (verbatim)
216	| DRAFT | "A Draft Invoice (default)" |
217	| SUBMITTED | "An Awaiting Approval Invoice" |
218	| DELETED | "A Deleted Invoice" |
219	| AUTHORISED | "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" |
220	| PAID | "An Invoice that is completely Paid" |
221	| VOIDED | "A Voided Invoice" |
222	
223	### Payment Types (verbatim)
224	| ACCRECPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Payment |
225	| ACCPAYPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Payment |
226	| ARCREDITPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Credit Payment (Refund) |
227	| APCREDITPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Credit Payment (Refund) |
228	| AROVERPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Overpayment Payment (Refund) |
229	| ARPREPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Receivable Prepayment Payment (Refund) |
230	| APPREPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Prepayment Payment (Refund) |
231	| APOVERPAYMENTPAYMENT | Accounts Payable Overpayment Payment (Refund) |
232	
233	### Payment Status Codes
234	`AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`.
235	
236	### Purchase Order Statuses
237	`DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED` (no descriptions or transition rules given on the Types page).
238	
239	### Currency handling on Types page
240	The Types page contains NO section on currency codes or CurrencyRate. All CurrencyRate/multicurrency facts above come from the Payments/Invoices/PO pages ([PAY][INV][PO]). Anything about supported currency code lists: UNVERIFIED on these four pages.
241	
242	---
243	
244	## Cross-cutting notes for the match/pay engine
245	
246	- Idempotency check-by-reference: for ACCPAY, `InvoiceNumber` is NON-unique [INV], so existence checks must combine fields (e.g. Contact + InvoiceNumber + Total) or store InvoiceID after create.
247	- Correct bill-payment flow per docs: create bill (POST, Type=ACCPAY, ContactID, LineItems) -> transition DRAFT/SUBMITTED -> AUTHORISED (POST status update) -> PUT Payments (Invoice.InvoiceID, Account.Code|AccountID, Date, Amount) -> Xero flips to PAID when fully paid. Reverse a payment via POST /Payments/{PaymentID} {"Status":"DELETED"}; void an unpaid approved bill via Status=VOIDED. [INV][PAY]
248
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 11:59:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md`

```text
1	# Xero Auth + API Conventions — Verified Fact Sheet
2	
3	Verified 2026-07-04 against the live official docs at developer.xero.com. Pages are client-side rendered (plain HTTP fetch returns an empty shell), so each page was rendered in Chrome and the visible text extracted. Every fact below is tagged with the source URL. Facts that could not be confirmed on the fetched pages are marked UNVERIFIED.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Custom Connections
8	
9	Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections/
10	
11	- **Token endpoint**: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`
12	- **Auth on the token request**: `Authorization: "Basic " + base64encode(client_id + ":" + client_secret)` with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`
13	- **grant_type**: `client_credentials` — "The request body will need to contain the grant type (client_credentials) and scope"
14	- **Scopes — configured at BOTH ends**:
15	  - At app creation: "Next you'll need to select the API scopes your integration will need and who will authorise the connection." (step 2)
16	  - In the token request body: `scope=A space separated list of the scopes required`
17	  - The Client Credentials guide adds: "Note: this differs from the Code and PKCE grant types where scopes are requested as parameters in the URL." (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/client-credentials/)
18	  - Scope changes coming: "From 29 April 2026, we're changing how scopes work for custom connections... All custom connections will have access to granular scopes... New custom connections will no longer have access to the `accounting.journals.read` scope... If you have an existing custom connection, you can continue using broad scopes until September 2027."
19	- **Token response fields**: `access_token`, `expires_in` ("The amount of seconds until the access token expires"), `token_type: Bearer`, `scope`. The access token is a JWT.
20	- **Token lifetime**: The custom-connections page itself gives no number (its example JWT has `nbf: 1589363023`, `exp: 1589364823` = 1800 s). The explicit statement is on the Token types page: "They are only valid for up to 30 minutes" — https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/token-types/
21	- **Bound to ONE organisation** (exact quote): "Custom Connections are a premium integration option that utilise the client credentials grant type to access data from a single Xero organisation. If your app needs to connect to multiple Xero organisations then you should use the code flow or PKCE flow instead."
22	- **Region restriction**: "Custom Connections are only available for Xero organisations in Australia, New Zealand, the UK, and the US and require an additional monthly subscription." Free connection to the Xero Demo Company for development; Custom Connection apps do not count toward the two-uncertified-app limit.
23	- **xero-tenant-id header**: UNVERIFIED as an explicit statement — the current custom-connections page never mentions the header. What the page does show (step 7, "Call the Xero API"): "Make calls against the Xero APIs by simply adding the following headers to your request:" followed by an example containing ONLY two headers:
24	  ```
25	  GET ../api.xro/2.0/Invoices
26	  Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token
27	  Accept: application/json
28	  ```
29	  i.e. no `xero-tenant-id` header in the documented call. Contrast with standard (code-flow) tokens, where the Token types page requires it: "Make calls against the Xero APIs by simply adding the following headers to your request: `Authorization: "Bearer " + access_token`; `xero-tenant-id: The tenantId context for the request.`" with example `xero-tenant-id: 45e4708e-d862-4111-ab3a-dd8cd03913e1` (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/token-types/). So the header is omitted from the custom-connection call example, but the sentence "the xero-tenant-id header is not required" does not appear on the current page.
30	- Related distinction (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/client-credentials/): "Custom Connection apps use a modified version of client credentials which is not described on this page. They can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints." Plain client-credentials tokens are the opposite: non-tenanted endpoints only.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 2. Rate limits
35	
36	Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/
37	
38	- **Per tenant** ("organisation, account or practice"):
39	  - Concurrent Limit: **5 calls in progress at one time**
40	  - Minute Limit: **60 calls per minute**
41	  - Daily Limit: **1,000 calls per day for starter, 5,000 calls per day for higher tiers**
42	- **App-wide**: "There is also a limit to the number of API calls your app can make per minute across all tenants. App Minute Limit: **10,000 calls per minute**"
43	- **Quota headers** (exact quote): "Each API response you receive will include the `X-DayLimit-Remaining`, `X-MinLimit-Remaining` and `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining` headers telling you the number of remaining against each limit."
44	- **429 behaviour** (exact quote): "Exceeding a rate limit will result in an HTTP 429 (too many requests) response. It will include an `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` header telling you which limit you have reached."
45	- **Retry-After** (exact quote): "If you have exceed the minute or daily limit you will also receive a `Retry-After` http header that tells you how many seconds to wait before making another request. Requests are counted against a fixed window which will reset at different times for each tenant. It is important to use the Retry-After header to know when you can start making calls again."
46	- **Limits are per tenant, not shared**: "the limits are per tenant. For example if two separate Xero organisations are connected to your application, each connection would have 5000 API calls available in a given 24 hour period for apps in the Core tier and above and 1000 API calls available for apps in the Starter tier."
47	- **Request size limit**: "The maximum request size limit for all APIs is 10MB." Recommended batching: "bundles of up to 50" elements. (Elsewhere on the page: "a ceiling of about 50 nodes per request is practical – this will ensure a request does not exceed the maximum size of 3.5MB.")
48	- **Uncertified app limits**: "each organisation or practice is limited to connecting a maximum of two uncertified apps." New apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections; Core gives up to 50 connections.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 3. Accounting API request conventions
53	
54	Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses
55	
56	- **Base URL / format**: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Resource}`. "By default all successful responses on the accounting API are returned as XML." JSON via `Accept: application/json`. Individual Invoices, Quotes and Credit Notes can be returned as PDF via `Accept: application/pdf`.
57	- **JSON date format**: Microsoft .NET style, e.g. `"DateTimeUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790)\/"` — "a unix timestamp value, but in miliseconds rather than seconds".
58	
59	### Pagination
60	- Endpoints (exact quote): "Paged results are available on the **Invoices, Contacts, CreditNotes, BankTransactions, ManualJournals, Payments, PurchaseOrders, Prepayments and Overpayments** endpoints."
61	- "To utilise paging you must append a page query parameter to the URL e.g. `?page=1`."
62	- Page size (exact quote): "The default page size is **100**, with a maximum of **1000** and a minimum of **1**. If users set a value outside the supported range, we automatically adjust it to the nearest supported page size. For example, `?page=1&pageSize=5000` will return 1000 items."
63	- Responses include a pagination object: `"pagination": { "page": 1, "pageSize": 10, "pageCount": 1, "itemCount": 1 }` (supersedes the old "fetch next page until empty" approach).
64	- The Limits page adds: endpoints supporting pagination let you "retrieve line item details for 100 invoices at a time"; Journals are "retrieved only in batches of 100" (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/).
65	
66	### If-Modified-Since
67	- Exact quote: "specify a UTC timestamp filter using the If-Modified-Since http parameter. Only items created or updated since the specified timestamp will be returned (accurate to the second)."
68	- "We recommend all calls to any endpoints that have large result sets use the if-modified-since parameter."
69	- Caveat (exact quote): "Not all changes will trigger a change of the UpdatedDateUTC field. These include changes to partially paid transactions which don't generate a journal such as DueDate or SentToContact, and Contact fields pulled from other sources such as Balances, IsSupplier, and isCustomer. As a result, transactions with these changes may not be returned with an If-Modified-Since query."
70	
71	### "where" filter
72	- "The where parameter allows you to filter on endpoints and elements that don't have explicit parameters... it must be encoded using percent encoding before it is appended to the URL."
73	- Exact examples from the docs:
74	  1. Strings use double quotes: `Type=="BANK"` → percent encoded: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts?where=Type%3D%3D%22BANK%22`
75	  2. Numbers and dates, `and`/`&&` to connect conditions: `AmountDue > 5000 and DueDate > DateTime(2015, 01, 01)`
76	  3. Date range: `Date >= DateTime(2015, 01, 01) && Date < DateTime(2015, 12, 31)`
77	- Performance warning: "Long, complex where queries can cause time outs... restrict your queries to simple == operations."
78	- Invoices and Contacts also have explicit comma-separated filter params, e.g. `?IDs=...,...`, `?ContactIDs=...&Statuses=AUTHORISED`, `?Statuses=DELETED,VOIDED`.
79	- `summaryOnly=true` on GET Contacts and Invoices returns a lightweight response.
80	
81	### order
82	- "append an order query string to the endpoint URL", e.g. `?order=EmailAddress`; descending: `?order=EmailAddress%20DESC`.
83	- "Secondary ordering, using the ID field, is now applied by default across a range of endpoints – Contacts, Payments, Batch Payments, Credit Notes, Invoices and Bank Transactions... The default order is `UpdatedDateUTC ASC, [ID] ASC`."
84	
85	### summarizeErrors + validation error envelope
86	- "If you plan to submit more than one entity per API call, we recommend that you append the `summarizeErrors=false` querystring... This ensures that every entity is returned back to you, each having their own status attribute." Status per entity: OK, WARNING or ERROR.
87	- "If you have a validation error in any of your objects you will receive a HTTP 200 status code (rather than HTTP 400) if you are using the summarizeErrors parameter."
88	- "The summarizeErrors option is only available for the Accounting API."
89	- Bulk response example (quoted from the page, abridged):
90	  ```json
91	  {
92	    "Invoices": [
93	      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "OK" },
94	      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "WARNING",
95	        "Warnings": [{ "Message": "Only AUTHORISED invoices may have SentToContact updated." }] },
96	      { "InvoiceID": "5ceffde5-...", "StatusAttributeString": "ERROR",
97	        "ValidationErrors": [{ "Description": "Invoice not of valid type for creation" }] }
98	    ]
99	  }
100	  ```
101	- HTTP 400 envelope (Elements[].ValidationErrors) — exact example from the Response Codes page (https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/responsecodes): "HTTP 400 responses include an 'ApiException' element in the response that contains a useful summary of the reason for the error."
102	  ```json
103	  {
104	    "ErrorNumber": 10,
105	    "Type": "ValidationException",
106	    "Message": "A validation exception occurred",
107	    "Elements": [{
108	      "ValidationErrors": [{
109	        "Message": "Email address must be valid"
110	      }]
111	    }]
112	  }
113	  ```
114	- PUT vs POST: "A PUT method will create new data in Xero, whereas a POST will either create new data or update existing data in Xero."
115	
116	---
117	
118	## 4. Idempotency
119	
120	Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/idempotent-requests/idempotency/
121	(Not mentioned on the three primary pages; this is the official guide, reached via the docs nav "Idempotent Requests".)
122	
123	- **Idempotency-Key header IS supported** (exact quote): "To guarantee idempotency, you need to include the 'Idempotency-Key' header on your requests. Xero will then cache the response to these requests and if subsequent requests are made with the same idempotency key, they won't be processed and instead the cached response will be returned."
124	- Mutating methods only: "Xero will only process idempotency for requests that mutate data, I.e. POST, PUT and PATCH. The idempotency key header will be ignored for all other HTTP methods."
125	- Key lifetime: "keys are stored for **6 minutes** from the time of the first call, after which they expire."
126	- Key reuse with a different request → HTTP 400: `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE is used with a different request.` (Scope is per app; a "different request" = any change to endpoint URL, request body, or HTTP method.)
127	- Max key length **128 characters** (over → 400: `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE with length KEY_LENGTH is too long.`). Header name is not case-sensitive. No server-side validation of key format — uniqueness is the caller's responsibility. Xero recommends concatenating four UUIDs.
128	- Errors are cached too: "If an idempotent request errors out internally, the error will be cached and returned when the request is re-run even if the internal error is resolved."
129	- Interaction with rate limits: "Idempotency is checked after rate limits have been applied and thus duplicate requests will count towards rate limits and reduce the limit remaining headers."
130	- The guide's example request includes headers `Authorization`, `Accept`, `Xero-tenant-id`, `Idempotency-Key` on `PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts`.
131	
132	---
133	
134	## Fetch notes
135	- All three requested primary URLs returned HTTP 200 (no 404s) but required JS rendering; WebFetch timed out repeatedly, so pages were rendered in Chrome and text extracted from the live DOM.
136	- Supplementary official pages used (reached via the docs nav): Client Credentials, Token types, Tenants, Idempotent requests, Response Codes — all under developer.xero.com.
137	- Single UNVERIFIED item: an explicit docs sentence stating the `xero-tenant-id` header is/isn't required for Custom Connections (the current page only implies "not required" by omitting it from the canonical call example).
138
```
</details></tool-use>

```typescript
// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/history-note.ts
//
// History & Notes — NOT in the Xero MCP server; raw Accounting API only.
// PUT /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history appends a note (this is how the agent's
// decision note lands on a bill or PO). Notes cannot be updated, deleted, or
// backdated; they display the creation date with user "System Generated".

import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";

/** Document types that support history/notes (from the History & Notes page). */
export type HistoryParent =
  | "BankTransactions"
  | "BatchPayments"
  | "Contacts"
  | "CreditNotes"
  | "Invoices"
  | "Items"
  | "ManualJournals"
  | "Overpayments"
  | "Payments"
  | "Prepayments"
  | "PurchaseOrders"
  | "RepeatingInvoices"
  | "Quotes";

export interface HistoryRecord {
  /** Type of change, e.g. "Approved", "Edited". Notes are the only type you can create. */
  Changes?: string;
  DateUTC?: string; // "/Date(...)/" ms epoch
  User?: string; // "System Generated" for API-created records
  Details: string;
}

export interface HistoryResponse {
  HistoryRecords: HistoryRecord[];
}

const MAX_NOTE_LENGTH = 2500;

/** Append one or more notes. Exact documented shape: {"HistoryRecords":[{"Details":"..."}]} */
export async function appendNotes(
  endpoint: HistoryParent,
  guid: string,
  details: string[],
): Promise<HistoryResponse> {
  for (const note of details) {
    if (note.length > MAX_NOTE_LENGTH) {
      throw new Error(
        `NoteTooLong: ${note.length} chars — Xero caps history Details at ${MAX_NOTE_LENGTH}`,
      );
    }
  }
  return xeroFetch<HistoryResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`, {
    method: "PUT", // POST behaves identically; there is no update or delete
    json: { HistoryRecords: details.map((d) => ({ Details: d })) },
  });
}

export async function getHistory(endpoint: HistoryParent, guid: string): Promise<HistoryRecord[]> {
  const res = await xeroFetch<HistoryResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`);
  return res.HistoryRecords;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Demo entrypoint:
//   npx tsx history-note.ts <InvoiceID> "note text"
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const [guid, note] = process.argv.slice(2);
  if (!guid || !note) {
    throw new Error('Usage: npx tsx history-note.ts <InvoiceID> "note text"');
  }
  await appendNotes("Invoices", guid, [note]);
  const records = await getHistory("Invoices", guid);
  console.log(`Document now has ${records.length} history records; latest:`);
  const latest = records[0];
  if (latest) console.log(`  [${latest.User ?? "?"}] ${latest.Details}`);
}

if (require.main === module) {
  main().catch((err) => {
    console.error(err);
    process.exitCode = 1;
  });
}

```

```
