<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md`

```text
1	# Xero prompt-library skill-writing style: fact sheet
2	
3	Sources (all in `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/`):
4	- `lovable/xero-integration-single-account/SKILL.md` (707 lines)
5	- `lovable/xero-integration-multi-account/SKILL.md` (868 lines)
6	- `javascript/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant`, 452 lines)
7	- `python/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`, 502 lines)
8	- `replit/xero-integration-single-account-skill` (plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter, 293 lines)
9	- `README.md` (repo index, no skill discipline content)
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. Document structure conventions
14	
15	### Frontmatter
16	Exactly two fields, always: `name` and `description`. No version, no tools, no metadata.
17	
18	- `name` is the kebab-case skill id (`xero-integration-single-account`, `xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`).
19	- `description` is a single long paragraph that does four jobs, in roughly this order:
20	  1. **What it builds**, with the discriminating word in CAPS: "Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation".
21	  2. **When to use it**: "Use whenever building a multi-tenant SaaS on Xero".
22	  3. **What it covers**, as a comma list of the hard parts: "Covers the full OAuth2 + PKCE lifecycle with the official `xero-node` SDK, per-user encrypted token storage, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits)."
23	  4. **A read-first command and a routing rule to the sibling skill**: "Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see 'Do you actually need this?' at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach."
24	
25	  The Replit variant adds an anti-pattern warning right in the description: "Read this before writing any Xero code; do NOT rely on generic xero-node knowledge."
26	
27	### Section order (consistent across all four full skills)
28	1. `# H1` restating the variant in plain words: "Multi-tenant Xero (each user connects their own Xero)".
29	2. **Variant check / decision gate first.** A blockquote or `## Do you actually need this?` section forcing the reader to confirm they picked the right skill, with an explicit pointer to the sibling: "Stop and use **`xero-integration-single-account`** instead." Each pair of skills cross-references the other by exact name. The deciding question is stated as one sentence: "The deciding question is *how many Xero logins exist*".
30	3. **Runtime context** ("Where this code runs in Lovable") — the platform facts that shape all code below, stated as numbered "runtime facts".
31	4. `## Decision check before building` — a 3-item ALL-must-be-true checklist.
32	5. `## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)` — see §2 below.
33	6. Numbered build sections in lifecycle order: `## 1. Register your own Xero app` → `## Scopes` → `## 2. Per-user token store` → `## 3.` (client/redirect resolver) → `## 4.` (start consent) → `## 5.` (callback) → `## 6.` (use per request / status UX).
34	7. Cross-cutting sections: `## Date + money + tax normalisation`, `## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)`, `## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)`, platform-gotcha subsection, `## Security checklist (do not skip)`, `## Don't want to hand-roll it?` (managed alternatives: Nango, Composio).
35	8. `## Verify` — always last. A numbered end-to-end acceptance list (they even use step `0.` and `0.5.` to force pre-click checks before step 1). Every item is observable behavior, e.g. "Post the same invoice twice with the same idempotency key and confirm only one invoice is created."
36	
37	### Description/heading phrasing
38	- Headings carry the verdict, not just the topic: "The redirect-URI resolver — read carefully", "Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)", "Security checklist (do not skip)", "CRITICAL: path mapping", "Failure handling (the rule that bites)".
39	- Section intros state *why the section exists* in one or two sentences before any code.
40	- Every code block opens with a file-path comment (`// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts`) so the reader knows where it lives.
41	- Sync-maintenance notes between sibling docs: "(This table is identical to the single-account skill — keep the two in sync if Xero's assignments change.)"
42	- External source-of-truth links with a staleness warning: "Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes"
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 2. Pre-flight check discipline (verbatim quotes)
47	
48	The Lovable skills open the build with a dedicated section. Header, verbatim:
49	
50	> ## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
51	>
52	> Three checks. Each one prevents a class of bug that produces a misleading
53	> upstream error from Xero. Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a
54	> human.
55	
56	**Check 1 — assert every secret non-empty, with the causal chain stated** (verbatim):
57	
58	> ### 1. Assert every secret is set and non-empty
59	>
60	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
61	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Fail fast
62	> with a clear message inside `xeroStart` **before** building the URL:
63	
64	```ts
65	function requireEnv(name: string) {
66	  const v = process.env[name];
67	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
68	    throw new Error(`${name} is empty — set it in Project Secrets and retry.`);
69	  }
70	  return v;
71	}
72	```
73	
74	> Call `requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID")`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_SCOPES`, and
75	> `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` at the top of every handler that uses them.
76	
77	**Check 2 — log the constructed request once, never the secret** (verbatim):
78	
79	> You want a server log you can read when something is wrong, without ever
80	> printing the secret value itself:
81	
82	```ts
83	console.log("[xero] authorize", {
84	  client_id_len: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID?.length,
85	  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
86	  scope: process.env.XERO_SCOPES,
87	});
88	```
89	
90	> If the next failure is `unauthorized_client` or `invalid redirect_uri`, this
91	> line tells you *exactly* which value Xero rejected.
92	
93	Note the trick: log `client_id_len` (the length), never the value.
94	
95	**Check 3 — smoke-test before any human clicks** (verbatim):
96	
97	> ### 3. Smoke-test `xeroStart` BEFORE clicking Connect
98	>
99	> Call the server function and inspect the returned URL: `client_id` must be a
100	> non-empty hex string, and `redirect_uri` must **exactly** match one of the URIs
101	> registered in your Xero app (including scheme, host, and path). If either is
102	> wrong, fix it here. Do not proceed to the popup — Xero's errors past this point
103	> are far less useful than the URL you can read right now.
104	
105	**Check 4 — validate key material shape, with the full failure story** (verbatim):
106	
107	> AES-256-GCM requires a **32-byte** key. A 32-character random string is NOT 32
108	> bytes once base64-decoded (it's ~24), and `createCipheriv` throws
109	> `Invalid key length` from deep inside `encrypt()` — which surfaces as an
110	> opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the
111	> single-use `code`. The user sees "something went wrong" with no way to retry
112	> without re-consenting, and no breadcrumb in the logs points at the key.
113	
114	Pattern to copy: each pre-flight check names (a) the invariant, (b) the misleading downstream symptom if skipped, (c) the exact code to assert it, (d) the moment to run it ("before you wire the Connect button to a human"). Boot-time assertion + named error, exactly the CLAUDE.md rule "Secrets in `.env` only, asserted non-empty at boot with named errors."
115	
116	They also make the *failure path itself* testable — Verify step 0.5, verbatim:
117	
118	> 0.5. **Force an encryption failure once.** Temporarily set `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` to
119	>    a 16-char string and click Connect. Confirm `/dashboard` shows
120	>    `?error=callback&detail=…` (with a readable message) rather than a blank
121	>    500 — this proves both the callback try/catch (§4) and the dashboard
122	>    banner (§6) actually wire failures back to the user. Restore the real
123	>    key afterwards.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## 3. Error → real-cause mappings (verbatim — the gold)
128	
129	These docs mostly encode error→cause as prose sentences and blockquote "traps", not literal tables. Every mapping found, copied verbatim with source:
130	
131	**`unauthorized_client` ← empty/missing client_id** (lovable single + multi, Pre-flight §1):
132	
133	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
134	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause.
135	
136	**`invalid redirect_uri` / `unauthorized_client` ← three redirect-origin traps** (lovable, §3 header block, verbatim):
137	
138	> ### ⚠️ Three traps, all of which produce `invalid redirect_uri` or `unauthorized_client`
139	>
140	> **Trap A — `getRequest()` / `request.url` on the server returns
141	> `http://localhost:8080`.** Under the Worker runtime the inbound request you
142	> see inside a server function is on the internal dev host, not the public URL.
143	> Never derive `redirect_uri` from it.
144	>
145	> **Trap B — In the Lovable editor, `window.location.origin` is
146	> `https://<project-id>.lovableproject.com`** (the iframe host), which is NOT
147	> a registered URI. Sending it raw produces `invalid redirect_uri` every time.
148	>
149	> **Trap C — A single `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret can't cover both preview and
150	> published.** If you set it to the published host the preview breaks (and
151	> vice-versa). Use the resolver below instead.
152	
153	**`unauthorized_client` / invalid-redirect ← port or trailing-slash mismatch** (javascript, Local-dev gotchas):
154	
155	> **Redirect URI must match exactly.** The most common local failure is a port or
156	> trailing-slash mismatch between `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and what's registered in the
157	> portal. If consent returns `unauthorized_client` or an invalid-redirect error,
158	> this is almost always why. Run the dev server on the same port you registered.
159	
160	**403 ← missing write scope** (lovable multi, Scopes; wrong-secret-shaped failures are scope failures here):
161	
162	> Shipping a read-only token and then calling `POST /Invoices` fails
163	> at runtime with a 403 / insufficient-scope error — and because scopes **cannot be
164	> widened on an existing token**, the only fix is to send every connected customer
165	> back through the whole consent flow. So decide up front: read, write, or both.
166	
167	and the compressed single-account form:
168	
169	> Mismatched scope → 403 at runtime and the only fix is
170	> to send the user through consent again.
171	
172	and the silent-default variant (lovable multi):
173	
174	> **Make the `XERO_SCOPES` fallback in your shared Xero config match your intent** — an
175	> all-`.read` default will silently hand every customer a read-only token if the
176	> secret is missing, and your first write will 403.
177	
178	**Opaque 500 on callback ← wrong-length `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (lovable, Pre-flight §4): see §2 above ("`createCipheriv` throws `Invalid key length` … surfaces as an opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the single-use `code`").
179	
180	**`TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` ← client recreated without state** (javascript, §5 callback, verbatim code comment):
181	
182	> // MUST recreate the client with the SAME state you used to build the consent URL.
183	> // `apiCallback` runs openid-client's state check: because the consent URL carries a
184	> // `state` param, the callback client must also know it, or you get the cryptic
185	> // `TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` and every connect fails.
186	
187	**401 `No connection found for replid ...` ← Repl not bound to the connector** (replit, setup checklist):
188	
189	> **Until the Repl is bound,
190	> every live call fails with `401` and a body like
191	> `No connection found for replid ... with connector: xero`.** If you see that
192	> 401, the code is probably fine — the binding is missing.
193	
194	**404 ← friendly alias paths** (replit, CRITICAL: path mapping):
195	
196	> The connector blueprint advertises friendly aliases like `/accounting/contacts` and
197	> `/accounting/invoices` — **these are wrong and return 404.** Use Xero's real
198	> REST paths with PascalCase resource names.
199	
200	> Verified empirically: `/connections` → 200, `/accounting/organisations` → 404,
201	> `/api.xro/2.0/Organisations` → 200.
202	
203	**Blank white page ← Xero consent framed in an iframe** (lovable + javascript):
204	
205	> Xero's login refuses to be framed (`frame-ancestors`), so a same-frame
206	> redirect from the Lovable preview iframe shows a **blank white page** that
207	> looks like the button is broken.
208	
209	**Access token dies in ~30 min, no recovery ← `offline_access` omitted** (all four):
210	
211	> **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and you get an access token
212	> that dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.
213	
214	**400 ← same idempotency key, different request** (all four, Idempotency "Rules that bite"):
215	
216	> **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A
217	> request differs if the URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key alongside the
218	> record and reuse the *identical* request when retrying.
219	
220	**Cached error ← retrying an errored keyed request** (all four):
221	
222	> **Errors are cached too.** If the keyed request errored internally, retrying with the
223	> *same* key returns the *same cached error* even after the underlying problem is
224	> fixed. To recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if
225	> not, create again with a **new** key.
226	
227	**429 ← rate limits, with the recovery** (all four):
228	
229	> **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide
230	> per-minute cap and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header
231	> and back off — multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Spread/queue
232	> calls per tenant.
233	
234	**Silent wrong-books write ← missing/wrong tenant header** (all four):
235	
236	> **Always send `Xero-Tenant-Id`** on every API call. There is no implicit "current
237	> org." With multiple connected orgs, the wrong id silently writes to the wrong
238	> company's books.
239	
240	**Next refresh fails ← rotated refresh token not persisted** (all four):
241	
242	> **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on
243	> every refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time or the next
244	> refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.
245	
246	**Sibling orgs flip to needs_reconnect ← only one row updated after rotation** (javascript §6, code comment, verbatim):
247	
248	> // CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: when one consent authorized several orgs, they all
249	> // share ONE token set. Refreshing rotates the refresh token and INVALIDATES the
250	> // old one, so updating only THIS row leaves every sibling org holding a dead token
251	> // (they'll wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on their next call). Update ALL rows
252	> // that shared the previous token set — e.g. match on the old ciphertext:
253	
254	**Duplicate contacts ← posting invoices with a contact name** (all four):
255	
256	> **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new
257	> contact name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or
258	> `ContactID`) via `getContacts` and reuse the id; otherwise you litter the org with
259	> duplicates.
260	
261	The one true error-shaped *table* in the corpus is the Replit **path mapping table** (Purpose | Path) plus its "verified empirically" line — the pattern being: verified request → expected status, wrong path → 404.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 4. Scope minimalism rules
266	
267	Canonical statement, repeated near-verbatim in all four skills:
268	
269	> Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run the OAuth consent
270	> flow to add more later. You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user
271	> must re-consent. To get a refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
272	
273	Supporting rules:
274	
275	- "**Always prefer the granular scopes** — use `accounting.invoices`, NOT `accounting.transactions`." (Broad scopes deprecated: granular assigned to all Web/PKCE apps since March 2026, custom connections since 29 April 2026, broad dead September 2027.)
276	- "**Read vs write — choose deliberately; you can't change it later without re-consent.** `.read` scopes are GET-only."
277	- Lovable adds an agent-behavior rule: "Do not ask the user what scopes to use - set the appropriate scopes for the integration to function."
278	- Replit: "**Scopes** — see the dedicated section below. Request the minimum set for what you're doing".
279	
280	Exact scope strings quoted in the docs:
281	
282	- Read-only dashboard (lovable single):
283	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`
284	- Write / invoicing app ("A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants", all four):
285	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`
286	- Typical invoice flow (replit):
287	  `offline_access openid email profile accounting.settings.read accounting.invoices accounting.contacts`
288	
289	Scope reference tables they maintain (structure worth copying): (1) identity scopes `offline_access` / `openid` / `profile` / `email`; (2) "Deprecated (broad) → New granular scopes" migration table (`accounting.transactions` → `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals`; same for `.read`; `accounting.reports.read` → eight `accounting.reports.*.read` scopes); (3) "Scope | Grants" table mapping each granular scope to the resources it unlocks (`accounting.invoices` → Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items; `accounting.payments` → Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments); (4) "never broad, use as-is" table (`accounting.settings`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.journals.read`, `accounting.budgets.read`). Always footed with the developer.xero.com scopes URL and "re-check periodically — dates and assignments change".
290	
291	---
292	
293	## 5. Token handling rules
294	
295	- **Lifetimes, stated with numbers every time:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days, **rotates on every refresh**, dies if unused for 60 days. Expiry stored as `expires_at` computed from `expires_in` seconds: `new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()` (JS) / `token["expires_at"]` epoch-seconds dict key (Python).
296	- **Refresh early, not at expiry:** JS refreshes when `expires_at <= now + 60_000` (60-second buffer); Python "Proactively refresh if within ~2 min of expiry" (`expires_at - time.time() < 120`).
297	- **Persist rotation immediately or break:** "tokenSet = await res.json();   // refresh_token ROTATES — must persist". And update **all sibling rows** sharing the old ciphertext (multi-org consents share one token set) — match siblings on the OLD ciphertext.
298	- **No long-lived clients / no caching:** "Make a fresh client per request and `setTokenSet(...)` from the DB. Do not keep a long-lived client — tokens expire and clients are per-user." Replit: "Never cache this — create a fresh instance per request (tokens rotate)."
299	- **Refresh failure is a state, not a retry loop:** "On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — do not silently retry forever." Status enum in the schema: `'active' | 'needs_reconnect'`. Surface it in UI with a reconnect CTA; a 60-day-idle token dies silently, so add a scheduled keep-alive refresh, not just reactive detection.
300	- **Storage:** tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM, key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` in the secret store, exactly 32 bytes base64); rotating token lives in the **database (a mutable store), never in a secret**; "never put per-user tokens in env".
301	- **Never-log / never-expose:** log `client_id_len`, not the value ("without ever printing the secret value itself"); "**Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage"; "Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`; never commit credentials"; status endpoints return "only non-sensitive metadata (never the token)".
302	- **`state` discipline:** random, persisted server-side with a 10-minute expiry, verified on callback, deleted after use — "single-use, replay-proof".
303	
304	---
305	
306	## 6. Tone and format
307	
308	- **Imperative second person, no hedging.** "Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a human." "Stop and use X instead." "If you reach for `xero-node` or start writing token-refresh code, stop — you are on the wrong path."
309	- **Warnings are structural, not decorative.** The single warning device is `⚠️` in a heading (`## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST`, `> ### ⚠️ Three traps...`, `> ### ⚠️ Open consent in a NEW TAB, opened SYNCHRONOUSLY in the click handler`) plus a blockquote body. Bold and CAPS carry emphasis inline: MUST, NOT, NEVER, ONE, THEIR OWN, FIRST, "byte-for-byte", "byte-match".
310	- **Every rule ships its consequence.** Not "include offline_access" but "Omit it and the access token dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent." Failure narratives are causal chains told to the end (wrong key → throw deep inside encrypt → opaque 500 → single-use code consumed → user must re-consent → no breadcrumb in logs).
311	- **Named recurring rubrics:** "Rules that bite", "(these bite in production)", "Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)", "Gotcha that fools people", "(do not skip)", "(skipping it costs hours)", "CRITICAL multi-tenant trap".
312	- **Tables vs prose:** tables only for enumerable reference data (scopes, scope grants, deprecation mapping, REST paths). Everything judgment-shaped (gotchas, security, idempotency rules) is bold-lead-in bullets: `**Rule.** Consequence and recovery.` Checklists (Decision check, Security checklist, Verify) are lists, with Verify numbered.
313	- **Code is placed, minimal, and commented with intent** — comments mark traps ("// sync!", "// refresh_token ROTATES — must persist", "// REQUIRED — wrong id writes to wrong books"), not narration. File-path comment on line 1 of each block.
314	- **Distrust of upstream errors is a theme:** fail fast locally because "Xero's errors past this point are far less useful than the URL you can read right now."
315	- **Empirical claims are labeled:** "Verified empirically", "**The paths below are verified — trust them, do not re-probe.**", and for unknowns: "probe it **read-only first** rather than guessing".
316	- Punctuation habits: heavy em-dash usage, `→` arrows for flows and mappings (`DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`; "Same key + different request → `400`"), backticked literals for every identifier, header, error string, and scope.
317	
318	---
319	
320	## 7. Platform-specific guidance — do NOT copy into a NestJS project
321	
322	Everything below is Lovable/Supabase/Replit plumbing, not Xero discipline. In NestJS: config assertion at boot (ConfigModule + named errors), one Xero module (`api/src/modules/xero/`), Postgres via your ORM, guards instead of RLS.
323	
324	**Lovable / TanStack Start / Cloudflare Workers:**
325	- `createServerFn` server functions; server routes under `src/routes/api/public/*` to "bypass Lovable's published-site auth gate". NestJS controllers are just public or guarded routes.
326	- "Do not use Supabase Edge Functions"; the legacy-port note (Deno Edge Functions, `Deno.env.get`, redeploy after secret change, `verify_jwt = false`, Web Crypto two-part `iv.ciphertext` blob) — all Deno/Supabase-specific.
327	- "Read `process.env.*` **inside** `.handler()`, not at module scope" (Worker per-request env injection). NestJS reads env once at boot — the opposite discipline.
328	- The whole `resolveRedirectUri` host-mapping resolver, `PROJECT_ID`, `id-preview--*.lovable.app` / `project--*.lovable.app` hosts, `*.lovableproject.com` iframe host, `getRequest()` returning `http://localhost:8080`. A NestJS app has one configured redirect URI per environment.
329	- Iframe/popup dance (`window.open("about:blank", "_blank")` synchronously before `await`) — Lovable editor-preview-specific; keep only the generic fact that Xero consent can't be framed.
330	- "Never import `@/integrations/supabase/client.server` at module scope of a `*.functions.ts` or route file" — client-bundle stripping concern, no NestJS equivalent.
331	- Lovable Cloud secrets / `generate_secret` with `length: 44` — keep the underlying rule (base64 of 32 random bytes), drop the tool.
332	
333	**Supabase data layer:**
334	- `supabaseAdmin`, `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, "Service-role key never goes to the browser".
335	- RLS incantations: `grant all ... to service_role`, `enable row level security`, "INTENTIONALLY no anon/authenticated policies", `auth.users(id)` FKs, `requireSupabaseAuth` middleware, `has_role(userId, 'admin')`.
336	- Supabase client returning Postgres `numeric` as strings.
337	- The unauthenticated-prototype "escape hatch" (warning banner, accepted-risks list) — Lovable demo-flow specific.
338	
339	**Replit connector:**
340	- `@replit/connectors-sdk` proxy pattern, `connectors.proxy("xero", path, ...)`, Repl binding (`proposeIntegration`/`addIntegration`), the `No connection found for replid` 401, redirect URI `https://replit.com/connectors/oauth/callback`, `pnpm --filter @workspace/<server>` install rule, Integrations → Manage reset.
341	
342	**Portable despite appearing in platform sections** (keep these): exact-match redirect URIs registered per environment; state persisted server-side because HTTP steps are separate invocations; callback errors funneled to a user-visible `?error&detail` redirect (never a bare 500, because the OAuth `code` is single-use); `Xero-Tenant-Id` on every call; idempotency keys on writes; `summarizeErrors=false`; `unitdp=4`; the `/Date(ms+offset)/` normaliser; encrypt-at-rest with validated 32-byte key; `needs_reconnect` status + keep-alive refresh; all Xero access isolated in one module.
343
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
---
name: xero-demo-seed
description: SPEC for seeding the demo Xero org so the data tells a story — the 15-supplier UK trade cast (contacts, AUTHORISED purchase orders, ACCPAY bills £80–£8,000 net with 20% UK VAT via LineAmountTypes Exclusive), EXACTLY two deliberate match gaps (Thameside Timber's £4,200 bill with NO goods receipt, and one bill £148 over its PO — outside the ±2%/£5 tolerance), the Google Sheet receipt-log schema, idempotent check-by-reference lookups with the exact where-filters, and the contacts → POs → bills → sheet ordering. Use when writing or reviewing ANYTHING in seed/, or when the demo org needs re-seeding or resetting. Read this BEFORE creating any contact, PO, or bill in the org. Facts and shapes only — the runnable seeding code lives in seed/, match/approval decisions live in api/src/modules/. For endpoint mechanics see xero-accounting-api; for MCP-vs-raw routing see xero-mcp-usage (POs have NO MCP tools — raw API only).
---

# Seeding the demo Xero org (the data must tell a story)

One seeded org: 15 UK trade supplier contacts, 15 AUTHORISED purchase orders, 15 ACCPAY bills, 14 receipt rows in a Google Sheet. 13 bills match clean; exactly 2 do not, by design — those 2 gaps ARE the demo. This file is the spec that `seed/` transcribes. It serves Gate G0; the G1 unit tests assert the exact match-states in the cast table below.

## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (before any write)

Run `scripts/preflight.ts` (read-only; compile per §Snippets). It asserts, in order:

1. **`XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are non-empty**, with named `MissingEnv` errors. Empty secrets surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Secrets live in `.env` at repo root — never committed, never logged; the script logs `client_id_len`, never the value.
2. **A `client_credentials` token mints and its granted `scope` contains `accounting.contacts` and `accounting.invoices`.** Custom-connection scopes are configured at BOTH ends — selected on the app config AND sent in the token request body. A token missing a scope 403s later at the worst moment; fail here with a named `MissingScope` error instead.
3. **`GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisations` returns 200** and the script prints the raw body so a human confirms it is the DEMO org (and that the base currency is GBP) before anything is written. Seeding a real org is unrecoverable on demo day.
4. (Manual, once) **Pick the expense account for line items**: set `XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE` in `.env` to an expense account whose default tax rate is 20% VAT on purchases. Omit `TaxType` on lines so the AccountCode default applies — that mechanism is documented; the exact UK 20% TaxType code (commonly cited as `INPUT2`) is UNVERIFIED — check before relying.

## Auth model — why there is no `XERO_TENANT_ID`

- This project uses a **Custom Connection** (`grant_type=client_credentials`), which per Xero's docs "utilise the client credentials grant type to access data from a **single Xero organisation**". One org per connection → no org ambiguity → nothing for a tenant header to disambiguate.
- Token endpoint: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` with `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...`.
- Xero's own custom-connection call example sends only `Authorization: Bearer ...` and `Accept` — **no `xero-tenant-id` header** (unlike code-flow tokens, where it is required). An explicit docs sentence "the header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — check before relying; the canonical example omitting it is the evidence.
- Token lifetime ~30 min (`expires_in` is authoritative); the response has **no `refresh_token`** — re-mint when needed. A full seed run fits in one token.
- Scopes for seeding: `accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`. Granular only (new custom connections get granular scopes from 29 April 2026); `accounting.invoices` grants Invoices AND PurchaseOrders.
- Custom Connections are UK-available (AU/NZ/UK/US only, paid subscription; free against the Xero Demo Company for development).

## The cast — 15 UK trade suppliers (transcribe exactly)

All amounts are net (ex VAT). Every document sets `"LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive"`; Xero adds 20% VAT from the account's default tax rate, so **Total = net × 1.20** (e.g. TT-8841: 4200.00 + 840.00 = 5040.00). Every net below lands on exact 2dp when multiplied by 1.2. One line item per document: `net = Qty × Unit`.

| # | Contact Name | Trade | PO # | Bill # (InvoiceNumber) | Unit £ | PO qty | Bill qty | PO net £ | Bill net £ | Line item description | Match state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Thameside Timber Ltd | Timber merchant | PO-1001 | TT-8841 | 70.00 | 60 | 60 | 4200.00 | 4200.00 | C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths | **MISSING_RECEIPT** (demo star) |
| 2 | Brixton Plumbing Merchants Ltd | Plumbers' merchant | PO-1002 | BPM-2210 | 74.00 | 25 | 27 | 1850.00 | 1998.00 | 15mm copper tube 3m and brassware bundle | **AMOUNT_MISMATCH** |
| 3 | Walthamstow Electrical Wholesale Ltd | Electrical wholesaler | PO-1003 | WEW-0345 | 93.25 | 10 | 10 | 932.50 | 932.50 | 6242Y twin and earth cable 2.5mm, 100m drum | MATCHED |
| 4 | Hackney Plant Hire Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1004 | HPH-7712 | 220.00 | 12 | 12 | 2640.00 | 2640.00 | 1.5t mini excavator, day rate | MATCHED |
| 5 | Bermondsey Skip Hire Ltd | Skip hire | PO-1005 | BSH-1108 | 340.00 | 1 | 1 | 340.00 | 340.00 | 8-yard builders skip, 2-week hire | MATCHED |
| 6 | Croydon Builders Merchants Ltd | Builders' merchant | PO-1006 | CBM-5521 | 45.00 | 84 | 84 | 3780.00 | 3780.00 | Aerated concrete blocks 100mm, per pack | MATCHED |
| 7 | Deptford Sheet Materials Ltd | Timber and sheet | PO-1007 | DSM-0092 | 31.60 | 40 | 40 | 1264.00 | 1264.00 | 18mm structural hardwood ply 2440x1220 | MATCHED |
| 8 | Ealing Electrical Factors Ltd | Electrical wholesaler | PO-1008 | EEF-4478 | 102.55 | 5 | 5 | 512.75 | 512.75 | 10-way consumer unit with RCBOs | MATCHED |
| 9 | Fulham Plumbing Supplies Ltd | Plumbers' merchant | PO-1009 | FPS-3301 | 689.95 | 1 | 1 | 689.95 | 689.95 | Unvented hot water cylinder 210L | MATCHED |
| 10 | Greenwich Tool and Plant Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1010 | GTP-6640 | 140.00 | 8 | 8 | 1120.00 | 1120.00 | Towable diesel generator, day rate | MATCHED |
| 11 | Ilford Aggregates and Skips Ltd | Skip hire and aggregates | PO-1011 | IAS-2287 | 65.00 | 12 | 12 | 780.00 | 780.00 | MOT Type 1 aggregate, bulk bag | MATCHED |
| 12 | Kentish Town Building Supplies Ltd | Builders' merchant | PO-1012 | KBS-9034 | 49.50 | 120 | 120 | 5940.00 | 5940.00 | Facing bricks, per pack | MATCHED |
| 13 | Leyton Roofing Supplies Ltd | Roofing merchant | PO-1013 | LRS-1156 | 38.50 | 60 | 60 | 2310.00 | 2310.00 | Concrete interlocking roof tiles, per bundle | MATCHED |
| 14 | Mile End Hire Centre Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1014 | MEH-8890 | 152.00 | 3 | 3 | 456.00 | 456.00 | Petrol cut-off saw, week hire | MATCHED |
| 15 | Peckham Decorating Supplies Ltd | Decorators' merchant | PO-1015 | PDS-0417 | 7.05 | 12 | 12 | 84.60 | 84.60 | Trade emulsion 10L, brilliant white | MATCHED |

Naming rules baked into the cast: no apostrophes or angle brackets (keeps `where` filters trivially encodable; Contact `Name` rejects angle brackets, leading/trailing whitespace, and repeating spaces), all references UPPERCASE-only (see Traps), net range £84.60–£5,940.00 inside the £80–£8,000 brief. Give each contact a plain-ASCII `EmailAddress` (`accounts@<slug>.co.uk`) — umlauts are unsupported.

## The story — exactly two gaps, everything else clean

- **#1 Thameside Timber (TT-8841, £4,200 net / £5,040 gross) — the demo star.** PO-1001 exists, AUTHORISED, amounts identical. The Google Sheet has **no row for PO-1001**. Expected engine verdict: `MISSING_RECEIPT`. The narrative: the biggest bill on the desk, looks perfect, and the one thing missing is proof anyone received the timber.
- **#2 Brixton Plumbing Merchants (BPM-2210).** PO-1002 authorises 25 × £74.00 = £1,850.00 net; the bill charges 27 × £74.00 = £1,998.00 net. Difference £148.00 = 8.0% of the PO. Tolerance is ±2% or £5, whichever is greater → max(£37.00, £5) = £37.00 — outside, net or gross. The sheet DOES have a receipt row for PO-1002 (qty 27, delivery was fine), so the only gap is bill vs PO: `AMOUNT_MISMATCH`.
- **#3–#15**: PO net = bill net, receipt row present with qty = bill qty → `MATCHED`. These are the auto-approve volume that makes the two exceptions stand out.

## Dates (relative to seed day D — always fresh)

| Field | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| PO `Date` | D−14 | Match rule requires PO date ≤ bill date |
| PO `DeliveryDate` | D−7 | Goods land a week later |
| Sheet `date` (receipt) | D−7 | Receipt signed on delivery day |
| Bill `Date` | D−5 | Invoice follows delivery |
| Bill `DueDate` | D+25 | 30 days from invoice, still open on demo day |

All `YYYY-MM-DD`.

## Purchase orders (raw Accounting API — no MCP tools exist for POs)

`POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders?summarizeErrors=false`

\```jsonc
{
  "PurchaseOrders": [
    {
      "Contact": { "ContactID": "<from contacts step>" }, // PO endpoint NEVER creates contacts
      "PurchaseOrderNumber": "PO-1001",                   // unique alphanumeric; auto-generated if omitted — always set it
      "Date": "<D-14>",
      "DeliveryDate": "<D-7>",
      "Status": "AUTHORISED",                             // create-as-AUTHORISED is shown in the official examples; reads as a real order
      "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
      "LineItems": [
        { "Description": "C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths",
          "Quantity": 60, "UnitAmount": 70.00, "AccountCode": "<XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE>" }
      ]
    }
    // ... one element per cast row; single batch call
  ]
}
\```

PO statuses: `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED`. No official PO status-transition table exists; anything beyond "official example deletes a DRAFT PO via Status=DELETED" is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. The demo later marks POs `BILLED` via a POST status update (implied mechanism; no official mark-as-billed example — UNVERIFIED — check before relying).

## Bills (ACCPAY invoices)

`POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?summarizeErrors=false`

\```jsonc
{
  "Invoices": [
    {
      "Type": "ACCPAY",
      "Contact": { "ContactID": "<from contacts step>" }, // NEVER a bare Name — that silently creates a duplicate contact
      "InvoiceNumber": "TT-8841",  // supplier's invoice ref; for ACCPAY this is NON-unique and displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI
      "Reference": "PO-1001",      // the PO join key the match engine reads
      "Date": "<D-5>",
      "DueDate": "<D+25>",
      "Status": "SUBMITTED",       // "Awaiting Approval" — the agent's approve action flips it to AUTHORISED live on stage
      "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
      "LineItems": [
        { "Description": "C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths",
          "Quantity": 60, "UnitAmount": 70.00, "AccountCode": "<XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE>" }
      ]
    }
    // ... one element per cast row; single batch call
  ]
}
\```

Why `SUBMITTED`: creating directly with DRAFT/SUBMITTED/AUTHORISED is documented; SUBMITTED → AUTHORISED is a legal transition and gives the demo its approval moment. Do NOT seed AUTHORISED (once AUTHORISED a bill can never go back and can only be VOIDED, and there is nothing left for the agent to do).

With `summarizeErrors=false` a batch returns **HTTP 200 even when elements fail** — the seed must check every element's `StatusAttributeString` and fail loudly printing its `ValidationErrors`. ACCPAY bills do not support discounts. UnitAmount is rounded to 2dp unless `unitdp=4` is passed — the cast is all 2dp, so don't pass it.

## Contacts

Create one per cast row (a batch `{"Contacts":[...]}` envelope is UNVERIFIED on the pages checked — 15 sequential calls are trivially inside the rate budget):

\```jsonc
// PUT https://api.xro/2.0/Contacts  (PUT = create-new per the API-wide rule)
{ "Name": "Thameside Timber Ltd", "EmailAddress": "accounts@thamesidetimber.co.uk" }
\```

Store the returned `ContactID` — Xero warns Contact `Name` "may no longer be a unique field" in future; ContactID is the only durable key. `IsSupplier` cannot be set: Xero flips it automatically when the first ACCPAY bill lands.

## Google Sheet receipt log

Columns, exactly and in this order: `date, po_ref, supplier, items, qty, signed_by, photo_link`.

One row per PO **except PO-1001** (14 rows total — the missing row IS the demo star's gap). `po_ref` joins to `PurchaseOrderNumber`; `supplier` matches Contact `Name`; `qty` = units actually received = bill qty (so #2's only defect is bill vs PO). `photo_link` points at a real delivery-note photo in Drive (live-data rule — replace placeholders before rehearsal). Three example rows (seed day 2026-07-04 → date = D−7):

| date | po_ref | supplier | items | qty | signed_by | photo_link |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-06-27 | PO-1002 | Brixton Plumbing Merchants Ltd | 15mm copper tube 3m and brassware bundle | 27 | D. Okafor | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1002 |
| 2026-06-27 | PO-1004 | Hackney Plant Hire Ltd | 1.5t mini excavator, day rate | 12 | S. Whelan | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1004 |
| 2026-06-27 | PO-1006 | Croydon Builders Merchants Ltd | Aerated concrete blocks 100mm, per pack | 84 | M. Price | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1006 |

## Idempotency — check-by-reference before EVERY create

The seed must be safe to re-run: look up by reference, skip on hit, create only what is absent. Never check by amount (see Traps). Exact lookups:

| Entity | Reference | Exact lookup | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Contact | `Name` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/Contacts?where=Name%3D%22Thameside%20Timber%20Ltd%22` (unencoded: `where=Name="Thameside Timber Ltd"`) | Optimised equals filter; case- and accent-insensitive; do NOT append `ToLower()` |
| Bill | `InvoiceNumber` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/Invoices?where=Type%3D%3D%22ACCPAY%22%20AND%20InvoiceNumber%3D%3D%22TT-8841%22&page=1` (unencoded: `where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND InvoiceNumber=="TT-8841"`) | Both fields optimised. ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique — ANY hit means "already seeded, skip" |
| Purchase order | `PurchaseOrderNumber` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100` then match `PurchaseOrderNumber` client-side | A generic `where` param is NOT documented on the PO endpoint (UNVERIFIED — check before relying) — do not use one. Paging is enforced by default; one page covers the cast; if the org ever holds >100 POs, walk `pagination.pageCount` |
| Sheet row | `po_ref` | Read the sheet, match `po_ref` string | Plain string compare, uppercase both sides |

Rules:

- The `where` value MUST be percent-encoded (spaces, quotes, `==`).
- Treat a hit whose `Status` is `DELETED` or `VOIDED` as absent and recreate (whether list GETs return deleted docs by default is UNVERIFIED — check before relying; this rule is safe either way).
- Persist returned `ContactID` / `PurchaseOrderID` / `InvoiceID` locally after create — they are the durable keys; references are only the lookup handles.
- Xero's `Idempotency-Key` header is NOT a substitute: keys expire after 6 minutes and cache errors too. Use it (one fresh UUID per create) to guard against double-submit within a run; check-by-reference is the durable guard across runs.

## Ordering — contacts → POs → bills → sheet rows

| Step | Why it cannot move |
|---|---|
| 1. Contacts | Both `/Invoices` and `/PurchaseOrders` require the `ContactID` of an EXISTING contact — the PO endpoint never creates contacts, and posting a bill with a bare contact name silently creates a duplicate contact. The ContactIDs minted here are inputs to steps 2–3. |
| 2. POs | Bills carry the PO number in `Reference`. Xero would not reject a bill referencing a nonexistent PO — the ordering is for referential truth: the match engine joins bill→PO, so the join target must exist before anything points at it. |
| 3. Bills | Consume ContactIDs (step 1) and PO numbers (step 2). |
| 4. Sheet rows | `po_ref` and `supplier` join back to steps 1–2; the sheet lives outside Xero, so it is written after Xero state is confirmed good. |

## Rate budget

60 calls/min/tenant, 5 concurrent, 5,000/day. First full run, sequential: 1 token + 1 org + 15 contact lookups + 15 contact creates + 1 PO list + 1 PO batch create + 1 bill lookup list + 1 bill batch create ≈ 36 calls — one minute of budget. Run strictly sequentially; on `429` read `Retry-After` and wait (`X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names the limit). Batch bills and POs in single `{"Invoices":[...]}` / `{"PurchaseOrders":[...]}` calls — never one call per document.

## Resetting seeded data

| Doc | Seeded status | Documented off-switch |
|---|---|---|
| Bill | SUBMITTED | POST status update to `DELETED` (legal from DRAFT/SUBMITTED). Once the demo has AUTHORISED it: `VOIDED` only — AUTHORISED can never return to DRAFT/SUBMITTED. If PAID: reverse the payment first (`POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` body `{"Status":"DELETED"}`), then void (void is documented only for approved invoices with no payments applied). |
| PO | AUTHORISED | Official delete example covers a DRAFT PO only; deleting an AUTHORISED PO is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Whether a DELETED PO's number can be reused is UNVERIFIED — if a PO must be replaced, bump to a fresh number series (PO-2001…) rather than fight it. |
| Contact | ACTIVE | No delete exists in the docs; `ContactStatus: ARCHIVED` is the only documented end-state ("can no longer be used in transactions"); the exact write path to set it is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Contacts are harmless to leave in place — re-runs skip them by Name. |

## Snippets

Both compile standalone under `strict`, `target=ES2022`, `module=commonjs`, `esModuleInterop`, `types=["node"]` — node builtins only, no npm deps, no top-level await. `.env` is loaded by the runner (e.g. `node --env-file=.env` on Node 20.6+); the scripts only read `process.env` and assert.

- `scripts/preflight.ts` — the pre-flight above: env assertions, token mint + scope check, `GET /Organisations` printout. Read-only. Run before every seed run.
- `scripts/check-by-reference.ts` — the three lookup functions with the exact filters from the idempotency table, plus a `main()` that probes the demo star's three references (Thameside Timber Ltd / PO-1001 / TT-8841) and prints EXISTS/ABSENT. Read-only. `seed/` imports the same shapes, not this file.

## Traps

- **Re-running with changed amounts creates near-duplicates.** Check by reference, never by amount. If you edit a cast amount, the reference lookup still hits the old document and skips — the org keeps the OLD amount, and "create if the amount differs" would mint a second TT-8841 (ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is non-unique; Xero will not stop you). To change an amount: delete/void the old doc first, or bump the reference.
- **ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is NON-unique** and displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI. An empty lookup does not mean Xero would block a duplicate. Store `InvoiceID` after create.
- **Xero auto-uppercases some references** UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Neutralised by construction: every seed reference (PO-1001, TT-8841) is already uppercase-only, and the match engine should compare references case-insensitively regardless.
- **Deleting seeded data is status-dependent.** Bills: DRAFT/SUBMITTED → `DELETED`; AUTHORISED → `VOIDED` only; PAID/VOIDED/DELETED are terminal. POs: only the DRAFT→DELETED path is documented. Plan resets BEFORE authorising things.
- **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated.** A bill posted with a contact `Name` instead of `ContactID` creates a NEW contact every time and litters the org. Duplicate suppliers appearing in the org = this bug.
- **Contact `Name` is a lookup handle, not a key.** Xero warns it may stop being unique; the optimised Name filter is case- and accent-insensitive; Name rejects leading/trailing whitespace and repeating spaces — trim before create AND before compare.
- **`Idempotency-Key` will not save a re-run.** Keys live 6 minutes and cache errors: a keyed request that failed returns the same cached error even after the cause is fixed — GET to check reality, recreate with a NEW key.
- **`summarizeErrors=false` hides failures in a 200.** Every batch element must be checked for `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` or the seed reports success while bills are silently missing.
- **Multi-invoice GETs without paging return NO line items.** Always add `page=1` when verifying seeded line details.
- **Updating a document's lines: omit an existing `LineItemID` and that line is DELETED.** If a re-run repairs a doc via POST update, echo every existing LineItemID.

## Errors → real cause

| Symptom | Real cause |
|---|---|
| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` empty or wrong — `client_id=undefined` got sent. Fix the env assertion trail, not Xero. |
| 401 mid-run | Access token expired (~30 min). No refresh token exists on client_credentials — mint a new token. |
| 403 on Contacts/Invoices/PurchaseOrders | Scope missing from the token: not sent in the token request body, or not enabled on the Custom Connection app config (both ends must agree). |
| 400 `Idempotency Key ... is used with a different request` | Same key reused within 6 min with a changed URL, body, or method. New create = new key. |
| The same error keeps returning after you fixed the cause | Cached idempotent error. GET to check whether the doc exists; recreate with a NEW key. |
| 429 | Rate limit — `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names which (minute/day/concurrent); wait `Retry-After` seconds. |
| HTTP 200 but documents missing in Xero | `summarizeErrors=false` batch: per-element `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` with `ValidationErrors` you did not read. |
| 400 `A validation exception occurred` | Read `Elements[].ValidationErrors`. e.g. "A Contact must be specified for this type of transaction" = PO posted without an existing ContactID. |
| Duplicate suppliers in the org | A bill was posted with `Contact: { "Name": ... }` instead of `ContactID`. |
| Bills list shows no line items | Multi-invoice GET without `page` — add `page=1`. |
| 400 on a filtered GET | `where` value not percent-encoded, or an unoptimised filter expression. |

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-auth
3	description: Xero Custom Connection (client_credentials) auth for THIS repo — exact token endpoint, the four project scopes and what each unlocks, in-memory token caching with proactive expiry and retry-on-401, why there is no XERO_TENANT_ID, and the Gate G0 verify script. Use when writing or debugging any code that obtains or uses a Xero access token, when a Xero call returns 401/403/unauthorized_client/invalid_client/invalid_scope, or before the first Xero call in a fresh environment. Read this BEFORE writing token code; there is exactly ONE token path in this repo (api/src/modules/xero/) — do not hand-roll a second.
4	---
5	
6	# Xero auth: Custom Connection, client_credentials, ONE org
7	
8	This project uses a **Custom Connection** app: client_credentials grant, bound to a **single Xero organisation**. No consent screen, no redirect URI, no refresh token, no tenant juggling. If you find yourself writing OAuth callback or PKCE code, stop — wrong flow.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` non-empty at boot, with named errors.** Empty/missing secrets that reach Xero surface as a misleading OAuth error that points away from the real cause — verified empirically 2026-07-04: an empty client id in the Basic header gets 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}` from the token endpoint (in code flow the same mistake surfaces as `unauthorized_client`). `.env` at repo root only; never committed, never logged. When logging, log lengths (`client_id_len`), never values.
13	2. **Confirm the app is a Custom Connection and has been authorised.** App creation includes selecting scopes and "who will authorise the connection" — an org admin must complete that authorisation before tokens are useful. (Exact failure mode of an unauthorised app: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. `verify-auth.ts` treats "token OK but zero connections" as this case.) Custom Connections only exist for AU/NZ/UK/US orgs and are free against the Demo Company.
14	3. **Portal scopes must cover what the token request asks for.** Scopes are configured at BOTH ends: selected on the app in the developer portal, AND sent space-separated in the token request body (unlike code/PKCE, where scopes ride the URL).
15	4. **Run Gate G0** before wiring anything else:
16	
17	```bash
18	npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
19	```
20	
21	Expected output ends with `G0 PASS` after printing org name, invoice count, and rate-limit headers. Any failure prints a named error — look it up in the table at the bottom.
22	
23	## Token endpoint — the facts
24	
25	| Fact | Value |
26	| --- | --- |
27	| Endpoint | `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` |
28	| Auth | `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id + ":" + client_secret)` — header, NOT body params |
29	| Content-Type | `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` — form-encoded body, NEVER JSON |
30	| Body | `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<url-encoded space-separated scopes>` |
31	| Response fields | `access_token` (a JWT), `expires_in` (seconds), `token_type: Bearer`, `scope` |
32	| Lifetime | up to 30 minutes (`expires_in` ~1800) |
33	| Refresh token | **None.** The response has no `refresh_token` field. "Refresh" = request a new token. `offline_access` is a code-flow concern — do not request it. |
34	
35	Minimal request (this exact shape lives in `scripts/token-cache.ts`):
36	
37	```ts
38	const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
39	const res = await fetch("https://identity.xero.com/connect/token", {
40	  method: "POST",
41	  headers: {
42	    Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
43	    "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
44	    Accept: "application/json",
45	  },
46	  body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scopes)}`,
47	});
48	```
49	
50	## Scopes: exactly four, and why nothing more
51	
52	Request the minimum. Env vars needed: `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` — that is all. There is deliberately **no `XERO_TENANT_ID`** (see next section).
53	
54	| Scope | Unlocks | Why this project needs it |
55	| --- | --- | --- |
56	| `accounting.transactions` | Broad scope = union of `accounting.invoices` (Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, **PurchaseOrders**, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items), `accounting.payments` (Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments), `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` | Bills (ACCPAY invoices), Purchase Orders, and payments are the match engine's subjects — read and write |
57	| `accounting.contacts` | Contacts, ContactGroups | Supplier resolution and dedup (contacts are NOT auto-deduplicated by email) |
58	| `accounting.attachments` | Attachments across most resources | Evidence documents attached to bills/POs via the raw Accounting API (no MCP tool exists — see `xero-accounting-api` skill) |
59	| `accounting.reports.read` | Broad scope = the eight `accounting.reports.*.read` reports (aged, balancesheet, banksummary, budgetsummary, executivesummary, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports) | Aged payables and financial context reads — read-only on purpose |
60	
61	What we deliberately do NOT request:
62	
63	- `openid profile email` — identity scopes for a human user; client_credentials has no user.
64	- `offline_access` — no refresh token exists in this grant; requesting it buys nothing.
65	- `accounting.settings[.read]` — grants Accounts, Organisation, TaxRates, etc. We don't read org configuration. Consequence: `GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisation` **403s** under our scopes; the org name comes from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` (`tenantName`) instead, which is what `verify-auth.ts` does.
66	- Payroll, budgets, journals — out of scope for this product.
67	
68	### Broad-scope migration (dates matter)
69	
70	From 29 April 2026 all custom connections have access to granular scopes; new custom connections lose `accounting.journals.read`; existing custom connections can keep using broad scopes until **September 2027**. Whether a custom connection created today can still request the broad `accounting.transactions` / `accounting.reports.read`: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Handle it empirically, the way Xero's own MCP server does: request broad, and on HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` retry with the granular equivalents. `verify-auth.ts` implements exactly this fallback and warns loudly when it triggers. Granular mapping for our set:
71	
72	| Broad (ours) | Granular equivalent |
73	| --- | --- |
74	| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals` |
75	| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read accounting.reports.balancesheet.read accounting.reports.banksummary.read accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read accounting.reports.executivesummary.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read accounting.reports.trialbalance.read accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |
76	| `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments` | never broad — use as-is |
77	
78	Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
79	
80	## Tenant id: omit the header
81	
82	Custom Connections "access data from a single Xero organisation" — one org, period (multi-org needs code/PKCE flow). The documented custom-connection API call sends **exactly two headers**:
83	
84	```
85	Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
86	Accept: application/json
87	```
88	
89	No `xero-tenant-id`. (An explicit docs sentence "the header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — but the canonical example omits it. Contrast: standard code-flow tokens REQUIRE `xero-tenant-id` on every call.) Xero's own MCP server sends a tenant id anyway, resolving it at runtime via `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` → `connections[0].tenantId`.
90	
91	**Rule for this repo:** omit the header on raw custom-connection calls. If a shared code path insists on one, resolve it from `GET /connections` at runtime — never hardcode it, never put it in `.env`. That is WHY there is no `XERO_TENANT_ID` env var.
92	
93	Note a docs wrinkle: the client-credentials page says Custom Connection apps "can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints", yet `GET /connections` demonstrably works with a custom-connection token — Xero's MCP server depends on it for tenant resolution. Trust the working call.
94	
95	## Token caching — do NOT copy Xero's MCP server
96	
97	The MCP server requests a brand-new token plus a `GET /connections` on **every** tool call: two wasted round-trips per call, all counted against the 60/min tenant limit. Do the opposite. `scripts/token-cache.ts` is the drop-in module:
98	
99	- Cache the token in memory; reuse until `expires_at - 60s` (60s buffer, same as Xero's CLI `TOKEN_BUFFER_MS`), computed from `expires_in`.
100	- Single-flight: concurrent callers share one in-progress token request.
101	- On **401**: invalidate, fetch a fresh token, retry exactly **once** (`xeroFetch` helper). On failure after retry, surface the error — no infinite loops.
102	- On **403**: never retry — that is a missing scope; the same token can never succeed.
103	- The cache holds the token in process memory only. Never persist it, never log it, never send it to the browser.
104	
105	NestJS wiring (illustrative — real module lives in `api/src/modules/xero/`):
106	
107	```ts
108	// api/src/modules/xero/xero.module.ts
109	{
110	  provide: XeroTokenCache,
111	  useFactory: (config: ConfigService) =>
112	    new XeroTokenCache({
113	      clientId: config.getOrThrow("XERO_CLIENT_ID"),
114	      clientSecret: config.getOrThrow("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"),
115	      scope: "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read",
116	    }),
117	  inject: [ConfigService],
118	}
119	```
120	
121	The constructor throws `MissingCredential` on empty values, so a misconfigured boot dies at startup, not mid-demo.
122	
123	## Rate-limit headers (read them, print them, respect them)
124	
125	| Header | Meaning |
126	| --- | --- |
127	| `X-MinLimit-Remaining` | calls left this minute (limit: 60/min per tenant) |
128	| `X-DayLimit-Remaining` | calls left today (5,000/day; 1,000 on starter tier) |
129	| `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining` | app-wide calls left this minute (10,000/min across all tenants) |
130	| `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` | on a 429: which limit you hit |
131	| `Retry-After` | on a 429: seconds to wait before calling again — use it, the window is fixed per tenant |
132	
133	Also: max 5 concurrent calls per tenant. Every token request counts too — another reason to cache.
134	
135	## Scripts
136	
137	| File | Purpose |
138	| --- | --- |
139	| `scripts/verify-auth.ts` | **Gate G0.** Parses repo-root `.env` itself (no dotenv), asserts env with named errors, fetches a token (with granular-scope fallback), GETs the org name via `/connections` and 1 invoice, prints org + invoice count + rate-limit headers. Run: `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts` from repo root. |
140	| `scripts/token-cache.ts` | In-memory token cache + `xeroFetch` retry-on-401 helper, shaped for a NestJS `useFactory` provider. Zero npm deps; compiles standalone under strict/ES2022/commonjs. |
141	
142	## Traps
143	
144	- **A misleading OAuth error from an empty or whitespace client id.** The cause is your env; the error points at Xero. Verified empirically 2026-07-04 against the live token endpoint: empty client id in the Basic header, or a missing Authorization header entirely → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`. (The Lovable skill's `unauthorized_client` symptom for `client_id=undefined` is the code-flow authorize URL, not this endpoint.) Assert non-empty **and trimmed** at boot — a trailing newline in `.env` counts as whitespace.
145	- **`invalid_client` from a wrong secret or wrong Basic encoding.** Verified empirically 2026-07-04: well-formed Basic header with wrong credentials → 400 `{"error":"invalid_client"}`. Base64 the literal `client_id:client_secret` — one colon, no newline, whole string encoded together; garbled encoding produces the same `invalid_client` (a structurally broken header degrades to `invalid_request`).
146	- **403 vs 401 are different diseases.** `401` = the token itself is bad or expired (>30 min) → new token, retry once. `403` = the token is fine but lacks a scope → fix the scope list at BOTH ends (portal + token request), get a NEW token. Retrying a 403 with the same token can never succeed.
147	- **The token endpoint wants form-encoding, not JSON.** `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a `key=value&key=value` body. Verified empirically 2026-07-04: a JSON body → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`, the same misleading error as a missing client id.
148	- **An org admin must authorise the Custom Connection before tokens work.** App setup chooses "who will authorise the connection"; until they do, the connection is dead. `verify-auth.ts` reports "token OK but zero connections" for this.
149	- **Broad scopes can 400 `invalid_scope` on newer apps.** Post-April-2026 granular migration — retry with the granular set (table above). `verify-auth.ts` does this automatically.
150	- **`GET /Organisation` 403s under our scopes.** It needs `accounting.settings[.read]`, which we deliberately don't request. Get the org name from `GET /connections` (`tenantName`).
151	- **Scope cannot be widened on an existing token.** Cheap fix here (unlike code flow): request a new token with the wider scope — provided the portal app has that scope enabled.
152	- **Every token request burns rate limit.** A token-per-call pattern (MCP server style) spends 2 of your 60/min on overhead. Cache.
153	
154	## Error → real cause
155	
156	Rows marked "verified empirically" were probed against the live token endpoint on 2026-07-04.
157	
158	| Symptom | Real cause | Fix |
159	| --- | --- | --- |
160	| Token 400 `invalid_request` (verified empirically) | Empty client id in the Basic header, missing Authorization header, or a JSON body instead of form-encoding | Boot-time `MissingEnv` assertion; check `.env` parsing/trimming; send form-encoded body |
161	| Token 400 `invalid_client` (verified empirically) | Wrong secret or wrong client id, incl. malformed Basic encoding (missing colon, double-encoded, newline in the base64 input) | Re-copy the secret; encode `id:secret` as one string |
162	| `unauthorized_client` | Code-flow symptom of `client_id=undefined` in the authorize URL — should not appear in this repo's client_credentials runtime; if seen, credentials are empty/wrong somewhere | Boot-time env assertion |
163	| Token 400 `invalid_scope` | Broad scope on a granular-only app, or a scope not enabled on the app in the portal | Retry with granular equivalents; enable the scope in the portal |
164	| API call 401 | Token expired (~30 min) or invalid | Invalidate cache, fetch new token, retry once (`xeroFetch`) |
165	| API call 403 | Token lacks the scope for that resource (e.g. `/Organisation` without `accounting.settings`) | Add the scope at both ends, request a NEW token; never retry with the same token |
166	| API call 429 + `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` | Rate limit hit (60/min, 5,000/day, 5 concurrent, 10,000/min app-wide) | Wait `Retry-After` seconds; batch reads; cache tokens |
167	| Token OK, `GET /connections` returns `[]` | Custom Connection not yet authorised by an org admin (UNVERIFIED that this is the only cause — check before relying) | Complete the authorisation step in the developer portal |
168	| `MissingEnv` / `MissingCredential` at boot | `.env` missing or var empty | Create `.env` at repo root with `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` |
169	
170	Related skills: `xero-mcp-usage` (MCP vs raw API routing), `xero-accounting-api` (Purchase Orders, Attachments, History — raw API only).
171	
172	<!--
173	Sources (fact-check trail):
174	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — token endpoint, Basic auth, form-encoded body, response fields, ~30 min lifetime (Token types page), single-org quote, AU/NZ/UK/US restriction, scopes-at-both-ends, granular migration dates (29 Apr 2026 / Sep 2027), two-header call example with no xero-tenant-id (explicit "not required" sentence absent = UNVERIFIED), "tenanted endpoints only" wrinkle, rate limits + X-*Limit-Remaining/X-Rate-Limit-Problem/Retry-After headers, pagination (page/pageSize min 1, pagination.itemCount). Underlying URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,client-credentials,token-types,limits}/, /documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses.
175	- .tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:127-203 — exact token request shape, token-per-call anti-pattern, invalid_scope granular fallback, GET /connections → connections[0].tenantId, boot env assertion.
176	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/lib/{auth.ts,oauth.ts,xero-client.ts} — TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000, refresh-on-401 retry pattern, /connections response shape incl. tenantName, Retry-After extraction.
177	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md:116-140 — unauthorized_client ← empty client_id causal chain, requireEnv/client_id_len discipline, scope-grants tables (accounting.invoices → PurchaseOrders etc., accounting.settings → Organisation), broad→granular migration table, 403 = missing scope, scope minimalism rules.
178	- Empirical probes 2026-07-04 against live https://identity.xero.com/connect/token: wrong creds → 400 {"error":"invalid_client"}; empty client id in Basic / no Authorization header / JSON body → 400 {"error":"invalid_request"}.
179	- UNVERIFIED items marked inline: explicit tenant-header-not-required sentence; unauthorised-app exact failure mode (and whether empty /connections has other causes); whether new post-Apr-2026 custom connections can still request broad scopes.

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
2	//
3	// Gate G0 — proves the Xero Custom Connection works end to end, before any
4	// feature code is written. Run from repo root:
5	//
6	//   npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
7	//
8	// What it does:
9	//   1. Parses <repo-root>/.env itself (no dotenv dep); vars already in
10	//      process.env win over the file.
11	//   2. Asserts XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named errors —
12	//      an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`).
13	//   3. Fetches a client_credentials token from identity.xero.com.
14	//      On 400 `invalid_scope` (granular-only app) it retries once with the
15	//      granular equivalents of the project's broad scopes.
16	//   4. GETs the connected organisation (name via GET /connections — our
17	//      scopes deliberately exclude accounting.settings, so GET /Organisation
18	//      would 403) and 1 invoice.
19	//   5. Prints org name, invoice count, and the rate-limit headers.
20	//
21	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Compiles standalone under
22	// strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
23	
24	import * as fs from "node:fs";
25	import * as path from "node:path";
26	
27	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
28	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
29	const INVOICES_URL = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1&pageSize=1";
30	
31	// The exact scopes this project needs — nothing more. See SKILL.md.
32	const PROJECT_SCOPES =
33	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
34	
35	// Granular equivalents (post-29-April-2026 custom connections may reject the
36	// broad scopes with 400 invalid_scope). Mapping per Xero's scope migration:
37	// accounting.transactions -> invoices+payments+banktransactions+manualjournals,
38	// accounting.reports.read -> the eight accounting.reports.*.read scopes.
39	const GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES = [
40	  "accounting.invoices",
41	  "accounting.payments",
42	  "accounting.banktransactions",
43	  "accounting.manualjournals",
44	  "accounting.contacts",
45	  "accounting.attachments",
46	  "accounting.reports.aged.read",
47	  "accounting.reports.balancesheet.read",
48	  "accounting.reports.banksummary.read",
49	  "accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read",
50	  "accounting.reports.executivesummary.read",
51	  "accounting.reports.profitandloss.read",
52	  "accounting.reports.trialbalance.read",
53	  "accounting.reports.taxreports.read",
54	].join(" ");
55	
56	class MissingEnvError extends Error {
57	  constructor(varName: string) {
58	    super(
59	      `${varName} is empty — set it in .env at repo root. ` +
60	        `If an empty client id reaches Xero it surfaces as a misleading OAuth error ` +
61	        `(invalid_request on the token endpoint, unauthorized_client in code flow), ` +
62	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
63	    );
64	    this.name = "MissingEnv";
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	class XeroTokenError extends Error {
69	  constructor(
70	    public readonly status: number,
71	    public readonly body: string,
72	  ) {
73	    super(`token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
74	    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	class XeroApiError extends Error {
79	  constructor(
80	    public readonly status: number,
81	    url: string,
82	    body: string,
83	  ) {
84	    super(`GET ${url} failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
85	    this.name = "XeroApiError";
86	  }
87	}
88	
89	// --- 1. Tiny .env parser (existing process.env wins; missing file tolerated) ---
90	
91	function loadDotEnv(envPath: string): void {
92	  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
93	    console.warn(`[verify-auth] no .env at ${envPath} — relying on process.env only`);
94	    return;
95	  }
96	  for (const rawLine of fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split(/\r?\n/)) {
97	    const line = rawLine.trim().replace(/^export\s+/, "");
98	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
99	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
100	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
101	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
102	    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
103	    if (
104	      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"') && value.length >= 2) ||
105	      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'") && value.length >= 2)
106	    ) {
107	      value = value.slice(1, -1);
108	    }
109	    const existing = process.env[key];
110	    if (existing === undefined || existing === "") {
111	      process.env[key] = value;
112	    }
113	  }
114	}
115	
116	// --- 2. Named env assertions (fail fast, never log the value itself) ---
117	
118	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
119	  const v = process.env[name];
120	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
121	    throw new MissingEnvError(name);
122	  }
123	  return v.trim();
124	}
125	
126	// --- 3. client_credentials token (Basic auth + form-encoded body, never JSON) ---
127	
128	interface TokenResult {
129	  accessToken: string;
130	  expiresIn: number;
131	  scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" | "granular fallback";
132	}
133	
134	async function requestToken(
135	  clientId: string,
136	  clientSecret: string,
137	  scope: string,
138	): Promise<{ accessToken: string; expiresIn: number }> {
139	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
140	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
141	    method: "POST",
142	    headers: {
143	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
144	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
145	      Accept: "application/json",
146	    },
147	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
148	  });
149	  const text = await res.text();
150	  if (!res.ok) {
151	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
152	  }
153	  const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
154	  if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
155	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
156	  }
157	  return {
158	    accessToken: parsed.access_token,
159	    expiresIn: typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0,
160	  };
161	}
162	
163	async function getTokenWithFallback(clientId: string, clientSecret: string): Promise<TokenResult> {
164	  const scopeOverride = process.env["XERO_SCOPES"];
165	  const scopes =
166	    scopeOverride !== undefined && scopeOverride.trim() !== "" ? scopeOverride.trim() : PROJECT_SCOPES;
167	  try {
168	    const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
169	    return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" };
170	  } catch (err) {
171	    if (err instanceof XeroTokenError && err.status === 400 && err.body.includes("invalid_scope")) {
172	      console.warn(
173	        "[verify-auth] broad scopes rejected (invalid_scope) — this app only has granular scopes. " +
174	          "Retrying with granular equivalents. Set XERO_SCOPES in .env to the granular set to silence this.",
175	      );
176	      const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES);
177	      return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "granular fallback" };
178	    }
179	    throw err;
180	  }
181	}
182	
183	// --- 4. Organisation + one invoice ---
184	
185	interface XeroConnection {
186	  tenantId?: string;
187	  tenantName?: string;
188	}
189	
190	async function getJson(url: string, accessToken: string): Promise<{ body: unknown; res: Response }> {
191	  // Per the custom-connections docs, API calls need exactly these two headers.
192	  // No xero-tenant-id: a custom connection is bound to a single organisation.
193	  const res = await fetch(url, {
194	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, Accept: "application/json" },
195	  });
196	  const text = await res.text();
197	  if (!res.ok) {
198	    throw new XeroApiError(res.status, url, text);
199	  }
200	  return { body: JSON.parse(text) as unknown, res };
201	}
202	
203	async function main(): Promise<void> {
204	  loadDotEnv(path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"));
205	
206	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
207	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
208	  // Log lengths only — never the values.
209	  console.log(
210	    `[verify-auth] env ok (client_id_len: ${clientId.length}, client_secret_len: ${clientSecret.length})`,
211	  );
212	
213	  const token = await getTokenWithFallback(clientId, clientSecret);
214	  console.log(
215	    `[verify-auth] token ok — scope set: ${token.scopeSetUsed}, expires_in: ${token.expiresIn}s`,
216	  );
217	
218	  const { body: connectionsBody } = await getJson(CONNECTIONS_URL, token.accessToken);
219	  const connections = Array.isArray(connectionsBody) ? (connectionsBody as XeroConnection[]) : [];
220	  if (connections.length === 0) {
221	    throw new XeroApiError(
222	      200,
223	      CONNECTIONS_URL,
224	      "token issued but no organisation is connected. Has a Xero org admin authorised this " +
225	        "Custom Connection app in the developer portal? Tokens are useless until that is done.",
226	    );
227	  }
228	  const first = connections[0];
229	  const orgName = first?.tenantName ?? first?.tenantId ?? "(unnamed connection)";
230	  console.log(`[verify-auth] organisation: ${orgName}`);
231	
232	  const { body: invoicesBody, res: invoicesRes } = await getJson(INVOICES_URL, token.accessToken);
233	  const inv = invoicesBody as { Invoices?: unknown[]; pagination?: { itemCount?: unknown } };
234	  const returned = Array.isArray(inv.Invoices) ? inv.Invoices.length : 0;
235	  const itemCount = typeof inv.pagination?.itemCount === "number" ? inv.pagination.itemCount : null;
236	  console.log(
237	    `[verify-auth] invoices returned: ${returned}` + (itemCount !== null ? ` (org total: ${itemCount})` : ""),
238	  );
239	
240	  console.log(
241	    "[verify-auth] rate limits remaining — " +
242	      `minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-MinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
243	      `day: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-DayLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
244	      `app-minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-AppMinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}`,
245	  );
246	  console.log("[verify-auth] G0 PASS");
247	}
248	
249	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
250	  if (err instanceof Error) {
251	    console.error(`[verify-auth] FAIL — ${err.name}: ${err.message}`);
252	  } else {
253	    console.error("[verify-auth] FAIL —", err);
254	  }
255	  console.error("[verify-auth] see the error table in .claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md");
256	  process.exit(1);
257	});
258
