<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md`

```text
1	# Xero prompt-library skill-writing style: fact sheet
2	
3	Sources (all in `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/`):
4	- `lovable/xero-integration-single-account/SKILL.md` (707 lines)
5	- `lovable/xero-integration-multi-account/SKILL.md` (868 lines)
6	- `javascript/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant`, 452 lines)
7	- `python/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`, 502 lines)
8	- `replit/xero-integration-single-account-skill` (plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter, 293 lines)
9	- `README.md` (repo index, no skill discipline content)
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. Document structure conventions
14	
15	### Frontmatter
16	Exactly two fields, always: `name` and `description`. No version, no tools, no metadata.
17	
18	- `name` is the kebab-case skill id (`xero-integration-single-account`, `xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`).
19	- `description` is a single long paragraph that does four jobs, in roughly this order:
20	  1. **What it builds**, with the discriminating word in CAPS: "Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation".
21	  2. **When to use it**: "Use whenever building a multi-tenant SaaS on Xero".
22	  3. **What it covers**, as a comma list of the hard parts: "Covers the full OAuth2 + PKCE lifecycle with the official `xero-node` SDK, per-user encrypted token storage, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits)."
23	  4. **A read-first command and a routing rule to the sibling skill**: "Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see 'Do you actually need this?' at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach."
24	
25	  The Replit variant adds an anti-pattern warning right in the description: "Read this before writing any Xero code; do NOT rely on generic xero-node knowledge."
26	
27	### Section order (consistent across all four full skills)
28	1. `# H1` restating the variant in plain words: "Multi-tenant Xero (each user connects their own Xero)".
29	2. **Variant check / decision gate first.** A blockquote or `## Do you actually need this?` section forcing the reader to confirm they picked the right skill, with an explicit pointer to the sibling: "Stop and use **`xero-integration-single-account`** instead." Each pair of skills cross-references the other by exact name. The deciding question is stated as one sentence: "The deciding question is *how many Xero logins exist*".
30	3. **Runtime context** ("Where this code runs in Lovable") — the platform facts that shape all code below, stated as numbered "runtime facts".
31	4. `## Decision check before building` — a 3-item ALL-must-be-true checklist.
32	5. `## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)` — see §2 below.
33	6. Numbered build sections in lifecycle order: `## 1. Register your own Xero app` → `## Scopes` → `## 2. Per-user token store` → `## 3.` (client/redirect resolver) → `## 4.` (start consent) → `## 5.` (callback) → `## 6.` (use per request / status UX).
34	7. Cross-cutting sections: `## Date + money + tax normalisation`, `## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)`, `## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)`, platform-gotcha subsection, `## Security checklist (do not skip)`, `## Don't want to hand-roll it?` (managed alternatives: Nango, Composio).
35	8. `## Verify` — always last. A numbered end-to-end acceptance list (they even use step `0.` and `0.5.` to force pre-click checks before step 1). Every item is observable behavior, e.g. "Post the same invoice twice with the same idempotency key and confirm only one invoice is created."
36	
37	### Description/heading phrasing
38	- Headings carry the verdict, not just the topic: "The redirect-URI resolver — read carefully", "Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)", "Security checklist (do not skip)", "CRITICAL: path mapping", "Failure handling (the rule that bites)".
39	- Section intros state *why the section exists* in one or two sentences before any code.
40	- Every code block opens with a file-path comment (`// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts`) so the reader knows where it lives.
41	- Sync-maintenance notes between sibling docs: "(This table is identical to the single-account skill — keep the two in sync if Xero's assignments change.)"
42	- External source-of-truth links with a staleness warning: "Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes"
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 2. Pre-flight check discipline (verbatim quotes)
47	
48	The Lovable skills open the build with a dedicated section. Header, verbatim:
49	
50	> ## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
51	>
52	> Three checks. Each one prevents a class of bug that produces a misleading
53	> upstream error from Xero. Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a
54	> human.
55	
56	**Check 1 — assert every secret non-empty, with the causal chain stated** (verbatim):
57	
58	> ### 1. Assert every secret is set and non-empty
59	>
60	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
61	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Fail fast
62	> with a clear message inside `xeroStart` **before** building the URL:
63	
64	```ts
65	function requireEnv(name: string) {
66	  const v = process.env[name];
67	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
68	    throw new Error(`${name} is empty — set it in Project Secrets and retry.`);
69	  }
70	  return v;
71	}
72	```
73	
74	> Call `requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID")`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_SCOPES`, and
75	> `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` at the top of every handler that uses them.
76	
77	**Check 2 — log the constructed request once, never the secret** (verbatim):
78	
79	> You want a server log you can read when something is wrong, without ever
80	> printing the secret value itself:
81	
82	```ts
83	console.log("[xero] authorize", {
84	  client_id_len: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID?.length,
85	  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
86	  scope: process.env.XERO_SCOPES,
87	});
88	```
89	
90	> If the next failure is `unauthorized_client` or `invalid redirect_uri`, this
91	> line tells you *exactly* which value Xero rejected.
92	
93	Note the trick: log `client_id_len` (the length), never the value.
94	
95	**Check 3 — smoke-test before any human clicks** (verbatim):
96	
97	> ### 3. Smoke-test `xeroStart` BEFORE clicking Connect
98	>
99	> Call the server function and inspect the returned URL: `client_id` must be a
100	> non-empty hex string, and `redirect_uri` must **exactly** match one of the URIs
101	> registered in your Xero app (including scheme, host, and path). If either is
102	> wrong, fix it here. Do not proceed to the popup — Xero's errors past this point
103	> are far less useful than the URL you can read right now.
104	
105	**Check 4 — validate key material shape, with the full failure story** (verbatim):
106	
107	> AES-256-GCM requires a **32-byte** key. A 32-character random string is NOT 32
108	> bytes once base64-decoded (it's ~24), and `createCipheriv` throws
109	> `Invalid key length` from deep inside `encrypt()` — which surfaces as an
110	> opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the
111	> single-use `code`. The user sees "something went wrong" with no way to retry
112	> without re-consenting, and no breadcrumb in the logs points at the key.
113	
114	Pattern to copy: each pre-flight check names (a) the invariant, (b) the misleading downstream symptom if skipped, (c) the exact code to assert it, (d) the moment to run it ("before you wire the Connect button to a human"). Boot-time assertion + named error, exactly the CLAUDE.md rule "Secrets in `.env` only, asserted non-empty at boot with named errors."
115	
116	They also make the *failure path itself* testable — Verify step 0.5, verbatim:
117	
118	> 0.5. **Force an encryption failure once.** Temporarily set `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` to
119	>    a 16-char string and click Connect. Confirm `/dashboard` shows
120	>    `?error=callback&detail=…` (with a readable message) rather than a blank
121	>    500 — this proves both the callback try/catch (§4) and the dashboard
122	>    banner (§6) actually wire failures back to the user. Restore the real
123	>    key afterwards.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## 3. Error → real-cause mappings (verbatim — the gold)
128	
129	These docs mostly encode error→cause as prose sentences and blockquote "traps", not literal tables. Every mapping found, copied verbatim with source:
130	
131	**`unauthorized_client` ← empty/missing client_id** (lovable single + multi, Pre-flight §1):
132	
133	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
134	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause.
135	
136	**`invalid redirect_uri` / `unauthorized_client` ← three redirect-origin traps** (lovable, §3 header block, verbatim):
137	
138	> ### ⚠️ Three traps, all of which produce `invalid redirect_uri` or `unauthorized_client`
139	>
140	> **Trap A — `getRequest()` / `request.url` on the server returns
141	> `http://localhost:8080`.** Under the Worker runtime the inbound request you
142	> see inside a server function is on the internal dev host, not the public URL.
143	> Never derive `redirect_uri` from it.
144	>
145	> **Trap B — In the Lovable editor, `window.location.origin` is
146	> `https://<project-id>.lovableproject.com`** (the iframe host), which is NOT
147	> a registered URI. Sending it raw produces `invalid redirect_uri` every time.
148	>
149	> **Trap C — A single `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret can't cover both preview and
150	> published.** If you set it to the published host the preview breaks (and
151	> vice-versa). Use the resolver below instead.
152	
153	**`unauthorized_client` / invalid-redirect ← port or trailing-slash mismatch** (javascript, Local-dev gotchas):
154	
155	> **Redirect URI must match exactly.** The most common local failure is a port or
156	> trailing-slash mismatch between `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and what's registered in the
157	> portal. If consent returns `unauthorized_client` or an invalid-redirect error,
158	> this is almost always why. Run the dev server on the same port you registered.
159	
160	**403 ← missing write scope** (lovable multi, Scopes; wrong-secret-shaped failures are scope failures here):
161	
162	> Shipping a read-only token and then calling `POST /Invoices` fails
163	> at runtime with a 403 / insufficient-scope error — and because scopes **cannot be
164	> widened on an existing token**, the only fix is to send every connected customer
165	> back through the whole consent flow. So decide up front: read, write, or both.
166	
167	and the compressed single-account form:
168	
169	> Mismatched scope → 403 at runtime and the only fix is
170	> to send the user through consent again.
171	
172	and the silent-default variant (lovable multi):
173	
174	> **Make the `XERO_SCOPES` fallback in your shared Xero config match your intent** — an
175	> all-`.read` default will silently hand every customer a read-only token if the
176	> secret is missing, and your first write will 403.
177	
178	**Opaque 500 on callback ← wrong-length `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (lovable, Pre-flight §4): see §2 above ("`createCipheriv` throws `Invalid key length` … surfaces as an opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the single-use `code`").
179	
180	**`TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` ← client recreated without state** (javascript, §5 callback, verbatim code comment):
181	
182	> // MUST recreate the client with the SAME state you used to build the consent URL.
183	> // `apiCallback` runs openid-client's state check: because the consent URL carries a
184	> // `state` param, the callback client must also know it, or you get the cryptic
185	> // `TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` and every connect fails.
186	
187	**401 `No connection found for replid ...` ← Repl not bound to the connector** (replit, setup checklist):
188	
189	> **Until the Repl is bound,
190	> every live call fails with `401` and a body like
191	> `No connection found for replid ... with connector: xero`.** If you see that
192	> 401, the code is probably fine — the binding is missing.
193	
194	**404 ← friendly alias paths** (replit, CRITICAL: path mapping):
195	
196	> The connector blueprint advertises friendly aliases like `/accounting/contacts` and
197	> `/accounting/invoices` — **these are wrong and return 404.** Use Xero's real
198	> REST paths with PascalCase resource names.
199	
200	> Verified empirically: `/connections` → 200, `/accounting/organisations` → 404,
201	> `/api.xro/2.0/Organisations` → 200.
202	
203	**Blank white page ← Xero consent framed in an iframe** (lovable + javascript):
204	
205	> Xero's login refuses to be framed (`frame-ancestors`), so a same-frame
206	> redirect from the Lovable preview iframe shows a **blank white page** that
207	> looks like the button is broken.
208	
209	**Access token dies in ~30 min, no recovery ← `offline_access` omitted** (all four):
210	
211	> **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and you get an access token
212	> that dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.
213	
214	**400 ← same idempotency key, different request** (all four, Idempotency "Rules that bite"):
215	
216	> **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A
217	> request differs if the URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key alongside the
218	> record and reuse the *identical* request when retrying.
219	
220	**Cached error ← retrying an errored keyed request** (all four):
221	
222	> **Errors are cached too.** If the keyed request errored internally, retrying with the
223	> *same* key returns the *same cached error* even after the underlying problem is
224	> fixed. To recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if
225	> not, create again with a **new** key.
226	
227	**429 ← rate limits, with the recovery** (all four):
228	
229	> **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide
230	> per-minute cap and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header
231	> and back off — multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Spread/queue
232	> calls per tenant.
233	
234	**Silent wrong-books write ← missing/wrong tenant header** (all four):
235	
236	> **Always send `Xero-Tenant-Id`** on every API call. There is no implicit "current
237	> org." With multiple connected orgs, the wrong id silently writes to the wrong
238	> company's books.
239	
240	**Next refresh fails ← rotated refresh token not persisted** (all four):
241	
242	> **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on
243	> every refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time or the next
244	> refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.
245	
246	**Sibling orgs flip to needs_reconnect ← only one row updated after rotation** (javascript §6, code comment, verbatim):
247	
248	> // CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: when one consent authorized several orgs, they all
249	> // share ONE token set. Refreshing rotates the refresh token and INVALIDATES the
250	> // old one, so updating only THIS row leaves every sibling org holding a dead token
251	> // (they'll wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on their next call). Update ALL rows
252	> // that shared the previous token set — e.g. match on the old ciphertext:
253	
254	**Duplicate contacts ← posting invoices with a contact name** (all four):
255	
256	> **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new
257	> contact name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or
258	> `ContactID`) via `getContacts` and reuse the id; otherwise you litter the org with
259	> duplicates.
260	
261	The one true error-shaped *table* in the corpus is the Replit **path mapping table** (Purpose | Path) plus its "verified empirically" line — the pattern being: verified request → expected status, wrong path → 404.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 4. Scope minimalism rules
266	
267	Canonical statement, repeated near-verbatim in all four skills:
268	
269	> Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run the OAuth consent
270	> flow to add more later. You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user
271	> must re-consent. To get a refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
272	
273	Supporting rules:
274	
275	- "**Always prefer the granular scopes** — use `accounting.invoices`, NOT `accounting.transactions`." (Broad scopes deprecated: granular assigned to all Web/PKCE apps since March 2026, custom connections since 29 April 2026, broad dead September 2027.)
276	- "**Read vs write — choose deliberately; you can't change it later without re-consent.** `.read` scopes are GET-only."
277	- Lovable adds an agent-behavior rule: "Do not ask the user what scopes to use - set the appropriate scopes for the integration to function."
278	- Replit: "**Scopes** — see the dedicated section below. Request the minimum set for what you're doing".
279	
280	Exact scope strings quoted in the docs:
281	
282	- Read-only dashboard (lovable single):
283	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`
284	- Write / invoicing app ("A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants", all four):
285	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`
286	- Typical invoice flow (replit):
287	  `offline_access openid email profile accounting.settings.read accounting.invoices accounting.contacts`
288	
289	Scope reference tables they maintain (structure worth copying): (1) identity scopes `offline_access` / `openid` / `profile` / `email`; (2) "Deprecated (broad) → New granular scopes" migration table (`accounting.transactions` → `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals`; same for `.read`; `accounting.reports.read` → eight `accounting.reports.*.read` scopes); (3) "Scope | Grants" table mapping each granular scope to the resources it unlocks (`accounting.invoices` → Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items; `accounting.payments` → Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments); (4) "never broad, use as-is" table (`accounting.settings`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.journals.read`, `accounting.budgets.read`). Always footed with the developer.xero.com scopes URL and "re-check periodically — dates and assignments change".
290	
291	---
292	
293	## 5. Token handling rules
294	
295	- **Lifetimes, stated with numbers every time:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days, **rotates on every refresh**, dies if unused for 60 days. Expiry stored as `expires_at` computed from `expires_in` seconds: `new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()` (JS) / `token["expires_at"]` epoch-seconds dict key (Python).
296	- **Refresh early, not at expiry:** JS refreshes when `expires_at <= now + 60_000` (60-second buffer); Python "Proactively refresh if within ~2 min of expiry" (`expires_at - time.time() < 120`).
297	- **Persist rotation immediately or break:** "tokenSet = await res.json();   // refresh_token ROTATES — must persist". And update **all sibling rows** sharing the old ciphertext (multi-org consents share one token set) — match siblings on the OLD ciphertext.
298	- **No long-lived clients / no caching:** "Make a fresh client per request and `setTokenSet(...)` from the DB. Do not keep a long-lived client — tokens expire and clients are per-user." Replit: "Never cache this — create a fresh instance per request (tokens rotate)."
299	- **Refresh failure is a state, not a retry loop:** "On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — do not silently retry forever." Status enum in the schema: `'active' | 'needs_reconnect'`. Surface it in UI with a reconnect CTA; a 60-day-idle token dies silently, so add a scheduled keep-alive refresh, not just reactive detection.
300	- **Storage:** tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM, key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` in the secret store, exactly 32 bytes base64); rotating token lives in the **database (a mutable store), never in a secret**; "never put per-user tokens in env".
301	- **Never-log / never-expose:** log `client_id_len`, not the value ("without ever printing the secret value itself"); "**Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage"; "Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`; never commit credentials"; status endpoints return "only non-sensitive metadata (never the token)".
302	- **`state` discipline:** random, persisted server-side with a 10-minute expiry, verified on callback, deleted after use — "single-use, replay-proof".
303	
304	---
305	
306	## 6. Tone and format
307	
308	- **Imperative second person, no hedging.** "Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a human." "Stop and use X instead." "If you reach for `xero-node` or start writing token-refresh code, stop — you are on the wrong path."
309	- **Warnings are structural, not decorative.** The single warning device is `⚠️` in a heading (`## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST`, `> ### ⚠️ Three traps...`, `> ### ⚠️ Open consent in a NEW TAB, opened SYNCHRONOUSLY in the click handler`) plus a blockquote body. Bold and CAPS carry emphasis inline: MUST, NOT, NEVER, ONE, THEIR OWN, FIRST, "byte-for-byte", "byte-match".
310	- **Every rule ships its consequence.** Not "include offline_access" but "Omit it and the access token dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent." Failure narratives are causal chains told to the end (wrong key → throw deep inside encrypt → opaque 500 → single-use code consumed → user must re-consent → no breadcrumb in logs).
311	- **Named recurring rubrics:** "Rules that bite", "(these bite in production)", "Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)", "Gotcha that fools people", "(do not skip)", "(skipping it costs hours)", "CRITICAL multi-tenant trap".
312	- **Tables vs prose:** tables only for enumerable reference data (scopes, scope grants, deprecation mapping, REST paths). Everything judgment-shaped (gotchas, security, idempotency rules) is bold-lead-in bullets: `**Rule.** Consequence and recovery.` Checklists (Decision check, Security checklist, Verify) are lists, with Verify numbered.
313	- **Code is placed, minimal, and commented with intent** — comments mark traps ("// sync!", "// refresh_token ROTATES — must persist", "// REQUIRED — wrong id writes to wrong books"), not narration. File-path comment on line 1 of each block.
314	- **Distrust of upstream errors is a theme:** fail fast locally because "Xero's errors past this point are far less useful than the URL you can read right now."
315	- **Empirical claims are labeled:** "Verified empirically", "**The paths below are verified — trust them, do not re-probe.**", and for unknowns: "probe it **read-only first** rather than guessing".
316	- Punctuation habits: heavy em-dash usage, `→` arrows for flows and mappings (`DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`; "Same key + different request → `400`"), backticked literals for every identifier, header, error string, and scope.
317	
318	---
319	
320	## 7. Platform-specific guidance — do NOT copy into a NestJS project
321	
322	Everything below is Lovable/Supabase/Replit plumbing, not Xero discipline. In NestJS: config assertion at boot (ConfigModule + named errors), one Xero module (`api/src/modules/xero/`), Postgres via your ORM, guards instead of RLS.
323	
324	**Lovable / TanStack Start / Cloudflare Workers:**
325	- `createServerFn` server functions; server routes under `src/routes/api/public/*` to "bypass Lovable's published-site auth gate". NestJS controllers are just public or guarded routes.
326	- "Do not use Supabase Edge Functions"; the legacy-port note (Deno Edge Functions, `Deno.env.get`, redeploy after secret change, `verify_jwt = false`, Web Crypto two-part `iv.ciphertext` blob) — all Deno/Supabase-specific.
327	- "Read `process.env.*` **inside** `.handler()`, not at module scope" (Worker per-request env injection). NestJS reads env once at boot — the opposite discipline.
328	- The whole `resolveRedirectUri` host-mapping resolver, `PROJECT_ID`, `id-preview--*.lovable.app` / `project--*.lovable.app` hosts, `*.lovableproject.com` iframe host, `getRequest()` returning `http://localhost:8080`. A NestJS app has one configured redirect URI per environment.
329	- Iframe/popup dance (`window.open("about:blank", "_blank")` synchronously before `await`) — Lovable editor-preview-specific; keep only the generic fact that Xero consent can't be framed.
330	- "Never import `@/integrations/supabase/client.server` at module scope of a `*.functions.ts` or route file" — client-bundle stripping concern, no NestJS equivalent.
331	- Lovable Cloud secrets / `generate_secret` with `length: 44` — keep the underlying rule (base64 of 32 random bytes), drop the tool.
332	
333	**Supabase data layer:**
334	- `supabaseAdmin`, `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, "Service-role key never goes to the browser".
335	- RLS incantations: `grant all ... to service_role`, `enable row level security`, "INTENTIONALLY no anon/authenticated policies", `auth.users(id)` FKs, `requireSupabaseAuth` middleware, `has_role(userId, 'admin')`.
336	- Supabase client returning Postgres `numeric` as strings.
337	- The unauthenticated-prototype "escape hatch" (warning banner, accepted-risks list) — Lovable demo-flow specific.
338	
339	**Replit connector:**
340	- `@replit/connectors-sdk` proxy pattern, `connectors.proxy("xero", path, ...)`, Repl binding (`proposeIntegration`/`addIntegration`), the `No connection found for replid` 401, redirect URI `https://replit.com/connectors/oauth/callback`, `pnpm --filter @workspace/<server>` install rule, Integrations → Manage reset.
341	
342	**Portable despite appearing in platform sections** (keep these): exact-match redirect URIs registered per environment; state persisted server-side because HTTP steps are separate invocations; callback errors funneled to a user-visible `?error&detail` redirect (never a bare 500, because the OAuth `code` is single-use); `Xero-Tenant-Id` on every call; idempotency keys on writes; `summarizeErrors=false`; `unitdp=4`; the `/Date(ms+offset)/` normaliser; encrypt-at-rest with validated 32-byte key; `needs_reconnect` status + keep-alive refresh; all Xero access isolated in one module.
343
```
</details></tool-use>

SKILL: xero-accounting-api (dir: undefined/xero-accounting-api/)
The raw Accounting API surface THIS project touches, request/response shapes trimmed to fields we actually use:
- Invoices type ACCPAY (bills): list with where/order/page (exact where syntax examples for Type=="ACCPAY" and Status filters), create, update status DRAFT->SUBMITTED->AUTHORISED (legal transitions + what locks), line items, LineAmountTypes.
- Purchase Orders: put a loud banner that these are NOT in the MCP server — raw API only. Full CRUD shapes, status flow, marking billed.
- Attachments: upload onto an invoice (raw bytes PUT, content-type, filename rules, the documented size cap), list, and the online-invoice visibility flag (IncludeOnline) with its constraints.
- History/Notes: append the agent's decision note to any document (exact PUT shape).
- Contacts: list + the fields relevant to fuzzy supplier matching (Name, EmailAddress), where-filter examples.
- Reports: aged payables by contact (required contactID param, trimmed response shape).
Also: pagination (100/page default — verify exact number), If-Modified-Since, where-filter quoting traps (URL-encoding, string quotes, DateTime syntax), the validation error envelope (Elements[].ValidationErrors) with a real example, and rate limits (60/min, 5000/day per tenant; 429 + Retry-After handling pattern in TS).
Include a scripts/ file with a minimal typed "xeroFetch" helper (token from env/cache, base URL, Accept: application/json, 429 retry honouring Retry-After, error envelope parsing) that other snippets reference — but keep it a thin HTTP helper, no business logic.
---

A claim you merely doubt but cannot refute with evidence is NOT a correction — list it under styleIssues prefixed "DOUBT:" instead. Report at most the 15 most important corrections, ranked by how badly they would burn us mid-build.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-accounting-api
3	description: The raw Xero Accounting API surface THIS project touches, over plain fetch with a Custom Connection (client_credentials, no xero-node SDK, no tenant id) — ACCPAY bills on /Invoices (where filters, create, DRAFT→SUBMITTED→AUTHORISED walk), Purchase Orders and Attachments and History notes (all three are NOT in the Xero MCP server — raw API is the ONLY path), Contacts for supplier matching, and the AgedPayablesByContact report. Covers pagination, If-Modified-Since, where-filter quoting traps, the Elements[].ValidationErrors envelope, rate limits and 429/Retry-After handling. Use when writing or reviewing ANY code in api/src/modules/xero/ that calls api.xro/2.0, when an MCP tool is missing (see xero-mcp-usage), or when debugging a Xero 400/403/429. Read this BEFORE writing any raw Accounting API call; do NOT rely on generic Xero knowledge.
4	---
5	
6	# Raw Accounting API (Custom Connection, plain fetch — no SDK)
7	
8	Base `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Resource}`. Send `Accept: application/json` on every call — Xero's default response format is XML. Bills are `/Invoices` with `Type: "ACCPAY"`; there is no `/Bills` endpoint. For token mechanics see the **`xero-auth`** skill; for paying bills see **`xero-payments`**; for what routes through MCP instead see **`xero-mcp-usage`**.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert env non-empty before any call.** `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` live in `.env` at the repo root, never committed, never logged. Empty values surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=` got sent), which points away from the real cause. `requireEnv()` in `scripts/xero-fetch.ts` throws `MissingEnv: XERO_CLIENT_ID is empty — ...` at first use.
13	2. **No tenant id — here is why.** A Custom Connection "utilise[s] the client credentials grant type to access data from a single Xero organisation" — one org per connection, so the tenant is implicit. The docs' canonical custom-connection API call carries exactly two headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and `Accept: application/json`. (An explicit docs sentence "the xero-tenant-id header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — check before relying; the basis is the single-org binding plus its omission from the documented call.)
14	3. **Scopes are set at BOTH ends** — selected at app creation AND passed as `scope` in every token request body (this differs from code/PKCE flow where scopes ride the URL). This project's app selects `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read` (see `.env.example`). A token minted without the needed scope 403s at runtime. Granular-scope migration: from 29 April 2026 all custom connections have granular scopes (`accounting.invoices` covers Invoices AND PurchaseOrders); existing connections may keep broad scopes until September 2027. Whether a brand-new custom connection can still select broad scopes today, and the exact granular report scope for aged payables: UNVERIFIED — check before relying (check the app dashboard scope picker).
15	4. **Smoke-test before wiring anything:** `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts` must print a bill count and a `pagination` object. If it fails here, fix auth first — Xero's errors deeper in are less useful.
16	5. **Reports need the reports role.** "An application that has been authorised by a Standard user without the 'reports' role will not be able to access the Reports ... endpoints (A HTTP 403 error will be returned)." Have the org admin who authorises the connection hold that role.
17	
18	## Conventions on every call
19	
20	| Fact | Value |
21	|---|---|
22	| JSON dates | `"UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790)\/"` — unix epoch in **milliseconds** inside |
23	| PUT vs POST | PUT creates only; POST creates **or updates** |
24	| Envelope | single object or plural-keyed array (`{"Invoices":[...]}`) — both accepted |
25	| Page size | default **100**, max **1000**, min 1; out-of-range auto-adjusted (`pageSize=5000` → 1000 items) |
26	| Pagination object | responses include `"pagination": {page, pageSize, pageCount, itemCount}` |
27	| Paged endpoints | Invoices, Contacts, CreditNotes, BankTransactions, ManualJournals, Payments, PurchaseOrders, Prepayments, Overpayments |
28	| Request cap | 10MB per request; batch writes in bundles of ~50 elements |
29	| `If-Modified-Since` | header, UTC, accurate to the second; recommended on all large result sets |
30	
31	**If-Modified-Since misses some changes.** Changes that don't bump `UpdatedDateUTC` are not returned: `DueDate`/`SentToContact` edits on partially paid transactions, and Contact `Balances`/`IsSupplier`/`IsCustomer`. Do not rely on it for those fields.
32	
33	### Rate limits (per tenant unless stated)
34	
35	| Limit | Value |
36	|---|---|
37	| Concurrent | 5 calls in progress |
38	| Minute | **60 calls/min** |
39	| Daily | 1,000/day (Starter tier) — **5,000/day** (higher tiers) |
40	| App-wide | 10,000 calls/min across all tenants |
41	
42	Every response carries `X-DayLimit-Remaining`, `X-MinLimit-Remaining`, `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining`. On **429** the `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` header names the limit hit; minute/daily breaches also send `Retry-After` (seconds) — wait exactly that long. `xeroFetch` does this. Batch reads and cache in Postgres; never poll in a loop.
43	
44	### Idempotency-Key header
45	
46	Send `Idempotency-Key` on POST/PUT (ignored on GET). Xero caches the response for **6 minutes**; same key replays the cached response. Max **128 chars**. Same key + a *different* request (URL, body, or method changed) → 400 `Idempotency Key ... is used with a different request.` **Errors are cached too** — after fixing the underlying problem, GET to check whether the resource exists, then retry with a NEW key. Keyed duplicates still count against rate limits.
47	
48	### `where` filter — quoting traps
49	
50	- The whole expression must be **percent-encoded** (the helper's `query` map does this; never hand-concatenate into the URL).
51	- Strings use **double quotes** and `==`: official invoice example `Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"` → encoded `where=Type%3d%3d%22ACCPAY%22+AND+Status%3d%3d%22AUTHORISED%22`.
52	- Dates use **`DateTime(2015, 01, 01)`** syntax, not ISO strings: `Date >= DateTime(2015, 01, 01) && Date < DateTime(2015, 12, 31)`. Join conditions with `and`/`&&`.
53	- Keep to simple `==` on optimised fields — "Long, complex where queries can cause time outs"; unoptimised filter/order producing >100k rows → 400.
54	- Contacts' optimised examples use a single `=` (`where=Name="ABC limited"`) and are case- and accent-insensitive — never append `ToLower()`/`ToUpper()`.
55	
56	### Validation error envelopes — two shapes
57	
58	HTTP **400** returns an ApiException (real documented example):
59	
60	```json
61	{
62	  "ErrorNumber": 10,
63	  "Type": "ValidationException",
64	  "Message": "A validation exception occurred",
65	  "Elements": [{ "ValidationErrors": [{ "Message": "Email address must be valid" }] }]
66	}
67	```
68	
69	With `?summarizeErrors=false` (batch writes) you instead get HTTP **200** and a per-element `StatusAttributeString` of `OK`/`WARNING`/`ERROR`, with `ValidationErrors` on the failed element (the docs' bulk example shows `{"Description": "Invoice not of valid type for creation"}` — note the key differs from the 400 shape; parse both). A 200 does NOT mean the write succeeded — always assert per element (`assertElementOk` in the helper).
70	
71	## Bills — `/Invoices` with `Type: "ACCPAY"`
72	
73	Fields this project uses (create/read):
74	
75	| Field | Notes |
76	|---|---|
77	| `Type` | `"ACCPAY"` — required on create |
78	| `Contact` | required; provide **only** `ContactID` |
79	| `LineItems` | at least one required; `Description` alone makes a valid line (1–4000 chars) |
80	| `LineItems[].UnitAmount` | rounded to **2dp** unless the request adds `?unitdp=4` |
81	| `LineItems[].TaxType` | override only when the `AccountCode` default tax is wrong |
82	| `InvoiceNumber` | ACCPAY: **NON-unique**, max 255, displays as **"Reference"** in the Xero UI |
83	| `Date` / `DueDate` | `YYYY-MM-DD`; `Date` defaults to today in the org timezone |
84	| `Status` | optional on create, defaults to `DRAFT`; may create directly as `SUBMITTED` or `AUTHORISED` |
85	| `LineAmountTypes` | `Exclusive` (default when omitted) / `Inclusive` / `NoTax` |
86	| `CurrencyCode` | defaults to org base currency |
87	| `Total` | computed: `SubTotal + TotalTax`; `LineAmount = Quantity × UnitAmount` (≤ 9,999,999,999.99) |
88	
89	ACCPAY does **not** support discounts (`DiscountRate`/`DiscountAmount` are ACCREC-only).
90	
91	### Status transitions (the documented table — everything else is illegal)
92	
93	| Existing | May become |
94	|---|---|
95	| `DRAFT` | `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED` |
96	| `SUBMITTED` | `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `DRAFT`, `DELETED` |
97	| `AUTHORISED` | `AUTHORISED`, `VOIDED` |
98	
99	- **`AUTHORISED` locks.** Journals are created and payments can now be applied; it can never return to `DRAFT`/`SUBMITTED` and cannot be deleted — only `VOIDED` (and voiding is shown only for invoices with no payments applied).
100	- **`PAID` is system-set** when fully paid. Never write it; a "paid" bill is a two-step: `AUTHORISED`, then a Payment (see `xero-payments`). No transitions are documented out of `PAID`, `VOIDED`, `DELETED` — treat as terminal.
101	- Invoices in a **locked period** cannot be updated. Partially/fully paid ACCPAY bills allow edits only to: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (with CIS/credit-note caveats), Url, line Description, AccountCode (non-CIS), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate.
102	- **Line-item update rule:** in an update, a line WITH `LineItemID` is updated, WITHOUT one is created, and any existing line whose id is missing from a supplied `LineItems` array is **deleted**. Status-only updates (as in the docs' delete example) send no `LineItems` at all.
103	
104	### Listing
105	
106	- Optimised `where` equality fields: Status, Contact.ContactID, Contact.Name, Contact.ContactNumber, Reference, InvoiceNumber, InvoiceId, Type, AmountPaid; range (`>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`) also on Date, DueDate, AmountDue.
107	- Faster than `where` for lists: `?Statuses=AUTHORISED,DRAFT`, `?IDs=`, `?InvoiceNumbers=`, `?ContactIDs=` (comma-separated). `SearchTerm` text-searches InvoiceNumber + Reference.
108	- Default order `UpdatedDateUTC ASC, InvoiceId ASC`; optimised order fields: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date.
109	- **Always page**: paged responses include full `LineItems`; an unpaged multi-invoice GET returns contact summaries and NO line details. `summaryOnly=true` drops Payments/LineItems/HasAttachments and forces pagination.
110	
111	### Idempotent create (check-by-reference)
112	
113	Because ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is non-unique, "does this bill already exist?" must combine fields — filter on `Contact.ContactID` + `InvoiceNumber` (both optimised) and compare `Total` — or better, persist the returned `InvoiceID` and never re-create. Belt-and-braces: a stable `Idempotency-Key` per logical write (6-minute window only — it protects against retries, not against re-runs tomorrow).
114	
115	## Purchase Orders — `/PurchaseOrders`
116	
117	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER.** The Xero MCP server has zero purchase-order tools (verified by grep of its `src/tools/` — no tool name contains "purchase"). Same for Attachments and History. The raw API below is the ONLY path. Do not go looking for an MCP tool.
118	
119	| Field | Notes |
120	|---|---|
121	| `Contact` | required; must be an **existing** contact — "The PurchaseOrders endpoint does not create new contacts" |
122	| `LineItems` | at least one required; if `Quantity` is set, `UnitAmount` must be too; `UnitAmount` rounds to **4dp** (no `unitdp` needed) |
123	| `PurchaseOrderNumber` | **unique**; auto-generated from org invoice settings when missing |
124	| `Status` | `DRAFT` (default) \| `SUBMITTED` \| `AUTHORISED` \| `BILLED` \| `DELETED` |
125	| `Date` / `DeliveryDate` / `Reference` / `LineAmountTypes` / `CurrencyCode` | as for bills |
126	
127	- **Create:** PUT (create-only) or POST, envelope `{"PurchaseOrders":[...]}`, `?summarizeErrors=false` for per-element errors. Line-item update rule identical to invoices.
128	- **Status update:** POST to `/PurchaseOrders/{PurchaseOrderID}` with the new `Status` (docs' example flips a draft PO to `DELETED`). **Marking billed** = same mechanism with `"Status": "BILLED"` — but Xero publishes no PO transition table and no explicit mark-as-billed example, so which transitions are legal is UNVERIFIED — check before relying (probe on the seeded org first). `SentToContact` can be set only on approved or billed POs.
129	- **Listing:** explicit params only — `?status=DRAFT`, `?DateFrom=2015-12-01&DateTo=2015-12-31`, `order`, `page`, `pageSize`; changed-since via `If-Modified-Since`. A generic `where` param is not documented for POs — UNVERIFIED — check before relying (use the explicit params).
130	- **Paging is enforced by default**: 100/page, `pageSize` up to 1000.
131	
132	## Attachments — `/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}`
133	
134	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER** — raw API only.
135	
136	- **Upload:** PUT or POST with the **raw file bytes** as the body — not JSON. `Content-Type` = the file's MIME type. PUT and POST are identical; the same filename on the same document **overwrites**. Example: `PUT /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}/Attachments/evidence.pdf`.
137	- **Limits:** **10 attachments per document, each up to 10MB** (the Contacts page says 25MB per file — the two official pages conflict; assume 10MB, do not rely on more without testing: UNVERIFIED above 10MB).
138	- **Filename rules:** any of `< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +` → rejected as Bad Request. Special characters must NOT be percent-encoded — except brackets, which MUST be encoded or the call fails.
139	- **List:** `GET /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/` → `Attachments[]` of `{AttachmentID, FileName, Url, MimeType, ContentLength}`. **Fetch content:** GET the `Url` (`.../Attachments/{Filename}`) — returns raw bytes with the file's `Content-Type` and a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header, no JSON wrapper.
140	- **`?IncludeOnline=true`** on the upload URL makes the attachment visible on the online invoice — but only for **accounts receivable invoices and AR credit notes**. It does not apply to ACCPAY bills, so it is irrelevant to this project's evidence uploads; the response echoes `"IncludeOnline": true` when used.
141	- Parents supporting attachments: Invoices, Receipts, Credit Notes, Repeating Invoices, Bank Transactions, Bank Transfers, Contacts, Accounts, Manual Journals, Purchase Orders, Quotes.
142	
143	## History & Notes — `/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history`
144	
145	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER** — raw API only. This is how the agent's decision note lands on a bill/PO.
146	
147	Exact PUT shape (POST behaves identically):
148	
149	```json
150	{ "HistoryRecords": [ { "Details": "Matched to PO-0042; approved under threshold rule." } ] }
151	```
152	
153	- `Details` max **2500 chars**; multiple notes per call allowed.
154	- The note displays the **date of creation** and user **"System Generated"** — notes cannot be backdated, updated, or deleted, and Notes are the only Change type you can create.
155	- GET returns `HistoryRecords[]` of `{Changes, DateUTC, User, Details}` (e.g. `Changes: "Approved"`).
156	- Supported parents: BankTransactions, BatchPayments, BankTransfers (via BankTransactions), Contacts, Creditnotes, Invoices, Items, ManualJournals, Overpayments, Payments, Prepayments, Purchase Orders, Repeating Invoices, Quotes.
157	
158	## Contacts — `/Contacts` (supplier matching fields)
159	
160	| Field | Notes |
161	|---|---|
162	| `ContactID` | the only stable key — Xero warns Name "may no longer be a unique field"; never key on Name |
163	| `Name` | required, max 255; no angle brackets, no leading/trailing/repeated whitespace |
164	| `EmailAddress` | max 255, umlauts not supported |
165	| `IsSupplier` | read-only — auto-set once an AP invoice exists against the contact; **cannot** be set via PUT/POST |
166	| `ContactStatus` | `ACTIVE` / `ARCHIVED` / `GDPRREQUEST` (only ACTIVE is usable in transactions) |
167	
168	- Optimised (equals-only, case- and accent-insensitive): `where=Name="ABC limited"`, `where=EmailAddress="email@example.com"`. Avoid `Contains`/`StartsWith` — unoptimised, and >100k rows → 400.
169	- `?SearchTerm=peter` — case-insensitive across Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress. This is the candidate source for fuzzy matching; the scoring itself lives in the match engine, not here.
170	- **Always page** (`?page=1`): unpaged GET returns a subset of fields. **Never `summaryOnly=true` for matching** — it excludes `IsSupplier`/`IsCustomer`.
171	- Contacts are **not auto-deduplicated by email** — posting an invoice with a new contact *name* creates a NEW contact every time. Look up first, reuse the `ContactID`.
172	
173	## Reports — `GET /Reports/AgedPayablesByContact`
174	
175	| Param | Notes |
176	|---|---|
177	| `ContactID` | **REQUIRED** — guid. Parameter table spells it `contactID`, the docs' example URL uses `ContactID`; casing tolerance UNVERIFIED — match the example: `?ContactID={guid}` |
178	| `date` | payments up to this date; defaults to end of current month |
179	| `fromDate` / `toDate` | payable-invoice window for the contact |
180	
181	Response: `Reports[0].Rows[]`, each `RowType` one of `Header` (column labels: Date, Reference, Due Date, blank, Total, Paid, Credited, Due), `Section` (nests `Rows`), `Row` (data — each Cell `{Value, Attributes}` where `Attributes` carries `{Id:"invoiceID", Value:<guid>}` for drill-down), `SummaryRow` (totals). See `reportDataRows()` in the script for the walk.
182	
183	## Snippets
184	
185	All compile standalone (strict, ES2022, commonjs, `@types/node` only) and import nothing but node builtins and `./xero-fetch`. Run with `npx tsx <file>` from the repo root with the `.env` vars exported into the environment first (tsx does not auto-load `.env`).
186	
187	| File | Shows |
188	|---|---|
189	| `scripts/xero-fetch.ts` | the thin helper: env assertion, token cache, `Accept: application/json`, query encoding, 429/`Retry-After` retry, 400-envelope parsing, `assertElementOk` |
190	| `scripts/bills.ts` | list ACCPAY with `where`/order/page/If-Modified-Since, create bill (PUT + idempotency key + `summarizeErrors=false`), legal status walk |
191	| `scripts/purchase-orders.ts` | PO create/list/get, status update incl. `BILLED` |
192	| `scripts/attachments.ts` | raw-bytes upload onto an invoice, filename guard, list |
193	| `scripts/history-note.ts` | append the agent's decision note (exact PUT shape), read history |
194	| `scripts/suppliers-and-report.ts` | contact lookup by Name/EmailAddress/searchTerm, aged payables + row walker |
195	
196	## Traps (these bite in production)
197	
198	- **ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is non-unique and shows up as "Reference" in the Xero UI.** Existence checks must combine ContactID + InvoiceNumber + Total, or persist `InvoiceID`.
199	- **A 200 is not success on batch writes.** With `summarizeErrors=false`, failed elements hide inside a 200 — assert `StatusAttributeString` per element.
200	- **Omitting `LineItemID`s in an update deletes those lines.** Send every existing line's id, or send no `LineItems` at all for status-only updates.
201	- **Unpaged list calls silently drop data**: multi-invoice GETs lose LineItems; unpaged Contacts lose fields; `summaryOnly` loses `IsSupplier`. Always `?page=`.
202	- **`If-Modified-Since` is not a full change feed** — `IsSupplier`, `Balances`, and DueDate-on-partially-paid edits don't bump `UpdatedDateUTC`.
203	- **Idempotency keys cache errors** for 6 minutes — recover with GET-then-new-key, never blind retry with the same key.
204	- **`unitdp` differs by endpoint**: invoice UnitAmounts are 2dp unless `?unitdp=4`; PO UnitAmounts are natively 4dp. Penny drift between a PO and its bill can be rounding, not fraud — the match engine must compare at consistent precision.
205	- **`AUTHORISED` is a one-way door** — no edits back to DRAFT, no delete, only VOIDED (unpaid only). Do the match BEFORE approving.
206	- **Locked periods reject updates** with a validation error even on otherwise-legal transitions.
207	- **`IncludeOnline` is AR-only** — it does nothing for ACCPAY bills.
208	- **History notes are permanent and timestamped at creation** — never write anything into a note you would not show an auditor.
209	- **The PO endpoint never creates contacts** (unlike invoice creation by contact name, which spawns duplicates) — resolve the ContactID first.
210	
211	## Error → real cause
212	
213	| Symptom | Real cause |
214	|---|---|
215	| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | empty/missing `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` — assert env before building the request |
216	| `invalid_scope` / 403 on a call that used to work | scope missing from app config or token request; scopes live at BOTH ends (see pre-flight 3) |
217	| 403 on `/Reports/...` only | authorising user lacks the 'reports' role |
218	| HTTP 400 + `ApiException` | validation failure — read `Elements[].ValidationErrors[].Message` |
219	| HTTP 200 but the entity never appeared | `summarizeErrors=false` swallowed a per-element `ERROR` — check `StatusAttributeString` |
220	| 429 | rate limit; `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names which; sleep `Retry-After` seconds (helper does) |
221	| 400 `Idempotency Key ... used with a different request` | same key, different URL/body/method within the 6-minute window |
222	| Same error repeats after the fix | cached idempotent error — GET to check existence, retry with a NEW key |
223	| 400 Bad Request on attachment upload | forbidden filename char (`< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +`), or unencoded brackets, or file over the cap |
224	| 400 on a list call | unoptimised `where`/`order` over >100k rows, or unencoded `where` expression |
225	| Line items vanished after an update | `LineItems` array supplied without every existing `LineItemID` |
226	| Can't set status `PAID` | PAID is system-set — create/approve to `AUTHORISED`, then apply a Payment |
227	| Can't move `AUTHORISED` back to `DRAFT` | illegal transition — AUTHORISED only stays or becomes `VOIDED` |
228	| Update rejected on an old bill | locked period, or the bill is partially/fully paid (only the whitelisted fields are editable) |
229	| Duplicate suppliers appearing | invoices posted with a contact *name* instead of a resolved `ContactID` |
230	| History note rejected | `Details` over 2500 chars |
231

241	-->
242
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/xero-fetch.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/xero-fetch.ts
2	//
3	// Thin typed HTTP helper for the Xero Accounting API over a Custom Connection
4	// (client_credentials grant, single organisation). Transport only: env
5	// assertion, token caching, JSON Accept header, query encoding, 429 retry
6	// honouring Retry-After, and error-envelope parsing. No business logic —
7	// match/approval decisions live in api/src/modules/.
8	
9	export const ACCOUNTING_BASE = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
10	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
11	
12	// Custom connections set scopes at app creation AND in each token request
13	// body. Override with XERO_SCOPES (space-separated); default mirrors the
14	// scopes named in .env.example.
15	const DEFAULT_SCOPES =
16	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
17	
18	export function requireEnv(name: string): string {
19	  const value = process.env[name];
20	  if (!value || value.trim() === "") {
21	    throw new Error(
22	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — set it in .env at the repo root. ` +
23	        `An empty client id/secret surfaces from Xero as "unauthorized_client", ` +
24	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
25	    );
26	  }
27	  return value;
28	}
29	
30	interface TokenResponse {
31	  access_token: string;
32	  expires_in: number; // seconds; access tokens live at most ~30 minutes
33	  token_type: string;
34	  scope?: string;
35	}
36	
37	let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAtMs: number } | null = null;
38	
39	/** Client-credentials token, cached in-process until 60s before expiry. */
40	export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
41	  if (cachedToken && cachedToken.expiresAtMs > Date.now() + 60_000) {
42	    return cachedToken.token;
43	  }
44	
45	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
46	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
47	  const scope = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || DEFAULT_SCOPES;
48	
49	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
50	    method: "POST",
51	    headers: {
52	      Authorization: `Basic ${Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64")}`,
53	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
54	    },
55	    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope }).toString(),
56	  });
57	
58	  if (!res.ok) {
59	    // Body carries Xero's OAuth error code (e.g. unauthorized_client, invalid_scope).
60	    // Never log the secret itself.
61	    const body = await res.text();
62	    throw new Error(`XeroTokenError: ${res.status} ${body.slice(0, 500)}`);
63	  }
64	
65	  const token = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
66	  cachedToken = {
67	    token: token.access_token,
68	    expiresAtMs: Date.now() + token.expires_in * 1000,
69	  };
70	  return cachedToken.token;
71	}
72	
73	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	// Error envelope (HTTP 400 ApiException, and per-element validation shapes)
75	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	export interface XeroValidationError {
78	  /** The 400 ApiException envelope uses "Message". */
79	  Message?: string;
80	  /** The docs' summarizeErrors=false bulk example uses "Description". */
81	  Description?: string;
82	}
83	
84	export interface XeroErrorElement {
85	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
86	  [key: string]: unknown;
87	}
88	
89	/** HTTP 400 body shape: { ErrorNumber, Type, Message, Elements[].ValidationErrors[] } */
90	export interface XeroApiException {
91	  ErrorNumber?: number;
92	  Type?: string;
93	  Message?: string;
94	  Elements?: XeroErrorElement[];
95	}
96	
97	export function flattenValidationErrors(envelope: XeroApiException): string[] {
98	  const out: string[] = [];
99	  for (const element of envelope.Elements ?? []) {
100	    for (const err of element.ValidationErrors ?? []) {
101	      const text = err.Message ?? err.Description;
102	      if (text) out.push(text);
103	    }
104	  }
105	  return out;
106	}
107	
108	export class XeroHttpError extends Error {
109	  constructor(
110	    readonly status: number,
111	    readonly envelope: XeroApiException | null,
112	    readonly rawBody: string,
113	  ) {
114	    const details = envelope ? flattenValidationErrors(envelope) : [];
115	    super(
116	      `XeroHttpError ${status}: ${envelope?.Message ?? rawBody.slice(0, 300)}` +
117	        (details.length > 0 ? ` | ValidationErrors: ${details.join("; ")}` : ""),
118	    );
119	    this.name = "XeroHttpError";
120	  }
121	}
122	
123	/**
124	 * With ?summarizeErrors=false a batch write returns HTTP 200 even when an
125	 * element failed — each element carries StatusAttributeString OK/WARNING/ERROR.
126	 */
127	export interface StatusAttributed {
128	  StatusAttributeString?: "OK" | "WARNING" | "ERROR";
129	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
130	  Warnings?: XeroValidationError[];
131	}
132	
133	export function assertElementOk<T extends StatusAttributed>(element: T, context: string): T {
134	  if (element.StatusAttributeString === "ERROR") {
135	    const messages = (element.ValidationErrors ?? [])
136	      .map((e) => e.Message ?? e.Description ?? "unknown")
137	      .join("; ");
138	    throw new Error(`XeroValidationError: ${context}: ${messages}`);
139	  }
140	  return element;
141	}
142	
143	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
144	// Core request
145	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	export interface XeroRequestInit {
148	  method?: "GET" | "PUT" | "POST";
149	  /** JSON payload — serialised and sent with Content-Type: application/json. */
150	  json?: unknown;
151	  /** Raw bytes (attachment uploads). Requires contentType. */
152	  body?: Uint8Array;
153	  /** Content-Type for raw `body` uploads — must be the file's MIME type. */
154	  contentType?: string;
155	  /** Query params (where, order, page, pageSize, ...). Values are URL-encoded here. */
156	  query?: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined>;
157	  /** Sent as Idempotency-Key. POST/PUT only; Xero caches 6 min; max 128 chars. */
158	  idempotencyKey?: string;
159	  /** Sent as If-Modified-Since (UTC, accurate to the second). */
160	  ifModifiedSince?: Date;
161	  /** Override Accept. Default application/json (Xero's default is XML). */
162	  accept?: string;
163	}
164	
165	const MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES = 3;
166	
167	function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
168	  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
169	}
170	
171	/**
172	 * Low-level call: auth + headers + query encoding + 429 retry. Throws
173	 * XeroHttpError on any non-2xx (with the parsed ApiException when present).
174	 *
175	 * No xero-tenant-id header: a Custom Connection is bound to exactly ONE
176	 * organisation, so the tenant is implicit — the documented custom-connection
177	 * call carries only Authorization + Accept (see SKILL.md).
178	 */
179	export async function xeroRequest(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<Response> {
180	  const token = await getAccessToken();
181	
182	  const url = new URL(`${ACCOUNTING_BASE}${path}`);
183	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(init.query ?? {})) {
184	    if (value !== undefined) url.searchParams.set(key, String(value));
185	  }
186	
187	  const headers: Record<string, string> = {
188	    Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
189	    Accept: init.accept ?? "application/json",
190	  };
191	  if (init.json !== undefined) headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
192	  if (init.body !== undefined) {
193	    if (!init.contentType) {
194	      throw new Error("MissingContentType: raw body uploads must set the file's MIME type");
195	    }
196	    headers["Content-Type"] = init.contentType;
197	  }
198	  if (init.idempotencyKey !== undefined) {
199	    if (init.idempotencyKey.length > 128) {
200	      throw new Error(
201	        `IdempotencyKeyTooLong: ${init.idempotencyKey.length} chars — Xero rejects keys over 128 with a 400`,
202	      );
203	    }
204	    headers["Idempotency-Key"] = init.idempotencyKey;
205	  }
206	  if (init.ifModifiedSince !== undefined) {
207	    headers["If-Modified-Since"] = init.ifModifiedSince.toUTCString();
208	  }
209	
210	  let body: string | ArrayBuffer | undefined;
211	  if (init.json !== undefined) {
212	    body = JSON.stringify(init.json);
213	  } else if (init.body !== undefined) {
214	    // Copy into a plain ArrayBuffer so the bytes satisfy fetch's BodyInit
215	    // regardless of the Uint8Array's backing buffer type.
216	    const buffer = new ArrayBuffer(init.body.byteLength);
217	    new Uint8Array(buffer).set(init.body);
218	    body = buffer;
219	  }
220	
221	  for (let attempt = 0; ; attempt++) {
222	    const res = await fetch(url, { method: init.method ?? "GET", headers, body });
223	
224	    if (res.status === 429 && attempt < MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES) {
225	      // Xero sends Retry-After (seconds) on minute/daily limits and
226	      // X-Rate-Limit-Problem naming the limit hit. Honour Retry-After.
227	      const retryAfterSec = Number(res.headers.get("retry-after") ?? "0");
228	      const waitMs = retryAfterSec > 0 ? retryAfterSec * 1000 : 1_000;
229	      console.warn(
230	        `[xero] 429 (limit: ${res.headers.get("x-rate-limit-problem") ?? "unknown"}) — retrying in ${waitMs}ms`,
231	      );
232	      await sleep(waitMs);
233	      continue;
234	    }
235	
236	    if (!res.ok) {
237	      const raw = await res.text();
238	      let envelope: XeroApiException | null = null;
239	      try {
240	        envelope = JSON.parse(raw) as XeroApiException;
241	      } catch {
242	        envelope = null;
243	      }
244	      throw new XeroHttpError(res.status, envelope, raw);
245	    }
246	
247	    return res;
248	  }
249	}
250	
251	/** JSON call — the default for everything except raw attachment content. */
252	export async function xeroFetch<T>(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<T> {
253	  const res = await xeroRequest(path, init);
254	  return (await res.json()) as T;
255	}
256
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts
2	//
3	// ACCPAY bills on the /Invoices endpoint: filtered list (where/order/page),
4	// create (PUT), and the DRAFT -> SUBMITTED -> AUTHORISED status walk (POST).
5	// Shapes trimmed to the fields this project reads/writes. Transport facts
6	// only — matching and approval decisions live in api/src/modules/.
7	
8	import { assertElementOk, xeroFetch, type StatusAttributed } from "./xero-fetch";
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Trimmed shapes
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	export type InvoiceStatus = "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "PAID" | "VOIDED" | "DELETED";
15	export type LineAmountTypes = "Exclusive" | "Inclusive" | "NoTax";
16	
17	export interface BillLineItem {
18	  /** Omit on create. On update: any existing line whose LineItemID is missing
19	   *  from a supplied LineItems array is DELETED and recreated. */
20	  LineItemID?: string;
21	  Description: string; // 1..4000 chars; a line can be Description-only
22	  Quantity?: number;
23	  UnitAmount?: number; // rounded to 2dp unless the request uses ?unitdp=4
24	  AccountCode?: string;
25	  TaxType?: string; // override only when the AccountCode default is wrong
26	  LineAmount?: number;
27	}
28	
29	export interface Bill {
30	  InvoiceID?: string;
31	  Type: "ACCPAY";
32	  Contact: { ContactID: string }; // only ContactID on create/update
33	  Date?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today in the org timezone
34	  DueDate?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
35	  /** NON-unique for ACCPAY — displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI. Max 255. */
36	  InvoiceNumber?: string;
37	  Status?: InvoiceStatus; // defaults to DRAFT on create
38	  LineAmountTypes?: LineAmountTypes; // defaults to Exclusive when omitted
39	  LineItems: BillLineItem[];
40	  SubTotal?: number;
41	  TotalTax?: number;
42	  Total?: number; // SubTotal + TotalTax (computed)
43	  AmountDue?: number;
44	  AmountPaid?: number;
45	  CurrencyCode?: string; // defaults to the org base currency
46	  UpdatedDateUTC?: string; // "/Date(1439434356790)/" — unix ms inside
47	}
48	
49	export interface Pagination {
50	  page: number;
51	  pageSize: number;
52	  pageCount: number;
53	  itemCount: number;
54	}
55	
56	export interface InvoicesResponse {
57	  Invoices: Array<Bill & StatusAttributed>;
58	  pagination?: Pagination;
59	}
60	
61	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	// List bills — where/order/page
63	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	
65	/**
66	 * where strings use double quotes and == (official invoice filter example:
67	 * Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"). Percent-encoding is handled by the
68	 * helper's query serialiser. Always page: paged responses include full
69	 * LineItems; unpaged multi-invoice GETs return NO line details.
70	 */
71	export async function listBills(
72	  options: {
73	    status?: InvoiceStatus;
74	    page?: number;
75	    pageSize?: number; // default 100, max 1000
76	    modifiedSince?: Date;
77	  } = {},
78	): Promise<InvoicesResponse> {
79	  const where = options.status
80	    ? `Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="${options.status}"`
81	    : `Type=="ACCPAY"`;
82	  return xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>("/Invoices", {
83	    query: {
84	      where,
85	      // Optimised order fields on Invoices: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date.
86	      order: "UpdatedDateUTC ASC",
87	      page: options.page ?? 1,
88	      pageSize: options.pageSize,
89	    },
90	    ifModifiedSince: options.modifiedSince,
91	  });
92	}
93	
94	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	// Create — PUT creates only; POST creates or updates
96	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	/**
99	 * Creates a bill (status defaults to DRAFT). summarizeErrors=false means a
100	 * validation failure comes back as HTTP 200 with a per-element
101	 * StatusAttributeString of ERROR — always assert it.
102	 *
103	 * The idempotency key must be stable per logical write and persisted by the
104	 * caller (Xero caches it for 6 minutes; same key + different body → 400).
105	 */
106	export async function createBill(
107	  bill: Omit<Bill, "InvoiceID">,
108	  idempotencyKey: string,
109	): Promise<Bill> {
110	  const res = await xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>("/Invoices", {
111	    method: "PUT",
112	    json: { Invoices: [bill] },
113	    query: { summarizeErrors: false, unitdp: 4 },
114	    idempotencyKey,
115	  });
116	  const created = res.Invoices[0];
117	  if (!created) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: PUT /Invoices returned no Invoices element");
118	  return assertElementOk(created, `create bill ${bill.InvoiceNumber ?? "(no number)"}`);
119	}
120	
121	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122	// Status transitions — POST /Invoices/{InvoiceID}
123	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
124	
125	/**
126	 * Legal transitions (from the official Invoices status table):
127	 *   DRAFT     -> DRAFT | SUBMITTED | AUTHORISED | DELETED
128	 *   SUBMITTED -> SUBMITTED | AUTHORISED | DRAFT | DELETED
129	 *   AUTHORISED -> AUTHORISED | VOIDED   (never back to DRAFT/SUBMITTED)
130	 * PAID is system-set when fully paid — never write it. Status-only updates
131	 * send no LineItems array (sending one without every LineItemID deletes lines).
132	 */
133	export async function setBillStatus(
134	  invoiceId: string,
135	  status: "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "DELETED" | "VOIDED",
136	  idempotencyKey: string,
137	): Promise<Bill> {
138	  const res = await xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>(`/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
139	    method: "POST",
140	    json: { Invoices: [{ InvoiceID: invoiceId, Status: status }] },
141	    idempotencyKey,
142	  });
143	  const updated = res.Invoices[0];
144	  if (!updated) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: POST /Invoices returned no Invoices element");
145	  return updated;
146	}
147	
148	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	// Demo entrypoint: npx tsx bills.ts  (lists first page of ACCPAY bills)
150	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	async function main(): Promise<void> {
153	  const res = await listBills({ page: 1, pageSize: 10 });
154	  console.log(`Fetched ${res.Invoices.length} bills (pagination:`, res.pagination, ")");
155	  for (const bill of res.Invoices) {
156	    console.log(`- ${bill.InvoiceNumber ?? "(no number)"} ${bill.Status} total=${bill.Total}`);
157	  }
158	}
159	
160	if (require.main === module) {
161	  main().catch((err) => {
162	    console.error(err);
163	    process.exitCode = 1;
164	  });
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/purchase-orders.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/purchase-orders.ts
2	//
3	// Purchase Orders — NOT in the Xero MCP server (zero purchase-order tools);
4	// the raw Accounting API is the ONLY path. Create (PUT), list (explicit
5	// params — a generic `where` is not documented for POs), and status updates
6	// (POST), including marking billed. Transport facts only.
7	
8	import { assertElementOk, xeroFetch, type StatusAttributed } from "./xero-fetch";
9	import type { Pagination } from "./bills";
10	
11	export type PurchaseOrderStatus = "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "BILLED" | "DELETED";
12	
13	export interface PurchaseOrderLineItem {
14	  /** Same rule as invoices: on update, lines missing their LineItemID are deleted. */
15	  LineItemID?: string;
16	  Description: string; // a line can be Description-only
17	  Quantity?: number; // if Quantity is specified, UnitAmount must be too
18	  UnitAmount?: number; // POs round UnitAmount to FOUR decimal places
19	  AccountCode?: string;
20	  TaxType?: string;
21	  LineAmount?: number;
22	}
23	
24	export interface PurchaseOrder {
25	  PurchaseOrderID?: string;
26	  /** Unique alphanumeric; auto-generated from org invoice settings when missing. */
27	  PurchaseOrderNumber?: string;
28	  /** Must reference an EXISTING contact — this endpoint never creates contacts. */
29	  Contact: { ContactID: string };
30	  Date?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today in the org timezone
31	  DeliveryDate?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
32	  Reference?: string;
33	  Status?: PurchaseOrderStatus; // examples create DRAFT by default or pass AUTHORISED
34	  LineAmountTypes?: "Exclusive" | "Inclusive" | "NoTax"; // Exclusive when omitted
35	  LineItems: PurchaseOrderLineItem[];
36	  SubTotal?: number;
37	  TotalTax?: number;
38	  Total?: number;
39	  CurrencyCode?: string;
40	  UpdatedDateUTC?: string;
41	}
42	
43	export interface PurchaseOrdersResponse {
44	  PurchaseOrders: Array<PurchaseOrder & StatusAttributed>;
45	  pagination?: Pagination;
46	}
47	
48	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	// List — paging is enforced by default on POs (100/page, pageSize up to 1000)
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	
52	export async function listPurchaseOrders(
53	  options: {
54	    status?: PurchaseOrderStatus; // explicit Status param, e.g. ?status=DRAFT
55	    dateFrom?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
56	    dateTo?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
57	    page?: number;
58	    pageSize?: number;
59	    modifiedSince?: Date;
60	  } = {},
61	): Promise<PurchaseOrdersResponse> {
62	  return xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>("/PurchaseOrders", {
63	    query: {
64	      Status: options.status,
65	      DateFrom: options.dateFrom,
66	      DateTo: options.dateTo,
67	      page: options.page ?? 1,
68	      pageSize: options.pageSize,
69	    },
70	    ifModifiedSince: options.modifiedSince,
71	  });
72	}
73	
74	export async function getPurchaseOrder(purchaseOrderId: string): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
75	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>(`/PurchaseOrders/${purchaseOrderId}`);
76	  const po = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
77	  if (!po) throw new Error(`XeroNotFound: PurchaseOrder ${purchaseOrderId} not in response`);
78	  return po;
79	}
80	
81	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	// Create — PUT creates only; POST creates or updates
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	
85	export async function createPurchaseOrder(
86	  purchaseOrder: Omit<PurchaseOrder, "PurchaseOrderID">,
87	  idempotencyKey: string,
88	): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
89	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>("/PurchaseOrders", {
90	    method: "PUT",
91	    json: { PurchaseOrders: [purchaseOrder] },
92	    query: { summarizeErrors: false },
93	    idempotencyKey,
94	  });
95	  const created = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
96	  if (!created) {
97	    throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: PUT /PurchaseOrders returned no PurchaseOrders element");
98	  }
99	  return assertElementOk(created, `create PO ${purchaseOrder.PurchaseOrderNumber ?? "(auto)"}`);
100	}
101	
102	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	// Status updates — POST with the new Status (official example: DRAFT -> DELETED)
104	// Marking billed = Status "BILLED" via the same mechanism. Xero publishes no
105	// PO transition table, so verify each transition against a live org.
106	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	export async function setPurchaseOrderStatus(
109	  purchaseOrderId: string,
110	  status: PurchaseOrderStatus,
111	  idempotencyKey: string,
112	): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
113	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>(`/PurchaseOrders/${purchaseOrderId}`, {
114	    method: "POST",
115	    json: { PurchaseOrders: [{ PurchaseOrderID: purchaseOrderId, Status: status }] },
116	    idempotencyKey,
117	  });
118	  const updated = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
119	  if (!updated) {
120	    throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: POST /PurchaseOrders returned no PurchaseOrders element");
121	  }
122	  return updated;
123	}
124	
125	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
126	// Demo entrypoint: npx tsx purchase-orders.ts  (lists first page of POs)
127	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	
129	async function main(): Promise<void> {
130	  const res = await listPurchaseOrders({ page: 1, pageSize: 10 });
131	  console.log(`Fetched ${res.PurchaseOrders.length} purchase orders`);
132	  for (const po of res.PurchaseOrders) {
133	    console.log(`- ${po.PurchaseOrderNumber} ${po.Status} total=${po.Total}`);
134	  }
135	}
136	
137	if (require.main === module) {
138	  main().catch((err) => {
139	    console.error(err);
140	    process.exitCode = 1;
141	  });
142	}
143
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/attachments.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/attachments.ts
2	//
3	// Attachments — NOT in the Xero MCP server; raw Accounting API only.
4	// Upload = raw file bytes PUT/POST to /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
5	// (NOT JSON). Content-Type must be the file's MIME type. 10 attachments per
6	// document, 10MB per file (documented cap on the Attachments page).
7	
8	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
9	import { basename } from "node:path";
10	import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";
11	
12	/** Filenames containing any of < > : " / \ | ? * NUL + are rejected as Bad Request. */
13	const FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS = /[<>:"/\\|?*\u0000+]/;
14	
15	const MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;
16	
17	export type AttachmentParent = "Invoices" | "PurchaseOrders" | "Contacts";
18	
19	export interface Attachment {
20	  AttachmentID: string;
21	  FileName: string;
22	  Url: string;
23	  MimeType: string;
24	  ContentLength: number;
25	  IncludeOnline?: boolean;
26	}
27	
28	export interface AttachmentsResponse {
29	  Attachments: Attachment[];
30	}
31	
32	/**
33	 * Docs rule: filename characters "should not be encoded unless they're
34	 * brackets. Brackets must be encoded in order for the call to go through."
35	 */
36	function encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName: string): string {
37	  return fileName
38	    .replace(/\(/g, "%28")
39	    .replace(/\)/g, "%29")
40	    .replace(/\[/g, "%5B")
41	    .replace(/\]/g, "%5D");
42	}
43	
44	export async function uploadAttachment(options: {
45	  endpoint: AttachmentParent;
46	  guid: string; // e.g. the InvoiceID of the bill the evidence belongs to
47	  filePath: string;
48	  mimeType: string; // e.g. "application/pdf", "image/png"
49	  fileName?: string; // defaults to basename(filePath)
50	  /**
51	   * IncludeOnline=true shows the attachment on the online invoice. Xero
52	   * supports this for accounts RECEIVABLE invoices and AR credit notes only —
53	   * it does not apply to ACCPAY bills.
54	   */
55	  includeOnline?: boolean;
56	}): Promise<Attachment> {
57	  const fileName = options.fileName ?? basename(options.filePath);
58	  if (FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS.test(fileName)) {
59	    throw new Error(
60	      `BadFileName: "${fileName}" contains a character Xero rejects as Bad Request ` +
61	        String.raw`(< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +)`,
62	    );
63	  }
64	
65	  const bytes = readFileSync(options.filePath);
66	  if (bytes.byteLength > MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES) {
67	    throw new Error(
68	      `AttachmentTooLarge: ${bytes.byteLength} bytes — the Attachments page caps files at 10MB ` +
69	        `(max 10 attachments per document)`,
70	    );
71	  }
72	
73	  // PUT and POST are identical here; uploading the same filename onto the
74	  // same document OVERWRITES the existing attachment.
75	  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(
76	    `/${options.endpoint}/${options.guid}/Attachments/${encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName)}`,
77	    {
78	      method: "PUT",
79	      body: new Uint8Array(bytes),
80	      contentType: options.mimeType,
81	      query: options.includeOnline ? { IncludeOnline: true } : undefined,
82	    },
83	  );
84	  const attachment = res.Attachments[0];
85	  if (!attachment) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: upload returned no Attachments element");
86	  return attachment;
87	}
88	
89	export async function listAttachments(
90	  endpoint: AttachmentParent,
91	  guid: string,
92	): Promise<Attachment[]> {
93	  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/Attachments/`);
94	  return res.Attachments;
95	}
96	
97	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	// Demo entrypoint:
99	//   npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>
100	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	
102	async function main(): Promise<void> {
103	  const [guid, filePath, mimeType] = process.argv.slice(2);
104	  if (!guid || !filePath || !mimeType) {
105	    throw new Error("Usage: npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>");
106	  }
107	  const uploaded = await uploadAttachment({ endpoint: "Invoices", guid, filePath, mimeType });
108	  console.log(`Uploaded ${uploaded.FileName} (${uploaded.ContentLength} bytes)`);
109	  const all = await listAttachments("Invoices", guid);
110	  console.log(`Document now has ${all.length}/10 attachments`);
111	}
112	
113	if (require.main === module) {
114	  main().catch((err) => {
115	    console.error(err);
116	    process.exitCode = 1;
117	  });
118	}
119
```
</details></tool-use>
