<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md`

```text
1	# Xero prompt-library skill-writing style: fact sheet
2	
3	Sources (all in `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/`):
4	- `lovable/xero-integration-single-account/SKILL.md` (707 lines)
5	- `lovable/xero-integration-multi-account/SKILL.md` (868 lines)
6	- `javascript/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant`, 452 lines)
7	- `python/SKILL.md` (`xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`, 502 lines)
8	- `replit/xero-integration-single-account-skill` (plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter, 293 lines)
9	- `README.md` (repo index, no skill discipline content)
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. Document structure conventions
14	
15	### Frontmatter
16	Exactly two fields, always: `name` and `description`. No version, no tools, no metadata.
17	
18	- `name` is the kebab-case skill id (`xero-integration-single-account`, `xero-integration-multi-tenant-python`).
19	- `description` is a single long paragraph that does four jobs, in roughly this order:
20	  1. **What it builds**, with the discriminating word in CAPS: "Build a MULTI-TENANT Xero integration where EACH end user of the app connects THEIR OWN Xero organisation".
21	  2. **When to use it**: "Use whenever building a multi-tenant SaaS on Xero".
22	  3. **What it covers**, as a comma list of the hard parts: "Covers the full OAuth2 + PKCE lifecycle with the official `xero-node` SDK, per-user encrypted token storage, refresh/rotation, tenant selection, scopes (including the granular-scope migration), and the Xero API gotchas that bite in production (date/money/tax normalisation, idempotent writes, rate limits)."
23	  4. **A read-first command and a routing rule to the sibling skill**: "Read this BEFORE writing any multi-tenant Xero code. If instead the app talks to a SINGLE Xero org that the builder owns, do NOT use this flow — see 'Do you actually need this?' at the top for the much simpler single-tenant approach."
24	
25	  The Replit variant adds an anti-pattern warning right in the description: "Read this before writing any Xero code; do NOT rely on generic xero-node knowledge."
26	
27	### Section order (consistent across all four full skills)
28	1. `# H1` restating the variant in plain words: "Multi-tenant Xero (each user connects their own Xero)".
29	2. **Variant check / decision gate first.** A blockquote or `## Do you actually need this?` section forcing the reader to confirm they picked the right skill, with an explicit pointer to the sibling: "Stop and use **`xero-integration-single-account`** instead." Each pair of skills cross-references the other by exact name. The deciding question is stated as one sentence: "The deciding question is *how many Xero logins exist*".
30	3. **Runtime context** ("Where this code runs in Lovable") — the platform facts that shape all code below, stated as numbered "runtime facts".
31	4. `## Decision check before building` — a 3-item ALL-must-be-true checklist.
32	5. `## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)` — see §2 below.
33	6. Numbered build sections in lifecycle order: `## 1. Register your own Xero app` → `## Scopes` → `## 2. Per-user token store` → `## 3.` (client/redirect resolver) → `## 4.` (start consent) → `## 5.` (callback) → `## 6.` (use per request / status UX).
34	7. Cross-cutting sections: `## Date + money + tax normalisation`, `## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)`, `## Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)`, platform-gotcha subsection, `## Security checklist (do not skip)`, `## Don't want to hand-roll it?` (managed alternatives: Nango, Composio).
35	8. `## Verify` — always last. A numbered end-to-end acceptance list (they even use step `0.` and `0.5.` to force pre-click checks before step 1). Every item is observable behavior, e.g. "Post the same invoice twice with the same idempotency key and confirm only one invoice is created."
36	
37	### Description/heading phrasing
38	- Headings carry the verdict, not just the topic: "The redirect-URI resolver — read carefully", "Other Xero gotchas (these bite in production)", "Security checklist (do not skip)", "CRITICAL: path mapping", "Failure handling (the rule that bites)".
39	- Section intros state *why the section exists* in one or two sentences before any code.
40	- Every code block opens with a file-path comment (`// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts`) so the reader knows where it lives.
41	- Sync-maintenance notes between sibling docs: "(This table is identical to the single-account skill — keep the two in sync if Xero's assignments change.)"
42	- External source-of-truth links with a staleness warning: "Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes"
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 2. Pre-flight check discipline (verbatim quotes)
47	
48	The Lovable skills open the build with a dedicated section. Header, verbatim:
49	
50	> ## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
51	>
52	> Three checks. Each one prevents a class of bug that produces a misleading
53	> upstream error from Xero. Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a
54	> human.
55	
56	**Check 1 — assert every secret non-empty, with the causal chain stated** (verbatim):
57	
58	> ### 1. Assert every secret is set and non-empty
59	>
60	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
61	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Fail fast
62	> with a clear message inside `xeroStart` **before** building the URL:
63	
64	```ts
65	function requireEnv(name: string) {
66	  const v = process.env[name];
67	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
68	    throw new Error(`${name} is empty — set it in Project Secrets and retry.`);
69	  }
70	  return v;
71	}
72	```
73	
74	> Call `requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID")`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_SCOPES`, and
75	> `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` at the top of every handler that uses them.
76	
77	**Check 2 — log the constructed request once, never the secret** (verbatim):
78	
79	> You want a server log you can read when something is wrong, without ever
80	> printing the secret value itself:
81	
82	```ts
83	console.log("[xero] authorize", {
84	  client_id_len: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID?.length,
85	  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
86	  scope: process.env.XERO_SCOPES,
87	});
88	```
89	
90	> If the next failure is `unauthorized_client` or `invalid redirect_uri`, this
91	> line tells you *exactly* which value Xero rejected.
92	
93	Note the trick: log `client_id_len` (the length), never the value.
94	
95	**Check 3 — smoke-test before any human clicks** (verbatim):
96	
97	> ### 3. Smoke-test `xeroStart` BEFORE clicking Connect
98	>
99	> Call the server function and inspect the returned URL: `client_id` must be a
100	> non-empty hex string, and `redirect_uri` must **exactly** match one of the URIs
101	> registered in your Xero app (including scheme, host, and path). If either is
102	> wrong, fix it here. Do not proceed to the popup — Xero's errors past this point
103	> are far less useful than the URL you can read right now.
104	
105	**Check 4 — validate key material shape, with the full failure story** (verbatim):
106	
107	> AES-256-GCM requires a **32-byte** key. A 32-character random string is NOT 32
108	> bytes once base64-decoded (it's ~24), and `createCipheriv` throws
109	> `Invalid key length` from deep inside `encrypt()` — which surfaces as an
110	> opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the
111	> single-use `code`. The user sees "something went wrong" with no way to retry
112	> without re-consenting, and no breadcrumb in the logs points at the key.
113	
114	Pattern to copy: each pre-flight check names (a) the invariant, (b) the misleading downstream symptom if skipped, (c) the exact code to assert it, (d) the moment to run it ("before you wire the Connect button to a human"). Boot-time assertion + named error, exactly the CLAUDE.md rule "Secrets in `.env` only, asserted non-empty at boot with named errors."
115	
116	They also make the *failure path itself* testable — Verify step 0.5, verbatim:
117	
118	> 0.5. **Force an encryption failure once.** Temporarily set `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` to
119	>    a 16-char string and click Connect. Confirm `/dashboard` shows
120	>    `?error=callback&detail=…` (with a readable message) rather than a blank
121	>    500 — this proves both the callback try/catch (§4) and the dashboard
122	>    banner (§6) actually wire failures back to the user. Restore the real
123	>    key afterwards.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## 3. Error → real-cause mappings (verbatim — the gold)
128	
129	These docs mostly encode error→cause as prose sentences and blockquote "traps", not literal tables. Every mapping found, copied verbatim with source:
130	
131	**`unauthorized_client` ← empty/missing client_id** (lovable single + multi, Pre-flight §1):
132	
133	> Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
134	> `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause.
135	
136	**`invalid redirect_uri` / `unauthorized_client` ← three redirect-origin traps** (lovable, §3 header block, verbatim):
137	
138	> ### ⚠️ Three traps, all of which produce `invalid redirect_uri` or `unauthorized_client`
139	>
140	> **Trap A — `getRequest()` / `request.url` on the server returns
141	> `http://localhost:8080`.** Under the Worker runtime the inbound request you
142	> see inside a server function is on the internal dev host, not the public URL.
143	> Never derive `redirect_uri` from it.
144	>
145	> **Trap B — In the Lovable editor, `window.location.origin` is
146	> `https://<project-id>.lovableproject.com`** (the iframe host), which is NOT
147	> a registered URI. Sending it raw produces `invalid redirect_uri` every time.
148	>
149	> **Trap C — A single `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret can't cover both preview and
150	> published.** If you set it to the published host the preview breaks (and
151	> vice-versa). Use the resolver below instead.
152	
153	**`unauthorized_client` / invalid-redirect ← port or trailing-slash mismatch** (javascript, Local-dev gotchas):
154	
155	> **Redirect URI must match exactly.** The most common local failure is a port or
156	> trailing-slash mismatch between `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` and what's registered in the
157	> portal. If consent returns `unauthorized_client` or an invalid-redirect error,
158	> this is almost always why. Run the dev server on the same port you registered.
159	
160	**403 ← missing write scope** (lovable multi, Scopes; wrong-secret-shaped failures are scope failures here):
161	
162	> Shipping a read-only token and then calling `POST /Invoices` fails
163	> at runtime with a 403 / insufficient-scope error — and because scopes **cannot be
164	> widened on an existing token**, the only fix is to send every connected customer
165	> back through the whole consent flow. So decide up front: read, write, or both.
166	
167	and the compressed single-account form:
168	
169	> Mismatched scope → 403 at runtime and the only fix is
170	> to send the user through consent again.
171	
172	and the silent-default variant (lovable multi):
173	
174	> **Make the `XERO_SCOPES` fallback in your shared Xero config match your intent** — an
175	> all-`.read` default will silently hand every customer a read-only token if the
176	> secret is missing, and your first write will 403.
177	
178	**Opaque 500 on callback ← wrong-length `TOKEN_ENC_KEY`** (lovable, Pre-flight §4): see §2 above ("`createCipheriv` throws `Invalid key length` … surfaces as an opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the single-use `code`").
179	
180	**`TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` ← client recreated without state** (javascript, §5 callback, verbatim code comment):
181	
182	> // MUST recreate the client with the SAME state you used to build the consent URL.
183	> // `apiCallback` runs openid-client's state check: because the consent URL carries a
184	> // `state` param, the callback client must also know it, or you get the cryptic
185	> // `TypeError: checks.state argument is missing` and every connect fails.
186	
187	**401 `No connection found for replid ...` ← Repl not bound to the connector** (replit, setup checklist):
188	
189	> **Until the Repl is bound,
190	> every live call fails with `401` and a body like
191	> `No connection found for replid ... with connector: xero`.** If you see that
192	> 401, the code is probably fine — the binding is missing.
193	
194	**404 ← friendly alias paths** (replit, CRITICAL: path mapping):
195	
196	> The connector blueprint advertises friendly aliases like `/accounting/contacts` and
197	> `/accounting/invoices` — **these are wrong and return 404.** Use Xero's real
198	> REST paths with PascalCase resource names.
199	
200	> Verified empirically: `/connections` → 200, `/accounting/organisations` → 404,
201	> `/api.xro/2.0/Organisations` → 200.
202	
203	**Blank white page ← Xero consent framed in an iframe** (lovable + javascript):
204	
205	> Xero's login refuses to be framed (`frame-ancestors`), so a same-frame
206	> redirect from the Lovable preview iframe shows a **blank white page** that
207	> looks like the button is broken.
208	
209	**Access token dies in ~30 min, no recovery ← `offline_access` omitted** (all four):
210	
211	> **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it and you get an access token
212	> that dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent.
213	
214	**400 ← same idempotency key, different request** (all four, Idempotency "Rules that bite"):
215	
216	> **Same key + a *different* request → `400` "used with a different request".** A
217	> request differs if the URL, body, or method changes. Persist the key alongside the
218	> record and reuse the *identical* request when retrying.
219	
220	**Cached error ← retrying an errored keyed request** (all four):
221	
222	> **Errors are cached too.** If the keyed request errored internally, retrying with the
223	> *same* key returns the *same cached error* even after the underlying problem is
224	> fixed. To recover, **GET to check whether the resource was actually created**; if
225	> not, create again with a **new** key.
226	
227	**429 ← rate limits, with the recovery** (all four):
228	
229	> **Rate limits (per tenant):** ~60 calls/minute, 5,000/day, plus an app-wide
230	> per-minute cap and ~5 concurrent requests. On **429** read the `Retry-After` header
231	> and back off — multi-tenant apps hit this fast when looping over orgs. Spread/queue
232	> calls per tenant.
233	
234	**Silent wrong-books write ← missing/wrong tenant header** (all four):
235	
236	> **Always send `Xero-Tenant-Id`** on every API call. There is no implicit "current
237	> org." With multiple connected orgs, the wrong id silently writes to the wrong
238	> company's books.
239	
240	**Next refresh fails ← rotated refresh token not persisted** (all four):
241	
242	> **Token lifetimes:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days and **rotates on
243	> every refresh**. You MUST persist the new refresh token each time or the next
244	> refresh fails. A refresh token also dies if unused for 60 days.
245	
246	**Sibling orgs flip to needs_reconnect ← only one row updated after rotation** (javascript §6, code comment, verbatim):
247	
248	> // CRITICAL multi-tenant trap: when one consent authorized several orgs, they all
249	> // share ONE token set. Refreshing rotates the refresh token and INVALIDATES the
250	> // old one, so updating only THIS row leaves every sibling org holding a dead token
251	> // (they'll wrongly flip to needs_reconnect on their next call). Update ALL rows
252	> // that shared the previous token set — e.g. match on the old ciphertext:
253	
254	**Duplicate contacts ← posting invoices with a contact name** (all four):
255	
256	> **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated by email.** Posting an invoice with a new
257	> contact name creates a NEW contact every time. Upsert yourself: look up by email (or
258	> `ContactID`) via `getContacts` and reuse the id; otherwise you litter the org with
259	> duplicates.
260	
261	The one true error-shaped *table* in the corpus is the Replit **path mapping table** (Purpose | Path) plus its "verified empirically" line — the pattern being: verified request → expected status, wrong path → 404.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 4. Scope minimalism rules
266	
267	Canonical statement, repeated near-verbatim in all four skills:
268	
269	> Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum needed and re-run the OAuth consent
270	> flow to add more later. You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; the user
271	> must re-consent. To get a refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
272	
273	Supporting rules:
274	
275	- "**Always prefer the granular scopes** — use `accounting.invoices`, NOT `accounting.transactions`." (Broad scopes deprecated: granular assigned to all Web/PKCE apps since March 2026, custom connections since 29 April 2026, broad dead September 2027.)
276	- "**Read vs write — choose deliberately; you can't change it later without re-consent.** `.read` scopes are GET-only."
277	- Lovable adds an agent-behavior rule: "Do not ask the user what scopes to use - set the appropriate scopes for the integration to function."
278	- Replit: "**Scopes** — see the dedicated section below. Request the minimum set for what you're doing".
279	
280	Exact scope strings quoted in the docs:
281	
282	- Read-only dashboard (lovable single):
283	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`
284	- Write / invoicing app ("A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants", all four):
285	  `openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`
286	- Typical invoice flow (replit):
287	  `offline_access openid email profile accounting.settings.read accounting.invoices accounting.contacts`
288	
289	Scope reference tables they maintain (structure worth copying): (1) identity scopes `offline_access` / `openid` / `profile` / `email`; (2) "Deprecated (broad) → New granular scopes" migration table (`accounting.transactions` → `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals`; same for `.read`; `accounting.reports.read` → eight `accounting.reports.*.read` scopes); (3) "Scope | Grants" table mapping each granular scope to the resources it unlocks (`accounting.invoices` → Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items; `accounting.payments` → Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments); (4) "never broad, use as-is" table (`accounting.settings`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.journals.read`, `accounting.budgets.read`). Always footed with the developer.xero.com scopes URL and "re-check periodically — dates and assignments change".
290	
291	---
292	
293	## 5. Token handling rules
294	
295	- **Lifetimes, stated with numbers every time:** access token ~30 min; refresh token ~60 days, **rotates on every refresh**, dies if unused for 60 days. Expiry stored as `expires_at` computed from `expires_in` seconds: `new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()` (JS) / `token["expires_at"]` epoch-seconds dict key (Python).
296	- **Refresh early, not at expiry:** JS refreshes when `expires_at <= now + 60_000` (60-second buffer); Python "Proactively refresh if within ~2 min of expiry" (`expires_at - time.time() < 120`).
297	- **Persist rotation immediately or break:** "tokenSet = await res.json();   // refresh_token ROTATES — must persist". And update **all sibling rows** sharing the old ciphertext (multi-org consents share one token set) — match siblings on the OLD ciphertext.
298	- **No long-lived clients / no caching:** "Make a fresh client per request and `setTokenSet(...)` from the DB. Do not keep a long-lived client — tokens expire and clients are per-user." Replit: "Never cache this — create a fresh instance per request (tokens rotate)."
299	- **Refresh failure is a state, not a retry loop:** "On a failed refresh, **mark the connection broken and prompt re-connect** — do not silently retry forever." Status enum in the schema: `'active' | 'needs_reconnect'`. Surface it in UI with a reconnect CTA; a 60-day-idle token dies silently, so add a scheduled keep-alive refresh, not just reactive detection.
300	- **Storage:** tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM, key from `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` in the secret store, exactly 32 bytes base64); rotating token lives in the **database (a mutable store), never in a secret**; "never put per-user tokens in env".
301	- **Never-log / never-expose:** log `client_id_len`, not the value ("without ever printing the secret value itself"); "**Never expose tokens to the browser** or any client-readable storage"; "Add `.env` (and any local token dumps) to `.gitignore`; never commit credentials"; status endpoints return "only non-sensitive metadata (never the token)".
302	- **`state` discipline:** random, persisted server-side with a 10-minute expiry, verified on callback, deleted after use — "single-use, replay-proof".
303	
304	---
305	
306	## 6. Tone and format
307	
308	- **Imperative second person, no hedging.** "Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a human." "Stop and use X instead." "If you reach for `xero-node` or start writing token-refresh code, stop — you are on the wrong path."
309	- **Warnings are structural, not decorative.** The single warning device is `⚠️` in a heading (`## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST`, `> ### ⚠️ Three traps...`, `> ### ⚠️ Open consent in a NEW TAB, opened SYNCHRONOUSLY in the click handler`) plus a blockquote body. Bold and CAPS carry emphasis inline: MUST, NOT, NEVER, ONE, THEIR OWN, FIRST, "byte-for-byte", "byte-match".
310	- **Every rule ships its consequence.** Not "include offline_access" but "Omit it and the access token dies in ~30 min with no way to refresh — the user must re-consent." Failure narratives are causal chains told to the end (wrong key → throw deep inside encrypt → opaque 500 → single-use code consumed → user must re-consent → no breadcrumb in logs).
311	- **Named recurring rubrics:** "Rules that bite", "(these bite in production)", "Local-dev gotchas (these bite during testing)", "Gotcha that fools people", "(do not skip)", "(skipping it costs hours)", "CRITICAL multi-tenant trap".
312	- **Tables vs prose:** tables only for enumerable reference data (scopes, scope grants, deprecation mapping, REST paths). Everything judgment-shaped (gotchas, security, idempotency rules) is bold-lead-in bullets: `**Rule.** Consequence and recovery.` Checklists (Decision check, Security checklist, Verify) are lists, with Verify numbered.
313	- **Code is placed, minimal, and commented with intent** — comments mark traps ("// sync!", "// refresh_token ROTATES — must persist", "// REQUIRED — wrong id writes to wrong books"), not narration. File-path comment on line 1 of each block.
314	- **Distrust of upstream errors is a theme:** fail fast locally because "Xero's errors past this point are far less useful than the URL you can read right now."
315	- **Empirical claims are labeled:** "Verified empirically", "**The paths below are verified — trust them, do not re-probe.**", and for unknowns: "probe it **read-only first** rather than guessing".
316	- Punctuation habits: heavy em-dash usage, `→` arrows for flows and mappings (`DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`; "Same key + different request → `400`"), backticked literals for every identifier, header, error string, and scope.
317	
318	---
319	
320	## 7. Platform-specific guidance — do NOT copy into a NestJS project
321	
322	Everything below is Lovable/Supabase/Replit plumbing, not Xero discipline. In NestJS: config assertion at boot (ConfigModule + named errors), one Xero module (`api/src/modules/xero/`), Postgres via your ORM, guards instead of RLS.
323	
324	**Lovable / TanStack Start / Cloudflare Workers:**
325	- `createServerFn` server functions; server routes under `src/routes/api/public/*` to "bypass Lovable's published-site auth gate". NestJS controllers are just public or guarded routes.
326	- "Do not use Supabase Edge Functions"; the legacy-port note (Deno Edge Functions, `Deno.env.get`, redeploy after secret change, `verify_jwt = false`, Web Crypto two-part `iv.ciphertext` blob) — all Deno/Supabase-specific.
327	- "Read `process.env.*` **inside** `.handler()`, not at module scope" (Worker per-request env injection). NestJS reads env once at boot — the opposite discipline.
328	- The whole `resolveRedirectUri` host-mapping resolver, `PROJECT_ID`, `id-preview--*.lovable.app` / `project--*.lovable.app` hosts, `*.lovableproject.com` iframe host, `getRequest()` returning `http://localhost:8080`. A NestJS app has one configured redirect URI per environment.
329	- Iframe/popup dance (`window.open("about:blank", "_blank")` synchronously before `await`) — Lovable editor-preview-specific; keep only the generic fact that Xero consent can't be framed.
330	- "Never import `@/integrations/supabase/client.server` at module scope of a `*.functions.ts` or route file" — client-bundle stripping concern, no NestJS equivalent.
331	- Lovable Cloud secrets / `generate_secret` with `length: 44` — keep the underlying rule (base64 of 32 random bytes), drop the tool.
332	
333	**Supabase data layer:**
334	- `supabaseAdmin`, `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, "Service-role key never goes to the browser".
335	- RLS incantations: `grant all ... to service_role`, `enable row level security`, "INTENTIONALLY no anon/authenticated policies", `auth.users(id)` FKs, `requireSupabaseAuth` middleware, `has_role(userId, 'admin')`.
336	- Supabase client returning Postgres `numeric` as strings.
337	- The unauthenticated-prototype "escape hatch" (warning banner, accepted-risks list) — Lovable demo-flow specific.
338	
339	**Replit connector:**
340	- `@replit/connectors-sdk` proxy pattern, `connectors.proxy("xero", path, ...)`, Repl binding (`proposeIntegration`/`addIntegration`), the `No connection found for replid` 401, redirect URI `https://replit.com/connectors/oauth/callback`, `pnpm --filter @workspace/<server>` install rule, Integrations → Manage reset.
341	
342	**Portable despite appearing in platform sections** (keep these): exact-match redirect URIs registered per environment; state persisted server-side because HTTP steps are separate invocations; callback errors funneled to a user-visible `?error&detail` redirect (never a bare 500, because the OAuth `code` is single-use); `Xero-Tenant-Id` on every call; idempotency keys on writes; `summarizeErrors=false`; `unitdp=4`; the `/Date(ms+offset)/` normaliser; encrypt-at-rest with validated 32-byte key; `needs_reconnect` status + keep-alive refresh; all Xero access isolated in one module.
343
```
</details></tool-use>

77

No `getPurchaseOrders`/`createPurchaseOrder` SDK call anywhere.

83	
84	## 3. Running against a Custom Connection (client_credentials)
85	
86	- Env vars read (src/clients/xero-client.ts:14-16): `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN`. Optional `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated, xero-client.ts:129-131). Loaded via `dotenv.config()` (xero-client.ts:12). Boot assert at xero-client.ts:19-21: throws unless `bearer_token || (client_id && client_secret)`.
87	- Mode decision (xero-client.ts:223-231): `bearer_token ? BearerTokenXeroClient : CustomConnectionsXeroClient` — i.e. `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` takes precedence; otherwise client_credentials with hardcoded `grant_type = "client_credentials"` (xero-client.ts:17).
88	- Command: `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` with env vars (README.md:71-76), or local build `node dist/index.js` (README.md:226-230). Transport is stdio only (src/index.ts:14-15).
89	- Tenant id: NOT an env var. Client-credentials mode fetches the token from `https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` then calls `https://api.xero.com/connections` and takes `connections[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:178-190). Bearer mode calls `updateTenants()` and takes `tenants[0].tenantId` (xero-client.ts:35-41, 219). Multi-org: always the first tenant, no selection mechanism.
90	- Default scopes if `XERO_SCOPES` unset: tries legacy V1 scope set first (`accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.settings accounting.reports.read payroll.*`, xero-client.ts:81-89), and on `invalid_scope` (HTTP 400) falls back to granular V2 set (`accounting.invoices accounting.payments ...`, xero-client.ts:92-106, retry logic 137-155).
91	
92	## 4. `create-payment`
93	
94	- Schema (src/tools/create/create-payment.tool.ts:18-34): required `invoiceId: string`, `accountId: string`, `amount: number` (`.positive()`); optional `date: string` (YYYY-MM-DD), `reference: string`.
95	- Handler (src/handlers/create-xero-payment.handler.ts:15-45): builds `Payment { invoice: {invoiceID}, account: {accountID}, amount, date: date || today, reference }` and calls `xeroClient.accountingApi.createPayment(tenantId, payment, undefined /* idempotencyKey */, getClientHeaders())` (line 36-41). **idempotencyKey is passed as `undefined`** — no idempotency.
96	- Constraints: only in the description text (tool.ts:8-13): invoice must be AUTHORISED and not fully paid, amount must not exceed amount due — Xero enforces these, not this code. Success text is mislabeled "Invoice created successfully" (tool.ts, response block).
97	
98	## 5. `update-invoice` — cannot change status
99	
100	- Schema (src/tools/update/update-invoice.tool.ts:36-50): required `invoiceId`; optional `lineItems[]` (description, quantity, unitAmount, accountCode, taxType, itemCode?, tracking?), `reference`, `dueDate`, `date`, `contactId`. **There is no `status` parameter.**
101	- Handler hard-blocks non-draft invoices (src/handlers/update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84):
102	  ```ts
103	  // Only allow updates to DRAFT invoices
104	  if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) {
105	    return { result: null, isError: true,
106	      error: `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ${invoiceStatus}`, };
107	  }
108	  ```
109	  The `Invoice` object it sends (handler.ts:39-45) contains only lineItems/reference/dueDate/date/contact — no status field. **Conclusion: the MCP server cannot move an invoice to AUTHORISED (or any status).** Approving a draft requires the raw Accounting API (POST Invoices with `"Status": "AUTHORISED"`).
110	- Warning in description: all line items must be re-supplied; omitted line items are removed (tool.ts:31-32).
111	
112	## 6. `list-invoices`
113	
114	- Schema (src/tools/list/list-invoices.tool.ts:16-22): `page: number` (required), `contactIds: string[]` optional, `invoiceNumbers: string[]` optional (providing invoiceNumbers also returns line items, tool.ts:59, 96-98).
115	- Handler SDK call (src/handlers/list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:15-31): `getInvoices(tenantId, ...)` with `where: undefined`, `order: "UpdatedDateUTC DESC"`, `invoiceNumbers`, `contactIDs`, **`statuses: undefined`** (line 22 — no status filter exposed), `page`, `includeArchived: false`, `summaryOnly: false`, **`pageSize: 10` hardcoded** (line 28), `searchTerm: undefined`. No date, no status, no where-clause filtering available through the tool.
116	
117	## 7. `list-aged-payables-by-contact`
118	
119	- Schema (src/tools/list/list-aged-payables-by-contact.tool.ts:10-18): required `contactId: string`; optional `reportDate` (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to end of current month), `invoicesFromDate`, `invoicesToDate`.
120	- Handler (src/handlers/list-aged-payables-by-contact.handler.ts:15-22): `accountingApi.getReportAgedPayablesByContact(tenantId, contactId, reportDate, invoicesFromDate, invoicesToDate, getClientHeaders())`. Returns report rows as raw JSON text.
121	
122	## 8. Rate limiting: NONE
123	
124	- `grep -rni "429|retry|rate" src/` — no retry, no backoff, no throttling, no rate-limit-remaining header reads anywhere in src/.
125	- The only 429 handling is a cosmetic error message: src/helpers/format-error.ts:35-36 maps HTTP 429 to `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."`. Callers must implement their own backoff/batching.
126	
127	## 9. SDK and token lifecycle
128	
129	- SDK: `xero-node` ^13.3.0 plus `axios` for the token/connections calls, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.23.4, `zod` 3.25 (package.json:27-31). `XeroClient` from xero-node is subclassed as `MCPXeroClient` (xero-client.ts:23).
130	- Token is NOT cached: every handler starts with `await xeroClient.authenticate()` (e.g. create-xero-payment.handler.ts:23, list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:13). In client-credentials mode `authenticate()` (xero-client.ts:195-203) requests a brand-new token from `identity.xero.com/connect/token` plus a `GET /connections` on every single tool call — 2 extra HTTP round-trips per call, no expiry check, no reuse. Bearer mode just re-sets the static token and re-fetches tenants (xero-client.ts:214-220).
131	- All SDK calls send header `user-agent: xero-mcp-server-<version>` via `getClientHeaders()` (src/helpers/get-client-headers.ts:3-9).
132	- Writes are non-idempotent: `idempotencyKey` is explicitly `undefined` in createPayment (create-xero-payment.handler.ts:39) and updateInvoice (update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:54).
133
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md`

```text
1	# Fact sheet: Xero prompt library — accounts-payable-symfony.txt
2	
3	Source: `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/php/accounts-payable-symfony.txt` (16 lines).
4	Cross-checked: `.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/php/invoicing-laravel.txt` (16 lines).
5	
6	Important caveat up front: both files are short app-idea prompts, not API documentation. They contain almost no field-level or status-level Xero detail. What little is load-bearing is quoted below; everything else is app-design fluff and is flagged as such.
7	
8	## 1. AP entities and Xero endpoints
9	
10	Verbatim, the only API-fact line in the Symfony prompt:
11	
12	> `Key Endpoints: /Invoices (bills), /PurchaseOrders, /Payments, /Contacts`
13	
14	Load-bearing facts extractable from that line:
15	
16	- Bills live on the **`/Invoices`** endpoint. The "(bills)" annotation is Xero's own confirmation that there is no separate `/Bills` endpoint; bills are Invoices with `Type: ACCPAY` (the ACCPAY type itself is NOT stated in the prompt, only implied by "(bills)").
17	- **`/PurchaseOrders`** is a first-class endpoint and is the anchor for "three-way matching against purchase orders and receipts" (verbatim phrase from the prompt body).
18	- **`/Payments`** and **`/Contacts`** round out the AP surface.
19	- NOT mentioned anywhere: `/Attachments`, `/BatchPayments`, `/CreditNotes`, field names, or any request/response shapes. Zero field names appear in either file.
20	
21	The Laravel invoicing prompt's endpoint line (AR side, for contrast):
22	
23	> `Key Endpoints: /Invoices, /Contacts, /Payments, /OnlineInvoices`
24	
25	- Adds **`/OnlineInvoices`** (the customer-facing pay-online URL for an invoice). AR-only; no AP equivalent listed.
26	
27	Auth (both files, verbatim fragments): SDK is **`xero-php-oauth2`**; Symfony: "OAuth 2.0 with league/oauth2-client"; Laravel: "OAuth 2.0 with Laravel Socialite provider". OAuth 2.0 is the only auth model mentioned.
28	
29	## 2. Bill status flow and approval workflow
30	
31	- **No Xero statuses are named.** DRAFT / SUBMITTED / AUTHORISED / PAID appear nowhere in either file. Do not cite this prompt as a source for the status lifecycle.
32	- Approval hint (verbatim): "Add approval workflows with email notifications, automatic payment scheduling based on terms, and early payment discount optimization."
33	  - Reading: approval is expected to be an app-side workflow layered on top of Xero, not something the prompt claims Xero provides. Payment timing is driven by supplier terms. Fluff-to-fact ratio high here; the only durable domain fact is that AP approval + scheduling-by-terms + early-payment-discount capture are the canonical feature set Xero itself markets for AP automation.
34	- Bill creation path hint (verbatim): "Implement OCR processing for scanned invoices using Tesseract, extracting line items and automatically creating bills in Xero." Fact: bills are created with line items (line-item granularity is the expected write shape). No field names given.
35	
36	## 3. Payment creation details
37	
38	- Nothing field-level. No account requirements, no `Account.Code`, no `Invoice.InvoiceID`, no amount/date fields mentioned.
39	- One useful architectural fact (verbatim): "integration with banking APIs for payment execution" — i.e. the prompt treats Xero `/Payments` as the **record** of payment while actual money movement happens through external banking APIs. Xero is the ledger, not the payment rail.
40	- Laravel side mirrors this for AR: customers "make payments via Stripe" and the app presumably records them against `/Payments`; also "implement database transactions for payment processing" (app-side advice, not a Xero fact).
41	
42	## 4. Filters, pagination, webhooks, rate limits
43	
44	- Symfony prompt: **none**. No `where=` examples, no pagination, no webhooks, no rate-limit guidance.
45	- Laravel prompt, verbatim: "Include webhook endpoints for Xero invoice updates and implement two-way sync to handle invoice edits from either system."
46	  - Fact: Xero webhooks fire on invoice updates and are the sanctioned mechanism for two-way sync (edits can originate in either system, so consumers must reconcile, not assume ownership).
47	- No rate-limit numbers in either file (our 60/min figure comes from elsewhere, not these prompts).
48	
49	## 5. Seed / demo-data hints
50	
51	- **None concrete.** No supplier names, no amounts, no currencies, no example invoice numbers in either file.
52	- Realistic-shape hints (all from the Symfony prompt body, useful for making seed data look like what Xero expects an AP org to contain):
53	  - Bills arrive via a "vendor portal for invoice submission" and via "scanned invoices" (OCR) — so seed bills should include both clean structured ones and messy near-duplicate ones.
54	  - "three-way matching against purchase orders and receipts" — seed data needs POs that correlate with bills (same supplier, overlapping line items, plausible quantity/price drift) plus receipt records.
55	  - "duplicate invoice detection using fuzzy matching" — seed at least one near-duplicate pair (same supplier, similar amount/reference) to exercise this.
56	  - "early payment discount optimization" and "payment scheduling based on terms" — give suppliers differing payment terms and at least one discount-if-early term.
57	  - "comprehensive audit trails and SOX compliance reporting" — every mutation should leave an audit row (aligns with our AuditEvent rule).
58	- Laravel prompt shape hints (AR, less relevant): line items "pulled from timesheet entries", recurring invoices, late-payment reminders.
59	
60	## Fluff register (do not treat as API facts)
61	
62	- Tech stack lines (Symfony 6, PHP 8.1+, PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Tesseract; Laravel 9+, MySQL, Redis, Stripe, DOMPDF) — stack suggestions only.
63	- "Use Symfony Messenger for async processing, implement Doctrine event listeners for audit logs" / "Use Laravel queues for PDF generation" — framework plumbing advice.
64	- OCR-with-Tesseract, spend-analytics dashboards, multi-language templates, DOMPDF — feature wishlist, no Xero content.
65	
66	## Net takeaways for our build
67	
68	1. Bills = `/Invoices` with the bills annotation; PO matching via `/PurchaseOrders`; payments recorded via `/Payments`; suppliers via `/Contacts`. That endpoint quartet is Xero's own definition of the AP surface.
69	2. Xero records payments; execution happens outside (banking API / rail). Matches our "Xero write = idempotent ledger entry" design.
70	3. Webhooks on invoice updates are the intended sync primitive (from the Laravel file).
71	4. Approval workflow, three-way match, duplicate detection, discount capture are app responsibilities — Xero gives no statuses or fields for them in this prompt, which validates our external match-engine architecture.
72	5. This source is too thin for field names, status flows, filters, pagination, or rate limits — pull those from the API reference or the MCP server code instead.
73
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Read match rules and gates in architecture.md

`sed -n 30,95p /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/context/architecture.md`

```text
                                        ▼
                        Next.js dashboard: bill list, match evidence
                        drill-down (the case file), audit trail
```

## Match engine (the deterministic core — no LLM inside)

Entities: `Bill`, `PurchaseOrder`, `GoodsReceipt`, `MatchResult`, `AuditEvent`, `ExceptionAction`.

Match logic, in order:
1. **PO link:** supplier fuzzy-normalised (LLM normalises names at ingest, engine compares normalised strings) + amount within tolerance (default ±2% or £5, whichever greater) + PO date ≤ invoice date. Line-level qty×price check when lines exist on both.
2. **Receipt link:** goods receipt referencing the PO (sheet row or parsed delivery note) with received qty ≥ invoiced qty per line, or total-level if lines absent.
3. **Verdict:** `MATCHED` → auto-path. `MISSING_PO` / `MISSING_RECEIPT` / `AMOUNT_MISMATCH` / `QTY_MISMATCH` → exception path with the gap named and the evidence found so far attached.

Every verdict writes an `AuditEvent` (input refs, rule hits, tolerances applied, actor=agent|human, timestamp). The audit trail IS the 20% architecture story — surface it in the UI.

## Xero API usage map (the 30% story — name these in the pitch)

| Call | Via | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |

Rate limits: 60 calls/min/tenant, 5,000/day — batch reads, cache org data in Postgres, never poll Xero in a loop.

## API contract (frontend ↔ backend)

NestJS decorators → Swagger at `/api/docs` → `openapi.json` committed → frontend runs `openapi-typescript` to generate `web/lib/api-types.ts`. The generated file is the ONLY source of request/response types in the frontend. Contract change = regenerate + commit together. No hand-typed fetch shapes, ever.

## Repo layout

```
encode-xero-hack/
  context/            # this suite — both agents read before writing
  api/                # NestJS (Claude Code owns)
    src/modules/{ingest,match,xero,whatsapp,audit}/
    openapi.json      # committed contract
  web/                # Next.js (Cursor owns)
    lib/api-types.ts  # generated, never hand-edited
  seed/               # demo-org seeding scripts + sheet fixtures
  .claude/skills/     # Xero skills generated via context/prompt.md
```

## Build order (gates, not suggestions)

| Gate | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| G0 | Custom Connection auth proven: one script lists invoices from the demo org. Skills generated (prompt.md). Demo org seeded (~15 bills, POs in Xero, receipts sheet, 2 deliberate gaps). |
| G1 | Match engine passing unit tests against seeded data: greens green, ambers amber, correct gap names. **Highest-risk-first: this is the project.** |
| G2 | Xero write path: approve + create Payment + attach evidence + history note, verified inside Xero UI. |
| G3 | Email ingest live (Make webhook + IMAP fallback), Claude parse → engine → verdict end to end. |
| G4 | Dashboard: bill list + evidence drill-down (the case file view) + audit trail. Contract-generated types only. |
| G5 | WhatsApp exception loop (Twilio sandbox): buzz, tap/reply, chase email out, reply-photo closes match. LAST. |
| G6 | Three full live rehearsals. Pitch ×2 out loud. Hotspot configured. |

Behind at any gate: cut from the bottom. WhatsApp buttons → numbered replies → if desperate, exception actions from the dashboard instead of WhatsApp. The match engine, Xero writes, and evidence UI are untouchable.

## Live-demo rules

1. Live data only. The demo mailbox, sheet, and Xero org are real; the arriving email and the delivery-note photo are sent live on stage.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Test ts-node on trivial file and with explicit compiler options

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check xero-auth skill for project scopes and Organisations endpoint

`grep -n -i "scope\|organisation" /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md | head -40`

```text
3:description: Xero Custom Connection (client_credentials) auth for THIS repo — exact token endpoint, the four project scopes and what each unlocks, in-memory token caching with proactive expiry and retry-on-401, why there is no XERO_TENANT_ID, and the Gate G0 verify script. Use when writing or debugging any code that obtains or uses a Xero access token, when a Xero call returns 401/403/unauthorized_client/invalid_client/invalid_scope, or before the first Xero call in a fresh environment. Read this BEFORE writing token code; there is exactly ONE token path in this repo (api/src/modules/xero/) — do not hand-roll a second.
8:This project uses a **Custom Connection** app: client_credentials grant, bound to a **single Xero organisation**. No consent screen, no redirect URI, no refresh token, no tenant juggling. If you find yourself writing OAuth callback or PKCE code, stop — wrong flow.
13:2. **Confirm the app is a Custom Connection and has been authorised.** App creation includes selecting scopes and "who will authorise the connection" — an org admin must complete that authorisation before tokens are useful. (Exact failure mode of an unauthorised app: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. `verify-auth.ts` treats "token OK but zero connections" as this case.) Custom Connections only exist for AU/NZ/UK/US orgs and are free against the Demo Company.
14:3. **Portal scopes must cover what the token request asks for.** Scopes are configured at BOTH ends: selected on the app in the developer portal, AND sent space-separated in the token request body (unlike code/PKCE, where scopes ride the URL).
30:| Body | `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<url-encoded space-separated scopes>` |
31:| Response fields | `access_token` (a JWT), `expires_in` (seconds), `token_type: Bearer`, `scope` |
46:  body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scopes)}`,
50:## Scopes: exactly four, and why nothing more
54:| Scope | Unlocks | Why this project needs it |
56:| `accounting.transactions` | Broad scope = union of `accounting.invoices` (Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, **PurchaseOrders**, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items), `accounting.payments` (Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments), `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` | Bills (ACCPAY invoices), Purchase Orders, and payments are the match engine's subjects — read and write |
59:| `accounting.reports.read` | Broad scope = the eight `accounting.reports.*.read` reports (aged, balancesheet, banksummary, budgetsummary, executivesummary, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports) | Aged payables and financial context reads — read-only on purpose |
63:- `openid profile email` — identity scopes for a human user; client_credentials has no user.
65:- `accounting.settings[.read]` — grants Accounts, Organisation, TaxRates, etc. We don't read org configuration. Consequence: `GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisation` **403s** under our scopes; the org name comes from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` (`tenantName`) instead, which is what `verify-auth.ts` does.
66:- Payroll, budgets, journals — out of scope for this product.
68:### Broad-scope migration (dates matter)
70:From 29 April 2026 all custom connections have access to granular scopes; new custom connections lose `accounting.journals.read`; existing custom connections can keep using broad scopes until **September 2027**. Whether a custom connection created today can still request the broad `accounting.transactions` / `accounting.reports.read`: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Handle it empirically, the way Xero's own MCP server does: request broad, and on HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` retry with the granular equivalents. `verify-auth.ts` implements exactly this fallback and warns loudly when it triggers. Granular mapping for our set:
78:Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
82:Custom Connections "access data from a single Xero organisation" — one org, period (multi-org needs code/PKCE flow). The documented custom-connection API call sends **exactly two headers**:
102:- On **403**: never retry — that is a missing scope; the same token can never succeed.
115:      scope: "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read",
139:| `scripts/verify-auth.ts` | **Gate G0.** Parses repo-root `.env` itself (no dotenv), asserts env with named errors, fetches a token (with granular-scope fallback), GETs the org name via `/connections` and 1 invoice, prints org + invoice count + rate-limit headers. Run: `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts` from repo root. |
146:- **403 vs 401 are different diseases.** `401` = the token itself is bad or expired (>30 min) → new token, retry once. `403` = the token is fine but lacks a scope → fix the scope list at BOTH ends (portal + token request), get a NEW token. Retrying a 403 with the same token can never succeed.
149:- **Broad scopes can 400 `invalid_scope` on newer apps.** Post-April-2026 granular migration — retry with the granular set (table above). `verify-auth.ts` does this automatically.
150:- **`GET /Organisation` 403s under our scopes.** It needs `accounting.settings[.read]`, which we deliberately don't request. Get the org name from `GET /connections` (`tenantName`).
151:- **Scope cannot be widened on an existing token.** Cheap fix here (unlike code flow): request a new token with the wider scope — provided the portal app has that scope enabled.
163:| Token 400 `invalid_scope` | Broad scope on a granular-only app, or a scope not enabled on the app in the portal | Retry with granular equivalents; enable the scope in the portal |
165:| API call 403 | Token lacks the scope for that resource (e.g. `/Organisation` without `accounting.settings`) | Add the scope at both ends, request a NEW token; never retry with the same token |
174:- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — token endpoint, Basic auth, form-encoded body, response fields, ~30 min lifetime (Token types page), single-org quote, AU/NZ/UK/US restriction, scopes-at-both-ends, granular migration dates (29 Apr 2026 / Sep 2027), two-header call example with no xero-tenant-id (explicit "not required" sentence absent = UNVERIFIED), "tenanted endpoints only" wrinkle, rate limits + X-*Limit-Remaining/X-Rate-Limit-Problem/Retry-After headers, pagination (page/pageSize min 1, pagination.itemCount). Underlying URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,client-credentials,token-types,limits}/, /documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses.
175:- .tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:127-203 — exact token request shape, token-per-call anti-pattern, invalid_scope granular fallback, GET /connections → connections[0].tenantId, boot env assertion.
177:- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md:116-140 — unauthorized_client ← empty client_id causal chain, requireEnv/client_id_len discipline, scope-grants tables (accounting.invoices → PurchaseOrders etc., accounting.settings → Organisation), broad→granular migration table, 403 = missing scope, scope minimalism rules.
179:- UNVERIFIED items marked inline: explicit tenant-header-not-required sentence; unauthorised-app exact failure mode (and whether empty /connections has other causes); whether new post-Apr-2026 custom connections can still request broad scopes.
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Read full scope-grants table from prompt library

`sed -n 116,140p /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md`

```text
| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` |
| `accounting.transactions.read` | `accounting.invoices.read`, `accounting.payments.read`, `accounting.banktransactions.read`, `accounting.manualjournals.read` |
| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read`, `accounting.reports.balancesheet.read`, `accounting.reports.banksummary.read`, `accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read`, `accounting.reports.executivesummary.read`, `accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`, `accounting.reports.trialbalance.read`, `accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |

What each granular scope covers (read variants are the same but GET-only):

| Scope | Grants |
| --- | --- |
| `accounting.invoices` | Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items |
| `accounting.payments` | Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments |
| `accounting.banktransactions` | BankTransactions, BankTransfers |
| `accounting.manualjournals` | ManualJournals |
| `accounting.reports.*.read` | the matching report only (balancesheet, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports = GSTReport/BASReport, etc.) |

### Accounting scopes that were never broad (use as-is)

| Scope | Grants |
| --- | --- |
| `accounting.settings` / `accounting.settings.read` | Accounts, BrandingThemes, Currencies, Items, InvoiceReminders, Organisation, TaxRates, TrackingCategories, Users |
| `accounting.contacts` / `accounting.contacts.read` | Contacts, ContactGroups |
| `accounting.attachments` / `accounting.attachments.read` | attachments across most resources |
| `accounting.journals.read` | Journals (general ledger) |
| `accounting.budgets.read` | Budgets |

A typical invoicing SaaS therefore wants:
```
</details></tool-use>

}

async function createInvoice(
  contactId: string,
  lineItems: InvoiceLineItem[],
  type: Invoice.TypeEnum,
  reference: string | undefined,
  date: string | undefined,
): Promise<Invoice | undefined> {
  await xeroClient.authenticate();

  const invoice: Invoice = {
    type: type,
    contact: {
      contactID: contactId,
    },
    lineItems: lineItems,
    date: date || new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0], // Use provided date or today's date
    dueDate: new Date(Date.now() + 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
      .toISOString()
      .split("T")[0], // 30 days from now
    ...(type === Invoice.TypeEnum.ACCPAY
      ? { invoiceNumber: reference }
      : { reference: reference }),
    status: Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT,
  };

  const response = await xeroClient.accountingApi.createInvoices(
    xeroClient.tenantId,
    {
      invoices: [invoice],
    }, // invoices
    true, //summarizeErrors
    undefined, //unitdp
    undefined, //idempotencyKey
    getClientHeaders(),
  );
  const createdInvoice = response.body.invoices?.[0];
  return createdInvoice;
}

/**
 * Create a new invoice in Xero
 */
export async function createXeroInvoice(
  contactId: string,
  lineItems: InvoiceLineItem[],
  type: Invoice.TypeEnum = Invoice.TypeEnum.ACCREC,
  reference?: string,
  date?: string,
): Promise<XeroClientResponse<Invoice>> {
  try {
    const createdInvoice = await createInvoice(
      contactId,
      lineItems,
      type,
      reference,
      date,
    );

14	      "docs-auth": "WROTE undefined/docs-auth.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md)\n\n- Custom Connections: POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token, grant_type=client_credentials, scopes set at app creation AND passed space-separated in token body; token = JWT, \"valid for up to 30 minutes\" (token-types page); bound to \"a single Xero organisation\" (quoted).\n- xero-tenant-id: current custom-connections page never states it explicitly; call example omits the header (only Authorization + Accept) — explicit sentence marked UNVERIFIED; standard-token requirement quoted from token-types page.\n- Rate limits: 5 concurrent / 60 per min / 1,000-5,000 per day per tenant; 10,000 per min app-wide; headers X-DayLimit-Remaining, X-MinLimit-Remaining, X-AppMinLimit-Remaining; 429 + X-Rate-Limit-Problem + Retry-After (seconds, fixed window) all quoted.\n- Conventions: page/pageSize pagination (default 100, max 1000, 9 endpoints listed), If-Modified-Since semantics + UpdatedDateUTC caveat, 3 exact where examples incl. Type==\"BANK\" and DateTime(2015, 01, 01), order + DESC, summarizeErrors=false, Elements[].ValidationErrors 400 envelope quoted from Response Codes page.\n- Idempotency: Idempotency-Key header supported on POST/PUT/PATCH, 6-min key cache, 128-char max, per-app scope, errors cached, counts against rate limits (official Idempotent Requests guide).\n- Method note: all pages are JS-rendered; WebFetch/curl got empty shells, so content was extracted from Chrome-rendered DOM of official developer.xero.com pages. No 404s.",
15	      "docs-accounting": "WROTE undefined/docs-accounting.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md)\n\n- All 4 official pages verified (WebFetch timed out; rendered via Chrome directly from developer.xero.com URLs).\n- Invoices: create needs Type+Contact(ContactID)+LineItems; POST=create/update, PUT=create-only; legal transitions DRAFT<->SUBMITTED->AUTHORISED->VOIDED only, DELETED only from DRAFT/SUBMITTED; PAID is system-set; locked-period bills not updatable.\n- ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique (shows as Reference in UI) — matters for idempotency.\n- Filtering: where=Type==\"ACCPAY\" AND Status==\"AUTHORISED\" is an official optimised example; Statuses/IDs/ContactIDs list params; page=100 default, invoice max pageSize UNVERIFIED (PO/Payments pages state up to 1000).\n- LineAmountTypes verbatim: Exclusive / Inclusive / NoTax; default Exclusive.\n- POs: statuses DRAFT/SUBMITTED/AUTHORISED/BILLED/DELETED; status changed via POST with PurchaseOrderID; mark-as-billed transition rules UNVERIFIED (no transition table published); no `where` param listed for GET.\n- Payments: PUT with Invoice.InvoiceID + Account (Code or AccountID) + Date + Amount; account must be BANK type or payments-enabled; Amount <= outstanding (partials OK, PAID auto when settled); invoice must be approved (AUTHORISED); reverse via POST /Payments/{id} {\"Status\":\"DELETED\"}; payments immutable otherwise; BankAmount preferred over CurrencyRate for multicurrency.\n- Types page has no CurrencyRate/currency-list section; that and other gaps are tagged UNVERIFIED in the sheet.",
16	      "docs-evidence": "WROTE undefined/docs-evidence.md (/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-evidence.md)\n- WebFetch timed out 2x on all URLs; extracted official markdown from Gatsby page-data JSON of each docs page instead (verbatim source content).\n- Attachments: POST/PUT raw bytes to /api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}, Content-Type = file MIME; 10 attachments/document; size cap CONFLICTS in official docs (Attachments page: 10MB, Contacts page: 25MB) — flagged, assume 10MB.\n- Filename chars rejected as Bad Request: < > : \" / \\ | ? * \\0 +; encode brackets only.\n- IncludeOnline=true on upload; AR invoices and AR credit notes only.\n- Supported entities: Invoices, Receipts, CreditNotes, RepeatingInvoices, BankTransactions, BankTransfers, Contacts, Accounts, ManualJournals, PurchaseOrders, Quotes.\n- History: GET/PUT/POST {Endpoint}/{Guid}/history; PUT body {\"HistoryRecords\":[{\"Details\":...}]}, Details max 2500 chars; cannot update notes, always \"System Generated\" + creation date (no backdating); 14 supported doc types listed.\n- Reports: GET /Reports/AgedPayablesByContact, contactID REQUIRED; optional date/fromDate/toDate; Rows RowType Header/Section/Row/SummaryRow, cells carry invoiceID Attributes.\n- Contacts: Name max 255 (uniqueness being deprecated — use ContactID), optimised where= equals on Name/EmailAddress/AccountNumber, searchTerm searches 6 fields, page/pageSize (default 100), IsSupplier read-only auto-set by AP invoices, summaryOnly EXCLUDES IsSupplier.\n- ContactStatus values ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/GDPRREQUEST confirmed from official Types page."

GET /connections is a non-tenanted endpoint (docs-auth.md:30), so counting it against a per-tenant minute limit is also unsupported. 284	            "problem": "architecture.md defines a line-level qty×price check inside the PO link and lists QTY_MISMATCH as a verdict distinct from AMOUNT_MISMATCH. An engine built faithfully to architecture.md will plausibly name this gap QTY_MISMATCH, turning the G1 assertion red or forcing the engine to be bent to fit the seed — on the demo's second flagship exception.",
285	            "fix": "Make the defect price-shaped: keep qty 25 on the bill and set UnitAmount £79.92 (25 × 79.92 = £1,998.00 net, ×1.2 = £2,397.60 exact 2dp), update the sheet receipt row qty to 25, keeping the £148 delta. Or, if the qty story is wanted, relabel the expected verdict QTY_MISMATCH and update the frontmatter/spec language. Either way, state the AMOUNT_MISMATCH vs QTY_MISMATCH precedence rule explicitly so the G1 tests and engine agree.",
286	            "evidence": "context/architecture.md line 40: 'Line-level qty×price check when lines exist on both.' and line 42: 'Verdict: MATCHED → auto-path. MISSING_PO / MISSING_RECEIPT / AMOUNT_MISMATCH / QTY_MISMATCH → exception path'. SKILL.md line 35 gives PO qty 25, Bill qty 27, identical Unit £74.00."
287	          },
288	          {
289	            "file": "/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/scripts/preflight.ts",
290	            "claim": "Line 77-85: the MissingScope guard asserts the granted scope 'contains' each seed scope via token.scope.includes(s), and SKILL.md line 15 sells this as failing fast instead of a 403 'at the worst moment'.",
291	            "problem": "String.includes does substring matching, so a granted scope of 'accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read' passes both checks ('accounting.contacts.read'.includes('accounting.contacts') is true). A read-only misconfiguration — exactly the failure class this guard exists to catch — false-passes preflight and then 403s on the first contact create mid-seed.",
292	            "fix": "Compare as a set of whole scope tokens: const granted = new Set(token.scope.split(/\\s+/)); if (!granted.has(s)) throw ... — same change in the SKILL.md description of step 2 ('granted scope contains' → 'granted scope lists exactly').",
293	            "evidence": "preflight.ts line 78: 'if (!token.scope.includes(s))'. Refutation is mechanical: 'accounting.contacts.read'.includes('accounting.contacts') === true in any JS runtime. The read-vs-write hazard is documented in .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md lines 160-176 ('Shipping a read-only token and then calling POST ... fails at runtime with a 403')."
294	          },
295	          {
296	            "file": "/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/SKILL.md",
297	            "claim": "Rate budget (line 178): 'First full run, sequential: 1 token + 1 org + 15 contact lookups + 15 contact creates + 1 PO list + 1 PO batch create + 1 bill lookup list + 1 bill batch create ≈ 36 calls — one minute of budget.'",
298	            "problem": "The arithmetic assumes single-call list lookups for bills and POs, but the skill's own idempotency table (line 156) specifies one where-filtered GET per InvoiceNumber (15 calls), and the shipped findPurchaseOrderByNumber re-fetches the full PO list on every invocation (15 calls if transcribed per cast row). The faithful transcription is ≈64 calls, which can cross the 60/min fixed window mid-seed and 429 — the budget as stated understates by ~2x. (The comma-separated ?InvoiceNumbers= param that would make a true 1-call bill lookup is documented but never mentioned.)",
299	            "fix": "Either restate the budget for per-reference lookups (≈64 calls, expect to straddle the minute window; the 429/Retry-After rule already covers it) or specify the batched lookups explicitly: one GET /Invoices?InvoiceNumbers=TT-8841,...&page=1 (optimised param, docs-accounting.md line 74) and one cached GET /PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100 matched client-side for all 15 rows.",
300	            "evidence": "SKILL.md line 156 idempotency table: bill lookup is 'GET /api.xro/2.0/Invoices?where=Type%3D%3D%22ACCPAY%22...InvoiceNumber%3D%3D%22TT-8841%22' — one call per bill. check-by-reference.ts lines 141-154: findPurchaseOrderByNumber does a fresh GET /PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100 per call. .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md line 40: 'Minute Limit: 60 calls per minute' against a fixed window (line 45)."
301	          },
302	          {
303	            "file": "/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/SKILL.md",
304	            "claim": "Rate budget (line 178): '60 calls/min/tenant, 5 concurrent, 5,000/day.'",
305	            "problem": "5,000/day is the higher-tier limit only. Xero's limits page says the daily limit is 1,000 calls/day for Starter tier, and new apps default to Starter — which is what a fresh hackathon Custom Connection app will be. Anyone budgeting polling loops against this skill's 5,000 figure is off by 5x. (architecture.md repeats the same figure, but the Xero doc is the primary source; the seed itself at ~36-64 calls is unaffected.)",
306	            "fix": "State both: '60/min, 5 concurrent, 1,000/day on Starter (new-app default) or 5,000/day on Core+ — check the X-DayLimit-Remaining response header for the real number.'",
307	            "evidence": ".tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md line 41: 'Daily Limit: 1,000 calls per day for starter, 5,000 calls per day for higher tiers' and line 48: 'New apps default to the starter tier with 5 connections'. Source page: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/."
308	          }
309	        ],
310	        "styleIssues": [
311	          "The literal marker 'UNVERIFIED — check before relying' is truncated in two places: SKILL.md line 128 says only 'a batch {\"Contacts\":[...]} envelope is UNVERIFIED on the pages checked', and SKILL.md line 185's second item says 'Whether a DELETED PO's number can be reused is UNVERIFIED — if a PO must be replaced...' — both drop the required '— check before relying' tail. All other 8 UNVERIFIED markers carry the full literal string.",
312	          "DOUBT: SKILL.md line 172 asserts as bare fact that 'Xero would not reject a bill referencing a nonexistent PO' — plausible (Reference/InvoiceNumber are free-text) and not refutable from the sources, but unevidenced and unmarked; also moot as written because Reference does not persist on ACCPAY (see correction 1).",
313	          "DOUBT: internal tension between SKILL.md line 163 ('Treat a hit whose Status is DELETED or VOIDED as absent and recreate') and line 185 ('Whether a DELETED PO's number can be reused is UNVERIFIED... bump to a fresh number series'). For POs, PurchaseOrderNumber is unique, so the blanket 'recreate' rule may fail exactly where line 185 warns; the idempotency rule should carve out POs.",
314	          "DOUBT: the rate budget counts '1 token' against the 60/min tenant budget, but the token mint goes to identity.xero.com, not api.xro — harmless padding, but it muddies the arithmetic the budget is supposed to make precise.",
315	          "check-by-reference.ts findPurchaseOrderByNumber fetches the entire PO list on every call; fine for the one-shot probe in main(), but the file is presented as 'the same shapes seed/ imports' — a per-row transcription wastes 14 redundant list GETs (feeds correction 5). A note to fetch-once-and-index would prevent it.",
316	          "The fact-check trail cites source files under '.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/...' — the directory literally is named 'undefined' (generator artifact) so the paths resolve today, but they will silently dangle if the refs are ever regenerated with a fixed name.",
317	          "Positive compliance notes for the record: frontmatter has name + trigger-bearing description; the Pre-flight section precedes all code; Traps and Errors→real-cause sections are present; both scripts compile clean under the exact stated options (strict, ES2022, commonjs, esModuleInterop, types=[node], tsc 6.0.3 + @types/node 20) with no top-level await, correct Basic-auth base64, correct form-encoding, and correct percent-encoding of where filters; no match/approval business logic appears in any snippet; assignment coverage is complete (cast table, two gaps, sheet schema + 3 joining rows, exact where-filters, ordering rationale, all three mandated traps)."
318	        ]

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-07-04 12:17:57Z)**_

You are applying verified fact-check corrections to the skill at /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-auth/ (SKILL.md, scripts/verify-auth.ts, scripts/token-cache.ts). Edit ONLY files inside that directory.

FIRST read the fix spec: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/fixspecs/xero-auth.json — it has a `corrections` array (apply EVERY one exactly as its `fix` field says) and a `styleIssues` array (triage below). Then read the skill files and apply.

CROSS-CUTTING DECISIONS (all five xero-* skills are being aligned to these — apply where relevant):
1. Runner is `npx tsx` everywhere. Every runnable script self-loads repo-root .env with a tiny fs parser that: skips comments/blank lines, tolerates a leading `export `, strips MATCHING single/double quotes around values, and never overrides vars already in process.env. If verify-auth.ts's parser lacks quote-stripping or export-tolerance, upgrade it — it is the reference implementation the other skills cite.
2. Token-endpoint error canon (verified empirically 2026-07-04): empty client id / missing Authorization / JSON body → 400 invalid_request; wrong-but-nonempty creds → 400 invalid_client; unauthorized_client → the app is not a Custom Connection / client_credentials grant not enabled (most common real cause); unauthorized_client-for-client_id=undefined is a code-flow authorize-URL symptom only.
3. Rate-limit canon: 60/min per tenant, 5 concurrent, daily 1,000 on starter tier (the new-app default) or 5,000 on higher tiers — check X-DayLimit-Remaining for the real number. Whether identity.xero.com token grants and GET /connections count toward the tenant budget is UNVERIFIED — never state that they do.
4. The UNVERIFIED marker must be the contiguous literal string "UNVERIFIED — check before relying" (a grep target). Fix every truncated/split variant.
5. Fact sheets have moved to /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/facts/*.md (they were at .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/ and .tmp/xero-refs/ root). Update the Sources HTML comment at the bottom of SKILL.md to the new paths.
6. Scope canon: the app is created with exactly `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read`; scripts request a subset of these, never more. Org identity comes from GET https://api.xero.com/connections (tenantName), NOT /Organisation (needs accounting.settings[.read] which we do not have).

STYLE-ISSUE TRIAGE for this skill (beyond the corrections array):
- Fix both truncated UNVERIFIED markers (styleIssues 1 and 2) to the contiguous literal.
- Add one Traps line: verify-auth.ts uses __dirname and works because no root package.json exists yet; if a root package.json ever declares "type":"module", the script needs import.meta.dirname or a CJS-safe path — G0 breaks otherwise.
- Add to Traps: the XERO_SCOPES env override as the escape hatch when the granular-fallback scope set does not match the portal app (currently only mentioned inside the script's warning text).
- Add a "verify once creds exist" note next to the claim that GET /Organisation 403s under our scopes (it is inferred from Xero's scope-grants table, not yet observed live).

Do NOT reintroduce the Lovable "empty client_id → unauthorized_client" phrasing anywhere (see decision 2). Keep the skill terse; do not grow it with filler. After editing, re-read your diffs for internal contradictions (the whole point of correction 2 was the file contradicting itself).

Return: a bullet list of what you changed per file, and any correction you could NOT apply with the reason.

10	    "Exact failure mode of a Custom Connection app not yet authorised by an org admin — verify-auth.ts treats 'token OK but GET /connections returns []' as this case, but whether the token request itself fails instead (and whether an empty /connections has other causes) is UNVERIFIED.",
11	    "Whether a NEW Custom Connection created after 29 April 2026 can still request the broad scopes accounting.transactions / accounting.reports.read — docs are ambiguous; handled empirically with a 400 invalid_scope -> granular-equivalents retry (mirroring Xero's own MCP server), so no UNVERIFIED fact is load-bearing in the runnable script.",
12	    "Two traps the assignment expected to be unverifiable were instead settled by live probes of https://identity.xero.com/connect/token on 2026-07-04 and are documented as 'verified empirically': wrong credentials -> 400 {\"error\":\"invalid_client\"}; empty client id in Basic header / missing Authorization header / JSON body -> 400 {\"error\":\"invalid_request\"} (notably NOT unauthorized_client, which is the code-flow symptom — the error table is adapted accordingly)."

19	      "problem": "No primary source supports it and the available evidence points the other way. The official limits page (quoted verbatim in .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md:43) says the quota headers arrive on 'Each API response' — i.e. api.xero.com Accounting API calls; the token endpoint is identity.xero.com, and a live probe (2026-07-04) shows its responses carry NO X-MinLimit-Remaining / X-DayLimit-Remaining / X-AppMinLimit-Remaining headers at all. GET /connections is a non-tenanted endpoint (docs-auth.md:30), so counting it against a per-tenant minute limit is also unsupported. During a demo-day 429 hunt this fabricated '2 of your 60/min' arithmetic would send someone auditing token traffic instead of the actual API call volume.",
20	      "fix": "Reword to what the sources actually support: token-per-call wastes two round-trips of latency per tool call (auth-toolkit.md §3), and whether token/connections requests count toward the tenant 60/min limit is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Drop the 'counted against the 60/min tenant limit' and '2 of your 60/min' phrasing (or verify with real credentials by comparing X-MinLimit-Remaining before/after a token request once G0 creds exist). The caching advice itself stands on latency + the documented 5-concurrent cap alone.",
21	      "evidence": "Live probe: `curl -sS -D - https://identity.xero.com/connect/token ...` returns HTTP/2 400 with zero rate-limit headers. docs-auth.md:43 (exact quote from https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/limits/): \"Each API response you receive will include the X-DayLimit-Remaining, X-MinLimit-Remaining and X-AppMinLimit-Remaining headers\". Neither docs-auth.md, auth-toolkit.md, nor any repo under .tmp/xero-refs/ ever states token-endpoint calls count toward tenant limits."

25	      "claim": "Line 12 header comment: \"an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`\".",
26	      "problem": "Refuted by live probe and by the skill's own body. An empty client id in the Basic header on the client_credentials token endpoint returns 400 {\"error\":\"invalid_request\"} — reproduced 2026-07-04 (`curl` with `Authorization: Basic base64(\":somesecret\")` → 400 invalid_request). SKILL.md:144 says exactly this and explicitly demotes unauthorized_client to a code-flow-only symptom, and the MissingEnvError message at verify-auth.ts:61 also states it correctly ('invalid_request on the token endpoint, unauthorized_client in code flow'). The file's own header contradicts its own error message, so a skimmer debugging a token failure greps for unauthorized_client and never sees it.",
27	      "fix": "Change the line-12 comment to match line 61 and SKILL.md:144: \"an empty client id surfaces from the token endpoint as 400 invalid_request (unauthorized_client is the code-flow symptom)\".",
28	      "evidence": "Live probe 2026-07-04: empty client id in Basic → HTTP 400 {\"error\":\"invalid_request\"}; wrong-but-nonempty creds → 400 {\"error\":\"invalid_client\"}. Internal contradiction: verify-auth.ts:61 vs verify-auth.ts:12; SKILL.md:144 (Traps) and SKILL.md:160-162 (error table)."
29	    }
30	  ],

33	    "SKILL.md:167 — error-table row uses a split marker '(UNVERIFIED that this is the only cause — check before relying)'; the literal contiguous string 'UNVERIFIED — check before relying' does not appear, so a grep for the marker misses this row.",

If a root package.json with \"type\":\"module\" is ever added, `__dirname` throws under tsx and G0 breaks; the skill never states this fragility.",
