<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-auth
3	description: Xero Custom Connection (client_credentials) auth for THIS repo — exact token endpoint, the four project scopes and what each unlocks, in-memory token caching with proactive expiry and retry-on-401, why there is no XERO_TENANT_ID, and the Gate G0 verify script. Use when writing or debugging any code that obtains or uses a Xero access token, when a Xero call returns 401/403/unauthorized_client/invalid_client/invalid_scope, or before the first Xero call in a fresh environment. Read this BEFORE writing token code; there is exactly ONE token path in this repo (api/src/modules/xero/) — do not hand-roll a second.
4	---
5	
6	# Xero auth: Custom Connection, client_credentials, ONE org
7	
8	This project uses a **Custom Connection** app: client_credentials grant, bound to a **single Xero organisation**. No consent screen, no redirect URI, no refresh token, no tenant juggling. If you find yourself writing OAuth callback or PKCE code, stop — wrong flow.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` non-empty at boot, with named errors.** Empty/missing secrets that reach Xero surface as a misleading OAuth error that points away from the real cause — verified empirically 2026-07-04: an empty client id in the Basic header gets 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}` from the token endpoint (in code flow the same mistake surfaces as `unauthorized_client`). `.env` at repo root only; never committed, never logged. When logging, log lengths (`client_id_len`), never values.
13	2. **Confirm the app is a Custom Connection and has been authorised.** App creation includes selecting scopes and "who will authorise the connection" — an org admin must complete that authorisation before tokens are useful. (Exact failure mode of an unauthorised app: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. `verify-auth.ts` treats "token OK but zero connections" as this case.) Custom Connections only exist for AU/NZ/UK/US orgs and are free against the Demo Company.
14	3. **Portal scopes must cover what the token request asks for.** Scopes are configured at BOTH ends: selected on the app in the developer portal, AND sent space-separated in the token request body (unlike code/PKCE, where scopes ride the URL).
15	4. **Run Gate G0** before wiring anything else:
16	
17	```bash
18	npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
19	```
20	
21	Expected output ends with `G0 PASS` after printing org name, invoice count, and rate-limit headers. Any failure prints a named error — look it up in the table at the bottom.
22	
23	## Token endpoint — the facts
24	
25	| Fact | Value |
26	| --- | --- |
27	| Endpoint | `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` |
28	| Auth | `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id + ":" + client_secret)` — header, NOT body params |
29	| Content-Type | `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` — form-encoded body, NEVER JSON |
30	| Body | `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<url-encoded space-separated scopes>` |
31	| Response fields | `access_token` (a JWT), `expires_in` (seconds), `token_type: Bearer`, `scope` |
32	| Lifetime | up to 30 minutes (`expires_in` ~1800) |
33	| Refresh token | **None.** The response has no `refresh_token` field. "Refresh" = request a new token. `offline_access` is a code-flow concern — do not request it. |
34	
35	Minimal request (this exact shape lives in `scripts/token-cache.ts`):
36	
37	```ts
38	const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
39	const res = await fetch("https://identity.xero.com/connect/token", {
40	  method: "POST",
41	  headers: {
42	    Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
43	    "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
44	    Accept: "application/json",
45	  },
46	  body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scopes)}`,
47	});
48	```
49	
50	## Scopes: exactly four, and why nothing more
51	
52	Request the minimum. Env vars needed: `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` — that is all. There is deliberately **no `XERO_TENANT_ID`** (see next section).
53	
54	| Scope | Unlocks | Why this project needs it |
55	| --- | --- | --- |
56	| `accounting.transactions` | Broad scope = union of `accounting.invoices` (Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, **PurchaseOrders**, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items), `accounting.payments` (Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments), `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` | Bills (ACCPAY invoices), Purchase Orders, and payments are the match engine's subjects — read and write |
57	| `accounting.contacts` | Contacts, ContactGroups | Supplier resolution and dedup (contacts are NOT auto-deduplicated by email) |
58	| `accounting.attachments` | Attachments across most resources | Evidence documents attached to bills/POs via the raw Accounting API (no MCP tool exists — see `xero-accounting-api` skill) |
59	| `accounting.reports.read` | Broad scope = the eight `accounting.reports.*.read` reports (aged, balancesheet, banksummary, budgetsummary, executivesummary, profitandloss, trialbalance, taxreports) | Aged payables and financial context reads — read-only on purpose |
60	
61	What we deliberately do NOT request:
62	
63	- `openid profile email` — identity scopes for a human user; client_credentials has no user.
64	- `offline_access` — no refresh token exists in this grant; requesting it buys nothing.
65	- `accounting.settings[.read]` — grants Accounts, Organisation, TaxRates, etc. We don't read org configuration. Consequence: `GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisation` **403s** under our scopes; the org name comes from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` (`tenantName`) instead, which is what `verify-auth.ts` does.
66	- Payroll, budgets, journals — out of scope for this product.
67	
68	### Broad-scope migration (dates matter)
69	
70	From 29 April 2026 all custom connections have access to granular scopes; new custom connections lose `accounting.journals.read`; existing custom connections can keep using broad scopes until **September 2027**. Whether a custom connection created today can still request the broad `accounting.transactions` / `accounting.reports.read`: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Handle it empirically, the way Xero's own MCP server does: request broad, and on HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` retry with the granular equivalents. `verify-auth.ts` implements exactly this fallback and warns loudly when it triggers. Granular mapping for our set:
71	
72	| Broad (ours) | Granular equivalent |
73	| --- | --- |
74	| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices accounting.payments accounting.banktransactions accounting.manualjournals` |
75	| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read accounting.reports.balancesheet.read accounting.reports.banksummary.read accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read accounting.reports.executivesummary.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read accounting.reports.trialbalance.read accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |
76	| `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments` | never broad — use as-is |
77	
78	Source of truth (re-check periodically — dates and assignments change): https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
79	
80	## Tenant id: omit the header
81	
82	Custom Connections "access data from a single Xero organisation" — one org, period (multi-org needs code/PKCE flow). The documented custom-connection API call sends **exactly two headers**:
83	
84	```
85	Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
86	Accept: application/json
87	```
88	
89	No `xero-tenant-id`. (An explicit docs sentence "the header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — but the canonical example omits it. Contrast: standard code-flow tokens REQUIRE `xero-tenant-id` on every call.) Xero's own MCP server sends a tenant id anyway, resolving it at runtime via `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` → `connections[0].tenantId`.
90	
91	**Rule for this repo:** omit the header on raw custom-connection calls. If a shared code path insists on one, resolve it from `GET /connections` at runtime — never hardcode it, never put it in `.env`. That is WHY there is no `XERO_TENANT_ID` env var.
92	
93	Note a docs wrinkle: the client-credentials page says Custom Connection apps "can only access tenanted endpoints and cannot access non-tenanted endpoints", yet `GET /connections` demonstrably works with a custom-connection token — Xero's MCP server depends on it for tenant resolution. Trust the working call.
94	
95	## Token caching — do NOT copy Xero's MCP server
96	
97	The MCP server requests a brand-new token plus a `GET /connections` on **every** tool call: two wasted round-trips per call, all counted against the 60/min tenant limit. Do the opposite. `scripts/token-cache.ts` is the drop-in module:
98	
99	- Cache the token in memory; reuse until `expires_at - 60s` (60s buffer, same as Xero's CLI `TOKEN_BUFFER_MS`), computed from `expires_in`.
100	- Single-flight: concurrent callers share one in-progress token request.
101	- On **401**: invalidate, fetch a fresh token, retry exactly **once** (`xeroFetch` helper). On failure after retry, surface the error — no infinite loops.
102	- On **403**: never retry — that is a missing scope; the same token can never succeed.
103	- The cache holds the token in process memory only. Never persist it, never log it, never send it to the browser.
104	
105	NestJS wiring (illustrative — real module lives in `api/src/modules/xero/`):
106	
107	```ts
108	// api/src/modules/xero/xero.module.ts
109	{
110	  provide: XeroTokenCache,
111	  useFactory: (config: ConfigService) =>
112	    new XeroTokenCache({
113	      clientId: config.getOrThrow("XERO_CLIENT_ID"),
114	      clientSecret: config.getOrThrow("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET"),
115	      scope: "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read",
116	    }),
117	  inject: [ConfigService],
118	}
119	```
120	
121	The constructor throws `MissingCredential` on empty values, so a misconfigured boot dies at startup, not mid-demo.
122	
123	## Rate-limit headers (read them, print them, respect them)
124	
125	| Header | Meaning |
126	| --- | --- |
127	| `X-MinLimit-Remaining` | calls left this minute (limit: 60/min per tenant) |
128	| `X-DayLimit-Remaining` | calls left today (5,000/day; 1,000 on starter tier) |
129	| `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining` | app-wide calls left this minute (10,000/min across all tenants) |
130	| `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` | on a 429: which limit you hit |
131	| `Retry-After` | on a 429: seconds to wait before calling again — use it, the window is fixed per tenant |
132	
133	Also: max 5 concurrent calls per tenant. Every token request counts too — another reason to cache.
134	
135	## Scripts
136	
137	| File | Purpose |
138	| --- | --- |
139	| `scripts/verify-auth.ts` | **Gate G0.** Parses repo-root `.env` itself (no dotenv), asserts env with named errors, fetches a token (with granular-scope fallback), GETs the org name via `/connections` and 1 invoice, prints org + invoice count + rate-limit headers. Run: `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts` from repo root. |
140	| `scripts/token-cache.ts` | In-memory token cache + `xeroFetch` retry-on-401 helper, shaped for a NestJS `useFactory` provider. Zero npm deps; compiles standalone under strict/ES2022/commonjs. |
141	
142	## Traps
143	
144	- **A misleading OAuth error from an empty or whitespace client id.** The cause is your env; the error points at Xero. Verified empirically 2026-07-04 against the live token endpoint: empty client id in the Basic header, or a missing Authorization header entirely → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`. (The Lovable skill's `unauthorized_client` symptom for `client_id=undefined` is the code-flow authorize URL, not this endpoint.) Assert non-empty **and trimmed** at boot — a trailing newline in `.env` counts as whitespace.
145	- **`invalid_client` from a wrong secret or wrong Basic encoding.** Verified empirically 2026-07-04: well-formed Basic header with wrong credentials → 400 `{"error":"invalid_client"}`. Base64 the literal `client_id:client_secret` — one colon, no newline, whole string encoded together; garbled encoding produces the same `invalid_client` (a structurally broken header degrades to `invalid_request`).
146	- **403 vs 401 are different diseases.** `401` = the token itself is bad or expired (>30 min) → new token, retry once. `403` = the token is fine but lacks a scope → fix the scope list at BOTH ends (portal + token request), get a NEW token. Retrying a 403 with the same token can never succeed.
147	- **The token endpoint wants form-encoding, not JSON.** `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a `key=value&key=value` body. Verified empirically 2026-07-04: a JSON body → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`, the same misleading error as a missing client id.
148	- **An org admin must authorise the Custom Connection before tokens work.** App setup chooses "who will authorise the connection"; until they do, the connection is dead. `verify-auth.ts` reports "token OK but zero connections" for this.
149	- **Broad scopes can 400 `invalid_scope` on newer apps.** Post-April-2026 granular migration — retry with the granular set (table above). `verify-auth.ts` does this automatically.
150	- **`GET /Organisation` 403s under our scopes.** It needs `accounting.settings[.read]`, which we deliberately don't request. Get the org name from `GET /connections` (`tenantName`).
151	- **Scope cannot be widened on an existing token.** Cheap fix here (unlike code flow): request a new token with the wider scope — provided the portal app has that scope enabled.
152	- **Every token request burns rate limit.** A token-per-call pattern (MCP server style) spends 2 of your 60/min on overhead. Cache.
153	
154	## Error → real cause
155	
156	Rows marked "verified empirically" were probed against the live token endpoint on 2026-07-04.
157	
158	| Symptom | Real cause | Fix |
159	| --- | --- | --- |
160	| Token 400 `invalid_request` (verified empirically) | Empty client id in the Basic header, missing Authorization header, or a JSON body instead of form-encoding | Boot-time `MissingEnv` assertion; check `.env` parsing/trimming; send form-encoded body |
161	| Token 400 `invalid_client` (verified empirically) | Wrong secret or wrong client id, incl. malformed Basic encoding (missing colon, double-encoded, newline in the base64 input) | Re-copy the secret; encode `id:secret` as one string |
162	| `unauthorized_client` | Code-flow symptom of `client_id=undefined` in the authorize URL — should not appear in this repo's client_credentials runtime; if seen, credentials are empty/wrong somewhere | Boot-time env assertion |
163	| Token 400 `invalid_scope` | Broad scope on a granular-only app, or a scope not enabled on the app in the portal | Retry with granular equivalents; enable the scope in the portal |
164	| API call 401 | Token expired (~30 min) or invalid | Invalidate cache, fetch new token, retry once (`xeroFetch`) |
165	| API call 403 | Token lacks the scope for that resource (e.g. `/Organisation` without `accounting.settings`) | Add the scope at both ends, request a NEW token; never retry with the same token |
166	| API call 429 + `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` | Rate limit hit (60/min, 5,000/day, 5 concurrent, 10,000/min app-wide) | Wait `Retry-After` seconds; batch reads; cache tokens |
167	| Token OK, `GET /connections` returns `[]` | Custom Connection not yet authorised by an org admin (UNVERIFIED that this is the only cause — check before relying) | Complete the authorisation step in the developer portal |
168	| `MissingEnv` / `MissingCredential` at boot | `.env` missing or var empty | Create `.env` at repo root with `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` |
169	
170	Related skills: `xero-mcp-usage` (MCP vs raw API routing), `xero-accounting-api` (Purchase Orders, Attachments, History — raw API only).
171	
172	<!--
173	Sources (fact-check trail):
174	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — token endpoint, Basic auth, form-encoded body, response fields, ~30 min lifetime (Token types page), single-org quote, AU/NZ/UK/US restriction, scopes-at-both-ends, granular migration dates (29 Apr 2026 / Sep 2027), two-header call example with no xero-tenant-id (explicit "not required" sentence absent = UNVERIFIED), "tenanted endpoints only" wrinkle, rate limits + X-*Limit-Remaining/X-Rate-Limit-Problem/Retry-After headers, pagination (page/pageSize min 1, pagination.itemCount). Underlying URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,client-credentials,token-types,limits}/, /documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses.
175	- .tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server/src/clients/xero-client.ts:127-203 — exact token request shape, token-per-call anti-pattern, invalid_scope granular fallback, GET /connections → connections[0].tenantId, boot env assertion.
176	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/lib/{auth.ts,oauth.ts,xero-client.ts} — TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000, refresh-on-401 retry pattern, /connections response shape incl. tenantName, Retry-After extraction.
177	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md:116-140 — unauthorized_client ← empty client_id causal chain, requireEnv/client_id_len discipline, scope-grants tables (accounting.invoices → PurchaseOrders etc., accounting.settings → Organisation), broad→granular migration table, 403 = missing scope, scope minimalism rules.
178	- Empirical probes 2026-07-04 against live https://identity.xero.com/connect/token: wrong creds → 400 {"error":"invalid_client"}; empty client id in Basic / no Authorization header / JSON body → 400 {"error":"invalid_request"}.
179	- UNVERIFIED items marked inline: explicit tenant-header-not-required sentence; unauthorised-app exact failure mode (and whether empty /connections has other causes); whether new post-Apr-2026 custom connections can still request broad scopes.
180	-->
181
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:18:09Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
2	//
3	// Gate G0 — proves the Xero Custom Connection works end to end, before any
4	// feature code is written. Run from repo root:
5	//
6	//   npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
7	//
8	// What it does:
9	//   1. Parses <repo-root>/.env itself (no dotenv dep); vars already in
10	//      process.env win over the file.
11	//   2. Asserts XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named errors —
12	//      an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`).
13	//   3. Fetches a client_credentials token from identity.xero.com.
14	//      On 400 `invalid_scope` (granular-only app) it retries once with the
15	//      granular equivalents of the project's broad scopes.
16	//   4. GETs the connected organisation (name via GET /connections — our
17	//      scopes deliberately exclude accounting.settings, so GET /Organisation
18	//      would 403) and 1 invoice.
19	//   5. Prints org name, invoice count, and the rate-limit headers.
20	//
21	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Compiles standalone under
22	// strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
23	
24	import * as fs from "node:fs";
25	import * as path from "node:path";
26	
27	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
28	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
29	const INVOICES_URL = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1&pageSize=1";
30	
31	// The exact scopes this project needs — nothing more. See SKILL.md.
32	const PROJECT_SCOPES =
33	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
34	
35	// Granular equivalents (post-29-April-2026 custom connections may reject the
36	// broad scopes with 400 invalid_scope). Mapping per Xero's scope migration:
37	// accounting.transactions -> invoices+payments+banktransactions+manualjournals,
38	// accounting.reports.read -> the eight accounting.reports.*.read scopes.
39	const GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES = [
40	  "accounting.invoices",
41	  "accounting.payments",
42	  "accounting.banktransactions",
43	  "accounting.manualjournals",
44	  "accounting.contacts",
45	  "accounting.attachments",
46	  "accounting.reports.aged.read",
47	  "accounting.reports.balancesheet.read",
48	  "accounting.reports.banksummary.read",
49	  "accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read",
50	  "accounting.reports.executivesummary.read",
51	  "accounting.reports.profitandloss.read",
52	  "accounting.reports.trialbalance.read",
53	  "accounting.reports.taxreports.read",
54	].join(" ");
55	
56	class MissingEnvError extends Error {
57	  constructor(varName: string) {
58	    super(
59	      `${varName} is empty — set it in .env at repo root. ` +
60	        `If an empty client id reaches Xero it surfaces as a misleading OAuth error ` +
61	        `(invalid_request on the token endpoint, unauthorized_client in code flow), ` +
62	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
63	    );
64	    this.name = "MissingEnv";
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	class XeroTokenError extends Error {
69	  constructor(
70	    public readonly status: number,
71	    public readonly body: string,
72	  ) {
73	    super(`token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
74	    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	class XeroApiError extends Error {
79	  constructor(
80	    public readonly status: number,
81	    url: string,
82	    body: string,
83	  ) {
84	    super(`GET ${url} failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
85	    this.name = "XeroApiError";
86	  }
87	}
88	
89	// --- 1. Tiny .env parser (existing process.env wins; missing file tolerated) ---
90	
91	function loadDotEnv(envPath: string): void {
92	  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
93	    console.warn(`[verify-auth] no .env at ${envPath} — relying on process.env only`);
94	    return;
95	  }
96	  for (const rawLine of fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split(/\r?\n/)) {
97	    const line = rawLine.trim().replace(/^export\s+/, "");
98	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
99	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
100	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
101	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
102	    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
103	    if (
104	      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"') && value.length >= 2) ||
105	      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'") && value.length >= 2)
106	    ) {
107	      value = value.slice(1, -1);
108	    }
109	    const existing = process.env[key];
110	    if (existing === undefined || existing === "") {
111	      process.env[key] = value;
112	    }
113	  }
114	}
115	
116	// --- 2. Named env assertions (fail fast, never log the value itself) ---
117	
118	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
119	  const v = process.env[name];
120	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
121	    throw new MissingEnvError(name);
122	  }
123	  return v.trim();
124	}
125	
126	// --- 3. client_credentials token (Basic auth + form-encoded body, never JSON) ---
127	
128	interface TokenResult {
129	  accessToken: string;
130	  expiresIn: number;
131	  scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" | "granular fallback";
132	}
133	
134	async function requestToken(
135	  clientId: string,
136	  clientSecret: string,
137	  scope: string,
138	): Promise<{ accessToken: string; expiresIn: number }> {
139	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
140	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
141	    method: "POST",
142	    headers: {
143	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
144	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
145	      Accept: "application/json",
146	    },
147	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
148	  });
149	  const text = await res.text();
150	  if (!res.ok) {
151	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
152	  }
153	  const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
154	  if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
155	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
156	  }
157	  return {
158	    accessToken: parsed.access_token,
159	    expiresIn: typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0,
160	  };
161	}
162	
163	async function getTokenWithFallback(clientId: string, clientSecret: string): Promise<TokenResult> {
164	  const scopeOverride = process.env["XERO_SCOPES"];
165	  const scopes =
166	    scopeOverride !== undefined && scopeOverride.trim() !== "" ? scopeOverride.trim() : PROJECT_SCOPES;
167	  try {
168	    const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
169	    return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" };
170	  } catch (err) {
171	    if (err instanceof XeroTokenError && err.status === 400 && err.body.includes("invalid_scope")) {
172	      console.warn(
173	        "[verify-auth] broad scopes rejected (invalid_scope) — this app only has granular scopes. " +
174	          "Retrying with granular equivalents. Set XERO_SCOPES in .env to the granular set to silence this.",
175	      );
176	      const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES);
177	      return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "granular fallback" };
178	    }
179	    throw err;
180	  }
181	}
182	
183	// --- 4. Organisation + one invoice ---
184	
185	interface XeroConnection {
186	  tenantId?: string;
187	  tenantName?: string;
188	}
189	
190	async function getJson(url: string, accessToken: string): Promise<{ body: unknown; res: Response }> {
191	  // Per the custom-connections docs, API calls need exactly these two headers.
192	  // No xero-tenant-id: a custom connection is bound to a single organisation.
193	  const res = await fetch(url, {
194	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, Accept: "application/json" },
195	  });
196	  const text = await res.text();
197	  if (!res.ok) {
198	    throw new XeroApiError(res.status, url, text);
199	  }
200	  return { body: JSON.parse(text) as unknown, res };
201	}
202	
203	async function main(): Promise<void> {
204	  loadDotEnv(path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"));
205	
206	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
207	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
208	  // Log lengths only — never the values.
209	  console.log(
210	    `[verify-auth] env ok (client_id_len: ${clientId.length}, client_secret_len: ${clientSecret.length})`,
211	  );
212	
213	  const token = await getTokenWithFallback(clientId, clientSecret);
214	  console.log(
215	    `[verify-auth] token ok — scope set: ${token.scopeSetUsed}, expires_in: ${token.expiresIn}s`,
216	  );
217	
218	  const { body: connectionsBody } = await getJson(CONNECTIONS_URL, token.accessToken);
219	  const connections = Array.isArray(connectionsBody) ? (connectionsBody as XeroConnection[]) : [];
220	  if (connections.length === 0) {
221	    throw new XeroApiError(
222	      200,
223	      CONNECTIONS_URL,
224	      "token issued but no organisation is connected. Has a Xero org admin authorised this " +
225	        "Custom Connection app in the developer portal? Tokens are useless until that is done.",
226	    );
227	  }
228	  const first = connections[0];
229	  const orgName = first?.tenantName ?? first?.tenantId ?? "(unnamed connection)";
230	  console.log(`[verify-auth] organisation: ${orgName}`);
231	
232	  const { body: invoicesBody, res: invoicesRes } = await getJson(INVOICES_URL, token.accessToken);
233	  const inv = invoicesBody as { Invoices?: unknown[]; pagination?: { itemCount?: unknown } };
234	  const returned = Array.isArray(inv.Invoices) ? inv.Invoices.length : 0;
235	  const itemCount = typeof inv.pagination?.itemCount === "number" ? inv.pagination.itemCount : null;
236	  console.log(
237	    `[verify-auth] invoices returned: ${returned}` + (itemCount !== null ? ` (org total: ${itemCount})` : ""),
238	  );
239	
240	  console.log(
241	    "[verify-auth] rate limits remaining — " +
242	      `minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-MinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
243	      `day: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-DayLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
244	      `app-minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-AppMinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}`,
245	  );
246	  console.log("[verify-auth] G0 PASS");
247	}
248	
249	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
250	  if (err instanceof Error) {
251	    console.error(`[verify-auth] FAIL — ${err.name}: ${err.message}`);
252	  } else {
253	    console.error("[verify-auth] FAIL —", err);
254	  }
255	  console.error("[verify-auth] see the error table in .claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md");
256	  process.exit(1);
257	});
258
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/token-cache.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/token-cache.ts
2	//
3	// In-memory client_credentials token cache for a Xero Custom Connection.
4	// Copy into api/src/modules/xero/ and register as a NestJS provider via
5	// useFactory (see SKILL.md) — this file itself has zero framework imports so
6	// it compiles standalone under strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
7	//
8	// Why this exists: Xero's own MCP server requests a brand-new token (plus a
9	// GET /connections) on EVERY call — two wasted round-trips per call, all
10	// counted against the 60/min tenant rate limit. Do the opposite:
11	//   - cache the token in memory until expiry minus a 60s buffer
12	//     (pattern: xero-command-line TOKEN_BUFFER_MS = 60_000),
13	//   - single-flight concurrent token requests,
14	//   - on 401 invalidate + fetch a fresh token + retry exactly once
15	//     (pattern: xero-command-line withRetry).
16	// client_credentials issues NO refresh_token (response fields: access_token,
17	// expires_in, token_type, scope) — "refresh" simply means asking again.
18	
19	const XERO_TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
20	
21	/** Refresh this long before expiry, so in-flight calls never race the deadline. */
22	const EXPIRY_BUFFER_MS = 60_000;
23	
24	export class MissingCredentialError extends Error {
25	  constructor(name: string) {
26	    super(
27	      `${name} is empty — if it reaches Xero the token endpoint answers a misleading ` +
28	        `400 invalid_request, which points away from the real cause. ` +
29	        `Set it in .env and fail at boot, not mid-request.`,
30	    );
31	    this.name = "MissingCredential";
32	  }
33	}
34	
35	export class XeroTokenError extends Error {
36	  constructor(
37	    public readonly status: number,
38	    public readonly body: string,
39	  ) {
40	    super(`Xero token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
41	    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
42	  }
43	}
44	
45	export interface XeroTokenCacheConfig {
46	  clientId: string;
47	  clientSecret: string;
48	  /** Space-separated scope list, sent in the token request body. */
49	  scope: string;
50	}
51	
52	export class XeroTokenCache {
53	  private accessToken: string | null = null;
54	  private expiresAtMs = 0;
55	  private inflight: Promise<string> | null = null;
56	
57	  constructor(private readonly config: XeroTokenCacheConfig) {
58	    if (config.clientId.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
59	    if (config.clientSecret.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
60	    if (config.scope.trim() === "") throw new MissingCredentialError("scope");
61	  }
62	
63	  /** Returns a valid access token, reusing the cached one when fresh. */
64	  async getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
65	    if (this.accessToken !== null && Date.now() < this.expiresAtMs - EXPIRY_BUFFER_MS) {
66	      return this.accessToken;
67	    }
68	    // Single-flight: concurrent callers share one token request instead of
69	    // each burning a round-trip against the rate limit.
70	    if (this.inflight === null) {
71	      this.inflight = this.requestToken().finally(() => {
72	        this.inflight = null;
73	      });
74	    }
75	    return this.inflight;
76	  }
77	
78	  /** Drop the cached token (call on a 401 before retrying). */
79	  invalidate(): void {
80	    this.accessToken = null;
81	    this.expiresAtMs = 0;
82	  }
83	
84	  private async requestToken(): Promise<string> {
85	    const basic = Buffer.from(`${this.config.clientId}:${this.config.clientSecret}`).toString(
86	      "base64",
87	    );
88	    const res = await fetch(XERO_TOKEN_URL, {
89	      method: "POST",
90	      headers: {
91	        Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
92	        // Form-encoded, NEVER JSON — the token endpoint is not a JSON API.
93	        "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
94	        Accept: "application/json",
95	      },
96	      body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(this.config.scope)}`,
97	    });
98	    const text = await res.text();
99	    if (!res.ok) {
100	      // Body carries Xero's OAuth error (unauthorized_client, invalid_scope, ...)
101	      // and never contains the secret, so it is safe to surface.
102	      throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
103	    }
104	    const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
105	    if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
106	      throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
107	    }
108	    const expiresInSec = typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0;
109	    this.accessToken = parsed.access_token;
110	    this.expiresAtMs = Date.now() + expiresInSec * 1000;
111	    return this.accessToken;
112	  }
113	}
114	
115	export interface XeroFetchInit {
116	  method?: string;
117	  headers?: Record<string, string>;
118	  body?: string;
119	}
120	
121	/**
122	 * fetch against the Xero API with the cached token; on 401, invalidate,
123	 * fetch a fresh token, and retry exactly once. 403 is NOT retried — that is
124	 * a missing scope, and the same token can never succeed.
125	 *
126	 * No xero-tenant-id header: a Custom Connection is bound to a single
127	 * organisation and the documented call sends only Authorization + Accept.
128	 */
129	export async function xeroFetch(
130	  cache: XeroTokenCache,
131	  url: string,
132	  init?: XeroFetchInit,
133	): Promise<Response> {
134	  const doFetch = async (): Promise<Response> => {
135	    const token = await cache.getAccessToken();
136	    return fetch(url, {
137	      method: init?.method ?? "GET",
138	      headers: {
139	        Accept: "application/json",
140	        ...(init?.headers ?? {}),
141	        Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
142	      },
143	      body: init?.body,
144	    });
145	  };
146	
147	  const first = await doFetch();
148	  if (first.status !== 401) {
149	    return first;
150	  }
151	  cache.invalidate();
152	  return doFetch();
153	}
154
```
</details></tool-use>

A reader following pre-flight top-to-bottom with secrets only in .env gets MissingEnv on the smoke test despite completing step 1 — reproduced live (tsx run threw MissingEnv). <tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-accounting-api
3	description: The raw Xero Accounting API surface THIS project touches, over plain fetch with a Custom Connection (client_credentials, no xero-node SDK, no tenant id) — ACCPAY bills on /Invoices (where filters, create, DRAFT→SUBMITTED→AUTHORISED walk), Purchase Orders and Attachments and History notes (all three are NOT in the Xero MCP server — raw API is the ONLY path), Contacts for supplier matching, and the AgedPayablesByContact report. Covers pagination, If-Modified-Since, where-filter quoting traps, the Elements[].ValidationErrors envelope, rate limits and 429/Retry-After handling. Use when writing or reviewing ANY code in api/src/modules/xero/ that calls api.xro/2.0, when an MCP tool is missing (see xero-mcp-usage), or when debugging a Xero 400/403/429. Read this BEFORE writing any raw Accounting API call; do NOT rely on generic Xero knowledge.
4	---
5	
6	# Raw Accounting API (Custom Connection, plain fetch — no SDK)
7	
8	Base `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/{Resource}`. Send `Accept: application/json` on every call — Xero's default response format is XML. Bills are `/Invoices` with `Type: "ACCPAY"`; there is no `/Bills` endpoint. For token mechanics see the **`xero-auth`** skill; for paying bills see **`xero-payments`**; for what routes through MCP instead see **`xero-mcp-usage`**.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert env non-empty before any call.** `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` live in `.env` at the repo root, never committed, never logged. Empty values surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=` got sent), which points away from the real cause. `requireEnv()` in `scripts/xero-fetch.ts` throws `MissingEnv: XERO_CLIENT_ID is empty — ...` at first use.
13	2. **No tenant id — here is why.** A Custom Connection "utilise[s] the client credentials grant type to access data from a single Xero organisation" — one org per connection, so the tenant is implicit. The docs' canonical custom-connection API call carries exactly two headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and `Accept: application/json`. (An explicit docs sentence "the xero-tenant-id header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — check before relying; the basis is the single-org binding plus its omission from the documented call.)
14	3. **Scopes are set at BOTH ends** — selected at app creation AND passed as `scope` in every token request body (this differs from code/PKCE flow where scopes ride the URL). This project's app selects `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read` (see `.env.example`). A token minted without the needed scope 403s at runtime. Granular-scope migration: from 29 April 2026 all custom connections have granular scopes (`accounting.invoices` covers Invoices AND PurchaseOrders); existing connections may keep broad scopes until September 2027. Whether a brand-new custom connection can still select broad scopes today, and the exact granular report scope for aged payables: UNVERIFIED — check before relying (check the app dashboard scope picker).
15	4. **Smoke-test before wiring anything:** `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts` must print a bill count and a `pagination` object. If it fails here, fix auth first — Xero's errors deeper in are less useful.
16	5. **Reports need the reports role.** "An application that has been authorised by a Standard user without the 'reports' role will not be able to access the Reports ... endpoints (A HTTP 403 error will be returned)." Have the org admin who authorises the connection hold that role.
17	
18	## Conventions on every call
19	
20	| Fact | Value |
21	|---|---|
22	| JSON dates | `"UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790)\/"` — unix epoch in **milliseconds** inside |
23	| PUT vs POST | PUT creates only; POST creates **or updates** |
24	| Envelope | single object or plural-keyed array (`{"Invoices":[...]}`) — both accepted |
25	| Page size | default **100**, max **1000**, min 1; out-of-range auto-adjusted (`pageSize=5000` → 1000 items) |
26	| Pagination object | responses include `"pagination": {page, pageSize, pageCount, itemCount}` |
27	| Paged endpoints | Invoices, Contacts, CreditNotes, BankTransactions, ManualJournals, Payments, PurchaseOrders, Prepayments, Overpayments |
28	| Request cap | 10MB per request; batch writes in bundles of ~50 elements |
29	| `If-Modified-Since` | header, UTC, accurate to the second; recommended on all large result sets |
30	
31	**If-Modified-Since misses some changes.** Changes that don't bump `UpdatedDateUTC` are not returned: `DueDate`/`SentToContact` edits on partially paid transactions, and Contact `Balances`/`IsSupplier`/`IsCustomer`. Do not rely on it for those fields.
32	
33	### Rate limits (per tenant unless stated)
34	
35	| Limit | Value |
36	|---|---|
37	| Concurrent | 5 calls in progress |
38	| Minute | **60 calls/min** |
39	| Daily | 1,000/day (Starter tier) — **5,000/day** (higher tiers) |
40	| App-wide | 10,000 calls/min across all tenants |
41	
42	Every response carries `X-DayLimit-Remaining`, `X-MinLimit-Remaining`, `X-AppMinLimit-Remaining`. On **429** the `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` header names the limit hit; minute/daily breaches also send `Retry-After` (seconds) — wait exactly that long. `xeroFetch` does this. Batch reads and cache in Postgres; never poll in a loop.
43	
44	### Idempotency-Key header
45	
46	Send `Idempotency-Key` on POST/PUT (ignored on GET). Xero caches the response for **6 minutes**; same key replays the cached response. Max **128 chars**. Same key + a *different* request (URL, body, or method changed) → 400 `Idempotency Key ... is used with a different request.` **Errors are cached too** — after fixing the underlying problem, GET to check whether the resource exists, then retry with a NEW key. Keyed duplicates still count against rate limits.
47	
48	### `where` filter — quoting traps
49	
50	- The whole expression must be **percent-encoded** (the helper's `query` map does this; never hand-concatenate into the URL).
51	- Strings use **double quotes** and `==`: official invoice example `Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"` → encoded `where=Type%3d%3d%22ACCPAY%22+AND+Status%3d%3d%22AUTHORISED%22`.
52	- Dates use **`DateTime(2015, 01, 01)`** syntax, not ISO strings: `Date >= DateTime(2015, 01, 01) && Date < DateTime(2015, 12, 31)`. Join conditions with `and`/`&&`.
53	- Keep to simple `==` on optimised fields — "Long, complex where queries can cause time outs"; unoptimised filter/order producing >100k rows → 400.
54	- Contacts' optimised examples use a single `=` (`where=Name="ABC limited"`) and are case- and accent-insensitive — never append `ToLower()`/`ToUpper()`.
55	
56	### Validation error envelopes — two shapes
57	
58	HTTP **400** returns an ApiException (real documented example):
59	
60	```json
61	{
62	  "ErrorNumber": 10,
63	  "Type": "ValidationException",
64	  "Message": "A validation exception occurred",
65	  "Elements": [{ "ValidationErrors": [{ "Message": "Email address must be valid" }] }]
66	}
67	```
68	
69	With `?summarizeErrors=false` (batch writes) you instead get HTTP **200** and a per-element `StatusAttributeString` of `OK`/`WARNING`/`ERROR`, with `ValidationErrors` on the failed element (the docs' bulk example shows `{"Description": "Invoice not of valid type for creation"}` — note the key differs from the 400 shape; parse both). A 200 does NOT mean the write succeeded — always assert per element (`assertElementOk` in the helper).
70	
71	## Bills — `/Invoices` with `Type: "ACCPAY"`
72	
73	Fields this project uses (create/read):
74	
75	| Field | Notes |
76	|---|---|
77	| `Type` | `"ACCPAY"` — required on create |
78	| `Contact` | required; provide **only** `ContactID` |
79	| `LineItems` | at least one required; `Description` alone makes a valid line (1–4000 chars) |
80	| `LineItems[].UnitAmount` | rounded to **2dp** unless the request adds `?unitdp=4` |
81	| `LineItems[].TaxType` | override only when the `AccountCode` default tax is wrong |
82	| `InvoiceNumber` | ACCPAY: **NON-unique**, max 255, displays as **"Reference"** in the Xero UI |
83	| `Date` / `DueDate` | `YYYY-MM-DD`; `Date` defaults to today in the org timezone |
84	| `Status` | optional on create, defaults to `DRAFT`; may create directly as `SUBMITTED` or `AUTHORISED` |
85	| `LineAmountTypes` | `Exclusive` (default when omitted) / `Inclusive` / `NoTax` |
86	| `CurrencyCode` | defaults to org base currency |
87	| `Total` | computed: `SubTotal + TotalTax`; `LineAmount = Quantity × UnitAmount` (≤ 9,999,999,999.99) |
88	
89	ACCPAY does **not** support discounts (`DiscountRate`/`DiscountAmount` are ACCREC-only).
90	
91	### Status transitions (the documented table — everything else is illegal)
92	
93	| Existing | May become |
94	|---|---|
95	| `DRAFT` | `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED` |
96	| `SUBMITTED` | `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `DRAFT`, `DELETED` |
97	| `AUTHORISED` | `AUTHORISED`, `VOIDED` |
98	
99	- **`AUTHORISED` locks.** Journals are created and payments can now be applied; it can never return to `DRAFT`/`SUBMITTED` and cannot be deleted — only `VOIDED` (and voiding is shown only for invoices with no payments applied).
100	- **`PAID` is system-set** when fully paid. Never write it; a "paid" bill is a two-step: `AUTHORISED`, then a Payment (see `xero-payments`). No transitions are documented out of `PAID`, `VOIDED`, `DELETED` — treat as terminal.
101	- Invoices in a **locked period** cannot be updated. Partially/fully paid ACCPAY bills allow edits only to: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (with CIS/credit-note caveats), Url, line Description, AccountCode (non-CIS), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate.
102	- **Line-item update rule:** in an update, a line WITH `LineItemID` is updated, WITHOUT one is created, and any existing line whose id is missing from a supplied `LineItems` array is **deleted**. Status-only updates (as in the docs' delete example) send no `LineItems` at all.
103	
104	### Listing
105	
106	- Optimised `where` equality fields: Status, Contact.ContactID, Contact.Name, Contact.ContactNumber, Reference, InvoiceNumber, InvoiceId, Type, AmountPaid; range (`>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`) also on Date, DueDate, AmountDue.
107	- Faster than `where` for lists: `?Statuses=AUTHORISED,DRAFT`, `?IDs=`, `?InvoiceNumbers=`, `?ContactIDs=` (comma-separated). `SearchTerm` text-searches InvoiceNumber + Reference.
108	- Default order `UpdatedDateUTC ASC, InvoiceId ASC`; optimised order fields: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date.
109	- **Always page**: paged responses include full `LineItems`; an unpaged multi-invoice GET returns contact summaries and NO line details. `summaryOnly=true` drops Payments/LineItems/HasAttachments and forces pagination.
110	
111	### Idempotent create (check-by-reference)
112	
113	Because ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is non-unique, "does this bill already exist?" must combine fields — filter on `Contact.ContactID` + `InvoiceNumber` (both optimised) and compare `Total` — or better, persist the returned `InvoiceID` and never re-create. Belt-and-braces: a stable `Idempotency-Key` per logical write (6-minute window only — it protects against retries, not against re-runs tomorrow).
114	
115	## Purchase Orders — `/PurchaseOrders`
116	
117	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER.** The Xero MCP server has zero purchase-order tools (verified by grep of its `src/tools/` — no tool name contains "purchase"). Same for Attachments and History. The raw API below is the ONLY path. Do not go looking for an MCP tool.
118	
119	| Field | Notes |
120	|---|---|
121	| `Contact` | required; must be an **existing** contact — "The PurchaseOrders endpoint does not create new contacts" |
122	| `LineItems` | at least one required; if `Quantity` is set, `UnitAmount` must be too; `UnitAmount` rounds to **4dp** (no `unitdp` needed) |
123	| `PurchaseOrderNumber` | **unique**; auto-generated from org invoice settings when missing |
124	| `Status` | `DRAFT` (default) \| `SUBMITTED` \| `AUTHORISED` \| `BILLED` \| `DELETED` |
125	| `Date` / `DeliveryDate` / `Reference` / `LineAmountTypes` / `CurrencyCode` | as for bills |
126	
127	- **Create:** PUT (create-only) or POST, envelope `{"PurchaseOrders":[...]}`, `?summarizeErrors=false` for per-element errors. Line-item update rule identical to invoices.
128	- **Status update:** POST to `/PurchaseOrders/{PurchaseOrderID}` with the new `Status` (docs' example flips a draft PO to `DELETED`). **Marking billed** = same mechanism with `"Status": "BILLED"` — but Xero publishes no PO transition table and no explicit mark-as-billed example, so which transitions are legal is UNVERIFIED — check before relying (probe on the seeded org first). `SentToContact` can be set only on approved or billed POs.
129	- **Listing:** explicit params only — `?status=DRAFT`, `?DateFrom=2015-12-01&DateTo=2015-12-31`, `order`, `page`, `pageSize`; changed-since via `If-Modified-Since`. A generic `where` param is not documented for POs — UNVERIFIED — check before relying (use the explicit params).
130	- **Paging is enforced by default**: 100/page, `pageSize` up to 1000.
131	
132	## Attachments — `/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}`
133	
134	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER** — raw API only.
135	
136	- **Upload:** PUT or POST with the **raw file bytes** as the body — not JSON. `Content-Type` = the file's MIME type. PUT and POST are identical; the same filename on the same document **overwrites**. Example: `PUT /api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}/Attachments/evidence.pdf`.
137	- **Limits:** **10 attachments per document, each up to 10MB** (the Contacts page says 25MB per file — the two official pages conflict; assume 10MB, do not rely on more without testing: UNVERIFIED above 10MB).
138	- **Filename rules:** any of `< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +` → rejected as Bad Request. Special characters must NOT be percent-encoded — except brackets, which MUST be encoded or the call fails.
139	- **List:** `GET /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/` → `Attachments[]` of `{AttachmentID, FileName, Url, MimeType, ContentLength}`. **Fetch content:** GET the `Url` (`.../Attachments/{Filename}`) — returns raw bytes with the file's `Content-Type` and a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header, no JSON wrapper.
140	- **`?IncludeOnline=true`** on the upload URL makes the attachment visible on the online invoice — but only for **accounts receivable invoices and AR credit notes**. It does not apply to ACCPAY bills, so it is irrelevant to this project's evidence uploads; the response echoes `"IncludeOnline": true` when used.
141	- Parents supporting attachments: Invoices, Receipts, Credit Notes, Repeating Invoices, Bank Transactions, Bank Transfers, Contacts, Accounts, Manual Journals, Purchase Orders, Quotes.
142	
143	## History & Notes — `/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history`
144	
145	> ⚠️ **NOT IN THE MCP SERVER** — raw API only. This is how the agent's decision note lands on a bill/PO.
146	
147	Exact PUT shape (POST behaves identically):
148	
149	```json
150	{ "HistoryRecords": [ { "Details": "Matched to PO-0042; approved under threshold rule." } ] }
151	```
152	
153	- `Details` max **2500 chars**; multiple notes per call allowed.
154	- The note displays the **date of creation** and user **"System Generated"** — notes cannot be backdated, updated, or deleted, and Notes are the only Change type you can create.
155	- GET returns `HistoryRecords[]` of `{Changes, DateUTC, User, Details}` (e.g. `Changes: "Approved"`).
156	- Supported parents: BankTransactions, BatchPayments, BankTransfers (via BankTransactions), Contacts, Creditnotes, Invoices, Items, ManualJournals, Overpayments, Payments, Prepayments, Purchase Orders, Repeating Invoices, Quotes.
157	
158	## Contacts — `/Contacts` (supplier matching fields)
159	
160	| Field | Notes |
161	|---|---|
162	| `ContactID` | the only stable key — Xero warns Name "may no longer be a unique field"; never key on Name |
163	| `Name` | required, max 255; no angle brackets, no leading/trailing/repeated whitespace |
164	| `EmailAddress` | max 255, umlauts not supported |
165	| `IsSupplier` | read-only — auto-set once an AP invoice exists against the contact; **cannot** be set via PUT/POST |
166	| `ContactStatus` | `ACTIVE` / `ARCHIVED` / `GDPRREQUEST` (only ACTIVE is usable in transactions) |
167	
168	- Optimised (equals-only, case- and accent-insensitive): `where=Name="ABC limited"`, `where=EmailAddress="email@example.com"`. Avoid `Contains`/`StartsWith` — unoptimised, and >100k rows → 400.
169	- `?SearchTerm=peter` — case-insensitive across Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress. This is the candidate source for fuzzy matching; the scoring itself lives in the match engine, not here.
170	- **Always page** (`?page=1`): unpaged GET returns a subset of fields. **Never `summaryOnly=true` for matching** — it excludes `IsSupplier`/`IsCustomer`.
171	- Contacts are **not auto-deduplicated by email** — posting an invoice with a new contact *name* creates a NEW contact every time. Look up first, reuse the `ContactID`.
172	
173	## Reports — `GET /Reports/AgedPayablesByContact`
174	
175	| Param | Notes |
176	|---|---|
177	| `ContactID` | **REQUIRED** — guid. Parameter table spells it `contactID`, the docs' example URL uses `ContactID`; casing tolerance UNVERIFIED — match the example: `?ContactID={guid}` |
178	| `date` | payments up to this date; defaults to end of current month |
179	| `fromDate` / `toDate` | payable-invoice window for the contact |
180	
181	Response: `Reports[0].Rows[]`, each `RowType` one of `Header` (column labels: Date, Reference, Due Date, blank, Total, Paid, Credited, Due), `Section` (nests `Rows`), `Row` (data — each Cell `{Value, Attributes}` where `Attributes` carries `{Id:"invoiceID", Value:<guid>}` for drill-down), `SummaryRow` (totals). See `reportDataRows()` in the script for the walk.
182	
183	## Snippets
184	
185	All compile standalone (strict, ES2022, commonjs, `@types/node` only) and import nothing but node builtins and `./xero-fetch`. Run with `npx tsx <file>` from the repo root with the `.env` vars exported into the environment first (tsx does not auto-load `.env`).
186	
187	| File | Shows |
188	|---|---|
189	| `scripts/xero-fetch.ts` | the thin helper: env assertion, token cache, `Accept: application/json`, query encoding, 429/`Retry-After` retry, 400-envelope parsing, `assertElementOk` |
190	| `scripts/bills.ts` | list ACCPAY with `where`/order/page/If-Modified-Since, create bill (PUT + idempotency key + `summarizeErrors=false`), legal status walk |
191	| `scripts/purchase-orders.ts` | PO create/list/get, status update incl. `BILLED` |
192	| `scripts/attachments.ts` | raw-bytes upload onto an invoice, filename guard, list |
193	| `scripts/history-note.ts` | append the agent's decision note (exact PUT shape), read history |
194	| `scripts/suppliers-and-report.ts` | contact lookup by Name/EmailAddress/searchTerm, aged payables + row walker |
195	
196	## Traps (these bite in production)
197	
198	- **ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is non-unique and shows up as "Reference" in the Xero UI.** Existence checks must combine ContactID + InvoiceNumber + Total, or persist `InvoiceID`.
199	- **A 200 is not success on batch writes.** With `summarizeErrors=false`, failed elements hide inside a 200 — assert `StatusAttributeString` per element.
200	- **Omitting `LineItemID`s in an update deletes those lines.** Send every existing line's id, or send no `LineItems` at all for status-only updates.
201	- **Unpaged list calls silently drop data**: multi-invoice GETs lose LineItems; unpaged Contacts lose fields; `summaryOnly` loses `IsSupplier`. Always `?page=`.
202	- **`If-Modified-Since` is not a full change feed** — `IsSupplier`, `Balances`, and DueDate-on-partially-paid edits don't bump `UpdatedDateUTC`.
203	- **Idempotency keys cache errors** for 6 minutes — recover with GET-then-new-key, never blind retry with the same key.
204	- **`unitdp` differs by endpoint**: invoice UnitAmounts are 2dp unless `?unitdp=4`; PO UnitAmounts are natively 4dp. Penny drift between a PO and its bill can be rounding, not fraud — the match engine must compare at consistent precision.
205	- **`AUTHORISED` is a one-way door** — no edits back to DRAFT, no delete, only VOIDED (unpaid only). Do the match BEFORE approving.
206	- **Locked periods reject updates** with a validation error even on otherwise-legal transitions.
207	- **`IncludeOnline` is AR-only** — it does nothing for ACCPAY bills.
208	- **History notes are permanent and timestamped at creation** — never write anything into a note you would not show an auditor.
209	- **The PO endpoint never creates contacts** (unlike invoice creation by contact name, which spawns duplicates) — resolve the ContactID first.
210	
211	## Error → real cause
212	
213	| Symptom | Real cause |
214	|---|---|
215	| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | empty/missing `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` — assert env before building the request |
216	| `invalid_scope` / 403 on a call that used to work | scope missing from app config or token request; scopes live at BOTH ends (see pre-flight 3) |
217	| 403 on `/Reports/...` only | authorising user lacks the 'reports' role |
218	| HTTP 400 + `ApiException` | validation failure — read `Elements[].ValidationErrors[].Message` |
219	| HTTP 200 but the entity never appeared | `summarizeErrors=false` swallowed a per-element `ERROR` — check `StatusAttributeString` |
220	| 429 | rate limit; `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names which; sleep `Retry-After` seconds (helper does) |
221	| 400 `Idempotency Key ... used with a different request` | same key, different URL/body/method within the 6-minute window |
222	| Same error repeats after the fix | cached idempotent error — GET to check existence, retry with a NEW key |
223	| 400 Bad Request on attachment upload | forbidden filename char (`< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +`), or unencoded brackets, or file over the cap |
224	| 400 on a list call | unoptimised `where`/`order` over >100k rows, or unencoded `where` expression |
225	| Line items vanished after an update | `LineItems` array supplied without every existing `LineItemID` |
226	| Can't set status `PAID` | PAID is system-set — create/approve to `AUTHORISED`, then apply a Payment |
227	| Can't move `AUTHORISED` back to `DRAFT` | illegal transition — AUTHORISED only stays or becomes `VOIDED` |
228	| Update rejected on an old bill | locked period, or the bill is partially/fully paid (only the whitelisted fields are editable) |
229	| Duplicate suppliers appearing | invoices posted with a contact *name* instead of a resolved `ContactID` |
230	| History note rejected | `Details` over 2500 chars |
231	
232	<!--
233	Sources (fact sheets verified 2026-07-04 against developer.xero.com, plus cloned repos):
234	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md            — prompt-library skill discipline; error→cause quotes; scope tables; contact-dedup trap
235	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md  — Invoices/PurchaseOrders/Payments/Types pages: fields, status tables, where examples, pagination
236	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-evidence.md    — Attachments/HistoryAndNotes/Reports/Contacts pages: upload mechanics, size-cap conflict, note shape, report rows, contact filters
237	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md        — custom connections, rate limits, requests-and-responses conventions, idempotency guide, 400 envelope example
238	- .tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md                 — AP endpoint quartet (/Invoices bills, /PurchaseOrders, /Payments, /Contacts)
239	- .tmp/xero-refs/mcp.md + xero-mcp-server repo — "no purchase order / attachment / history tools" (verified by grep)
240	Doc URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/{invoices,purchaseorders,attachments,historyandnotes,reports,contacts,types,requests-and-responses,responsecodes}; /documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,limits}; /documentation/guides/idempotent-requests/idempotency
241	-->
242

 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/xero-fetch.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/xero-fetch.ts
2	//
3	// Thin typed HTTP helper for the Xero Accounting API over a Custom Connection
4	// (client_credentials grant, single organisation). Transport only: env
5	// assertion, token caching, JSON Accept header, query encoding, 429 retry
6	// honouring Retry-After, and error-envelope parsing. No business logic —
7	// match/approval decisions live in api/src/modules/.
8	
9	export const ACCOUNTING_BASE = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
10	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
11	
12	// Custom connections set scopes at app creation AND in each token request
13	// body. Override with XERO_SCOPES (space-separated); default mirrors the
14	// scopes named in .env.example.
15	const DEFAULT_SCOPES =
16	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
17	
18	export function requireEnv(name: string): string {
19	  const value = process.env[name];
20	  if (!value || value.trim() === "") {
21	    throw new Error(
22	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — set it in .env at the repo root. ` +
23	        `An empty client id/secret surfaces from Xero as "unauthorized_client", ` +
24	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
25	    );
26	  }
27	  return value;
28	}
29	
30	interface TokenResponse {
31	  access_token: string;
32	  expires_in: number; // seconds; access tokens live at most ~30 minutes
33	  token_type: string;
34	  scope?: string;
35	}
36	
37	let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAtMs: number } | null = null;
38	
39	/** Client-credentials token, cached in-process until 60s before expiry. */
40	export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
41	  if (cachedToken && cachedToken.expiresAtMs > Date.now() + 60_000) {
42	    return cachedToken.token;
43	  }
44	
45	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
46	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
47	  const scope = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || DEFAULT_SCOPES;
48	
49	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
50	    method: "POST",
51	    headers: {
52	      Authorization: `Basic ${Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64")}`,
53	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
54	    },
55	    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope }).toString(),
56	  });
57	
58	  if (!res.ok) {
59	    // Body carries Xero's OAuth error code (e.g. unauthorized_client, invalid_scope).
60	    // Never log the secret itself.
61	    const body = await res.text();
62	    throw new Error(`XeroTokenError: ${res.status} ${body.slice(0, 500)}`);
63	  }
64	
65	  const token = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
66	  cachedToken = {
67	    token: token.access_token,
68	    expiresAtMs: Date.now() + token.expires_in * 1000,
69	  };
70	  return cachedToken.token;
71	}
72	
73	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	// Error envelope (HTTP 400 ApiException, and per-element validation shapes)
75	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	export interface XeroValidationError {
78	  /** The 400 ApiException envelope uses "Message". */
79	  Message?: string;
80	  /** The docs' summarizeErrors=false bulk example uses "Description". */
81	  Description?: string;
82	}
83	
84	export interface XeroErrorElement {
85	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
86	  [key: string]: unknown;
87	}
88	
89	/** HTTP 400 body shape: { ErrorNumber, Type, Message, Elements[].ValidationErrors[] } */
90	export interface XeroApiException {
91	  ErrorNumber?: number;
92	  Type?: string;
93	  Message?: string;
94	  Elements?: XeroErrorElement[];
95	}
96	
97	export function flattenValidationErrors(envelope: XeroApiException): string[] {
98	  const out: string[] = [];
99	  for (const element of envelope.Elements ?? []) {
100	    for (const err of element.ValidationErrors ?? []) {
101	      const text = err.Message ?? err.Description;
102	      if (text) out.push(text);
103	    }
104	  }
105	  return out;
106	}
107	
108	export class XeroHttpError extends Error {
109	  constructor(
110	    readonly status: number,
111	    readonly envelope: XeroApiException | null,
112	    readonly rawBody: string,
113	  ) {
114	    const details = envelope ? flattenValidationErrors(envelope) : [];
115	    super(
116	      `XeroHttpError ${status}: ${envelope?.Message ?? rawBody.slice(0, 300)}` +
117	        (details.length > 0 ? ` | ValidationErrors: ${details.join("; ")}` : ""),
118	    );
119	    this.name = "XeroHttpError";
120	  }
121	}
122	
123	/**
124	 * With ?summarizeErrors=false a batch write returns HTTP 200 even when an
125	 * element failed — each element carries StatusAttributeString OK/WARNING/ERROR.
126	 */
127	export interface StatusAttributed {
128	  StatusAttributeString?: "OK" | "WARNING" | "ERROR";
129	  ValidationErrors?: XeroValidationError[];
130	  Warnings?: XeroValidationError[];
131	}
132	
133	export function assertElementOk<T extends StatusAttributed>(element: T, context: string): T {
134	  if (element.StatusAttributeString === "ERROR") {
135	    const messages = (element.ValidationErrors ?? [])
136	      .map((e) => e.Message ?? e.Description ?? "unknown")
137	      .join("; ");
138	    throw new Error(`XeroValidationError: ${context}: ${messages}`);
139	  }
140	  return element;
141	}
142	
143	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
144	// Core request
145	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	export interface XeroRequestInit {
148	  method?: "GET" | "PUT" | "POST";
149	  /** JSON payload — serialised and sent with Content-Type: application/json. */
150	  json?: unknown;
151	  /** Raw bytes (attachment uploads). Requires contentType. */
152	  body?: Uint8Array;
153	  /** Content-Type for raw `body` uploads — must be the file's MIME type. */
154	  contentType?: string;
155	  /** Query params (where, order, page, pageSize, ...). Values are URL-encoded here. */
156	  query?: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined>;
157	  /** Sent as Idempotency-Key. POST/PUT only; Xero caches 6 min; max 128 chars. */
158	  idempotencyKey?: string;
159	  /** Sent as If-Modified-Since (UTC, accurate to the second). */
160	  ifModifiedSince?: Date;
161	  /** Override Accept. Default application/json (Xero's default is XML). */
162	  accept?: string;
163	}
164	
165	const MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES = 3;
166	
167	function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
168	  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
169	}
170	
171	/**
172	 * Low-level call: auth + headers + query encoding + 429 retry. Throws
173	 * XeroHttpError on any non-2xx (with the parsed ApiException when present).
174	 *
175	 * No xero-tenant-id header: a Custom Connection is bound to exactly ONE
176	 * organisation, so the tenant is implicit — the documented custom-connection
177	 * call carries only Authorization + Accept (see SKILL.md).
178	 */
179	export async function xeroRequest(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<Response> {
180	  const token = await getAccessToken();
181	
182	  const url = new URL(`${ACCOUNTING_BASE}${path}`);
183	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(init.query ?? {})) {
184	    if (value !== undefined) url.searchParams.set(key, String(value));
185	  }
186	
187	  const headers: Record<string, string> = {
188	    Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
189	    Accept: init.accept ?? "application/json",
190	  };
191	  if (init.json !== undefined) headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
192	  if (init.body !== undefined) {
193	    if (!init.contentType) {
194	      throw new Error("MissingContentType: raw body uploads must set the file's MIME type");
195	    }
196	    headers["Content-Type"] = init.contentType;
197	  }
198	  if (init.idempotencyKey !== undefined) {
199	    if (init.idempotencyKey.length > 128) {
200	      throw new Error(
201	        `IdempotencyKeyTooLong: ${init.idempotencyKey.length} chars — Xero rejects keys over 128 with a 400`,
202	      );
203	    }
204	    headers["Idempotency-Key"] = init.idempotencyKey;
205	  }
206	  if (init.ifModifiedSince !== undefined) {
207	    headers["If-Modified-Since"] = init.ifModifiedSince.toUTCString();
208	  }
209	
210	  let body: string | ArrayBuffer | undefined;
211	  if (init.json !== undefined) {
212	    body = JSON.stringify(init.json);
213	  } else if (init.body !== undefined) {
214	    // Copy into a plain ArrayBuffer so the bytes satisfy fetch's BodyInit
215	    // regardless of the Uint8Array's backing buffer type.
216	    const buffer = new ArrayBuffer(init.body.byteLength);
217	    new Uint8Array(buffer).set(init.body);
218	    body = buffer;
219	  }
220	
221	  for (let attempt = 0; ; attempt++) {
222	    const res = await fetch(url, { method: init.method ?? "GET", headers, body });
223	
224	    if (res.status === 429 && attempt < MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES) {
225	      // Xero sends Retry-After (seconds) on minute/daily limits and
226	      // X-Rate-Limit-Problem naming the limit hit. Honour Retry-After.
227	      const retryAfterSec = Number(res.headers.get("retry-after") ?? "0");
228	      const waitMs = retryAfterSec > 0 ? retryAfterSec * 1000 : 1_000;
229	      console.warn(
230	        `[xero] 429 (limit: ${res.headers.get("x-rate-limit-problem") ?? "unknown"}) — retrying in ${waitMs}ms`,
231	      );
232	      await sleep(waitMs);
233	      continue;
234	    }
235	
236	    if (!res.ok) {
237	      const raw = await res.text();
238	      let envelope: XeroApiException | null = null;
239	      try {
240	        envelope = JSON.parse(raw) as XeroApiException;
241	      } catch {
242	        envelope = null;
243	      }
244	      throw new XeroHttpError(res.status, envelope, raw);
245	    }
246	
247	    return res;
248	  }
249	}
250	
251	/** JSON call — the default for everything except raw attachment content. */
252	export async function xeroFetch<T>(path: string, init: XeroRequestInit = {}): Promise<T> {
253	  const res = await xeroRequest(path, init);
254	  return (await res.json()) as T;
255	}
256
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/bills.ts
2	//
3	// ACCPAY bills on the /Invoices endpoint: filtered list (where/order/page),
4	// create (PUT), and the DRAFT -> SUBMITTED -> AUTHORISED status walk (POST).
5	// Shapes trimmed to the fields this project reads/writes. Transport facts
6	// only — matching and approval decisions live in api/src/modules/.
7	
8	import { assertElementOk, xeroFetch, type StatusAttributed } from "./xero-fetch";
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Trimmed shapes
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	export type InvoiceStatus = "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "PAID" | "VOIDED" | "DELETED";
15	export type LineAmountTypes = "Exclusive" | "Inclusive" | "NoTax";
16	
17	export interface BillLineItem {
18	  /** Omit on create. On update: any existing line whose LineItemID is missing
19	   *  from a supplied LineItems array is DELETED and recreated. */
20	  LineItemID?: string;
21	  Description: string; // 1..4000 chars; a line can be Description-only
22	  Quantity?: number;
23	  UnitAmount?: number; // rounded to 2dp unless the request uses ?unitdp=4
24	  AccountCode?: string;
25	  TaxType?: string; // override only when the AccountCode default is wrong
26	  LineAmount?: number;
27	}
28	
29	export interface Bill {
30	  InvoiceID?: string;
31	  Type: "ACCPAY";
32	  Contact: { ContactID: string }; // only ContactID on create/update
33	  Date?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today in the org timezone
34	  DueDate?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
35	  /** NON-unique for ACCPAY — displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI. Max 255. */
36	  InvoiceNumber?: string;
37	  Status?: InvoiceStatus; // defaults to DRAFT on create
38	  LineAmountTypes?: LineAmountTypes; // defaults to Exclusive when omitted
39	  LineItems: BillLineItem[];
40	  SubTotal?: number;
41	  TotalTax?: number;
42	  Total?: number; // SubTotal + TotalTax (computed)
43	  AmountDue?: number;
44	  AmountPaid?: number;
45	  CurrencyCode?: string; // defaults to the org base currency
46	  UpdatedDateUTC?: string; // "/Date(1439434356790)/" — unix ms inside
47	}
48	
49	export interface Pagination {
50	  page: number;
51	  pageSize: number;
52	  pageCount: number;
53	  itemCount: number;
54	}
55	
56	export interface InvoicesResponse {
57	  Invoices: Array<Bill & StatusAttributed>;
58	  pagination?: Pagination;
59	}
60	
61	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	// List bills — where/order/page
63	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	
65	/**
66	 * where strings use double quotes and == (official invoice filter example:
67	 * Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="AUTHORISED"). Percent-encoding is handled by the
68	 * helper's query serialiser. Always page: paged responses include full
69	 * LineItems; unpaged multi-invoice GETs return NO line details.
70	 */
71	export async function listBills(
72	  options: {
73	    status?: InvoiceStatus;
74	    page?: number;
75	    pageSize?: number; // default 100, max 1000
76	    modifiedSince?: Date;
77	  } = {},
78	): Promise<InvoicesResponse> {
79	  const where = options.status
80	    ? `Type=="ACCPAY" AND Status=="${options.status}"`
81	    : `Type=="ACCPAY"`;
82	  return xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>("/Invoices", {
83	    query: {
84	      where,
85	      // Optimised order fields on Invoices: InvoiceId, UpdatedDateUTC, Date.
86	      order: "UpdatedDateUTC ASC",
87	      page: options.page ?? 1,
88	      pageSize: options.pageSize,
89	    },
90	    ifModifiedSince: options.modifiedSince,
91	  });
92	}
93	
94	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	// Create — PUT creates only; POST creates or updates
96	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	/**
99	 * Creates a bill (status defaults to DRAFT). summarizeErrors=false means a
100	 * validation failure comes back as HTTP 200 with a per-element
101	 * StatusAttributeString of ERROR — always assert it.
102	 *
103	 * The idempotency key must be stable per logical write and persisted by the
104	 * caller (Xero caches it for 6 minutes; same key + different body → 400).
105	 */
106	export async function createBill(
107	  bill: Omit<Bill, "InvoiceID">,
108	  idempotencyKey: string,
109	): Promise<Bill> {
110	  const res = await xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>("/Invoices", {
111	    method: "PUT",
112	    json: { Invoices: [bill] },
113	    query: { summarizeErrors: false, unitdp: 4 },
114	    idempotencyKey,
115	  });
116	  const created = res.Invoices[0];
117	  if (!created) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: PUT /Invoices returned no Invoices element");
118	  return assertElementOk(created, `create bill ${bill.InvoiceNumber ?? "(no number)"}`);
119	}
120	
121	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122	// Status transitions — POST /Invoices/{InvoiceID}
123	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
124	
125	/**
126	 * Legal transitions (from the official Invoices status table):
127	 *   DRAFT     -> DRAFT | SUBMITTED | AUTHORISED | DELETED
128	 *   SUBMITTED -> SUBMITTED | AUTHORISED | DRAFT | DELETED
129	 *   AUTHORISED -> AUTHORISED | VOIDED   (never back to DRAFT/SUBMITTED)
130	 * PAID is system-set when fully paid — never write it. Status-only updates
131	 * send no LineItems array (sending one without every LineItemID deletes lines).
132	 */
133	export async function setBillStatus(
134	  invoiceId: string,
135	  status: "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "DELETED" | "VOIDED",
136	  idempotencyKey: string,
137	): Promise<Bill> {
138	  const res = await xeroFetch<InvoicesResponse>(`/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
139	    method: "POST",
140	    json: { Invoices: [{ InvoiceID: invoiceId, Status: status }] },
141	    idempotencyKey,
142	  });
143	  const updated = res.Invoices[0];
144	  if (!updated) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: POST /Invoices returned no Invoices element");
145	  return updated;
146	}
147	
148	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	// Demo entrypoint: npx tsx bills.ts  (lists first page of ACCPAY bills)
150	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	async function main(): Promise<void> {
153	  const res = await listBills({ page: 1, pageSize: 10 });
154	  console.log(`Fetched ${res.Invoices.length} bills (pagination:`, res.pagination, ")");
155	  for (const bill of res.Invoices) {
156	    console.log(`- ${bill.InvoiceNumber ?? "(no number)"} ${bill.Status} total=${bill.Total}`);
157	  }
158	}
159	
160	if (require.main === module) {
161	  main().catch((err) => {
162	    console.error(err);
163	    process.exitCode = 1;
164	  });
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>
