<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-payments/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-payments
3	description: Create a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY bill via the raw Xero Accounting API (PUT /Payments, plain fetch, Custom Connection client_credentials — no tenant id). Use when recording a bill payment in Xero, checking why a payment write was rejected, verifying the payment-account prerequisite, or reversing a payment. Covers required fields (Invoice.InvoiceID, Account.Code/AccountID, Date, Amount), the BANK-or-payments-enabled account rule, partial vs full payment effects on invoice Status/AmountDue, idempotency discipline, and the rejection causes that bite. Read this BEFORE writing any payment code; do NOT use the MCP server's create-payment (no idempotency — see xero-mcp-usage skill).
4	---
5	
6	# Paying an ACCPAY bill: `PUT /Payments`
7	
8	Money moves outside Xero (bank rail). Xero is the ledger: a Payment row records that the bill was paid. This skill is facts + runnable snippets only — the decision to pay lives in `api/src/modules/`, never here.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	Each check prevents a misleading upstream error.
13	
14	1. **Assert secrets non-empty.** Empty `XERO_CLIENT_ID` surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), pointing away from the real cause. At boot:
15	
16	```ts
17	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
18	  const v = process.env[name];
19	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
20	    throw new Error(`MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. Set it in .env and retry.`);
21	  }
22	  return v;
23	}
24	```
25	
26	2. **Request BOTH scopes in the token body.** The pre-flight invoice GET needs `accounting.invoices`; the write needs `accounting.payments` (grants Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments). Granular scopes apply to custom connections since 29 April 2026 — use granular, not `accounting.transactions`. Missing write scope → 403 at `PUT /Payments`, and a token's scope cannot be widened — mint a new token.
27	
28	3. **Fact-check the payment account once per org.** The account must be type `BANK` **or** have "enable payments to this account" switched on (edit account screen, Chart of Accounts). Check via `where` filter (GET /Accounts likely needs `accounting.settings.read` — scope-to-Accounts mapping UNVERIFIED — check before relying; if you get 403 here, add that scope):
29	
30	```ts
31	const where = encodeURIComponent(`Code=="${accountCode}"`);
32	const res = await fetch(`https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts?where=${where}`, {
33	  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" },
34	});
35	const { Accounts } = (await res.json()) as { Accounts?: Array<{ Type?: string }> };
36	const acc = Accounts?.[0];
37	if (!acc) throw new Error(`AccountNotFound: no account with Code ${accountCode}`);
38	if (acc.Type !== "BANK") console.warn(`[xero] account is ${acc.Type} — confirm payments are enabled on it`, acc);
39	```
40	
41	4. **Fact-check the invoice: `Status === "AUTHORISED"` and `AmountDue >= amount`.** Built into `scripts/create-payment.ts` below — it GETs the invoice and fails with `InvoiceNotAuthorised` / `AmountExceedsAmountDue` before attempting the write. This restates Xero's documented constraints as named local errors; it is not approval logic.
42	
43	## Auth: why there is no tenant id
44	
45	Custom Connection = `client_credentials` grant, bound to **one** Xero organisation ("access data from a single Xero organisation" — multi-org needs code/PKCE flow instead). One org means nothing to select, so no `XERO_TENANT_ID` env var and no `xero-tenant-id` header: the documented custom-connection API call carries only `Authorization: Bearer` + `Accept`. (The docs omit the header from the canonical example; an explicit "not required" sentence does not appear — UNVERIFIED — check before relying.)
46	
47	Token: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` with `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`, body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...` (scopes in the BODY — differs from code/PKCE where scopes ride in the URL). Access token is a JWT, valid up to 30 minutes. Only `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are needed, from `.env`, never committed, never logged (log `client_id_len`, not the value).
48	
49	## The write
50	
51	Endpoint: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments` — **PUT creates. POST deletes (reverses). There is no update** ("Payments cannot be modified, only created and deleted").
52	
53	Minimum body (official single-payment example shape):
54	
55	```json
56	{
57	  "Invoice": { "InvoiceID": "96df0dff-43ec-4899-a7d9-e9d63ef12b19" },
58	  "Account": { "Code": "001" },
59	  "Date": "2026-07-04",
60	  "Amount": 32.06
61	}
62	```
63	
64	| Field | Facts |
65	|---|---|
66	| `Invoice` | Exactly ONE identifier object per payment (Invoice, CreditNote, Prepayment, or Overpayment). Identify by `InvoiceID`. `InvoiceNumber` also works but is NON-unique on ACCPAY — always use `InvoiceID`. |
67	| `Account` | `Code` or `AccountID`. "Not all accounts have a code value" — `AccountID` is always safe. Must satisfy the BANK-or-payments-enabled prerequisite. |
68	| `Date` | Date the payment is being made, `YYYY-MM-DD`. |
69	| `Amount` | In the **invoice's** currency. Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing (`AmountDue`). |
70	| Optional | `Reference`, `IsReconciled`, `BankAmount`, `CurrencyRate`, `Status`. |
71	
72	Envelope: single object, or `{"Payments": [...]}` for multiple (add `?summarizeErrors=false` for per-element `ValidationErrors`). Multiple bills in one transaction → `BatchPayments` endpoint instead. Send `Accept: application/json` or you get XML.
73	
74	### Partial vs full payment
75	
76	- Partial payments are allowed (any Amount ≤ AmountDue). Invoice `Status` stays `AUTHORISED` — the enum's own definition: "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid". `AmountDue` drops, `AmountPaid` rises (computed fields).
77	- Full payment: "Once an invoice is fully paid the status will change to PAID." `PAID` is system-set — you cannot write it. Creating a paid bill is always two steps: create/approve to `AUTHORISED`, then `PUT /Payments`.
78	- After partial/full payment, an ACCPAY bill is mostly frozen: only Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (with exceptions), Url, line Description/AccountCode (non-CIS), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate remain updatable.
79	
80	### Created payment
81	
82	Response envelope `{"Payments": [...]}` with `PaymentID`. Payment status codes: `AUTHORISED`, `DELETED`. A bill payment shows PaymentType `ACCPAYPAYMENT`. Reverse with `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` body `{"Status": "DELETED"}` (payments created via batch payments and receipts are excluded from this delete method).
83	
84	### Multicurrency
85	
86	A payment is multicurrency if the payment account or the invoice is in a non-base currency. `Amount` is always invoice-currency. If the bank account currency differs, set `BankAmount` (account currency) and let Xero calculate `CurrencyRate` — "If you know this amount, specifying it is preferred to setting CurrencyRate". Non-multicurrency payments have `CurrencyRate` 1; a CurrencyRate validation warning may be returned if the rate appears incorrect.
87	
88	## Runnable snippet
89	
90	`scripts/create-payment.ts` — standalone (strict TS, commonjs, Node 18+ fetch, zero deps; compile-verified):
91	
92	```
93	ts-node scripts/create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE]
94	```
95	
96	It: asserts env → gets a client_credentials token → GETs the invoice and fails with named errors unless `AUTHORISED` and `AmountDue >= amount` → skips if a non-deleted payment with the same `Reference` already exists on the invoice (check-by-reference) → PUTs the payment with a deterministic `Idempotency-Key` → prints `PaymentID` → re-GETs the invoice and prints new `Status`/`AmountDue`/`AmountPaid`. 3 API calls + 1 token call — well under the 60/min tenant limit.
97	
98	## Traps
99	
100	- **Paying a non-AUTHORISED invoice.** Payments apply to approved AR/AP invoices only; `DRAFT`/`SUBMITTED` create no journals and cannot take payments. Approve first: `POST /Invoices/{InvoiceID}` with `{"Status": "AUTHORISED"}` (legal from DRAFT and SUBMITTED; the MCP server cannot do this — raw API only). Exact ValidationError text for this rejection is not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
101	- **Wrong account class.** Non-`BANK` account without payments enabled → validation rejection (exact message not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying). Fix in the UI (edit account screen) or via the Accounts API — SDK field `enablePaymentsToAccount` (confirmed in XeroAPI/xero-command-line `accounts update`); raw JSON property name `EnablePaymentsToAccount` UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
102	- **Currency mismatch.** `Amount` is invoice-currency, not account-currency. A currency the org is not subscribed to fails validation — documented example message: `Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR`. Cross-currency: prefer `BankAmount` over hand-set `CurrencyRate`; a suspect rate returns a validation warning.
103	- **Amount exceeding AmountDue.** Documented constraint: "Must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice". Exact rejection message not published — UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Always compare against a FRESH `AmountDue` (another partial payment may have landed since your cache).
104	- **Idempotency: check-by-reference BEFORE create.** Project rule: every Xero write checks for an existing record first. Give each payment a unique `Reference`, check the invoice's `Payments` array before writing, persist the returned `PaymentID` in Postgres. The `Idempotency-Key` header is only a retry guard: keys expire after **6 minutes**, max **128 chars**, same key + different request → 400 `Idempotency Key: KEY_VALUE is used with a different request.`, and **errors are cached** — a rerun with the same key can replay a stale error after the real cause is fixed (GET to check, then retry with a new key).
105	- **PUT/POST are inverted vs habit.** `PUT /Payments` creates; `POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` deletes. Nothing updates a payment — to correct one, delete and re-create.
106	- **`PAID` cannot be written and `AUTHORISED` cannot be re-drafted.** PAID is system-set on full payment. An AUTHORISED invoice can only stay AUTHORISED or go VOIDED (and voiding is shown only for invoices with no payments applied) — delete the payment first if you must void.
107	- **Batch responses lie about success.** With `summarizeErrors=false`, validation failures come back **HTTP 200** with per-element `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` — check `ValidationErrors` per element, never the HTTP status alone.
108	
109	## Error → real cause
110	
111	| You see | Real cause |
112	|---|---|
113	| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` empty or wrong — `client_id=undefined` was sent. Assert env first. |
114	| `invalid_scope` (HTTP 400) from the token endpoint | Scope string not granted to the app (e.g. broad scope on a post-Apr-2026 custom connection). Request granular: `accounting.invoices accounting.payments`. |
115	| `403` on the API call | Token missing the needed scope. Scopes cannot be widened on an existing token — mint a new one with the right scope (and match the app config). |
116	| `400` with `"Type": "ValidationException"` (`ErrorNumber` 10) | Field-level rejection — the truth is in `Elements[].ValidationErrors[].Message`, not the top-level message. |
117	| HTTP 200 but element has `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` | You sent `summarizeErrors=false`; per-element `ValidationErrors` carry the failure. |
118	| `400` `Idempotency Key: ... is used with a different request.` | Same key, changed URL/body/method. Persist the key with the record and retry byte-identically, or use a new key. |
119	| Same error repeats after the cause is fixed | Cached idempotent error (keys live 6 min). GET to check whether the payment exists; if not, re-create with a NEW key. |
120	| `429` + `Retry-After` header | Rate limit: 60/min per tenant, 5 concurrent, 1,000–5,000/day. `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names the limit hit; wait `Retry-After` seconds. |
121	| XML instead of JSON | Missing `Accept: application/json` — XML is the Accounting API default. |
122	| `404` on the invoice GET | Wrong `InvoiceID`, or wrong path — the real path is `/api.xro/2.0/Invoices/{InvoiceID}` (PascalCase resource names; friendly alias paths 404). |
123	
124	<!--
125	Sources (fact-checked 2026-07-04):
126	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md — §4 Payments (min body, one-identifier rule, Account.Code/AccountID, Amount<=outstanding, account BANK/payments-enabled prerequisite, partial->AUTHORISED / full->PAID, PUT create / POST delete, BankAmount/CurrencyRate, "Organisation is not subscribed to currency EUR" example, Payment statuses AUTHORISED|DELETED, ACCPAYPAYMENT), §1 Invoices (status transition table, AUTHORISED="apply payments", ACCPAY InvoiceNumber non-unique, post-payment updatable fields, summaryOnly excludes Payments — basis for the invoice Payments array check).
127	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — §1 Custom Connections (token endpoint, Basic auth, scope in body, single-org quote, two-header call example / xero-tenant-id omission marked UNVERIFIED, 30-min token), §2 rate limits + 429/Retry-After/X-Rate-Limit-Problem, §3 request conventions (XML default, where-filter encoding, summarizeErrors semantics, ApiException envelope, PUT-vs-POST), §4 Idempotency-Key (6 min, 128 chars, different-request 400 message, errors cached).
128	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md — skill format, pre-flight discipline, requireEnv/unauthorized_client causal chain, client_id_len logging, scope grants table (accounting.invoices / accounting.payments), granular-scope dates, idempotency "rules that bite".
129	- .tmp/xero-refs/mcp.md — §4 create-payment passes idempotencyKey undefined, §5 update-invoice cannot set status (why approval must be raw API), §3 custom-connection env (XERO_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, no tenant env).
130	- .tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md — AP surface = /Invoices (bills) + /PurchaseOrders + /Payments + /Contacts; Xero is the ledger, money moves on external rails.
131	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-command-line/src/commands/accounts/update.ts:57,73 + src/lib/validators.ts:181 — enablePaymentsToAccount SDK field.
132	- scripts/create-payment.ts compile-verified: tsc 6.0.3, --strict --target ES2022 --module commonjs --esModuleInterop --types node.
133	-->

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/purchase-orders.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/purchase-orders.ts
2	//
3	// Purchase Orders — NOT in the Xero MCP server (zero purchase-order tools);
4	// the raw Accounting API is the ONLY path. Create (PUT), list (explicit
5	// params — a generic `where` is not documented for POs), and status updates
6	// (POST), including marking billed. Transport facts only.
7	
8	import { assertElementOk, xeroFetch, type StatusAttributed } from "./xero-fetch";
9	import type { Pagination } from "./bills";
10	
11	export type PurchaseOrderStatus = "DRAFT" | "SUBMITTED" | "AUTHORISED" | "BILLED" | "DELETED";
12	
13	export interface PurchaseOrderLineItem {
14	  /** Same rule as invoices: on update, lines missing their LineItemID are deleted. */
15	  LineItemID?: string;
16	  Description: string; // a line can be Description-only
17	  Quantity?: number; // if Quantity is specified, UnitAmount must be too
18	  UnitAmount?: number; // POs round UnitAmount to FOUR decimal places
19	  AccountCode?: string;
20	  TaxType?: string;
21	  LineAmount?: number;
22	}
23	
24	export interface PurchaseOrder {
25	  PurchaseOrderID?: string;
26	  /** Unique alphanumeric; auto-generated from org invoice settings when missing. */
27	  PurchaseOrderNumber?: string;
28	  /** Must reference an EXISTING contact — this endpoint never creates contacts. */
29	  Contact: { ContactID: string };
30	  Date?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today in the org timezone
31	  DeliveryDate?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
32	  Reference?: string;
33	  Status?: PurchaseOrderStatus; // examples create DRAFT by default or pass AUTHORISED
34	  LineAmountTypes?: "Exclusive" | "Inclusive" | "NoTax"; // Exclusive when omitted
35	  LineItems: PurchaseOrderLineItem[];
36	  SubTotal?: number;
37	  TotalTax?: number;
38	  Total?: number;
39	  CurrencyCode?: string;
40	  UpdatedDateUTC?: string;
41	}
42	
43	export interface PurchaseOrdersResponse {
44	  PurchaseOrders: Array<PurchaseOrder & StatusAttributed>;
45	  pagination?: Pagination;
46	}
47	
48	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	// List — paging is enforced by default on POs (100/page, pageSize up to 1000)
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	
52	export async function listPurchaseOrders(
53	  options: {
54	    status?: PurchaseOrderStatus; // explicit Status param, e.g. ?status=DRAFT
55	    dateFrom?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
56	    dateTo?: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
57	    page?: number;
58	    pageSize?: number;
59	    modifiedSince?: Date;
60	  } = {},
61	): Promise<PurchaseOrdersResponse> {
62	  return xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>("/PurchaseOrders", {
63	    query: {
64	      Status: options.status,
65	      DateFrom: options.dateFrom,
66	      DateTo: options.dateTo,
67	      page: options.page ?? 1,
68	      pageSize: options.pageSize,
69	    },
70	    ifModifiedSince: options.modifiedSince,
71	  });
72	}
73	
74	export async function getPurchaseOrder(purchaseOrderId: string): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
75	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>(`/PurchaseOrders/${purchaseOrderId}`);
76	  const po = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
77	  if (!po) throw new Error(`XeroNotFound: PurchaseOrder ${purchaseOrderId} not in response`);
78	  return po;
79	}
80	
81	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	// Create — PUT creates only; POST creates or updates
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	
85	export async function createPurchaseOrder(
86	  purchaseOrder: Omit<PurchaseOrder, "PurchaseOrderID">,
87	  idempotencyKey: string,
88	): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
89	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>("/PurchaseOrders", {
90	    method: "PUT",
91	    json: { PurchaseOrders: [purchaseOrder] },
92	    query: { summarizeErrors: false },
93	    idempotencyKey,
94	  });
95	  const created = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
96	  if (!created) {
97	    throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: PUT /PurchaseOrders returned no PurchaseOrders element");
98	  }
99	  return assertElementOk(created, `create PO ${purchaseOrder.PurchaseOrderNumber ?? "(auto)"}`);
100	}
101	
102	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	// Status updates — POST with the new Status (official example: DRAFT -> DELETED)
104	// Marking billed = Status "BILLED" via the same mechanism. Xero publishes no
105	// PO transition table, so verify each transition against a live org.
106	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	export async function setPurchaseOrderStatus(
109	  purchaseOrderId: string,
110	  status: PurchaseOrderStatus,
111	  idempotencyKey: string,
112	): Promise<PurchaseOrder> {
113	  const res = await xeroFetch<PurchaseOrdersResponse>(`/PurchaseOrders/${purchaseOrderId}`, {
114	    method: "POST",
115	    json: { PurchaseOrders: [{ PurchaseOrderID: purchaseOrderId, Status: status }] },
116	    idempotencyKey,
117	  });
118	  const updated = res.PurchaseOrders[0];
119	  if (!updated) {
120	    throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: POST /PurchaseOrders returned no PurchaseOrders element");
121	  }
122	  return updated;
123	}
124	
125	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
126	// Demo entrypoint: npx tsx purchase-orders.ts  (lists first page of POs)
127	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	
129	async function main(): Promise<void> {
130	  const res = await listPurchaseOrders({ page: 1, pageSize: 10 });
131	  console.log(`Fetched ${res.PurchaseOrders.length} purchase orders`);
132	  for (const po of res.PurchaseOrders) {
133	    console.log(`- ${po.PurchaseOrderNumber} ${po.Status} total=${po.Total}`);
134	  }
135	}
136	
137	if (require.main === module) {
138	  main().catch((err) => {
139	    console.error(err);
140	    process.exitCode = 1;
141	  });
142	}
143
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-payments/scripts/create-payment.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-payments/scripts/create-payment.ts
2	//
3	// Create a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY invoice via the raw Accounting API.
4	// Custom connection (client_credentials): the token is bound to exactly ONE Xero
5	// organisation, so no xero-tenant-id header and no XERO_TENANT_ID env var.
6	//
7	// Usage:
8	//   ts-node create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE]
9	//   or via env: INVOICE_ID, ACCOUNT_CODE, AMOUNT, PAYMENT_DATE, PAYMENT_REFERENCE
10	//
11	// Env (export from .env at repo root before running; never commit, never log):
12	//   XERO_CLIENT_ID, XERO_CLIENT_SECRET   required
13	//   XERO_SCOPES                          optional, default "accounting.invoices accounting.payments"
14	//
15	// Compiles standalone: strict, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, types=["node"].
16	// Node 18+ (native fetch). No npm deps.
17	
18	const XERO_TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
19	const XERO_API = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
20	
21	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
22	  const v = process.env[name];
23	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
24	    throw new Error(
25	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. Set it in .env and retry.`,
26	    );
27	  }
28	  return v;
29	}
30	
31	function arg(pos: number, envName: string): string | undefined {
32	  return process.argv[2 + pos] ?? process.env[envName];
33	}
34	
35	interface ValidationError {
36	  Message?: string;
37	}
38	interface ApiException {
39	  ErrorNumber?: number;
40	  Type?: string;
41	  Message?: string;
42	  Elements?: Array<{ ValidationErrors?: ValidationError[] }>;
43	}
44	interface XeroPayment {
45	  PaymentID?: string;
46	  Status?: string;
47	  Amount?: number;
48	  Date?: string;
49	  Reference?: string;
50	}
51	interface XeroInvoice {
52	  InvoiceID?: string;
53	  Type?: string;
54	  Status?: string;
55	  AmountDue?: number;
56	  AmountPaid?: number;
57	  CurrencyCode?: string;
58	  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
59	}
60	interface InvoiceEnvelope {
61	  Invoices?: XeroInvoice[];
62	}
63	interface PaymentEnvelope {
64	  Payments?: XeroPayment[];
65	}
66	
67	async function getToken(clientId: string, clientSecret: string, scopes: string): Promise<string> {
68	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
69	  const res = await fetch(XERO_TOKEN_URL, {
70	    method: "POST",
71	    headers: {
72	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
73	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
74	    },
75	    // Custom connections take scopes in the BODY (unlike code/PKCE flows, where
76	    // scopes ride in the authorize URL).
77	    body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "client_credentials", scope: scopes }).toString(),
78	  });
79	  if (!res.ok) {
80	    throw new Error(`TokenError: ${res.status} from identity.xero.com — ${await res.text()}`);
81	  }
82	  const json = (await res.json()) as { access_token?: string };
83	  if (!json.access_token) {
84	    throw new Error("TokenError: token response contained no access_token");
85	  }
86	  return json.access_token;
87	}
88	
89	async function getInvoice(token: string, invoiceId: string): Promise<XeroInvoice> {
90	  // No xero-tenant-id header: a custom connection token is scoped to a single org.
91	  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Invoices/${invoiceId}`, {
92	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, Accept: "application/json" },
93	  });
94	  if (res.status === 404) {
95	    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: no invoice with InvoiceID ${invoiceId}`);
96	  }
97	  if (!res.ok) {
98	    throw new Error(`InvoiceFetchError: ${res.status} — ${await res.text()}`);
99	  }
100	  const json = (await res.json()) as InvoiceEnvelope;
101	  const inv = json.Invoices?.[0];
102	  if (!inv) {
103	    throw new Error(`InvoiceNotFound: empty Invoices envelope for ${invoiceId}`);
104	  }
105	  return inv;
106	}
107	
108	function formatRejection(status: number, bodyText: string): string {
109	  try {
110	    const api = JSON.parse(bodyText) as ApiException;
111	    const msgs = (api.Elements ?? [])
112	      .flatMap((e) => e.ValidationErrors ?? [])
113	      .map((v) => v.Message)
114	      .filter((m): m is string => typeof m === "string");
115	    if (msgs.length > 0) {
116	      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} ${api.Type ?? ""} — ${msgs.join(" | ")}`;
117	    }
118	    if (api.Message) {
119	      return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${api.Message}`;
120	    }
121	  } catch {
122	    // not JSON — fall through to raw body
123	  }
124	  return `PaymentRejected: ${status} — ${bodyText}`;
125	}
126	
127	async function main(): Promise<void> {
128	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
129	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
130	  const scopes = process.env.XERO_SCOPES?.trim() || "accounting.invoices accounting.payments";
131	
132	  const invoiceId = arg(0, "INVOICE_ID");
133	  const accountCode = arg(1, "ACCOUNT_CODE");
134	  const amountRaw = arg(2, "AMOUNT");
135	  const date = arg(3, "PAYMENT_DATE") ?? new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD
136	  const reference = arg(4, "PAYMENT_REFERENCE");
137	
138	  if (!invoiceId || !accountCode || !amountRaw) {
139	    throw new Error(
140	      "Usage: create-payment.ts <INVOICE_ID> <ACCOUNT_CODE> <AMOUNT> [DATE] [REFERENCE] (or set INVOICE_ID/ACCOUNT_CODE/AMOUNT env vars)",
141	    );
142	  }
143	  const amount = Number(amountRaw);
144	  if (!Number.isFinite(amount) || amount <= 0) {
145	    throw new Error(`BadAmount: "${amountRaw}" is not a positive number`);
146	  }
147	
148	  // Never log the secret — length only.
149	  console.log("[xero] token request", { client_id_len: clientId.length, scopes });
150	  const token = await getToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
151	
152	  // ---- Pre-flight FACT-CHECK (not business logic) --------------------------
153	  // These asserts restate Xero's documented constraints so a bad write fails
154	  // here with a named local error instead of an opaque ValidationException.
155	  // The decision to pay was made upstream in api/src/modules/ — nothing below
156	  // decides anything.
157	  const inv = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);
158	
159	  if (inv.Status !== "AUTHORISED") {
160	    throw new Error(
161	      `InvoiceNotAuthorised: invoice ${invoiceId} has status ${inv.Status ?? "unknown"} — ` +
162	        `Xero applies payments to AUTHORISED invoices only (DRAFT/SUBMITTED create no journals). ` +
163	        `Approve it first: POST /Invoices/${invoiceId} {"Status":"AUTHORISED"}.`,
164	    );
165	  }
166	  if (typeof inv.AmountDue !== "number" || inv.AmountDue < amount) {
167	    throw new Error(
168	      `AmountExceedsAmountDue: AmountDue is ${inv.AmountDue ?? "unknown"}, requested ${amount} — ` +
169	        `Amount must be less than or equal to the outstanding amount owing on the invoice.`,
170	    );
171	  }
172	
173	  // Idempotency check-by-reference: this project requires every Xero write to
174	  // check for an existing record before creating. Best-effort here via the
175	  // invoice's Payments array; the durable record is PaymentID persisted in
176	  // Postgres by the caller. Pass a unique REFERENCE to make reruns no-ops.
177	  if (reference) {
178	    const existing = (inv.Payments ?? []).find(
179	      (p) => p.Reference === reference && p.Status !== "DELETED",
180	    );
181	    if (existing) {
182	      console.log(
183	        `[xero] payment already exists — idempotent skip. PaymentID=${existing.PaymentID ?? "?"} Reference=${reference}`,
184	      );
185	      console.log(`[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${inv.Status} AmountDue=${inv.AmountDue}`);
186	      return;
187	    }
188	  }
189	
190	  // ---- The write. PUT /Payments creates; POST /Payments/{id} deletes. ------
191	  const payment = {
192	    Invoice: { InvoiceID: invoiceId },
193	    Account: { Code: accountCode },
194	    Date: date,
195	    Amount: amount,
196	    ...(reference ? { Reference: reference } : {}),
197	  };
198	  // Deterministic Idempotency-Key: identical retries within Xero's 6-minute
199	  // cache window return the cached response instead of double-paying. This is
200	  // a retry guard only — the durable guarantee is the check above + Postgres.
201	  const idempotencyKey = `pay:${invoiceId}:${accountCode}:${amount}:${date}:${reference ?? ""}`.slice(0, 128);
202	
203	  const res = await fetch(`${XERO_API}/Payments`, {
204	    method: "PUT",
205	    headers: {
206	      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
207	      Accept: "application/json",
208	      "Content-Type": "application/json",
209	      "Idempotency-Key": idempotencyKey,
210	    },
211	    body: JSON.stringify(payment),
212	  });
213	  const bodyText = await res.text();
214	  if (!res.ok) {
215	    throw new Error(formatRejection(res.status, bodyText));
216	  }
217	
218	  const created = (JSON.parse(bodyText) as PaymentEnvelope).Payments?.[0];
219	  if (!created?.PaymentID) {
220	    throw new Error(`PaymentParseError: 2xx response but no PaymentID — body: ${bodyText}`);
221	  }
222	  console.log(
223	    `[xero] created PaymentID=${created.PaymentID} Status=${created.Status ?? "?"} Amount=${created.Amount ?? amount}`,
224	  );
225	
226	  // Re-read the invoice: AmountDue drops by Amount; Status flips to PAID only
227	  // when fully paid (system-set), stays AUTHORISED while partially paid.
228	  const after = await getInvoice(token, invoiceId);
229	  console.log(
230	    `[xero] invoice ${invoiceId}: Status=${after.Status ?? "?"} AmountDue=${after.AmountDue ?? "?"} AmountPaid=${after.AmountPaid ?? "?"}`,
231	  );
232	}
233	
234	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
235	  console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
236	  process.exitCode = 1;
237	});
238	
239	export {};
240
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:18:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/attachments.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/attachments.ts
2	//
3	// Attachments — NOT in the Xero MCP server; raw Accounting API only.
4	// Upload = raw file bytes PUT/POST to /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}
5	// (NOT JSON). Content-Type must be the file's MIME type. 10 attachments per
6	// document, 10MB per file (documented cap on the Attachments page).
7	
8	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
9	import { basename } from "node:path";
10	import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";
11	
12	/** Filenames containing any of < > : " / \ | ? * NUL + are rejected as Bad Request. */
13	const FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS = /[<>:"/\\|?*\u0000+]/;
14	
15	const MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;
16	
17	export type AttachmentParent = "Invoices" | "PurchaseOrders" | "Contacts";
18	
19	export interface Attachment {
20	  AttachmentID: string;
21	  FileName: string;
22	  Url: string;
23	  MimeType: string;
24	  ContentLength: number;
25	  IncludeOnline?: boolean;
26	}
27	
28	export interface AttachmentsResponse {
29	  Attachments: Attachment[];
30	}
31	
32	/**
33	 * Docs rule: filename characters "should not be encoded unless they're
34	 * brackets. Brackets must be encoded in order for the call to go through."
35	 */
36	function encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName: string): string {
37	  return fileName
38	    .replace(/\(/g, "%28")
39	    .replace(/\)/g, "%29")
40	    .replace(/\[/g, "%5B")
41	    .replace(/\]/g, "%5D");
42	}
43	
44	export async function uploadAttachment(options: {
45	  endpoint: AttachmentParent;
46	  guid: string; // e.g. the InvoiceID of the bill the evidence belongs to
47	  filePath: string;
48	  mimeType: string; // e.g. "application/pdf", "image/png"
49	  fileName?: string; // defaults to basename(filePath)
50	  /**
51	   * IncludeOnline=true shows the attachment on the online invoice. Xero
52	   * supports this for accounts RECEIVABLE invoices and AR credit notes only —
53	   * it does not apply to ACCPAY bills.
54	   */
55	  includeOnline?: boolean;
56	}): Promise<Attachment> {
57	  const fileName = options.fileName ?? basename(options.filePath);
58	  if (FORBIDDEN_FILENAME_CHARS.test(fileName)) {
59	    throw new Error(
60	      `BadFileName: "${fileName}" contains a character Xero rejects as Bad Request ` +
61	        String.raw`(< > : " / \ | ? * NUL +)`,
62	    );
63	  }
64	
65	  const bytes = readFileSync(options.filePath);
66	  if (bytes.byteLength > MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES) {
67	    throw new Error(
68	      `AttachmentTooLarge: ${bytes.byteLength} bytes — the Attachments page caps files at 10MB ` +
69	        `(max 10 attachments per document)`,
70	    );
71	  }
72	
73	  // PUT and POST are identical here; uploading the same filename onto the
74	  // same document OVERWRITES the existing attachment.
75	  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(
76	    `/${options.endpoint}/${options.guid}/Attachments/${encodeAttachmentFileName(fileName)}`,
77	    {
78	      method: "PUT",
79	      body: new Uint8Array(bytes),
80	      contentType: options.mimeType,
81	      query: options.includeOnline ? { IncludeOnline: true } : undefined,
82	    },
83	  );
84	  const attachment = res.Attachments[0];
85	  if (!attachment) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: upload returned no Attachments element");
86	  return attachment;
87	}
88	
89	export async function listAttachments(
90	  endpoint: AttachmentParent,
91	  guid: string,
92	): Promise<Attachment[]> {
93	  const res = await xeroFetch<AttachmentsResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/Attachments/`);
94	  return res.Attachments;
95	}
96	
97	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	// Demo entrypoint:
99	//   npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>
100	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	
102	async function main(): Promise<void> {
103	  const [guid, filePath, mimeType] = process.argv.slice(2);
104	  if (!guid || !filePath || !mimeType) {
105	    throw new Error("Usage: npx tsx attachments.ts <InvoiceID> <filePath> <mimeType>");
106	  }
107	  const uploaded = await uploadAttachment({ endpoint: "Invoices", guid, filePath, mimeType });
108	  console.log(`Uploaded ${uploaded.FileName} (${uploaded.ContentLength} bytes)`);
109	  const all = await listAttachments("Invoices", guid);
110	  console.log(`Document now has ${all.length}/10 attachments`);
111	}
112	
113	if (require.main === module) {
114	  main().catch((err) => {
115	    console.error(err);
116	    process.exitCode = 1;
117	  });
118	}
119
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/history-note.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/history-note.ts
2	//
3	// History & Notes — NOT in the Xero MCP server; raw Accounting API only.
4	// PUT /{Endpoint}/{Guid}/history appends a note (this is how the agent's
5	// decision note lands on a bill or PO). Notes cannot be updated, deleted, or
6	// backdated; they display the creation date with user "System Generated".
7	
8	import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";
9	
10	/** Document types that support history/notes (from the History & Notes page). */
11	export type HistoryParent =
12	  | "BankTransactions"
13	  | "BatchPayments"
14	  | "Contacts"
15	  | "CreditNotes"
16	  | "Invoices"
17	  | "Items"
18	  | "ManualJournals"
19	  | "Overpayments"
20	  | "Payments"
21	  | "Prepayments"
22	  | "PurchaseOrders"
23	  | "RepeatingInvoices"
24	  | "Quotes";
25	
26	export interface HistoryRecord {
27	  /** Type of change, e.g. "Approved", "Edited". Notes are the only type you can create. */
28	  Changes?: string;
29	  DateUTC?: string; // "/Date(...)/" ms epoch
30	  User?: string; // "System Generated" for API-created records
31	  Details: string;
32	}
33	
34	export interface HistoryResponse {
35	  HistoryRecords: HistoryRecord[];
36	}
37	
38	const MAX_NOTE_LENGTH = 2500;
39	
40	/** Append one or more notes. Exact documented shape: {"HistoryRecords":[{"Details":"..."}]} */
41	export async function appendNotes(
42	  endpoint: HistoryParent,
43	  guid: string,
44	  details: string[],
45	): Promise<HistoryResponse> {
46	  for (const note of details) {
47	    if (note.length > MAX_NOTE_LENGTH) {
48	      throw new Error(
49	        `NoteTooLong: ${note.length} chars — Xero caps history Details at ${MAX_NOTE_LENGTH}`,
50	      );
51	    }
52	  }
53	  return xeroFetch<HistoryResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`, {
54	    method: "PUT", // POST behaves identically; there is no update or delete
55	    json: { HistoryRecords: details.map((d) => ({ Details: d })) },
56	  });
57	}
58	
59	export async function getHistory(endpoint: HistoryParent, guid: string): Promise<HistoryRecord[]> {
60	  const res = await xeroFetch<HistoryResponse>(`/${endpoint}/${guid}/history`);
61	  return res.HistoryRecords;
62	}
63	
64	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	// Demo entrypoint:
66	//   npx tsx history-note.ts <InvoiceID> "note text"
67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	
69	async function main(): Promise<void> {
70	  const [guid, note] = process.argv.slice(2);
71	  if (!guid || !note) {
72	    throw new Error('Usage: npx tsx history-note.ts <InvoiceID> "note text"');
73	  }
74	  await appendNotes("Invoices", guid, [note]);
75	  const records = await getHistory("Invoices", guid);
76	  console.log(`Document now has ${records.length} history records:`);
77	  for (const record of records) {
78	    console.log(`  [${record.User ?? "?"}] ${record.Changes ?? "Note"}: ${record.Details}`);
79	  }
80	}
81	
82	if (require.main === module) {
83	  main().catch((err) => {
84	    console.error(err);
85	    process.exitCode = 1;
86	  });
87	}
88
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/suppliers-and-report.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-accounting-api/scripts/suppliers-and-report.ts
2	//
3	// Contacts (the fields supplier matching needs) and the AgedPayablesByContact
4	// report (requires ContactID). Fetch-and-shape only — the fuzzy matching
5	// itself is the match engine's job (api/src/modules/), not this file's.
6	
7	import { xeroFetch } from "./xero-fetch";
8	import type { Pagination } from "./bills";
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Contacts
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	export interface Contact {
15	  ContactID: string; // the ONLY stable key — do not key on Name
16	  Name: string; // max 255; no angle brackets, leading/trailing or repeated spaces
17	  EmailAddress?: string; // max 255; umlauts not supported
18	  ContactStatus?: "ACTIVE" | "ARCHIVED" | "GDPRREQUEST";
19	  /** Read-only: auto-set once any AP invoice exists for the contact. Never settable. */
20	  IsSupplier?: boolean;
21	}
22	
23	export interface ContactsResponse {
24	  Contacts: Contact[];
25	  pagination?: Pagination;
26	}
27	
28	/**
29	 * Optimised exact-match filter. Contacts' documented examples use single `=`
30	 * (where=Name="ABC limited") and are case- and accent-insensitive — never
31	 * append ToLower()/ToUpper(). Values are embedded in double quotes, so a name
32	 * containing `"` cannot go through `where`; use searchContacts() for those.
33	 *
34	 * Always pass `page`: unpaged GET /Contacts returns only a subset of fields.
35	 * Never use summaryOnly=true for supplier matching — it excludes IsSupplier.
36	 */
37	export async function findContactsByName(name: string): Promise<Contact[]> {
38	  const res = await xeroFetch<ContactsResponse>("/Contacts", {
39	    query: { where: `Name="${name}"`, page: 1 },
40	  });
41	  return res.Contacts;
42	}
43	
44	export async function findContactsByEmail(email: string): Promise<Contact[]> {
45	  const res = await xeroFetch<ContactsResponse>("/Contacts", {
46	    query: { where: `EmailAddress="${email}"`, page: 1 },
47	  });
48	  return res.Contacts;
49	}
50	
51	/**
52	 * Case-insensitive text search across Name, FirstName, LastName,
53	 * ContactNumber, CompanyNumber, EmailAddress — the raw-candidate source for
54	 * fuzzy supplier matching.
55	 */
56	export async function searchContacts(term: string, page = 1): Promise<ContactsResponse> {
57	  return xeroFetch<ContactsResponse>("/Contacts", {
58	    query: { searchTerm: term, page },
59	  });
60	}
61	
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	// Reports — Aged Payables By Contact (GET only; ContactID is REQUIRED)
64	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	
66	export interface ReportCell {
67	  Value?: string;
68	  /** Data rows carry {Id:"invoiceID", Value:<guid>} for drill-down. */
69	  Attributes?: Array<{ Id: string; Value: string }>;
70	}
71	
72	export interface ReportRow {
73	  RowType: "Header" | "Section" | "Row" | "SummaryRow";
74	  Title?: string;
75	  Cells?: ReportCell[];
76	  Rows?: ReportRow[]; // Section rows nest their data rows here
77	}
78	
79	export interface Report {
80	  ReportID: string;
81	  ReportName: string;
82	  ReportType: string;
83	  ReportTitles?: string[];
84	  ReportDate?: string;
85	  UpdatedDateUTC?: string;
86	  Rows: ReportRow[];
87	}
88	
89	export interface ReportResponse {
90	  Reports: Report[];
91	}
92	
93	/**
94	 * Header columns for this report: Date, Reference, Due Date, (blank), Total,
95	 * Paid, Credited, Due. Defaults to "payments up to the end of the current
96	 * month" when `date` is omitted.
97	 */
98	export async function agedPayablesByContact(
99	  contactId: string,
100	  options: { date?: string; fromDate?: string; toDate?: string } = {},
101	): Promise<ReportResponse> {
102	  return xeroFetch<ReportResponse>("/Reports/AgedPayablesByContact", {
103	    query: {
104	      ContactID: contactId, // param casing follows the docs' own example URL
105	      date: options.date,
106	      fromDate: options.fromDate,
107	      toDate: options.toDate,
108	    },
109	  });
110	}
111	
112	/** Flatten the nested Rows tree into data rows + the invoiceID attribute. */
113	export function reportDataRows(report: Report): Array<{ cells: string[]; invoiceId?: string }> {
114	  const out: Array<{ cells: string[]; invoiceId?: string }> = [];
115	  const walk = (rows: ReportRow[]): void => {
116	    for (const row of rows) {
117	      if (row.RowType === "Row" && row.Cells) {
118	        const invoiceId = row.Cells.flatMap((c) => c.Attributes ?? []).find(
119	          (a) => a.Id === "invoiceID",
120	        )?.Value;
121	        const entry: { cells: string[]; invoiceId?: string } = {
122	          cells: row.Cells.map((c) => c.Value ?? ""),
123	        };
124	        if (invoiceId) entry.invoiceId = invoiceId;
125	        out.push(entry);
126	      }
127	      if (row.Rows) walk(row.Rows);
128	    }
129	  };
130	  walk(report.Rows);
131	  return out;
132	}
133	
134	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	// Demo entrypoint:
136	//   npx tsx suppliers-and-report.ts "Supplier Name"
137	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	
139	async function main(): Promise<void> {
140	  const name = process.argv[2];
141	  if (!name) throw new Error('Usage: npx tsx suppliers-and-report.ts "Supplier Name"');
142	
143	  const matches = await findContactsByName(name);
144	  const supplier = matches[0];
145	  if (!supplier) {
146	    console.log(`No exact contact match for "${name}" — try searchContacts()`);
147	    return;
148	  }
149	  console.log(`Contact: ${supplier.Name} (${supplier.ContactID}) IsSupplier=${supplier.IsSupplier}`);
150	
151	  const report = await agedPayablesByContact(supplier.ContactID);
152	  const firstReport = report.Reports[0];
153	  if (!firstReport) throw new Error("XeroEmptyResponse: no Reports element returned");
154	  for (const row of reportDataRows(firstReport)) {
155	    console.log(`- [${row.invoiceId ?? "no-id"}] ${row.cells.join(" | ")}`);
156	  }
157	}
158	
159	if (require.main === module) {
160	  main().catch((err) => {
161	    console.error(err);
162	    process.exitCode = 1;
163	  });
164	}
165
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Show xero-auth skill token error table lines

`grep -n "invalid_request\|invalid_client\|unauthorized_client" /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md | head -40`

```text
3:description: Xero Custom Connection (client_credentials) auth for THIS repo — exact token endpoint, the four project scopes and what each unlocks, in-memory token caching with proactive expiry and retry-on-401, why there is no XERO_TENANT_ID, and the Gate G0 verify script. Use when writing or debugging any code that obtains or uses a Xero access token, when a Xero call returns 401/403/unauthorized_client/invalid_client/invalid_scope, or before the first Xero call in a fresh environment. Read this BEFORE writing token code; there is exactly ONE token path in this repo (api/src/modules/xero/) — do not hand-roll a second.
12:1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` non-empty at boot, with named errors.** Empty/missing secrets that reach Xero surface as a misleading OAuth error that points away from the real cause — verified empirically 2026-07-04: an empty client id in the Basic header gets 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}` from the token endpoint (in code flow the same mistake surfaces as `unauthorized_client`). `.env` at repo root only; never committed, never logged. When logging, log lengths (`client_id_len`), never values.
144:- **A misleading OAuth error from an empty or whitespace client id.** The cause is your env; the error points at Xero. Verified empirically 2026-07-04 against the live token endpoint: empty client id in the Basic header, or a missing Authorization header entirely → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`. (The Lovable skill's `unauthorized_client` symptom for `client_id=undefined` is the code-flow authorize URL, not this endpoint.) Assert non-empty **and trimmed** at boot — a trailing newline in `.env` counts as whitespace.
145:- **`invalid_client` from a wrong secret or wrong Basic encoding.** Verified empirically 2026-07-04: well-formed Basic header with wrong credentials → 400 `{"error":"invalid_client"}`. Base64 the literal `client_id:client_secret` — one colon, no newline, whole string encoded together; garbled encoding produces the same `invalid_client` (a structurally broken header degrades to `invalid_request`).
147:- **The token endpoint wants form-encoding, not JSON.** `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a `key=value&key=value` body. Verified empirically 2026-07-04: a JSON body → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`, the same misleading error as a missing client id.
160:| Token 400 `invalid_request` (verified empirically) | Empty client id in the Basic header, missing Authorization header, or a JSON body instead of form-encoding | Boot-time `MissingEnv` assertion; check `.env` parsing/trimming; send form-encoded body |
161:| Token 400 `invalid_client` (verified empirically) | Wrong secret or wrong client id, incl. malformed Basic encoding (missing colon, double-encoded, newline in the base64 input) | Re-copy the secret; encode `id:secret` as one string |
162:| `unauthorized_client` | Code-flow symptom of `client_id=undefined` in the authorize URL — should not appear in this repo's client_credentials runtime; if seen, credentials are empty/wrong somewhere | Boot-time env assertion |
177:- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md:116-140 — unauthorized_client ← empty client_id causal chain, requireEnv/client_id_len discipline, scope-grants tables (accounting.invoices → PurchaseOrders etc., accounting.settings → Organisation), broad→granular migration table, 403 = missing scope, scope minimalism rules.
178:- Empirical probes 2026-07-04 against live https://identity.xero.com/connect/token: wrong creds → 400 {"error":"invalid_client"}; empty client id in Basic / no Authorization header / JSON body → 400 {"error":"invalid_request"}.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-mcp-usage
3	description: Decide whether a Xero call in THIS project goes through the Xero MCP server (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server) or the raw Accounting API. Use when wiring any Xero read or write, when launching the MCP server against our Custom Connection env (XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET), or when an MCP tool seems to be missing. Covers the exact registered tool names and params for the ~10 tools this project uses, their hard limitations (list-invoices cannot filter by status, update-invoice cannot flip status, no idempotency on writes), and the confirmed MCP gaps — Purchase Orders, Attachments, and History have NO MCP tools and route to the xero-accounting-api skill. Read this BEFORE assuming an MCP tool exists.
4	---
5	
6	# Xero MCP server vs raw Accounting API — routing for this project
7	
8	The MCP server (`@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server`, facts verified against **v0.0.16**) covers invoices, contacts, payments, and reports. It does NOT cover Purchase Orders, Attachments, or History — those go through the raw Accounting API (`.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`). This skill is the routing table plus the exact capabilities and limits of the MCP tools we use. Tool inventory in any version newer than 0.0.16: UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
11	
12	1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are set and non-empty.** Empty secrets surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. The MCP server boot-asserts this itself (`xero-client.ts:19-21`, throws unless `bearer_token || (client_id && client_secret)`), but assert in our code first — the message is better.
13	2. **There is no tenant-id env var, on purpose.** In client_credentials (Custom Connection) mode the MCP server fetches a token from `identity.xero.com/connect/token`, then calls `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` and silently uses `connections[0].tenantId`. No selection mechanism exists. So the pre-flight invariant is: **exactly ONE org authorised on this Custom Connection**, or writes can land in the wrong books.
14	3. **Set `XERO_SCOPES` explicitly in `.env`** to the scopes assigned to the Custom Connection app (see `.env.example` at repo root). If unset, the MCP server requests its own hardcoded legacy V1 set (including `payroll.*`) and only falls back to a granular V2 set on an `invalid_scope` HTTP 400. Whether a scope request that is only partially assigned to a custom connection fails with `invalid_scope` or returns a narrower token: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Setting `XERO_SCOPES` verbatim to the app's assigned scopes sidesteps the question.
15	4. **Do not set `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN`** unless you mean it — it takes precedence over client id/secret and switches the server to bearer mode.
16	5. **Run the smoke test before wiring anything to the MCP server:**
17	
18	```sh
19	npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
20	```
21	
22	It performs the exact same token grant + `/connections` call the MCP server does on every tool invocation, prints the tenant the server will silently pick, and fails with a named error (`MissingEnv`, `TokenGrantFailed`, `NoConnections`) instead of the misleading upstream one.
23	
24	## The routing table (from `context/architecture.md` — verbatim, the pitch anchor)
25	
26	| Call | Via | Purpose |
27	|---|---|---|
28	| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
29	| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
30	| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
31	| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
32	| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
33	| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
34	| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
35	| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |
36	
37	> ⚠️ **One row of this table is wrong and will burn you: "approve bill (status update) | MCP tool".** Verified against the MCP source: `update-invoice` has **no `status` parameter** and its handler hard-blocks anything that is not a draft — `if (invoiceStatus !== Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT) { return { isError: true, error: "Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ..." } }` (`update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84`). The `Invoice` object it sends contains only lineItems/reference/dueDate/date/contact — no status field. **The MCP server cannot move an invoice to AUTHORISED (or any status).** Build the approve path on the raw Accounting API: `POST /Invoices` with `"Status": "AUTHORISED"` — see `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`. Keep the table verbatim for the pitch; route the code correctly.
38	
39	## Confirmed ABSENT from the MCP server — do not go looking
40	
41	Verified by grep over `xero-mcp-server/src/` (all 51 registered tools inventoried):
42	
43	- **NO Purchase Order tools.** No tool name contains "purchase". The only hits are item `purchaseDetails` fields and the phrase "purchase invoices" in the create-invoice description.
44	- **NO Attachment tools.** Only read-only `hasAttachments` display flags. No upload/download/list.
45	- **NO History/Notes tools.** No `getInvoiceHistory` / `createHistoryRecord` anywhere.
46	- Also absent: get-invoice-by-id, delete/void invoice, email-invoice, batch payments.
47	
48	All of those route to the raw Accounting API → `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`. If an MCP tool for them appears to exist, you are hallucinating it.
49	
50	## Running the MCP server locally (Custom Connection)
51	
52	- **Command:** `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest` (or a local build: `node dist/index.js`). Transport is **stdio only** — no port, no HTTP; the MCP client spawns it as a child process.
53	- **Env consumed** (`src/clients/xero-client.ts:14-16`): `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, optional `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` (takes precedence), optional `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated). The server calls `dotenv.config()` at boot, so launching it with cwd = repo root picks up our `.env` directly:
54	
55	```sh
56	# from repo root — .env supplies XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET / XERO_SCOPES
57	npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest
58	```
59	
60	If the spawning MCP client controls cwd, pass the three vars through its `env` block instead — values injected from the environment, never literals in a committed config file.
61	
62	- **Auth per call, not per boot:** every tool handler starts with `authenticate()`, which requests a **brand-new** client_credentials token plus a fresh `GET /connections` on every single tool call — 2 extra HTTP round-trips per call, no caching, no expiry check. Budget for the latency; never loop tool calls.
63	- **Token grant shape** (what the smoke-test script reproduces): `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token`, HTTP **Basic** auth `base64(clientId:clientSecret)` — not body params — with form-encoded body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=<urlencoded>`.
64	
65	## The 10 tools this project uses
66	
67	Exact registered names, key params, handler files (paths under `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/`).
68	
69	| Tool | Key params | Handler | Project use |
70	|---|---|---|---|
71	| `list-invoices` | `page: number` **required**; `contactIds?: string[]`; `invoiceNumbers?: string[]` (supplying invoiceNumbers also returns line items) | list-xero-invoices.handler.ts | ingest ACCPAY bills already in Xero |
72	| `create-invoice` | `contactId: string`; `lineItems[]` (`description`, `quantity`, `unitAmount`, `accountCode`, `taxType`, `itemCode?`, `tracking?`); `type: "ACCREC" \| "ACCPAY"` — **pass `"ACCPAY"`, default is ACCREC**; `reference?`; `date?` (YYYY-MM-DD) | create-xero-invoice.handler.ts | bills arriving by email; always created **DRAFT** |
73	| `update-invoice` | `invoiceId` required; `lineItems?`, `reference?`, `dueDate?`, `date?`, `contactId?`. **No `status` param.** | update-xero-invoice.handler.ts | fix a DRAFT bill before approval — nothing else |
74	| `create-payment` | `invoiceId`, `accountId`, `amount` (positive) required; `date?` (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults today), `reference?` | create-xero-payment.handler.ts | the money moment |
75	| `list-payments` | `page` (default 1); `invoiceNumber?`, `invoiceId?`, `paymentId?`, `reference?` | list-xero-payments.handler.ts | check-by-reference before create-payment |
76	| `list-aged-payables-by-contact` | `contactId` required; `reportDate?` (defaults end of current month), `invoicesFromDate?`, `invoicesToDate?` (all YYYY-MM-DD) | list-aged-payables-by-contact.handler.ts | dashboard context strip |
77	| `list-contacts` | `page?`; `searchTerm?` (case-insensitive over Name, FirstName, LastName, ContactNumber, EmailAddress) | list-xero-contacts.handler.ts | resolve supplier name → `contactId` |
78	| `create-contact` | `name` required; `email?` (must be valid email), `phone?` | create-xero-contact.handler.ts | new supplier arriving by email |
79	| `list-accounts` | none | list-xero-accounts.handler.ts | `accountCode` for invoice lines, `accountId` for payments |
80	| `list-organisation-details` | none | list-xero-organisation-details.handler.ts | org sanity check |
81	
82	## Param limitations — know these before building
83	
84	- **`list-invoices` cannot filter by status, date, or where-clause.** The handler hardcodes `where: undefined`, `statuses: undefined`, `order: "UpdatedDateUTC DESC"`, `includeArchived: false`, `summaryOnly: false`, and **`pageSize: 10`**. You cannot ask MCP for "all AUTHORISED bills" or "bills since Tuesday" — page through and filter in our code, or use the raw API.
85	- **`list-invoices` is also the only "get one invoice" path** (no get-by-id tool): pass `invoiceNumbers: ["INV-123"]` — that variant returns line items. It matches by invoice **number**, not InvoiceID.
86	- **`update-invoice` replaces the whole line set.** All line items must be re-supplied; omitted line items are **removed**. DRAFT invoices only.
87	- **`create-invoice` always creates DRAFT** (handler sets `Invoice.StatusEnum.DRAFT`) and returns a deep link. Type defaults to ACCREC — always pass `"ACCPAY"` for bills.
88	- **No MCP write is idempotent.** `idempotencyKey` is explicitly passed as `undefined` in createPayment and updateInvoice. Our CLAUDE.md rule (check-by-reference before create) is the only duplicate protection: `list-payments` with `reference`/`invoiceId` before `create-payment`; `list-invoices` with `invoiceNumbers` before `create-invoice`.
89	- **`create-payment` constraints are enforced by Xero, not the tool:** invoice must be AUTHORISED and not fully paid; amount must not exceed amount due. The tool only validates `amount > 0`.
90	- **`list-payments` pages at 10/page** (hardcoded `pageSize: 10` in the handler). `list-contacts` passes `pageSize: undefined` (Xero default; the tool description says 100/page).
91	
92	## Snippet
93	
94	- `scripts/check-mcp-env.ts` — plain-fetch smoke test of the Custom Connection env, reproducing the MCP server's own auth path (token grant with Basic auth + `GET /connections`, first-tenant selection). Run it before wiring the MCP server; run it again whenever auth errors appear mid-build.
95	
96	## Traps
97	
98	- **Status flips are not an MCP capability.** See the warning under the routing table. `update-invoice` has no `status` param and hard-blocks non-DRAFT invoices. DRAFT → AUTHORISED goes through the raw Accounting API, full stop.
99	- **Rate limits are shared.** Every Accounting API call the MCP server makes consumes the SAME per-tenant budget (60 calls/min, 5,000/day) as our raw-fetch calls from `api/src/modules/xero/`. The MCP server has **zero** rate-limit handling — no retry, no backoff, no `Retry-After` read; its only 429 behaviour is a cosmetic error string: `"Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment."` (`format-error.ts:35-36`). Our module's budget accounting must count MCP tool calls too. Whether the `identity.xero.com` token calls and `GET /connections` also count against the Accounting API tenant budget: UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
100	- **Every tool call costs 2 hidden HTTP round-trips** (fresh token + `/connections`) because the server never caches auth. A 5-tool sequence is 15 HTTP calls. Batch reads on our side; never poll via MCP.
101	- **First-tenant roulette.** More than one org authorised on the Custom Connection means the MCP server silently picks `connections[0]` — the wrong-books failure mode. The smoke-test script warns when it sees more than one connection.
102	- **`create-payment` success text lies:** it reports "Invoice created successfully". It created a Payment. Do not string-match success messages; check for the error shape instead.
103	- **`update-invoice` deletes lines you forget.** Re-supply every line item on every update.
104	- **Unset `XERO_SCOPES` triggers scope guessing** (legacy V1 set with `payroll.*` first, granular V2 on `invalid_scope`). Set it explicitly to the app's assigned scopes.
105	- **`@latest` drift.** Everything here is verified against v0.0.16. Pin or re-verify the tool inventory if the version moves.
106	
107	## Error → real cause
108	
109	| Symptom | Real cause |
110	|---|---|
111	| MCP server throws at boot before any tool runs | Neither `XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN` nor `XERO_CLIENT_ID`+`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` present (boot assert, `xero-client.ts:19-21`) — check `.env` and the spawner's env block |
112	| `unauthorized_client` on token grant | Empty/missing client id or secret (`client_id=undefined` got sent) — run `scripts/check-mcp-env.ts` |
113	| HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` | Requested scope not available to this Custom Connection; happens when `XERO_SCOPES` is unset and the server guesses. Set `XERO_SCOPES` to the app's assigned scopes |
114	| Tool calls fail though the server booted | Token grant works but no org authorised: `GET /connections` returned 0 — the org admin has not authorised the Custom Connection |
115	| `Cannot update invoice because it is not a draft. Current status: ...` | `update-invoice` hard-blocks non-DRAFT invoices; approve via raw Accounting API instead |
116	| `Too many requests to Xero. Please try again in a moment.` | 429 — shared 60/min tenant budget exhausted (our raw calls count too); MCP does no backoff, ours must |
117	| Writes appear in the wrong Xero org | Multiple orgs on the connection; server silently used `connections[0]` |
118	| "Invoice created successfully" after a payment | Mislabeled success text in `create-payment` — it is a Payment |
119	| Line items vanished after `update-invoice` | Omitted lines are removed; re-supply the full line set |
120	| Duplicate bill/payment after a retry | No idempotency key on MCP writes — check-by-reference (`list-invoices` / `list-payments`) before every create |
121	| An MCP tool for POs/Attachments/History "should exist" but does not | It does not. Route to `.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api` |
122	
123	<!--
124	Sources (load-bearing facts):
125	- .tmp/xero-refs/mcp.md — MCP server fact sheet (v0.0.16): tool inventory + handler paths (§1), confirmed-absent tools (§2), custom-connection run/env/tenant/scope facts (§3, citing src/clients/xero-client.ts:12-21,81-106,129-131,159-190,195-231; README.md:71-76,226-230; src/index.ts:14-15), create-payment schema/no-idempotency (§4, create-payment.tool.ts:18-34, create-xero-payment.handler.ts:15-45), update-invoice no-status + DRAFT-only block (§5, update-invoice.tool.ts:36-50, update-xero-invoice.handler.ts:77-84), list-invoices hardcoded filters/pageSize 10 (§6, list-xero-invoices.handler.ts:15-31), aged-payables params (§7), no rate limiting + format-error.ts:35-36 (§8), per-call authenticate()/no token cache (§9).
126	- .tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md — client_credentials grant shape (Basic auth, form body, identity.xero.com/connect/token; xero-client.ts:159-174), tenant from GET /connections first entry (xero-client.ts:176-190), scope selection logic (xero-client.ts:127-157), Custom Connection requirement (xero-agent-toolkit/README.md:67), no PO/attachment/history tools (§4).
127	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server/ (repo, verified directly): src/tools/create/create-invoice.tool.ts (params, ACCREC default, DRAFT status via create-xero-invoice.handler.ts:39, idempotencyKey undefined at :49), src/tools/list/list-contacts.tool.ts (page/searchTerm; handler pageSize undefined), src/tools/create/create-contact.tool.ts (name/email/phone), src/tools/list/list-accounts.tool.ts (no params), src/tools/list/list-payments.tool.ts (params; handler pageSize 10), src/tools/list/list-organisation-details.tool.ts (no params), src/tools/list/list-invoices.tool.ts:16 (page required), package.json (v0.0.16).
128	- context/architecture.md — routing table (reproduced verbatim), 60/min + 5,000/day budget, skills layout.
129	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md — skill format discipline (pre-flight-first, error→cause mapping, verbatim-quote practice).

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
2	//
3	// Smoke-test the Custom Connection env EXACTLY the way the Xero MCP server
4	// (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server v0.0.16) consumes it on every tool call:
5	//   1. POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token  (client_credentials,
6	//      HTTP Basic base64(clientId:clientSecret), form-encoded body)
7	//   2. GET  https://api.xero.com/connections          (server uses connections[0])
8	//
9	// Run from repo root:  npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage/scripts/check-mcp-env.ts
10	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Never logs secret values.
11	
12	import * as fs from "node:fs";
13	import * as path from "node:path";
14	// `process` is the Node global (typed via types:["node"]) — importing the
15	// namespace would make `process.exitCode` a read-only binding under strict TS.
16	
17	// Default = the scopes .env.example says the Custom Connection app was created
18	// with. Override with XERO_SCOPES in .env (recommended: always set it, so the
19	// MCP server skips its own scope-guessing fallback).
20	const DEFAULT_SCOPES =
21	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
22	
23	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
24	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
25	
26	interface TokenResponse {
27	  access_token: string;
28	  expires_in: number;
29	  token_type: string;
30	}
31	
32	interface XeroConnection {
33	  tenantId: string;
34	  tenantName?: string;
35	  tenantType?: string;
36	}
37	
38	// Minimal .env loader (KEY=VALUE lines, # comments) — no dotenv dependency.
39	// Only fills vars that are not already set in the process environment.
40	function loadDotEnv(): void {
41	  const candidates = [
42	    path.resolve(process.cwd(), ".env"),
43	    path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"),
44	  ];
45	  const envPath = candidates.find((p) => fs.existsSync(p));
46	  if (!envPath) {
47	    return; // vars may come from the shell; requireEnv() decides below
48	  }
49	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split("\n");
50	  for (const rawLine of lines) {
51	    const line = rawLine.trim();
52	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
53	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
54	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
55	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
56	    const value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
57	    if (process.env[key] === undefined || process.env[key] === "") {
58	      process.env[key] = value;
59	    }
60	  }
61	  console.log(`[check-mcp-env] loaded ${envPath}`);
62	}
63	
64	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
65	  const value = process.env[name];
66	  if (value === undefined || value.trim() === "") {
67	    throw new Error(
68	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client. ` +
69	        `Set it in .env at the repo root (see .env.example) and retry.`,
70	    );
71	  }
72	  return value.trim();
73	}
74	
75	async function main(): Promise<void> {
76	  loadDotEnv();
77	
78	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
79	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
80	
81	  const rawScopes = process.env.XERO_SCOPES;
82	  const scope =
83	    rawScopes !== undefined && rawScopes.trim() !== "" ? rawScopes.trim() : DEFAULT_SCOPES;
84	
85	  if (rawScopes === undefined || rawScopes.trim() === "") {
86	    console.warn(
87	      "[check-mcp-env] XERO_SCOPES is not set. This script defaults to the .env.example scope set, " +
88	        "but the MCP server would GUESS (legacy V1 set incl. payroll.*, then granular V2 on invalid_scope). " +
89	        "Set XERO_SCOPES explicitly to the app's assigned scopes.",
90	    );
91	  }
92	
93	  if (process.env.XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN !== undefined && process.env.XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN !== "") {
94	    console.warn(
95	      "[check-mcp-env] XERO_CLIENT_BEARER_TOKEN is set — the MCP server would use bearer mode and " +
96	        "IGNORE XERO_CLIENT_ID/XERO_CLIENT_SECRET. Unset it unless that is intended.",
97	    );
98	  }
99	
100	  // Log lengths, never values.
101	  console.log("[check-mcp-env] token request", {
102	    client_id_len: clientId.length,
103	    client_secret_len: clientSecret.length,
104	    scope,
105	  });
106	
107	  // 1. client_credentials grant — Basic auth header, form-encoded body.
108	  const credentials = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
109	  const tokenRes = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
110	    method: "POST",
111	    headers: {
112	      Authorization: `Basic ${credentials}`,
113	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
114	      Accept: "application/json",
115	    },
116	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
117	  });
118	
119	  if (!tokenRes.ok) {
120	    const body = await tokenRes.text();
121	    throw new Error(
122	      `TokenGrantFailed: HTTP ${tokenRes.status} from ${TOKEN_URL} — body: ${body}. ` +
123	        `unauthorized_client = wrong/empty client id or secret; ` +
124	        `invalid_scope = a requested scope is not assigned to the Custom Connection app.`,
125	    );
126	  }
127	
128	  const token = (await tokenRes.json()) as TokenResponse;
129	  console.log(
130	    `[check-mcp-env] token grant OK (token_type=${token.token_type}, expires_in=${token.expires_in}s)`,
131	  );
132	
133	  // 2. GET /connections — the MCP server silently uses connections[0].tenantId.
134	  const connRes = await fetch(CONNECTIONS_URL, {
135	    headers: {
136	      Authorization: `Bearer ${token.access_token}`,
137	      Accept: "application/json",
138	    },
139	  });
140	
141	  if (!connRes.ok) {
142	    const body = await connRes.text();
143	    throw new Error(
144	      `ConnectionsFailed: HTTP ${connRes.status} from ${CONNECTIONS_URL} — body: ${body}.`,
145	    );
146	  }
147	
148	  const connections = (await connRes.json()) as XeroConnection[];
149	
150	  if (connections.length === 0) {
151	    throw new Error(
152	      "NoConnections: token grant succeeded but GET /connections returned 0 orgs — " +
153	        "the org admin has not authorised the Custom Connection yet. " +
154	        "The MCP server would boot, then every tool call would fail.",
155	    );
156	  }
157	
158	  const first = connections[0];
159	  console.log(
160	    `[check-mcp-env] ${connections.length} connection(s). ` +
161	      `MCP server will use connections[0]: tenantId=${first.tenantId} (${first.tenantName ?? "unnamed"})`,
162	  );
163	
164	  if (connections.length > 1) {
165	    console.warn(
166	      "[check-mcp-env] WARNING: more than one org is authorised on this Custom Connection. " +
167	        "The MCP server always uses the FIRST with no selection mechanism — wrong-books risk. " +
168	        "Keep exactly one org authorised.",
169	    );
170	  }
171	
172	  console.log("[check-mcp-env] OK — this env is safe to hand to `npx -y @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server@latest`.");
173	}
174	
175	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
176	  console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
177	  process.exitCode = 1;
178	});
179
```
</details></tool-use>
