 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
2	//
3	// Gate G0 — proves the Xero Custom Connection works end to end, before any
4	// feature code is written. Run from repo root:
5	//
6	//   npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
7	//
8	// What it does:
9	//   1. Parses <repo-root>/.env itself (no dotenv dep); vars already in
10	//      process.env win over the file.
11	//   2. Asserts XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named errors —
12	//      an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`).
13	//   3. Fetches a client_credentials token from identity.xero.com.
14	//      On 400 `invalid_scope` (granular-only app) it retries once with the
15	//      granular equivalents of the project's broad scopes.
16	//   4. GETs the connected organisation (name via GET /connections — our
17	//      scopes deliberately exclude accounting.settings, so GET /Organisation
18	//      would 403) and 1 invoice.
19	//   5. Prints org name, invoice count, and the rate-limit headers.
20	//
21	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Compiles standalone under
22	// strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
23	
24	import * as fs from "node:fs";
25	import * as path from "node:path";
26	
27	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
28	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
29	const INVOICES_URL = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1&pageSize=1";
30	
31	// The exact scopes this project needs — nothing more. See SKILL.md.
32	const PROJECT_SCOPES =
33	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
34	
35	// Granular equivalents (post-29-April-2026 custom connections may reject the
36	// broad scopes with 400 invalid_scope). Mapping per Xero's scope migration:
37	// accounting.transactions -> invoices+payments+banktransactions+manualjournals,
38	// accounting.reports.read -> the eight accounting.reports.*.read scopes.
39	const GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES = [
40	  "accounting.invoices",
41	  "accounting.payments",
42	  "accounting.banktransactions",
43	  "accounting.manualjournals",
44	  "accounting.contacts",
45	  "accounting.attachments",
46	  "accounting.reports.aged.read",
47	  "accounting.reports.balancesheet.read",
48	  "accounting.reports.banksummary.read",
49	  "accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read",
50	  "accounting.reports.executivesummary.read",
51	  "accounting.reports.profitandloss.read",
52	  "accounting.reports.trialbalance.read",
53	  "accounting.reports.taxreports.read",
54	].join(" ");
55	
56	class MissingEnvError extends Error {
57	  constructor(varName: string) {
58	    super(
59	      `${varName} is empty — set it in .env at repo root. ` +
60	        `If an empty client id reaches Xero it surfaces as a misleading OAuth error ` +
61	        `(invalid_request on the token endpoint, unauthorized_client in code flow), ` +
62	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
63	    );
64	    this.name = "MissingEnv";
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	class XeroTokenError extends Error {
69	  constructor(
70	    public readonly status: number,
71	    public readonly body: string,
72	  ) {
73	    super(`token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
74	    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	class XeroApiError extends Error {
79	  constructor(
80	    public readonly status: number,
81	    url: string,
82	    body: string,
83	  ) {
84	    super(`GET ${url} failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
85	    this.name = "XeroApiError";
86	  }
87	}
88	
89	// --- 1. Tiny .env parser (existing process.env wins; missing file tolerated) ---
90	
91	function loadDotEnv(envPath: string): void {
92	  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
93	    console.warn(`[verify-auth] no .env at ${envPath} — relying on process.env only`);
94	    return;
95	  }
96	  for (const rawLine of fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split(/\r?\n/)) {
97	    const line = rawLine.trim().replace(/^export\s+/, "");
98	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
99	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
100	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
101	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
102	    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
103	    if (
104	      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"') && value.length >= 2) ||
105	      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'") && value.length >= 2)
106	    ) {
107	      value = value.slice(1, -1);
108	    }
109	    const existing = process.env[key];
110	    if (existing === undefined || existing === "") {
111	      process.env[key] = value;
112	    }
113	  }
114	}
115	
116	// --- 2. Named env assertions (fail fast, never log the value itself) ---
117	
118	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
119	  const v = process.env[name];
120	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
121	    throw new MissingEnvError(name);
122	  }
123	  return v.trim();
124	}
125	
126	// --- 3. client_credentials token (Basic auth + form-encoded body, never JSON) ---
127	
128	interface TokenResult {
129	  accessToken: string;
130	  expiresIn: number;
131	  scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" | "granular fallback";
132	}
133	
134	async function requestToken(
135	  clientId: string,
136	  clientSecret: string,
137	  scope: string,
138	): Promise<{ accessToken: string; expiresIn: number }> {
139	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
140	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
141	    method: "POST",
142	    headers: {
143	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
144	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
145	      Accept: "application/json",
146	    },
147	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
148	  });
149	  const text = await res.text();
150	  if (!res.ok) {
151	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
152	  }
153	  const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
154	  if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
155	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
156	  }
157	  return {
158	    accessToken: parsed.access_token,
159	    expiresIn: typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0,
160	  };
161	}
162	
163	async function getTokenWithFallback(clientId: string, clientSecret: string): Promise<TokenResult> {
164	  const scopeOverride = process.env["XERO_SCOPES"];
165	  const scopes =
166	    scopeOverride !== undefined && scopeOverride.trim() !== "" ? scopeOverride.trim() : PROJECT_SCOPES;
167	  try {
168	    const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
169	    return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" };
170	  } catch (err) {
171	    if (err instanceof XeroTokenError && err.status === 400 && err.body.includes("invalid_scope")) {
172	      console.warn(
173	        "[verify-auth] broad scopes rejected (invalid_scope) — this app only has granular scopes. " +
174	          "Retrying with granular equivalents. Set XERO_SCOPES in .env to the granular set to silence this.",
175	      );
176	      const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES);
177	      return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "granular fallback" };
178	    }
179	    throw err;
180	  }
181	}
182	
183	// --- 4. Organisation + one invoice ---
184	
185	interface XeroConnection {
186	  tenantId?: string;
187	  tenantName?: string;
188	}
189	
190	async function getJson(url: string, accessToken: string): Promise<{ body: unknown; res: Response }> {
191	  // Per the custom-connections docs, API calls need exactly these two headers.
192	  // No xero-tenant-id: a custom connection is bound to a single organisation.
193	  const res = await fetch(url, {
194	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, Accept: "application/json" },
195	  });
196	  const text = await res.text();
197	  if (!res.ok) {
198	    throw new XeroApiError(res.status, url, text);
199	  }
200	  return { body: JSON.parse(text) as unknown, res };
201	}
202	
203	async function main(): Promise<void> {
204	  loadDotEnv(path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"));
205	
206	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
207	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
208	  // Log lengths only — never the values.
209	  console.log(
210	    `[verify-auth] env ok (client_id_len: ${clientId.length}, client_secret_len: ${clientSecret.length})`,
211	  );
212	
213	  const token = await getTokenWithFallback(clientId, clientSecret);
214	  console.log(
215	    `[verify-auth] token ok — scope set: ${token.scopeSetUsed}, expires_in: ${token.expiresIn}s`,
216	  );
217	
218	  const { body: connectionsBody } = await getJson(CONNECTIONS_URL, token.accessToken);
219	  const connections = Array.isArray(connectionsBody) ? (connectionsBody as XeroConnection[]) : [];
220	  if (connections.length === 0) {
221	    throw new XeroApiError(
222	      200,
223	      CONNECTIONS_URL,
224	      "token issued but no organisation is connected. Has a Xero org admin authorised this " +
225	        "Custom Connection app in the developer portal? Tokens are useless until that is done.",
226	    );
227	  }
228	  const first = connections[0];
229	  const orgName = first?.tenantName ?? first?.tenantId ?? "(unnamed connection)";
230	  console.log(`[verify-auth] organisation: ${orgName}`);
231	
232	  const { body: invoicesBody, res: invoicesRes } = await getJson(INVOICES_URL, token.accessToken);
233	  const inv = invoicesBody as { Invoices?: unknown[]; pagination?: { itemCount?: unknown } };
234	  const returned = Array.isArray(inv.Invoices) ? inv.Invoices.length : 0;
235	  const itemCount = typeof inv.pagination?.itemCount === "number" ? inv.pagination.itemCount : null;
236	  console.log(
237	    `[verify-auth] invoices returned: ${returned}` + (itemCount !== null ? ` (org total: ${itemCount})` : ""),
238	  );
239	
240	  console.log(
241	    "[verify-auth] rate limits remaining — " +
242	      `minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-MinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
243	      `day: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-DayLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
244	      `app-minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-AppMinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}`,
245	  );
246	  console.log("[verify-auth] G0 PASS");
247	}
248	
249	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
250	  if (err instanceof Error) {
251	    console.error(`[verify-auth] FAIL — ${err.name}: ${err.message}`);
252	  } else {
253	    console.error("[verify-auth] FAIL —", err);
254	  }
255	  console.error("[verify-auth] see the error table in .claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md");
256	  process.exit(1);
257	});
258
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Read history-note evidence lines in fact sheet

`sed -n '70,90p' /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/facts/docs-evidence.md; echo "==="; grep -n "backdate\|delete" /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/facts/docs-evidence.md | head; echo "==="; grep -rn "old paths\|undefined/" /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/ 2>/dev/null | head -5`

```text
{ "HistoryRecords": [ { "Details": "Previous payment failed" }, { "Details": "Defer the payment" } ] }
```
- `Details`: "The note to be recorded against a single document. **Max Length 2500 characters.**"
- Multiple notes can be added in one call (array).
- "The note will be displayed in history & notes showing the **date of creation** and '**System Generated**' as the user that created it."

### What you CANNOT do [HIST]
- POST is not an update: "In this case a POST is exactly the same as a PUT. **It's not possible to update history and notes.**"
- "Notes are the only type of Change that can be manually created. All other types of Change are created automatically when certain actions occur (e.g. an invoice is paid)." — you cannot fabricate other Change types via the API.
- No date/timestamp field exists on the PUT payload and the note always shows the date of creation — i.e. **notes cannot be backdated** (direct consequence of the documented payload/behaviour; the literal word "backdate" does not appear — that phrasing is inference).
- Deleting notes: not mentioned anywhere on the page; no DELETE method is listed (only GET/PUT/POST) — deletion is unsupported per the documented method list, but an explicit "cannot delete" statement is UNVERIFIED.

---

## 3. Reports — Aged Payables By Contact

### Endpoint [REP]
- URL: `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/AgedPayablesByContact` — exact report name in URL is `AgedPayablesByContact`. Method: GET only.
- Description verbatim: "Returns aged payables up to the end of the current month by default."
- General: every report endpoint is `https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Reports/[Report Name]`, returned as XML (default) or JSON. [REP]
- Permissions: "An application that has been authorised by a Standard user without the 'reports' role will not be able to access the Reports, Journals, and ManualJournals endpoints (A HTTP 403 error will be returned in this case)." [REP]
===
79:- No date/timestamp field exists on the PUT payload and the note always shows the date of creation — i.e. **notes cannot be backdated** (direct consequence of the documented payload/behaviour; the literal word "backdate" does not appear — that phrasing is inference).
80:- Deleting notes: not mentioned anywhere on the page; no DELETE method is listed (only GET/PUT/POST) — deletion is unsupported per the documented method list, but an explicit "cannot delete" statement is UNVERIFIED.
===
```

FIRST read the fix spec: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-devansh-hackathonProjects-encode-xero-hack/95d25a4a-0993-42c8-9334-c58a60a7ec73/scratchpad/fixspecs/xero-demo-seed.json — 6 corrections; apply EVERY one. Concrete decisions where the spec offers options:
- Correction 1 (ACCPAY Reference does not persist — THE demo-killer): restructure as follows. InvoiceNumber = the supplier's invoice number (TT-8841 etc.) and stays the idempotency key. The PO join key rides in LineItems[0].Description as natural supplier text (e.g. "Sawn timber C24 45x145, as per PO-1001") — realistic, and the ingest parser extracts PO refs from line text at the edges per architecture.md. Update the bill create shape, the ordering-table rationale, the match-engine join contract note, and the sheet rows if they reference bill Reference. Add a loud Traps line: Invoice.Reference is ACCREC-only and silently dropped on ACCPAY bills (evidence: official OpenAPI spec + the MCP server's own ACCPAY reference→invoiceNumber remap).
- Correction 2 (preflight org check 403s): replace the /Organisation check with GET https://api.xero.com/connections (print tenantName), matching xero-auth's verify-auth.ts. Demote the confirm-GBP step to a manual pre-flight checklist item ("check org settings in the Xero UI"), noting an automated BaseCurrency check needs accounting.settings.read which the project app does not carry.
- Correction 3 (gap #2 could be read as QTY_MISMATCH): make the defect price-shaped exactly as the fix suggests — bill qty stays 25 (same as PO), UnitAmount £79.92 → net £1,998.00, VAT £399.60, gross £2,397.60 vs PO 25 × £74.00 = £1,850.00 net; £148.00 net delta, outside max(2% = £37, £5). Update the cast table row, story text, and the sheet receipt row (qty 25). Also add the one-line statement that AMOUNT_MISMATCH vs QTY_MISMATCH precedence must be pinned in the G1 tests.
- Correction 4 (scope substring check): whole-token Set comparison exactly as the fix says.
- Correction 5 (rate budget ~2x understated): specify the batched lookups explicitly — ONE GET /Invoices?InvoiceNumbers=TT-8841,...(all 15)&page=1 and ONE GET /PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100 cached and matched client-side; restate the budget with the real arithmetic.
- Correction 6 (5,000/day): state 1,000/day starter (new-app default) / 5,000 higher tiers, check X-DayLimit-Remaining.

CROSS-CUTTING DECISIONS (all five xero-* skills are being aligned — apply where relevant):
1. SEED_SCOPES must be a SUBSET of the app's four assigned scopes (accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read): use `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts`. There is no separate seed app; requesting a scope outside the assigned set 400s invalid_scope.
2. .env parser canon in scripts: skip comments/blanks, tolerate leading `export `, strip MATCHING single/double quotes, never override existing process.env. Upgrade both scripts' parsers if they differ. Runner is `npx tsx`.
3. The UNVERIFIED marker must be the contiguous literal "UNVERIFIED — check before relying" — fix the two truncated instances (SKILL.md ~line 128 and ~185) and any others.
4. Fact sheets moved to /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/facts/*.md — update the Sources HTML comment paths.
5. Token-endpoint error canon: empty client id → 400 invalid_request; wrong creds → 400 invalid_client; unauthorized_client → app not a Custom Connection. Align any error-table rows.

Remaining styleIssue triage:
- PO idempotency carve-out: the blanket "treat DELETED/VOIDED hits as absent and recreate" rule must exclude POs (PurchaseOrderNumber is unique; whether a DELETED PO's number can be reused is UNVERIFIED — check before relying); for POs, bump to a fresh number series as line ~185 already says. Make the two sections agree.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Find verified error and scope rows in xero-auth SKILL.md

`grep -n -i "invalid_request\|invalid_client\|unauthorized_client\|accounting.settings\|Accounts\b\|rate limit\|1,000\|5,000\|60/min" /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md | head -40`

```text
3:description: Xero Custom Connection (client_credentials) auth for THIS repo — exact token endpoint, the four project scopes and what each unlocks, in-memory token caching with proactive expiry and retry-on-401, why there is no XERO_TENANT_ID, and the Gate G0 verify script. Use when writing or debugging any code that obtains or uses a Xero access token, when a Xero call returns 401/403/unauthorized_client/invalid_client/invalid_scope, or before the first Xero call in a fresh environment. Read this BEFORE writing token code; there is exactly ONE token path in this repo (api/src/modules/xero/) — do not hand-roll a second.
12:1. **Assert `XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` non-empty at boot, with named errors.** Empty/missing secrets that reach Xero surface as a misleading OAuth error that points away from the real cause — verified empirically 2026-07-04: an empty client id in the Basic header gets 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}` from the token endpoint (in code flow the same mistake surfaces as `unauthorized_client`). `.env` at repo root only; never committed, never logged. When logging, log lengths (`client_id_len`), never values.
65:- `accounting.settings[.read]` — grants Accounts, Organisation, TaxRates, etc. We don't read org configuration. Consequence: `GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisation` **403s** under our scopes; the org name comes from `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` (`tenantName`) instead, which is what `verify-auth.ts` does.
97:The MCP server requests a brand-new token plus a `GET /connections` on **every** tool call: two wasted round-trips per call, all counted against the 60/min tenant limit. Do the opposite. `scripts/token-cache.ts` is the drop-in module:
127:| `X-MinLimit-Remaining` | calls left this minute (limit: 60/min per tenant) |
128:| `X-DayLimit-Remaining` | calls left today (5,000/day; 1,000 on starter tier) |
144:- **A misleading OAuth error from an empty or whitespace client id.** The cause is your env; the error points at Xero. Verified empirically 2026-07-04 against the live token endpoint: empty client id in the Basic header, or a missing Authorization header entirely → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`. (The Lovable skill's `unauthorized_client` symptom for `client_id=undefined` is the code-flow authorize URL, not this endpoint.) Assert non-empty **and trimmed** at boot — a trailing newline in `.env` counts as whitespace.
145:- **`invalid_client` from a wrong secret or wrong Basic encoding.** Verified empirically 2026-07-04: well-formed Basic header with wrong credentials → 400 `{"error":"invalid_client"}`. Base64 the literal `client_id:client_secret` — one colon, no newline, whole string encoded together; garbled encoding produces the same `invalid_client` (a structurally broken header degrades to `invalid_request`).
147:- **The token endpoint wants form-encoding, not JSON.** `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a `key=value&key=value` body. Verified empirically 2026-07-04: a JSON body → 400 `{"error":"invalid_request"}`, the same misleading error as a missing client id.
150:- **`GET /Organisation` 403s under our scopes.** It needs `accounting.settings[.read]`, which we deliberately don't request. Get the org name from `GET /connections` (`tenantName`).
152:- **Every token request burns rate limit.** A token-per-call pattern (MCP server style) spends 2 of your 60/min on overhead. Cache.
160:| Token 400 `invalid_request` (verified empirically) | Empty client id in the Basic header, missing Authorization header, or a JSON body instead of form-encoding | Boot-time `MissingEnv` assertion; check `.env` parsing/trimming; send form-encoded body |
161:| Token 400 `invalid_client` (verified empirically) | Wrong secret or wrong client id, incl. malformed Basic encoding (missing colon, double-encoded, newline in the base64 input) | Re-copy the secret; encode `id:secret` as one string |
162:| `unauthorized_client` | Code-flow symptom of `client_id=undefined` in the authorize URL — should not appear in this repo's client_credentials runtime; if seen, credentials are empty/wrong somewhere | Boot-time env assertion |
165:| API call 403 | Token lacks the scope for that resource (e.g. `/Organisation` without `accounting.settings`) | Add the scope at both ends, request a NEW token; never retry with the same token |
166:| API call 429 + `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` | Rate limit hit (60/min, 5,000/day, 5 concurrent, 10,000/min app-wide) | Wait `Retry-After` seconds; batch reads; cache tokens |
174:- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — token endpoint, Basic auth, form-encoded body, response fields, ~30 min lifetime (Token types page), single-org quote, AU/NZ/UK/US restriction, scopes-at-both-ends, granular migration dates (29 Apr 2026 / Sep 2027), two-header call example with no xero-tenant-id (explicit "not required" sentence absent = UNVERIFIED), "tenanted endpoints only" wrinkle, rate limits + X-*Limit-Remaining/X-Rate-Limit-Problem/Retry-After headers, pagination (page/pageSize min 1, pagination.itemCount). Underlying URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,client-credentials,token-types,limits}/, /documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses.
177:- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md + .tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/javascript/SKILL.md:116-140 — unauthorized_client ← empty client_id causal chain, requireEnv/client_id_len discipline, scope-grants tables (accounting.invoices → PurchaseOrders etc., accounting.settings → Organisation), broad→granular migration table, 403 = missing scope, scope minimalism rules.
178:- Empirical probes 2026-07-04 against live https://identity.xero.com/connect/token: wrong creds → 400 {"error":"invalid_client"}; empty client id in Basic / no Authorization header / JSON body → 400 {"error":"invalid_request"}.
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-demo-seed
3	description: SPEC for seeding the demo Xero org so the data tells a story — the 15-supplier UK trade cast (contacts, AUTHORISED purchase orders, ACCPAY bills £80–£8,000 net with 20% UK VAT via LineAmountTypes Exclusive), EXACTLY two deliberate match gaps (Thameside Timber's £4,200 bill with NO goods receipt, and one bill £148 over its PO — outside the ±2%/£5 tolerance), the Google Sheet receipt-log schema, idempotent check-by-reference lookups with the exact where-filters, and the contacts → POs → bills → sheet ordering. Use when writing or reviewing ANYTHING in seed/, or when the demo org needs re-seeding or resetting. Read this BEFORE creating any contact, PO, or bill in the org. Facts and shapes only — the runnable seeding code lives in seed/, match/approval decisions live in api/src/modules/. For endpoint mechanics see xero-accounting-api; for MCP-vs-raw routing see xero-mcp-usage (POs have NO MCP tools — raw API only).
4	---
5	
6	# Seeding the demo Xero org (the data must tell a story)
7	
8	One seeded org: 15 UK trade supplier contacts, 15 AUTHORISED purchase orders, 15 ACCPAY bills, 14 receipt rows in a Google Sheet. 13 bills match clean; exactly 2 do not, by design — those 2 gaps ARE the demo. This file is the spec that `seed/` transcribes. It serves Gate G0; the G1 unit tests assert the exact match-states in the cast table below.
9	
10	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (before any write)
11	
12	Run `scripts/preflight.ts` (read-only; compile per §Snippets). It asserts, in order:
13	
14	1. **`XERO_CLIENT_ID` and `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` are non-empty**, with named `MissingEnv` errors. Empty secrets surface from Xero as `unauthorized_client` (because `client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Secrets live in `.env` at repo root — never committed, never logged; the script logs `client_id_len`, never the value.
15	2. **A `client_credentials` token mints and its granted `scope` contains `accounting.contacts` and `accounting.invoices`.** Custom-connection scopes are configured at BOTH ends — selected on the app config AND sent in the token request body. A token missing a scope 403s later at the worst moment; fail here with a named `MissingScope` error instead.
16	3. **`GET /api.xro/2.0/Organisations` returns 200** and the script prints the raw body so a human confirms it is the DEMO org (and that the base currency is GBP) before anything is written. Seeding a real org is unrecoverable on demo day.
17	4. (Manual, once) **Pick the expense account for line items**: set `XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE` in `.env` to an expense account whose default tax rate is 20% VAT on purchases. Omit `TaxType` on lines so the AccountCode default applies — that mechanism is documented; the exact UK 20% TaxType code (commonly cited as `INPUT2`) is UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
18	
19	## Auth model — why there is no `XERO_TENANT_ID`
20	
21	- This project uses a **Custom Connection** (`grant_type=client_credentials`), which per Xero's docs "utilise the client credentials grant type to access data from a **single Xero organisation**". One org per connection → no org ambiguity → nothing for a tenant header to disambiguate.
22	- Token endpoint: `POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token` with `Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...`.
23	- Xero's own custom-connection call example sends only `Authorization: Bearer ...` and `Accept` — **no `xero-tenant-id` header** (unlike code-flow tokens, where it is required). An explicit docs sentence "the header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — check before relying; the canonical example omitting it is the evidence.
24	- Token lifetime ~30 min (`expires_in` is authoritative); the response has **no `refresh_token`** — re-mint when needed. A full seed run fits in one token.
25	- Scopes for seeding: `accounting.contacts accounting.invoices`. Granular only (new custom connections get granular scopes from 29 April 2026); `accounting.invoices` grants Invoices AND PurchaseOrders.
26	- Custom Connections are UK-available (AU/NZ/UK/US only, paid subscription; free against the Xero Demo Company for development).
27	
28	## The cast — 15 UK trade suppliers (transcribe exactly)
29	
30	All amounts are net (ex VAT). Every document sets `"LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive"`; Xero adds 20% VAT from the account's default tax rate, so **Total = net × 1.20** (e.g. TT-8841: 4200.00 + 840.00 = 5040.00). Every net below lands on exact 2dp when multiplied by 1.2. One line item per document: `net = Qty × Unit`.
31	
32	| # | Contact Name | Trade | PO # | Bill # (InvoiceNumber) | Unit £ | PO qty | Bill qty | PO net £ | Bill net £ | Line item description | Match state |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| 1 | Thameside Timber Ltd | Timber merchant | PO-1001 | TT-8841 | 70.00 | 60 | 60 | 4200.00 | 4200.00 | C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths | **MISSING_RECEIPT** (demo star) |
35	| 2 | Brixton Plumbing Merchants Ltd | Plumbers' merchant | PO-1002 | BPM-2210 | 74.00 | 25 | 27 | 1850.00 | 1998.00 | 15mm copper tube 3m and brassware bundle | **AMOUNT_MISMATCH** |
36	| 3 | Walthamstow Electrical Wholesale Ltd | Electrical wholesaler | PO-1003 | WEW-0345 | 93.25 | 10 | 10 | 932.50 | 932.50 | 6242Y twin and earth cable 2.5mm, 100m drum | MATCHED |
37	| 4 | Hackney Plant Hire Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1004 | HPH-7712 | 220.00 | 12 | 12 | 2640.00 | 2640.00 | 1.5t mini excavator, day rate | MATCHED |
38	| 5 | Bermondsey Skip Hire Ltd | Skip hire | PO-1005 | BSH-1108 | 340.00 | 1 | 1 | 340.00 | 340.00 | 8-yard builders skip, 2-week hire | MATCHED |
39	| 6 | Croydon Builders Merchants Ltd | Builders' merchant | PO-1006 | CBM-5521 | 45.00 | 84 | 84 | 3780.00 | 3780.00 | Aerated concrete blocks 100mm, per pack | MATCHED |
40	| 7 | Deptford Sheet Materials Ltd | Timber and sheet | PO-1007 | DSM-0092 | 31.60 | 40 | 40 | 1264.00 | 1264.00 | 18mm structural hardwood ply 2440x1220 | MATCHED |
41	| 8 | Ealing Electrical Factors Ltd | Electrical wholesaler | PO-1008 | EEF-4478 | 102.55 | 5 | 5 | 512.75 | 512.75 | 10-way consumer unit with RCBOs | MATCHED |
42	| 9 | Fulham Plumbing Supplies Ltd | Plumbers' merchant | PO-1009 | FPS-3301 | 689.95 | 1 | 1 | 689.95 | 689.95 | Unvented hot water cylinder 210L | MATCHED |
43	| 10 | Greenwich Tool and Plant Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1010 | GTP-6640 | 140.00 | 8 | 8 | 1120.00 | 1120.00 | Towable diesel generator, day rate | MATCHED |
44	| 11 | Ilford Aggregates and Skips Ltd | Skip hire and aggregates | PO-1011 | IAS-2287 | 65.00 | 12 | 12 | 780.00 | 780.00 | MOT Type 1 aggregate, bulk bag | MATCHED |
45	| 12 | Kentish Town Building Supplies Ltd | Builders' merchant | PO-1012 | KBS-9034 | 49.50 | 120 | 120 | 5940.00 | 5940.00 | Facing bricks, per pack | MATCHED |
46	| 13 | Leyton Roofing Supplies Ltd | Roofing merchant | PO-1013 | LRS-1156 | 38.50 | 60 | 60 | 2310.00 | 2310.00 | Concrete interlocking roof tiles, per bundle | MATCHED |
47	| 14 | Mile End Hire Centre Ltd | Plant hire | PO-1014 | MEH-8890 | 152.00 | 3 | 3 | 456.00 | 456.00 | Petrol cut-off saw, week hire | MATCHED |
48	| 15 | Peckham Decorating Supplies Ltd | Decorators' merchant | PO-1015 | PDS-0417 | 7.05 | 12 | 12 | 84.60 | 84.60 | Trade emulsion 10L, brilliant white | MATCHED |
49	
50	Naming rules baked into the cast: no apostrophes or angle brackets (keeps `where` filters trivially encodable; Contact `Name` rejects angle brackets, leading/trailing whitespace, and repeating spaces), all references UPPERCASE-only (see Traps), net range £84.60–£5,940.00 inside the £80–£8,000 brief. Give each contact a plain-ASCII `EmailAddress` (`accounts@<slug>.co.uk`) — umlauts are unsupported.
51	
52	## The story — exactly two gaps, everything else clean
53	
54	- **#1 Thameside Timber (TT-8841, £4,200 net / £5,040 gross) — the demo star.** PO-1001 exists, AUTHORISED, amounts identical. The Google Sheet has **no row for PO-1001**. Expected engine verdict: `MISSING_RECEIPT`. The narrative: the biggest bill on the desk, looks perfect, and the one thing missing is proof anyone received the timber.
55	- **#2 Brixton Plumbing Merchants (BPM-2210).** PO-1002 authorises 25 × £74.00 = £1,850.00 net; the bill charges 27 × £74.00 = £1,998.00 net. Difference £148.00 = 8.0% of the PO. Tolerance is ±2% or £5, whichever is greater → max(£37.00, £5) = £37.00 — outside, net or gross. The sheet DOES have a receipt row for PO-1002 (qty 27, delivery was fine), so the only gap is bill vs PO: `AMOUNT_MISMATCH`.
56	- **#3–#15**: PO net = bill net, receipt row present with qty = bill qty → `MATCHED`. These are the auto-approve volume that makes the two exceptions stand out.
57	
58	## Dates (relative to seed day D — always fresh)
59	
60	| Field | Value | Why |
61	|---|---|---|
62	| PO `Date` | D−14 | Match rule requires PO date ≤ bill date |
63	| PO `DeliveryDate` | D−7 | Goods land a week later |
64	| Sheet `date` (receipt) | D−7 | Receipt signed on delivery day |
65	| Bill `Date` | D−5 | Invoice follows delivery |
66	| Bill `DueDate` | D+25 | 30 days from invoice, still open on demo day |
67	
68	All `YYYY-MM-DD`.
69	
70	## Purchase orders (raw Accounting API — no MCP tools exist for POs)
71	
72	`POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders?summarizeErrors=false`
73	
74	```jsonc
75	{
76	  "PurchaseOrders": [
77	    {
78	      "Contact": { "ContactID": "<from contacts step>" }, // PO endpoint NEVER creates contacts
79	      "PurchaseOrderNumber": "PO-1001",                   // unique alphanumeric; auto-generated if omitted — always set it
80	      "Date": "<D-14>",
81	      "DeliveryDate": "<D-7>",
82	      "Status": "AUTHORISED",                             // create-as-AUTHORISED is shown in the official examples; reads as a real order
83	      "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
84	      "LineItems": [
85	        { "Description": "C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths",
86	          "Quantity": 60, "UnitAmount": 70.00, "AccountCode": "<XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE>" }
87	      ]
88	    }
89	    // ... one element per cast row; single batch call
90	  ]
91	}
92	```
93	
94	PO statuses: `DRAFT`, `SUBMITTED`, `AUTHORISED`, `BILLED`, `DELETED`. No official PO status-transition table exists; anything beyond "official example deletes a DRAFT PO via Status=DELETED" is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. The demo later marks POs `BILLED` via a POST status update (implied mechanism; no official mark-as-billed example — UNVERIFIED — check before relying).
95	
96	## Bills (ACCPAY invoices)
97	
98	`POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?summarizeErrors=false`
99	
100	```jsonc
101	{
102	  "Invoices": [
103	    {
104	      "Type": "ACCPAY",
105	      "Contact": { "ContactID": "<from contacts step>" }, // NEVER a bare Name — that silently creates a duplicate contact
106	      "InvoiceNumber": "TT-8841",  // supplier's invoice ref; for ACCPAY this is NON-unique and displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI
107	      "Reference": "PO-1001",      // the PO join key the match engine reads
108	      "Date": "<D-5>",
109	      "DueDate": "<D+25>",
110	      "Status": "SUBMITTED",       // "Awaiting Approval" — the agent's approve action flips it to AUTHORISED live on stage
111	      "LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
112	      "LineItems": [
113	        { "Description": "C24 treated carcassing 47x150mm, 4.8m lengths",
114	          "Quantity": 60, "UnitAmount": 70.00, "AccountCode": "<XERO_SEED_ACCOUNT_CODE>" }
115	      ]
116	    }
117	    // ... one element per cast row; single batch call
118	  ]
119	}
120	```
121	
122	Why `SUBMITTED`: creating directly with DRAFT/SUBMITTED/AUTHORISED is documented; SUBMITTED → AUTHORISED is a legal transition and gives the demo its approval moment. Do NOT seed AUTHORISED (once AUTHORISED a bill can never go back and can only be VOIDED, and there is nothing left for the agent to do).
123	
124	With `summarizeErrors=false` a batch returns **HTTP 200 even when elements fail** — the seed must check every element's `StatusAttributeString` and fail loudly printing its `ValidationErrors`. ACCPAY bills do not support discounts. UnitAmount is rounded to 2dp unless `unitdp=4` is passed — the cast is all 2dp, so don't pass it.
125	
126	## Contacts
127	
128	Create one per cast row (a batch `{"Contacts":[...]}` envelope is UNVERIFIED on the pages checked — 15 sequential calls are trivially inside the rate budget):
129	
130	```jsonc
131	// PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Contacts  (PUT = create-new per the API-wide rule)
132	{ "Name": "Thameside Timber Ltd", "EmailAddress": "accounts@thamesidetimber.co.uk" }
133	```
134	
135	Store the returned `ContactID` — Xero warns Contact `Name` "may no longer be a unique field" in future; ContactID is the only durable key. `IsSupplier` cannot be set: Xero flips it automatically when the first ACCPAY bill lands.
136	
137	## Google Sheet receipt log
138	
139	Columns, exactly and in this order: `date, po_ref, supplier, items, qty, signed_by, photo_link`.
140	
141	One row per PO **except PO-1001** (14 rows total — the missing row IS the demo star's gap). `po_ref` joins to `PurchaseOrderNumber`; `supplier` matches Contact `Name`; `qty` = units actually received = bill qty (so #2's only defect is bill vs PO). `photo_link` points at a real delivery-note photo in Drive (live-data rule — replace placeholders before rehearsal). Three example rows (seed day 2026-07-04 → date = D−7):
142	
143	| date | po_ref | supplier | items | qty | signed_by | photo_link |
144	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
145	| 2026-06-27 | PO-1002 | Brixton Plumbing Merchants Ltd | 15mm copper tube 3m and brassware bundle | 27 | D. Okafor | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1002 |
146	| 2026-06-27 | PO-1004 | Hackney Plant Hire Ltd | 1.5t mini excavator, day rate | 12 | S. Whelan | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1004 |
147	| 2026-06-27 | PO-1006 | Croydon Builders Merchants Ltd | Aerated concrete blocks 100mm, per pack | 84 | M. Price | https://drive.google.com/file/d/receipt-po-1006 |
148	
149	## Idempotency — check-by-reference before EVERY create
150	
151	The seed must be safe to re-run: look up by reference, skip on hit, create only what is absent. Never check by amount (see Traps). Exact lookups:
152	
153	| Entity | Reference | Exact lookup | Notes |
154	|---|---|---|---|
155	| Contact | `Name` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/Contacts?where=Name%3D%22Thameside%20Timber%20Ltd%22` (unencoded: `where=Name="Thameside Timber Ltd"`) | Optimised equals filter; case- and accent-insensitive; do NOT append `ToLower()` |
156	| Bill | `InvoiceNumber` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/Invoices?where=Type%3D%3D%22ACCPAY%22%20AND%20InvoiceNumber%3D%3D%22TT-8841%22&page=1` (unencoded: `where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND InvoiceNumber=="TT-8841"`) | Both fields optimised. ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique — ANY hit means "already seeded, skip" |
157	| Purchase order | `PurchaseOrderNumber` | `GET /api.xro/2.0/PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100` then match `PurchaseOrderNumber` client-side | A generic `where` param is NOT documented on the PO endpoint (UNVERIFIED — check before relying) — do not use one. Paging is enforced by default; one page covers the cast; if the org ever holds >100 POs, walk `pagination.pageCount` |
158	| Sheet row | `po_ref` | Read the sheet, match `po_ref` string | Plain string compare, uppercase both sides |
159	
160	Rules:
161	
162	- The `where` value MUST be percent-encoded (spaces, quotes, `==`).
163	- Treat a hit whose `Status` is `DELETED` or `VOIDED` as absent and recreate (whether list GETs return deleted docs by default is UNVERIFIED — check before relying; this rule is safe either way).
164	- Persist returned `ContactID` / `PurchaseOrderID` / `InvoiceID` locally after create — they are the durable keys; references are only the lookup handles.
165	- Xero's `Idempotency-Key` header is NOT a substitute: keys expire after 6 minutes and cache errors too. Use it (one fresh UUID per create) to guard against double-submit within a run; check-by-reference is the durable guard across runs.
166	
167	## Ordering — contacts → POs → bills → sheet rows
168	
169	| Step | Why it cannot move |
170	|---|---|
171	| 1. Contacts | Both `/Invoices` and `/PurchaseOrders` require the `ContactID` of an EXISTING contact — the PO endpoint never creates contacts, and posting a bill with a bare contact name silently creates a duplicate contact. The ContactIDs minted here are inputs to steps 2–3. |
172	| 2. POs | Bills carry the PO number in `Reference`. Xero would not reject a bill referencing a nonexistent PO — the ordering is for referential truth: the match engine joins bill→PO, so the join target must exist before anything points at it. |
173	| 3. Bills | Consume ContactIDs (step 1) and PO numbers (step 2). |
174	| 4. Sheet rows | `po_ref` and `supplier` join back to steps 1–2; the sheet lives outside Xero, so it is written after Xero state is confirmed good. |
175	
176	## Rate budget
177	
178	60 calls/min/tenant, 5 concurrent, 5,000/day. First full run, sequential: 1 token + 1 org + 15 contact lookups + 15 contact creates + 1 PO list + 1 PO batch create + 1 bill lookup list + 1 bill batch create ≈ 36 calls — one minute of budget. Run strictly sequentially; on `429` read `Retry-After` and wait (`X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names the limit). Batch bills and POs in single `{"Invoices":[...]}` / `{"PurchaseOrders":[...]}` calls — never one call per document.
179	
180	## Resetting seeded data
181	
182	| Doc | Seeded status | Documented off-switch |
183	|---|---|---|
184	| Bill | SUBMITTED | POST status update to `DELETED` (legal from DRAFT/SUBMITTED). Once the demo has AUTHORISED it: `VOIDED` only — AUTHORISED can never return to DRAFT/SUBMITTED. If PAID: reverse the payment first (`POST /Payments/{PaymentID}` body `{"Status":"DELETED"}`), then void (void is documented only for approved invoices with no payments applied). |
185	| PO | AUTHORISED | Official delete example covers a DRAFT PO only; deleting an AUTHORISED PO is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Whether a DELETED PO's number can be reused is UNVERIFIED — if a PO must be replaced, bump to a fresh number series (PO-2001…) rather than fight it. |
186	| Contact | ACTIVE | No delete exists in the docs; `ContactStatus: ARCHIVED` is the only documented end-state ("can no longer be used in transactions"); the exact write path to set it is UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Contacts are harmless to leave in place — re-runs skip them by Name. |
187	
188	## Snippets
189	
190	Both compile standalone under `strict`, `target=ES2022`, `module=commonjs`, `esModuleInterop`, `types=["node"]` — node builtins only, no npm deps, no top-level await. `.env` is loaded by the runner (e.g. `node --env-file=.env` on Node 20.6+); the scripts only read `process.env` and assert.
191	
192	- `scripts/preflight.ts` — the pre-flight above: env assertions, token mint + scope check, `GET /Organisations` printout. Read-only. Run before every seed run.
193	- `scripts/check-by-reference.ts` — the three lookup functions with the exact filters from the idempotency table, plus a `main()` that probes the demo star's three references (Thameside Timber Ltd / PO-1001 / TT-8841) and prints EXISTS/ABSENT. Read-only. `seed/` imports the same shapes, not this file.
194	
195	## Traps
196	
197	- **Re-running with changed amounts creates near-duplicates.** Check by reference, never by amount. If you edit a cast amount, the reference lookup still hits the old document and skips — the org keeps the OLD amount, and "create if the amount differs" would mint a second TT-8841 (ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is non-unique; Xero will not stop you). To change an amount: delete/void the old doc first, or bump the reference.
198	- **ACCPAY `InvoiceNumber` is NON-unique** and displays as "Reference" in the Xero UI. An empty lookup does not mean Xero would block a duplicate. Store `InvoiceID` after create.
199	- **Xero auto-uppercases some references** UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Neutralised by construction: every seed reference (PO-1001, TT-8841) is already uppercase-only, and the match engine should compare references case-insensitively regardless.
200	- **Deleting seeded data is status-dependent.** Bills: DRAFT/SUBMITTED → `DELETED`; AUTHORISED → `VOIDED` only; PAID/VOIDED/DELETED are terminal. POs: only the DRAFT→DELETED path is documented. Plan resets BEFORE authorising things.
201	- **Contacts are not auto-deduplicated.** A bill posted with a contact `Name` instead of `ContactID` creates a NEW contact every time and litters the org. Duplicate suppliers appearing in the org = this bug.
202	- **Contact `Name` is a lookup handle, not a key.** Xero warns it may stop being unique; the optimised Name filter is case- and accent-insensitive; Name rejects leading/trailing whitespace and repeating spaces — trim before create AND before compare.
203	- **`Idempotency-Key` will not save a re-run.** Keys live 6 minutes and cache errors: a keyed request that failed returns the same cached error even after the cause is fixed — GET to check reality, recreate with a NEW key.
204	- **`summarizeErrors=false` hides failures in a 200.** Every batch element must be checked for `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` or the seed reports success while bills are silently missing.
205	- **Multi-invoice GETs without paging return NO line items.** Always add `page=1` when verifying seeded line details.
206	- **Updating a document's lines: omit an existing `LineItemID` and that line is DELETED.** If a re-run repairs a doc via POST update, echo every existing LineItemID.
207	
208	## Errors → real cause
209	
210	| Symptom | Real cause |
211	|---|---|
212	| `unauthorized_client` from the token endpoint | `XERO_CLIENT_ID`/`XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` empty or wrong — `client_id=undefined` got sent. Fix the env assertion trail, not Xero. |
213	| 401 mid-run | Access token expired (~30 min). No refresh token exists on client_credentials — mint a new token. |
214	| 403 on Contacts/Invoices/PurchaseOrders | Scope missing from the token: not sent in the token request body, or not enabled on the Custom Connection app config (both ends must agree). |
215	| 400 `Idempotency Key ... is used with a different request` | Same key reused within 6 min with a changed URL, body, or method. New create = new key. |
216	| The same error keeps returning after you fixed the cause | Cached idempotent error. GET to check whether the doc exists; recreate with a NEW key. |
217	| 429 | Rate limit — `X-Rate-Limit-Problem` names which (minute/day/concurrent); wait `Retry-After` seconds. |
218	| HTTP 200 but documents missing in Xero | `summarizeErrors=false` batch: per-element `StatusAttributeString: "ERROR"` with `ValidationErrors` you did not read. |
219	| 400 `A validation exception occurred` | Read `Elements[].ValidationErrors`. e.g. "A Contact must be specified for this type of transaction" = PO posted without an existing ContactID. |
220	| Duplicate suppliers in the org | A bill was posted with `Contact: { "Name": ... }` instead of `ContactID`. |
221	| Bills list shows no line items | Multi-invoice GET without `page` — add `page=1`. |
222	| 400 on a filtered GET | `where` value not percent-encoded, or an unoptimised filter expression. |
223	
224	<!--
225	Sources (fact-check trail):
226	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md — skill format/discipline, requireEnv pattern + unauthorized_client causal chain, idempotency-key rules (6 min, cached errors, 400 different-request), 429/Retry-After, contact-dedup gotcha, scope minimalism/granular scopes, /api.xro/2.0/Organisations verified 200 (Replit path table). Underlying: XeroAPI/xero-prompt-library SKILL.md files.
227	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md — Invoices (ACCPAY, statuses + legal transitions, InvoiceNumber non-unique + displays-as-Reference, create envelopes, summarizeErrors, optimised where fields incl. Type/InvoiceNumber/Contact.Name, pagination, LineItemID delete-on-omit, no-discounts-on-ACCPAY, unitdp), PurchaseOrders (fields, PurchaseOrderNumber unique/auto-generate, AUTHORISED-on-create shown in examples, DRAFT→DELETED example, no documented `where` param, paging enforced 100/page), LineAmountTypes Exclusive default, Payments delete/reverse. Underlying: developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/{invoices,purchaseorders,payments,types}, verified 2026-07-04.
228	- .tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md — AP surface = /Invoices (bills) + /PurchaseOrders + /Payments + /Contacts; three-way-match seed-shape hints. Underlying: xero-prompt-library/php/accounts-payable-symfony.txt.
229	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-evidence.md — Contacts (Name required/constraints/uniqueness warning, ContactID durable key, IsSupplier auto-set, optimised where=Name="..." case/accent-insensitive, searchTerm, pagination), ContactStatus ARCHIVED. Underlying: developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/contacts, verified 2026-07-04.
230	- .tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md — custom connections (token endpoint, Basic auth, client_credentials, single-org quote, no tenant header in canonical example, scopes at both ends, token response fields incl. no refresh_token, ~30 min lifetime), rate limits (60/min, 5 concurrent, Retry-After, X-Rate-Limit-Problem), where-encoding, summarizeErrors 200 behaviour, pagination object, Idempotency-Key guide facts. Underlying: developer.xero.com oauth2 guides + requests-and-responses, verified 2026-07-04.
231	- .tmp/xero-refs/xero-mcp-server/src/handlers — grepped 2026-07-04: zero PurchaseOrder handlers → POs are raw-API only.
232	- Tolerance ±2%/£5 and match-state names (MATCHED / MISSING_RECEIPT / AMOUNT_MISMATCH): context/architecture.md (project truth, not a Xero fact).
233	- Auto-uppercase of references: searched all fact sheets and cloned repos 2026-07-04 — no evidence either way; left UNVERIFIED.
234	-->

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:19:27Z)**_

All five fix agents are running in parallel. I'll get notified as each completes; once all five are done I'll compile-check every snippet, run a consistency sweep across the skills, attempt Gate G0, and commit.

**Summary of what the adversarial verification caught** (the reason this pass was worth it):

- **Demo-killer**: the seed spec put the PO join key in the bill's `Reference` field — but `Reference` is ACCREC-only and Xero silently drops it on ACCPAY bills. Every seeded bill would have come back with no PO link and all 15 would verdict MISSING_PO at Gate G1. The fix moves the PO ref into line-item text (realistic supplier phrasing) with `InvoiceNumber` as the idempotency key.
- **Gate-blocker**: the seed preflight checked org identity via `GET /Organisation`, which 403s under our four scopes (needs `accounting.settings.read`) — the seed could never start. Switched to `GET /connections`.
- **Judged-moment failure**: the payments skill documented `ts-node`, which errors outright on Node 22 (`ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION`), and its script never loaded `.env`, so the "must work first try" payment snippet failed on try one, twice over. Also: its idempotency check silently skipped when the optional reference arg was omitted, violating the every-write-idempotent rule.
- **Story-breaker**: the amount-mismatch demo gap was qty-shaped (27 vs 25 at the same unit price), which a faithful match engine names QTY_MISMATCH, not AMOUNT_MISMATCH — the G1 test would disagree with the engine. Now price-shaped.
- **Quota trap**: three skills stated 5,000 calls/day, but new Xero apps default to the starter tier at 1,000/day — off by 5x for exactly the app we'll create tomorrow.
- Plus gold mined from the MCP server source: `create-invoice` remaps `reference` → `InvoiceNumber` for ACCPAY (Reference left empty), hardcodes DueDate to today+30 with no param, and `update-invoice` refuses non-DRAFT invoices — so approving bills must go through the raw API.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/scripts/preflight.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/scripts/preflight.ts
2	//
3	// Read-only pre-flight for the demo-org seed. Run BEFORE any seed write.
4	// Compiles standalone: strict, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, esModuleInterop,
5	// types=["node"]. Node builtins only. Node 18+ (global fetch).
6	//
7	// .env is loaded by the runner (e.g. `node --env-file=.env` on Node 20.6+);
8	// this script only reads process.env and asserts.
9	//
10	// Asserts, in order:
11	//   1. XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named MissingEnv errors)
12	//   2. client_credentials token mints and carries the seed scopes (MissingScope)
13	//   3. GET /Organisations returns 200 — prints the raw body so a human confirms
14	//      this is the DEMO org before anything is written into it.
15	//
16	// Custom Connections are bound to ONE Xero organisation, so there is no
17	// XERO_TENANT_ID env var and no xero-tenant-id header anywhere below.
18	
19	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
20	const API_BASE = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
21	
22	// Granular scopes only. accounting.invoices covers Invoices AND PurchaseOrders;
23	// accounting.contacts covers Contacts. Scopes must ALSO be enabled on the
24	// Custom Connection app config — they are configured at both ends.
25	const SEED_SCOPES = "accounting.contacts accounting.invoices";
26	
27	export interface TokenResponse {
28	  access_token: string;
29	  expires_in: number;
30	  token_type: string;
31	  scope: string;
32	}
33	
34	export function requireEnv(name: string): string {
35	  const v = process.env[name];
36	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
37	    throw new Error(
38	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client, ` +
39	        `which points away from the real cause. Set it in .env and retry.`,
40	    );
41	  }
42	  return v;
43	}
44	
45	export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<TokenResponse> {
46	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
47	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
48	
49	  // Log the shape, never the secret.
50	  console.log("[seed:preflight] token request", {
51	    client_id_len: clientId.length,
52	    scope: SEED_SCOPES,
53	  });
54	
55	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
56	    method: "POST",
57	    headers: {
58	      Authorization:
59	        "Basic " + Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64"),
60	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
61	    },
62	    body: new URLSearchParams({
63	      grant_type: "client_credentials",
64	      scope: SEED_SCOPES,
65	    }).toString(),
66	  });
67	
68	  if (!res.ok) {
69	    const body = await res.text();
70	    throw new Error(
71	      `TokenRequestFailed: HTTP ${res.status} from ${TOKEN_URL} — ${body}`,
72	    );
73	  }
74	
75	  const token = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
76	
77	  for (const s of SEED_SCOPES.split(" ")) {
78	    if (!token.scope.includes(s)) {
79	      throw new Error(
80	        `MissingScope: token granted "${token.scope}" but the seed needs "${s}" — ` +
81	          `enable it on the Custom Connection app config AND send it in the token ` +
82	          `request body (custom-connection scopes are configured at both ends).`,
83	      );
84	    }
85	  }
86	
87	  return token;
88	}
89	
90	async function main(): Promise<void> {
91	  const token = await getAccessToken();
92	  console.log("[seed:preflight] token OK", {
93	    expires_in: token.expires_in,
94	    scope: token.scope,
95	  });
96	
97	  // Smoke test + org identity check. NO xero-tenant-id header: a custom
98	  // connection token is already bound to exactly one organisation.
99	  const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/Organisations`, {
100	    headers: {
101	      Authorization: `Bearer ${token.access_token}`,
102	      Accept: "application/json",
103	    },
104	  });
105	  const body = await res.text();
106	  if (!res.ok) {
107	    throw new Error(
108	      `OrgCheckFailed: HTTP ${res.status} from GET /Organisations — ${body}`,
109	    );
110	  }
111	
112	  // Print raw JSON, don't parse: a human must confirm this is the DEMO org
113	  // (and that the base currency is GBP) before any seed write.
114	  console.log(
115	    "[seed:preflight] GET /Organisations 200 — confirm this is the DEMO org before seeding:",
116	  );
117	  console.log(body);
118	}
119	
120	if (require.main === module) {
121	  main().catch((err: unknown) => {
122	    console.error(err);
123	    process.exitCode = 1;
124	  });
125	}
126
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-demo-seed/scripts/check-by-reference.ts
2	//
3	// Read-only. The three check-by-reference lookups the seed MUST run before
4	// every create (idempotency: skip on hit, create only what is absent).
5	// Compiles standalone: strict, target=ES2022, module=commonjs, esModuleInterop,
6	// types=["node"]. Node builtins only. Node 18+ (global fetch).
7	//
8	// Exact lookups (see SKILL.md idempotency table):
9	//   Contact:  GET /Contacts?where=Name="<Contact Name>"
10	//             optimised equals filter; case- and accent-insensitive
11	//   Bill:     GET /Invoices?where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND InvoiceNumber=="<ref>"&page=1
12	//             both fields optimised; ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique —
13	//             ANY hit means "already seeded, skip"
14	//   PO:       GET /PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100 + client-side match on
15	//             PurchaseOrderNumber — a generic `where` param is NOT documented
16	//             on the PO endpoint, so none is used here.
17	//
18	// .env is loaded by the runner (e.g. `node --env-file=.env` on Node 20.6+).
19	
20	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
21	const API_BASE = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0";
22	const SEED_SCOPES = "accounting.contacts accounting.invoices";
23	
24	interface TokenResponse {
25	  access_token: string;
26	  expires_in: number;
27	  token_type: string;
28	  scope: string;
29	}
30	
31	export interface XeroContact {
32	  ContactID: string;
33	  Name: string;
34	}
35	
36	export interface XeroInvoice {
37	  InvoiceID: string;
38	  InvoiceNumber: string;
39	  Status: string;
40	  Total?: number;
41	}
42	
43	export interface XeroPurchaseOrder {
44	  PurchaseOrderID: string;
45	  PurchaseOrderNumber: string;
46	  Status: string;
47	}
48	
49	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
50	  const v = process.env[name];
51	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
52	    throw new Error(
53	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — this surfaces from Xero as unauthorized_client, ` +
54	        `which points away from the real cause. Set it in .env and retry.`,
55	    );
56	  }
57	  return v;
58	}
59	
60	async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
61	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
62	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
63	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
64	    method: "POST",
65	    headers: {
66	      Authorization:
67	        "Basic " + Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64"),
68	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
69	    },
70	    body: new URLSearchParams({
71	      grant_type: "client_credentials",
72	      scope: SEED_SCOPES,
73	    }).toString(),
74	  });
75	  if (!res.ok) {
76	    const body = await res.text();
77	    throw new Error(
78	      `TokenRequestFailed: HTTP ${res.status} from ${TOKEN_URL} — ${body}`,
79	    );
80	  }
81	  const token = (await res.json()) as TokenResponse;
82	  return token.access_token;
83	}
84	
85	async function apiGet<T>(accessToken: string, pathAndQuery: string): Promise<T> {
86	  // No xero-tenant-id header: custom connection tokens are bound to one org.
87	  const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${pathAndQuery}`, {
88	    headers: {
89	      Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`,
90	      Accept: "application/json",
91	    },
92	  });
93	  if (!res.ok) {
94	    const body = await res.text();
95	    throw new Error(
96	      `XeroGetFailed: HTTP ${res.status} from GET ${pathAndQuery} — ${body}`,
97	    );
98	  }
99	  return (await res.json()) as T;
100	}
101	
102	/** Contact check-by-reference. Unencoded filter: where=Name="Thameside Timber Ltd" */
103	export async function findContactByName(
104	  accessToken: string,
105	  name: string,
106	): Promise<XeroContact | null> {
107	  const where = encodeURIComponent(`Name="${name}"`);
108	  const data = await apiGet<{ Contacts?: XeroContact[] }>(
109	    accessToken,
110	    `/Contacts?where=${where}`,
111	  );
112	  return data.Contacts?.[0] ?? null;
113	}
114	
115	/**
116	 * Bill check-by-reference. Unencoded filter:
117	 *   where=Type=="ACCPAY" AND InvoiceNumber=="TT-8841"
118	 * Returns ALL hits: ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique in Xero, so the caller
119	 * treats a non-empty result as "already seeded — skip".
120	 */
121	export async function findBillsByInvoiceNumber(
122	  accessToken: string,
123	  invoiceNumber: string,
124	): Promise<XeroInvoice[]> {
125	  const where = encodeURIComponent(
126	    `Type=="ACCPAY" AND InvoiceNumber=="${invoiceNumber}"`,
127	  );
128	  const data = await apiGet<{ Invoices?: XeroInvoice[] }>(
129	    accessToken,
130	    `/Invoices?where=${where}&page=1`,
131	  );
132	  return data.Invoices ?? [];
133	}
134	
135	/**
136	 * PO check-by-reference. No documented `where` support on /PurchaseOrders —
137	 * page through and match PurchaseOrderNumber client-side. Paging is enforced
138	 * by default; one page of 100 covers the whole 15-PO cast. If the org ever
139	 * holds more than 100 POs, walk pages until the pagination pageCount.
140	 */
141	export async function findPurchaseOrderByNumber(
142	  accessToken: string,
143	  poNumber: string,
144	): Promise<XeroPurchaseOrder | null> {
145	  const data = await apiGet<{ PurchaseOrders?: XeroPurchaseOrder[] }>(
146	    accessToken,
147	    "/PurchaseOrders?page=1&pageSize=100",
148	  );
149	  return (
150	    (data.PurchaseOrders ?? []).find(
151	      (po) => po.PurchaseOrderNumber === poNumber,
152	    ) ?? null
153	  );
154	}
155	
156	async function main(): Promise<void> {
157	  const accessToken = await getAccessToken();
158	
159	  // Probe the demo star's three references.
160	  const contact = await findContactByName(accessToken, "Thameside Timber Ltd");
161	  console.log(
162	    `Contact "Thameside Timber Ltd": ${contact ? `EXISTS (${contact.ContactID})` : "ABSENT"}`,
163	  );
164	
165	  const po = await findPurchaseOrderByNumber(accessToken, "PO-1001");
166	  console.log(
167	    `PurchaseOrder PO-1001: ${po ? `EXISTS (${po.PurchaseOrderID}, ${po.Status})` : "ABSENT"}`,
168	  );
169	
170	  const bills = await findBillsByInvoiceNumber(accessToken, "TT-8841");
171	  console.log(
172	    `Bill TT-8841: ${bills.length > 0 ? `EXISTS (${bills.length} hit(s) — ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is non-unique)` : "ABSENT"}`,
173	  );
174	}
175	
176	if (require.main === module) {
177	  main().catch((err: unknown) => {
178	    console.error(err);
179	    process.exitCode = 1;
180	  });
181	}
182
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./.claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts`

```text
1	// .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
2	//
3	// Gate G0 — proves the Xero Custom Connection works end to end, before any
4	// feature code is written. Run from repo root:
5	//
6	//   npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts
7	//
8	// What it does:
9	//   1. Parses <repo-root>/.env itself (no dotenv dep); vars already in
10	//      process.env win over the file.
11	//   2. Asserts XERO_CLIENT_ID / XERO_CLIENT_SECRET non-empty (named errors —
12	//      an empty client id surfaces from Xero as `unauthorized_client`).
13	//   3. Fetches a client_credentials token from identity.xero.com.
14	//      On 400 `invalid_scope` (granular-only app) it retries once with the
15	//      granular equivalents of the project's broad scopes.
16	//   4. GETs the connected organisation (name via GET /connections — our
17	//      scopes deliberately exclude accounting.settings, so GET /Organisation
18	//      would 403) and 1 invoice.
19	//   5. Prints org name, invoice count, and the rate-limit headers.
20	//
21	// No npm deps. Node 18+ (native fetch). Compiles standalone under
22	// strict / ES2022 / commonjs / types=["node"].
23	
24	import * as fs from "node:fs";
25	import * as path from "node:path";
26	
27	const TOKEN_URL = "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token";
28	const CONNECTIONS_URL = "https://api.xero.com/connections";
29	const INVOICES_URL = "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Invoices?page=1&pageSize=1";
30	
31	// The exact scopes this project needs — nothing more. See SKILL.md.
32	const PROJECT_SCOPES =
33	  "accounting.transactions accounting.contacts accounting.attachments accounting.reports.read";
34	
35	// Granular equivalents (post-29-April-2026 custom connections may reject the
36	// broad scopes with 400 invalid_scope). Mapping per Xero's scope migration:
37	// accounting.transactions -> invoices+payments+banktransactions+manualjournals,
38	// accounting.reports.read -> the eight accounting.reports.*.read scopes.
39	const GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES = [
40	  "accounting.invoices",
41	  "accounting.payments",
42	  "accounting.banktransactions",
43	  "accounting.manualjournals",
44	  "accounting.contacts",
45	  "accounting.attachments",
46	  "accounting.reports.aged.read",
47	  "accounting.reports.balancesheet.read",
48	  "accounting.reports.banksummary.read",
49	  "accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read",
50	  "accounting.reports.executivesummary.read",
51	  "accounting.reports.profitandloss.read",
52	  "accounting.reports.trialbalance.read",
53	  "accounting.reports.taxreports.read",
54	].join(" ");
55	
56	class MissingEnvError extends Error {
57	  constructor(varName: string) {
58	    super(
59	      `${varName} is empty — set it in .env at repo root. ` +
60	        `If an empty client id reaches Xero it surfaces as a misleading OAuth error ` +
61	        `(invalid_request on the token endpoint, unauthorized_client in code flow), ` +
62	        `which points away from the real cause.`,
63	    );
64	    this.name = "MissingEnv";
65	  }
66	}
67	
68	class XeroTokenError extends Error {
69	  constructor(
70	    public readonly status: number,
71	    public readonly body: string,
72	  ) {
73	    super(`token request failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
74	    this.name = "XeroTokenError";
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	class XeroApiError extends Error {
79	  constructor(
80	    public readonly status: number,
81	    url: string,
82	    body: string,
83	  ) {
84	    super(`GET ${url} failed with HTTP ${status}: ${body}`);
85	    this.name = "XeroApiError";
86	  }
87	}
88	
89	// --- 1. Tiny .env parser (existing process.env wins; missing file tolerated) ---
90	
91	function loadDotEnv(envPath: string): void {
92	  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
93	    console.warn(`[verify-auth] no .env at ${envPath} — relying on process.env only`);
94	    return;
95	  }
96	  for (const rawLine of fs.readFileSync(envPath, "utf8").split(/\r?\n/)) {
97	    const line = rawLine.trim().replace(/^export\s+/, "");
98	    if (line === "" || line.startsWith("#")) continue;
99	    const eq = line.indexOf("=");
100	    if (eq <= 0) continue;
101	    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
102	    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
103	    if (
104	      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"') && value.length >= 2) ||
105	      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'") && value.length >= 2)
106	    ) {
107	      value = value.slice(1, -1);
108	    }
109	    const existing = process.env[key];
110	    if (existing === undefined || existing === "") {
111	      process.env[key] = value;
112	    }
113	  }
114	}
115	
116	// --- 2. Named env assertions (fail fast, never log the value itself) ---
117	
118	function requireEnv(name: string): string {
119	  const v = process.env[name];
120	  if (v === undefined || v.trim() === "") {
121	    throw new MissingEnvError(name);
122	  }
123	  return v.trim();
124	}
125	
126	// --- 3. client_credentials token (Basic auth + form-encoded body, never JSON) ---
127	
128	interface TokenResult {
129	  accessToken: string;
130	  expiresIn: number;
131	  scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" | "granular fallback";
132	}
133	
134	async function requestToken(
135	  clientId: string,
136	  clientSecret: string,
137	  scope: string,
138	): Promise<{ accessToken: string; expiresIn: number }> {
139	  const basic = Buffer.from(`${clientId}:${clientSecret}`).toString("base64");
140	  const res = await fetch(TOKEN_URL, {
141	    method: "POST",
142	    headers: {
143	      Authorization: `Basic ${basic}`,
144	      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
145	      Accept: "application/json",
146	    },
147	    body: `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=${encodeURIComponent(scope)}`,
148	  });
149	  const text = await res.text();
150	  if (!res.ok) {
151	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, text);
152	  }
153	  const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { access_token?: unknown; expires_in?: unknown };
154	  if (typeof parsed.access_token !== "string" || parsed.access_token === "") {
155	    throw new XeroTokenError(res.status, "response body had no access_token");
156	  }
157	  return {
158	    accessToken: parsed.access_token,
159	    expiresIn: typeof parsed.expires_in === "number" ? parsed.expires_in : 0,
160	  };
161	}
162	
163	async function getTokenWithFallback(clientId: string, clientSecret: string): Promise<TokenResult> {
164	  const scopeOverride = process.env["XERO_SCOPES"];
165	  const scopes =
166	    scopeOverride !== undefined && scopeOverride.trim() !== "" ? scopeOverride.trim() : PROJECT_SCOPES;
167	  try {
168	    const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, scopes);
169	    return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "project (broad)" };
170	  } catch (err) {
171	    if (err instanceof XeroTokenError && err.status === 400 && err.body.includes("invalid_scope")) {
172	      console.warn(
173	        "[verify-auth] broad scopes rejected (invalid_scope) — this app only has granular scopes. " +
174	          "Retrying with granular equivalents. Set XERO_SCOPES in .env to the granular set to silence this.",
175	      );
176	      const t = await requestToken(clientId, clientSecret, GRANULAR_FALLBACK_SCOPES);
177	      return { ...t, scopeSetUsed: "granular fallback" };
178	    }
179	    throw err;
180	  }
181	}
182	
183	// --- 4. Organisation + one invoice ---
184	
185	interface XeroConnection {
186	  tenantId?: string;
187	  tenantName?: string;
188	}
189	
190	async function getJson(url: string, accessToken: string): Promise<{ body: unknown; res: Response }> {
191	  // Per the custom-connections docs, API calls need exactly these two headers.
192	  // No xero-tenant-id: a custom connection is bound to a single organisation.
193	  const res = await fetch(url, {
194	    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, Accept: "application/json" },
195	  });
196	  const text = await res.text();
197	  if (!res.ok) {
198	    throw new XeroApiError(res.status, url, text);
199	  }
200	  return { body: JSON.parse(text) as unknown, res };
201	}
202	
203	async function main(): Promise<void> {
204	  loadDotEnv(path.resolve(__dirname, "../../../../.env"));
205	
206	  const clientId = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID");
207	  const clientSecret = requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_SECRET");
208	  // Log lengths only — never the values.
209	  console.log(
210	    `[verify-auth] env ok (client_id_len: ${clientId.length}, client_secret_len: ${clientSecret.length})`,
211	  );
212	
213	  const token = await getTokenWithFallback(clientId, clientSecret);
214	  console.log(
215	    `[verify-auth] token ok — scope set: ${token.scopeSetUsed}, expires_in: ${token.expiresIn}s`,
216	  );
217	
218	  const { body: connectionsBody } = await getJson(CONNECTIONS_URL, token.accessToken);
219	  const connections = Array.isArray(connectionsBody) ? (connectionsBody as XeroConnection[]) : [];
220	  if (connections.length === 0) {
221	    throw new XeroApiError(
222	      200,
223	      CONNECTIONS_URL,
224	      "token issued but no organisation is connected. Has a Xero org admin authorised this " +
225	        "Custom Connection app in the developer portal? Tokens are useless until that is done.",
226	    );
227	  }
228	  const first = connections[0];
229	  const orgName = first?.tenantName ?? first?.tenantId ?? "(unnamed connection)";
230	  console.log(`[verify-auth] organisation: ${orgName}`);
231	
232	  const { body: invoicesBody, res: invoicesRes } = await getJson(INVOICES_URL, token.accessToken);
233	  const inv = invoicesBody as { Invoices?: unknown[]; pagination?: { itemCount?: unknown } };
234	  const returned = Array.isArray(inv.Invoices) ? inv.Invoices.length : 0;
235	  const itemCount = typeof inv.pagination?.itemCount === "number" ? inv.pagination.itemCount : null;
236	  console.log(
237	    `[verify-auth] invoices returned: ${returned}` + (itemCount !== null ? ` (org total: ${itemCount})` : ""),
238	  );
239	
240	  console.log(
241	    "[verify-auth] rate limits remaining — " +
242	      `minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-MinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
243	      `day: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-DayLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}, ` +
244	      `app-minute: ${invoicesRes.headers.get("X-AppMinLimit-Remaining") ?? "n/a"}`,
245	  );
246	  console.log("[verify-auth] G0 PASS");
247	}
248	
249	main().catch((err: unknown) => {
250	  if (err instanceof Error) {
251	    console.error(`[verify-auth] FAIL — ${err.name}: ${err.message}`);
252	  } else {
253	    console.error("[verify-auth] FAIL —", err);
254	  }
255	  console.error("[verify-auth] see the error table in .claude/skills/xero-auth/SKILL.md");
256	  process.exit(1);
257	});
258
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
32:| InvoiceNumber | ACCREC: unique alphanumeric. **ACCPAY: NON-unique alphanumeric code; "This value will also display as Reference in the UI."** Max length 255. Auto-generated for ACCREC if missing. [INV] |
33:| Reference | "Additional reference number" (max length 255). [INV] |
59:- Partially/fully PAID ACCPAY bills can only have these fields updated: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (unless payment was via Credit Note or lines contain a CisLabourExpense account), Url, LineItems Description, AccountCode (except CIS account codes), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate. [INV]
73:- Optimised where fields (equals): Status, Contact.ContactID, Contact.Name, Contact.ContactNumber, Reference, InvoiceNumber, InvoiceId, Type, AmountPaid; (equals+range >, >=, <, <=): Date, DueDate, AmountDue.
75:- `SearchTerm` does case-insensitive text search across InvoiceNumber and Reference.
116:| Reference | "Additional reference number". |
168:- Optional: Reference, IsReconciled (boolean; useful for conversion scenarios), BankAmount, CurrencyRate, Status. [PAY]
246:- Idempotency check-by-reference: for ACCPAY, `InvoiceNumber` is NON-unique [INV], so existence checks must combine fields (e.g. Contact + InvoiceNumber + Total) or store InvoiceID after create.
===
55:- PAID is set by the system: "Once an invoice is fully paid the status will change to PAID." You cannot create PAID directly — create AUTHORISED then apply a payment ("Creating a PAID Invoice" is a two-step process). [INV]
59:- Partially/fully PAID ACCPAY bills can only have these fields updated: Reference, DueDate, InvoiceNumber, BrandingThemeID, Contact (unless payment was via Credit Note or lines contain a CisLabourExpense account), Url, LineItems Description, AccountCode (except CIS account codes), Tracking, PlannedPaymentDate. [INV]
179:- Invoice status stays AUTHORISED while partially paid: AUTHORISED = "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" [TYP]; "Once an invoice is fully paid the system will move it to the PAID status." [INV]
219:| AUTHORISED | "An Invoice that is Approved and Awaiting Payment OR partially paid" |

SKILL.md:13:2. **Confirm the app is a Custom Connection and has been authorised.** A non-Custom-Connection app (client_credentials grant not enabled) answers 400 `unauthorized_client` at the token endpoint — the most common real cause of that error. App creation includes selecting scopes and "who will authorise the connection" — an org admin must complete that authorisation before tokens are useful. (Exact failure mode of an unauthorised app: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. `verify-auth.ts` treats "token OK but zero connections" as this case.) Custom Connections only exist for AU/NZ/UK/US orgs and are free against the Demo Company.
SKILL.md:70:From 29 April 2026 all custom connections have access to granular scopes; new custom connections lose `accounting.journals.read`; existing custom connections can keep using broad scopes until **September 2027**. Whether a custom connection created today can still request the broad `accounting.transactions` / `accounting.reports.read`: UNVERIFIED — check before relying. Handle it empirically, the way Xero's own MCP server does: request broad, and on HTTP 400 `invalid_scope` retry with the granular equivalents. `verify-auth.ts` implements exactly this fallback and warns loudly when it triggers. Granular mapping for our set:
SKILL.md:89:No `xero-tenant-id`. (An explicit docs sentence "the header is not required" does not exist — UNVERIFIED — check before relying — but the canonical example omits it. Contrast: standard code-flow tokens REQUIRE `xero-tenant-id` on every call.) Xero's own MCP server sends a tenant id anyway, resolving it at runtime via `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` → `connections[0].tenantId`.
SKILL.md:97:The MCP server requests a brand-new token plus a `GET /connections` on **every** tool call: two wasted round-trips of latency per call. (Whether identity.xero.com token grants and `GET /connections` count toward the tenant 60/min budget is UNVERIFIED — check before relying — the caching case stands on latency and the 5-concurrent cap alone.) Do the opposite. `scripts/token-cache.ts` is the drop-in module:
SKILL.md:133:Also: max 5 concurrent calls per tenant. Whether identity.xero.com token grants and `GET /connections` count toward these budgets is UNVERIFIED — check before relying.
SKILL.md:169:| Token OK, `GET /connections` returns `[]` | Custom Connection not yet authorised by an org admin (that this is the only cause is UNVERIFIED — check before relying) | Complete the authorisation step in the developer portal |
SKILL.md:176:- .tmp/xero-refs/facts/docs-auth.md — token endpoint, Basic auth, form-encoded body, response fields, ~30 min lifetime (Token types page), single-org quote, AU/NZ/UK/US restriction, scopes-at-both-ends, granular migration dates (29 Apr 2026 / Sep 2027), two-header call example with no xero-tenant-id (explicit "not required" sentence absent = UNVERIFIED), "tenanted endpoints only" wrinkle, rate limits + X-*Limit-Remaining/X-Rate-Limit-Problem/Retry-After headers, pagination (page/pageSize min 1, pagination.itemCount). Underlying URLs: developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/{custom-connections,client-credentials,token-types,limits}/, /documentation/api/accounting/requests-and-responses.
SKILL.md:181:- UNVERIFIED items marked inline: explicit tenant-header-not-required sentence; unauthorised-app exact failure mode (and whether empty /connections has other causes); whether new post-Apr-2026 custom connections can still request broad scopes.
